text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said: Islam is based on (the following) five (principles):
1. To testify that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and Muhammad is Allah's Messenger (п·ә).
2. To offer the (compulsory congregational) prayers dutifully and perfectly.
3. To pay Zakat (i.e. obligatory charity) . 
4. To perform Hajj. (i.e. Pilgrimage to Mecca)
5. To observe fast during the month of Ramadan.
narrator: Narrated Ibn 'Umar:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Faith (Belief) consists of more than sixty branches (i.e. parts). And Haya (This term "Haya" covers a large number of concepts which are to be taken together; amongst them are self respect, modesty, bashfulness, and scruple, etc.) is a part of faith."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A Muslim is the one who avoids harming Muslims with his tongue and hands. And a Muhajir (emigrant) is the one who gives up (abandons) all what Allah has forbidden."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr:

text: Some people asked Allah's Messenger (п·ә), "Whose Islam is the best? i.e. (Who is a very good Muslim)?" He replied, "One who avoids harming the Muslims with his tongue and hands."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa:

text: A man asked the Prophet (п·ә) , "What sort of deeds or (what qualities of) Islam are good?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, 'To feed (the poor) and greet those whom you know and those whom you do not Know (See Hadith No. 27).
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "None of you will have faith till he wishes for his (Muslim) brother what he likes for himself."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "By Him in Whose Hands my life is, none of you will have faith till he loves me more than his father and his children."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said "None of you will have faith till he loves me more than his father, his children and all mankind."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever possesses the following three qualities will have the sweetness (delight) of faith: 1. The one to whom Allah and His Apostle becomes dearer than anything else. 
2. Who loves a person and he loves him only for Allah's sake. 
3. Who hates to revert to Atheism (disbelief) as he hates to be thrown into the fire."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Love for the Ansar is a sign of faith and hatred for the Ansar is a sign of hypocrisy."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "A time will soon come when the best property of a Muslim will be sheep which he will take on the top of mountains and the places of rainfall (valleys) so as to flee with his religion from afflictions."
narrator: Narrated Abu Said Al-Khudri:

text: Whenever Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ordered the Muslims to do something, he used to order them deeds which were easy for them to do, (according to their strength and endurance). They said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! We are not like you. Allah has forgiven your past and future sins." So Allah's Apostle became angry and it was apparent on his face. He said, "I am the most Allah fearing, and know Allah better than all of you do."
narrator: Narrated 'Aisha:

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever possesses the following three qualities will taste the sweetness of faith: 
1. The one to whom Allah and His Apostle become dearer than anything else. 
2. Who loves a person and he loves him only for Allah's sake. 
3. Who hates to revert to disbelief (Atheism) after Allah has brought (saved) him out from it, as he hates to be thrown in fire."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "When the people of Paradise will enter Paradise and the people of Hell will go to Hell, Allah will order those who have had faith equal to the weight of a grain of mustard seed to be taken out from Hell. So they will be taken out but (by then) they will be blackened (charred). Then they will be put in the river of Haya' (rain) or Hayat (life) (the Narrator is in doubt as to which is the right term), and they will revive like a grain that grows near the bank of a flood channel. Don't you see that it comes out yellow and twisted"
narrator: Narrated Abu Said Al-Khudri:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "While I was sleeping I saw (in a dream) some people wearing shirts of which some were reaching up to the breasts only while others were even shorter than that. Umar bin Al-Khattab was shown wearing a shirt that he was dragging." The people asked, "How did you interpret it? (What is its interpretation) O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" He (the Prophet (п·ә) ) replied, "It is the Religion."
narrator: Narrated Abu Said Al-Khudri:

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) passed by an Ansari (man) who was admonishing his brother regarding Haya'. On that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Leave him as Haya' is a part of faith." (See Hadith 9)
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah (bin 'Umar):

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said: "I have been ordered (by Allah) to fight against the people until they testify that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and that Muhammad is Allah's Messenger (п·ә), and offer the prayers perfectly and give the obligatory charity, so if they perform that, then they save their lives and property from me except for Islamic laws and then their reckoning (accounts) will be done by Allah."
narrator: Narrated Ibn 'Umar:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was asked, "What is the best deed?" He replied, "To believe in Allah and His Apostle (Muhammad). The questioner then asked, "What is the next (in goodness)? He replied, "To participate in Jihad (religious fighting) in Allah's Cause." The questioner again asked, "What is the next (in goodness)?" He replied, "To perform Hajj (Pilgrim age to Mecca) 'Mubrur, (which is accepted by Allah and is performed with the intention of seeking Allah's pleasure only and not to show off and without committing a sin and in accordance with the traditions of the Prophet)."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: A person asked Allah's Messenger (п·ә) . "What (sort of) deeds in or (what qualities of) Islam are good?" He replied, "To feed (the poor) and greet those whom you know and those whom you don't know."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said: "I was shown the Hell-fire and that the majority of its dwellers were women who were ungrateful." It was asked, "Do they disbelieve in Allah?" (or are they ungrateful to Allah?) He replied, "They are ungrateful to their husbands and are ungrateful for the favors and the good (charitable deeds) done to them. If you have always been good (benevolent) to one of them and then she sees something in you (not of her liking), she will say, 'I have never received any good from you."
narrator: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas:

text: At Ar-Rabadha I met Abu Dhar who was wearing a cloak, and his slave, too, was wearing a similar one. I asked about the reason for it. He replied, "I abused a person by calling his mother with bad names." The Prophet said to me, 'O Abu Dhar! Did you abuse him by calling his mother with bad names You still have some characteristics of ignorance. Your slaves are your brothers and Allah has put them under your command. So whoever has a brother under his command should feed him of what he eats and dress him of what he wears. Do not ask them (slaves) to do things beyond their capacity (power) and if you do so, then help them.' "
narrator: Narrated Al-Ma'rur:

text: While I was going to help this man ('Ali Ibn Abi Talib), Abu Bakra met me and asked, "Where are you going?" I replied, "I am going to help that person." He said, "Go back for I have heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, 'When two Muslims fight (meet) each other with their swords, both the murderer as well as the murdered will go to the Hell-fire.' I said, 'O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! It is all right for the murderer but what about the murdered one?' Allah's Messenger (п·ә) replied, "He surely had the intention to kill his companion."
narrator: Narrated Al-Ahnaf bin Qais:

text: When the following Verse was revealed: "It is those who believe and confuse not their belief with wrong (worshipping others besides Allah.)" (6:83), the companions of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) asked, "Who is amongst us who had not done injustice (wrong)?" Allah revealed: "No doubt, joining others in worship with Allah is a great injustice (wrong) indeed." (31.13)
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah:

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The signs of a hypocrite are three: 
1. Whenever he speaks, he tells a lie. 
2. Whenever he promises, he always breaks it (his promise ). 
3. If you trust him, he proves to be dishonest. (If you keep something as a trust with him, he will not return it.)"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever has the following four (characteristics) will be a pure hypocrite and whoever has one of the following four characteristics will have one characteristic of hypocrisy unless and until he gives it up.
1. Whenever he is entrusted, he betrays. 
2. Whenever he speaks, he tells a lie. 
3. Whenever he makes a covenant, he proves treacherous. 
4. Whenever he quarrels, he behaves in a very imprudent, evil and insulting manner."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever establishes the prayers on the night of Qadr out of sincere faith and hoping to attain Allah's rewards (not to show off) then all his past sins will be forgiven."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The person who participates in (Holy battles) in Allah's cause and nothing compels him to do so except belief in Allah and His Apostles, will be recompensed by Allah either with a reward, or booty (if he survives) or will be admitted to Paradise (if he is killed in the battle as a martyr). Had I not found it difficult for my followers, then I would not remain behind any sariya going for Jihad and I would have loved to be martyred in Allah's cause and then made alive, and then martyred and then made alive, and then again martyred in His cause."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said: "Whoever establishes prayers during the nights of Ramadan faithfully out of sincere faith and hoping to attain Allah's rewards (not for showing off), all his past sins will be forgiven."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever observes fasts during the month of Ramadan out of sincere faith, and hoping to attain Allah's rewards, then all his past sins will be forgiven."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Religion is very easy and whoever overburdens himself in his religion will not be able to continue in that way. So you should not be extremists, but try to be near to perfection and receive the good tidings that you will be rewarded; and gain strength by worshipping in the mornings, the afternoons, and during the last hours of the nights." (See Fath-ul-Bari, Page 102, Vol 1).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If a person embraces Islam sincerely, then Allah shall forgive all his past sins, and after that starts the settlement of accounts, the reward of his good deeds will be ten times to seven hundred times for each good deed and one evil deed will be recorded as it is unless Allah forgives it."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa'id Al Khudri:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If any one of you improve (follows strictly) his Islamic religion then his good deeds will be rewarded ten times to seven hundred times for each good deed and a bad deed will be recorded as it is."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) came while a woman was sitting with me. He said, "Who is she?" I replied, "She is so and so," and told him about her (excessive) praying. He said disapprovingly, "Do (good) deeds which is within your capacity (without being overtaxed) as Allah does not get tired (of giving rewards) but (surely) you will get tired and the best deed (act of Worship) in the sight of Allah is that which is done regularly."
narrator: Narrated 'Aisha:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever said "None has the right to be worshipped but Allah and has in his heart good (faith) equal to the weight of a barley grain will be taken out of Hell. And whoever said: "None has the right to be worshipped but Allah and has in his heart good (faith) equal to the weight of a wheat grain will be taken out of Hell. And whoever said, "None has the right to be worshipped but Allah and has in his heart good (faith) equal to the weight of an atom will be taken out of Hell."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Once a Jew said to me, "O the chief of believers! There is a verse in your Holy Book Which is read by all of you (Muslims), and had it been revealed to us, we would have taken that day (on which it was revealed as a day of celebration." 'Umar bin Al-Khattab asked, "Which is that verse?" The Jew replied, "This day I have perfected your religion For you, completed My favor upon you, And have chosen for you Islam as your religion." (5:3) 'Umar replied,"No doubt, we know when and where this verse was revealed to the Prophet. It was Friday and the Prophet (п·ә) was standing at 'Arafat (i.e. the Day of Hajj)"
narrator: Narrated 'Umar bin Al-Khattab:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "(A believer) who accompanies the funeral procession of a Muslim out of sincere faith and hoping to attain Allah's reward and remains with it till the funeral prayer is offered and the burial ceremonies are over, he will return with a reward of two Qirats. Each Qirat is like the size of the (Mount) Uhud. He who offers the funeral prayer only and returns before the burial, will return with the reward of one Qirat only."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Abusing a Muslim is Fusuq (an evil doing) and killing him is Kufr (disbelief)."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah:

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went out to inform the people about the (date of the) night of decree (Al-Qadr) but there happened a quarrel between two Muslim men. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I came out to inform you about (the date of) the night of Al-Qadr, but as so and so and so and so quarrelled, its knowledge was taken away (I forgot it) and maybe it was better for you. Now look for it in the 7th, the 9th and the 5th (of the last 10 nights of the month of Ramadan)."
narrator: Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit:

text: I was informed by Abu Sufyan that Heraclius said to him, "I asked you whether they (followers of Muhammad) were increasing or decreasing. You replied that they were increasing. And in fact, this is the way of true Faith till it is complete in all respects. I further asked you whether there was anybody, who, after embracing his (the Prophets) religion (Islam) became displeased and discarded it. You replied in the negative, and in fact, this is (a sign of) true faith. When its delight enters the heart and mixes with them completely, nobody can be displeased with it."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The reward of deeds depends upon the intention and every person will get the reward according to what he has intended. So whoever emigrated for Allah and His Apostle, then his emigration was for Allah and His Apostle. And whoever emigrated for worldly benefits or for a woman to marry, his emigration was for what he emigrated for."
narrator: Narrated 'Umar bin Al-Khattab:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If a man spends on his family (with the intention of having a reward from Allah) sincerely for Allah's sake then it is a (kind of) alms-giving in reward for him.
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas'ud:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "You will be rewarded for whatever you spend for Allah's sake even if it were a morsel which you put in your wife's mouth."
narrator: Narrated Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas:

text:
I gave the pledge of allegiance to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) for the following: 
1. offer prayers perfectly 
2. pay the Zakat (obligatory charity) 
3. and be sincere and true to every Muslim.
narrator: Narrated Jarir bin Abdullah:

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) remained behind us in a journey. He joined us while we were performing ablution for the prayer which was over-due. We were just passing wet hands over our feet (and not washing them properly) so the Prophet (п·ә) addressed us in a loud voice and said twice or thrice: "Save your heels from the fire."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Amongst the trees, there is a tree, the leaves of which do not fall and is like a Muslim. Tell me the name of that tree." Everybody started thinking about the trees of the desert areas. And I thought of the date-palm tree but felt shy to answer the others then asked, "What is that tree, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ?" He replied, "It is the date-palm tree."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Amongst the trees, there is a tree, the leaves of which do not fall and is like a Muslim. Tell me the name of that tree." Everybody started thinking about the trees of the desert areas. And I thought of the date-palm tree. The others then asked, "Please inform us what is that tree, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" He replied, "It is the date-palm tree."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar:

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) gave a letter to a person and ordered him to go and deliver it to the Governor of Bahrain. (He did so) and the Governor of Bahrain sent it to Chousroes, who read that letter and then tore it to pieces. (The sub-narrator (Ibn Shihab) thinks that Ibn Al-Musaiyab said that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) invoked Allah against them (saying), "May Allah tear them into pieces, and disperse them all totally.)"
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Abbas:

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) wrote a letter or had an idea of writing a letter. The Prophet (п·ә) was told that they (rulers) would not read letters unless they were sealed. So the Prophet (п·ә) got a silver ring made with "Muhammad Allah's Messenger (п·ә)" engraved on it. As if I were just observing its white glitter in the hand of the Prophet.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to take care of us in preaching by selecting a suitable time, so that we might not get bored. (He abstained from pestering us with sermons and knowledge all the time).
narrator: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Facilitate things to people (concerning religious matters), and do not make it hard for them and give them good tidings and do not make them run away (from Islam).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik:

text: `Abdullah used to give a religious talk to the people on every Thursday. Once a man said, "O Aba `Abdur-Rahman! (By Allah) I wish if you could preach us daily." He replied, "The only thing which prevents me from doing so, is that I hate to bore you, and no doubt I take care of you in preaching by selecting a suitable time just as the Prophet (п·ә) used to do with us, for fear of making us bored."
narrator: Narrated Abu Wail:

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "If Allah wants to do good to a person, He makes him comprehend the religion. I am just a distributor, but the grant is from Allah. (And remember) that this nation (true Muslims) will keep on following Allah's teachings strictly and they will not be harmed by any one going on a different path till Allah's order (Day of Judgment) is established."
narrator: Narrated Muawiya:

text: We were with the Prophet (п·ә) and fresh dates of a palm tree were brought to him. On that he said, "Amongst the trees, there is a tree which resembles a Muslim." I wanted to say that it was the datepalm tree but as I was the youngest of all (of them) I kept quiet. And then the Prophet (п·ә) said, "It is the date-palm tree."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do not wish to be like anyone except in two cases. (The first is) A person, whom Allah has given wealth and he spends it righteously; (the second is) the one whom Allah has given wisdom (the Holy Qur'an) and he acts according to it and teaches it to others." (Fath-al-Bari page 177 Vol. 1)
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas`ud:

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) embraced me and said, "O Allah! Bestow on him the knowledge of the Book (Qur'an).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:

text: Once I came riding a she-ass and had (just) attained the age of puberty. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was offering the prayer at Mina. There was no wall in front of him and I passed in front of some of the row while they were offering their prayers. There I let the she-ass loose to graze and entered the row, and nobody objected to it.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:

text: When I was a boy of five, I remember, the Prophet (п·ә) took water from a bucket (used for getting water out of a well) with his mouth and threw it on my face.
narrator: Narrated Mahmud bin Rabi`a:

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "From among the portents of the Hour are (the following): 
1. Religious knowledge will be taken away (by the death of Religious learned men). 
2. (Religious) ignorance will prevail.
3. Drinking of Alcoholic drinks (will be very common).
4. There will be prevalence of open illegal sexual intercourse.
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text:
I will narrate to you a Hadith and none other than I will tell you about after it. I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying: From among the portents of the Hour are (the following): 
1. Religious knowledge will decrease (by the death of religious learned men). 
2. Religious ignorance will prevail. 
3. There will be prevalence of open illegal sexual intercourse. 
4. Women will increase in number and men will decrease in number so much so that fifty women will be looked after by one man.
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "While I was sleeping, I saw that a cup full of milk was brought to me and I drank my fill till I noticed (the milk) its wetness coming out of my nails. Then I gave the remaining milk to `Umar Ibn Al-Khattab" The companions of the Prophet (п·ә) asked, "What have you interpreted (about this dream)? "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ,!" he replied, "(It is religious) knowledge."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar:

text: Somebody said to the Prophet (during his last Hajj), "I did the slaughtering before doing the Rami.' The Prophet (п·ә) beckoned with his hand and said, "There is no harm in that." Then another person said. "I got my head shaved before offering the sacrifice." The Prophet (п·ә) beckoned with his hand saying, "There is no harm in that."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "(Religious) knowledge will be taken away (by the death of religious scholars) ignorance (in religion) and afflictions will appear; and Harj will increase." It was asked, "What is Harj, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" He replied by beckoning with his hand indicating "killing." (Fath-al-Bari Page 192, Vol. 1)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: `Uqba bin Al-Harith said that he had married the daughter of Abi Ihab bin `Aziz. Later on a woman came to him and said, "I have suckled (nursed) `Uqba and the woman whom he married (his wife) at my breast." `Uqba said to her, "Neither I knew that you have suckled (nursed) me nor did you tell me." Then he rode over to see Allah's Messenger (п·ә) at Medina, and asked him about it. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "How can you keep her as a wife when it has been said (that she is your foster-sister)?" Then `Uqba divorced her, and she married another man.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi Mulaika:

text: Once a man said to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I may not attend the (compulsory congregational) prayer because so and so (the Imam) prolongs the prayer when he leads us for it. The narrator added: "I never saw the Prophet (п·ә) more furious in giving advice than he was on that day. The Prophet said, "O people! Some of you make others dislike good deeds (the prayers). So whoever leads the people in prayer should shorten it because among them there are the sick the weak and the needy (having some jobs to do).
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud Al-Ansari:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was asked about things which he did not like, but when the questioners insisted, the Prophet got angry. He then said to the people, "Ask me anything you like." A man asked, "Who is my father?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "Your father is Hudhafa." Then another man got up and said, "Who is my father, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ?" He replied, "Your father is Salim, Maula (the freed slave) of Shaiba." So when `Umar saw that (the anger) on the face of the Prophet (п·ә) he said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! We repent to Allah (Our offending you).
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa:

text: One day Allah's Messenger (п·ә) came out (before the people) and `Abdullah bin Hudhafa stood up and asked (him) "Who is my father?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "Your father is Hudhafa." The Prophet (п·ә) told them repeatedly (in anger) to ask him anything they liked. `Umar knelt down before the Prophet (п·ә) and said thrice, "We accept Allah as (our) Lord and Islam as (our) religion and Muhammad as (our) Prophet." After that the Prophet (п·ә) became silent.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik:

text: Whenever the Prophet (п·ә) asked permission to enter, he knocked the door thrice with greeting and whenever he spoke a sentence (said a thing) he used to repeat it thrice. (See Hadith No. 261, Vol. 8).
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Whenever the Prophet (п·ә) spoke a sentence (said a thing), he used to repeat it thrice so that the people could understand it properly from him and whenever he asked permission to enter, (he knocked the door) thrice with greeting.
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) remained behind us in a journey. He joined us while we were performing ablution for the `Asr prayer which was over-due. We were just passing wet hands over our feet (not washing them properly) so the Prophet (п·ә) addressed us in a loud voice and said twice or thrice, "Save your heels from the fire."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr:

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said "Three persons will have a double reward: 
1. A Person from the people of the scriptures who believed in his prophet (Jesus or Moses) and then believed in the Prophet (п·ә) 
Muhammad (i .e. has embraced Islam). 
2. A slave who discharges his duties to Allah and his master. 
3. A master of a woman-slave who teaches her good manners and educates her in the best possible way (the religion) and 
 manumits her and then marries her."
narrator: Narrated Abu Burda's father:

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) came out while Bilal was accompanying him. He went towards the women thinking that they had not heard him (i.e. his sermon). So he preached them and ordered them to pay alms. (Hearing that) the women started giving alms; some donated their ear-rings, some gave their rings and Bilal was collecting them in the corner of his garment.
narrator: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas:

text: I said: "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Who will be the luckiest person, who will gain your intercession on the Day of Resurrection?" Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said: O Abu Huraira! "I have thought that none will ask me about it before you as I know your longing for the (learning of) Hadiths. The luckiest person who will have my intercession on the Day of Resurrection will be the one who said sincerely from the bottom of his heart "None has the right to be worshipped but Allah."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "Allah does not take away the knowledge, by taking it away from (the hearts of) the people, but takes it away by the death of the religious learned men till when none of the (religious learned men) remains, people will take as their leaders ignorant persons who when consulted will give their verdict without knowledge. So they will go astray and will lead the people astray."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr bin Al-`As:

text: Some women requested the Prophet (п·ә) to fix a day for them as the men were taking all his time. On that he promised them one day for religious lessons and commandments. Once during such a lesson the Prophet said, "A woman whose three children die will be shielded by them from the Hell fire." On that a woman asked, "If only two die?" He replied, "Even two (will shield her from the Hell-fire).
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:

text: as above (the sub narrators are different). Abu Huraira qualified the three children referred to in the above mentioned Hadith as not having reached the age of committing sins (i.e. age of puberty) .
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:

text: Whenever `Aisha (the wife of the Prophet) heard anything which she did not understand, she used to ask again till she understood it completely. Aisha said: "Once the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever will be called to account (about his deeds on the Day of Resurrection) will surely be punished." I said, "Doesn't Allah say: "He surely will receive an easy reckoning." (84.8) The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "This means only the presentation of the accounts but whoever will be argued about his account, will certainly be ruined."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abu Mulaika:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said. No doubt your blood, property, the sub-narrator Muhammad thought that Abu Bakra had also mentioned and your honor (chastity), are sacred to one another as is the sanctity of this day of yours in this month of yours. It is incumbent on those who are present to inform those who are absent." (Muhammad the Sub-narrator used to say, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) told the truth.") The Prophet (п·ә) repeated twice: "No doubt! Haven't I conveyed Allah's message to you.
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakra:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do not tell a lie against me for whoever tells a lie against me (intentionally) then he will surely enter the Hell-fire."
narrator: Narrated `Ali:

text: I said to my father, 'I do not hear from you any narration (Hadith) of Allah s Apostle as I hear (his narration) from so and so?" Az-Zubair replied. l was always with him (the Prophet) and I heard him saying "Whoever tells a lie against me (intentionally) then (surely) let him occupy, his seat in Hellfire.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Az-Zubair:

text: The fact which stops me from narrating a great number of Hadiths to you is that the Prophet (п·ә) said: "Whoever tells a lie against me intentionally, then (surely) let him occupy his seat in Hell-fire."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "Whoever (intentionally) ascribes to me what I have not said then (surely) let him occupy his seat in Hell-fire."
narrator: Narrated Salama:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Name yourselves with my name (use my name) but do not name yourselves with my Kunya name (i.e. Abul Qasim). And whoever sees me in a dream then surely he has seen me for Satan cannot impersonate me. And whoever tells a lie against me (intentionally), then (surely) let him occupy his seat in Hell-fire."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: There is none among the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) who has narrated more Hadiths than I except `Abdullah bin `Amr (bin Al-`As) who used to write them and I never did the same.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: One night Allah's Messenger (п·ә) got up and said, "Subhan Allah! How many afflictions have been descended tonight and how many treasures have been disclosed! Go and wake the sleeping lady occupants of these dwellings (his wives) up (for prayers). A well-dressed (soul) in this world may be naked in the Hereafter. "
narrator: Narrated Um Salama:

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) led us in the `Isha' prayer during the last days of his life and after finishing it (the prayer) (with Taslim) he said: "Do you realize (the importance of) this night?" Nobody present on the surface of the earth tonight will be living after the completion of one hundred years from this night."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I stayed overnight in the house of my aunt Maimuna bint Al-Harith (the wife of the Prophet (п·ә) ) while the Prophet (п·ә) was there with her during her night turn. The Prophet (п·ә) offered the `Isha' prayer (in the mosque), returned home and after having prayed four rak`at, he slept. Later on he got up at night and then asked whether the boy (or he used a similar word) had slept? Then he got up for the prayer and I stood up by his left side but he made me stand to his right and offered five rak`at followed by two more rak`at. Then he slept and I heard him snoring and then (after a while) he left for the (Fajr) prayer.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:

text: I said to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) "I hear many narrations (Hadiths) from you but I forget them." Allah's Apostle said, "Spread your Rida' (garment)." I did accordingly and then he moved his hands as if filling them with something (and emptied them in my Rida') and then said, "Take and wrap this sheet over your body." I did it and after that I never forgot any thing. Narrated Ibrahim bin Al-Mundhir: Ibn Abi Fudaik narrated the same as above (Hadith...119) but added that the Prophet (п·ә) had moved his hands as if filling them with something and then he emptied them in the Rida' of Abu Huraira.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: I have memorized two kinds of knowledge from Allah's Messenger (п·ә) . I have propagated one of them to you and if I propagated the second, then my pharynx (throat) would be cut (i.e. killed).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to me during Hajjat-al-Wida`: Let the people keep quiet and listen. Then he said (addressing the people), "Do not (become infidels) revert to disbelief after me by striking the necks (cutting the throats) of one another (killing each other).
narrator: Narrated Jarir:

text: A man came to the Prophet (п·ә) and asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! What kind of fighting is in Allah's cause? (I ask this), for some of us fight because of being enraged and angry and some for the sake of his pride and haughtiness." The Prophet (п·ә) raised his head (as the questioner was standing) and said, "He who fights so that Allah's Word (Islam) should be superior, then he fights in Allah's cause."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa:

text: I saw the Prophet (п·ә) near the Jamra and the people were asking him questions (about religious problems). A man asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I have slaughtered the Hadi (animal) before doing the Rami." The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "Do the Rami (now) and there is no harm." Another person asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I got my head shaved before slaughtering the animal." The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "Do the slaughtering (now) and there is no harm." So on that day, when the Prophet (п·ә) was asked about anything as regards the ceremonies of Hajj performed before or after its due time his reply was, "Do it (now) and there is no harm."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah:

text: Ibn Az-Zubair said to me, "Aisha used to tell you secretly a number of things. What did she tell you about the Ka`ba?" I replied, "She told me that once the Prophet (п·ә) said, 'O `Aisha! Had not your people been still close to the pre-Islamic period of ignorance (infidelity)! I would have dismantled the Ka`ba and would have made two doors in it; one for entrance and the other for exit." Later on Ibn Az-Zubair did the same.
narrator: Narrated Aswad:

text: I was informed that the Prophet (п·ә) had said to Mu`adh, "Whosoever will meet Allah without associating anything in worship with Him will go to Paradise." Mu`adh asked the Prophet, "Should I not inform the people of this good news?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "No, I am afraid, lest they should depend upon it (absolutely).
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Um-Sulaim came to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and said, "Verily, Allah is not shy of (telling you) the truth. Is it necessary for a woman to take a bath after she has a wet dream (nocturnal sexual discharge?) The Prophet replied, "Yes, if she notices a discharge." Um Salama, then covered her face and asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Does a woman get a discharge?" He replied, "Yes, let your right hand be in dust (An Arabic expression you say to a person when you contradict his statement meaning "you will not achieve goodness"), and that is why the son resembles his mother."
narrator: Narrated Um Salama:

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Amongst the trees there is a tree, the leaves of which do not fall and is like a Muslim, tell me the name of that tree." Everybody started thinking about the trees of the desert areas and I thought of the date-palm tree but felt shy (to answer). The others asked, "O Allah's Apostle! inform us of it." He replied, "it is the date-palm tree." I told my father what had come to my mind and on that he said, "Had you said it I would have preferred it to such and such a thing that I might possess."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:

text: I used to get the emotional urethral discharge frequently so I requested Al-Miqdad to ask the Prophet (п·ә) about it. Al-Miqdad asked him and he replied, "One has to perform ablution (after it)." (See Hadith No. 269).
narrator: Narrated `Ali:

text: "A man got up in the mosque and said: O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) 'At which place you order us that we should assume the Ihram?' Allah's Messenger (п·ә) replied, 'The residents of Medina should assure the Ihram from Dhil-Hulaifa, the people of Syria from Al-Juhfa and the people of Najd from Qarn." Ibn `Umar further said, "The people consider that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) had also said, 'The residents of Yemen should assume Ihram from Yalamlam.' " Ibn `Umar used to say, "I do not: remember whether Allah's Messenger (п·ә) had said the last statement or not?"
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: A man asked the Prophet (п·ә) : "What (kinds of clothes) should a Muhrim (a Muslim intending to perform `Umra or Hajj) wear? He replied, "He should not wear a shirt, a turban, trousers, a head cloak or garment scented with saffron or Wars (kinds of perfumes). And if he has no slippers, then he can use Khuffs (socks made from thick fabric or leather) but the socks should be cut short so as to make the ankles bare." (See Hadith No. 615, Vol. 2).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar:

text: Allah enjoined the prayer when He enjoined it, it was two rak`at only (in every prayer) both when in residence or on journey. Then the prayers offered on journey remained the same, but (the rak`at of) the prayers for non-travelers were increased.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§  the mother of believers:

text: We were ordered to bring out our menstruating women and veiled women in the religious gatherings and invocation of Muslims on the two `Id festivals. These menstruating women were to keep away from their Musalla. A woman asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ' What about one who does not have a veil?" He said, "Let her share the veil of her companion."
narrator: Narrated Um `Atiya:

text: Once Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ prayed with his Izar tied to his back while his clothes were Lying beside him on a wooden peg. Somebody asked him, "Do you offer your prayer in a single Izar?" He replied, "I did so to show it to a fool like you. Had anyone of us two garments in the lifetime of the Prophet?"
narrator: Narrated Muhammad bin Al-Munkadir:

text: I saw Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§  praying in a single garment and he said that he had seen the Prophet (п·ә) praying in a single garment.
narrator: Narrated Muhammad bin Al Munkadir:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) prayed in one garment and crossed its ends.
narrator: Narrated `Umar bin Abi Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I saw the Prophet (п·ә) offering prayers in a single garment in the house of Um-Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§ and he had crossed its ends around his shoulders.
narrator: Narrated `Umar bin Abi Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: In the house of Um-Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§ I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) offering prayers, wrapped in a single garment around his body with its ends crossed round his shoulders.
narrator: Narrated `Umar bin Abi Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A person asked Allah's Messenger (п·ә) about the offering of the prayer in a single garment. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) replied, "Has every one of you got two garments?"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "None of you should offer prayer in a single garment that does not cover the shoulders."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: I bear witness that I heard the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) saying, "Whoever prays in a single garment must cross its ends (over the shoulders)".
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: The men used to pray with the Prophet (п·ә) with their Izars tied around their necks as boys used to do; therefore the Prophet (п·ә) told the women not to raise their heads till the men sat down straight (while praying).
narrator: Narrated Sahl:

text: Once I was traveling with the Prophet (п·ә) and he said, "O Mughira! take this container of water." I took it and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went far away till he disappeared. He answered the call of nature and was wearing a Syrian cloak. He tried to take out his hands from its sleeve but it was very tight so he took out his hands from under it. I poured water and he performed ablution like that for prayers and passed his wet hands over his Khuff (socks made from thick fabric or leather) and then prayed .
narrator: Narrated Mughira bin Shu`b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: While Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was carrying stones (along) with the people of Mecca for (the building of) the Ka`ba wearing an Izar (waist-sheet cover), his uncle Al-`Abbas said to him, "O my nephew! (It would be better) if you take off your Izar and put it over your shoulders underneath the stones." So he took off his Izar and put it over his shoulders, but he fell unconscious and since then he had never been seen naked.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§ :

text:
A man stood up and asked the Prophet (п·ә) about praying in a single garment. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Has every one of you two garments?" A man put a similar question to `Umar on which he replied, "When Allah makes you wealthier then you should clothe yourself properly during prayers. Otherwise one can pray with an Izar and a Rida' (a sheet covering the upper part of the body.) Izar and a shirt, Izar and a Qaba', trousers and a Rida, trousers and a shirt or trousers and a Qaba', Tubban and a Qaba' or Tubban and a shirt."
 (The narrator added, "I think that he also said a Tubban and a Rida. ")
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
A person asked Allah's Messenger (п·ә), "What should a Ihram wear?" He replied, "He should not wear shirts, trousers, a burnus (a hooded cloak), or clothes which are stained with saffron or Werse (a kind of perfume). Whoever does not find a sandal to wear can wear Khuffs (socks made from thick fabric or leather), but these should be cut short so as not to cover the ankles.
And Ibn Abi Zayb also narrated this Hadith from Nafi, he also narrated the same from the Holy Prophet (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) forbade Ishtimal-As-Samma' (wrapping one's body with a garment so that one cannot raise its end or take one's hand out of it). He also forbade Al-Ihtiba' (sitting on buttocks with knees close to `Abdomen and feet apart with the hands circling the knees) while wrapping oneself with a single garment, without having a part of it over the private parts.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) forbade two kinds of sales i.e. Al-Limais and An-Nibadh (the former is a kind of sale in which the deal is completed if the buyer touches a thing, without seeing or checking it properly and the latter is a kind of a sale in which the deal is completed when the seller throws a thing towards the buyer giving him no opportunity to see, touch or check it) and (the Prophet (п·ә) forbade) also Ishtimal-As- Samma' and Al-Ihtiba' in a single garment.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: On the Day of Nahr (10th of Dhul-Hijja, in the year prior to the last Hajj of the Prophet (п·ә) when Abu Bakr was the leader of the pilgrims in that Hajj) Abu Bakr sent me along with other announcers to Mina to make a public announcement: "No pagan is allowed to perform Hajj after this year and no naked person is allowed to perform the Tawaf around the Ka`ba. Then Allah's Messenger (п·ә) sent `Ali to read out the Surat Bara'a (at-Tauba) to the people; so he made the announcement along with us on the day of Nahr in Mina: "No pagan is allowed to perform Hajj after this year and no naked person is allowed to perform the Tawaf around the Ka`ba."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I went to Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ and he was praying wrapped in a garment and his Rida was Lying beside him. When he finished the prayers, I said "O `Abdullah! You pray (in a single garment) while your Rida' is lying beside you." He replied, "Yes, I did it intentionally so that the ignorant ones like you might see me. I saw the Prophet (п·ә) praying like this. "
narrator: Narrated Muhammad bin Al-Munkadir:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to offer the Fajr prayer and some believing women covered with their veiling sheets used to attend the Fajr prayer with him and then they would return to their homes unrecognized .
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§ :

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) prayed in a Khamisa (a square garment) having marks. During the prayer, he looked at its marks. So when he finished the prayer he said, "Take this Khamisa of mine to Abu Jahm and get me his Inbijaniya (a woolen garment without marks) as it (the Khamisa) has diverted my attention from the prayer." 
Narrated `Aisha: 
The Prophet (п·ә) said, 'I was looking at its (Khamisa's) marks during the prayers and I was afraid that it may put me in trial (by taking away my attention).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§ :

text: `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§  had a Qiram (a thin marked woolen curtain) with which she had screened one side of her home. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Take away this Qiram of yours, as its pictures are still displayed in front of me during my prayer (i.e. they divert my attention from the prayer).
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was given a silken Farruj as a present. He wore it while praying. When he had finished his prayer, he took it off violently as if with a strong aversion to it and said, "It is not the dress of Allah-fearing pious people."
narrator: Narrated `Uqba bin 'Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was praying while I was in my menses, sitting beside him and sometimes his clothes would touch me during his prostration." Maimuna added, "He prayed on a Khumra (a small mat sufficient just for the face and the hands while prostrating during prayers).
narrator: Narrated Maimuna ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫШ§ :

text:
"My grandmother Mulaika invited Allah's Messenger (п·ә) for a meal which she herself had prepared. He ate from it and said, 'Get up! I will lead you in the prayer.' " 
Anas added, "I took my Hasir, washed it with water as it had become dark because of long use and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) stood on it. The orphan (Damira or Ruh) and I aligned behind him and the old lady (Mulaika) stood behind us. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) led us in the prayer and offered two rak`at and then left."
narrator: Narrated 'Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to pray on Khumra.
narrator: Narrated Maimuna ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "I used to sleep in front o Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and my legs were opposite his Qibla and in prostration he pushed my legs and I withdrew then and when he stood, I stretched them.' `Aisha added, "In those days the houses were without lights."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ( the wife of the Prophet п·ә):

text: Allah Messenger п·ә prayed while I was lying like a dead body on his family bed between him and his Qibla.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) prayed while `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ was lying between him and his Qibla on the bed on which they used to sleep.
narrator: Narrated `Urwa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We used to pray with the Prophet (п·ә) and some of us used to place the ends of their clothes at the place of prostration because of scorching heat.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  :

text: whether the Prophet (п·ә) had ever, prayed with his shoes on. He replied "Yes."
narrator: Narrated Abu Maslama Sa`id bin Yazid Al-Azdi I asked Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "I saw Jarir bin `Abdullah urinating. Then he performed ablution and passed his (wet) hands over his Khuffs (socks made from thick fabric or leather), stood up and prayed. He was asked about it. He replied that he had seen the Prophet (п·ә) doing the same." They approved of this narration as Jarir was one of those who embraced Islam very late.
narrator: Narrated Hammam bin Al-Harith:

text: I helped the Prophet (п·ә) in performing ablution and he passed his wet hands over his Khuffs and prayed.
narrator: Narrated Al-Mughira bin Shu'ba:

text: He saw a person bowing and prostrating imperfectly. When he finished his Salat, Hudhaifa told him that he had not offered Salat. The subnarrator added, "I think that Hudhaifa also said Were you to die you would die on a "Sunna" (legal way) other than that of Muhammad (п·ә)."
narrator: Narrated Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "When the Prophet (п·ә) prayed, he used to separate his arms from his body so widely that the whiteness of his armpits was visible."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin Malik Ibn Buhaina:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever prays like us and faces our Qibla and eats our slaughtered animals is a Muslim and is under Allah's and His Apostle's protection. So do not betray Allah by betraying those who are in His protection."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "I have been ordered to fight the people till they say: 'None has the right to be worshipped but Allah.' And if they say so, pray like our prayers, face our Qibla and slaughter as we slaughter, then their blood and property will be sacred to us and we will not interfere with them except legally and their reckoning will be with Allah."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "O Abu Hamza! What makes the life and property of a person sacred?" He replied, "Whoever says, 'None has the right to be worshipped but Allah', faces our Qibla during the prayers, prays like us and eats our slaughtered animal, then he is a Muslim, and has got the same rights and obligations as other Muslims have."
narrator: Narrated Maimun bin Siyah that he asked Anas bin Malik:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "While defecating, neither face nor turn your back to the Qibla but face either east or west." Abu Aiyub added. "When we arrived in Sham we came across some lavatories facing the Qibla; therefore we turned ourselves while using them and asked for Allah's forgiveness."
narrator: Narrated Abu Aiyub Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Someone came to Ibn `Umar and said, "Here is Allah's Messenger (п·ә) entering the Ka`ba." Ibn `Umar said, "I went there but the Prophet (п·ә) had come out of the Ka`ba and I found Bilal standing between its two doors. I asked Bilal, 'Did the Prophet (п·ә) pray in the Ka`ba?' Bilal replied, 'Yes, he prayed two rak`at between the two pillars which are to your left on entering the Ka`ba. Then Allah's Messenger (п·ә) came out and offered a two-rak`at prayer facing the Ka`ba.' "
narrator: Narrated Mujahid:

text: When the Prophet (п·ә) entered the Ka`ba, he invoked Allah in each and every side of it and did not pray till he came out of it, and offered a two-rak`at prayer facing the Ka`ba and said, "This is the Qibla."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to pray (optional, non-obligatory prayer) while riding on his mount (Rahila) wherever it turned, and whenever he wanted to pray the compulsory prayer he dismounted and prayed facing the Qibla.
narrator: Narrated Jabir:

text: While the people were offering the Fajr prayer at Quba' (near Medina), someone came to them and said: "It has been revealed to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) tonight, and he has been ordered to pray facing the Ka`ba." So turn your faces to the Ka`ba. Those people were facing Sham (Jerusalem) so they turned their faces towards Ka`ba (at Mecca).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:

text: "Once the Prophet (п·ә) offered five rak`at in Zuhr prayer. He was asked, "Is there an increase in the prayer?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "And what is it?" They said, "You have prayed five rak`at.' So he bent his legs and performed two prostrations (of Sahu).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) saw some sputum in the direction of the Qibla (on the wall of the mosque) and he disliked that and the sign of disgust was apparent from his face. So he got up and scraped it off with his hand and said, "Whenever anyone of you stands for the prayer, he is speaking in private to his Lord or his Lord is between him and his Qibla. So, none of you should spit in the direction of the Qibla but one can spit to the left or under his foot." The Prophet (п·ә) then took the corner of his sheet and spat in it and folded it and said, "Or you can do this. "
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saw sputum on the wall of the mosque in the direction of the Qibla and scraped it off. He faced the people and said, "Whenever any one of you is praying, he should not spit in front of him because in the prayer Allah is in front of him."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saw some nasal secretions, expectoration or sputum on the wall of the mosque in the direction of the Qibla and scraped it off.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , (the mother of faithful believers):

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saw some expectoration on the wall of the mosque; he took gravel and scraped it off and said, "If anyone of you wanted to spit he should neither spit in front of him nor on his right but he could spit either on his left or under his left foot."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira and Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saw some expectoration on the wall of the mosque; he took gravel and scraped it off and said, "If anyone of you wanted to spit, he should neither spit in front of him nor on his right but could spit either on his left or under his left foot."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira and Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "None of you should spit in front or on his right but he could spit either on his left or under his foot."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A faithful believer while in prayer is speaking in private to his Lord, so he should neither spit in front of him nor to his right side but he could spit either on his left or under his foot."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) saw sputum on (the wall of) the mosque in the direction of the Qibla and scraped it off with gravel. Then he forbade Spitting in front or on the right, but allowed it on one's left or under one's left foot.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Spitting in the mosque is a sin and its expiation is to bury it."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Prophet said, "If anyone of you stands for prayer, he should not spit in front of him because in prayer he is speaking in private to Allah and he should not spit on his right as there is an angel, but he can spit either on his left or under his left foot and bury it (i.e. expectoration).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) saw expectoration (on the wall of the mosque) in the direction of the Qibla and scraped it off with his hand. It seemed that he disliked it and the sign of disgust was apparent from his face. He said, "If anyone of you stands for the prayer, he is speaking in private to his Lord, (or) his Lord is between him and his Qibla, therefore he should not spit towards his Qibla, but he could spit either on his left or under his foot." Then he took the corner of his sheet and spat in it, folded it and said, "Or do this."
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Do you consider or see that my face is towards the Qibla? By Allah, neither your submissiveness nor your bowing is hidden from me, surely I see you from my back."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) led us in a prayer and then got up on the pulpit and said, "In your prayer and bowing, I certainly see you from my back as I see you (while looking at you.)"
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ordered for a horse race; the trained horses were to run from a place called Al-Hafya' to Thaniyat Al-Wada` and the horses which were not trained were to run from Al-Thaniya to the Masjid (mosque of) Bani Zuraiq. The sub narrator added: Ibn `Umar was one of those who took part in the race.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I found the Prophet (п·ә) in the mosque along with some people. He said to me, "Did Abu Talha send you?" I said, "Yes". He said, "For a meal?" I said, "Yes." Then he said to his companions, "Get up." They set out and I was ahead of them.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! If a man finds another man with his wife, (committing adultery) should the husband kill him?" Later on I saw them (the man and his wife) doing Li`an in the mosque (taking oaths, one accusing, and the other denying adultery).
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) came to my house and said, "Where do you like me to pray?" I pointed to a place. The Prophet then said, "Allahu Akbar", and we aligned behind him and he offered a two-rak`at prayer.
narrator: Narrated `Itban bin Malik:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to start every thing from the right (for good things) whenever it was possible in all his affairs; for example: in washing, combing or wearing shoes.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Um Habiba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ mentioned about a church they had seen in Ethiopia in which there were pictures. They told the Prophet (п·ә) about it, on which he said, "If any religious man dies amongst those people they would build a place of worship at his grave and make these pictures in it. They will be the worst creature in the sight of Allah on the Day of Resurrection."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) prayed in the sheep fold." Later on I heard him saying, "He prayed in the sheep folds before the construction of the, mosque."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "I saw Ibn `Umar praying while taking his camel as a Sutra in front of him and he said, "I saw the Prophet doing the same."
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:

text: The sun eclipsed and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) offered the eclipse prayer and said, "I have been shown the Hellfire (now) and I never saw a worse and horrible sight than the sight I have seen today."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) had said, "Offer some of your prayers (Nawafil) at home, and do not take your houses as graves."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§  :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Do not enter (the places) of these people where Allah's punishment had fallen unless you do so weeping. If you do not weep, do not enter (the places of these people) because Allah's curse and punishment which fell upon them may fall upon you."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ told Allah's Messenger (п·ә) about a church which she had seen in Ethiopia and which was called Mariya. She told him about the pictures which she had seen in it. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If any righteous pious man dies amongst them, they would build a place of worship at his grave and make these pictures in it; they are the worst creatures in the sight of Allah."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§  :

text: When the last moment of the life of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) came he started putting his 'Khamisa' on his face and when he felt hot and short of breath he took it off his face and said, "May Allah curse the Jews and Christians for they built the places of worship at the graves of their Prophets." The Prophet (п·ә) was warning (Muslims) of what those had done.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha and `Abdullah bin `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "May Allah's curse be on the Jews for they built the places of worship at the graves of their Prophets."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I used to sleep in the mosque of the Prophet (п·ә) while I was young and unmarried.
narrator: Narrated Nafi `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:

text: I saw seventy of As-Suffa men and none of them had a Rida' (a garment covering the upper part of the body). They had either Izars (only) or sheets which they tied round their necks. Some of these sheets reached the middle of their legs and some reached their heels and they used to gather them with their hands lest their private parts should become naked.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  :

text: I went to the Prophet (п·ә) in the mosque (the sub-narrator Mas`ar thought that Jabir had said, "In the forenoon.") He ordered me to pray two rak`at. He owed me some money and he repaid it to me and gave more than what was due to me.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If anyone of you enters a mosque, he should pray two rak`at before sitting."
narrator: Narrated Abu Qatada Al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The angels keep on asking Allah's forgiveness for anyone of you, as long as he is at his Musalla (praying place) and he does not pass wind (Hadath). They say, 'O Allah! Forgive him, O Allah! be Merciful to him."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: In the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) the mosque was built of adobes, its roof of the leaves of date-palms and its pillars of the stems of date-palms. Abu Bakr did not alter it. `Umar expanded it on the same pattern as it was in the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) by using adobes, leaves of date-palms and changing the pillars into wooden ones. `Uthman changed it by expanding it to a great extent and built its walls with engraved stones and lime and made its pillars of engraved stones and its roof of teak wood.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:

text: "Go to Abu Sa`id and listen to what he narrates." So we went and found him in a garden looking after it. He picked up his Rida', wore it and sat down and started narrating till the topic of the construction of the mosque reached. He said, "We were carrying one adobe at a time while `Ammar was carrying two. The Prophet (п·ә) saw him and started removing the dust from his body and said, "May Allah be Merciful to `Ammar. He will be inviting them (i.e. his murderers, the rebellious group) to Paradise and they will invite him to Hell-fire." `Ammar said, "I seek refuge with Allah from affliction."
narrator: Narrated `Ikrima Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said to me and to his son `Ali:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) sent someone to a woman telling her to "Order her slave, carpenter, to prepare a wooden pulpit for him to sit on."
narrator: Narrated Sahl ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A woman said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Shall I get something constructed for you to sit on as I have a slave who is a carpenter?" He replied, "Yes, if you like." So she had that pulpit constructed.
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: when people argued too much about his intention to reconstruct the mosque of Allah's Messenger (п·ә), "You have talked too much. I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, 'Whoever built a mosque, (Bukair thought that `Asim, another sub-narrator, added, "Intending Allah's Pleasure"), Allah would build for him a similar place in Paradise.' "
narrator: Narrated `Uthman bin `Affan:

text: "A man passed through the mosque carrying arrows. Allah's Apostle said to him, 'Hold them by their heads.' "
narrator: Narrated `Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever passes through our mosques or markets with arrows should hold them by their heads lest he should injure a Muslim."
narrator: Narrated Abu Burda bin `Abdullah (on the authority of his father):

text: I asked Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ "By Allah! Tell me the truth whether you heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, 'O Hassan! Reply on behalf of Allah's Messenger (п·ә). O Allah! Help him with the Holy Spirit." Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "Yes . "
narrator: Narrated Hassan bin Thabit Al-Ansari:

text:
One day I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) at the door of my house while some Ethiopians were playing in the mosque (displaying their skill with spears). Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was screening me with his Rida' so as to enable me to see their display. 
(`Urwa said that `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said, "I saw the Prophet (п·ә) and the Ethiopians were playing with their spears.")
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: In the mosque l asked Ibn Abi Hadrad to pay the debts which he owed to me and our voices grew louder. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) heard that while he was in his house. So he came to us raising the curtain of his room and said, "O Ka`b!" I replied, "Labaik, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)!" He said, "O Ka`b! reduce your debt to one half," gesturing with his hand. I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I have done so." Then Allah's Apostle said (to Ibn Abi Hadrad), "Get up and pay the debt to him."
narrator: Narrated Ka`b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A black man or a black woman used to sweep the mosque and he or she died. The Prophet (п·ә) asked about her (or him). He was told that she (or he) had died. He said, "Why did you not inform me? Show me his grave (or her grave)." So he went to her (his) grave and offered her (his) funeral prayer."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When the verses of Surat "Al-Baqara"' about the usury Riba were revealed, the Prophet (п·ә) went to the mosque and recited them in front of the people and then banned the trade of alcohol.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "A man or a woman used to clean the mosque." (A sub-narrator said, 'Most probably a woman..') Then he narrated the Hadith of the Prophet
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Last night a big demon (afreet) from the Jinns came to me and wanted to interrupt my prayers (or said something similar) but Allah enabled me to overpower him. I wanted to fasten him to one of the pillars of the mosque so that all of you could See him in the morning but I remembered the statement of my brother Solomon (as stated in Quran): My Lord! Forgive me and bestow on me a kingdom such as shall not belong to anybody after me (38.35)." The sub narrator Rauh said, "He (the demon) was dismissed humiliated."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) sent some horsemen to Najd and they brought a man called Thumama bin Uthal from Bani Hanifa. They fastened him to one of the pillars of the mosque. The Prophet (п·ә) came and ordered them to release him. He went to a (garden of) date-palms near the mosque, took a bath and entered the mosque again and said, "None has the right to be worshipped but Allah and Muhammad is His Apostle" (i.e. he embraced Islam).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: On the day of Al-Khandaq (battle of the Trench), the medial arm vein of (Sa`d bin Mu`ad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) was injured and the Prophet (п·ә) pitched a tent in the mosque to look after him. There was another tent for Banu Ghaffar in the mosque and the blood started flowing from Sa`d's tent to the tent of Bani Ghaffar. They shouted, "O occupants of the tent! What is coming from you to us?" They found that Sa`d' wound was bleeding profusely and Sa`d died in his tent.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I complained to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) that I was sick. He told me to perform the Tawaf behind the people while riding. So I did so and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was praying beside the Ka`ba and reciting the Sura starting with "Wat-tur wa kitabin mastur."
narrator: Narrated Um Salama:

text: Two of the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) departed from him on a dark night and were led by two lights like lamps (going in front of them from Allah as a miracle) lighting the way in front of them, and when they parted, each of them was accompanied by one of these lights till he reached their (respective) houses.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) in his fatal illness came out with a piece of cloth tied round his head and sat on the pulpit. After thanking and praising Allah he said, "There is no one who had done more favor to me with life and property than Abu Bakr bin Abi Quhafa. If I were to take a Khalil, I would certainly have taken Abu- Bakr but the Islamic brotherhood is superior. Close all the small doors in this mosque except that of Abu Bakr."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) arrived at Mecca and sent for `Uthman bin Talha. He opened the gate of the Ka`ba and the Prophet, Bilal, Usama bin Zaid and `Uthman bin Talha entered the Ka`ba and then they closed its door (from inside). They stayed there for an hour, and then came out." Ibn `Umar added, "I quickly went to Bilal and asked him (whether the Prophet (п·ә) had prayed). Bilal replied, 'He prayed in it.' I asked, 'Where?' He replied, 'Between the two pillars.' "Ibn `Umar added, "I forgot to ask how many rak`at he (the Prophet) had prayed in the Ka`ba."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) sent some horse men to Najd and they brought a man called Thumama bin Uthal from Bani Hanifa. They fastened him to one of the pillars of the mosque.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I was standing in the mosque and somebody threw a gravel at me. I looked and found that he was `Umar bin Al-Khattab. He said to me, "Fetch those two men to me." When I did, he said to them, "Who are you? (Or) where do you come from?" They replied, "We are from Ta'if." `Umar said, "Were you from this city (Medina) I would have punished you for raising your voices in the mosque of Allah's Messenger (п·ә)."
narrator: Narrated Al-Sa'ib bin Yazid:

text: During the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) I asked Ibn Abi Hadrad in the mosque to pay the debts which he owed to me and our voices grew so loud that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) heard them while he was in his house. So he came to us after raising the curtain of his room. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "O Ka`b bin Malik!" I replied, "Labaik, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)." He gestured with his hand to me to reduce the debt to one half. I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) have done it." Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said (to Ibn Hadrad), "Get up and pay it."
narrator: Narrated Ka`b bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "While the Prophet (п·ә) was on the pulpit, a man asked him how to offer the night prayers. He replied, 'Pray two rak`at at a time and then two and then two and so on, and if you are afraid of the dawn (the approach of the time of the Fajr prayer) pray one rak`a and that will be the witr for all the rak`at which you have offered." Ibn `Umar said, "The last rak`at of the night prayer should be odd, for the Prophet (п·ә) ordered it to be so.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§:

text: A man came to the Prophet (п·ә) while he was delivering the sermon and asked him how to offer the night prayers. The Prophet (п·ә) replied, 'Pray two rak`at at a time and then two and then two and so on and if you are afraid of dawn (the approach of the time of the Fajr prayer) pray one rak`a and that will be the witr for all the rak`at which you have prayed." Narrated 'Ubaidullah bin `Abdullah bin `Umar: A man called the Prophet (п·ә) while he was in the mosque.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) lying flat (on his back) in the mosque with one leg on the other." Narrated Sa`id bin Al-Musaiyab that `Umar and `Uthman used to do the same.
narrator: Narrated `Abbad bin Tamim that his uncle:

text: I had seen my parents following Islam since I attained the age of puberty. Not a day passed but the Prophet (п·ә) visited us, both in the mornings and evenings. My father Abu Bakr thought of building a mosque in the courtyard of his house and he did so. He used to pray and recite the Qur'an in it. The pagan women and their children used to stand by him and look at him with surprise. Abu Bakr was a Softhearted person and could not help weeping while reciting the Qur'an. The chiefs of the Quraish pagans became afraid of that (i.e. that their children and women might be affected by the recitation of Qur'an).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§,(the wife of the Prophet)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) clasped his hands, by interlacing his fingers.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar or Ibn `Amr  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЪҫЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "O `Abdullah bin `Amr! What will be your condition when you will be left with the sediments of (worst) people?" (They will be in conflict with each other).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ that:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A faithful believer to a faithful believer is like the bricks of a wall, enforcing each other." While (saying that) the Prophet (п·ә) clasped his hands, by interlacing his fingers.
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
"I saw Salim bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ looking for some places on the way and prayed there. He narrated that his father used to pray there, and had seen the Prophet (п·ә) praying at those very places." 
Narrated Nafi` on the authority of Ibn `Umar who said, "I used to pray at those places." Musa the narrator added, "I asked Salim on which he said, 'I agree with Nafi` concerning those places, except the mosque situated at the place called Sharaf Ar-Rawha."
narrator: Narrated Musa bin `Uqba:

text: The prophet п·ә performed the prayer at the place where the small Mosque is located that is near to the Mosque located on the height of Rauha. Hadrat Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ knew that place where the prophet п·ә perform prayer. He would say: When you stand up in the Mosque to perform prayer, that place lies in your right direction. When you are going to Makkah Mukarramah, the Mosque is located on the right side of the way. There is a stone's throw or a similar distance between this small Mosque and the big one.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The prophet п·ә would alight under any shady tree or any extensive and soft place near Rowaitha on the right side of the way (and then he would move on: Irfan) till he left the mound away that was nearly two miles away from Rowaitha. The upper part of this tree has snapped off and it has bent down from the middle. Now, it is standing on its root and there are many mounds near to it.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The prophet п·ә performed prayer on the mound that lies behind Arj (a place) when you are going towards "Hadbah" (a colossal mountain: Irfan). Two or three graves lie near to this Mosque, and there are stones on the upper and lower sides of these graves. On the right side of the way, near the trees which are on the way, Hadrat Abdullah , when the sun passed the meridian, would leave Arj at noon among these trees and perform the Zuhr prayers in that Mosque.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Apostle п·ә would dismount near those colossal trees which are on the left side of the way  and are connected with the pass near to Harsha (a mountain : Irfan) and this distance from the way is within an arrow's range. And Hadrat Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ would perform the prayer in the direction of that tree which was the nearest to the way and the tallest one.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The prophet п·ә would dismount near that pass which is near to Marr-Iz-Zahran in the direction of Madinah Munawwarah. And when he would descend from Safrawat, he п·ә would alight in the depression of that pass (valley: Irfan) on the left side of the way if you are going to Makkah Mukarramah. There is a stone's throw distance between the route and the place where Allah's Apostle п·ә would alight.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: When the prophet п·ә would alight at Dee Tuwa, he п·ә would spend night there and after performing the Fajr prayer, he proceeded to Makkah Mukarramah. The place where Allah' Apostle п·ә would perform prayer was the concrete mound and not that Mosque which had been built there but was the concrete mound under it.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The prophet п·ә would face the both edges (ways: irfan) of that mountain which is between it and the long mountain in the direction of the ka,aba. Then, he п·ә kept the Mosque which had been built their on the left sideof that Mosque which is situated on the edge of the mound. And the place where the prophet п·ә would perform prayer is on the black mound under it, and you, leaving an area of ten yards or similar to it from the mound, should perform prayer before the both edges of the mountain that is between you and the kaaba.
narrator: Narrator Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Once I came riding a she-ass when I had just attained the age of puberty. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was offering the prayer at Mina with no wall in front of him and I passed in front of some of the row. There I dismounted and let my she-ass loose to graze and entered the row and nobody objected to me about it.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
