text: Whenever Allah's Messenger (п·ә) came out on `Id day, he used to order that a Harba (a short spear) to be planted in front of him (as a Sutra for his prayer) and then he used to pray facing it with the people behind him and used to do the same while on a journey. After the Prophet (п·ә) , this practice was adopted by the Muslim rulers (who followed his traditions).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) led us, and prayed a two-rak`at Zuhr prayer and then a tworak` at `Asr prayer at Al-Batha' [??] with a short spear (planted) in front of him (as a Sutra) while women and donkeys were passing in front of him (beyond that stick).
narrator: Narrated `Aun bin Abi Juhaifa I heard my father saying:

text: The distance between the Musalla of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and the wall was just sufficient for a sheep to pass through .
narrator: Narrated Sahl (bin Sa`d):

text: The distance between the wall of the mosque and the pulpit was hardly enough for a sheep to pass through.
narrator: Narrated Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to get a Harba planted in front of him (as a Sutra) and pray behind it.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) came to us at midday and water was brought for his ablution. He performed ablution and led us in Zuhr and `Asr prayers with a short stpear (or stick) planted in front of him (as a Sutra), while women and donkeys were passing beyond it."
narrator: Narrated `Aun bin Abi Juhaifa that he had heard his father saying:

text: Whenever the Prophet (п·ә) went for answering the call of nature, I and another boy used to go after him with a staff, a stick or a short spear (or stick) and a tumbler of water and when he finished from answering the call of nature we would hand that tumbler of water to him.
narrator: Narrated Anas Ibn Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) came out at midday and offered a two-rak`at Zuhr and `Asr prayers at Al-Batha and a short spear (or stick) was planted in front of him (as a Sutra). He performed ablution and the people took the remaining water left after his ablution and rubbed their bodies with it.
narrator: Narrated Abu Juhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I used to accompany Salama bin Al-Akwa` ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and he used to pray behind the pillar which was near the place where the Qur'ans were kept. I said, "O Abu Muslim! I see you always seeking to pray behind this pillar." He replied, "I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) always seeking to pray near that pillar."
narrator: Narrated Yazid bin Al `Ubaid:

text: I saw the most famous people amongst the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) hurrying towards the pillars at the Maghrib prayer before the Prophet (п·ә) came for the prayer.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) entered the Ka`ba along with Usama bin Zaid, `Uthman bin Talha and Bilal and remained there for a long time. When they came out, I was the first man to enter the Ka`ba. I asked Bilal "Where did the Prophet (п·ә) pray?" Bilal replied, "Between the two front Pillars."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) entered the Ka`ba along with Usama bin Zaid, Bilal and `Uthman bin Talha Al-Hajabi and closed the door and stayed there for some time. I asked Bilal when he came out, 'What did the Prophet (п·ә) do?' He replied, 'He offered prayer with one pillar to his left and one to his right and three behind.' In those days the Ka`ba was supported by six pillars." Malik said: "There were two pillars on his (the Prophet's) right side."
narrator: Narrated Nafi,`Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Whenever 'Abdullah entered the Ka'bah, he used to go ahead leaving the door of the Ka'bah behind him. He would proceed on till the remaining distance between him and the opposite wall about three cubits. Then he would off prayer there where the Prophet (п·ә) had offered Salat, as Bilal informed me. Ibn 'Umar said, "It does not matter for any of us to offer prayers at any place inside the Ka'bah."
narrator: Narrated Nafi':

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) used to make his she-camel sit across and he would pray facing it (as a Sutra)." I asked, "What would the Prophet (п·ә) do if the she-camel was provoked and moved?" He said, "He would take its camel-saddle and put it in front of him and pray facing its back part (as a Sutra). And Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§  used to do the same." (This indicates that one should not pray except behind a Sutra).
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:

text: Do you make us (women) equal to dogs and donkeys? While I used to lie in my bed, the Prophet (п·ә) would come and pray facing the middle of the bed. I used to consider it not good to stand in front of him in his prayers. So I used to slip away slowly and quietly from the foot of the bed till I got out of my guilt.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Zaid bin Khalid sent him to Abi Juhaim to ask him what he had heard from Allah's Messenger (п·ә) about a person passing in front of another person who was praying. Abu Juhaim replied, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'If the person who passes in front of another person in prayer knew the magnitude of his sin he would prefer to wait for 40 (days, months or years) rather than to pass in front of him." Abu An-Nadr said, "I do not remember exactly whether he said 40 days, months or years."
narrator: Narrated Busr bin Sa`id that:

text: The things which annul the prayers were mentioned before me. They said, "Prayer is annulled by a dog, a donkey and a woman (if they pass in front of the praying people)." I said, "You have made us (i.e. women) dogs. I saw the Prophet (п·ә) praying while I used to lie in my bed between him and the Qibla. Whenever I was in need of something, I would slip away. for I disliked to face him."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§  :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to pray while I was sleeping across in his bed in front of him. Whenever he wanted to pray witr, he would wake me up and I would pray witr.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "I used to sleep in front of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) with my legs opposite his Qibla (facing him); and whenever he prostrated, he pushed my feet and I withdrew them and whenever he stood, I stretched them." `Aisha added, "In those days there were no lamps in the houses."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ the wife of the Prophet п·ә :

text: The things which annul prayer were mentioned before me (and those were): a dog, a donkey and a woman. I said, "You have compared us (women) to donkeys and dogs. By Allah! I saw the Prophet (п·ә) praying while I used to lie in (my) bed between him and the Qibla. Whenever I was in need of something, I disliked to sit and trouble the Prophet. So, I would slip away by the side of his feet."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to get up at night and pray while I used to lie across between him and the Qibla on his family's bed.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Shihab:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was praying and he was carrying Umama the daughters of Zainab, the daughter of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and she was the daughter of 'As bin Rabi`a bin `Abd Shams. When he prostrated, he put her down and when he stood, he carried her (on his neck).
narrator: Narrated Abu Qatada Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: My bed was beside the praying place (Musalla) of the Prophet (п·ә) and sometimes his garment fell on me while I used to lie in my bed.
narrator: Narrated Maimuna bint Al-Harith ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to pray while I used to sleep beside him during my periods (menses) and in prostration his garment used to touch me.
narrator: Narrated Maimuna ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: It is not good that you people have made us (women) equal to dogs and donkeys. No doubt I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) praying while I used to lie between him and the Qibla and when he wanted to prostrate, he pushed my legs and I withdrew them.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I gave the pledge of allegiance to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) for to offer prayers perfectly, to pay Zakat regularly, and to give good advice to every Muslim.
narrator: Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man kissed a woman (unlawfully) and then went to the Prophet (п·ә) and informed him. Allah revealed: And offer prayers perfectly At the two ends of the day And in some hours of the night (i.e. the five compulsory prayers). Verily! good deeds remove (annul) the evil deeds (small sins) (11.114). The man asked Allah's Messenger (п·ә), "Is it for me?" He said, "It is for all my followers."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I asked the Prophet (п·ә) "Which deed is the dearest to Allah?" He replied, "To offer the prayers at their early stated fixed times." I asked, "What is the next (in goodness)?" He replied, "To be good and dutiful to your parents" I again asked, "What is the next (in goodness)?" He replied, 'To participate in Jihad (religious fighting) in Allah's cause." `Abdullah added, "I asked only that much and if I had asked more, the Prophet (п·ә) would have told me more."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "If there was a river at the door of anyone of you and he took a bath in it five times a day would you notice any dirt on him?" They said, "Not a trace of dirt would be left." The Prophet (п·ә) added, "That is the example of the five prayers with which Allah blots out (annuls) evil deeds."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ,that he heard the Messenger of Allah п·ә :

text: "I do not find (now-a-days) things as they were (practiced) at the time of the Prophet." Somebody said "The prayer (is as it was.)" Anas said, "Have you not done in the prayer what you have done?
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ:

text: He visited Anas bin Malik at Damascus and found him weeping and asked him why he was weeping. He replied, "I do not know anything which I used to know during the life-time of Allah's Apostle except this prayer which is being lost (not offered as it should be)."
narrator: Narrated Az-Zuhri that:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whenever anyone of you offers his prayer he is speaking in private to his Lord. So he should not spit to his right but under his left foot." Qatada said, "He should not spit in front of him but to his left or under his feet." And Shu`ba said, "He should not spit in front of him, nor to his right but to his left or under his foot." Anas said: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "He should neither spit in the direction of his Qibla nor to his right but to his left or under his foot."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do the prostration properly and do not put your forearms flat with elbows touching the ground like a dog. And if you want to spit, do not spit in front, nor to the right for the person in prayer is speaking in private to his Lord."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If it is very hot, then pray the Zuhr prayer when it becomes (a bit) cooler, as the severity of the heat is from the raging of the Hell-fire."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Mu'adh-dhin (call-maker) of the Prophet (п·ә) pronounced the Adhan (call) for the Zuhr prayer but the Prophet said, "Let it be cooler, let it be cooler." Or said, 'Wait, wait, because the severity of heat is from the raging of the Hell-fire. In severe hot weather, pray when it becomes (a bit) cooler and the shadows of hillocks appear."
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) said, "In very hot weather delay the Zuhr prayer till it becomes (a bit) cooler because the severity of heat is from the raging of the Hell-fire.
 The Hell-fire of Hell complained to its Lord saying: O Lord! My parts are eating (destroying) one another. So Allah allowed it to take two breaths, one in the winter and the other in the summer. The breath in the summer is at the time when you feel the severest heat and the breath in the winter is at the time when you feel the severest cold."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Pray Zuhr prayer when it becomes (a bit) cooler as the severity of heat is from the raging of the Hell-fire."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We were with the Prophet (п·ә) on a journey and the Mu'adh-dhin (call maker for the prayer) wanted to pronounce the Adhan (call) for the Zuhr prayer. The Prophet (п·ә) said, 'Let it become cooler." He again (after a while) wanted to pronounce the Adhan but the Prophet (п·ә) said to him, "Let it become cooler till we see the shadows of hillocks." The Prophet (п·ә) added, "The severity of heat is from the raging of the Hell-fire, and in very hot weather pray (Zuhr) when it becomes cooler."
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar Al-Ghifari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) used to offer the Fajr (prayer) when one could recognize the person sitting by him (after the prayer) and he used to recite between 60 to 100 Ayat (verses) of the Qur'an. He used to offer the Zuhr prayer as soon as the sun declined (at noon) and the `Asr at a time when a man might go and return from the farthest place in Medina and find the sun still hot. (The sub-narrator forgot what was said about the Maghrib). He did not mind delaying the `Isha prayer to one third of the night or the middle of the night."
narrator: Narrated Abu Barza ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When we offered the Zuhr prayers behind Allah's Messenger (п·ә) we used to prostrate on our clothes to protect ourselves from the heat.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) prayed eight rak`at for the Zuhr and `Asr, and seven for the Maghrib and `Isha prayers in Medina." Ayyub said, "Perhaps those were rainy nights." He (Jabir bin Zaid) said: "May be."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to offer the `Asr prayer when the sunshine had not disappeared from my chamber.
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to offer the `Asr prayers at a time when the sunshine was still inside my chamber and no shadow had yet appeared in it.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to pray the `Asr prayers at a time when the sunshine was still inside my chamber and no shadow had yet appeared in it.
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: We used to pray the `Asr prayer and after that if someone happened to go to the tribe of Bani `Amr bin `Auf, he would find them still praying the `Asr (prayer).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We prayed the Zuhr prayer with `Umar bin `Abdul `Aziz and then went to Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and found him offering the `Asr prayer. I asked him, "O uncle! Which prayer have you offered?" He said 'The `Asr and this is (the time of) the prayer of Allah s Apostle which we used to pray with him."
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakr bin `Uthman bin Sahl bin Hunaif that he heard Abu Umama saying:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to offer the `Asr prayer at a time when the sun was still hot and high and if a person went to Al-`Awali (a place) of Medina, he would reach there when the sun was still high. Some of Al-`Awali of Medina were about four miles or so from the town.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We used to pray the `Asr and after that if one of US went to Quba' he would arrive there while the sun was still high.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever misses the `Asr prayer (intentionally) then it is as if he lost his family and property."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We were with Buraida ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ in a battle on a cloudy day and he said, "Offer the `Asr prayer early as the Prophet said, "Whoever leaves the `Asr prayer, all his (good) deeds will be annulled."
narrator: Narrated Abu Al-Malih:

text:
"We were with the Prophet (п·ә) and he looked at the moon--full-moon--and said, 'Certainly you will see your Lord as you see this moon and you will have no trouble in seeing Him. So if you can avoid missing (through sleep or business, etc.) a prayer before the sunrise (Fajr) and a prayer before sunset (`Asr), you must do so.' He then recited Allah's Statement: 
  ___And celebrate the praises Of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before (its) setting." (50.39)
           Isma`il said, "Offer those prayers and do not miss them."
narrator: Narrated Jarir:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Angels come to you in succession by night and day and all of them get together at the time of the Fajr and `Asr prayers. Those who have passed the night with you (or stayed with you) ascend (to the Heaven) and Allah asks them, though He knows everything about you, well, "In what state did you leave my slaves?" The angels reply: "When we left them they were praying and when we reached them, they were praying."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If anyone of you can get one rak`a of the `Asr prayer before sunset, he should complete his prayer. If any of you can get one rak`a of the Fajr prayer before sunrise, he should complete his prayer."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The example of Muslims, Jews and Christians is like the example of a man who employed laborers to work for him from morning till night. They worked till midday and they said, 'We are not in need of your reward.' So the man employed another batch and said to them, 'Complete the rest of the day and yours will be the wages I had fixed (for the first batch). They worked until the time of the `Asr prayer and said, 'Whatever we have done is for you.' He employed another batch. They worked for the rest of the day till sunset, and they received the wages of the two former batches."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We used to offer the Maghrib prayer with the Prophet (п·ә) and after finishing the prayer one of us may go away and could still see as Par as the spots where one's arrow might reach when shot by a bow.
narrator: Narrated Rafi` bin Khadij ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The era of the Hajjaj came (and he used to lead the prayer late, so) we asked Hazrat Jabir bin `Abdullah about it, and he said: "The Prophet (п·ә) used to pray the Zuhr at midday, and the `Asr at a time when the sun was still bright, the Maghrib after sunset (at its stated time) and the `Isha at a variable time. Whenever he saw the people assembled (for `Isha' prayer) he would pray earlier and if the people delayed, he would delay the prayer. And they or the Prophet (п·ә) used to offer the Fajr Prayers when it still dark."
narrator: Narrated Muhammad bin Amr bin Hasan bin Ali:

text: We used to pray the Maghrib prayer with the Prophet (п·ә) when the sun disappeared from the horizon.
narrator: Narrated Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) prayed seven rak`at together and eight rak`at together.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do not be influenced by bedouins regarding the name of your Maghrib prayer which is called `Isha' by them."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah Al-Muzani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "One night Allah's Messenger (п·ә) led us in the `Isha' prayer and that is the one called Al-`Atma [??] by the people. After the completion of the prayer, he faced us and said, "Do you know the importance of this night? Nobody present on the surface of the earth tonight will be living after one hundred years from this night." (See Hadith No. 575).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We asked Jabir bin `Abdullah about the prayers of the Prophet (п·ә) . He said, "He used to pray Zuhr prayer at midday, the `Asr when the sun was still hot, and the Maghrib after sunset (at its stated time). The `Isha was offered early if the people gathered, and used to be delayed if their number was less; and the morning prayer was offered when it was still dark. "
narrator: Narrated Muhammad bin `Amr:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) once delayed the `Isha' prayer and that was during the days when Islam still had not spread. The Prophet (п·ә) did not come out till `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ informed him that the women and children had slept. Then he came out and said to the people of the mosque: "None amongst the dwellers of the earth has been waiting for it (`Isha prayer) except you."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) disliked to sleep before the `Isha' prayer and to talk after it.
narrator: Narrated Abu Barza ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) delayed the `Isha' prayer till `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reminded him by saying, "The prayer!" The women and children have slept. Then the Prophet (п·ә) came out and said, 'None amongst the dwellers of the earth has been waiting for it (the prayer) except you." `Urwa said, "Nowhere except in Medina the prayer used to be offered (in those days)." He further said, "The Prophet (п·ә) used to offer the `Isha' prayer in the period between the disappearance of the twilight and the end of the first third of the night."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) delayed the `Isha' prayer till midnight and then he offered the prayer and said, "The people prayed and slept but you have been in prayer as long as you have been waiting for it (the prayer)." 
Anas added: As if I am looking now at the glitter of the ring of the Prophet (п·ә) on that night.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
We were with the Prophet (п·ә) on a full moon night. He looked at the moon and said, "You will certainly see your Lord as you see this moon, and there will be no trouble in seeing Him. So if you can avoid missing (through sleep, business, etc.) a prayer before the rising of the sun (Fajr) and before its setting (`Asr) you must do so. 
He (the Prophet (п·ә) ) then recited the following verse: 
   ''. . . And celebrate the praises Of Your Lord before The rising of the sun And before (its) setting." (50.39)
narrator: Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah:

text: My father said, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'Whoever prays the two cool prayers (`Asr and Fajr) will go to Paradise.' "
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakr bin Abi Musa:

text: "We took the "Suhur" (the meal taken before dawn while fasting is observed) with the Prophet (п·ә) and then stood up for the (morning) prayer." I asked him how long the interval between the two (Suhur and prayer) was. He replied, 'The interval between the two was just sufficient to recite fifty to Sixth 'Ayat."
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) and Zaid bin Thabit took the 'Suhur' together and after finishing the meal, the Prophet (п·ә) stood up and prayed (Fajr prayer)." I asked Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "How long was the interval between finishing their 'Suhur' and starting the prayer?" He replied, "The interval between the two was just sufficient to recite fifty 'Ayat." (Verses of the Qur'an).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I used to take the "Suhur" meal with my family and hasten so as to catch the Fajr (morning prayer) with Allah's Messenger (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The believing women covered with their veiling sheets used to attend the Fajr prayer with Allah's Apostle, and after finishing the prayer they would return to their home and nobody could recognize them because of darkness.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever could get one rak`a (of the Fajr prayer) before sunrise, he has got the (morning) prayer and whoever could get one rak`a of the `Asr prayer before sunset, he has got the (`Asr) prayer."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever could get one rak`a of a prayer, (in its proper time) he has got the prayer."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) forbade praying after the Fajr prayer till the sun rises and after the `Asr prayer till the sun sets." Narrated Ibn `Abbas: Some people told me the same narration (as above).
narrator: Narrated `Umar:

text: Ibn `Umar said, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'If the edge of the sun appears (above the horizon) delay the prayer till it becomes high, and if the edge of the sun disappears, delay the prayer till it sets (disappears completely).' "

Allah's Messenger (п·ә) forbade two kinds of sales, two kinds of dresses, and two prayers. He forbade offering prayers after the Fajr prayer till the rising of the sun and after the `Asr prayer till its setting. He also forbade "Ishtimal-Assama [??] " and "al-Ihtiba" in one garment in such a way that one's private parts are exposed towards the sky. He also forbade the sales called "Munabadha" and "Mulamasa." (See Hadith No. 354 and 355 Vol. 3).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "None of you should try to pray at sunrise or sunset."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "There is no prayer after the morning prayer till the sun rises, and there is no prayer after the `Asr prayer till the sun sets."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: You offer a prayer which I did not see being offered by Allah's Messenger (п·ә) when we were in his company and he certainly had forbidden it (i.e. two rak`at after the `Asr prayer).
narrator: Narrated Muawiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) forbade the offering of two prayers:
1. after the morning prayer till the sunrises. 
2. after the `Asr prayer till the sun sets.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I pray as I saw my companions praying. I do not forbid praying at any time during the day or night except at sunset and sunrise.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: By Allah, Who took away the Prophet. The Prophet (п·ә) never missed them (two rak`at) after the `Asr prayer till he met Allah and he did not meet Allah till it became heavy for him to pray while standing so he used to offer most of the prayers while sitting. (She meant the two rak`at after `Asr) He used to pray them in the house and never prayed them in the mosque lest it might be hard for his followers and he loved what was easy for them .
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "O son of my sister! The Prophet (п·ә) never missed two prostrations (i.e. rak`at) after the `Asr prayer in my house."
narrator: Narrated`Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) never missed two rak`at before the Fajr prayer and after the `Asr prayer openly and secretly.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Whenever the Prophet (п·ә) come to me after the `Asr prayer, he always prayed two rak`at.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha:

text: I was with Buraida on a cloudy day and he said, "Offer the `Asr prayer earlier as the Prophet (п·ә) said, 'Whoever leaves the `Asr prayer will have all his (good) deeds annulled." (See Hadith No. 527 and 528)
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abu Malih [??]:

text: On the day of Al-Khandaq (the battle of trench.) `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came cursing the disbelievers of Quraish after the sun had set and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) I could not offer the `Asr prayer till the sun had set." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "By Allah! I, too, have not prayed." So we turned towards Buthan, and the Prophet (п·ә) performed ablution and we too performed ablution and offered the `Asr prayer after the sun had set, and then he offered the Maghrib prayer.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If anyone forgets a prayer he should pray that prayer when he remembers it. There is no expiation except to pray the same." Then he recited: "Establish prayer for My (i.e. Allah's) remembrance." (20.14).
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came cursing the disbelievers (of Quraish) on the day of Al-Khandaq (the battle of Trench) and said, "I could not offer the `Asr prayer till the sun had set. Then we went to Buthan and he offered the (`Asr) prayer after sunset and then he offered the Maghrib prayer.
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Hell-fire of Hell complained to its Lord saying: O Lord! My parts are eating (destroying) one another. So Allah allowed it to take two breaths, one in the winter and the other in the summer. The breath in the summer is at the time when you feel the severest heat and the breath in the winter is at the time when you feel the severest cold."
"Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'Do not pray at the time of sunrise and at the time of sunset.'
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The people mentioned the fire and the bell (they suggested those as signals to indicate the starting of prayers), and by that they mentioned the Jews and the Christians. Then Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was ordered to pronounce Adhan for the prayer by saying its wordings twice, and for the Iqama (the call for the actual standing for the prayers in rows) by saying its wordings once. (Iqama is pronounced when the people are ready for the prayer).
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When the Muslims arrived at Medina, they used to assemble for the prayer, and used to guess the time for it. During those days, the practice of Adhan for the prayers had not been introduced yet. Once they discussed this problem regarding the call for prayer. Some people suggested the use of a bell like the Christians, others proposed a trumpet like the horn used by the Jews, but `Umar was the first to suggest that a man should call (the people) for the prayer; so Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ordered Bilal to get up and pronounce the Adhan for prayers.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was ordered to repeat the wording of the Adhan for prayers twice, and to pronounce the wording of the Iqama once except "Qad-qamat-is-salat".
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When the number of Muslims increased they discussed the question as to how to know the time for the prayer by some familiar means. Some suggested that a fire be lit (at the time of the prayer) and others put forward the proposal to ring the bell. Bilal was ordered to pronounce the wording of Adhan twice and of the Iqama once only.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was ordered to pronounce the wording of Adhan twice and of Iqama once only." The sub narrator Isma`il said, "I mentioned that to Aiyub and he added (to that), "Except Iqama (i.e. Qadqamat- is-salat which should be said twice).
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When the Adhan is pronounced Satan takes to his heels and passes wind with noise during his flight in order not to hear the Adhan. When the Adhan is completed he comes back and again takes to his heels when the Iqama is pronounced and after its completion he returns again till he whispers into the heart of the person (to divert his attention from his prayer) and makes him remember things which he does not recall to his mind before the prayer and that causes him to forget how much he has prayed."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "I see you liking sheep and the wilderness. So whenever you are with your sheep or in the wilderness and you want to pronounce Adhan for the prayer raise your voice in doing so, for whoever hears the Adhan, whether a human being, a jinn or any other creature, will be a witness for you on the Day of Resurrection." Abu Sa`id added, "I heard it (this narration) from Allah's Messenger (п·ә)."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whenever you hear the Adhan, say what the Mu'adh-dhin is saying.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
He had heard Muawiya repeating the words of Adhan up to "Wa ash-hadu anna Muhammadan rasulul-lah (and I testify that Muhammad is Allah's Messenger (п·ә).)"
Hadrat Ishaq, Wahb bin Jareer, Hisham have reported from Yahya similar to it.
narrator: Narrated `Isa bin Talha:

text: "Some of my companions told me that Hisham had said, "When the Mu'adh-dhin said, "Haiyi `alassala (come for the prayer)." Muawiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "La hawla wala quwata illa billah (There is neither might nor any power except with Allah)" and added, "We heard your Prophet saying the same."
narrator: Narrated Yahya as above (586) and added:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever after listening to the Adhan says, 'Allahumma Rabba hadhihi-dda` watit-tammah, was-salatil qa'imah, ati Muhammadan al-wasilata wal-fadilah, wa b`ath-hu maqaman mahmudan-il-ladhi wa`adtahu' [O Allah! Lord of this perfect call (perfect by not ascribing partners to You) and of the regular prayer which is going to be established, give Muhammad the right of intercession and illustriousness, and resurrect him to the best and the highest place in Paradise that You promised him (of)], then my intercession for him will be allowed on the Day of Resurrection".
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If the people knew the reward for pronouncing the Adhan and for standing in the first row (in congregational prayers) and found no other way to get that except by drawing lots they would draw lots, and if they knew the reward of the Zuhr prayer (in the early moments of its stated time) they would race for it (go early) and if they knew the reward of `Isha' and Fajr (morning) prayers in congregation, they would come to offer them even if they had to crawl."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once on a rainy muddy day, Ibn `Abbas delivered a sermon in our presence and when the Mu'adhdhin pronounced the Adhan and said, "Haiyi `ala-s-sala(t) (come for the prayer)" Ibn `Abbas ordered him to say 'Pray at your homes.' The people began to look at each other (surprisingly). Ibn `Abbas said. "It was done by one who was much better than I (i.e. the Prophet (п·ә) or his Mu'adh-dhin), and it is a license.'
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Al-Harith:

text: My father said that Allah s Apostle said, "Bilal pronounces 'Adhan at night, so keep on eating and drinking (Suhur) till Ibn Um Maktum pronounces Adhan." Salim added, "He was a blind man who would not pronounce the Adhan unless he was told that the day had dawned."
narrator: Narrated Salim bin `Abdullah:

text: It was the pratice of Allah's Apostle п·ә that When the Mu'adh-dhin pronounced the Adhan for Fajr prayer and the dawn became evident the Prophet ordered a two rak`at light prayer (Sunna) before the Iqama of the compulsory (congregational) prayer.
narrator: Narrated Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to offer two light rak`at between the Adhan and the Iqama of the Fajr prayer.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ pronounces the Adhan at night, so keep on eating and drinking (Suhur) till Ibn Um Maktum pronounces the Adhan."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Bilal pronounces the Adhan at night, so eat and drink (Suhur) till Ibn Um Maktum pronounces the Adhan."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said thrice, "There is a prayer between the two Adhans (Adhan and Iqama)," and added, "For the one who wants to pray."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mughaffal Al-Muzani:

text: "When the Mu'adh-dhin pronounced the Adhan, some of the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) would proceed to the pillars of the mosque (for the prayer) till the Prophet (п·ә) arrived and in this way they used to pray two rak`at before the Maghrib prayer. There used to be a little time between the Adhan and the Iqama." Shu`ba said, "There used to be a very short interval between the two (Adhan and Iqama).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to pray two light rak`at before the morning (compulsory) prayer after the day dawned and the Mu'adh-dhin had finished his Adhan. He then would lie on his right side till the Mu'adh-dhin came to pronounce the Iqama.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The prophet said, "There is a prayer between the two Adhans (Adhan and Iqama), there is a prayer between the two Adhans." And then while saying it the third time he added, "For the one who wants to (pray).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mughaffal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I came to the Prophet (п·ә) with some men from my tribe and stayed with him for twenty nights. He was kind and merciful to us. When he realized our longing for our families, he said to us, "Go back and stay with your families and teach them the religion, and offer the prayer and one of you should pronounce the Adhan for the prayer when its time is due and the oldest one amongst you should lead the prayer."
narrator: Narrated Malik bin Huwairith ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We were in the company of the Prophet (п·ә) on a journey and the Mu'adh-dhin wanted to pronounce the Adhan for the (Zuhr) prayer. The Prophet (п·ә) said to him, "Let it become cooler." Then he again wanted to pronounce the Adhan but the Prophet; said to him, "Let it become cooler." The Mu'adh-dhin again wanted to pronounce the Adhan for the prayer but the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Let it become cooler," till the shadows of the hillocks become equal to their sizes. The Prophet (п·ә) added, "The severity of the heat is from the raging of Hell."
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Two men came to the Prophet (п·ә) with the intention of a journey. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "When (both of) you set out, pronounce Adhan and then Iqama and the oldest of you should lead the prayer."
narrator: Narrated Malik bin Huwairith:

text: Once in a cold night, Ibn `Umar pronounced the Adhan for the prayer at Dajnan (the name of a mountain) and then said, "Pray at your homes", and informed us that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to tell the Mu'adh-dhin to pronounce Adhan and say, "Pray at your homes" at the end of the Adhan on a rainy or a very cold night during the journey."
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:

text: "I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) at a place called Al-Abtah. Bilal came and informed him about the prayer and then came out with a short spear (or stick) and planted it in front of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) at Al-Abtah and pronounced the Iqama."
narrator: Narrated The father of `Aun bin Abi Juhaifa said:

text: My father said, "I saw Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ turning his face from side to side while pronouncing the Adhan for the prayer."
narrator: Narrated `Aun bin Abi Juhaifa:

text: My father said, "While we were praying with the Prophet (п·ә) he heard the noise of some people. After the prayer he said, 'What is the matter?' They replied 'We were hurrying for the prayer.' He said, 'Do not make haste for the prayer, and whenever you come for the prayer, you should come with calmness, and pray whatever you get (with the people) and complete the rest which you have missed."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "When you hear the Iqama, proceed to offer the prayer with calmness and solemnity and do not make haste. And pray whatever you are able to pray and complete whatever you have missed.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: My father said. "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'If the Iqama is pronounced then do not stand for the prayer till you see me (in front of you).' "
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi Qatada:

text: My father said, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'If the Iqama is pronounced, then do not stand for the prayer till you see me (in front of you) and do it calmly.' "
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went out (of the mosque) when the Iqama had been pronounced and the rows straightened. The Prophet (п·ә) stood at his Musalla (praying place) and we waited for the Prophet (п·ә) to begin the prayer with Takbir. He left and asked us to remain in our places. We kept on standing till the Prophet returned and the water was trickling from his head for he had taken a bath (of Janaba).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once Iqama was pronounced and the people had straightened the rows, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went forward (to lead the prayer) but he was Junub, so he said, "Remain in your places." And he went out, took a bath and returned with water trickling from his head. Then he led the prayer.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: On the day of Al-Khandaq (the trench), `Umar bin Al-Khattab went to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! By Allah, I could not pray (the `Asr) till the sun had set." `Umar told this to the Prophet at the time when a fasting person had done Iftar (taken his meals). The Prophet (п·ә) then went to Buthan and I was with him. He performed ablution and offered the `Asr prayer after the sun had set and then the Maghrib prayer.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Once the Iqama was pronounced and the Prophet (п·ә) was talking to a man (in a low voice) in a corner of the mosque and he did not lead the prayer till (some of) the people had slept (dozed in a sitting posture) .
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once Iqama was pronounced a man came to the Prophet (п·ә) and detained him (from the prayer).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "By Him in Whose Hand my soul is I was about to order for collecting firewood (fuel) and then order Someone to pronounce the Adhan for the prayer and then order someone to lead the prayer then I would go from behind and burn the houses of men who did not present themselves for the (compulsory congregational) prayer. By Him, in Whose Hands my soul is, if anyone of them had known that he would get a bone covered with good meat or two (small) pieces of meat present in between two ribs, he would have turned up for the `Isha' prayer.'
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The prayer in congregation is twenty seven times superior to the prayer offered by person alone."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The prayer in congregation is twenty five times superior to the prayer offered by person alone."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
"I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, 'The reward of a prayer in congregation is twenty five times greater than that of a prayer offered by a person alone. The angels of the night and the angels of the day gather at the time of Fajr prayer.' " 
Abu Huraira then added, "Recite the Holy Book if you wish, for "Indeed, the recitation of the Qur'an in the early dawn (Fajr prayer) is ever witnessed." (17:78).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira :

text: The reward of the congregational prayer is twenty seven times greater (than that of the prayer offered by a person alone).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "Abu Ad-Darda' entered the house in an angry mood. I said to him. 'What makes you angry?' He replied, 'By Allah! I do not find the followers of Muhammad doing those good things (which they used to do before) except the offering of congregational prayer." (This happened in the last days of Abu Ad-Darda' during the rule of `Uthman) .
narrator: Narrated Um Ad-Darda:

text: "The people who get tremendous reward for the prayer are those who are farthest away (from the mosque) and then those who are next farthest and so on. Similarly one who waits to pray with the Imam has greater reward than one who prays and goes to bed. "
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reports that the prophet (п·ә) said:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "No prayer is heavier upon the hypocrites than the Fajr and the `Isha' prayers and if they knew what is in them (in reward), they would have attended them, even if (it was) crawling. Certainly, I felt the urge to order the Mu'adh-dhin (call-maker) so that he would pronounce Iqama, then order a man to lead the people (in prayer), then take a flame of fire so that I burn (the houses) upon those who had not left for the prayer yet."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said (to two persons), "Whenever the prayer time becomes due, you should pronounce Adhan and then Iqama and the older of you should lead the prayer."
narrator: Narrated Malik bin Huwairith:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The angels keep on asking for Allah's Blessing and Forgiveness for anyone of you as long as he is at his Musalla (praying place) and does not do Hadath (passes wind). The angels say, 'O Allah! Forgive him and be Merciful to him.' Each one of you is in the prayer as long as he is waiting for the prayer and nothing but the prayer detains him from going to his family."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Did Allah's Messenger (п·ә) wear a ring?" He said, "Yes. Once he delayed the `Isha' prayer till midnight and after the prayer, he faced us and said, 'The people prayed and have slept and you remained in prayer as long as you waited for it.' " Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ added, "As if I were just now observing the glitter of his ring."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Allah will prepare for him who goes to the mosque (every) morning and in the afternoon (for the congregational prayer) an honorable place in Paradise with good hospitality for (what he has done) every morning and afternoon goings.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) passed by a man praying two rak`at after the Iqama (had been pronounced). When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) completed the prayer, the people gathered around him (the Prophet) or that man and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to him (protesting), Are there four rak`at in Fajr prayer? Are there four rak`at in Fajr prayer?"
Ghundar and Muadh corroborated him from shoban in Malik's Hadith; and reported it Ibn-e-Ishaq,  Sa'd from Hadrat Abdullah bin Buhainah; and reported it Hammad, Sa'd, Hafs from Maalik.
narrator: Narrated Malik Ibn Buhaina:

text:
"When the Prophet (п·ә) became seriously ill and his disease became aggravated he asked for permission from his wives to be nursed in my house and he was allowed. He came out with the help of two men and his legs were dragging on the ground. He was between Al-`Abbas and another man." 'Ubaidullah said, "I told Ibn `Abbas what `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ had narrated and he said, 'Do you know who was the (second) man whose name `Aisha
ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ did not mention'" I said, 'No.' Ibn `Abbas said, 'He was `Ali Ibn Abi Talib.' "
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Once on a very cold and stormy night, Ibn `Umar pronounced the Adhan for the prayer and then said, "Pray in your homes." He (Ibn `Umar) added. "On very cold and rainy nights Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to order the Mu'adh-dhin to say, 'Pray in your homes.' "
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:

text: `Itban bin Malik used to lead his people (tribe) in prayer and was a blind man, he said to Allah's Apostle , "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! At times it is dark and flood water is flowing (in the valley) and I am blind man, so please pray at a place in my house so that I can take it as a Musalla (praying place)." So Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went to his house and said, "Where do you like me to pray?" 'Itban pointed to a place in his house and Allah's Messenger (п·ә), offered the prayer there.
narrator: Narrated Mahmud bin Rabi` Al-Ansari:

text: Ibn `Abbas addressed us on a (rainy and) muddy day and when the Mu'adh-dhin said, "Come for the prayer" Ibn `Abbas ordered him to say, "Pray in your homes." The people began to look at one another with surprise as if they did not like it. Ibn `Abbas said, "It seems that you thought ill of it but no doubt it was done by one who was better than I (i.e. the Prophet). It (the prayer) is a strict order and I disliked to bring you out." Ibn `Abbas narrated the same as above but he said, "I did not like you to make you sinful (in refraining from coming to the mosque) and to come (to the mosque) covered with mud up to the knees."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Al-Harith:

text: A cloud came and it rained till the roof started leaking and in those days the roof used to be of the branches of date-palms. Iqama was pronounced and I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә)s prostrating in water and mud and even I saw the mark of mud on his forehead.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "A man from Ansar said to the Prophet, 'I cannot pray with you (in congregation).' He was a very fat man and he prepared a meal for the Prophet (п·ә) and invited him to his house. He spread out a mat for the Prophet, and washed one of its sides with water, and the Prophet (п·ә) prayed two rak`at on it." A man from the family of Al-Jaruid asked Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "Did the Prophet (п·ә) used to pray the Duha (forenoon) prayer?" Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I did not see him praying the Duha prayer except on that day."
narrator: Narrated Anas  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If supper is served, and Iqama is pronounced one should start with the supper."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If the supper is served start having it before praying the Maghrib prayer and do not be hasty in finishing it."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'If the supper is served for anyone of you and the Iqama is pronounced, start with the supper and don't be in haste (and carry on eating) till you finish it." If food was served for Ibn `Umar and Iqama was pronounced, he never came to the prayer till he finished it (i.e. food) in spite of the fact that he heard the recitation (of the Qur'an) by the Imam (in the prayer).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If anyone of you is having his meals, he should not hurry up till he is; satisfied even if the prayer has been started."
Ibrahim bin Al-Mundhir has  reported this Hadith from Wahb bin Uthman and Wahb is  Madni.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) eating a piece of meat from the shoulder of a sheep and he was called for the prayer. He stood up, put down the knife and prayed but did not perform ablution.''
narrator: Narrated Ja`far bin `Amr bin Umaiya that his father said:

text: "What did the Prophet (п·ә) use to do in his house?" She replied, "He used to keep himself busy serving his family and when it was the time for prayer he would go for it."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "Malik bin Huwairith came to this Mosque of ours and said, 'I pray in front of you and my aim is not to lead the prayer but to show you the way in which the Prophet (п·ә) used to pray.' " I asked Abu Qilaba, "How did he use to pray?' " He replied, "(The Prophet (п·ә) used to pray) like this Sheikh of ours and the Sheikh used to sit for a while after the prostration, before getting up after the first rak`a. "
narrator: Narrated Abu Qilaba :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) became sick and when his disease became aggravated, he said, "Tell Abu Bakr to lead the prayer." `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "He is a softhearted man and would not be able to lead the prayer in your place." The Prophet (п·ә) said again, "Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer." She repeated the same reply but he said, "Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer. You are the companions of Joseph." So the messenger went to Abu Bakr (with that order) and he led the people in prayer in the lifetime of the Prophet.
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to lead the people in prayer during the fatal illness of the Prophet (п·ә) till it was Monday. When the people aligned (in rows) for the prayer the Prophet (п·ә) lifted the curtain of his house and started looking at us and was standing at that time. His face was (glittering) like a page of the Qur'an and he smiled cheerfully. We were about to be put to trial for the pleasure of seeing the Prophet, Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ retreated to join the row as he thought that the Prophet (п·ә) would lead the prayer. The Prophet (п·ә) beckoned us to complete the prayer and he let the curtain fall. On the same day he died."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) did not come out for three days. The people stood for the prayer and Abu Bakr went ahead to lead the prayer. (In the meantime) the Prophet (п·ә) caught hold of the curtain and lifted it. When the face of the Prophet (п·ә) appeared we had never seen a scene more pleasing than the face of the Prophet (п·ә) as it appeared then. The Prophet (п·ә) beckoned to Abu Bakr to lead the people in the prayer and then let the curtain fall. We did not see him (again) till he died.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) became seriously ill, he was told about the prayer. He said, 'Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in the prayer.' `Aisha said, 'Abu Bakr is a softhearted man and he would be overpowered by his weeping if he recited the Qur'an.' He said to them, 'Tell him (Abu Bakr) to lead the prayer. The same reply was given to him. He said again, 'Tell him to lead the prayer. You (women) are the companions of Joseph."
narrator: Narrated Hamza bin `Abdullah:

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ordered Abu Bakr to lead the people in the prayer during his illness and so he led them in prayer." `Urwa, a sub narrator, added, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) felt a bit relieved and came out and Abu Bakr was leading the people. When Abu Bakr saw the Prophet (п·ә) he retreated but the Prophet beckoned him to remain there. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) sat beside Abu Bakr. Abu Bakr was following the prayer of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and the people were following the prayer of Abu Bakr."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: We went to the Prophet (п·ә) and we were all young men and stayed with him for about twenty nights. The Prophet (п·ә) was very merciful. He said, "When you return home, impart religious teachings to your families and tell them to offer perfectly such and such a prayer at such and such a time and such and such a prayer at such and such a time. And at the time of the prayer one of you should pronounce the Adhan and the oldest of you should lead the prayer."
narrator: Narrated Malik bin Huwairith ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (came to my house and) asked permission for entering and I allowed him. He asked, "Where do you like me to pray in your house?" I pointed to a place which I liked. He stood up for prayer and we aligned behind him and he finished the prayer with Taslim and we did the same.
narrator: Narrated `Itban bin Malik Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) during his illness prayed at his house while sitting whereas some people prayed behind him standing. The Prophet (п·ә) beckoned them to sit down. On completion of the prayer, he said, 'The Imam is to be followed: bow when he bows, raise up your heads (stand erect) when he raises his head and when he says, 'Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah' (Allah heard those who sent praises to Him) say then 'Rabbana wa laka l-hamd' (O our Lord! All the praises are for You), and if he prays sitting then pray sitting."
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ the mother of the believers:

text: When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah" none of us bent his back (for prostration) till the Prophet (п·ә) prostrated and then we would prostrate after him. Narrated Abu 'Is-haq: As above.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara (and he was not a liar) :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Isn't he who raises his head before the Imam afraid that Allah may transform his head into that of a donkey or his figure (face) into that of a donkey?"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
When the earliest emigrants came to Al-`Usba [??] a place in Quba', before the arrival of the Prophet- Salim 
ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  , the slave of Abu Hudhaifa, who knew the Qur'an more than the others used to lead them in prayer.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Listen and obey (your chief) even if an Ethiopian whose head is like a raisin were made your chief."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If the Imam leads the prayer correctly then he and you will receive the rewards but if he makes a mistake (in the prayer) then you will receive the reward for the prayer and the sin will be his."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I went to 'Uthman bin Affan while he was besieged, and said to him, "You are the chief of all Muslims in general and you see what has befallen you. We are led in the Salat (prayer) by a leader of Al-Fitan (trials and afflictions etc.) and we are afraid of being sinful in following him." 'Uthman said. "As-Salat (the prayers) is the best of all deeds so when the people do good deeds do the same with them and when they do bad deeds, avoid those bad deeds." Az-Zuhri said, "In our opinion one should not offer Salat behind an effeminate person unless there is no alternative."
narrator: Narrated 'Ubaid-Ullah bin Adi bin Khiyar:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to Abu-Dhar, "Listen and obey (your chief) even if he is an Ethiopian with a head like a raisin."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik:

text: Once I passed the night in the house of my aunt Maimuna. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) offered the `Isha' prayer and then came to the house and offered four rak`at an slept. Later on, he woke up and stood for the prayer and I stood on his left side. He drew me to his right and prayed five rak`at and then two. He then slept till I heard him snoring (or heard his breath sounds). Afterwards he went out for the morning prayer.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text:
One night I slept at the house of (my aunt) Maimuna and the Prophet (п·ә) was there on that night. He performed ablution and stood up for the prayer. I joined him and stood on his left side but he drew me to his right and prayed thirteen rak`at and then slept till I heard his breath sounds. And whenever he slept, he used to breathe with audible sounds. The Mu'adh-dhin came to the Prophet (п·ә) and he went out and prayed the morning prayer) without repeating the ablution.
Amr said: I narrated this Hadith to Bukair and Bukair said: It is similarly reported from Kuraib.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Once I passed the night in the house of my aunt Maimuna. The Prophet (п·ә) stood for the night prayer and I joined him and stood on his left side but he drew me to his right by holding me by the head.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Mu`adh bin Jabal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to perform the  prayer along with the Prophet (п·ә) then he went back  and  led his people in the prayer.
narrator: Narrated Hadrat Jaabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Mu`adh bin Jabal used to pray with the Prophet (п·ә) and then go to lead his people in prayer Once he led the `Isha' prayer and recited Surat "Al-Baqara." Somebody left the prayer and Mu`adh criticized him. The news reached the Prophet (п·ә) and he said to Mu`adh, 'You are putting the people to trial,' and repeated it thrice (or said something similar) and ordered him to recite two medium Suras of Mufassal." (`Amr said that he had forgotten the names of those Suras).
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah :

text: A man came and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! By Allah, I keep away from the morning prayer only because So and so prolongs the prayer when he leads us in it." The narrator said, "I never saw Allah's Apostle more furious in giving advice than he was at that time. He then said, "Some of you make people dislike good deeds (the prayer). So whoever among you leads the people in prayer should shorten it because among them are the weak, the old and the needy."
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If anyone of you leads the people in the prayer, he should shorten it for amongst them are the weak, the sick and the old; and if anyone among your prays alone then he may prolong (the prayer) as much as he wishes. "
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:

text: A man came and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I keep away from the morning prayer because so-and-so (Imam) prolongs it too much." Allah's Messenger (п·ә) became furious and I had never seen him more furious than he was on that day. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "O people! Some of you make others dislike the prayer, so whoever becomes an Imam he should shorten the prayer, as behind him are the weak, the old and the needy.''
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to pray a short prayer (in congregation) but used to offer it in a perfect manner.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
"The Prophet (п·ә) said, 'When I stand for prayer, I intend to prolong it but on hearing the cries of a child, I cut it short, as I dislike to trouble the child's mother.' "
Bishr bin Bakr, Ibn-e-Mubarak and Baqiyya corroborated him from Auzai
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin 'Abi Qatada:

text: I never prayed behind any Imam a prayer lighter and more perfect than that behind the Prophet (п·ә) and he used to cut short the prayer whenever he heard the cries of a child lest he should put the child's mother to trial.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "When I start the prayer I intend to prolong it, but on hearing the cries of a child, I cut short the prayer because I know that the cries of the child will incite its mother's passions."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
The Prophet п·ә , said, "Whenever I start the prayer I intend to prolong it, but on hearing the cries of a child, I cut short the prayer because I know that the cries of the child will incite its mother's passions."
And Musa bin Isma'il said to us that Aban bin Yazid narrated to us, Qatadah said to us, Anas said to us that this hadith was narrated from the Prophet (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Mu`adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to pray with the Prophet (п·ә) and then go and lead his people (tribe) in the prayer.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) prayed two rak`at (instead of four) and finished his prayer. Dhul-Yadain asked him whether the prayer had been reduced or whether he had forgotten. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) asked the people whether Dhul-Yadain was telling the truth. The people replied in the affirmative. Then Allah's Apostle stood up, offered the remaining two rak`at and then finished his prayer with Taslim and then said, "Allahu Akbar." He followed it with two prostrations like ordinary prostrations or a bit longer.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) prayed two rak`at of Zuhr prayer (instead of four) and he was told that he had prayed two rak`at only. Then he prayed two more rak`at and finished them with the Taslim followed by two prostrations.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Straighten your rows or Allah will alter your faces."
narrator: Narrated An-Nu`man bin 'Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Straighten your rows, for I see you from behind my back.'
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once the Iqama was pronounced and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) faced us and said, "Straighten your rows and stand closer together, for I see you from behind my back.'
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Martyrs are those who die because of drowning, plague, an Abdominal disease, or of being buried alive by a falling building." And then he added, "If the people knew the Reward for the Zuhr prayer in its early time, they would race for it. If they knew the reward for the `Isha' and the Fajr prayers in congregation, they would join them even if they had to crawl. If they knew the reward for the first row, they would draw lots for it."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The Imam is (appointed) to be followed. So do not differ from him, bow when he bows, and say, "Rabbana-lakal hamd" if he says "Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah"; and if he prostrates, prostrate (after him), and if he prays sitting, pray sitting all together, and straighten the rows for the prayer, as the straightening of the rows is amongst those things which make your prayer a correct and perfect one. (See Hadith No. 657).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Straighten your rows as the straightening of rows is essential for a perfect and correct prayer. "
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
When he arrived in Madina, he was asked: Which thing have you found contrary to that of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) time? He said: "I have not found any change except this that you do not stand in alignment in your prayers."
Uqba bin has reported from Bushir bin Yasar that Hadrat Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came to us in Madinah Munawwarah and said this.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Straighten your rows for I see you from behind my back." Anas added, "Everyone of us used to put his shoulder with the shoulder of his companion and his foot with the foot of his companion."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I prayed with the Prophet (п·ә) one night and stood on his left side. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) caught hold of my head from behind and drew me to his right and then offered the prayer and slept. Later the Mu'adh-dhin came and the Prophet (п·ә) stood up for prayer without performing ablution.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: One night an orphan and I offered the prayers behind the Prophet (п·ә) in my house and my mother (Um Sulaim) was standing behind us (by herself forming a row).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: One night I stood to the left of the Prophet (п·ә) in the prayer but he caught hold of me by the hand or by the shoulder (arm) till he made me stand on his right and beckoned with his hand (for me) to go from behind (him). (Al-Kashmaihani [??] , Fath-ul-Bari).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to pray in his room at night. As the wall of the room was LOW, the people saw him and some of them stood up to follow him in the prayer. In the morning they spread the news. The following night the Prophet (п·ә) stood for the prayer and the people followed him. This went on for two or three nights. Thereupon Allah's Messenger (п·ә) did not stand for the prayer the following night, and did not come out. In the morning, the people asked him about it. He replied, that he way afraid that the night prayer might become compulsory.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) had a mat which he used to spread during the day and use as a curtain at night. So a number of people gathered at night facing it and prayed behind him.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) made a small room in the month of Ramadan (Sa`id said, "I think that Zaid bin Thabit said that it was made of a mat") and he prayed there for a few nights, and so some of his companions prayed behind him. When he came to know about it, he kept on sitting. In the morning, he went out to them and said, "I have seen and understood what you did. You should pray in your houses, for the best prayer of a person is that which he prays in his house except the compulsory prayers."
Affan, Wohaib, Musa, Abu Nasr, Busr , Hadrat Zaid bin Thabit  has reported  the same from  the prophet (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) rode a horse and fell down and the right side of his body was injured. On that day he prayed one of the prayers sitting and we also prayed behind him sitting. When the Prophet (п·ә) finished the prayer with Taslim, he said, "The Imam is to be followed and if he prays standing then pray standing, and bow when he bows, and raise your heads when he raises his head; prostrate when he prostrates; and if he says "Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah", you should say, "Rabbana wa laka l-hamd.:
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) fell from a horse and got injured so he led the prayer sitting and we also prayed sitting. When he completed the prayer he said, "The Imam is to be followed; if he says Takbir then say Takbir, bow if he bows; raise your heads when he raises his head, when he says, 'Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah say, 'Rabbana laka l-hamd', and prostrate when he prostrates."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The Imam is to be followed. Say the Takbir when he says it; bow if he bows; if he says 'Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah', say, ' Rabbana wa laka l-hamd', prostrate if he prostrates and pray sitting altogether if he prays sitting."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Mujahid said: "Regarding Allah's Statement: "We record that which they have sent before (them), and their traces" (36.12). 'Their traces' means 'their steps.' " And Anas said that the people of Bani Salima wanted to shift to a place near the Prophet (п·ә) but Allah's Messenger (п·ә) disliked the idea of leaving their houses uninhabited and said, "Don't you think that you will get the reward for your footprints." Mujahid said, "Their foot prints mean their foot steps and their going on foot."

"Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to raise both his hands up to the level of his shoulders when opening the prayer; and on saying the Takbir for bowing. And on raising his head from bowing he used to do the same and then say "Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah, Rabbana wa laka l-hamd." And he did not do that (i.e. raising his hands) in prostrations.
narrator: Narrated Salim bin `Abdullah:

text: I saw that whenever Allah's Messenger (п·ә) stood for the prayer, he used to raise both his hands up to the shoulders, and used to do the same on saying the Takbir for bowing and on raising his head from it and used to say, "Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah". But he did not do that (i.e. raising his hands) in prostrations.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I saw Malik bin Huwairith saying Takbir and raising both his hands (on starting the prayers and raising his hands on bowing and also on raising his head after bowing. Malik bin Huwairith said, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) did the same."
narrator: Narrated Abu Qilaba:

text: I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) opening the prayer with the Takbir and raising his hands to the level of his shoulders at the time of saying the Takbir, and on saying the Takbir for bowing he did the same; and when he said, "Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah ", he did the same and then said, "Rabbana wa laka lhamd." But he did not do the same on prostrating and on lifting the head from it."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Whenever Ibn `Umar started the prayer with Takbir, he used to raise his hands: whenever he bowed, he used to raise his hands (before bowing) and also used to raise his hands on saying, "Sami`a l-lahu liman hamidah", and he used to do the same on rising from the second rak`a (for the 3rd rak`a). Ibn `Umar said: "The Prophet (п·ә) used to do the same."
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:

text:
The people were ordered to place the right hand on the left forearm in the prayer. Abu Hazim said, "I knew that the order was from the Prophet (п·ә) ."
Ismail said: This  statement is 'ascribed to' and did not say: "Sahl ascribed this statment ".
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "You see me facing the Qibla; but, by Allah, nothing is hidden from me regarding your bowing and submissiveness and I see you from behind my back."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Perform the bowing and the prostrations properly. By Allah, I see you from behind me (or from behind my back) when you bow or prostrate."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet, Abu Bakr and `Umar used to start the prayer with "Al hamdu li l-lahi Rabbi l-`alamin (All praise is but to Allah, Lord of the Worlds).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Did Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to recite (the Qur'an) in the Zuhr and the `Asr prayers. He replied in the affirmative. We said, "How did you come to know about it?" He said, "By the movement of his beard."
narrator: Narrated Khabbab:

text: Whenever we offered prayer with the Prophet (п·ә) and he raised his head from the bowing, we used to remain standing till we saw him prostrating .
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (And Al-Bara was not a liar) :

text: Once solar eclipse occurred during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә). He offered the eclipse prayer. His companions asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! We saw you trying to take something while standing at your place and then we saw you retreating." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I was shown Paradise and wanted to have a bunch of fruit from it. Had I taken it, you would have eaten from it as long as the world remains."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) led us in prayer and then went up to the pulpit and beckoned with both hands towards the Qibla of the mosque and then said, "When I started leading you in prayer, I saw the display of Paradise and Hell on the wall of the mosque (facing the Qibla). I never saw good and bad as I have seen today." He repeated the last statement thrice.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "What is wrong with those people who look towards the sky during the prayer?" His talk grew stern while delivering this speech and he said, "They should stop (looking towards the sky during the prayer); otherwise their eyesight would be taken away."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I asked Allah's Messenger (п·ә) about looking hither and thither in prayer. He replied, "It is a way of stealing by which Satan takes away (a portion) from the prayer of a person."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) prayed on a Khamisa with marks on it and said, "The marks on it diverted my attention, take this Khamisa to Abu Jahm and bring an Inbijaniya (from him.)"
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) saw expectoration in the direction of the Qibla of the mosque while he was leading the prayer, and scratched it off. After finishing the prayer, he said, "Whenever any of you is in prayer he should know that Allah is in front of him. So none should spit in front of him in the prayer."
Musa bin Uqbah and Ibn-e-Abu Rawwad have reported  it from Nafi'i.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: While the Muslims were offering the Fajr prayer, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) suddenly appeared before them by living the curtain of the dwelling place of `Aisha, looked towards the Muslims who were standing in rows. He smiled with pleasure. Abu Bakr started retreating to join the row on the assumption that the Prophet wanted to come out for the prayer. The Muslims intended to leave the prayer (and were on the verge of being put to trial), but the Prophet (п·ә) beckoned them to complete their prayer and then he let the curtain fall. He died in the last hours of that day.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever does not recite Al-Fatiha in his prayer, his prayer is invalid."
narrator: Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Sa`d said, "I used to pray with them a prayer similar to that of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) (the prayer of Zuhr and `Asr) reducing nothing from them. I used to prolong the first two rak`at and shorten the last two rak`at." `Umar said to Sa`d "This was what we thought about you."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin Samura:

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) in Zuhr prayers used to recite Al-Fatiha along with two other Suras in the first two rak`at: a long one in the first rak`a and a shorter (Sura) in the second, and at times the verses were audible. In the `Asr prayer the Prophet (п·ә) used to recite Al-Fatiha and two more Suras in the first two rak`at and used to prolong the first rak`a. And he used to prolong the first rak`a of the Fajr prayer and shorten the second.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reports that his father said:

text: Did the Prophet (п·ә) used to recite the Qur'an in the Zuhr and the `Asr prayers? He replied in the affirmative. We said, "How did you come to know that?" He said, "From the movement of his beard."
narrator: Narrated Khabbab:

text: Did the Prophet (п·ә) used to recite the Qur'an in the Zuhr and the `Asr prayers. He replied in the affirmative. I said, "How did you come to know that?" He replied, "From the movement of his beard."
