Sometimes Allah's Messenger (п·ә) would not fast (for so many days) that we thought that he would not fast that month and he sometimes used to fast (for so many days) that we thought he would not leave fasting throughout that month and (as regards his prayer and sleep at night), if you wanted to see him praying at night, you could see him praying and if you wanted to see him sleeping, you could see him sleeping.
Sulaiman and Abu Khalid Ahmar corroborated him from Humaid.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Satan puts three knots at the back of the head of any of you if he is asleep. On every knot he reads and exhales the following words, 'The night is long, so stay asleep.' When one wakes up and remembers Allah, one knot is undone; and when one performs ablution, the second knot is undone, and when one prays the third knot is undone and one gets up energetic with a good heart in the morning; otherwise one gets up lazy and with a mischievous heart."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said in his narration of a dream that he saw, "He whose head was being crushed with a stone was one who learnt the Qur'an but never acted on it, and slept ignoring the compulsory prayers."
narrator: Narrated Samura bin Jundab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A person was mentioned before the Prophet (п·ә) and he was told that he had kept on sleeping till morning and had not got up for the prayer. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Satan urinated in his ears."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Our Lord, the Blessed, the Superior, comes every night down on the nearest Heaven to us when the last third of the night remains, saying: "Is there anyone to invoke Me, so that I may respond to invocation? Is there anyone to ask Me, so that I may grant him his request? Is there anyone seeking My forgiveness, so that I may forgive him?"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "How is the night prayer of the Prophet?" She replied, "He used to sleep early at night, and get up in its last part to pray, and then return to his bed. When the Mu'adh-dhin pronounced the Adhan, he would get up. If he was in need of a bath he would take it; otherwise he would perform ablution and then go out (for the prayer)."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I asked `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , "How is the prayer of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) during the month of Ramadan." She said, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) never exceeded eleven rak`at in Ramadan or in other months; he used to offer four rak`at-- do not ask me about their beauty and length, then four rak`at, do not ask me about their beauty and length, and then three rak`at." Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ further said, "I said, 'O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Do you sleep before offering the witr prayer?' He replied, 'O `Aisha! My eyes sleep but my heart remains awake'!"
narrator: Narrated Abu Salma bin `Abdur Rahman:

text: I did not see the Prophet (п·ә) reciting (the Qur'an) in the night prayer while sitting except when he became old; when he used to recite while sitting, and when thirty or forty verses remained from the Sura, he would get up and recite them and then bow.
narrator: Narrated `Aish ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: At the time of the Fajr prayer the Prophet (п·ә) asked Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "Tell me of the best deed you did after embracing Islam, for I heard your footsteps in front of me in Paradise." Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ replied, "I did not do anything worth mentioning except that whenever I performed ablution during the day or night, I prayed after that ablution as much as was written for me."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) entered the Mosque and saw a rope hanging in between its two pillars. He said, "What is this rope?" The people said, "This rope is for Zainab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ who, when she feels tired, holds it (to keep standing for the prayer.)" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Don't use it. Remove the rope. You should pray as long as you feel active, and when you get tired, sit down."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A woman from the tribe of Bani Asad was sitting with me and Allah's Apostle (п·ә) came to my house and said, "Who is this?" I said, "(She is) So and so. She does not sleep at night because she is engaged in prayer." The Prophet (п·ә) said disapprovingly: Do (good) deeds which is within your capacity as Allah never gets tired of giving rewards till you get tired of doing good deeds."
narrator: Narrated 'Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to me, "O `Abdullah! Do not be like so and so who used to pray at night and then stopped the night prayer."
Hadrat Umer bin Hakam bin Thauban reports that Abu Salamah has similarly reported this Hadith to him.
And Amr Bin Abu Salamah has corroborated him from Auzai.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr bin Al-`As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to me, "I have been informed that you offer Salat (prayer) all the night and observe Saum (fast) during the day." I said, "(Yes) I do so." He said, "If you do so, your eye sight will become weak and you will become weak. No doubt, your body has right on you, and your family has right on you, so observe Saum (for some days) and do not observe it (for some days), offer Salat (for sometime) and then sleep."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text:
That once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 
"Your brother, i.e. `Abdullah bin Rawaha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ does not say obscene (referring to his verses): Amongst us is Allah's Messenger (п·ә), who recites His Book when it dawns. He showed us the guidance, after we were blind. We believe that whatever he says will come true. And he spends his nights in such a way as his sides do not touch his bed. While the pagans were deeply asleep."
Oqail corroborated him and Zubaidi said: Zuhri informed me from Saeed and Aaraj and they did so from Hadrat Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) offered the `Isha' prayer (and then got up at the Tahajjud time) and offered eight rak`at and then offered two rak`at while sitting. He then offered two rak`at in between the Adhan and Iqama (of the Fajr prayer) and he never missed them.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to lie down on his right side, after offering two rak`at (Sunna) of the Fajr prayer.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: After offering the Sunna of the Fajr prayer, the Prophet (п·ә) used to talk to me, if I happen to be awake; otherwise he would lie down till the Iqama call was proclaimed (for the Fajr prayer).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: After offering the two rak`at (Sunna) the Prophet (п·ә) used to talk to me, if I happen to be awake; otherwise he would lie down.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was never more regular and particular in offering any Nawafil than the two rak`at (Sunna) of the Fajr prayer.
narrator: Narrated Hadrat Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to offer thirteen rak`at in the night prayer and on hearing the Adhan for the morning prayer, he used to offer two light rak`at.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to make the two rak`at before the Fajr prayer so light that I would wonder whether he recited Al-Fatiha (or not).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If anyone of you enters a Mosque, he should not sit until he has offered a tworak` at prayer."
narrator: Narrated Abu Qatada bin Rabi Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) led us and offered a two rak`at prayer and then went away.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I offered with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) a two rak`at prayer before the Zuhr prayer and two rak`at after the Zuhr prayer, two rak`at after Jumua, Maghrib and `Isha' prayers.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§:

text: While delivering a sermon, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "If anyone of you comes while the Imam is delivering the sermon or has come out for it, he should offer a two rak`at prayer."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I offered with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) eight rak`at (of Zuhr and `Asr prayers) together and seven rak`at (the Maghrib and the `Isha' prayers) together.' " I said, "O Abu Ash-shatha! I think he must have prayed the Zuhr late and the `Asr early; the `Isha early and the Maghrib late." Abu Ash-sha'tha' said, "I also think so." (See Hadith No. 518 Vol. 1).
narrator: Narrated  Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "Do you offer the Duha prayer?" He replied in the negative. I further asked, "Did `Umar use to pray it?" He (Ibn `Umar) replied in the negative. I again asked, "Did Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ use to pray it?" He replied in the negative. I again asked, "Did the Prophet (п·ә) use to pray it?" Ibn `Umar replied, "I don't think he did."
narrator: Narrated  Ibn `Umar:

text: Only Um Hani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ narrated to me that she had seen the Prophet (п·ә) offering the Duha prayer. She said, "On the day of the conquest of Mecca, the Prophet (п·ә) entered my house, took a bath and offered eight rak`at (of Duha prayers. I had never seen the Prophet (п·ә) offering such a light prayer but he performed bowing and prostrations perfectly .
narrator: Narrated `Abdur Rahman bin Abi Laila:

text: I never saw the Prophet (п·ә) offering the Duha prayer but I always offer it.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: My friend (the Prophet) advised me to do three things and I shall not leave them till I die, these are: To fast three days every month, to offer the Duha prayer, and to offer witr before sleeping.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "An Ansari man, who was very fat, said to the Prophet, 'I am unable to present myself for the prayer with you.' He prepared a meal for the Prophet (п·ә) and invited him to his house. He washed one side of a mat with water and the Prophet (п·ә) offered two Rakat on it." So and so, the son of so and so, the son of Al-Jarud asked Anas, "Did the Prophet (п·ә) use to offer the Duha prayer?" Anas replied, "I never saw him praying (the Duha prayer) except on that day."
narrator: Narrated  Anas bin Malik al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
I remember ten Rakat of Nawafil from the Prophet, two Rakat before the Zuhr prayer and two after it; two Rakat after Maghrib prayer in his house, and two Rakat after 'Isha' prayer in his house, and two Rakat before the Fajr prayer and at that time nobody would enter the house of the Prophet.
Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ told me that When the Moadhdhin would pronounce call to prayer at dawn, he п·ә would perform two units
narrator: Narrated Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) never missed four rak`at before the Zuhr prayer and two rak`at before the Fajr prayer.
Ibn-u-Abi Adiyy and Amr corroborated him from shobah.
narrator: Narrated Aisha:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Pray before the Maghrib (compulsory) prayer." He (said it thrice) and in the third time, he said, "Whoever wants to offer it can do so." He said so because he did not like the people to take it as a tradition.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah Al-Muzni ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I went to `Uqba bin 'Amir Al-Juhani  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and said, "Is it not surprising that Abi Tamim offers two rak`at before the Maghrib prayer?" `Uqba said, "We used to do so in the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә)." I asked him, "What prevents you from offering it now?" He replied, "Business."
narrator: Narrated Marthad bin `Abdullah Al-Yazani:

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Offer some of your prayers in your houses and do not make them graves."
Abdul Wahab corroborated him from Ayyub.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Uma ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I heard Abu Said ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ saying four words. He said, "I heard the Prophet (п·ә) (saying the following narrative)." He had participated in twelve holy battles with the Prophet.
narrator: Narrated Quza'a:
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do not set out on a journey except for three Mosques i.e. Al-Masjid-AI-Haram, the Mosque of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) , and the Mosque of Al-Aqsa, (Mosque of Jerusalem)."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "One prayer in my Mosque is better than one thousand prayers in any other mosque excepting Al-Masjid-AI-Haram."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) used to go to the Mosque of Quba every Saturday (sometimes) walking and (sometimes) riding." `Abdullah (Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ used to do the same.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§:
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to go to the Mosque of Quba (sometimes) walking and sometimes riding. Ibn-e-Numair, Obaidullah,  Nafi` 'i "He then would offer two rak`at (in the Mosque of Quba)."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Between my house and the pulpit there is a garden of the gardens of Paradise."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Zaid Al-Mazini ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  :
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Between my house and my pulpit there is a garden of the gardens of Paradise, and my pulpit is on my fountain tank (i.e. Al-Kauthar)."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text:
(freed slave of) Ziyad: I heard Abu Sa`id Al-khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrating four things from the Prophet (п·ә) and I appreciated them very much. He said, conveying the words of the Prophet. 
(1) "A woman should not go on a two day journey except with her husband or a Dhi-Mahram.
 (2) No fasting is permissible on two days: `Id-ul-Fitr and `Id-ul-Adha. 
(3) No prayer after two prayers, i.e. after the Fajr prayer till the sunrises and after the `Asr prayer till the sun sets. (4) Do not prepare yourself for a journey except to three Mosques, i.e. Al-Masjid-AI-Haram, the Mosque of Aqsa (Jerusalem) and my Mosque."
narrator: Narrated Qaza'a Maula:
chapterName: Virtues of Prayer at Masjid Makkah and Madinah

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) once led us in a prayer and offered two rak`at and got up (for the third rak`a) without sitting (after the second rak`a). The people also got up with him, and when he was about to finish his prayer, we waited for him to finish the prayer with Taslim but he said Takbir before Taslim and performed two prostrations while sitting and then finished the prayer with Taslim.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Buhaina ШұШ¶Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) got up after the second rak`a of the Zuhr prayer without sitting in between (the second and the third rak`at). When he finished the prayer he performed two prostrations (of Sahu) and then finished the prayer with Taslim.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Buhaina:

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) offered five rak`at in the Zuhr prayer, and somebody asked him whether there was some increase in the prayer. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "What is that?" He said, "You have offered five rak`at." So Allah's Messenger (п·ә) performed two prostrations of Sahu after Taslim.
narrator: Narrated' `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) led us in the `Asr or the Zuhr prayer and finished it with Taslim. Dhul-Yadain said to him, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Has the prayer been reduced?" The Prophet (п·ә) asked his companions in the affirmative. So Allah's Messenger (п·ә) I offered two more rak`at and then performed two prostrations (of Sahu). Sa`d said, "I saw that 'Urwa bin Az-Zubair had offered two rak`at in the Maghrib prayer and finished it with Taslim. He then talked (and when he was informed about it) he completed the rest of his prayer and performed two prostrations, and said, 'The Prophet (п·ә) prayed like this.' "
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: (the ally of Bani `Abdul Muttalib) Allah's Messenger (п·ә) stood up for the Zuhr prayer and he should have sat (after the second rak`a but he stood up for the third rak`a without sitting for Tashah-hud) and when he finished the prayer he performed two prostrations and said Takbir on each prostration while sitting, before ending (the prayer) with Taslim; and the people too performed the two prostrations with him instead of the sitting he forgot. Ibn-e-Jurail corroborated him from Ibn-e-Shihab in connected with Takbeer.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Buhaina Al-Asdi:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When the call for prayer is made, Satan takes to his heels passing wind so that he may not hear the Adhan and when the call is finished he comes back, and when the Iqama is pronounced, Satan again takes to his heels, and when the Iqama is finished he comes back again and tries to interfere with the person and his thoughts and say, "Remember this and that (which he has not thought of before the prayer)", till the praying person forgets how much he has prayed. If anyone of you does not remember whether he has offered three or four rak`at then he should perform two prostrations of Sahu while sitting.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When anyone of you stands for the prayers, Satan comes and puts him in doubts till he forgets how many rak`at he has prayed. So if this happens to anyone of you, he should perform two prostrations of Sahu while sitting.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I went to `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and she was standing praying and the people, too, were standing (praying). So I said, "What is the matter with the people?" She beckoned with her head towards the sky. I said, "(Is there) a sign?" She nodded intending to say, "Yes."
narrator: Narrated Asma ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) during his illness prayed in his house sitting, whereas some people followed him standing, but the Prophet (п·ә) beckoned them to sit down. On completion of the prayer he said, "The Imam is to be followed. So, bow when he bows, and raise your head when he raises his head." (See Hadith No. 657 Vol 1 for taking the verdict).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ the wife of the Prophet:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) sent Mu`adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ to Yemen and said, "Invite the people to testify that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and I am Allah's Messenger (п·ә), and if they obey you to do so, then teach them that Allah has enjoined on them five prayers in every day and night (in twenty-four hours), and if they obey you to do so, then teach them that Allah has made it obligatory for them to pay the Zakat from their property and it is to be taken from the wealthy among them and given to the poor."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: A Bedouin came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "Tell me of such a deed as will make me enter Paradise, if I do it." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Worship Allah, and worship none along with Him, offer the (five) prescribed compulsory prayers perfectly, pay the compulsory Zakat, and fast the month of Ramadan." The Bedouin said, "By Him, in Whose Hands my life is, I will not do more than this." When he (the Bedouin) left, the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever likes to see a man of Paradise, then he may look at this man." Narrated Abu Zur'a: From the Prophet (п·ә) the same as above.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I gave the pledge of allegiance to the Prophet (п·ә) for offering prayer perfectly, giving Zakat, and giving good advice to every Muslim.
narrator: Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever is made wealthy by Allah and does not pay the Zakat of his wealth, then on the Day of Resurrection his wealth will be made like a baldheaded poisonous male snake with two black spots over the eyes. The snake will encircle his neck and bite his cheeks and say, 'I am your wealth, I am your treasure.' " Then the Prophet (п·ә) recited the holy verses:-- 'Let not those who withhold . . .' (to the end of the verse). (3.180).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We went out with 'Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ and a bedouin said (to 'Abdullah), "Tell me about Allah's saying: "And those who hoard up gold and silver (Al-Kanz - money, gold, silver etc., the Zakat of which has not been paid) and spend it not in the Way of Allah (V.9:34)." Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "Whoever hoarded them and did not pay the Zakat thereof, then woe to him. But these holy Verses were revealed before the Verses of Zakat. So when the Verses of Zakat were revealed, Allah made Zakat a purifier of the property."
narrator: Narrated Khalid bin Aslam:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "No Zakat is due on property mounting to less than five Uqiyas (of silver), and no Zakat is due on less than five camels, and there is no Zakat on less than five Wasqs." (A Wasqs equals 60 Sa's) & (1 Sa=3 K gms App.)
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "There is no envy except in two: a person whom Allah has given wealth and he spends it in the right way, and a person whom Allah has given wisdom (i.e. religious knowledge) and he gives his decisions accordingly and teaches it to the others."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "O people! Give in charity as a time will come upon you when a person will wander about with his object of charity and will not find anybody to accept it, and one (who will be requested to take it) will say, "If you had brought it yesterday, would have taken it, but today I am not in need of it."
narrator: Narrated Haritha bin Wahab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The Hour (Day of Judgment) will not be established till your wealth increases so much so that one will be worried, for no one will accept his Zakat and the person to whom he will give it will reply, 'I am not in need of it.' "
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A time will come upon the people when a person will wander about with gold as Zakat and will not find anybody to accept it, and one man will be seen followed by forty women to be their guardian because of scarcity of men and great number of women. "
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When the verses of charity were revealed, we used to work as porters. A man came and distributed objects of charity in abundance. And they (the people) said, "He is showing off." And another man came and gave a Sa (a small measure of food grains); they said, "Allah is not in need of this small amount of charity." And then the Divine Inspiration came: "Those who criticize such of the believers who give in charity voluntarily and those who could not find to give in charity except what is available to them." (9.79).
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Whenever Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ordered us to give in charity, we used to go to the market and work as porters and get a Mudd (a special measure of grain) and then give it in charity. (Those were the days of poverty) and today some of us have one hundred thousand.
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard the prophet (п·ә) saying ",Save yourself from Hell-fire even by giving half a date-fruit in charity."
narrator: Narrated `Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying:

text: A lady along with her two daughters came to me asking (for some alms), but she found nothing with me except one date which I gave to her and she divided it between her two daughters, and did not eat anything herself, and then she got up and went away. Then the Prophet (п·ә) came in and I informed him about this story. He said, "Whoever is put to trial by these daughters and he treats them generously (with benevolence) then these daughters will act as a shield for him from Hell-Fire." (See Hadith No. 24, Vol. 8).
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: A man came to the Prophet (п·ә) and asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Which charity is the most superior in reward?" He replied, "The charity which you practice while you are healthy, niggardly and afraid of poverty and wish to become wealthy. Do not delay it to the time of approaching death and then say, 'Give so much to such and such, and so much to such and such.' And it has already belonged to such and such (as it is too late)."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Some of the wives of the Prophet (п·ә) asked him, "Who amongst us will be the first to follow you (i.e. die after you)?" He said, "Whoever has the longest hand." So they started measuring their hands with a stick and Sauda's hand turned out to be the longest. (When Zainab bint Jahsh died first of all in the caliphate of `Umar), we came to know that the long hand was a symbol of practicing charity, so she was the first to follow the Prophet (п·ә) and she used to love to practice charity. (Sauda died later in the caliphate of Muawiya).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: My grandfather, my father and I gave the pledge of allegiance to Allah's Messenger (п·ә). The Prophet (п·ә) got me engaged and then got me married. One day I went to the Prophet (п·ә) with a complaint. My father Yazid had taken some gold coins for charity and kept them with a man in the mosque (to give them to the poor) But I went and took them and brought them to him (my father). My father said, "By Allah! I did not intend to give them to you. " I took (the case) to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) . On that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "O Yazid! You will be rewarded for what you intended. O Man! Whatever you have taken is yours."
narrator: Narrated Ma'n bin Yazid:

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "(O people!) Give in charity (for Allah's cause) because a time will come when a person will carry his object of charity from place to place (and he will not find any person to take it) and any person whom he shall request to take it, I will reply, 'If you had brought it yesterday I would have taken it, but today I am not in need of it."
narrator: Narrated Haritha bin Wahab Al-Khuza`i ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When a woman gives in charity some of the foodstuff (which she has in her house) without spoiling it, she will receive the reward for what she has spent, and her husband will receive the reward because of his earning, and the storekeeper will also have a reward similar to it. The reward of one will not decrease the reward of the others . "
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The best charity is that which is practiced by a wealthy person. And start giving first to your dependents."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The upper hand is better than the lower hand (i.e. he who gives in charity is better than him who takes it). One should start giving first to his dependents. And the best object of charity is that which is given by a wealthy person (from the money which is left after his expenses). And whoever abstains from asking others for some financial help, Allah will give him and save him from asking others, Allah will make him self-sufficient." Hadrat Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ also reported it from the prophet (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Hakim bin Hizam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) while he was on the pulpit speaking about charity, to abstain from asking others for some financial help and about begging others, saying, "The upper hand is better than the lower hand. The upper hand is that of the giver and the lower (hand) is that of the beggar."
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) offered the `Asr prayer and then hurriedly went to his house and returned immediately. I (or somebody else) asked him (as to what was the matter) and he said, "I left at home a piece of gold which was from the charity and I disliked to let it remain a night in my house, so I got it distributed . "
narrator: Narrated `Uqba bin Al-Harith ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) went out for the `Id prayer on the `Id day and offered a two rak`at prayer; and he neither offered a prayer before it or after it. Then he went towards the women along with Bilal. He preached them and ordered them to give in charity. And some (amongst the women) started giving their forearm bangles and earrings.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "Whenever a beggar came to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) or he was asked for something, he used to say (to his companions), "Help and recommend him and you will receive the reward for it; and Allah will bring about what He will through His Prophet's tongue."
narrator: Narrated Abu Burda bin Abu Musa that his father:

text:
The Prophet (п·ә) said to me, "Do not withhold your money, (for if you did so) Allah would withhold His blessings from you."
Narrated `Abda: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do not withhold your money by counting it (i.e. hoarding it), (for if you did so), Allah would also withhold His blessings from you."
narrator: Narrated Asma ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: She had gone to the Prophet (п·ә) and he said, "Do not shut your money bag; otherwise Allah too will withhold His blessings from you. Spend (in Allah's Cause) as much as you can afford. "
narrator: Narrated Asma' bint Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I said to Allah's Messenger (п·ә), "Before embracing Islam I used to do good deeds like giving in charity, slave-manumitting, and the keeping of good relations with Kith and kin. Shall I be rewarded for those deeds?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "You became Muslim with all those good deeds (Without losing their reward)."
narrator: Narrated Hakim bin Hizam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When a woman gives in charity from her husband's meals without wasting the property of her husband, she will get a reward for it, and her husband too will get a reward for what he earned and the storekeeper will have the reward likewise."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "An honest Muslim storekeeper who carries out the orders of his master and pays fully what he has been ordered to give with a good heart and pays to that person to whom he was ordered to pay, is regarded as one of the two charitable persons."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If a woman gives in charity from her husband's house ..." (See next hadith)
narrator: Narrated 'Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If a woman gives in charity from her husband's house .." The Prophet (п·ә) also said, "If a lady gives meals (in charity) from her husband's house without spoiling her husband's property, she will get a reward and her husband will also get a reward likewise. The husband will get a reward because of his earnings and the woman because of her spending."
narrator: Narrate Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "When a woman gives in charity from her house meals in Allah's Cause without spoiling her husband's property, she will get a reward for it, and her husband will also get the reward for his earnings and the storekeeper will get a reward likewise."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Every day two angels come down from Heaven and one of them says, 'O Allah! Compensate every person who spends in Your Cause,' and the other (angel) says, 'O Allah! Destroy every miser."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Every Muslim has to give in charity." The people asked, "O Allah's Prophet! If someone has nothing to give, what will he do?" He said, "He should work with his hands and benefit himself and also give in charity (from what he earns)." The people further asked, "If he cannot find even that?" He replied, "He should help the needy who appeal for help." Then the people asked, "If he cannot do that?" He replied, "Then he should perform good deeds and keep away from evil deeds and this will be regarded as charitable deeds."
narrator: Narrated Abu Burda:

text: A sheep was sent to me (Nusaiba Al-Ansariya) (in charity) and I sent some of it to `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ . The Prophet asked `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ for something to eat. `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ replied that there was nothing except what Nusaiba Al-Ansariya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ had sent of that sheep. The Prophet (п·ә) said to her, "Bring it as it has reached its place."
narrator: Narrated Um 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "There is no Zakat on less than five camels and also there is no Zakat on less than five Awaq (of silver). (5 Awaq = 22 Fransa Riyals of Yemen or 200 Dirhams.) And there is no Zakat on less than five Awsuq. (A special measure of food-grains, and one Wasq equals 60 Sa's.) (For gold 20, Dinars i.e. equal to 12 Guinea English. No Zakat for less than 12 Guinea (English) of gold or for silver less than 22 Fransa Riyals of Yemen.)
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ wrote to me what Allah had instructed His Apostle (п·ә) to do regarding the one who had to pay one Bint Makhad (i.e. one year-old she-camel) as Zakat, and he did not have it but had got Bint Labun (two year old she-camel). (He wrote that) it could be accepted from him as Zakat, and the collector of Zakat would return him 20 Dirhams or two sheep; and if the Zakat payer had not a Bint Makhad, but he had Ibn Labun (a two year old he-camel) then it could be accepted as his Zakat, but he would not be paid anything .
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I am a witness that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) offered the Id prayer before delivering the sermon and then he thought that the women would not be able to hear him (because of the distance), so he went to them along with Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who was spreading his garment. The Prophet (п·ә) advised and ordered them to give in charity. So the women started giving their ornaments (in charity). (The sub-narrator Aiyub pointed towards his ears and neck meaning that they gave ornaments from those places such as earrings and necklaces.)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ wrote to me what was made compulsory by Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and that was (regarding the payments of Zakat): Neither the property of different people may be taken together nor the joint property may be split for fear of (paying more, or receiving less) Zakat.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ wrote to me what Allah's Messenger (п·ә) has made compulsory (regarding Zakat) and this was mentioned in it: If a property is equally owned by two partners, they should pay the combined Zakat and it will be considered that both of them have paid their Zakat equally.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A Bedouin asked Allah's Messenger (п·ә) about the emigration. The Prophet (p.b.u.h) said, "May Allah have mercy on you! The matter of emigration is very hard. Have you got camels? Do you pay their Zakat?" The Bedouin said, "Yes, I have camels and I pay their Zakat." The Prophet (п·ә) said, Work beyond the seas and Allah will not decrease (waste) any of your good deeds." (See Hadith No. 260 Vol. 5).
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ wrote to me what Allah had ordered His Apostle (about Zakat) which goes: Neither an old nor a defected animal, nor a male-goat may be taken as Zakat except if the Zakat collector wishes (to take it).
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "By Allah! If they (pay me the Zakat and) withhold even a she-kid which they used to pay during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә), I will fight with them for it."
`Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "It was nothing but Allah Who opened Abu Bakr's chest towards the decision to fight, and I came to know that his decision was right."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) sent Mu`adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ to Yemen, he said (to him), "YOU are going to people of a (Divine) Book. First of all invite them to worship Allah (alone) and when they come to know Allah, inform them that Allah has enjoined on them, five prayers in every day and night; and if they start offering these prayers, inform them that Allah has enjoined on them, the Zakat. And it is to be taken from the rich amongst them and given to the poor amongst them; and if they obey you in that, take Zakat from them and avoid (don't take) the best property of the people as Zakat."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "No Zakat is imposed on less than five Awsuq of dates; no Zakat is imposed on less than five Awaq of silver, and no Zakat is imposed on less than five camels."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "There is no Zakat either on a horse or a slave belonging to a Muslim."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There is no Zakat either on a slave or on a horse belonging to a Muslim."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I said "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Shall I receive a reward if I spend for the sustenance of Abu Salama's offspring, and in fact they are also my sons?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "Spend on them and you will get a reward for what you spend on them."
narrator: Narrated Zainab bint Um Salama:

text: Some Ansari persons asked for (something) from Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and he gave them. They again asked him for (something) and he again gave them. And then they asked him and he gave them again till all that was with him finished. And then he said "If I had anything. I would not keep it away from you. (Remember) Whoever abstains from asking others, Allah will make him contented, and whoever tries to make himself self-sufficient, Allah will make him self-sufficient. And whoever remains patient, Allah will make him patient. Nobody can be given a blessing better and greater than patience."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "By Him in Whose Hand my life is, it is better for anyone of you to take a rope and cut the wood (from the forest) and carry it over his back and sell it (as a means of earning his living) rather than to ask a person for something and that person may give him or not."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "It is better for anyone of you to take a rope (and cut) and bring a bundle of wood (from the forest) over his back and sell it and Allah will save his face (from the Hell-Fire) because of that, rather than to ask the people who may give him or not."
narrator: Narrated Az-Zubair bin Al-`Awwam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to give me something but I would say to him, "would you give it to a poorer and more needy one than l?" The Prophet (п·ә) said to me, "Take it. If you are given something from this property, without asking for it or having greed for it take it; and if not given, do not run for it."
narrator: Narrated `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The poor person is not the one who asks a morsel or two (of meals) from the others, but the poor is the one who has nothing and is ashamed to beg from others."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The clerk of Al-Mughira bin Shu`ba narrated, "Muawiya wrote to Al-Mughira bin Shu`ba: Write to me something which you have heard from the Prophet (p.b.u.h) ." So Al-Mughira wrote: I heard the Prophet saying, "Allah has hated for you three things: -1. Vain talks, (useless talk) that you talk too much or about others. -2. Wasting of wealth (by extravagance) -3. And asking too many questions (in disputed religious matters) or asking others for something (except in great need). (See Hadith No. 591, Vol. Ill)
narrator: Narrated Ash-Shu`bi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The poor person is not the one who goes round the people and ask them for a mouthful or two (of meals) or a date or two but the poor is that who has not enough (money) to satisfy his needs and whose condition is not known to others, that others may give him something in charity, and who does not beg of people."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "No doubt, it is better for a person to take a rope and proceed in the morning to the mountains and cut the wood and then sell it, and eat from this income and give alms from it than to ask others for something." Abu Abdullah said: Sualih bin Kaisaan was older than Zuhri and he met hadrat Ibn-e-Umer.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There is no Zakat on less than five Awsuq (of dates), or on less than five camels, or on less than five Awaq of silver." (22 Yemeni Riyals Faransa).
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Dates used to be brought to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) immediately after being plucked. Different persons would bring their dates till a big heap collected (in front of the Prophet). Once Al-Hasan and Al-Husain were playing with these dates. One of them took a date and put it in his mouth. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) looked at him and took it out from his mouth and said, "Don't you know that Muhammad's offspring do not eat what is given in charity?"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) had forbidden the sale of dates till they were good (ripe), and when it was asked what it meant, the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Till there is no danger of blight."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) had forbidden the sale of fruits till they were ripe (free from blight).
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) forbade the selling of fruits until they were ripe. The Prophet (p.b.u.h) added, "It means that they become red ."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ gave a horse in charity in Allah's Cause and later he saw it being sold in the market and intended to purchase it. Then he went to the Prophet (п·ә) and asked his permission. The Prophet said, "Do not take back what you have given in charity." For this reason, Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ never purchased the things which he had given in charity, and in case he had purchased something (unknowingly) he would give it in charity again.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Once I gave a horse in Allah's Cause (in charity) but that person did not take care of it. I intended to buy it, as I thought he would sell it at a low price. So, I asked the Prophet (п·ә) about it. He said, "Neither buy, nor take back your alms which you have given, even if the seller were willing to sell it for one Dirham, for he who takes back his alms is like the one who swallows his own vomit."
narrator: Narrated `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Al-Hasan bin `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ took a date from the dates given in charity and put it in his mouth. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Expel it from your mouth. Don't you know that we do not eat a thing which is given in charity?"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) saw a dead sheep which had been given in charity to a freed slave-girl of Maimuna ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Prophet (п·ә) . The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Why don't you get the benefit of its hide?" They said, "It is dead." He replied, "Only to eat (its meat) is illegal."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: `Aisha intended to buy Barira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (a slave-girl) in order to manumit her and her masters intended to put the condition that her Al-wala would be for them. `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ mentioned that to the Prophet (п·ә) who said to her, "Buy her, as the "Wala" is for the manumitted." Once some meat was presented to the Prophet (п·ә) and `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said to him, "This (meat) was given in charity to Barira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ." He said, "It is an object of charity for Barira but a gift for us."
narrator: Narrated Al-Aswad:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) went to `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and asked her whether she had something (to eat). She replied that she had nothing except the mutton (piece) which Nusaiba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (Um 'Atiyya) had sent to us (Buraira) in charity." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "It has reached its place and now it is not a thing of charity but a gift for us."
narrator: Narrated Um 'Atiyya Al-Ansariya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Some meat was presented to the Prophet (п·ә) and it had been given to Barira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (the freed slave-girl of Aisha) in charity. He said, "This meat is a thing of charity for Barira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ but it is a gift for us." Abu Dawood, Shobah, Qatadah, Hadrat Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported it from  the prophet (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Anas:

text: Whenever a person came to the Prophet (п·ә) with his alms, the Prophet (п·ә) would say, "O Allah! Send your Blessings upon so and so." My father went to the Prophet (п·ә) with his alms and the Prophet (п·ә) said, "O Allah! Send your blessings upon the offspring of Abu Aufa."
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah bin Abu Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A man from Bani Israel asked someone from Bani Israel to give him a loan of one thousand Dinars and the later gave it to him. The debtor went on a voyage (when the time for the payment of the debt became due) but he did not find a boat, so he took a piece of wood and bored it and put 1000 diners in it and threw it into the sea. The creditor went out and took the piece of wood to his family to be used as fire-wood." (See Hadith No. 488 B, Vol. 3). And the Prophet (п·ә) narrated the narration (and said), "When he sawed the wood, he found his money."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "There is no compensation for one killed or wounded by an animal or by falling in a well, or because of working in mines; but Khumus is compulsory on Rikaz."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) appointed a man called Ibn Al-Lutbiya, from the tribe of Al-Asd to collect Zakat from Bani Sulaim. When he returned, (after collecting the Zakat) the Prophet (п·ә) checked the account with him.
narrator: Narrated Abu Humaid Al-Sa`idi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I took `Abdullah bin Abu Talha to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) to perform Tahnik for him. (Tahnik was a custom among the Muslims that whenever a child was born they used to take it to the Prophet (п·ә) who would chew a piece of date and put a part of its juice in the child's mouth). I saw the Prophet (п·ә) and he had an instrument for branding in his hands and was branding the camels of Zakat.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) enjoined the payment of one Sa' of dates or one Sa' of barley as Zakat-ul-Fitr on every Muslim slave or free, male or female, young or old, and he ordered that it be paid before the people went out to offer the `Id prayer. (One Sa' = 3 Kilograms approx.)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) made it incumbent on all the slave or free Muslims, male or female, to pay one Sa' of dates or barley as Zakat-ul-Fitr.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We used to give one Sa' of barley as Sadaqat-ul-Fitr (per head).
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We used to give one Sa' of meal or one Sa' of barley or one Sa' of dates, or one Sa' of cottage cheese or one Sa' of Raisins (dried grapes) as Zakat-ul-Fitr.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) ordered (Muslims) to give one Sa' of dates or one Sa' of barley as Zakat-ul-Fitr. The people rewarded two Mudds of wheat as equal to that.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§  :

text: In the lifetime of the Prophet (п·ә) we used to give one Sa' of food or one Sa' of dates or one Sa' of barley or one Sa' of Raisins (dried grapes) as Sadaqat-ul-Fitr. And when Muawiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ became the Caliph and the wheat was (available in abundance) he said, "I think (observe) that one Mudd (of wheat) equals two Mudds (of any of the above mentioned things).
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) ordered the people to pay Zakat-ul-Fitr before going to the `Id prayer.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: In the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) , we used to give one Sa' of food (edible things) as Sadaqat-ul-Fitr (to the poor). Hadrat Abu Saeed said: Our food used to be either of barley, raisins (dried grapes), cottage cheese or dates.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) has made Sadaqat-ul-Fitr obligatory, (and it was), either one Sa' of barley or one Sa' of dates (and its payment was obligatory) on young and old people, and on free men as well as on slaves.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Medina is a sanctuary from that place to that. Its trees should not be cut and no heresy should be innovated nor any sin should be committed in it, and whoever innovates in it an heresy or commits sins (bad deeds), then he will incur the curse of Allah, the angels, and all the people." (See Hadith No. 409, Vol 9).
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) came to Medina and ordered a mosque to be built and said, "O Bani Najjar! Suggest to me the price (of your land)." They said, "We do not want its price except from Allah" (i.e. they wished for a reward from Allah for giving up their land freely). So, the Prophet (п·ә) ordered the graves of the pagans to be dug out and the land to be leveled, and the date-palm trees to be cut down. The cut datepalms were fixed in the direction of the Qibla of the mosque.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I have made Medina a sanctuary between its two (Harrat) mountains." The Prophet (п·ә) went to the tribe of Bani Haritha and said (to them), "I see that you have gone out of the sanctuary," but looking around, he added, "No, you are inside the sanctuary."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "I was ordered to migrate to a town which will swallow (conquer) other towns and is called Yathrib and that is Medina, and it turns out (bad) persons as a furnace removes the impurities of iron.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We came with the Prophet (п·ә) from Tabuk, and when we reached near Medina, the Prophet (п·ә) said, "This is Tabah."
narrator: Narrated Abu Humaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: If I saw deers grazing in Medina, I would not chase them, for Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "(Medina) is a sanctuary between its two mountains."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "The people will leave Medina in spite of the best state it will have, and none except the wild birds and the beasts of prey will live in it, and the last persons who will die will be two shepherds from the tribe of Muzaina, who will be driving their sheep towards Medina, but will find nobody in it, and when they reach the valley of Thaniyat-al-Wada`, they will fall down on their faces dead."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Verily, Belief returns and goes back to Medina as a snake returns and goes back to its hole (when in danger).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "None plots against the people of Medina but that he will be dissolved (destroyed) like the salt is dissolved in water."
narrator: Narrated Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) stood at the top of a (looked out from upon one) castle amongst the castles (or the high buildings) of Medina and said, "Do you see what I see? (No doubt) I see the spots where afflictions will take place among your houses (and these afflictions will be) as numerous as the spots where raindrops fall." This tradition has been followed by Muammar and Sulaiman bin Kathir on the authority of Zuhri.
narrator: Narrated Usama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The terror caused by Al-Masih Ad-Dajjal will not enter Medina and at that time Medina will have seven gates and there will be two angels at each gate guarding them."
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "There are angels guarding the entrances (or roads) of Medina, neither plague nor Ad-Dajjal will be able to enter it."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There will be no town which Ad-Dajjal will not enter except Mecca and Medina, and there will be no entrance (road) (of both Mecca and Medina) but the angels will be standing in rows guarding it against him, and then Medina will shake with its inhabitants thrice (i.e. three earthquakes will take place) and Allah will expel all the non-believers and the hypocrites from it."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A bedouin came to the Prophet (п·ә) and gave a pledge of allegiance for embracing Islam. The next day he came with fever and said (to the Prophet (п·ә) ), "Please cancel my pledge (of embracing Islam and of emigrating to Medina)." The Prophet (п·ә) refused (that request) three times and said, "Medina is like a furnace, it expels out the impurities (bad persons) and selects the good ones and makes them perfect."
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When the Prophet (п·ә) went out for (the battle of) Uhud, some of his companions (hypocrites) returned (home). A party of the believers remarked that they would kill those (hypocrites) who had returned, but another party said that they would not kill them. So, this Divine Inspiration was revealed: "Then what is the matter with you that you are divided into two parties concerning the hypocrites." (4.88) The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Medina expels the bad persons from it, as fire expels the impurities of iron."
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "O Allah! Bestow on Medina twice the blessings You bestowed on Mecca." Uthman bin Umer has corroborated him from Younus.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Whenever the Prophet (п·ә) returned from a journey and observed the walls of Medina, he would make his Mount go fast, and if he was on an animal (i.e. a horse), he would make it gallop because of his love for Medina.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: (The people of) Bani Salama intended to shift near the mosque (of the Prophet) but Allah's Messenger (п·ә) disliked to see Medina vacated and said, "O the people of Bani Salama! Don't you think that you will be rewarded for your footsteps which you take towards the mosque?" So, they stayed at their old places.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There is a garden from the gardens of Paradise between my house and my pulpit, and my pulpit is on my Lake Fount (Al-Kauthar).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ :

text: O Allah! Grant me martyrdom in Your cause, and let my death be in the city of Your Apostle." Ibn-e-Jurai, Rauh bin Qasim, Zaid bin Aslam, her mother, Hadrat Hafsa bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reports: I heard from Hadrat Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ the same. Hisham, Zaid, his father, Hadrat Hafsah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reports: I heard from Hadrat Umer  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) observed the fast on the 10th of Muharram ('Ashura), and ordered (Muslims) to fast on that day, but when the fasting of the month of Ramadan was prescribed, the fasting of the 'Ashura' was abandoned. `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ did not use to fast on that day unless it coincided with his routine fasting by chance.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: (The tribe of) Quraish used to fast on the day of Ashura' in the Pre-Islamic period, and then Allah's Apostle ordered (Muslims) to fast on it till the fasting in the month of Ramadan was prescribed; whereupon the Prophet (п·ә) said, "He who wants to fast (on 'Ashura') may fast, and he who does not want to fast may not fast."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There is a gate in Paradise called Ar-Raiyan, and those who observe fasts will enter through it on the Day of Resurrection and none except them will enter through it. It will be said, 'Where are those who used to observe fasts?' They will get up, and none except them will enter through it. After their entry the gate will be closed and nobody will enter through it."
narrator: Narrated Sahl  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When Ramadan begins, the gates of Paradise are opened."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When the month of Ramadan starts, the gates of the heaven are opened and the gates of Hell are closed and the devils are chained."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "When you see the crescent (of the month of Ramadan), start fasting, and when you see the crescent (of the month of Shawwal), stop fasting; and if the sky is overcast (and you can't see it) then regard the month of Ramadan as of 30 days." Others have reported from Uqail and Younus with reference to Laith: For the new moon of Ramadan.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever established prayers on the night of Qadr out of sincere faith and hoping for a reward from Allah, then all his previous sins will be forgiven; and whoever fasts in the month of Ramadan out of sincere faith, and hoping for a reward from Allah, then all his previous sins will be forgiven."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was the most generous amongst the people, and he used to be more so in the month of Ramadan when Gabriel visited him, and Gabriel used to meet him on every night of Ramadan till the end of the month. The Prophet (п·ә) used to recite the Holy Qur'an to Gabriel, and when Gabriel met him, he used to be more generous than a fast wind (which causes rain and welfare).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever does not give up forged speech and evil actions, Allah is not in need of his leaving his food and drink (i.e. Allah will not accept his fasting.)"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: While I was walking with `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ he said, "We were in the company of the Prophet (п·ә) and he said, 'He who can afford to marry should marry, because it will help him refrain from looking at other women, and save his private parts from committing illegal sexual relation; and he who cannot afford to marry is advised to fast, as fasting will diminish his sexual power."
narrator: Narrated 'Alqama:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) mentioned Ramadan and said, "Do not fast unless you see the crescent (of Ramadan), and do not give up fasting till you see the crescent (of Shawwal), but if the sky is overcast (if you cannot see it), then act on estimation (i.e. count Sha'ban as 30 days).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The month (can be) 29 nights (i.e. days), and do not fast till you see the moon, and if the sky is overcast, then complete Sha'ban as thirty days."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The month is like this and this," (at the same time he showed the fingers of both his hands thrice) and left out one thumb on the third time.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) or Abul-Qasim said, "Start fasting on seeing the crescent (of Ramadan), and give up fasting on seeing the crescent (of Shawwal), and if the sky is overcast (and you cannot see it), complete thirty days of Sha'ban."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) vowed to keep aloof from his wives for a period of one month, and after the completion of 29 days he went either in the morning or in the afternoon to his wives. Someone said to him "You vowed that you would not go to your wives for one month." He replied, "The month is of 29 days."
narrator: Narrated Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) vowed to keep aloof from his wives for one month, and he had dislocation of his leg. So, he stayed in a Mashruba for 29 nights and then came down. Some people said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! You vowed to stay aloof for one month," He replied, "The month is of 29 days."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The two months of `Id i.e. Ramadan and Dhul-Hijja, do not decrease (in superiority).
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "We are an illiterate nation; we neither write, nor know accounts. The month is like this and this, i.e. sometimes of 29 days and sometimes of thirty days."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "None of you should fast a day or two before the month of Ramadan unless he has the habit of fasting (Nawafil) (and if his fasting coincides with that day) then he can fast that day."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When the above verses were revealed: 'Until the white thread appears to you, distinct from the black thread,' I took two (hair) strings, one black and the other white, and kept them under my pillow and went on looking at them throughout the night but could not make anything out of it. So, the next morning I went to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and told him the whole story. He explained to me, "That verse means the darkness of the night and the whiteness of the dawn."
narrator: Narrated `Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When the following verses were revealed: 'Eat and drink until the white thread appears to you, distinct from the black thread' and of dawn was not revealed, some people who intended to fast, tied black and white threads to their legs and went on eating till they differentiated between the two. Allah then revealed the words, 'of dawn', and it became clear that meant night and day.
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Saud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to pronounce the Adhan at night, so Allah's Messenger (п·ә)? said, "Carry on taking your meals (eat and drink) till Ibn Um Maktum ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ pronounces the Adhan, for he does not pronounce it till it is dawn. Qasim bin Muhammad said: There was only as much interval between their calls to prayer as is that one would ascend and the other would descend.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I used to take my Suhur meals with my family and then hurry up for presenting myself for the (Fajr) prayer with Allah's Messenger (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "We took the Suhur with the Prophet (п·ә) . Then he stood for the prayer." I asked, "What was the interval between the Suhur and the Adhan?" He replied, "The interval was sufficient to recite fifty verses of the Qur'an."
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) fasted for days continuously; the people also did the same but it was difficult for them. So, the Prophet (п·ә) forbade them (to fast continuously for more than one day). They slid, "But you fast without break (no food was taken in the evening or in the morning)." The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "I am not like you, for I am provided with food and drink (by Allah).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Take Suhur as there is a blessing in it."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) ordered a person on 'Ashura' (the tenth of Muharram) to announce, "Whoever has eaten, should not eat any more, but fast, and who has not eaten should not eat, but complete his fast (till the end of the day).
narrator: Narrated Salama bin Al-Akwa`:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to kiss and embrace (his wives) while he was fasting, and he had more power to control his desires than any of you. Hadrat Ibn-e-Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:  Щ…ЩҺШўШұЩҗШЁЩҸвҖҸ means needs. Taus says вҖҸШЈЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҗЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҗШұЩ’ШЁЩҺШ©Щҗ means the dunce who has no need of women.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to kiss some of his wives while he was fasting," and then she smiled.
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "While I was (lying) with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) underneath a woolen sheet, I got the menstruation, and then slipped away and put on the clothes (which I used to wear) in menses. He asked, "What is the matter? Did you get your menses?" I replied in the affirmative and then entered underneath that woolen sheet. I and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to take a bath from one water pot and he used to kiss me while he was fasting."
narrator: Narrated Zainab bint Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reports that her mother said:

text: (At times) in Ramadan the Prophet (п·ә) used to take a bath in the morning not because of a wet dream and would continue his fast.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: My father and I went to `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and she said, "I testify that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) at times used to get up in the morning in a state of Janaba from sexual intercourse, not from a wet dream and then he would fast that day." Then he went to Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and she also narrated a similar thing.
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakr bin `Abdur-Rahman:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If somebody eats or drinks forgetfully then he should complete his fast, for what he has eaten or drunk, has been given to him by Allah."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said that he had been burnt (ruined). The Prophet (п·ә) asked him what was the matter. He replied, "I had sexual intercourse with my wife in Ramadan (while I was fasting)." Then a basket full of dates was brought to the Prophet (п·ә) and he asked, "Where is the burnt (ruined) man?" He replied, "I am present." The Prophet (п·ә) told him to give that basket in charity (as expiation).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was cupped while he was in the state of lhram, and also while he was observing a fast.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was cupped while he was fasting.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Anas bin Malik was asked whether they disliked the cupping for a fasting person. He replied in the negative and said, "Only if it causes weakness." Shababah has reported from Shobah in the prophet times.
narrator: Narrated Thabit Al-Bunani:

text: Hamza bin `Amr Al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I fast continuously."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Hamza bin `Amr Al-Aslami asked the Prophet, "Should I fast while traveling?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "You may fast if you wish, and you may not fast if you wish."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (the wife of the Prophet):

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) set out for Mecca in Ramadan and he fasted, and when he reached Al-Kadid, he broke his fast and the people (with him) broke their fast too. (Abu `Abdullah said, "Al-Kadid is a land covered with water between Usfan and Qudaid.")
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We set out with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) on one of his journeys on a very hot day, and it was so hot that one had to put his hand over his head because of the severity of heat. None of us was fasting except the Prophet and Ibn Rawaha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Abu Ad-Darda ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was on a journey and saw a crowd of people, and a man was being shaded (by them). He asked, "What is the matter?" They said, "He (the man) is fasting." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "It is not righteousness that you fast on a journey."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We used to travel with the Prophet (п·ә) and neither did the fasting persons criticize those who were not fasting, nor did those who were not fasting criticize the fasting ones.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) set out from Medina to Mecca and he fasted till he reached 'Usfan, where he asked for water and raised his hand to let the people see him, and then broke the fast, and did not fast after that till he reached Mecca, and that happened in Ramadan." Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§  used to say, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) (sometimes) fasted and (sometimes) did not fast during the journeys so whoever wished to fast could fast, and whoever wished not to fast, could do so."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ recited the verse: "They had a choice either to fast or to feed a poor person for every day, and said that the order of this Verse was canceled.
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:

text: Sometimes I missed some days of Ramadan, but could not fast in lieu of them except in the month of Sha'ban." Said Yahya, a sub-narrator, "She used to be busy serving the Prophet (п·ә) ."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Isn't it true that a woman does not pray and does not fast on menstruating? And that is the defect (a loss) in her religion."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whoever died and he ought to have fasted (the missed days of Ramadan) then his guardians must fast on his behalf." Ibn-e- Wahab corroborated him from Amr and reported it Yahya bin Ayyub from Ibn-e-Abi Jafar.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: A man came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! My mother died and she ought to have fasted one month (for her missed Ramadan). Shall I fast on her behalf?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied in the affirmative and said, "Allah's debts have more right to be paid." In another narration a woman is reported to have said, "My sister died..." Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ : A woman said to the Prophet (п·ә) "My mother died and she had vowed to fast but she didn't fast." In another narration Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ is reported to have said, "A woman said to the Prophet, "My mother died while she ought to have fasted for fifteen days."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When night falls from this side and the day vanishes from this side and the sun sets, then the fasting person should break his fast."
narrator: Narrated `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We were traveling with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and he was fasting, and when the sun set, he said to (someone), "Get down and mix Sawiq with water for us." He replied, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! (Will you wait) till it is evening?" The Prophet (п·ә) again said, "Get down and mix Sawiq with water for us." He replied, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! It is still daytime." The Prophet (п·ә) said again, "Get down and mix Sawiq with water for us." So, he got down and carried out that order. The Prophet (п·ә) then said, "When you see night falling from this side, the fasting person should break his fast," and he beckoned with his finger towards the east.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi `Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The people will remain on the right path as long as they hasten the breaking of the fast."
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I was with the Prophet (п·ә) on a journey, and he observed the fast till evening. The Prophet (п·ә) said to a man, "Get down and mix Sawiq with water for me." He replied, "Will you wait till it is evening?" The Prophet said, "Get down and mix Sawiq with water for me; when you see night falling from this side, the fasting person should break his fast."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abi `Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "We broke our fast during the lifetime of the Prophet (п·ә) on a cloudy day and then the sun appeared." Hisham was asked, "Were they ordered to fast in lieu of that day?" He replied, "It had to be made up for." Ma`mar said, "I heard Hisham saying, "I don't know whether they fasted in lieu of that day or not."
narrator: Narrated Asma bint Abi Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) sent a messenger to the village of the Ansar in the morning of the day of 'Ashura' (10th of Muharram) to announce: 'Whoever has eaten something should not eat but complete the fast, and whoever is observing the fast should complete it.' "She further said, "Since then we used to fast on that day regularly and also make our boys fast. We used to make toys of wool for the boys and if anyone of them cried for, he was given those toys till it was the time of the breaking of the fast."
narrator: Narrated Ar-Rubi' bint Mu'awadh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do not practice Al-Wisal (fasting continuously without breaking one's fast in the evening or eating before the following dawn)." The people said to the Prophet, "But you practice Al- Wisal?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "I am not like any of you, for I am given food and drink (by Allah) during the night."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) forbade Al-Wisal. The people said (to him), "But you practice it?" He said, "I am not like you, for I am given food and drink by Allah."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: That he had heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "Do not fast continuously (practice Al-Wisal), and if you intend to lengthen your fast, then carry it on only till the Suhur (before the following dawn)." The people said to him, "But you practice (Al-Wisal), O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)!" He replied, "I am not similar to you, for during my sleep I have One Who makes me eat and drink."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) forbade Al-Wisal out of mercy to them. They said to him, "But you practice Al- Wisal?" He said, "I am not similar to you, for my Lord gives me food and drink. " Imam Abu Abdullah Bukhari said: Uthman, the reporter, has not mentioned. ШұЩҺШӯЩ’Щ…ЩҺШ©ЩӢ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said twice, "(O you people) Be cautious! Do not practice Al-Wisal." The people said to him, "But you practice Al-Wisal?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "My Lord gives me food and drink during my sleep. Do that much of deeds which is within your ability."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Do not fast continuously day and night (practice Al-Wisal) and if anyone of you intends to fast continuously day and night, he should continue till the Suhur time." They said, "But you practice Al-Wisal, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)!" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I am not similar to you;. during my sleep I have One Who makes me eat and drink."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to fast till one would say that he would never stop fasting, and he would abandon fasting till one would say that he would never fast. I never saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) fasting for a whole month except the month of Ramadan, and did not see him fasting in any month more than in the month of Sha'ban.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) never fasted in any month more than in the month of Sha'ban. He used to say, "Do those deeds which you can do easily, as Allah will not get tired (of giving rewards) till you get bored and tired (of performing religious deeds)." The most beloved prayer to the Prophet (п·ә) was the one that was done regularly (throughout the life) even if it were little. And whenever the Prophet (п·ә) offered a prayer he used to offer it regularly .
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) never fasted a full month except the month of Ramadan, and he used to fast till one could say, "By Allah, he will never stop fasting," and he would abandon fasting till one would say, "By Allah, he will never fast."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
