Then we heard the trunk sending a sound like of a pregnant she-camel till the Prophet (п·ә) came to it, and put his hand over it, then it became quiet."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The Hour will not be established till you fight a nation wearing hairy shoes, and till you fight the Turks, who will have small eyes, red faces and flat noses; and their faces will be like flat shields." And you will find that the best people are those who hate responsibility of ruling most of all till they are chosen to be the rulers. And the people are of different natures: The best in the pre-lslamic period are the best in Islam. A time will come when any of you will love to see me rather than to have his family and property doubled."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The Hour will not be established till you fight with the Khudh and the Kirman from among the non-Arabs. They will be of red faces, flat noses and small eyes; their faces will look like flat shields, and their shoes will be of hair." Excluding him, Abdur Razzaq has also reported this Hadith.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: I enjoyed the company of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) for three years, and during the other years of my life, never was I so anxious to understand the (Prophet's) traditions as I was during those three years. I heard him saying, beckoning with his hand in this way, "Before the Hour you will fight with people who will have hairy shoes and live in Al-Bazir." (Sufyan, the sub-narrator once said, "And they are the people of Al-Bazir.")
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "Near the Hour you will fight with people who will wear hairy shoes; and you will also fight people with flat faces like shields."
narrator: Narrated `Umar bin Taghlib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "The Jews will fight with you, and you will be given victory over them so that a stone will say, 'O Muslim! There is a Jew behind me; kill him!' "
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A time will come when the people will wage holy war, and it will be asked, 'Is there any amongst you who has enjoyed the company of Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?' They will say: 'Yes.' And then victory will be bestowed upon them. They will wage holy war again, and it will be asked: 'Is there any among you who has enjoyed the company of the companions of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ?' They will say: 'Yes.' And then victory will be bestowed on them."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) once came out and offered the funeral prayer for the martyrs of Uhud, and proceeded to the pulpit and said, "I shall be your predecessor and a witness on you, and I am really looking at my sacred Fount now, and no doubt, I have been given the keys of the treasures of the world. By Allah, I am not afraid that you will worship others along with Allah, but I am afraid that you will envy and fight one another for worldly fortunes."
narrator: Narrated `Uqba bin `Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) stood on one of the high buildings (of Medina) and said, "Do you see what I see? I see affliction pouring among your hours like raindrops."
narrator: Narrated Usama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) came to her in a state of fear saying, "None has the right to be worshiped but Allah! Woe to the Arabs because of evil that has come near. Today a hole has been made in the wall of Gog and Magog as large as this." pointing with two of his fingers making a circle. Zainab said, "I said, 'O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Shall we be destroyed though amongst us there are pious people? ' He said, 'Yes, if evil increases."
narrator: Narrated Zainab bint Jahsh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) woke up and said, "Glorified be Allah: What great (how many) treasures have been sent down, and what great (how many ) afflictions have been sent down!"
narrator: Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: "I notice that you like sheep and you keep them; so take care of them and their food, for I have heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, 'A time will come upon the people when the best of a Muslim's property will be sheep, which he will take to the tops of mountains and to the places of rain-falls to run away with his religion in order to save it from afflictions.' "
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "There will be afflictions (and at the time) the sitting person will be better than the standing one, and the standing one will be better than the walking, and the walking will be better than the running. And whoever will look towards those afflictions, they will overtake him, and whoever will find a refuge or a shelter, should take refuge in it." The same narration is reported by Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , with the addition, "(The Prophet (п·ә) said), 'Among the prayers there is a prayer the missing of which will be to one like losing one's family and property."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Soon others will be preferred to you, and there will be things which you will not like." The companions of the Prophet (п·ә) asked, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! What do you order us to do (in this case)? " He said, "(I order you) to give the rights that are on you and to ask your rights from Allah."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "This branch from Quraish will ruin the people." The companions of the Prophet (п·ә) asked, "What do you order us to do (then)?" He said, "I would suggest that the people keep away from them.'' Muhmood said: Abu Dawood narrated us the Hadith that Shobah informed us with reference to Abu Tayyah, (he says that) hehas heard the same from Abu Zurah.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira:
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: I was with Marwan and Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and heard Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ saying, "I heard the trustworthy, truly inspired one (i.e. the Prophet (п·ә) ) saying, 'The destruction of my followers will be brought about by the hands of some youngsters from Quraish." Marwan asked, "Youngsters?" Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "If you wish, I would name them: They are the children of so-and-so and the children of so-and-so."
narrator: Narrated Sa`id Al-Umawi:
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: My companions learned (something about) good (through asking the Prophet) while I learned (something about) evil.
narrator: Narrated Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The Day of (Judgment) will not be established till there is a war between two groups whose claims (or religion) will be the same."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The Hour will not be established till there is a war between two groups among whom there will be a great number of casualties, though the claims (or religion) of both of them will be one and the same. And the Hour will not be established till there appear about thirty liars, all of whom will be claiming to be the messengers of Allah. "
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: A man recited Surat-al-Kahf (in his prayer) and in the house there was a (riding) animal which got frightened and started jumping. The man finished his prayer with Taslim, but behold! A mist or a cloud hovered over him. He informed the Prophet (п·ә) of that and the Prophet (п·ә) said, "O so-and-so! Recite, for this (mist or cloud) was a sign of peace descending for the recitation of Qur'an."
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) paid a visit to a sick bedouin. The Prophet (п·ә) when visiting a patient used to say, "No harm will befall you! May Allah cure you! May Allah cure you!" So the Prophet (п·ә) said to the bedouin. "No harm will befall you. May Allah cure you!" The bedouin said, "You say, may Allah cure me? No, for it is a fever which boils in (the body of) an old man, and will lead him to the grave." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Yes, then may it be as you say."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When Khosrau perishes, there will be no (more) Khosrau after him, and when Caesar perishes, there will be no more Caesar after him. By Him in Whose Hands Muhammad's life is, you will spend the treasures of both of them in Allah's Cause."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "When Khosrau perishes, there will be no more Khosrau a after him, and when Caesar perishes, there will be no more Caesar after him," The Prophet (п·ә) also said, "You will spend the treasures of both of them in Allah's Cause."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) in his fatal illness, called his daughter Fatima ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and told her a secret because of which she started weeping. Then he called her and told her another secret, and she started laughing. When I asked her about that, she replied, The Prophet (п·ә) told me that he would die in his fatal illness, and so I wept, but then he secretly told me that from amongst his family, I would be the first to join him, and so I laughed."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ used to treat Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ very favorably `Abdur Rahman bin `Auf ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said to him. "We also have sons that are equal to him (but you are partial to him.)" `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "It is because of his knowledge." Then `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ about the interpretation of the Verse:- 'When come the Help of Allah and the conquest (of Mecca) (110.1) Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said. "It portended the death of Allah's Messenger (п·ә), which Allah had informed him of." `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I do not know from this Verse but what you know."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) in his fatal illness came out, wrapped with a sheet, and his head was wrapped with an oiled bandage. He sat on the pulpit, and praising and glorifying Allah, he said, "Now then, people will increase but the Ansar will decrease in number, so much so that they, compared with the people, will be just like the salt in the meals. So, if any of you should take over the authority by which he can either benefit some people or harm some others, he should accept the goodness of their good people (i.e. Ansar) and excuse the faults of their wrong-doers." That was the last gathering which the Prophet (п·ә) attended.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) brought out Al-Hasan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and took him up the pulpit along with him and said, "This son of mine is a Saiyid (i.e. chief) and I hope that Allah will help him bring about reconciliation between two Muslim groups."
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) had informed us of the death of Ja`far and Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ before the news of their death reached us, and his eyes were shedding tears.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: (Once) the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Have you got carpets?" I replied, "Whence can we get carpets?" He said, "But you shall soon have carpets." I used to say to my wife, "Remove your carpets from my sight," but she would say, "Didn't the Prophet (п·ә) tell you that you would soon have carpets?" So I would give up my request.
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "I saw (in a dream) the people assembled in a gathering, and then Abu Bakr          
ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ got up and drew one or two buckets of water (from a well) but there was weakness in his drawing. May Allah forgive him. Then `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took the bucket and in his hands it turned into a very large bucket. I had never seen anyone amongst: the people who could draw the water as strongly as `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ till all the people drank their fill and watered their camels that knelt down there. Hadrat Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ told Hammam and the prophet (п·ә) told Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ: Abu Bakr drew two buckets.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: I got the news that Gabriel came to the Prophet (п·ә) while Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ was present. Gabriel started talking (to the Prophet (п·ә) and then left. The Prophet (п·ә) said to Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, "(Do you know) who it was?" (or a similar question). She said, "It was Dihya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (a handsome person amongst the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) )." Later on Um Salama said, "By Allah! I thought he was none but Dihya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , till I heard the Prophet (п·ә) talking about Gabriel in his sermon." (The Sub-narrator asked Abu `Uthman, "From where have you heard this narration?" He replied, "From Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .")
narrator: Narrated Abu `Uthman:
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: During the lifetime of the Prophet (п·ә) the moon was split into two parts and on that the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Bear witness (to thus).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Masud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Meccan people requested Allah's Messenger (п·ә) to show them a miracle, and so he showed them the splitting of the moon.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The moon was split into two parts during the lifetime of the Prophet.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Once two men from the companions of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went out of the house of the Prophet (п·ә) on a very dark night. They were accompanied by two things that resembled two lamps lighting the way in front of them, and when they parted, each of them was accompanied by one of those two things (lamps) till they reached their homes.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Some of my followers will remain victorious (and on the right path) till the Last Day comes, and they will still be victorious."
narrator: Narrated Al-Mughira bin Shu`ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "A group of people amongst my followers will remain obedient to Allah's orders and they will not be harmed by anyone who will not help them or who will oppose them, till Allah's Order (the Last Day) comes upon them while they are still on the right path." Umair, Maalik bin Yukhamir, Hadrat Muadh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ says: And they will be in Egypt. Hadrat Moaviyah says that Maalik claims that he has heard from Hadrat Muadh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that they are in Egypt.
narrator: Narrated Muawiya:
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "There is always goodness in horses till the Day of Resurrection. "
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There is always goodness in horses."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) reached Khaibar in the early morning and the people of Khaibar came out with their spades, and when they saw the Prophet (п·ә) they said, "Muhammad and his army!" and returned hurriedly to take refuge in the fort. The Prophet (п·ә) raised his hands and said, "Allah is Greater! Khaibar is ruined ! If we approach a nation, then miserable is the morning of those who are warned."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I hear many narrations from you but I forget them." He said, "Spread your covering sheet." I spread my sheet and he moved both his hands as if scooping something and emptied them in the sheet and said, "Wrap it." I wrapped it round my body, and since then I have never forgotten.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Virtues and Merits of the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'The best of my followers are those living in my generation (i.e. my contemporaries). and then those who will follow the latter" `Imran ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ added, "I do not remember whether he mentioned two or three generations after his generation, then the Prophet (п·ә) added, 'There will come after you, people who will bear witness without being asked to do so, and will be treacherous and untrustworthy, and they will vow and never fulfill their vows, and fatness will appear among them."
narrator: Narrated `Imran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The best people are those living in my generation, and then those who will follow them, and then those who will follow the latter. Then there will come some people who will bear witness before taking oaths, and take oaths before bearing witness." (Ibrahim, a sub-narrator said, "They used to beat us for witnesses and covenants when we were still children.")
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I said to the Prophet (п·ә) while I was in the Cave. "If any of them should look under his feet, he would see us." He said, "O Abu Bakr! What do you think of two (persons) the third of whom is Allah?"
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We used to compare the people as to who was better during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) . We used to regard Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as the best, then `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, and then `Uthman .
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If I were to take a Khalil, I would have taken Abu Bakr, but he is my brother and my companion (in Islam).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If I were to take a Khalil, I would have taken him (i.e. Abu Bakr) as a Khalil, but the Islamic brotherhood is better."
narrator: Narrated Aiyub:

text: The people of Kufa sent a letter to Ibn Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§, asking about (the inheritance of) (paternal) grandfather. He replied that the right of the inheritance of (paternal) grandfather is the same as that of father if the father is dead) and added, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, ' If I were to take a Khalil from this nation, I would have taken him (i.e. Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi Mulaika ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: A woman came to the Prophet (п·ә) who ordered her to return to him again. She said, "What if I came and did not find you?" as if she wanted to say, "If I found you dead?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If you should not find me, go to Abu Bakr."
narrator: Narrated Jubair bin Mut`im:

text: I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and there was none with him but five slaves, two women and Abu Bakr (i.e. those were the only converts to Islam then).
narrator: Narrated `Ammar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) deputed me to read the Army of Dhat-as-Salasil. I came to him and said, "Who is the most beloved person to you?" He said, " `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ." I asked, "Among the men?" He said, "Her father." I said, "Who then?" He said, "Then `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ." He then named other men.
narrator: Narrated `Amr bin Al-As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "While a shepherd was amongst his sheep, a wolf attacked them and took away one sheep. When the shepherd chased the wolf, the wolf turned towards him and said, 'Who will be its guard on the day of wild animals when nobody except I will be its shepherd. And while a man was driving a cow with a load on it, it turned towards him and spoke to him saying, 'I have not been created for this purpose, but for ploughing." The people said, "Glorified be Allah." The Prophet said, "But I believe in it and so does Abu Bakr end `Umar."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Allah will not look on the Day of Judgment at him who drags his robe (behind him) out of pride." Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said "One side of my robe slacks down unless I get very cautious about it." Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "But you do not do that with a pride." Musa bin Uqbah inquired from Saalim whether Hadrat Abdullah bin Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:  Ш¬ЩҺШұЩ‘ЩҺ ШҘЩҗШІЩҺШ§ШұЩҺЩҮЩҸ. He replied: No but I have heard "Ш«ЩҺЩҲЩ’ШЁЩҺЩҮЩҸвҖҸ".
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text:
("When the Prophet (п·ә) was on his death-bed) he looked up and said thrice, (Amongst) the Highest Companion (See Qur'an 4.69)' Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, Allah benefited the people by their two speeches. 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  frightened the people some of whom were hypocrites whom Allah caused to abandon Islam because of 'Umar's speech. Then Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ led the people to True Guidance and acquainted them with the right path they were to follow so that they went out reciting:
-- "Muhammad is no more than an Apostle and indeed many Apostles have passed away before him.." (3.144)
narrator: Narrator `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I asked my father (`Ali bin Abi Talib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ), "Who are the best people after Allah's Messenger (п·ә) ?" He said, "Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ." I asked, "Who then?" He said, "Then `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . " I was afraid he would say "Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , so I said, "Then you?" He said, "I am only an ordinary person.
narrator: Narrated Muhammad bin Al-Hanafiya:

text: The Prophet (п·ә)said, "Do not abuse my companions for if any one of you spent gold equal to Uhud (in Allah's Cause) it would not be equal to a Mud or even a half Mud spent by one of them." Moreover, Jareer, Abdullah bin Dawood, Abu Moaviyah, Muhadir has corroborated him from Aamash.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) once climbed the mountain of Uhud with Abu Bakr, `Umar and `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… . The mountain shook with them. The Prophet (п·ә) said (to the mountain), "Be firm, O Uhud! For on you there are no more than a Prophet, a Siddiq and two martyrs.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I asked `Abdullah bin `Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , "What was the worst thing the pagans did to Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" He said, "I saw `Uqba bin Abi Mu'ait coming to the Prophet (п·ә) while he was praying.' `Uqba put his sheet round the Prophet's neck and squeezed it very severely. Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came and pulled `Uqba away from the Prophet and said, "Do you intend to kill a man just because he says: 'My Lord is Allah, and he has brought forth to you the Evident Signs from your Lord?"
narrator: Narrated `Urwa bin Az-Zubair:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I saw myself (in a dream) entering Paradise, and behold! I saw Ar-Rumaisa', Abu Talha's wife. I heard footsteps. I asked, Who is it? Somebody said, 'It is Bilal' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ Then I saw a palace and a lady sitting in its courtyard. I asked, 'For whom is this palace?' Somebody replied, 'It is for `Umar.' I intended to enter it and see it, but I thought of your (`Umar's) Ghira (and gave up the attempt)." `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "Let my parents be sacrificed for you, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! How dare I think of my Ghira (self-respect) being offended by you?
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: While we were with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) he said, "While I was sleeping, I saw myself in Paradise, and suddenly I saw a woman performing ablution beside a palace. I asked, 'For whom is this palace?' They replied, 'It is for `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .' Then I remembered `Umar's Ghira (self-respect) and went away quickly." `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ wept and said, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! How dare I think of my ghira (self-respect) being offended by you?
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "While I was sleeping, I saw myself drinking (i.e. milk), and I was so contented that I saw the milk flowing through my nails. Then I gave (the milk) to `Umar." They (i.e. the companions of the Prophet) asked, "What do you interpret it?" He said, "Knowledge."
narrator: Narrated Hamza's father:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "In a dream I saw myself drawing water from a well with a bucket. Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came and drew a bucket or two weakly. May Allah forgive him. Then `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came and the bucket turned into a very large one in his hands. I had never seen such a mighty person as he in doing such hard work till all the people drank to their satisfaction and watered their camels that knelt down there. Ibn-e-Jubair says: Abqari means eye-cathing pillows. Yahya says that "ШІШұШ§ШЁЫҢ" means hemmed and frilled carpet.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We have been powerful since `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ embraced Islam.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) ascended the mountain of Uhud and he was accompanied by Abu Bakr, `Umar and `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… . The mountain shook beneath them. The Prophet (п·ә) hit it with his foot and said, "O Uhud ! Be firm, for on you there is none but a Prophet, a Siddiq and a martyr (i.e. and two martyrs).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ asked me about some matters concerning `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . He said, "Since Allah's Messenger (п·ә) died. I have never seen anybody more serious, hard working and generous than `Umar bin Al-Khattab (till the end of his life."
narrator: Narrated Aslam:

text: A man asked the Prophet (п·ә) about the Hour (i.e. Day of Judgment) saying, "When will the Hour be?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "What have you prepared for it?" The man said, "Nothing, except that I love Allah and His Apostle." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "You will be with those whom you love." We had never been so glad as we were on hearing that saying of the Prophet (i.e., "You will be with those whom you love.") Therefore, I love the Prophet, Abu Bakr and `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , and I hope that I will be with them because of my love for them though my deeds are not similar to theirs.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Among the nations before you there used to be people who were inspired (though they were not prophets). And if there is any of such a persons amongst my followers, it is 'Umar." 
Zakariyyah bin Abi Zaidah has added with reference to Sa'd, Abu Salamah and Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Among the nation of Bani Israel who lived before you, there were men who used to be inspired with guidance though they were not prophets, and if there is any of such persons amongst my followers, it is 'Umar."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Whilst a shepherd was amongst his sheep, a wolf attacked them and took away a sheep. The shepherd chased it and got that sheep freed from the wolf. The wolf turned towards the shepherd and said, 'Who will guard the sheep on the day of wild animals when it will have no shepherd except myself?" The people said, "Glorified be Allah." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "But I believe in it and so do Abu Bakr and `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ although Abu Bakr and `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ were not present there (at the place of the event).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "While I was sleeping, the people were presented to me (in a dream). They were wearing shirts, some of which were merely covering their (chests). and some were a bit longer. `Umar was presented before me and his shirt was so long that he was dragging it." They asked, "How have you interpreted it, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" He said, "Religion."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We were with the Prophet (п·ә) while he was holding `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ by the hand.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Hisham ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: During the lifetime of the Prophet (п·ә) we considered Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  as peerless and then `Umar and then `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (coming next to him in superiority) and then we used not to differentiate between the companions of the Prophet. Without giving them superiority to one another, Abdullah (bin Sualih) has also reported from Abdul Azeez in the same way.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) ascended the mountain of Uhud and Abu Bakr, `Umar and `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… were accompanying him. The mountain gave a shake (i.e. trembled underneath them) . The Prophet (п·ә) said, "O Uhud ! Be calm." I think that the Prophet (п·ә) hit it with his foot, adding, "For upon you there are none but a Prophet, a Siddiq and two martyrs."
narrator: Narrated Anas  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to 'Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "Will you not be pleased from this that you are to me like Aaron was to Moses Ш№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…?"
narrator: Narrated Saad:

text: Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said (to the people of 'Iraq), "Judge as you used to judge, for I hate differences (and I do my best ) till the people unite as one group, or I die as my companions have died." Imam Ibn-e-Sireen thinks that most of the narrations about Hadrat Ali  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ are fabricated.
narrator: Narrated Ubaida:

text: Whenever Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ greeted Ibn Jafar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , he used to say: "As-salamu-'Alaika (i.e. Peace be on you) O son of Dhu-l-Janahain (son of the two-winged person).
narrator: Narrated Ash-Shu`bi:

text: Whenever there was drought, `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to ask Allah for rain through Al-`Abbas bin `Abdul Muttalib      ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, saying, "O Allah! We used to request our Prophet to ask You for rain, and You would give us. Now we request the uncle of our Prophet to ask You for rain, so give us rain." And they would be given rain."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Look at Muhammad through his family (i.e. if you are no good to his family you are not good to him).
narrator: Narrator Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Fatima is a part of me, and he who makes her angry, makes me angry."
narrator: Narrated Al-Miswar bin Makhrama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) called his daughter Fatima ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ during his illness in which he died, and told her a secret whereupon she wept. Then he called her again and told her a secret whereupon she laughed. When I asked her about that he would die in the course of the illness during which he died, so I wept. He again spoke to me in secret and informed me that I would be the first of his family to follow him (after his death) and on that I laughed."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: While I was with `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , a man came to him and said, "Appoint your successor." `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "Has such successor been named?" He replied, "Yes, Az-Zubair." `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, thrice, "By Allah! Indeed you know that he is the best of you."
narrator: Narrated Marwan bin Al-Hakam:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Every prophet used to have a Hawari (i.e. disciple), and my Hawari is Az-Zubair bin Al-`Awwam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ."
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: During the battle of Al-Ahzab, I and `Umar bin Abi-Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ were kept behind with the women. Behold! I saw (my father) Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ riding his horse, going to and coming from Bani Quraiza twice or thrice. So when I came back I said, "O my father! I saw you going to and coming from Bani Quraiza?" He said, "Did you really see me, O my son?" I said, "Yes." He said, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'Who will go to Bani Quraiza and bring me their news?' So I went, and when I came back, Allah's Apostle mentioned for me both his parents saying, "Let my father and mother be sacrificed for you."'
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: On the day of the battle of Al-Yarmuk, the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) said to Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "Will you attack the enemy vigorously so that we may attack them along with you?" So Az-Zubair attacked them, and they inflicted two wounds over his shoulder, and in between these two wounds there was an old scar he had received on the day of the battle of Badr When I was a child, I used to insert my fingers into those scars in play.
narrator: Narrated `Urwa:

text: During one of the Ghazawat in which Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was fighting, none remained with the Prophet (п·ә) but Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ.
narrator: Narrated Abu `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I saw Talha's paralyzed hand with which he had protected the Prophet (from an arrow) .
narrator: Narrated Qais bin Abi Hazim:

text: On the day of the battle of Uhud the Prophet (п·ә) mentioned for me both his parents (i.e. saying, "Let my parents be sacrificed for you.").
narrator: Narrated Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: No doubt, (for some time) I stood for one-third of the Muslims.
narrator: Narrated Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: No man embraced Islam before the day on which I embraced Islam, and no doubt, I remained for seven days as one third of the then extant Muslims. Abu Osamah has reported from Hashim likewise.
narrator: Narrated Sa`d bin Abi Waqqas:

text: "I was the first amongst the 'Arabs who shot an arrow for Allah's Cause. We used to fight along with the Prophets, while we had nothing to eat except the leaves of trees so that one's excrete would look like the excrete balls of camel or a sheep, containing nothing to mix them together. Today Banu Asad tribe blame me for not having understood Islam. I would be a loser if my deeds were in vain." Those people complained about Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ to `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, claiming that he did not offer his prayers perfectly.
narrator: Narrated Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) sent an army under the command of Usama bin Zaid. When some people criticized his leadership, the Prophet (п·ә) said, "If you are criticizing Usama's leadership, you used to criticize his father's leadership before. By Allah! He was worthy of leadership and was one of the dearest persons to me, and (now) this (i.e. Usama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) is one of the dearest to me after him (i.e. Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "A Qaif (i.e. one skilled in recognizing the lineage of a person through Physiognomy and through examining the body parts of an infant) came to me while the Prophet (п·ә) was present, and Usama bin Zaid and Zaid bin Haritha were Lying asleep. The Qa'if said. These feet (of Usama and his father) are of persons belonging to the same lineage.' " The Prophet (п·ә) was pleased with that saying which won his admiration, and he told `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ of it.
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The people of the Quraish tribe were worried about the Makhzumiya woman. They said. "Nobody dare speak to him (i.e. the Prophet (п·ә) ) except Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ as he is the most beloved to Allah's Messenger (п·ә)."
narrator: Narrated 'Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "A woman from Bani Makhzumiya committed a theft and the people said, 'Who can intercede with the Prophet (п·ә) for her?' So nobody dared speak to him (i.e. the Prophet) but Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ spoke to him. The Prophet said, 'If a reputable man amongst the children of Bani Israel committed a theft, they used to forgive him, but if a poor man committed a theft, they would cut his hand. But I would cut even the hand of Fatima  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (i.e. the daughter of the Prophet) if she committed a theft."
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: One day Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , while in the Mosque, looked at a man who was dragging his clothes while walking in one of the corners of the Mosque He said, "See who is that. I wish he was near to me." Somebody then said (to Ibn `Umar), "Don't you know him, O Abu `Abdur-Rahman? He is Muhammad bin Usama." On that Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ bowed his head and dug the earth with his hands and then, said, "If Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saw him, he would have loved him."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Dinar:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to take him (i.e. Usama) and Al-Hassan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (in his lap) and say: "O Allah! Love them, as I love them."
narrator: Narrated Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "Al-Hajjaj bin Aiman bin Um Aiman and Aiman Ibn Um Aiman was Usama's brother from the maternal side, and he was one of the Ansar. He was seen by Ibn 'Umar not performing his bowing and prostrations in a perfect manner. So Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ told him to repeat his prayer.
narrator: Narrator Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: While he was in the company of 'Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , Al-Hajjaj bin Aiman came in and (while praying) he did not perform his bowing and prostrations properly. So Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ told him to repeat his prayer. When he went away, Ibn 'Umar asked me, "Who is he?" I said, "Al-Hajjaj bin Um Aiman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ." Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "If Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saw him, he would have loved him." Then Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ mentioned the love of the Prophet (п·ә) for the children of Um Aiman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§. Sulaiman said that Um Aiman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ was one of the nurses of the Prophet.
narrator: Narrator Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) had said to her, "`Abdullah is a pious man."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar from Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ his sister:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, " Every nation has an extremely trustworthy man, and the trustworthy man of this (i.e. Muslim) nation is Abu 'Ubaida bin Al-Jarrah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to the people of Nijran, "I will send you the most trustworthy man." (Every one of) the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) was looking forward (to be that person). He then sent Abu 'Ubaida ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) talking at the pulpit while Al-Hasan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was sitting beside him, and he (i.e. the Prophet ) was once looking at the people and at another time Al-Hasan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and saying, "This son of mine is a Saiyid (i.e. chief) and perhaps Allah will bring about an agreement between two sects of the Muslims through him."
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to take him and Al-Hasan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and used to say, "O Allah! I love them, so please love them," or said something similar.
narrator: Narrated Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The head of Al-Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was brought to 'Ubaidullah bin Ziyad and was put in a tray, and then Ibn Ziyad started playing with a stick at the nose and mouth of Al-Husain's head and saying something about his handsome features." Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ then said (to him), "Al-Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ resembled the Prophet more than the others did." Anas added, "His (i.e. Al-Husain's) hair was dyed with Wasma (i.e. a kind of plant used as a dye).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I saw the Prophet (п·ә) carrying Al-Hasan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ on his shoulder an saying, "O Allah! I love him, so please love him."
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: You carrying Al-Hasan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and saying, "Let my father be sacrificed for you; you resemble the Prophet and not `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ," while `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was laughing at this.
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to say, "Please Muhammad (i.e. the Prophet) by doing good to his family."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: None resembled the Prophet (п·ә) more than Al-Hasan bin `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ did.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A person asked `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ whether a Muslim could kill flies. I heard him saying (in reply). "The people of Iraq are asking about the killing of flies while they themselves murdered the son of the daughter of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) . The Prophet (п·ә) said, They (i.e. Hasan and Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§) are my two sweet basils in this world."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abi Nu'm:

text: `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to say, "Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is our chief, and he manumitted our chief," meaning Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said to Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "If you have bought me for yourself then keep me (for yourself), but if you have bought me for Allah's Sake, then leave me for Allah's Work."
narrator: Narrated Qais:

text: The prophet п·ә cuddled me to his chest and said: O Allah! Teach him wisdom. Abu Mamar narrated us this Hadith and he said: O Allah! Teach him wisdom. Abu Mamar narrated us this Hadith and he said Abu Warith narrated us this Hadith that he п·әsaid: Teach him the Book. Musa narrated us this Hadith and Wohaib narrated him the Hadith like this from Khalid.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) had informed the people about the death of Zaid, Ja`far and Ibn Rawaha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… before the news of their death reached them. He said with his eyes flowing with tears, "Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took the flag and was martyred; then Ja`far ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took the flag and was martyred, and then Ibn Rawaha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took the flag and was martyred. Finally the flag was taken by one of Allah's Swords (i.e. Khalid bin Al-Walid  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) and Allah gave them (i.e. the Muslims) victory."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: `Abdullah (bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§) was mentioned before `Abdullah bin `Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ . The latter said, "That is a man I continue to love because I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, ' Learn the recitation of the Qur'an from (any of these) four persons: `Abdullah bin Masud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , Salim the freed slave of Abu Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , Ubai bin Ka`b, and Mu`adh bin Jabal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… ." I do not remember whether he mentioned Ubai first or Mu`adh.
narrator: Narrated Masruq:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) neither talked in an insulting manner nor did he ever speak evil intentionally. He used to say, "The most beloved to me amongst you is the one who has the best character and manners." He added, " Learn the Qur'an from (any of these) four persons. `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , Salim the freed slave of Abu Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , Ubai bin Ka`b, and Mu`adh bin Jabal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We asked Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ to tell us of a person resembling (to some extent) the Prophet (п·ә) in good appearance and straight forward behavior so that we may learn from him (good manners and acceptable conduct). Hudhaifa replied, "I do not know anybody resembling the Prophet (to some extent) in appearance and conduct more than Ibn Um `Abd.
narrator: Narrated `Abdur-Rahman bin Yazid:

text: My brother and I came from Yemen, and for some time we continued to consider `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ as one of the members of the family of the Prophet (п·ә) because we used to see him and his mother going in the house of the Prophet (п·ә) very often.
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Muawiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ offered one rak`a witr prayer after the `Isha prayer, and at that time a freed slave of Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ was present. He (i.e. the slave) went to Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ (and told him that Muawiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ offered one rak`a witr prayer). Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "Leave him, for he was in the company of Allah's Messenger (п·ә)."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abi Mulaika:

text: Somebody said to Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , "Can you speak to the chief of the believers Mu`awiyah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , as he does not pray except one rak`a as witr?" Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ replied, "He is a Faqih (i.e. a learned man who can give religious verdicts) ."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abi Mulaika:

text: Muawiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said (to the people), "You offer a prayer which we, who were the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) never saw the Prophet (п·ә) offering, and he forbade its offering," i.e. the two rak`at after the compulsory `Asr prayer.
narrator: Narrated Humran bin Aban:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Fatima is a part of me, and whoever makes her angry, makes me angry."
narrator: Narrated Al-Miswar bin Makhrama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said (to me), 'O Aish (`Aisha)! This is Gabriel greeting you.' I said, 'Peace and Allah's Mercy and Blessings be on him, you see what I don't see' " She was addressing Allah 's Apostle.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Many amongst men attained perfection but amongst women none attained the perfection except Mary, the daughter of `Imran and Asiya, the wife of Pharaoh. And the superiority of `Aisha to other women is like the superiority of Tharid (i.e. an Arabic dish) to other meals."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The superiority of `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ over other women is like the superiority of Tharid to other meals."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ became sick and Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ went to see her and said, "O mother of the believers! You are leaving for truthful fore-runners i.e. for Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and Abu Bakr.
narrator: Narrated Al-Qasim bin Muhammad:

text: When `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ sent `Ammar and Al-Hasan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ to (the people of) Kufa to urge them to fight, `Ammar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ addressed them saying, "I know that she (i.e. `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) is the wife of the Prophet (п·ә) in this world and in the Hereafter (world to come), but Allah has put you to test, whether you will follow Him (i.e. Allah) or her."
narrator: Narrated Abu Wail:

text: That she borrowed a necklace from Asma' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and it was lost. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) sent some of his companions to look for it. During their journey the time of prayer was due and they prayed without ablution. When they returned to the Prophet (п·ә) they complained about it. So the Divine Verse of Tayammum was revealed. Usaid bin Hudair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said (to `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§), "May Allah reward you handsomely. By Allah, whenever you have a difficulty, Allah took you out of it and brought with it, a Blessing for the Muslims."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was in his fatal illness, he started visiting his wives and saying, "Where will I be tomorrow?" He was anxious to be in `Aisha's home. `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said, "So when it was my day, the Prophet became silent (no longer asked the question).
narrator: Narrated Hisham's father:

text: Once, while I was sitting beside Zaid bin Al-Arqam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , he was asked, "How many Ghazwat did the Prophet undertake?" Zaid replied, "Nineteen." They said, "In how many Ghazwat did you join him?" He replied, "Seventeen." I asked, "Which of these was the first?" He replied, "Al-`Ashira or Al- `Ashiru." When I mentioned it to Qatadah, he replied: Oshair.
narrator: Narrated Abu 'Is-haq:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I never failed to join Allah's Messenger (п·ә) in any of his Ghazawat except in the Ghazwa of Tabuk. However, I did not take part in the Ghazwa of Badr, but none who failed to take part in it, was blamed, for Allah's Messenger (п·ә) had gone out to meet the caravans of (Quraish, but Allah caused them (i.e. Muslims) to meet their enemy unexpectedly (with no previous intention) .
narrator: Narrated Ka`b bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I witnessed Al-Miqdad bin Al-Aswad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ in a scene which would have been dearer to me than anything had I been the hero of that scene. He (i.e. Al-Miqdad) came to the Prophet (п·ә) while the Prophet (п·ә) was urging the Muslims to fight with the pagans. Al-Miqdad said, "We will not say as the People of Moses said: Go you and your Lord and fight you two. (5.27). But we shall fight on your right and on your left and in front of you and behind you." I saw the face of the Prophet (п·ә) getting bright with happiness, for that saying delighted him.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Masud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of the battle of Badr, the Prophet (п·ә) said, "O Allah! I appeal to You (to fulfill) Your Covenant and Promise. O Allah! If Your Will is that none should worship You (then give victory to the pagans)." Then Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took hold of him by the hand and said, "This is sufficient for you." The Prophet came out saying, "Their multitude will be put to flight and they will show their backs." (54.45)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The believers who failed to join the Ghazwa of Badr and those who took part in it are not equal (in reward). means that those who did not participate in the Battle of Badr cannot be equal to those who participated in the Battle of Badr.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I and Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ were considered too young to take part in the battle of Badr.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I and Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ were considered too young (to take part) in the battle of Badr, and the number of the Emigrant warriors were over sixty (men) and the Ansar were over 240.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The companions of (the Prophet) Muhammad who took part in Badr, told me that their number was that of Saul's (i.e. Talut's) companions who crossed the river (of Jordan) with him and they were over three-hundred-and-ten men. Hadrat Braa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ says: By Allah, none crossed the river with him but a believer. (See Qur'an 2:249)
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: We, the Companions of Muhammad used to say that the number of the warriors of Badr was the same as the number of Saul's companions who crossed the river (of Jordan) with him, and none crossed the river with him but a believer, and the were over three-hundred-and-ten men.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: We used to say that the warriors of Badr were over three-hundred-and-ten, as many as the Taloot's Companions of Saul who crossed the river with him; and none crossed the river with him but a believer.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) faced the Ka`ba and invoked evil on some people of Quraish, on Shaiba bin Rabi`a, `Utba bin Rabi`a, Al-Walid bin `Utba and Abu Jahl bin Hisham. I bear witness, by Allah, that I saw them all dead in Badr and their corpses had due to heat, and that day was really a scorcher.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of the Battle of Badr, I reached near Abu Jehl while there were still some signs of life in him. So Abu Jahl said to me: Is there anyone better than I amongst the people you have killed?
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Who will go and see what has happened to Abu Jahl?" Ibn Mas`ud went and found that the two sons of 'Afra had struck him fatally (and he was in his last breaths). `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "Are you Abu Jahl?" And took him by the beard. Abu Jahl said, "Can there be a man superior to one you have killed or one whom his own folk have killed?" Ahmad bin Yousuf' report also contains ''ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҺ ШЈЩҺШЁЩҸЩҲ Ш¬ЩҺЩҮЩ’Щ„ЩҚ'' .
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Badr, the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Who will go and see what has happened to Abu Jahl?" Ibn Mas`ud went and found that the two sons of 'Afra had struck him fatally. `Abdullah bin Mas`ud got hold of his beard and said, "'Are you Abu Jahl?" He replied, "Can there be a man more superior to one whom his own folk have killed (or you have killed)?" Hadrat Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ has also reported the same.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The story of Badr, namely, the narration regarding the sons of 'Afra'.
narrator: Narrated `Abdur-Rahman bin `Auf (the grandfather of Salih bin Ibrahim):
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "I shall be the first man to kneel down before (Allah), the Beneficent to receive His judgment on the day of Resurrection (in my favor)." Qais bin Ubad also said, "The following Verse was revealed in their connection:-- "These two opponents believers and disbelievers) Dispute with each other About their Lord." (22.19) Qais said that they were those who fought on the day of Badr, namely, Hamza, `Ali, 'Ubaida or Abu 'Ubaida bin Al-Harith, Shaiba bin Rabi`a, `Utba and Al-Wahd bin `Utba.
narrator: Narrated Ali bin Abi Talib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The following Holy Verse:-- "These two opponents (believers & disbelievers) dispute with each other about their Lord," (22.19) was revealed concerning six men from Quraish, namely, `Ali, Hamza, 'Ubaida bin Al-Harith; Shaiba bin Rabi`a, `Utba bin Rabi`a and Al-Walid bin `Utba.
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The following Holy Verse:-- "These two opponents (believers and disbelievers) dispute with each other about their Lord." (22.19) was revealed concerning us.
narrator: Narrated `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: These Holy Verses were revealed in connection with those six persons on the day of Badr, and then he reported as per former Hadith.
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The following Holy verse:-- "These two opponents (believers and disbelievers) disputing with each other about their Lord," (22.19) was revealed concerning those men who fought on the day of Badr, namely, Hamza, `Ali, Ubaida bin Al-Harith ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…  , `Utba and Shaiba----the two sons of Rabi`a-- and Al-Walid bin `Utba.
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: A man asked Al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  and I was listening, "Did `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ take part in (the battle of) Badr?" Al-Bara' said, "(Yes). he even met (his enemies) in a duel and was clad in two armors (one over the other).
narrator: Narrated Abu 'Is-haq:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "I had an agreement with Umaiya bin Khalaf (that he would look after my relatives and property in Mecca, and I would look after his relatives and property in Medina)." `Abdur-Rahman then mentioned the killing of Umaiya and his son on the day of Badr, and Bilal said, "Woe to me if Umaiya remains safe (i.e. alive), I will not get delivered (on the Resurrection Day).
narrator: Narrated `Abdur-Rahman bin `Auf ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) recited Surat-an-Najm and then prostrated himself, and all who were with him prostrated too. But an old man took a handful of dust and touched his forehead with it saying, "This is sufficient for me. Hadrat Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ says: " Later on I saw him killed as an infidel.
narrator: Narrated 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: That his father said, "The sword of Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was decorated with silver." Hisham added, "The sword of `Urwa was (also) decorated with silver. "
narrator: Narrated Hisham ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with the reference to his father:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: By Allah! These are the Infidels of the Quraish who change the favour with unthankfulness. Amr (bin Dinaar), the sub narrator says: They (who changed the favour with unthankfulness) are the Quraish, and Muhammad is the favour of Allah. And the Verse: ''And they have brought  their people into the abode of ruin,'' means they reached Hell on the day of Badr.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) stood at the well of Badr (which contained the corpses of the pagans) and said, "Have you found true what your lord promised you?" Then he further said, "They now hear what I say." This was mentioned before `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and she said, "But the Prophet (п·ә) said, 'Now they know very well that what I used to tell them was the truth.' Then she recited (the Holy Verse):-- "You cannot make the dead hear... ...till the end of Verse)." (30.52)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Haritha was martyred on the day (of the battle) of Badr, and he was a young boy then. His mother came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! You know how dear Haritha is to me. If he is in Paradise, I shall remain patient, and hope for reward from Allah, but if it is not so, then you shall see what I do?" He said, "May Allah be merciful to you! Have you lost your senses? Do you think there is only one Paradise? There are many Paradises and your son is in the (most superior) Paradise of Al- Firdaus."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Badr, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to us, "When the enemy comes near to you, shoot at them but use your arrows sparingly (so that your arrows should not be wasted).
narrator: Narrated Usaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of (the battle of) Badr, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to us, "When your enemy comes near to you (i.e. overcome you by sheer number), shoot at them but use your arrows sparingly."
narrator: Narrated Abu Usaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Uhud the Prophet (п·ә) appointed `Abdullah bin Jubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as chief of the archers, and seventy among us were injured and martyred. On the day (of the battle) of Badr, the Prophet (п·ә) and his companions had inflicted 140 casualties on the pagans, 70 were taken prisoners, and 70 were killed. Abu Sufyan said, "This is a day of (revenge) for the day of Badr and the issue of war is undecided ."
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The good is what Allah gave us later on (after Uhud), and the reward of truthfulness is what Allah gave us after the day (of the battle) of Badr."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ was once told that Said bin Zaid bin 'Amr bin Nufail ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , one of the Badr warriors, had fallen ill on a Friday. Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ rode to him late in the forenoon. The time of the Friday prayer approached and Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ did not take part in the Friday prayer.
narrator: Narrated Nafi:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Gabriel came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "How do you look upon the warriors of Badr among yourselves?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "As the best of the Muslims." or said a similar statement. On that, Gabriel said, "And so are the Angels who participated in the Badr (battle).
narrator: Narrated Rifaa (who was one of the Badr warriors) :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Rifa`a ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was one of the warriors of Badr while (his father) Rafi` ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was one of the people of Al-`Aqaba (i.e. those who gave the pledge of allegiance at Al-`Aqaba). Rafi` used to say to his son, "I would not have been happier if I had taken part in the Badr battle instead of taking part in the 'Aqaba pledge." He would say that Hadrat Gibriel Ш№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… asked the prophet п·ә about it. As is mentioned above.
narrator: Narrated Mu`adh bin Rifa`a bin Rafi`:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: An Angle inquired of the prophet п·ә. Yahya says that Yazeed bin Al-Haad told him that he was along with Hadrat Muadh the day he narrated this Hadith. Yazeed states that Hadrat Muadh said: The name of the Angle who inquired was Hadrat Gibriel Ш№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… .
narrator: Narrated Mu`adh bin Rifaah:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said on the day (of the battle) of Badr, "This is Gabriel Ш№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… holding the head of his horse and equipped with arms for the battle.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Abu Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ died and did not leave any offspring, and he was one of the Badr warriors.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Abu Sa`id bin Malik Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ returned from a journey and his family offered him some meat of sacrifices offered at `Id ul Adha. On that he said, "I will not eat it before asking (whether it is allowed)." He went to his maternal brother, Qatada bin Ni 'man ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , who was one of the Badr warriors, and asked him about it. Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "After your departure, an order was issued by the Prophet (п·ә) cancelling the prohibition of eating sacrifices after three days."
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin khubaib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: (who was one of the Badr warriors) Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Give me the pledge of allegiance."
narrator: Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) came to me the night my marriage was consummated and sat down on my bed as you (the sub-narrator) are sitting now, and small girls were beating the tambourine and singing in lamentation of my father who had been killed on the day of the battle of Badr. Then one of the girls said, "There is a Prophet amongst us who knows what will happen tomorrow." The Prophet (п·ә) said (to her)," Do not say this, but go on saying what you have spoken before."
narrator: Narrated Ar-Rubai bint Muauwidh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Abu Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , a companion of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and one of those who fought at Badr together with Allah's Apostle told me that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said. "Angels do not enter a house in which there is a dog or a picture" He meant the images of creatures that have souls.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ led the funeral prayer of Sahl bin Hunaif ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and said, "He was one of the warriors of Badr."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Maqal:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A man's spending on his family is a deed of charity."
narrator: Narrated Abu Masud Al-Badri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "Al-Mughira bin Shu`ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ delayed the `Asr prayer when he was the ruler of Al-Kufa. On that, Abu Mas`ud. `Uqba bin `Amr Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , the grand-father of Zaid bin Hasan, who was one of the Badr warriors, came in and said, (to Al-Mughira), 'You know that Gabriel came down and offered the prayer and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) prayed five prescribed prayers, and Gabriel said (to the Prophet (п·ә) ), "I have been ordered to do so (i.e. offer these five prayers at these fixed stated hours of the day). Bashir bin Abu Masoud would report this Hadith from his father liktwise.
narrator: Narrated `Umar bin `Abdul `Aziz:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "It is sufficient for one to recite the last two Verses of Surat-al-Baqara at night." Abdur Rehman (bin Yazeed) narrates: I visited Hadrat Abu Masoud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ When he was performing circumambulation  of the Ka'bah and asked him of it, so he narrated me the Hadith in the same way.
narrator: Narrated Abu Masud Al-Badri  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: `Itban bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who was one of the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) and one of the warriors of Badr, came to Allah's Messenger (п·ә).
narrator: Narrated Mahmud bin Ar-Rabi:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I asked Al-Husain bin Muhammad who was one of the sons of Salim and one of the nobles amongst them, about the narration of Mahmud bin Ar-Rabi 'from `Itban bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and he confirmed it.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Shihab:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: who was one of the leaders of Bani `Adi and his father participated in the battle of Badr in the company of the Prophet. `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ appointed Qudama bin Maz'un ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as ruler of Bahrain, Qudama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was one of the warriors of the battle of Badr and was the maternal uncle of `Abdullah bin `Umar and Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… .
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr bin Rabi`a ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: His two paternal uncles who had fought in the battle of Badr informed him that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) forbade the renting of fields. I said to Salim, "Do you rent your land?" He said, "Yes, for Rafi`  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is mistaken."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I saw Rifa`a bin Rafi` Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who was a Badr warrior.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Shaddad bin Al-Had Al-Laithi:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ used to kill all kinds of snakes until Abu Lubaba Al-Badri told him that the Prophet (п·ә) had forbidden the killing of harmless snakes living in houses and called Jinan. So Ibn `Umar gave up killing them.
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Some men of the Ansar requested Allah's Messenger (п·ә) to allow them to see him, they said, "Allow us to forgive the ransom of our sister's son, `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "By Allah, you will not leave a single Dirham of it!"
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said on the day of Badr, "Who will go and see what has happened to Abu Jahl?" Ibn Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ went and saw him struck by the two sons of 'Afra and was on the point of death . Ibn Mas`ud said, "Are you Abu Jahl?" Abu Jahl replied, "Can there be a man more superior to the one whom you have killed (or as Sulaiman said, or his own folk have killed.)?" Abu Jahl added, "Would that I had been killed by other than a mere farmer. "
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "When the Prophet (п·ә) died I said to Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , 'Let us go to our Ansari brethren.' We met two pious men from them, who had fought in the battle of Badr." When I mentioned this to `Urwa bin Az-Zubair, he said, "Those two pious men were 'Uwaim bin Sa`ida and Manbin Adi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ ."
narrator: Narrated `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Badr warriors were given five thousand (Dirhams) each, yearly. `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I will surely give them more than what I will give to others."
narrator: Narrated Qais:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) reciting Surat-at-Tur in Maghrib prayer, and that was at a time when belief was first planted in my heart.
narrator: Narrated Jubair bin Mut'im:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: When the first civil strife (in Islam) took place because of the murder of 'Uthman       ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , it left none of the Badr warriors alive. When the second civil strife, that is the battle of Al-Harra, took place, it left none of the Hudaibiya treaty companions alive. Then the third civil strife took place and it did not subside till it had exhausted all the strength of the people.
narrator: Narrated Said bin Al-Musaiyab:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "I heard `Urwa bin Az-Zubair. Sa`id bin Al-Musaiyab, 'Alqama bin Waqqas and 'Ubaidullah bin `Abdullah each narrating part of the narrative concerning `Aisha  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ the wife of the Prophet. `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said: When I and Um Mistah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ were returning, Um Mistah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ stumbled by treading on the end of her robe, and on that she said, 'May Mistah be ruined.' I said, 'You have said a bad thing, you curse a man who took part in the battle of Badri Companion? Then she narrated the whole episode of calumny."
narrator: Narrated Az-Zuhri :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Badr, (Quraishi) Emigrants received 100 shares of the war booty."
narrator: Narrated Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Bani An-Nadir and Bani Quraiza fought (against the Prophet (п·ә) violating their peace treaty), so the Prophet exiled Bani An-Nadir and allowed Bani Quraiza to remain at their places (in Medina) taking nothing from them till they fought against the Prophet (п·ә) again) . He then killed their men and distributed their women, children and property among the Muslims, but some of them came to the Prophet (п·ә) and he granted them safety, and they embraced Islam. He exiled all the Jews from Medina. They were the Jews of Bani Qainuqa', the tribe of `Abdullah bin Salam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and the Jews of Bani Haritha and all the other Jews of Medina.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I mentioned to Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ Surat-Hashr. He said, "Call it Surat-an-Nadir." Hoshaim has has also reported from Abu Bishr likewise.
narrator: Narrated Sa`id bin Jubair:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Some people used to allot some date palm trees to the Prophet (п·ә) as gift till he conquered Banu Quraiza and Bani An-Nadir, where upon he started returning their date palms to them.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) had the date-palm trees of Bani Al-Nadir burnt and cut down at a place called Al-Buwaira. Allah then revealed: "What you cut down of the date-palm trees (of the enemy) Or you left them standing on their stems. It was by Allah's Permission." (59.5)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) burnt the date-palm trees of Bani An-Nadir. Hassan bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said the following poetic Verses about this event:-- "the terrible burning of Al-Buwaira Has been received indifferently By the nobles of Bani Luai (The masters and nobles of Quraish)." Abu Sufyan bin Al-Harith (i.e. the Prophet's cousin who was still a disbeliever then) replied to Hassan, saying in poetic verses:-- "May Allah bless that burning And set all its (i.e. Medina's) Parts on burning fire. You will see who is far from it (i.e. Al-Buwaira) And which of our lands will be Harmed by it (i.e. the burning of Al- Buwaira).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)
