text: Fatima ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and Al-`Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came to Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , claiming their inheritance of the Prophet's land of Fadak and his share from Khaibar. Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, 'Our property is not inherited, and whatever we leave is to be given in charity. But the family of Muhammad can take their sustenance from this property.' By Allah, I would love to do good to the Kith and kin of Allah's Apostle rather than to my own Kith and kin."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) sent a group of persons to Abu Rafi`. `Abdullah bin Atik entered his house at night, while he was sleeping, and killed him.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara bin Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Uhud. the Prophet (п·ә) said, "This is Gabriel holding the head of his horse and equipped with war material.'
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: A meal was brought to `Abdur-Rahman bin `Auf ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ while he was fasting. He said, "Mus`ab bin `Umar was martyred, and he was better than I, yet he was shrouded in a Burda (i.e. a sheet) so that, if his head was covered, his feet became naked, and if his feet were covered, his head became naked." `Abdur-Rahman added, "Hamza was martyred and he was better than 1. Then worldly wealth was bestowed upon us and we were given thereof too much. We are afraid that the reward of our deeds have been given to us in this life." `Abdur-Rahman then started weeping so much that he left the food.
narrator: Narrated Sa`d bin Ibrahim:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of the battle of Uhud, a man came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "Can you tell me where I will be if I should get martyred?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "In Paradise." The man threw away some dates he was carrying in his hand, and fought till he was martyred .
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: When we wrote the Holy Qur'an, I missed one of the Verses of Surat-al-Ahzab which I used to hear Allah's Messenger (п·ә) reciting. Then we searched for it and found it with Khuza`ima bin Thabit Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . The Verse was:-- 'Among the Believers are men Who have been true to Their Covenant with Allah, Of them, some have fulfilled Their obligations to Allah (i.e. they have been Killed in Allah's Cause), And some of them are (still) waiting" (33.23) So we wrote this in its place in the Qur'an.
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: This Verse: "When two of your parties almost Decided to fall away..." was revealed in our connection, i.e. Bani Salama and Bani Haritha and I would not have liked that, if it was not revealed, for Allah said:-- But Allah was their Protector.....(3.122)
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to me, "Have you got married O Jabir?" I replied, "Yes." He asked "What, a virgin or a matron?" I replied, "Not a virgin but a matron." He said, "Why did you not marry a young girl who would have fondled with you?" I replied, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! My father was martyred on the day of Uhud and left nine (orphan) daughters who are my nine sisters; so I disliked to have another young girl of their age, but (I sought) an (elderly) woman who could comb their hair and look after them." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "You have done the right thing."
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) on the day of the battle of Uhud accompanied by two men fighting on his behalf. They were dressed in white and were fighting as bravely as possible. I had never seen them before, nor did I see them later on.
narrator: Narrated Sa`d bin Abi Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) took out a quiver (of arrows) for me on the day of Uhud and said, "Throw (arrows); let my father and mother be sacrificed for you."
narrator: Narrated Sa`d bin Abi Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) mentioned both his father and mother for me on the day of the battle of Uhud.
narrator: Narrated Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) mentioned both his father and mother for me on the day of the battle of Uhud." He meant when the Prophet (п·ә) said (to Sa`d) while the latter was fighting. "Let my father and mother be sacrificed for you!"
narrator: Narrated Sa`d bin Abi Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I have never heard the Prophet (п·ә) mentioning both his father and mother for anybody other than Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I have never heard the Prophet (п·ә) mentioning his father and mother for anybody other than Sa`d bin Malik. I heard him saying on the day of Uhud, "O Sa`d throw (arrows)! Let my father and mother be sacrificed for you !"
narrator: Narrated `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: In the battles in which the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) fought the infidels. At one point in some of the Ghazwa (Ahud) there was no one left with him except Talha and Saad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§.
narrator: Narrated `Uthman:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I have been in the company of `Abdur Rahman bin `Auf, Talha bin 'Ubaidullah, Al-Miqdad and Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…, and I heard none of them narrating anything from the Prophet (п·ә) excepting the fact that I heard Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrating about the day of Uhud (battle) .
narrator: Narrated As-Saib bin Yazid:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I saw Talha's paralyzed hand with which he had protected the Prophet (п·ә) on the day of Uhud.
narrator: Narrated Qais:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) appointed `Abdullah bin Jubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ as the commander of the cavalry archers on the day of the battle of Uhud. Then they returned defeated, and that what is referred to by Allah's Statement:-- "And the Apostle (Muhammad) was in your rear calling you back." (3.153)
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I was amongst those who were overtaken by slumber until my sword fell from my hand on several occasions. The sword fell and I picked it up, and it fell again, and I picked it up."
narrator: Narrated Abu Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: He heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә), when raising his head from bowing of the first rak`a of the morning prayer, saying, "O Allah! Curse so-and-so and so-and-so" after he had said, "Allah hears him who sends his praises to Him. Our Lord, all the Praises are for you!" So Allah revealed:-- "Not for you (O Muhammad! )......(till the end of Verse) they are indeed wrong-doers." (3.128)
narrator: Narrated Salim's father:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to invoke evil upon Safwan bin Umaiya, Suhail bin `Amr and Al-Harith bin Hisham. So the Verse was revealed:-- "Not for you (O Muhammad!)......(till the end of Verse) For they are indeed wrong-doers." (3.128)
narrator: Narrated Salim bin `Abdullah:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) (pointing to his broken canine tooth) said, "Allah's Wrath has become severe on the people who harmed His Prophet. Allah's Wrath has become severe on the man who is killed by the Apostle of Allah in Allah's Cause."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Wrath became severe on him whom the Prophet (п·ә) had killed in Allah's Cause. Allah's Wrath became severe on the people who caused the face of Allah's Prophet to bleed.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "By Allah, I know who washed the wounds of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and who poured water (for washing them), and with what he was treated." Sahl added, "Fatima ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the daughter of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to wash the wounds, and `Ali bin Abi Talib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to pour water from a shield. When Fatima ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ saw that the water aggravated the bleeding, she took a piece of a mat, burnt it, and inserted its ashes into the wound so that the blood was congealed (and bleeding stopped). His canine tooth got broken on that day, and face was wounded, and his helmet was broken on his head."
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Wrath gets severe on a person killed by a prophet, and Allah's Wrath became severe on him who had caused the face of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) to bleed.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: We do not know of any tribe amongst the 'Arab tribes who lost more martyrs than Al-Ansar, and they will have superiority on the Day of Resurrection. Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ told us that seventy from the Ansar were martyred on the day of Uhud, and seventy on the day (of the battle of) Bir Ma'una, and seventy on the day of Al-Yamama. Anas added, "The battle of Bir Ma'una took place during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and the battle of Al-Yamama, during the caliphate of Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and it was the day when Musailamah Al-Kadhdhab was killed."
narrator: Narrated Qatada:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I saw in a dream that I moved a sword and its blade got broken, and that symbolized the casualties which the believers suffered on the day of Uhud. Then I moved it again, and it became as perfect as it had been, and that symbolized the Conquest (of Mecca) which Allah helped us to achieve, and the union of all the believers. I (also) saw cows in the dream, and what Allah does is always beneficial. Those cows appeared to symbolize the faithful believers (who were martyred) on the day of Uhud."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "This is a mountain that loves us and is loved by us."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: When the mountain of Uhud appeared before Allah's Messenger (п·ә) he said, "This IS a mountain that loves us and is loved by us. O, Allah! Abraham made Mecca a Sanctuary, and I have made Medina (i.e. the area between its two mountains) a Sanctuary as well."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: One day the Prophet (п·ә) went out and offered the (funeral) prayer for the people (i.e. martyrs) of Uhud as he used to offer a funeral prayer for any dead person, and then (after returning) he ascended the pulpit and said, "I am your predecessor before you, and I am a witness upon you, and I am looking at my Tank just now, and I have been given the keys of the treasures of the world (or the keys of the world). By Allah, I am not afraid that you will worship others besides Allah after me, but I am afraid that you will compete with each other for (the pleasures of) this world."
narrator: Narrated `Uqba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The person who killed Khubaib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was Abu Sarua (i.e. `Uqba bin Al-Harith).
narrator: Narrated Jabir:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said Al-Qunut for one month after the posture of Bowing, invoking evil upon some 'Arab tribes.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: When Haram bin Milhan, his uncle was stabbed on the day of Bir Ma'una he sprinkled his blood over his face and his head this way and then said, "I have succeeded, by the Lord of the Ka`ba.'
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said Al-Qunut after Bowing (i.e. Ar-Ruku') for one month, invoking evil upon (the tribes of) Ril and Dhakwan. He used to say, "Usaiya disobeyed Allah and His Apostle."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) invoked evil upon those (people) who killed his companions at Bir Mauna for 30 days (in the morning prayer). He invoked evil upon (tribes of) Ril, Lihyan and Usaiya who disobeyed Allah and His Apostle. Allah revealed a Qur'anic Verse to His Prophet regarding those who had been killed, i.e. the Muslims killed at Bir Ma'una, and we recited the Verse till later it was cancelled. (The Verse was:) 'Inform our people that we have met our Lord, and He is pleased with us, and we are pleased with Him."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) inspected him on the day of Uhud while he was fourteen years old, and the Prophet (п·ә) did not allow him to take part in the battle. He was inspected again by the Prophet (п·ә) on the day of Al- Khandaq (i.e. battle of the Trench) while he was fifteen years old, and the Prophet (п·ә) allowed him to take Part in the battle.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: We were with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) in the Trench, and some were digging the trench while we were carrying the earth on our shoulders. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'O Allah! There is no life except the life of the Hereafter, so please forgive the Emigrants and the Ansar."
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went out towards the Khandaq (i.e. Trench) and saw the Emigrants and the Ansar digging the trench in the cold morning. They had no slaves to do that (work) for them. When the Prophet saw their hardship and hunger, he said, 'O Allah! The real life is the life of the Hereafter, so please forgive Ansar and the Emigrants." They said in reply to him, "We are those who have given the Pledge of allegiances to Muhammad for to observe Jihad as long as we live."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: As regards the following Qur'anic Verse:-- "When they came on you from above and from below you (from east and west of the valley) and when the eyes grew wild and the hearts reached up to the throats....." (33.10) That happened on the day of Al-Khandaq (i.e. Trench).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was carrying earth on the day of Al-Khandaq till his `Abdomen was fully covered with dust, and he was saying, "By Allah, without Allah we would not have been guided, neither would we have given in charity, nor would we have prayed. So (O Allah), please send Sakina (i.e. calmness) upon us, and make our feet firm if we meet the enemy as the enemy have rebelled against us, and if they intended affliction, (i.e. want to frighten us and fight against us then we would not flee but withstand them)." The Prophet (п·ә) used to raise his voice saying, "Abaina! Abaina! (i.e. would not, we would not).
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I have been made victorious by As-Saba (i.e. an easterly wind) and the Ad nation was destroyed by Ad-Dabur (i.e. a westerly wind).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The first day (i.e. Ghazwa) I participated in, was the day of Al-Khandaq (i.e. Trench).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Al-Ahzab (i.e. clans) the Prophet (п·ә) said, (After this battle) we will go to attack them(i.e. the infidels) and they will not come to attack us."
narrator: Narrated Sulaiman bin Surd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: When the clans were driven away, I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "From now onwards we will go to attack them (i.e. the infidels) and they will not come to attack us, but we will go to them."
narrator: Narrated Sulaiman bin Surd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Al-Khandaq (i.e. Trench), the Prophet (п·ә) said '(Let) Allah fill their (i.e. the infidels') houses and graves with fire just as they have prevented us from offering the Middle Prayer (i.e. `Asr prayer) till the sun had set."
narrator: Narrated `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came on the day of Al-Khandaq after the sun had set and he was abusing the infidels of Quraish saying, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I was unable to offer the (`Asr) prayer till the sun was about to set." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "By Allah, I have not offered this (i.e. `Asr) prayer." So we came down along with the Prophet (п·ә) to Buthan where he performed ablution for the prayer and then we performed the ablution for it. Then he offered the `Asr prayer after the sun had set, and after it he offered the Maghrib prayer.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Al-Ahzab (i.e. clans), Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, 'Who will bring us the news of the people (i.e. the clans of Quraish infidels)?" Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I." The Prophet (п·ә) again said, "Who will bring us the news of the people?" Az Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I." The Prophet (п·ә) again said, "Who will bring us the news of the people?" Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I." The Prophet (п·ә) then said, "Every prophet has his Hawari (i.e. disciplespecial helper); my disciple is Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to say, "None has the right to be worshipped except Allah Alone (Who) honored His Warriors and made His Slave victorious, and He (Alone) defeated the (infidel) clans; so there is nothing after Him.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) invoked evil upon the clans saying, "Allah, the Revealer of the Holy Book (i.e. the Qur'an), the Quick Taker of the accounts! Please defeat the clans. O Allah! Defeat them and shake them."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abi `Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Whenever Allah's Messenger (п·ә) returned from a Ghazwa, Hajj or `Umra, he used to start (saying), "Allahu- Akbar," thrice and then he would say, "None has the right to be worshipped except Allah alone Who has no partners. To Him belongs the Kingdom, all praises are for Him, and He is able to do all things (i.e. Omnipotent). We are returning with repentance (to Allah) worshipping, prostrating, and praising our Lord. Allah has fulfilled His Promise, made His Slave victorious, and He (Alone) defeated the clans (of infidels) ."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: When the Prophet (п·ә) returned from Al-Khandaq (i.e. Trench) and laid down his arms and took a bath, Gabriel came and said (to the Prophet (п·ә) ), You have laid down your arms? By Allah, we angels have not laid them down yet. So set out for them." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Where to go?" Gabriel said, "Towards this side," pointing towards Banu Quraiza. So the Prophet (п·ә) went out towards them.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: As if I am just now looking at the dust rising in the street of Banu Ghanm (in Medina) because of the marching of Gabriel's regiment when Allah's Messenger (п·ә) set out to Banu Quraiza (to attack them).
narrator: Narrated Anas  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Al-Ahzab (i.e. Clans) the Prophet (п·ә) said, "None of you Muslims) should offer the `Asr prayer but at Banu Quraiza's place." The `Asr prayer became due for some of them on the way. Some of those said, "We will not offer it till we reach it, the place of Banu Quraiza," while some others said, "No, we will pray at this spot, for the Prophet (п·ә) did not mean that for us." Later on It was mentioned to the Prophet (п·ә) and he did not berate any of the two groups.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The people of (Banu) Quraiza agreed to accept the verdict of Sa`d bin Mu`adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . So the Prophet (п·ә) sent for Sa`d, and the latter came (riding) a donkey and when he approached the Mosque, the Prophet (п·ә) said to the Ansar, "Get up for your chief or for the best among you." Then the Prophet (п·ә) said (to Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ)." These (i.e. Banu Quraiza) have agreed to accept your verdict." Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "Kill their (men) warriors and take their offspring as captives, "On that the Prophet (п·ә) said, "You have judged according to Allah's Judgment," or said, "according to the King's judgment."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to Hassan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "Abuse them (with your poems), and Gabriel is with you (i.e, supports you)." (Through another group of sub narrators) Al-Bara bin Azib said, "On the day of Quraiza's (besiege), Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to Hassan bin Thabit, 'Abuse them (with your poems), and Gabriel is with you (i.e. supports you).' "
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "On the day of Quraiza's (siege), Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to Hassan bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , 'Abuse them (with your poems), and Jibril is with you.'"
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) led his Companions in the Fear Prayer in the seventh Ghazwa i.e. the Ghazwa of Dhat-ur-Riqa. Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "The Prophet (п·ә) offered the Fear Prayer at a place called Dhi-Qarad."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin Abdullah (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§):
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) led the people in the Fear Prayer on the day of Muharib and Tha'laba (i.e. the day of the battle of Dhat-ur-Riqa').
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) set out for the battle of Dhat-ur-Riqa' at a place called Nakhl and he met a group of people from Ghatafan, but there was no clash (between them); the people were afraid of each other and the Prophet (п·ә) offered the two raka'at of the Fear prayer." Narrated Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : "I fought in the company of the Prophet (п·ә) on the day of al-Qarad."
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "We went out in the company of the Prophet (п·ә) for a Ghazwa and we were six persons having one camel which we rode in rotation. So, (due to excessive walking) our feet became thin and my feet became thin and my nail dropped, and we used to wrap our feet with the pieces of cloth, and for this reason, the Ghazwa was named Dhat-ur-Riqa as we wrapped our feet with rags." When Abu- Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrated this (Hadith), he felt regretful to do so and said, as if he disliked to have disclosed a good deed of his.
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "We were with the Prophet (п·ә) at Nakhl," and then he mentioned the Fear prayer. Imam Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ says: ''Out of all reports, I have heard about the fear prayer, this is the best.'' Narrated Al-Qasim bin Muhammad: The Prophet (п·ә) offered the Fear prayer in the Ghazwa of Banu Anmar.
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I took part in a Ghazwa towards Najd along with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and we clashed with the enemy, and we lined up for them.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) led the Fear-prayer with one of the two batches of the army while the other (batch) faced the enemy. Then the first batch went away and took places of their companions (i.e. second batch) and the second batch came and he led his second rak`a with them. Then he (i.e. the Prophet: finished his prayer with Taslim and then each of the two batches got up and completed their remaining one rak`a.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: He had participated in a Ghazwa towards Najd in the company of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) .
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "We were with the Prophet (п·ә) at Nakhl and he offered the Fear prayer." Abu Huraira  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I offered the Fear prayer with the Prophet (п·ә) during the Ghazwa (i.e. the battle) of Najd." Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came to the Prophet (п·ә) during the day of Khaibar.
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I saw the Prophet (п·ә) offering his Nawafil prayer on his Mount facing the East during the Ghazwa of Anmar.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "Have you heard that `Ali' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was one of those who slandered `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ?" I replied, "No, but two men from your people (named) Abu Salama bin `Abdur-Rahman and Abu Bakr bin `Abdur-Rahman bin Al-Harith have informed me that Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ told them that `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ remained silent about her case."Then the people asked Hisham bin Yusuf (or Zuhri) again. He said the same thing, "Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…Ш§", did not doubt it, did not say the word "Щ…ШіЩҠШҰШ§" and omitted the word "Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ" (that is, Zahri did not answer anything else to Walid and the word "Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…Ш§" was in the old version).
narrator: Narrated Al-Walid bin `Abdul Malik:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ used to recite this Verse:-- 'Ida taliqunahu bi-alsinatikum' (24.15) "(As you tell lie with your tongues.)" and used to say "Al-Walaq" means "telling of a lie. "She knew this Verse more than anybody else as it was revealed about her.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abi Malaika:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: I started abusing Hassan in front of `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ . She said, "Do not abuse him as he used to defend Allah's Apostle (against the infidels). `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ added, "Once Hassan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took the permission from the Prophet (п·ә) to say poetic verses against the infidels. On that the Prophet (п·ә) said, 'How will you exclude my forefathers (from that)? Hassan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ replied, 'I will take you out of them as one takes a hair out of the dough." Hisham's father added, "I abused Hassan as he was one of those who spoke against `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ."
narrator: Narrated Hisham's father:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) performed four `Umras, all in the month of Dhul-Qa'da, except the one which he performed with his Hajj (i.e. in Dhul-Hijja). He performed one `Umra from Al-Hudaibiya in Dhul- Qa'da, another `Umra in the following year in Dhul Qa'da a third from Al-Jirana where he distributed the war booty of Hunain, in Dhul Qa'da, and the fourth `Umra he performed was with his Hajj.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: We set out with the Prophet (п·ә) in the year of Al-Hudaibiya, and all his companions assumed the state of Ihram but I did not.
narrator: Narrated Abu Qatada:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: They were in the company of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) on the day of Al-Hudaibiya and their number was 1400 or more. They camped at a well and drew its water till it was dried. When they informed Allah's Apostle of that, he came and sat over its edge and said, "Bring me a bucket of its water." When it was brought, he spat and invoked (Allah) and said, "Leave it for a while." Then they quenched their thirst and watered their riding animals (from that well) till they departed.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara bin Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "I have been informed that Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said that the number (of Al-Hudaibiya Muslim warriors) was 1400." Sa`id said to me, "Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrated to me that they were 1500 who gave the Pledge of allegiance to the Prophet (п·ә) on the day of Al-Hudaibiya.' Abu Dawood said: Qurrah has reported from Qatabah. Moreover, Muhammad bin  Bashshaar from Abu Dawood and he from Shobah have reported it.
narrator: Narrated  Sa`id bin Al-Musaiyab:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: On the day of Al-Hudaibiya, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to us' "You are the best people on the earth!" We were 1400 then. If I could see now, I would have shown you the place of the Tree (beneath which we gave the Pledge of Allegiance)."  Amash with reference to Salim has reported 1400 from Hadrat Jaabir."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: "The people (who gave the Pledge of allegiance) under the Tree numbered 1300 and the number of Bani Aslam was 1/8 of the Emigrants." This tradition was followed by Muhammad bin Bashar, narrated by Abu Dawud Tayyalsi, and Shuba narrated it from him.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Abi `Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Who was among those (who had given the Pledge of allegiance) under the Tree: Pious people will die in succession, and there will remain the dregs of society who will be like the useless residues of dates and barley and Allah will pay no attention to them.
narrator: Narrated Mirdas Al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: The Prophet (п·ә) accompanied by more than one thousand Conmpanions, set out and when they reached Dhul-Hulaifa, he garlanded and marked his Hadi and assumed the state of Ihram for Umrah. I cannot count the time I have heard this Hadith from Sufyan till I heard him say: I forget from Zuhri the mentioning of garlanding and marking. I am not sure whether I have forgotten the place of marking and garlanding or the complete Hadith.
narrator: Narrated Marwan and Al-Miswar bin Makhrama:
chapterName: Military Expeditions led by the Prophet (PBUH) (Al-Maghaazi)

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "When the Imam says: 'Ghair-il-Maghdubi `alaihim Walad-Dallin (i.e. not the path of those who earn Your Anger, nor the path of those who went astray (1.7)), then you must say, 'Ameen', for if one's utterance of 'Ameen' coincides with that of the angels, then his past sins will be forgiven."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I asked the Prophet, "What is the greatest sin in the Sight of Allah?" He said, "That you set up a rival unto Allah though He Alone created you." I said, "That is indeed a great sin." Then asked, "What is next?" He said, "To kill your son lest he should share your food with you." I asked, "What is next?" He said, "To commit illegal sexual intercourse with the wife of your neighbor."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The Kam'a (i.e. a kind of edible fungus) is like the Manna (in that it is obtained without effort) and its water is a (medicine) cure for eye trouble."
narrator: Narrated Sa`id bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "It was said to the children of Israel, 'Enter the gate (of the town), prostrate (in humility) and say: Hittatun (i.e. repentance) i.e. O Allah! Forgive our sins.' But they entered by dragging themselves on their buttocks, so they did something different (from what they had been ordered to do) and said, 'Hittatun,' but added, "A grain in a hair."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "Our best Qur'an reciter is Ubai and our best judge is `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ; and in spite of this, we leave some of the statements of Ubai ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ because Ubai ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ says, 'I do not leave anything that I have heard from Allah's Messenger (п·ә) while Allah: "Whatever verse (Revelations) do We abrogate or cause to be forgotten but We bring a better one or similar to it." (2.106)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Allah said, 'The son of Adam tells a lie against me though he has no right to do so, and he abuses Me though he has no right to do so. As for his telling a lie against Me, it is that he claims that I cannot recreate him as I created him before; and as for his abusing Me, it is his statement that I have offspring. No! Glorified be Me! I am far from taking a wife or offspring.' "
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The people of the Scripture (Jews) used to recite the Torah in Hebrew and they used to explain it in Arabic to the Muslims. On that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Do not believe the people of the Scripture or disbelieve them, but say:-- "We believe in Allah and what is revealed to us." (2.136)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: While some people were offering Fajr prayer in the Quba' mosque, some-one came and said, "Allah has revealed to the Prophet (п·ә) Qur'anic instructions that you should face the Ka`ba (while praying) so you too, should face it." Those people then turned towards the Ka`ba.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: None remains of those who prayed facing both Qiblas (that is, Jerusalem and Mecca) except myself.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: While some people were offering morning prayer at Quba' a man came to them and said, "A Qur'anic Order has been revealed to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) tonight that he should face the Ka`ba at Mecca (in prayer), so you too should turn your faces towards it." At that moment their faces were towards Sham (i.e. Jerusalem) (and on hearing that) they turned towards the Ka`ba (at Mecca).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: While some people were offering Fajr prayer at Quba' (mosque), some-one came to them and said, "Tonight some Qur'anic Verses have been revealed to the Prophet (п·ә) and he has been ordered to face the Ka`ba (at Mecca) (during prayers), so you too should turn your faces towards it." At that time their faces were towards Sham (Jerusalem) so they turned towards the Ka`ba (at Mecca).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: We prayed along with the Prophet (п·ә) facing Jerusalem for sixteen or seventeen months. Then Allah ordered him to turn his face towards the Qibla (in Mecca):-- "And from whence-so-ever you start forth (for prayers) turn your face in the direction of (the Sacred Mosque of Mecca) Al-Masjid-ul Haram.." (2.149)
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: While some people were at Quba (offering) morning prayer, a man came to them and said, "Last night Qur'anic Verses have been revealed whereby the Prophet (п·ә) has been ordered to face the Ka`ba (at Mecca), so you too should face it." So they, keeping their postures, turned towards the Ka`ba. Formerly the people were facing Sham (Jerusalem) (Allah said):-- "And from whence-so-ever you start forth (for prayers), turn your face in the direction of the Sacred Mosque of Mecca (Al-Masjid-ul-Haram), and whence-so-ever you are, turn your face towards it (when you pray)" (2.150)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: While some people were offering Fajr prayer at Quba mosque, someone came to them and said, "Qur'anic literature" has been revealed to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) tonight, and he has been ordered to face the Ka`ba (of Mecca) so you too, should turn your faces towards it. Their faces were then towards Sham (Jerusalem), so they turned towards the Qibla (i.e. Ka`ba of Mecca).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: "We used to consider (i.e. going around) them a custom of the Pre-islamic period of Ignorance, so when Islam came, we gave up going around them. Then Allah revealed" "Verily, Safa and Marwa (i.e. two mountains at Mecca) are among the Symbols of Allah. So it is not harmful of those who perform the Hajj of the House (of Allah) or perform the Umra to ambulate (Tawaf) between them." (2.158)
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said one statement and I said another. The Prophet (п·ә) said "Whoever dies while still invoking anything other than Allah as a rival to Allah, will enter Hell (Fire)." And I said, "Whoever dies without invoking anything as a rival to Allah, will enter Paradise."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The prescribed Law of Allah is the equality in punishment (i.e. Al-Qisas)." (In cases of murders, etc.)
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Fasting was observed on the day of 'Ashura' (i.e. 10th of Muharram) by the people of the Pre-lslamic Period. But when (the order of compulsory fasting) in the month of Ramadan was revealed, the Prophet said, "It is up to one to fast on it (i.e. day of 'Ashura') or not."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The people used to fast on the day of 'Ashura' before fasting in Ramadan was prescribed but when (the order of compulsory fasting in) Ramadan was revealed,  he п·ә said: whosoever wishes, it was up to one to fast on it (i.e. 'Ashura') or not.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: That Al-Ash'ath entered upon him while he was eating. Al-Ash'ath said, "Today is 'Ashura." I said (to him), "Fasting had been observed (on such a day) before (the order of compulsory fasting in) Ramadan was revealed. But when (the order of fasting in) Ramadan was revealed, fasting (on 'Ashura') was given up, so come and eat."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: During the Pre-lslamic Period of ignorance the Quraish used to observe fasting on the day of 'Ashura', and the Prophet (п·ә) himself used to observe fasting on it too. But when he came to Medina, he fasted on that day and ordered the Muslims to fast on it. When (the order of compulsory fasting in ) Ramadan was revealed, fasting in Ramadan became an obligation, and fasting on 'Ashura' was given up, and who ever wished to fast (on it) did so, and whoever did not wish to fast on it, did not fast.
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: He reciting the Divine Verse:-- "And for those who can fast they had a choice either fast, or feed a poor for every day.." (2.184) Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "This Verse is not abrogated, but it is meant for old men and old women who have no strength to fast, so they should feed one poor person for each day of fasting (instead of fasting).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: He recited: "They had a choice, either fast or feed a poor for every day.." and added, "This Verse is abrogated."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When the Divine Revelation: "For those who can fast, they had a choice either fast, or feed a poor for every day," (2.184) was revealed, it was permissible for one to give a ransom and give up fasting, till the Verse succeeding it was revealed and abrogated it. Imam Bukhari says: Bokair bin Abdullah died before Yazeed, his teacher.
narrator: Narrated Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When the order of compulsory fasting of Ramadan was revealed, the people did not have sexual relations with their wives for the whole month of Ramadan, but some men cheated themselves (by violating that restriction). So Allah revealed: "Allah is aware that you were deceiving yourselves but He accepted your repentance and forgave you.." (3.187)
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: He took a white rope (or thread) and a black one, and when some part of the night had passed, he looked at them but he could not distinguish one from the other. The next morning he said, "O Allah's Apostle! I put (a white thread and a black thread) underneath my pillow." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Then your pillow is too wide if the white thread (of dawn) and the black thread (of the night) are underneath your pillow! "
narrator: Narrated `Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! What is the meaning of the white thread distinct from the black thread? Are these two threads?" He said, "You are not intelligent if you watch the two threads." He then added, "No, it is the darkness of the night and the whiteness of the day.''
narrator: Narrated `Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Verse "And eat and drink until the white thread appears to you distinct from the black thread." was revealed, but: '... of dawn' was not revealed (along with it) so some men, when intending to fast, used to tie their legs, one with white thread and the other with black thread and would keep on eating till they could distinguish one thread from the other. Then Allah revealed' ... of dawn,' whereupon they understood that meant the night and the day.
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: In the Pre-lslamic Period when the people assumed Ihram, they would enter their houses from the back. So Allah revealed:-- "And it is not righteousness that you enter houses from the back, but the righteous man is he who fears Allah, obeys His Orders and keeps away from what He has forbidden. So enter houses through their doors." (2.189)
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: "The Verse:-- "And spend (of your wealth) in the Cause of Allah and do not throw yourselves in destruction," (2.195) was revealed concerning spending in Allah's Cause (i.e. Jihad).
narrator: Narrated Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I sat with Ka`b bin Ujra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ in this mosque, i.e. Kufa Mosque, and asked him about the meaning of: "Pay a ransom (i.e. Fidya) of either fasting or . . . . (2.196)" He said, "I was taken to the Prophet (п·ә) while lice were falling on my face. The Prophet (п·ә) said, 'I did not think that your trouble reached to such an extent. Can you afford to slaughter a sheep (as a ransom for shaving your head)?' I said, 'No.' He said, 'Then fast for three days, or feed six poor persons by giving half a Sa of food for each and shave your head.' So the above Verse was revealed especially for me and generally for all of you."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Maqal:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Verse of Hajj-at-Tamatu was revealed in Allah's Book, so we performed it with Allah's Messenger (п·ә), and nothing was revealed in Qur'an to make it illegal, nor did the Prophet (п·ә) prohibit it till he died. But the man (who regarded it illegal) just expressed what his own mind suggested.
narrator: Narrated `Imran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: `Ukaz, Mijanna and Dhul-Majaz were markets during the Pre-islamic Period. They (i.e. Muslims) considered it a sin to trade there during the Hajj time (i.e. season), so this Verse was revealed:-- "There is no harm for you if you seek of the Bounty of your Lord during the Hajj season." (2.198)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Quraish people and those who embraced their religion, used to stay at Muzdalifa and used to call themselves Al-Hums, while the rest of the Arabs used to stay at `Arafat. When Islam came, Allah ordered His Prophet to go to `Arafat and stay at it, and then pass on from there, and that is what is meant by the Statement of Allah:--"Then depart from the place whence all the people depart......" (2.199)
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to say, "O Allah! Our Lord! Give us in this world that, which is good and in the Hereafter that, which is good and save us from the torment of the Fire." (2.201)
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The most hated man in the Sight of Allah is the one who is the most quarrelsome." Hadrat Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ has reported the afore-mention Hadrat from the prophet п·ә.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Whenever Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ recited the Qur'an, he would not speak to anyone till he had finished his recitation. Once I held the Qur'an and he recited Surat-al-Baqara from his memory and then stopped at a certain Verse and said, "Do you know in what connection this Verse was revealed?" I replied, "No." He said, "It was revealed in such-and-such connection." Ibn `Umar then resumed his recitation. Nafi` added regarding the Verse:--"So go to your tilth when or how you will" Ibn `Umar said, "It means one should approach his wife in .."
narrator: Narrated Nafi`:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Jews used to say: "If one has sexual intercourse with his wife from the back, then she will deliver a squint-eyed child." So this Verse was revealed:-- "Your wives are a tilth unto you; so go to your tilth when or how you will." (2.223)
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The sister of Ma'qal bin Yasar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was divorced by her husband who left her till she had fulfilled her term of 'Iddat (i.e. the period which should elapse before she can Remarry) and then he wanted to remarry her but Maqal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ refused, so this Verse was revealed:-- "Do not prevent them from marrying their (former) husbands." (2.232)
narrator: Narrated Ma'qal bin Yasar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I said to `Uthman bin `Affan (while he was collecting the Qur'an) regarding the Verse:-- "Those of you who die and leave wives ..." (2.240) "This Verse was abrogated by an other Verse. So why should you write it? (Or leave it in the Qur'an)?" `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said. "O son of my brother! I will not shift anything of it from its place."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: On the day of Al-Khandaq (the battle of the Trench). the Prophet (п·ә) said, "They (i.e. pagans prevented us from offering the middle (the Best) Prayer till the sun had set. May Allah fill their graves, their houses (or their bodies) with fire." Yahya, the reporter, has got a doubt whether he п·ә said: The houses or the bellies.
narrator: Narrated `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ(through two chains):
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: We used to speak while in prayer. One of us used to speak to his brother (while in prayer) about his need, till the Verse was revealed:-- "Guard strictly the (five obligatory) prayers, especially the middle (the Best) (`Asr) Prayer and stand before Allah with obedience (and not to speak to others during the prayers)." Then we were ordered not to speak in the prayers.
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Arqam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I said to `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "This Verse which is in Surat-al-Baqara: "Those of you who die and leave widows behind...without turning them out." has been abrogated by another Verse. Why then do you write it (in the Qur'an)?" `Uthman said. "Leave it (where it is), O the son of my brother, for I will not shift anything of it (i.e. the Qur'an) from its original position." Homaid, the reporter, says: Or he said something like this.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "We have more right to be in doubt than Abraham when he said, 'My Lord! Show me how You give life to the dead.' He said, 'Do you not believe?' He said, 'Yes (I believe) but to be stronger in Faith.' "(2.260)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The poor person is not the one for whom a date or two or a morsel or two (of food is sufficient but the poor person is he who does not (beg or) ask the people (for something) or show his poverty at all. Recite if you wish, (Allah's Statement): "They do not beg of people at all." (2.273)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When the Verses of Surat-al-Baqara regarding usury (i.e. Riba) were revealed, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) recited them before the people and then he prohibited the trade of alcoholic liquors.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When the last Verses of Surat-al-Baqara were revealed. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went out and recited them in the Mosque and prohibited the trade of alcoholic liquors.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When the last Verses of Surat-al-Baqara were revealed, the Prophet (п·ә) read them in the Mosque and prohibited the trade of alcoholic liquors.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When the last Verses of Surat-al-Baqara were revealed, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) stood up and recited them before us and then prohibited the trade of alcoholic liquors.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The last Verse (in the Qur'an) revealed to the Prophet (п·ә) was the Verse dealing with usury (i.e. Riba).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: This Verse:--"Whether you show what is in your minds or conceal it.." (2.284) was abrogated.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: A man from the companions of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) who I think, was Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "The Verse:-- "Whether you show what is in your minds or conceal it...." was abrogated by the Verse following it."
narrator: Narrated Marwan Al-Asghar:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: ''The Prophet (п·ә) said, 'No child is born but that, Satan touches it when it is born whereupon it starts crying loudly because of being touched by Satan, except Mary and her son." Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ then said, "Recite, if you wish: "And I seek Refuge with You (Allah) for her and her offspring from Satan, the outcast." (3.36)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: A man displayed some merchandise in the market and took an oath that he had been offered a certain price for it while in fact he had not, in order to cheat a man from the Muslims. So then was revealed:-- "Verily! Those who purchase a small gain at the cost of Allah's Covenant and their oaths..."(3.77)
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Abu `Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Abu Talha distributed the garden between Hassan and Ubai ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§, but he did not give me anything there of although I was a nearer relative to him.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Verse:--"You (true Muslims) are the best of peoples ever raised up for mankind." means, the best of peoples for the people, as you bring them with chains on their necks till they embrace Islam.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Verse:--"When two parties from among you were about to lose heart, but Allah was their Protector," (3.122) was revealed concerning us, and we were the two parties, i.e. Banu Haritha and Banu Salama, and we do not wish (that it had not been revealed) or I would not have been pleased (if it had not been revealed), for Allah says:--"...Allah was their Protector."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: And he heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) on raising his head from the bowing in the last rak`a in the Fajr prayer, saying, "O Allah, curse such-and-such person and such-and-such person, and such-and-such person," after saying, "Allah hears him who sends his praises to Him, O our Lord, all praise is for you." So Allah revealed:--"Not for you (O Muhammad) (but for Allah) is the decision, verily they are indeed wrongdoers." (3.128) Ishaq bin Rashid has also reported it from Zuhri.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) appointed `Abdullah bin Jubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as the commander of the infantry during the battle of Uhud. They returned defeated, and that is what is meant by:-- "And the Apostle was calling them back in the rear. None remained with the Prophet (п·ә) then, but twelve men."
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara bin Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Slumber overtook us during the battle of Uhud while we were in the front files. My sword would fall from my hand and I would pick it up, and again it would fall down and I would pick it up again.
narrator: Narrated Abu Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah is Sufficient for us and He Is the Best Disposer of affairs," was said by Abraham when he was thrown into the fire; and it was said by Muhammad when they (i.e. hypocrites) said, "A great army is gathering against you, therefore, fear them," but it only increased their faith and they said: "Allah is Sufficient for us, and He is the Best Disposer (of affairs, for us)." (3.173)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The last statement of Abraham when he was thrown into the fire was:--"Allah is Sufficient for us and He is the Best Disposer (of affairs for us)." (3.173)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Anyone whom Allah has given wealth but he does not pay its Zakat, then, on the Day of Resurrection, his wealth will be presented to him in the shape of a bald-headed poisonous male snake with two poisonous glands in its mouth and it will encircle itself round his neck and bite him over his cheeks and say, "I am your wealth; I am your treasure." Then the Prophet (п·ә) recited this Divine Verse:-- "And let not those who covetously withhold of that which Allah has bestowed upon them of His Bounty." (3.180)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: During the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә), some men among the hypocrites used to remain behind him (i.e. did not accompany him) when he went out for a Ghazwa and they would be pleased to stay at home behind Allah's Messenger (п·ә) When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) returned (from the battle) they would put forward (false) excuses and take oaths, wishing to be praised for what they had not done. So there was revealed:-- "Think not that those who rejoice in what they have done, and love to be praised for what they have not done.." (3.188)
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: There was an orphan (girl) under the care of a man. He married her and she owned a date palm (garden). He married her just because of that and not because he loved her. So the Divine Verse came regarding his case: "If you fear that you shall not be able to deal justly with the orphan girls..." (4.3) The sub-narrator added: I think he (i.e. another sub-narrator) said, "That orphan girl was his partner in that datepalm (garden) and in his property."
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: regarding the Statement of Allah: "And whoever amongst the guardian is rich, he should take no wages, but if he is poor, let him have for himself what is just and reasonable (according to his work). This Verse was revealed regarding the orphan's property. If the guardian is poor, he can take from the property of the orphan, what is just and reasonable according to his work and the time he spends on managing it.
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: ( regarding the verse), "And when the relatives and the orphans and the poor are present at the time of division, "this verse and its order is valid and not abrogated." Similarly, Hadrat Saeed (bin Javir) has reported it from Hadrat Ibn-e-Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ .
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) and Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came on foot to pay me a visit (during my illness) at Banu Salama's (dwellings). The Prophet (п·ә) found me unconscious, so he asked for water and performed the ablution from it and sprinkled some water over it. I came to my senses and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! What do you order me to do as regards my wealth?" So there was revealed:-- "Allah commands you as regards your children's (inheritance):" (4.11)
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: (In the Pre-Islamic Period ) the children used to inherit all the property but the parents used to inherit only through a will. So Allah cancelled that which He liked to cancel and put decreed that the share of a son was to be twice the share of a daughter, and for the parents one-sixth for each one of them, or one third, and for the wife one-eighth or one-fourth, and for the husband one-half, or one-fourth.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: regarding the Divine Verse: "O you who believe! You are forbidden to inherit women against their will, and you should not treat them with harshness that you may take back part of the (Mahr) dower you have given them." (4.19) (Before this revelation) if a man died, his relatives used to have the right to inherit his wife, and one of them could marry her if he would, or they would give her in marriage if they wished, or, if they wished, they would not give her in marriage at all, and they would be more entitled to dispose her, than her own relatives. So the above Verse was revealed in this connection.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to me, "Recite (of the Qur'an) for me," I said, "Shall I recite it to you although it had been revealed to you?" He said, "I like to hear (the Qur'an) from others." So I recited Surat-an- Nisa' till I reached: "How (will it be) then when We bring from each nation a witness, and We bring you (O Muhammad) as a witness against these people?" (4.41) Then he said, "Stop!" And behold, his eyes were overflowing with tears."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Masud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The necklace of Asma' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ was lost, so the Prophet (п·ә) sent some men to look for it. The time for the prayer became due and they had not performed ablution and could not find water, so they offered the prayer without ablution. Then Allah revealed (the Verse of Tayammum).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Verse: "Obey Allah and Obey the Apostle and those of you (Muslims) who are in authority." (4.59) was revealed in connection with `Abdullah bin Hudhafa bin Qais bin `Adi' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ when the Prophet (п·ә) appointed him as the commander of a Sariyya (army detachment).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "No prophet gets sick but he is given the choice to select either this world or the Hereafter." `Aisha added: During his fatal illness, his voice became very husky and I heard him saying: "In the company of those whom is the Grace of Allah, of the prophets, the Siddiqin (those followers of the prophets who were first and foremost to believe in them), the martyrs and the pious.' (4.69) And from this I came to know that he has been given the option.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: My mother and I were among the weak and oppressed (Muslims at Mecca).
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ recited:-- "Except the weak ones among men women and children," (4.98) and said, "My mother and I were among those whom Allah had excused." Ibn-e-Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ says ''ШӯШөШұШӘ'' means straitened. ''ШӘЩ„ЩҲЩҲШ§вҖҸ'' means the place where one emigrates. ''ШұШ§ШәЩ…ШӘ''  means I have forsaken my people. ''Щ…ЩҲЩӮЩҲШӘШ§вҖҸ'' means: A fixed time.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abi Mulaika:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Regarding the Verse:-- "Then what is the matter with you that you are divided into two parties about the hypocrites?" (4.88) Some of the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) returned from the battle of Uhud (i.e. refused to fight) whereupon the Muslims got divided into two parties; one of them was in favor of their execution and the other was not in favour of it. So there ware revealed: "Then what is the matter with you that you are divided into two parties about the hypocrites?" (4.88). Then the Prophet (п·ә) said "It (i.e. Medina) is a Tayyaboh (good), it expels impurities as the fire expels the impurities of silver."
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The people of Kufa disagreed (disputed) about the above Verse. So I went to Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ and asked him about it. He said, "This Verse:-- "And whoever kills a believer intentionally, his recompense is Hell." was revealed last of all (concerning premeditated murder) and nothing abrogated it."
narrator: Narrated Sa`id bin Jubair:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Regarding the Verse: "And say not to anyone who offers you peace (by accepting Islam), You are not a believer." There was a man amidst his goats. The Muslims pursued him, and he said (to them) "Peace be on you." But they killed him and took over his goats. Thereupon Allah revealed in that concern, the above Verse up to:-- "...seeking the perishable good of this life." (4.94) i.e. those goats. Hadrat Ibn-e-Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reads ''Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…вҖҸ'' in the verse.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) dictated to him: "Not equal are those of the believers who sit (at home) and those who strive and fight in the Cause of Allah." Zaid added: Ibn Um Maktum ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came while the Prophet (п·ә) was dictating to me and said, "O Allah's Apostle! By Allah, if I had the power to fight (in Allah's Cause), I would," and he was a blind man. So Allah revealed to his Apostle while his thigh was on my thigh, and his thigh became so heavy that I was afraid it might fracture my thigh. Then that state of the Prophet (п·ә) passed and Allah revealed:-- "Except those who are disabled (by injury or are blind or lame etc).
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When the Verse:-- "Not equal are those of the believers who sit (at home)" (4.95) was revealed, Allah Apostle called for Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who wrote it. In the meantime Ibn Um Maktum ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came and complained of his blindness, so Allah revealed: "Except those who are disabled (by injury or are blind or lame..." etc.) (4.95)
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Not equal are those believers who sat (at home) and did not join the Badr battle and those who joined the Badr battle.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: "Except the weak ones" (4.98) and added: My mother was one of those whom Allah excused.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: While the Prophet (п·ә) was offering the `Isha' prayer, he said, "Allah hears him who sends his praises to Him," and then said before falling in prostration, "O Allah, save `Aiyash bin Rabi`a. O Allah, save Salama bin Hisham. O Allah, save Al-Walid bin Al-Wahd. O Allah, save the weak ones among the believers. O Allah, let Your punishment be severe on the tribe of Mudar. O Allah, inflict upon them years (of famine) like the years of Joseph."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Regarding the Verse: "Because of the inconvenience of rain or because you are ill." (4.102) (It was revealed in connection with) `Abdur-Rahman bin `Auf ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who was wounded.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Regarding the Verse:--"If a woman fears cruelty or desertion on her husband's part." (4.128) It is about a man who has a woman (wife) and he does not like her and wants to divorce her but she says to him, "I make you free as regards myself." So this Verse was revealed in this connection.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "None has the right to say that I am better than Jonah bin Matta."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever says that I am better than Jonah bin Matta, is a liar."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The last Sura that was revealed was Bara'a, and the last Verse that was revealed was: "They ask you for a legal verdict, Say: Allah's directs (thus) about those who leave no descendants or ascendants as heirs." (4.176)
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Jews said to `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "You (i.e. Muslims) recite a Verse, and had it been revealed to us, we would have taken the day of its revelation as a day of celebration." `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I know very well when and where it was revealed, and where Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was when it was revealed. (It was revealed on) the day of `Arafat (Hajj Day), and by Allah, I was at `Arafat" Sufyan, a sub-narrator said: I am in doubt whether the Verse:-- "This day I have perfected your religion for you." was revealed on a Friday or not.
narrator: Narrated Tariq bin Shihab:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: On the day of Badr, Al-Miqdad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! We do not say to you as the children of Israel said to Moses, 'Go you and your Lord and fight you two; we are sitting here, (5.24) but (we say). "Proceed, and we are with you." That seemed to delight Allah's Messenger (п·ә) greatly. Wakee, Sufyan, Mukhariq reported it from Tariq bin Shihab that Hadrat Miqdad  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said the same to the Prophet п·ә .
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah (bin Masud) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Whoever tells that Muhammad concealed part of what was revealed to him, is a liar, for Allah says:-- "O Apostle (Muhammad)! Proclaim (the Message) which has been sent down to you from your Lord." (5.67)
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: This Verse: "Allah will not punish you for what is unintentional in your oaths." (5.89) was revealed about a man's state men (during his talk), "No, by Allah," and "Yes, by Allah."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Her father (Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) never broke his oath till Allah revealed the order of the legal expiation for oath. Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "If I ever take an oath (to do something) and later find that to do something else is better, then I accept Allah's permission and do that which is better, (and do the legal expiation for my oath ) ".
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: We used to participate in the holy wars carried on by the Prophet (п·ә) and we had no women (wives) with us. So we said (to the Prophet (п·ә) ). "Shall we castrate ourselves?" But the Prophet (п·ә) forbade us to do that and thenceforth he allowed us to marry a woman (temporarily) by giving her even a garment, and then he recited: "O you who believe! Do not make unlawful the good things which Allah has made lawful for you."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: (The Verse of) prohibiting alcoholic drinks was revealed when there were in Medina five kinds of (alcoholic) drinks none of which was produced from grapes.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: We had no alcoholic drink except that which was produced from dates and which you call Fadikh. While I was standing offering drinks to Abu Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and so-and-so and so-and-so, a man cam and said, "Has the news reached you? They said, "What is that?" He said. "Alcoholic drinks have been prohibited. They said, "Spill (the contents of these pots, O Anas! "Then they neither asked about it (alcoholic drinks) nor returned it after the news from that man.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))
