text: Some people drank alcoholic beverages in the morning (of the day) of the Uhud battle and on the same day they were killed as martyrs, and that was before wine was prohibited.
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I heard `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ while he was on the pulpit of the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "Now then O people! The revelation about the prohibition of alcoholic drinks was revealed; and alcoholic drinks are extracted from five things: Grapes, dates, honey, wheat and barley. And the alcoholic drink is that which confuses and stupefies the mind."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) delivered a sermon the like of which I had never heard before. He said, "If you but knew what I know then you would have laughed little and wept much." On hearing that, the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) covered their faces and the sound of their weeping was heard. A man said, "Who is my father?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "So-and-so." So this Verse was revealed: "Ask not about things which, if made plain to you, may cause you trouble." (5.101) Nadr and Rauh bin Obadah have also reported it from Shobah.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Some people were asking Allah's Messenger (п·ә) questions mockingly. A man would say, "Who is my father?" Another man whose she-camel had gone astray would say, "Where is my she-camel? "So Allah revealed this Verse in this connection: "O you who believe! Ask not about things which, if made plain to you, may cause you trouble." (5.101)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "I saw Hell and its different portions were consuming each other and saw `Amr dragging his intestines (in it), and he was the first person to establish the tradition of letting animals loose (for the idols).
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "You will be gathered (on the Day of Resurrection) and some people will be driven (by the angels) to the left side (and taken to Hell) whereupon I will say as the pious slave (Jesus) said:- - "And I was a witness over them while I dwelt amongst them...the ALMIGHTY, the All Wise." (5.117-118)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The key of the Unseen are five: Verily with Allah (Alone) is the knowledge of the Hour He sends down the rain and knows what is in the wombs. No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die. Verily, Allah is All-Knower, All-Aware." (31.34)
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When this Verse was revealed: "Say: He has power to send torment on you from above." (6.65) Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "O Allah! I seek refuge with Your Face (from this punishment)." And when the verse: "or send torment from below your feet," (was revealed), Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "(O Allah!) I seek refuge with Your Face (from this punishment)." (But when there was revealed): "Or confuse you in party strife and make you to taste the violence of one another." (6.65) Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "This is lighter (or, this is easier).
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When:"...and confuse not their belief with wrong." (6.82) was revealed, the Prophet's companions said, "Which of us has not done wrong?" Then there was revealed:-- "Verily joining others in worship with Allah is a tremendous wrong indeed." (31.13)
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Nobody has the rights to say that I am better than Jonah bin Matta."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Nobody has the right to say that I am better than Jonah bin Matta."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: He asked Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , "Is there a prostration Surat-al-Sa`d?" (38.24) Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "Yes," and then recited: "We gave...So follow their guidance." (6.85,90) Then he said, "He (David ) is one them (i.e. those prophets)." Mujahid narrated: I asked Ibn `Abbas  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ (regarding the above Verse). He said, "Your Prophet (Muhammad) was one of those who were ordered to follow them."
narrator: Narrated Mujahid:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "May Allah curse the Jews! When Allah forbade them to eat the fat of animals, they melted it and sold it, and utilized its price! " Moreover, Abu Asim, Abdul Hameed, Yazeed, Ataa bin Rabih, Hadrat Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported it from the prophet п·ә.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: "None has more sense of ghaira than Allah therefore - He prohibits shameful sins (illegal sexual intercourse, etc.) whether committed openly or secretly. And none loves to be praised more than Allah does, and for this reason He praises Himself." I asked Abu Wali, "Did you hear it from `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ?" He said, "Yes," I said, "Did `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ascribe it to Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" He said, "Yes."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The Hour will not be established until the sun rises from the West: and when the people see it, then whoever will be living on the surface of the earth will have faith, and that is (the time) when no good will it do to a soul to believe then, if it believed not before." (6.158)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The hour will not be established till the sun rises from the West; and when it rises (from the West) and the people see it, they all will believe. And that is (the time) when no good will it do to a soul to believe then." Then he recited the whole Verse (6.158)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "None has more sense of ghaira than Allah, and for this He has forbidden shameful sins whether committed openly or secretly, and none loves to be praised more than Allah does, and this is why He Praises Himself."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Al-Kam'a is like the Mann (sweet resin or gum) (in that it grows naturally without human care) and its water is a cure for the eye diseases."
narrator: Narrated Sa`id Ibn Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "It was said to the children of Israel, 'Enter the gate in prostration and say Hitatun. (7.161) We shall forgive you, your faults.' But they changed (Allah's Order) and entered, dragging themselves on their buttocks and said, 'Habatun (a grain) in a Sha'ratin (hair).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: (The Verse) "Hold to forgiveness; command what is right..." was revealed by Allah except in connection with the character of the people.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Az Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah ordered His Prophet to forgive the people their misbehavior (towards him).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Az-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I asked Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ regarding Surat-al-Anfal. He said, "It was revealed in connection with the Battle of Badr. "Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҲЩғШ©'' means: edge. ''Щ…ШұШҜЩҒЩҠЩҶвҖҸ'' means: flocks after flocks. ''ШұШҜЩҒЩҶЩҠ'' means: come after me. ''Ш°ЩҲЩӮЩҲШ§вҖҸ'' means taste the torment, experience it. It is not a taste of mouth. ''ЩҒЩҠШұЩғЩ…ЩҮвҖҸ'': gather them. ''ШҙШұШҜвҖҸ'' : To put apart. ''Ш¬ЩҶШӯЩҲШ§вҖҸ'' means: demanded. ''ЩҠЩҸШ«Щ’Ш®ЩҗЩҶЩҺвҖҸ'' means: to hold supremacy. Mujahid said: ''Щ…ЩғШ§ШЎвҖҸ'' means: To produce a sound by inserting fingers into the mouth. ''ШӘШөШҜЩҠШ©вҖҸ'' means whistle. ''ЩҠШ«ШЁШӘЩҲЩғвҖҸ'' means: so as to confine you.
narrator: Narrated Sa`id bin Jubair:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Regarding the Verse: "Verily! The worst of beasts in the Sight of Allah are the deaf and the dumb---- those who understand not." (8.22) (The people referred to here) were some persons from the tribe of Bani `Abd-Addar.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: While I was praying, Allah's Messenger (п·ә) passed me and called me, but I did not go to him until I had finished the prayer. Then I went to him, and he said, "What prevented you from coming to me? Didn't Allah say:-- "O you who believe! Answer the call of Allah (by obeying Him) and His Apostle when He calls you?" He then said, "I will inform you of the greatest Sura in the Qur'an before I leave (the mosque)." When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) got ready to leave (the mosque), I reminded him. He said, "It is: 'Praise be to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.' (i.e. Surat-al-Fatiha) As-sab'a Al-Mathani (the seven repeatedly recited Verses).
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id bin Al-Mu'alla ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Abu Jahl said, "O Allah! If this (Qur'an) is indeed the Truth from You, then rain down on us a shower of stones from the sky or bring on us a painful torment." So Allah revealed:-- "But Allah would not punish them while you were amongst them, nor He will punish them while they seek (Allah's) forgiveness..." (8.33) And why Allah should not punish them while they turn away (men) from Al- Masjid-al-Haram (the Sacred Mosque of Mecca)..." (8.33-34)
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Abu Jahl said, "O Allah! If this (Qur'an) is indeed the Truth from You), then rain down on us a shower of stones from the sky or bring on us a painful punishment." So there was revealed:-- 'But Allah would not punish them while you (Muhammad) were amongst them, nor will He punish them while they seek (Allah's) Forgiveness. And why Allah should not punish them while they stop (men) from Al-Masjid-al-Haram ..' (8.33-34)
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: "What do you think about 'Qit-alal-Fitnah' (fighting caused by afflictions)." Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said (to him), "And do you understand what an affliction is? Muhammad used to fight against the pagans, and his fighting with them was an affliction, (and his fighting was) not like your fighting which is carried on for the sake of ruling."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The last Verse that was revealed was: 'They ask you for a legal verdict: Say: Allah directs (thus) about Al-Kalalah (those who leave no descendants or ascendants as heirs).' And the last Sura which was revealed was Baraatun (9) .
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ sent him during the Hajj in which Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was made the chief of the pilgrims by Allah's Messenger (п·ә) before (the year of) Hajjat al-Wada` in a group (of announcers) to announce before the people; 'No pagan shall perform the Hajj after this year, and none shall perform the Tawaf around the Ka`ba in a naked state. Humaid used to say The Day of Nahr is the day of Al- Hajj Al-Akbar (the Greatest Day) because of the narration of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: We were with Hudhaifa and he said, "None remains of the people described by this Verse (9.12), "Except three, and of the hypocrites except four." A bedouin said, "You the companions of Muhammad! Tell us (things) and we do not know that about those who break open our houses and steal our precious things? ' He (Hudhaifa) replied, "Those are Al Fussaq (rebellious wrongdoers) (not disbelievers or hypocrites). Really, none remains of them (hypocrite) but four, one of whom is a very old man who, if he drinks water, does not feel its coldness."
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Wahb:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The Kanz (money, the Zakat of which is not paid) of anyone of you will appear in the form of bald-headed poisonous male snake on the Day of Resurrection."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: "What has brought you to this land?" He said, "We were at Sham and I recited the Verse: "They who hoard up gold and silver and spend them not in the way of Allah; announce to them a painful torment, " (9.34) where upon Muawiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, 'This Verse is not for us, but for the people of the Scripture.' Then I said, 'But it is both for us (Muslim) and for them.' "
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar Gaffari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: We went out with 'AbdullГЈh bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ and he said, "This (Verse) was revealed before the prescription of Zakat, and when ZakГЈt was prescribed, Allah made it a means of purifying one's wealth."
narrator: Narrated KhГЈlid bin Aslam:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Time has come back to its original state which it had when Allah created the Heavens and the Earth; the year is twelve months, four of which are sacred. Three of them are in succession; Dhul-Qa'da, Dhul-Hijja and Al-Muharram, and (the fourth being) Rajab Mudar (named after the tribe of Mudar as they used to respect this month) which stands between Jumad (ath-thani) and Sha'ban."
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I was in the company of the Prophet (п·ә) in the cave, and on seeing the traces of the pagans, I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) If one of them (pagans) should lift up his foot, he will see us." He said, "What do you think of two, the third of whom is Allah?"
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When there occurred a tiff between him and Abdullah bin Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ (in cinnected with Caliphat), I said: His father is Hadrat Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , his mother is Hadrat Asma ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , his maternal aunt is Hadrat Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , his maternal grandfather is Hadrat Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and his maternal grandmother is Hadrat Safiyyah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ . I asked Sufyan to narrate its authority. He said: Narrated me this Hadith; then a man engaged him in conversation and he did not say that Ibn-e-Juraij narrated it.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: Something was sent to the Prophet (п·ә) and he distributed it amongst four (men) and said, "I want to attract their hearts,(to Islam thereby)," A man said (to the Prophet (п·ә) ), "You have not done justice." Thereupon the Prophet (п·ә) said, "There will emerge from the offspring of this (man) some people who will renounce the religion."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: When we were ordered to give alms, we began to work as porters (to earn something we could give in charity). Abu `Aqil came with one half of a Sa` (special measure for food grains) and another person brought more than he did. So the hypocrites said, "Allah is not in need of the alms of this (i.e. Abu `Aqil); and this other person did not give alms but for showing off." Then Allah revealed:-- 'Those who criticize such of the Believers who give charity voluntarily and those who could not find to give in charity except what is available to them.' (9.79)
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: "Allah's Messenger (п·ә), used to order us to give alms. So one of us would exert himself to earn one Mud (special measure of wheat or dates, etc.,) to give in charity; while today one of us may have one hundred thousand." Shaqiq said: As if Abu Masud referred to himself.
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud Al-Ansari:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: I heard Ka`b bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ at the time he remained behind and did not join (the battle of) Tabuk, saying, "By Allah, no blessing has Allah bestowed upon me, besides my guidance to Islam, better than that of helping me speak the truth to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) otherwise I would have told the Prophet (п·ә) a lie and would have been ruined like those who had told a lie when the Divine Inspiration was revealed:-- "They will swear by Allah to you (Muslims) when you return to them.. the rebellious people." (9.95-96)
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Ka`b:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: He out of Hadrat Ka'b's sons would lead his father when he turned blind, that he heard Ka`b bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ talking about the Verse:-- 'And to the three (He also forgave) who remained behind.' (9.118) saying in the last portion of his talk, "(I said), 'As a part (sign) of my repentance, I would like to give up all my property in the cause of Allah and His Apostle,' The Prophet (п·ә) said to me, 'Keep some of your wealth as it is good for you." (To the three (He also forgave) who remained behind till for them the earth, vast as it is, was straitened..." (9.118)
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Ka`b:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: He used to accompany Ka'b bin Malik. I heard Ka`b bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ talking about the story of the battle of Tabuk when he remained behind, "By Allah, I do not know anyone whom Allah has helped for telling the truth more than me since I mentioned that truth to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) till today, I have never intended to tell a lie. And Allah revealed to His Apostle: "Verily! Allah has forgiven the Prophet, the Muhajirin............ and be with those who are true (in words and deeds)." (9.117-119) (See Hadith No. 702 Vol 5).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Ka`b:
chapterName: Prophetic Commentary on the Qur'an (Tafseer of the Prophet (PBUH))

text: The Prophet (п·ә) remained in Mecca for ten years, during which the Qur'an used to be revealed to him; and he stayed in Medina for ten years.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha and Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… :

text: I was informed that Gabriel came to the Prophet (п·ә) while Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ was with him. Gabriel started talking (to the Prophet). Then the Prophet (п·ә) asked Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , "Who is this?" She replied, "He is Dihya (al-Kalbi)." When Gabriel had left, Um Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said, "By Allah, I did not take him for anybody other than him (i.e. Dihya) till I heard the sermon of the Prophet (п·ә) wherein he informed about the news of Gabriel." The subnarrator asked Abu `Uthman: From whom have you heard that? Abu `Uthman said: From Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§.
narrator: Narrated Abu `Uthman:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Every Prophet was given miracles because of which people believed, but what I have been given, is Divine Inspiration which Allah has revealed to me. So I hope that my followers will outnumber the followers of the other Prophets on the Day of Resurrection."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah sent down His Divine Inspiration to His Apostle continuously and abundantly during the period preceding his death till He took him unto Him. That was the period of the greatest part of revelation; and Allah's Messenger (п·ә) died after that.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) fell ill and did not offer the night prayer (Tahajjud prayer) for a night or two. A woman (the wife of Abu Lahab) came to him and said, "O Muhammad ! I do not see but that your Satan has left you." Then Allah revealed (Surat-Ad-Duha): 'By the fore-noon, and by the night when it darkens (or is still); Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor hated you.' (93)
narrator: Narrated Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: (The Caliph `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ordered Zaid bin Thabit, Sa`id bin Al-As, `Abdullah bin Az-Zubair and `Abdur- Rahman bin Al-Harith bin Hisham ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… to write the Qur'an in the form of a book (Mushafs) and said to them. "In case you disagree with Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (Al-Ansari) regarding any dialectic Arabic utterance of the Qur'an, then write it in the dialect of Quraish, for the Qur'an was revealed in this dialect." So they did it.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "A verse from Surat Ahzab was missed by me when we copied the Qur'an and I used to hear Allah's Messenger (п·ә) reciting it. So we searched for it and found it with Khuza`ima bin Thabit Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . (That Verse was): Among the Believers are men who have been true in their covenant with Allah.' (33.23)
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit added:

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ sent for me and said, "You used to write the Divine Revelations for Allah's Messenger (п·ә) : So you should search for (the Qur'an and collect) it." I started searching for the Qur'an till I found the last two Verses of Surat at-Tauba with Abi Khuza`ima Al-Ansari and I could not find these Verses with anybody other than him. (They were): 'Verily there has come unto you an Apostle (Muhammad) from amongst yourselves. It grieves him that you should receive any injury or difficulty ...' (9.128-129)
narrator: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Gabriel recited the Qur'an to me in one way. Then I requested him (to read it in another way), and continued asking him to recite it in other ways, and he recited it in several ways till he ultimately recited it in seven different ways."
narrator: Narrated Ibn-e-Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Surat Bani-lsrael, Al-Kahf (The Cave), Maryam, Taha, Al-Anbiya' (The prophets) are amongst my first earnings and my old property, and (in fact) they are my old property.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I learnt, 'Glorify the Name of your Lord the Most High' (Surat al-A'la) No 87, before the Prophet (п·ә) came (to Medina) .
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
"I learnt An-Naza'ir which the Prophet (п·ә) used to recite in pairs in each rak`a." Then `Abdullah got up and Alqama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ accompanied him to his house, and when Alqama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came out, we asked him (about those Suras). He said, "They are twenty Suras that start from the beginning of Al- Mufassal, according to the arrangement done be Ibn Mas`ud, sequence, first twenty chapters are long (Mufassal) and the others are Hawameem, ШӯЩ… ШҜШ®Ш§ЩҶ and
Ш№Щ… ЫҢШӘШіШ§ШЎ Щ„ЩҲЩҶ .
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was the most generous person, and he used to become more so (generous) particularly in the month of Ramadan because Gabriel used to meet him every night of the month of Ramadan till it elapsed. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to recite the Qur'an for him. When Gabriel met him, he used to become more generous than the fast wind in doing good.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Gabriel used to repeat the recitation of the Qur'an with the Prophet (п·ә) once a year, but he repeated it twice with him in the year he died. The Prophet (п·ә) used to stay in I`tikaf for ten days every year (in the month of Ramadan), but in the year of his death, he stayed in I`tikaf for twenty days.
narrator: Narrated Abu-Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: `Abdullah bin `Amr mentioned `Abdullah bin Masud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ and said, "I shall ever love that man, for I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, 'Take (learn) the Qur'an from four: `Abdullah bin Masud, Salim, Mu`adh and Ubai bin Ka`b.' "
narrator: Narrated Masruq:

text: Once `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ delivered a sermon before us and said, "By Allah, I learnt over seventy Suras direct from Allah's Messenger (п·ә) . By Allah, the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) came to know that I am one of those who know Allah's Book best of all of them, yet I am not the best of them." Shaqiq added: I sat in his religious gathering and I did not hear anybody opposing him (in his speech).
narrator: Narrated Shaqiq bin Salama:

text: While we were in the city of Hims (in Syria), Ibn Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ recited Surat Yusuf. A man said to him), "It was not revealed in this way." Then Ibn Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "I recited it in this way before Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and he confirmed my recitation by saying, 'Well done!' " Ibn Mas`ud detected the smell of wine from the man's mouth, so he said to him, "Aren't you ashamed of telling a lie about Allah's Book and (along with this) you drink alcoholic liquors too?" Then he lashed him according to the law.
narrator: Narrated 'Alqama:

text: By Allah other than Whom none has the right to be worshipped! There is no Sura revealed in Allah's Book but I know at what place it was revealed; and there is no Verse revealed in Allah's Book but I know about whom it was revealed. And if I know that there is somebody who knows Allah's Book better than I, and he is at a place that camels can reach, I would go to him.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah (bin Mas`ud) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I asked Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : "Who collected the Qur'an at the time of the Prophet (п·ә) ?" He replied, "Four, all of whom were from the Ansar: Ubai bin Ka`b, Mu`adh bin Jabal, Zaid bin Thabit and Abu Zaid." Fadl, Husain bin Waqid, Thumah reported likewise from Hdrat Anas  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Qatada:

text: When the Prophet (п·ә) died, none had collected the Qur'an but four persons;: Abu Ad-Darda'. Mu`adh bin Jabal, Zaid bin Thabit and Abu Zaid. We were the inheritor (of Abu Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… ) as he had no offspring .
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik:

text: `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, Ubai was the best of us in the recitation (of the Qur'an) yet we leave some of what he recites.' Ubai says, 'PI have taken it from the mouth of Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and will not leave for anything whatever." But Allah said "None of Our Revelations do We abrogate or cause to be forgotten but We substitute something better or similar." 2.106
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: While I was praying, the Prophet (п·ә) called me but I did not respond to his call. Later I said, "O Allah's Apostle! I was praying." He said, "Didn't Allah say: 'O you who believe! Give your response to Allah (by obeying Him) and to His Apostle when he calls you'?" (8.24) He then said, "Shall I not teach you the most superior Surah in the Qur'an?" He said, '(It is), 'Praise be to Allah, the Lord of the worlds. ' (i.e., Surat Al-Fatiha) which consists of seven repeatedly recited Verses and the Magnificent Qur'an which was given to me."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Mu'alla ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The prophet п·ә said, Whoever recites two verses ... (text as in the following hadith)
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If somebody recited the last two Verses of Surat Al-Baqara at night, that will be sufficient for him."
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man was reciting Surat Al-Kahf and his horse was tied with two ropes beside him. A cloud came down and spread over that man, and it kept on coming closer and closer to him till his horse started jumping (as if afraid of something). When it was morning, the man came to the Prophet, and told him of that experience. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "That was As-Sakina (tranquility) which descended because of (the recitation of) the Qur'an."
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man heard another man reciting (Surat-Al-Ikhlas) 'Say He is Allah, (the) One.' (112. 1) repeatedly. The next morning he came to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and informed him about it as if he thought that it was not enough to recite. On that Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "By Him in Whose Hand my life is, this Surah is equal to one-third of the Qur'an!"
narrator: Narrated Abu Said Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: My brother, Qatada bin An-Nau'man ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "A man performed the night prayer late at night in the lifetime of the Prophet (п·ә) and he read: 'Say: He is Allah, (the) One,' (112.1) and read nothing besides that. The next morning a man went to the Prophet (п·ә) ,~ and told him about that . (The Prophet (п·ә) replied the same as (in Hadith 532) above.)
narrator: Narrated Abu Said Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to his companions, "Is it difficult for any of you to recite one third of the Qur'an in one night?" This suggestion was difficult for them so they said, "Who among us has the power to do so, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" Allah Apostle replied: " Allah (the) One, the Self-Sufficient Master Whom all creatures need.' (Surat Al-Ikhlas 112.1--to the End) is equal to one third of the Qur'an." Imam Abu Abdullah (Imam Bukhari) has recorded it incompletely transmitted from Ibrahim and transmitted with complete chain from Dahhak Mashriqi.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Whenever Allah's Messenger (п·ә) became sick, he would recite Mu'awwidhat (Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An- Nas) and then blow his breath over his body. When he became seriously ill, I used to recite (these two Suras) and rub his hands over his body hoping for its blessings.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Whenever the Prophet (п·ә) went to bed every night, he used to cup his hands together and blow over it after reciting Surat Al-Ikhlas, Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An-Nas, and then rub his hands over whatever parts of his body he was able to rub, starting with his head, face and front of his body. He used to do that three times.
narrator: Narrated 'Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Shaddad bin Ma'qil and I entered upon Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ . Shaddad bin Ma'qil asked him, "Did the Prophet (п·ә) leave anything (besides the Qur'an)?" He replied. "He did not leave anything except what is Between the two bindings (of the Qur'an)." Then we visited Muhammad bin Al-Hanafiyya and asked him (the same question). He replied, "The Prophet (п·ә) did not leave except what is between the bindings (of the Qur'an).
narrator: Narrated `Abdul `Aziz bin Rufai':

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The example of him (a believer) who recites the Qur'an is like that of a citron which tastes good and smells good. And he (a believer) who does not recite the Qur'an is like a date which is good in taste but has no smell. And the example of a dissolute wicked person who recites the Qur'an is like the Raihana (sweet basil) which smells good but tastes bitter. And the example of a dissolute wicked person who does not recite the Qur'an is like the colocynth which tastes bitter and has no smell.
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I asked `Abdullah bin Abi `Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , "Did the Prophet (п·ә) make a will (to appoint his successor or bequeath wealth)?" He replied, "No." I said, "How is it prescribed then for the people to make wills, and they are ordered to do so while the Prophet (п·ә) did not make any will?" He said, "He made a will wherein he recommended Allah's Book."
narrator: Narrated Talha:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Allah does not listen to a prophet as He listens to a prophet who recites the Qur'an in a pleasant tone." The companion of the sub-narrator (Abu Salama) said, "It means, reciting it aloud."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) I said, "Allah does not listen to a prophet as He listens to a prophet who recites the Qur'an in a loud and pleasant tone." Sufyan said, "This saying means: a prophet who regards the Qur'an as something that makes him dispense with many worldly pleasures."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Not to wish to be the like except of two men. A man whom Allah has given the knowledge of the Book and he recites it during the hours of the night, and a man whom Allah has given wealth, and he spends it in charity during the night and the hours of the day."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) I said, "Not to wish to be the like of except two men: A man whom Allah has taught the Qur'an and he recites it during the hours of the night and during the hours of the day, and his neighbor listens to him and says, 'I wish I had been given what has been given to so-and-so, so that I might do what he does; and a man whom Allah has given wealth and he spends it on what is just and right, whereupon an other man May say, 'I wish I had been given what so-and-so has been given, for then I would do what he does."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The best among you (Muslims) are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it." Sa'd bin Obaidah narrates; Hadrat Abu Abdur Rehman Sulami taught the Quran from the reign of Hadrat Uthman to the time Hajjaj held the office of governor. He (Abu Abdur Rehman) said: This is the Hadith which has seated me here (for teaching the Quran).
narrator: Narrated `Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The most superior among you (Muslims) are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it."
narrator: Narrated `Uthman bin `Affan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A lady came to the Prophet (п·ә) and declared that she had decided to offer herself to Allah and His Apostle. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I am not in need of women." A man said (to the Prophet) "Please marry her to me." The Prophet (п·ә) said (to him), "Give her a garment." The man said, "I cannot afford it." The Prophet said, "Give her anything, even if it were an iron ring." The man apologized again. The Prophet then asked him, "What do you know by heart of the Qur'an?" He replied, "I know such-andsuch portion of the Qur'an (by heart)." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Then I marry her to you for that much of the Qur'an which you know by heart."
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The example of the person who knows the Qur'an by heart is like the owner of tied camels. If he keeps them tied, he will control them, but if he releases them, they will run away."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "It is a bad thing that some of you say, 'I have forgotten such-and-such verse of the Qur'an,' for indeed, he has been caused (by Allah) to forget it. So you must keep on reciting the Qur'an because it escapes from the hearts of men faster than camel do." Hadrat Uthman from Jareer and he from Mansoor has reported like the Hadith mention above. Moreover, Bishr from Ibn-e-Mubarak and he from Shobah has corroborated him. Moreover, Ibn-e-Juraij, Abdah, Shqeeq heard heard from Hadrat Abdullah bin Masoud and he heard from the prophet п·ә.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Keep on reciting the Qur'an, for, by Him in Whose Hand my life is, Qur'an runs away (is forgotten) faster than camels that are released from their tying ropes."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) reciting Surat-al-Fath on his she-camel on the day of the Conquest of Mecca.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mughaffal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Those Suras which you people call the Mufassal, are the Muhkam. And Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "Allah's Apostle died when I was a boy of ten years, and I had learnt the Muhkam (of the Qur'an).
narrator: Narrated Sa`id bin Jubair:

text: "I have learnt all the Muhkam Suras during the life time of Allah's Messenger (п·ә)." I said to him, 'What is meant by the Muhkam?" He replied, "The Mufassal."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) heard a man reciting the Qur'an in the mosque and said, "May Allah bestow His Mercy on him, as he has reminded me of such-and-such Verses of such a Surah." Hadrat Hisham says: From that chapter which was obliterated from the memory. Ali bin Mus'hir, Abdah has corroborated him from Hisham.
narrator: Narrated 'Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) heard a man reciting the Qur'an at night, and said, "May Allah bestow His Mercy on him, as he has reminded me of such-and-such Verses of such-and-such Suras, which I was caused to forget."
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Why does anyone of the people say, 'I have forgotten such-and-such Verses (of the Qur'an)?' He, in fact, is caused (by Allah) to forget."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If one recites the last two Verses of Surat-al-Baqara at night, it is sufficient for him (for that night).
narrator: Narrated Abu Mas`ud Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) heard a reciter reciting, the Qur'an in the mosque at night. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "May Allah bestow His Mercy on him, as he has remind ed me of such-and-such Verses of such and-such Suras, which I missed!"
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: We went to `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ in the morning and a man said, "Yesterday I recited all the Mufassal Suras." On that `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "That is very quick, and we have the (Prophet's) recitation, and I remember very well the recitation of those Suras which the Prophet (п·ә) used to recite, and they were eighteen Suras from the Mufassal, and two Suras from the Suras that start with Ha Mim.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I asked Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ about the recitation of the Prophet. He said, "He used to pray long (certain sounds) very much.
narrator: Narrated Qatada:

text: "How was the recitation (of the Qur'an) of the Prophet?' He replied, "It was characterized by the prolongation of certain sounds." He then recited: In the Name of Allah, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful prolonging the pronunciation of 'In the Name of Allah, 'the most Beneficent,' and 'the Most Merciful.
narrator: Narrated Anas  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I saw the Prophet (п·ә) reciting (Qur'an) while he was riding on his she camel or camel which was moving, carrying him. He was reciting Surat Fath or part of Surat Fath very softly and in an Attractive vibrating tone.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mughaffal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to him' "O Abu Musa! You have been given one of the mazamir (sweet melodious voices) of the family of David.'
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to him, "Recite the Qur'an to me." `Abdullah said, "Shall I recite (the Qur'an) to you while it has been revealed to you?" He said, "I like to hear it from others."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to me, "Recite (the Qur'an) to me." I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә) Shall I recite (the Qur'an) to you while it has been revealed to you?" He said, "Yes." So I recited Surat-An-Nisa' (The Women), but when I recited the Verse: 'How (will it be) then when We bring from each nation a witness and We bring you (O Muhammad) as a witness against these people.' (4.41) He said, "Enough for the present," I looked at him and behold! His eyes were overflowing with tears
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "I wanted to see how much of the Qur'an can be enough (to recite in prayer) and I could not find a Surah containing less than three Verses, therefore I said to myself), "One ought not to recite less than three (Quranic) Verses (in prayer)." Narrated Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If somebody recites the last two Verses of Surat al-Baqara at night, it will be sufficient for him.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Shubruma :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) asked me, "How long does it take you to finish the recitation of the whole Qur'an?"
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to me, "Recite the whole Qur'an in one month's time." I said, "But I have power (to do more than that)." Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Then finish the recitation of the Qur'an in seven days, and do not finish it in less than this period."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said (to me), "Recite the Qur'an to me." I said, "Shall I recite (it) to you while it has been revealed to you?" He said, "I like to hear it from another person." So I recited Surat An-Nisa (The Women) till I reached the Verse: 'How (will it be) then when We bring from each nation a witness, and We bring you (O Muhammad) as a witness against these people.' (4.41) Then he said to me, "Stop!" Thereupon I saw his eyes overflowing with tears.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah (bin Mas`ud) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said to me, "Recite Qur'an to me." I said to him. "Shall I recite (it) to you while it has been revealed to you?" He said, "I like to hear it from another person."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Masud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "In the last days (of the world) there will appear young people with foolish thoughts and ideas. They will give good talks, but they will go out of Islam as an arrow goes out of its game, their faith will not exceed their throats. So, wherever you find them, kill them, for there will be a reward for their killers on the Day of Resurrection."
narrator: Narrated `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "There will appear some people among you whose prayer will make you look down upon yours, and whose fasting will make you look down upon yours, but they will recite the Qur'an which will not exceed their throats (they will not act on it) and they will go out of Islam as an arrow goes out through the game whereupon the archer would examine the arrowhead but see nothing, and look at the unfeathered arrow but see nothing, and look at the arrow feathers but see nothing, and finally he suspects to find something in the lower part of the arrow."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The example of a believer who recites the Qur'an and acts on it, like a citron which tastes nice and smells nice. And the example of a believer who does not recite the Qur'an but acts on it, is like a date which tastes good but has no smell. And the example of a hypocrite who recites the Qur'an is like a Raihana (sweet basil) which smells good but tastes bitter And the example of a hypocrite who does not recite the Qur'an is like a colocynth which tastes bitter and has a bad smell."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Recite (and study) the Qur'an as long as you agree about its interpretation, but if you have any difference of opinion (as regards to its interpretation and meaning) then you should stop reciting it (for the time being).
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He heard a man reciting a Quranic Verse which he had heard the Prophet (п·ә) reciting in a different way. So he took that man to the Prophet (and told him the story). The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Both of you are reciting in a correct way, so carry on reciting." The Prophet (п·ә) further added, "The nations which were before you were destroyed (by Allah) because they differed."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "The owner of this house." he pointed to `Abdullah's house, "said, 'I asked the Prophet (п·ә) 'Which deed is loved most by Allah?" He replied, 'To offer prayers at their early (very first) stated times.' " `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  asked, "What is the next (in goodness)?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "To be good and dutiful to one's parents," `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked, "What is the next (in goodness)?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "To participate in Jihad for Allah's Cause." `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ added, "The Prophet (п·ә) narrated to me these three things, and if I had asked more, he would have told me more."
narrator: Narrated Abi `Amr 'Ash-Shaibani:

text: A man came to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Who is more entitled to be treated with the best companionship by me?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Your mother." The man said. "Who is next?" The Prophet said, "Your mother." The man further said, "Who is next?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Your mother." The man asked for the fourth time, "Who is next?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Your father. " Ibn-e-Shubrumah and Yahya bin Ayyub have reported.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man said to the Prophet, "Shall I participate in Jihad?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Are your parents living?" The man said, "Yes." the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Do Jihad for their benefit."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said. "It is one of the greatest sins that a man should curse his parents." It was asked (by the people), "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! How does a man curse his parents?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "'The man abuses the father of another man and the latter abuses the father of the former and abuses his mother."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Allah has forbidden you ( 1 ) to be undutiful to your mothers (2) to withhold (what you should give) or (3) demand (what you do not deserve), and (4) to bury your daughters alive. And Allah has disliked that (A) you talk too much about others ( B), ask too many questions (in religion), or (C) waste your property."
narrator: Narrated Al-Mughira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said thrice, "Shall I not inform you of the biggest of the great sins?" We said, "Yes, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)" He said, "To join partners in worship with Allah: to be undutiful to one's parents." The Prophet (п·ә) sat up after he had been reclining and added, "And I warn you against giving forged statement and a false witness; I warn you against giving a forged statement and a false witness." The Prophet kept on saying that warning till we thought that he would not stop.
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) mentioned the greatest sins or he was asked about the greatest sins. He said, "To join partners in worship with Allah; to kill a soul which Allah has forbidden to kill; and to be undutiful or unkind to one's parents." The Prophet (п·ә) added, "Shall I inform you of the biggest of the great sins? That is the forged statement or the false witness." Shu`ba (the sub-narrator) states that most probably the Prophet said, "the false witness."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: My mother came to me, hoping (for my favor) during the lifetime of the Prophet. I asked the Prophet, "May I treat her kindly?" He replied, "Yes." Ibn 'Uyaina said, "Then Allah revealed: 'Allah forbids you not with regards to those who fought not against you because of religion, and drove you not out from your homes, that you should show them kindness and deal justly with them.'.......(60.8)
narrator: Narrated Asma' bint Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "My mother who was a Mushrikah (pagan, etc.), came with her father during the period of peace pact between the Muslims and the Quraish infidels. I went to seek the advice of the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "My mother has arrived and she is hoping (for my favor)." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Yes, be good to your mother."
narrator: Narrated Asma ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: That Heraclius sent for him and said, "What did he, i.e. the Prophet (п·ә) order you?" I replied, "He orders us to offer prayers; to give alms; to be chaste; and to keep good relations with our relatives.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sufyan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: It was said" "O Allah's Messenger! Inform me of a deed which will make me enter Paradise." (continues through a different chain in the next hadith)
narrator: Narrated Abu Ayyub Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Inform me of a deed which will make me enter Paradise." The people said, "What is the matter with him? What is the matter with him?" Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "He has something to ask (what he needs greatly)." The Prophet (п·ә) said (to him), (In order to enter Paradise) you should worship Allah and join none in worship with Him: You should offer prayers perfectly, give obligatory charity (Zakat), and keep good relations with your Kith and kin." He then said, "Leave it!" (The sub-narrator said, "It seems that the Prophet (п·ә) was riding his she camel."
narrator: Narrated Abu Aiyub Al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "The person who severs the bond of kinship will not enter Paradise."
narrator: Narrated Jubair bin Mut`im ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "Who ever is pleased that he be granted more wealth and that his lease of life be pro longed, then he should keep good relations with his Kith and kin."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah 's Apostle said, "Whoever loves that he be granted more wealth and that his lease of life be prolonged then he should keep good relations with his Kith and kin."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik Шұ Ш¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The word 'Ar-Rahm (womb) derives its name from Ar-Rahman (i.e., one of the names of Allah) and Allah said: 'I will keep good relation with the one who will keep good relation with you, (womb i.e. Kith and Kin) and sever the relation with him who will sever the relation with you, (womb, i.e. Kith and Kin).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The word 'Ar-Rahm' (womb) derives its name from 'Ar- Rahman' (i.e. Allah). So whosoever keeps good relations with it (womb i.e. Kith and kin), Allah will keep good relations with him, and whosoever will sever it (i.e. severs his bonds of Kith and kin) Allah too will sever His relations with him.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha (the wife of the Prophet) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying openly not secretly, "The family of Abu so-and-so (i.e. Talib) are not among my protectors." `Amr said that there was a blank space (1) in the Book of Muhammad bin Ja`far. He added, "My Protector is Allah and the righteous believing people." `Amr bin Al-`As added: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, 'But they (that family) have kinship (Rahm) with me and I will be good and dutiful to them. "
narrator: Narrated `Amr bin Al-`As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Al-Wasil is not the one who recompenses the good done to him by his relatives, but Al-Wasil is the one who keeps good relations with those relatives who had severed the bond of kinship with him."
narrator: Narrated Aamash:

text: That he said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! What do you think about my good deeds which I used to do during the period of ignorance (before embracing Islam) like keeping good relations with my Kith and kin, manumitting of slaves and giving alms etc; Shall I receive the reward for that?" Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "You have embraced Islam with all those good deeds which you did. It is also said: Abul Yaman has reported ''Atahannathu''. Mamar, Sualih and Ibn-e-Musafir said: Atahannothu.'' Ibn-e-Ishaq says that ''Atahannathu'' means to do good deeds. Similarly, Hisham has reported from his father.
narrator: Narrated Hakim bin Hizam:

text: I was present when a man asked Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ about the blood of mosquitoes. Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "From where are you?" The man replied. "From Iraq." Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said, "Look at that! he is asking me about the blood of Mosquitoes while they (the Iraqis ) have killed the (grand) son of the Prophet. I have heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "They (Hasan and Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ ) are my two sweet-smelling flowers in this world."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abi Na'm:

text: A lady along with her two daughters came to me asking me (for some alms), but she found nothing with me except one date which I gave to her and she divided it between her two daughters, and then she got up and went away. Then the Prophet (п·ә) came in and I informed him about this story. He said, "Whoever is in charge of (put to test by) these daughters and treats them generously, then they will act as a shield for him from the (Hell) Fire."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha (the wife of the Prophet) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) came out towards us, while carrying Umamah, the daughter of Abi Al-As (his granddaughter) over his shoulder. He prayed, and when he wanted to bow, he put her down, and when he stood up, he lifted her up.
narrator: Narrated Abu Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) kissed Al-Hasan bin `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ while Al-Aqra' bin H`Abis ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ at-Tamim was sitting beside him. Al-Aqra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "I have ten children and I have never kissed anyone of them," Allah's Messenger (п·ә) cast a look at him and said, "Whoever is not merciful to others will not be treated mercifully."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A bedouin came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "You (people) kiss the boys! We don't kiss them." The Prophet said, "I cannot put mercy in your heart after Allah has taken it away from it."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Some Sabi (i.e. war prisoners, children and woman only) were brought before the Prophet (п·ә) and behold, a woman amongst them was milking her breasts to feed and whenever she found a child amongst the captives, she took it over her chest and nursed it (she had lost her child but later she found him) the Prophet said to us, "Do you think that this lady can throw her son in the fire?" We replied, "No, if she has the power not to throw it (in the fire)." The Prophet (п·ә) then said, "Allah is more merciful to His slaves than this lady to her son."
narrator: Narrated `Umar bin Al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, Allah divided Mercy into one hundred parts. He kept ninety nine parts with Him and sent down one part to the earth, and because of that, its one single part, His Creations are merciful to each other, so that even the mare lifts up its hoofs away from its baby animal, lest it should trample on it."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I said 'O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Which sin is the greatest?" He said, "To set up a rival unto Allah, though He Alone created you." I said, "What next?" He said, "To kill your son lest he should share your food with you." I further asked, "What next?" He said, "To commit illegal sexual intercourse with the wife of your neighbor." And then Allah revealed as proof of the statement of the Prophet: 'Those who invoke not with Allah any other god alongwith Allah, and kill not the same which Allah has forbidden unjustly and don't commit adultery, and whosover does this, shall get the punishment''.)................. (to end of verse)...' (25.68)
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) took a child in his lap for Tahnik (i.e. he chewed a date in his mouth and put its juice in the mouth of the child). The child urinated on him, so he asked for water and poured it over the place of the urine.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) used to put me on (one of) his thighs and put Al-Hasan bin `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ on his other thigh, and then embrace us and say, "O Allah! Please be Merciful to them, as I am merciful to them. " Taimi narrates: I thought that I had heard such-and-such A hadith from Abu Uthman except this one. When I saw the record, I found this Hadith written there as I had heard it.
narrator: Narrated Usama bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I never felt so jealous of any woman as I did of Khadija ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , though she had died three years before the Prophet married me, and that was because I heard him mentioning her too often, and because his Lord had ordered him to give her the glad tidings that she would have a palace in Paradise, made of Qasab and because he used to slaughter a sheep and distribute its meat among her friends.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I and the person who looks after an orphan and provides for him, will be in Paradise like this," putting his index and middle fingers together.
narrator: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said "The one who looks after and works for a widow and for a poor person, is like a warrior fighting for Allah's Cause or like a person who fasts during the day and prays all the night." Narrated Abu Huraira that the Prophet (п·ә) said as above.
narrator: Narrated Safwan bin Salim:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The one who looks after and works for a widow and for a poor person is like a warrior fighting for Allah's Cause." (The narrator Al-Qa'nabi is not sure whether he also said "Like the one who prays all the night without slackness and fasts continuously and never breaks his fast.")
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We came to the Prophet (п·ә) and we were (a few) young men of approximately equal age and stayed with him for twenty nights. Then he thought that we were anxious for our families, and he asked us whom we had left behind to look after our families, and we told him. He was kindhearted and merciful, so he said, "Return to your families and teach them (religious knowledge) and order them (to do good deeds) and offer your prayers in the way you saw me offering my prayers, and when the stated time for the prayer becomes due, then one of you should pronounce its call (i.e. the Adhan), and the eldest of you should lead you in prayer.
narrator: Narrated Abu Sulaiman and Malik bin Huwairith ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) stood up for the prayer and we too stood up along with him. Then a bedouin shouted while offering prayer. "O Allah! Bestow Your Mercy on me and Muhammad only and do not bestow it on anybody else along with us." When the Prophet (п·ә) had finished his prayer with Taslim, he said to the Bedouin, "You have limited (narrowed) a very vast (thing)," meaning Allah's Mercy.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "You see the believers as regards their being merciful among themselves and showing love among themselves and being kind, resembling one body, so that, if any part of the body is not well then the whole body shares the sleeplessness (insomnia) and fever with it."
narrator: Narrated An-Nu`man bin Bashir:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If any Muslim plants any plant and a human being or an animal eats of it, he will be rewarded as if he had given that much in charity."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "He who is not merciful to others, will not be treated mercifully.
narrator: Narrated Jarir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said "Gabriel continued to recommend me about treating the neighbors Kindly and politely so much so that I thought he would order me to make them as my heirs.
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Allah' Apostle said, Gabriel kept on recommending me about treating the neighbors in a kind and polite manner, so much so that I thought that he would order (me) to make them (my) heirs."
narrator: Narrates Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "By Allah, he does not believe! By Allah, he does not believe! By Allah, he does not believe!" It was said, "Who is that, O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)?" He said, "That person whose neighbor does not feel safe from his evil." Humaid bin Aswad, Uthman bin Umer, Abu Bakr bin Ayyash and Shoib bin Ishaq from Ibn-e-Abi Dhiib and he from Maqburi  and he has reported it from Hadrat Abu Hurairah  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Abu Shuraih:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to say, "O Muslim ladies! A neighbouress should not look down upon the present of her neighbouress even it were the hooves of a sheep."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Anybody who believes in Allah and the Last Day should not harm his neighbor, and anybody who believes in Allah and the Last Day should entertain his guest generously and anybody who believes in Allah and the Last Day should talk what is good or keep quiet. (i.e. abstain from all kinds of evil and dirty talk).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! I have two neighbors! To whom shall I send my gifts?" He said, "To the one whose gate in nearer to you."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, Enjoining, all that is good is a Sadaqa."
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "On every Muslim there is enjoined (a compulsory) Sadaqa (alms)." They (the people) said, "If one has nothing?' He said, "He should work with his hands so that he may benefit himself and give in charity." They said, "If he cannot work or does not work?" He said, "Then he should help the oppressed unhappy person (by word or action or both)." They said, "If he does not do it?" He said, "Then he should enjoin what is good (or said what is reasonable).' They said, "If he does not do that''' He said, "Then he should refrain from doing evil, for that will be considered for Him as a Sadaqa (charity) . "
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) mentioned the (Hell) Fire and sought refuge (with Allah) from it, and turned his face to the other side. He mentioned the (Hell) Fire again and took refuge (with Allah) from it and turned his face to the other side. (Shu`ba, the sub-narrator, said, "I have no doubt that the Prophet (п·ә) repeated it twice.") The Prophet (п·ә) then said, "(O people!) Save yourselves from the (Hell) Fire even if with one half of a date fruit (given in charity), and if this is not available, then (save yourselves) by saying a good pleasant friendly word."
narrator: Narrated `Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A group of Jews entered upon the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "As-Samu-Alaikum." (i.e. death be upon you). I understood it and said, "Wa-Alaikum As-Samu wal-la'n. (death and the curse of Allah be Upon you)." Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said "Be calm, O `Aisha! Allah loves that on, should be kind and lenient in all matters." I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Haven't you heard what they (the Jews) have said?" Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said "I have (already) said (to them) "And upon you ! "
narrator: Narrated `Aisha (the wife of the Prophet) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: A bedouin urinated in the mosque and the people ran to (beat) him. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Do not interrupt his urination (i.e. let him finish)." Then the Prophet (п·ә) asked for a tumbler of water and poured the water over the place of urine.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "A believer to another believer is like a building whose different parts enforce each other." The Prophet (п·ә) then clasped his hands with the fingers interlaced. (At that time) the Prophet (п·ә) was sitting and a man came and begged or asked for something. The Prophet (п·ә) faced us and said, "Help and recommend him and you will receive the reward for it, and Allah will bring about what He will through His Prophet's tongue."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Whenever a beggar or a person in need came to the Prophet, the Prophet would say "Help and recommend him and you will receive the reward for it, and Allah will bring about what he will through His Prophet's tongue
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: They enter the presence of Hadrat Abdullah bin 'Amr when Hadrat Moaviya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ had come to Koufah. He, then, talking about Allah's Apostle (п·ә), said: He п·ә was neither foul-mouthed nor did he make use of obscene words. He also declared that Allah's Apostle п·ә said: The best amongst you is the one who is regarding character.
narrator: Narrated Masruq:

text: The Jews came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "As-Samu 'Alaikum" (death be on you). `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said (to them), "(Death) be on you, and may Allah curse you and shower His wrath upon you!" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Be calm, O `Aisha ! You should be kind and lenient, and beware of harshness and Fuhsh (i.e. bad words)." She said (to the Prophet), "Haven't you heard what they (Jews) have said?" He said, "Haven't you heard what I have said (to them)? I said the same to them, and my invocation against them will be accepted while theirs against me will be rejected (by Allah). "
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was not one who would abuse (others) or say obscene words, or curse (others), and if he wanted to admonish anyone of us, he used to say: "What is wrong with him, his forehead be dusted!"
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was the best among the people (both in shape and character) and was the most generous of them, and was the bravest of them. Once, during the night, the people of Medina got afraid (of a sound). So the people went towards that sound, but the Prophet (п·ә) having gone to that sound before them, met them while he was saying, "Don't be afraid, don't be afraid." (At that time) he was riding a horse belonging to Abu Talha and it was naked without a saddle, and he was carrying a sword slung at his neck. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I found it (the horse) like a sea, or, it is the sea indeed."
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Never was the Prophet (п·ә) asked for a thing to be given for which his answer was 'no'.
narrator: Narrated Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We were sitting with `Abdullah bin `Amr who was narrating to us (Hadith): He said, "Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was neither a Fahish nor a Mutafahhish, and he used to say, 'The best among you are the best in character (having good manners)."'
narrator: Narrated Masruq:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Time will pass rapidly, good deeds will decrease, and miserliness will be thrown (in the hearts of the people), and the Harj (will increase)." They asked, "What is the Harj?" He replied, "(It is) killing (murdering), (it is) murdering (killing).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I served the Prophet (п·ә) for ten years, and he never said to me, "Uf" (a minor harsh word denoting impatience) and never blamed me by saying, "Why did you do so or why didn't you do so?"
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) use to do at home. She replied. "He used to keep himself busy serving his family and when it was time for the prayer, he would get up for prayer."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "If Allah loves a person, He calls Gabriel saying: 'Allah loves so and so; O Gabriel, love him.' Gabriel would love him, and then Gabriel would make an announcement among the residents of the Heaven, 'Allah loves so-and-so, therefore, you should love him also.' So, all the residents of the Heavens would love him and then he is granted the pleasure of the people of the earth."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "None will have the sweetness (delight) of Faith (a) till he loves a person and loves him only for Allah's sake, (b) and till it becomes dearer to him to be thrown in the fire than to revert to disbelief (Heathenism) after Allah has brought him out of it, (c) and till Allah and His Apostle become dearer to him than anything else."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) forbade laughing at a person who passes wind, and said, "How does anyone of you beat his wife as he beats the stallion camel and then he may embrace (sleep with) her?" And Hisham said, "As he beats his slave"
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Zam`a ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Abusing a Muslim is Fusuq (i.e., an evil-doing), and killing him is Kufr (disbelief).Ghundar has also reported from Shobah likewise.
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: He heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "If somebody accuses another of Fusuq (by calling him 'Fasiq' i.e. a wicked person) or accuses him of Kufr, such an accusation will revert to him (i.e. the accuser) if his companion (the accused) is innocent."
narrator: Narrated Abu Dhar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) was neither a Fahish (one who had a bad tongue) nor a Sabbaba (one who abuses others) and he used to say while admonishing somebody, "What is wrong with him? May dust be on his forehead!"
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man from the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Two men abused each other in front of the Prophet (п·ә) and one of them became angry and his anger became so intense that his face became swollen and changed. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I know a word the saying of which will cause him to relax if he does say it." Then a man went to him and informed him of the statement of the Prophet (п·ә) and said, "Seek refuge with Allah from Satan." On that, angry man said, 'Do you find anything wrong with me? Am I insane? Go away!"
narrator: Narrated Sulaiman bin Surad:

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) went out to inform the people about the (date of the Night of decree (Al-Qadr). There happened a quarrel between two Muslim men. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I came out to inform you about the Night of Al-Qadr, but as so-and-so and so-and-so quarrelled, so the news about it had been taken away; and may be it was better for you. So look for it in the ninth, the seventh, or the fifth (of the last ten days of Ramadan).
narrator: Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The best family among the Ansar is the Banu An-Najjar. "
narrator: Narrated Abu Usaid As-Sa`idi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man asked permission to enter upon Allah's Messenger (п·ә). The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Admit him. What an evil brother of his people or a son of his people." But when the man entered, the Prophet (п·ә) spoke to him in a very polite manner. (And when that person left) I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! You had said what you had said, yet you spoke to him in a very polite manner?" The Prophet (п·ә) said, "O `Aisha! The worst people are those whom the people desert or leave in order to save themselves from their dirty language or from their transgression."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Once the Prophet (п·ә) went through the grave-yards of Medina and heard the voices of two humans who were being tortured in their graves. The Prophet (п·ә) said, "They are being punished, but they are not being punished because of a major sin, yet their sins are great. One of them used not to save himself from (being soiled with) the urine, and the other used to go about with calumnies (Namima)." Then the Prophet asked for a green palm tree leaf and split it into two pieces and placed one piece on each grave, saying, "I hope that their punishment may be abated as long as these pieces of the leaf are not dried."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "A Qattat will not enter Paradise."
narrator: Narrated Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whoever does not give up false statements (i.e. telling lies), and evil deeds, and speaking bad words to others, Allah is not in need of his (fasting) leaving his food and drink." Imam Ahmad narrates a man made me understand its authority.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The worst people in the Sight of Allah on the Day of Resurrection will be the double faced people who appear to some people with one face and to other people with another face."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Once Allah's Messenger (п·ә) divided and distributed (the war booty). An Ansar man said, "By Allah ! Muhammad, by this distribution, did not intend to please Allah." So I came to Allah's Messenger (п·ә) and informed him about it whereupon his face became changed with anger and he said, "May Allah bestow His Mercy on Moses for he was hurt with more than this, yet he remained patient."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) heard a man praising another man and he was exaggerating in his praise. The Prophet (п·ә) said (to him). "You have destroyed (or cut) the back of the man."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A man was mentioned before the Prophet (п·ә) and another man praised him greatly The Prophet (п·ә) said, "May Allah's Mercy be on you ! You have cut the neck of your friend." The Prophet (п·ә) repeated this sentence many times and said, "If it is indispensable for anyone of you to praise someone, then he should say, 'I think that he is so-and-so," if he really thinks that he is such. Allah is the One Who will take his accounts (as He knows his reality) and no-one can sanctify anybody before Allah." (Khalid said, "Woe to you," instead of "Allah's Mercy be on you.")
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakra:

text: "When Allah's Messenger (п·ә) mentioned what he mentioned about (the hanging of) the Izar (waist sheet), Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! My Izar slackens on one side (without my intention)." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "You are not among those (who, out of pride) drag their Izars behind them."
narrator: Narrated Salim reported from his father (Hadrat Ibne-e-Umer ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§):

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the worst of false tales; and do not look for the others' faults and do not spy, and do not be jealous of one another, and do not desert (cut your relation with) one another, and do not hate one another; and O Allah's worshipers! Be brothers (as Allah has ordered you!")
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Do not hate one another, and do not be jealous of one another, and do not desert each other, and O, Allah's worshipers! Be brothers. Lo! It is not permissible for any Muslim to desert (not talk to) his brother (Muslim) for more than three days."
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the worst of false tales. and do not look for the others' faults, and do not do spying on one another, and do not practice Najsh, and do not be jealous of one another and do not hate one another, and do not desert (stop talking to) one another. And O, Allah's worshipers! Be brothers!"
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "I do not think that so-and-so and so-and-so know anything of our religion." (And Al-Laith said, "These two persons were among the hypocrites.")
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: "The Prophet (п·ә) entered upon me one day and said, 'O `Aisha! I do not think that so-and-so and so-and-so know anything of our religion which we follow."'
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying. "All the sins of my followers will be forgiven except those of the Mujahirin (those who commit a sin openly or disclose their sins to the people).
