The other is a man whom Allah has given wealth and he spends it in the right way, in which case one may say, 'If I were given the same as he has been given, I would do the same as he is doing."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Not to wish to be the like of except the like of two (persons): a man whom Allah has given the knowledge of the Qur'an and he recites it during the hours of the night and the hours of the day; and a man whom Allah has given wealth and he spends it (in Allah's Cause) during the hours of the night and during the hours of the day." I heard it many a time from Sufyan but he did not mention the word ''Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш®ЩҺШЁЩҺШұЩҺ '' though it is amongst the authentic Ahadith of him.
narrator: Narrated Salim's father ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Our Prophet has informed us our Lord's Message that whoever of us is martyred, will go to Paradise.
narrator: Narrated Al-Mughira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Whoever tells you that the Prophet (п·ә) concealed anything. Masrooq states that Hadrat Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said: Don't testify him who says to you that the prophet п·ә concealed anything if the revelation, for Allah said: 'O Apostle Muhammad! Proclaim (the Message) which has been sent down to you from your Lord, and if you do it not, then you have not conveyed His Message.' (5.67)
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: A man asked the Prophet (п·ә) "What deeds are the best?" The Prophet (п·ә) said: "(1) To perform the (daily compulsory) prayers at their (early) stated fixed times, (2) to be good and dutiful to one's own parents, (3) and to participate in Jihad in Allah's Cause."
narrator: Narrated Ibn Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "My Lord says, 'If My slave comes nearer to me for a span, I go nearer to him for a cubit; and if he comes nearer to Me for a cubit, I go nearer to him for the span of outstretched arms; and if he comes to Me walking, I go to him running.' "
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Perhaps the Prophet (п·ә) mentioned the following (as Allah's Saying): "If My slave comes nearer to Me for a span, I go nearer to him for a cubit; and if he comes nearer to Me for a cubit; I go nearer to him for the span of outstretched arms. Motamir states that he heard it from his father, he from Hadrat Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that the prophet п·ә reports it from his Lord.
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said that your Lord said, "Every (sinful) deed can be expiated; and the fast is for Me, so I will give the reward for it; and the smell which comes out of the mouth of a fasting person, is better in Allah's Sight than the smell of musk." (See Hadith No. 584)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said that his Lord said: "It does not befit a slave that he should say that he is better than Jonah (Yunus) bin Matta him to his father.
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "I saw Allah's Messenger (п·ә) on the day of the Conquest of Mecca, riding his she-camel and reciting Surat-al-Fath (48) or part of Surat-al-Fath. He recited it in a vibrating and pleasant voice. Then Mu'awiya recited as `Abdullah bin Mughaffal had done and said, "Were I not afraid that the people would crowd around me, I would surely recite in a vibrating pleasant voice as Ibn Mughaffal did, imitating the Prophet." I asked Muawiya, "How did he recite in that tone?" He said thrice, "A, A , A."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin Al-Maghaffal Al-Muzani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
Heraclius called for his translator and then asked for the letter of the Prophet (п·ә), and the former read it (thus): 
"In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Merciful. (This letter is) from Muhammad bin 'Abdullah, to Heraclius.
 "...O people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians): Come to a word that is just between us and you that we worship none but Allah..." (V.3:64)
narrator: Narrated Abu Sufyan bin Harb:

text: The people of the Scripture used to read the Torah in Hebrew and explain it to the Muslims in Arabic. Then Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "Do not believe the people of the Scripture, and do not disbelieve them, but say, 'We believe in Allah and whatever has been revealed...' (3.84)
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "Allah does not listen to anything as He listens to the recitation of the Qur'an by a Prophet who recites it in attractive audible sweet sounding voice."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (when the slanderers said what they said about her): I went to my bed knowing at that time that I was innocent and that Allah would reveal my innocence, but by Allah, I never thought that Allah would reveal in my favor a revelation which would be recited, for I considered myself too unimportant to be talked about by Allah in the Divine Revelation that was to be recited. So Allah revealed the ten Verses (of Surat-an-Nur). 'Those who brought a false charge........' (24.11-20)
narrator: Narrated Abdullah:

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) reciting Surat at-Tin waz Zaitun (By the Fig and the Olive) in the `Isha' prayer and I have never heard anybody with a better voice or recitation than his.
narrator: Narrated Al-Bara':

text: The Prophet (п·ә) was hiding himself in Mecca and used to recite the (Qur'an) in a loud voice. When the pagans heard him they would abuse the Qur'an and the one who brought it, so Allah said to His Prophet: 'Neither say your prayer aloud, nor say it in a low tone.' (17.110)
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: "I have notice that you like sheep and the desert, so when you are looking after your sheep or when you are in the desert and want to pronounce the Adhan, raise your voice, for no Jinn, human being or any other things hear the Mu`adh-dhin's voice but will be a witness for him on the Day of Resurrection." Abu Sa`id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ added, "I heard this from Allah's Messenger (п·ә)."
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to recite the Qur'an with his head in my lap while I used to be in my periods (having menses).
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Why should a doer (people) try to do good deeds?' The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Everybody will find easy to do such deeds as will lead him to his destined place for which he has been created.'
narrator: Narrated `Imran ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: While the Prophet (п·ә) was in a funeral procession, he took a stick and started scraping the earth with it and said, "There is none of you but has his place assigned either in Hell or in Paradise." They (the people) said, "Shall we not depend upon that (and give up doing any deeds)?' He said, " Carry on doing (good deeds) for everybody will find it easy to do such deeds as will lead him to his destined place for which he has been created ." (And then the Prophet (п·ә) recited the Verse):-- 'As for him who gives (in charity) and keeps his duty to Allah...' (92.5)
narrator: Narrated `Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying, "Before Allah created the creations, He wrote a Book (wherein He has written): My Mercy has preceded my Anger." and that (Book) is written with Him over the Throne."
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) saying: "Before Allah created the creations, He wrote a Book (wherein He has written): "My Mercy has preceded my Anger.' And that is written with Him over the Throne." (see Hadith 3194)
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said, "The painter of these pictures will be punished on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be said to them, Make alive what you have created.' "
narrator: Narrated Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The painters of these pictures will be punished on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be said to them, 'Make alive what you have created."
narrator: Narrated Ibn `Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying, "Allah said, 'Who are most unjust than those who try to create something like My creation? I challenge them to create even a smallest ant, a wheat grain or a barley grain.' "
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, 'The example of a believer who recites the Qur'an is that of a citron (a citrus fruit) which is good in taste and good in smell. And the believer who does not recite the Qur'an is like a date which has a good taste but no smell. And the example of an impious person who recites the Qur'an is that of Ar-Rihana (an aromatic plant) which smells good but is bitter in taste. And the example of an impious person who does not recite the Qur'an is that of a colocynth which is bitter in taste and has no smell."
narrator: Narrated Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Some people asked the Prophet (п·ә) regarding the soothsayers. He said, "They are nothing." They said, "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Some of their talks come true." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "That word which happens to be true is what a Jinn snatches away by stealth (from the Heaven) and pours it in the ears of his friend (the foreteller) with a sound like the cackling of a hen. The soothsayers then mix with that word, one hundred lies."
narrator: Narrated `Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There will emerge from the East some people who will recite the Qur'an but it will not exceed their throats and who will go out of (renounce) the religion (Islam) as an arrow passes through the game, and they will never come back to it unless the arrow, comes back to the middle of the bow (by itself) (i.e., impossible). The people asked, "What will their signs be?" He said, "Their sign will be the habit of shaving (of their beards and their heads). (Fath-ul-Bari, Page 322, Vol. 17th)
narrator: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "(There are) two words which are dear to the Beneficent (Allah) and very light (easy) for the tongue (to say), but very heavy in weight in the balance. They are: ''Subhan Allah wa-bi hamdihi'' and ''Subhan Allah Al-`Azim." (see Hadith 6682).
narrator: Narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq said to the Prophet (п·ә) "O Allah's Messenger (п·ә)! Teach me an invocation with which I may invoke Allah in my prayers." The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Say: O Allah! I have wronged my soul very much (oppressed myself), and none forgives the sins but You; so please bestow Your Forgiveness upon me. No doubt, You are the Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."
narrator: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Amr:

text: When Ma'bad discussed the problem pertaining to Divine Decree, we refuted that. He (the narrator) said I and Humaid b. Abdur-Rahman Himyari argued. And they carried on the conversation about the purport of the hadith related by Kahmas and its chain of transmission too, and there is some variation of words.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Yahya b. Ya'mur that:

text: We met Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ and we discussed about the Divine Decree, and what they talked about it and he narrated the hadith that has been transmitted by 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ from the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). There is a slight variation in that.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Yahya bin Ya'mur and Humaid bin 'Abdur-Rahman that they said:

text: A similar Hadith was narrator from Yahya bin Ya'mar, from Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, from the prophet п·ә.

This hadith is narrated to us on the authority of Muhammad bin 'Abdullah bin Numair, on the authority of Muhammad bin Bishr, on the authority of Abd Hayyan al-Taymi with the exception that in this narration (instead of the words (Iza Waladat al'amah rabbaha), the words are (Iza Waladat al'amah Ba'laha), i.e, when slave-girl gives birth to her master.
The Prophet remarked: By his father, he shall succeed if he were true (to what he professed), or By his father, he would enter heaven if he were true (to what he professed).
narrator: Another hadith, the like of which has been narrated by Malik (bin Anas) (and mentioned above) is also reported by Talha bin 'Ubaidullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, with the only variation:

text: 'We were forbidden in the Quran to ask the messenger of Allah п·ә about anything (needlessly), "and he quoted a similar Hadith (as no. 102).
narrator: It was narrated that Thabit said: "Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: A similar Hadith (as no. 104) was reported by Musa bin Talha who narrated it from Abu Ayyub ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ , from the prophet п·ә.

A man came to the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and said: Direct me to a deed which draws me near to Paradise and takes me away from the Fire (of Hell). Upon this he (the Holy Prophet п·ә) said: You worship Allah and never associate anything with Him, establish prayer, and pay Zakat, and do good to your kin. When he turned his back, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) remarked: If he adheres to what he has been ordered to do, he would enter Paradise.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Ayyub вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: Nu'man bin Qawqal вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢcame to the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and said: Would I enter Paradise if I say the obligatory prayers and deny myself that which is forbidden and treat that as lawful what has been made permissible (by the Shari'ah)? The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) replied in the affirmative.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: A similar hadith is narrated on Jabir's authority in which the following words are added: I will do nothing more.

A man once said to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ): Shall I enter Paradise in case I say the obligatory prayers, observe the (fasts) of Ramadan and treat that as lawful which has been made permissible (by the Shari'ah) and deny myself that what is forbidden, and make no addition to it? He (the Holy Prophet) replied in the affirmative. He (the inquirer) said: By Allah, I would add nothing to it.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jabir:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said: (The superstructure of) al-Islam is raised on five (pillars), i. e. the oneness of Allah, the establishment of prayer, payment of Zakat, the, fast of Ramadan, Pilgrimage (to Mecca). A person said (to 'Abdullah b. Umar the narrator): Which of the two precedes the other-Pilgrimage or the fasts of Rarnadan? Upon this he (the narrator) replied: No (it is not the Pilgrimage first) but the fasts of Ramadan precede the Pilgrimage.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of ('Abdullah) son of Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said: (The superstructure of) al-Islam is raised on five (pillars), i. e. Allah (alone) should be worshipped, and (all other gods) beside Him should be (categorically) denied. Establishment of prayer, the payment of Zakat, Pilgrimage to the House, and the fast of Ramadan (are the other obligatory acts besides the belief in the oneness of Allah and denial of all other gods).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of ('Abdullah) son of 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: (The superstructure of) al-Islam is raised on five (pillars), testifying (the fact) that there is no god but Allah, that Muhammad is His bondsman and messenger, and the establishment of prayer, payment of Zakat, Pilgrimage to the House (Ka'ba) and the fast of Ramadan.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah son of 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: A man said to 'Abdullah son of 'Umar (ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ), Why don't you carry out a military expedition? Upon which he replied: I heard the messenger of Allah (п·ә) say: Verily, al-Islam is founded on five (pillars): testifying the fact that there is no god but Allah, establishment of prayer, payment of Zakat, fast of Ramadan and Pilgrimage to the House.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Ta'us:

text: in which the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: I forbid you to prepare nabidh in a gourd, hollowed block of wood, a varnished jar or receptacle. Ibn Mu'adh made this addition on the authority of his father that the Messenger of Allah said to Ashajj, of the tribe of 'Abdul-Qais: You possess two qualities which are liked by Allah: insight and deliberateness.
narrator: There is another hadith narrated on the authority of Ibn Abbas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ(the contents of which are similar to the one) narrated by Shu'ba

text: When a delegation from Abdul-Qais came to Messenger of Allah п·ә... and he narrated a Hadith similar to that of Ibn 'Ulayyah (no. 118), but he said: "And they put small dates, dates and water in it." And he did not say: "Sa'eed said: 'Or he said: "Dates."
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Saeed Al-Khudri

text: The prophet п·ә sent Mu'adh вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ to Yemen and said: "you are going to people..." and he narrated a Hadith similar to that of Waki (no. 121).
narrator: It was narrated from Ibn-e-Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: I have been commanded to fight against people so long as they do not declare that there is no god but Allah, and he who professed it was guaranteed the protection of his property and life on my behalf except for the right affairs rest with Allah.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He heard the Messenger of Allah say: I have been commanded to fight against people, till they testify to the fact that there is no god but Allah, and believe in me (that) I am the messenger (from the Lord) and in all that I have brought. And when they do it, their blood and riches are guaranteed protection on my behalf except where it is justified by law, and their affairs rest with Allah.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: "I have been commanded to fight the people"... and he narrated a Hadith similar to that narrated by Ibn Al-Musayyab from Abu Hurairah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ (no. 125).
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: "I have been commanded to fight the people until they say La ilaha illallah. If they say La ilaha illallah, their blood and their wealth are protected from me, expect for a right that is due, and their reckoning will be with Allah." Then he recited: "You are only one who reminds. You are not a dictator over them.
narrator: It was narrated that Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah said: I have been commanded to fight against people till they testify that there is no god but Allah, that Muhammad is the messenger of Allah, and they establish prayer, and pay Zakat and if they do it, their blood and property are guaranteed protection on my behalf except when justified by law, and their affairs rest with Allah.
narrator: It has been narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin 'Umar:

text: It Is narrated on the authority of Abu Malik: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) say: He who professed that there is no god but Allah and made a denial of everything which the people worship beside Allah, his property and blood became inviolable, an their affairs rest with Allah.
He heard the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) say: He who held belief in the unity of Allah, and then narrated what has been stated above.
narrator: Abu Malik narrated on the authority of his father:

text: The Hadith of Salih ended with the words, "And Allah revealed concerning him, and he did not quote the two Verses. "And repeating this statement." And in the narration of Mamar, in place of this statement' is the words: 'And he did not cease.'
narrator:
The same hadith is mentioned through a different chain except it ends where it mentions that Allah revealed the verses and it does not mention the verses. There is also a slight variation in words.
A similar report (as no. 32) was narrated from Az-Zuhri with this chain, expect that:

text: The Messenger of Allah said to his uncle at the time of his death: Make a profession of it that there is no god but Allah and I will bear testimony (of your being a Muslim) on the Day of judgment. But he (Abu Talib) refused to do so. Then Allah revealed this verse: Verily thou canst not guide to the right path whom thou lovest. And it is Allah Who guideth whom He will and He knoweth best who are the guided (xxviii. 56).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah said to his uncle (at the time of his death): Make a profession of it that there is no god but Allah and I will bear testimony (of your being a Muslim) on the Day of judgment. He (Abu Talib) said: Were it not the fear of the Quraysh blaming me (and) saying that it was the fear of (approaching death) that induced me to do so, I would have certainly delighted your eyes. It was then that Allah revealed: Verily thou canst not guide to the right path whom thou lovest. And it is Allah Who guideth whom He will and He knoweth best who are the guided (xxviii-56).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said. He who died knowing (fully well) that there is no god but Allah entered Paradise.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Uthman вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He heard Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ saying this: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) uttering these words (as stated above).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Humran:

text: The messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: He who said: There is no god but Allah, He is One and there is no associate with Him, that Muhammad is his servant and His messenger, that Christ is servant and the son of His slave-girl and he (Christ) His word which He communicated to Mary and is His Spirit, that Paradise is a fact and Hell is a fact, Allah would make him (he who affirms these truths enter Paradise through any one of its eight doors which he would like.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Ubadah bin Samit ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah would make him (he who affirms these truths) enter Paradise through one of the eight doors which he would like.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Umar bin Hani with the same chain of transmitters with the exception of these words:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Mu'adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ , do you know the right of Allah over His bondsmen? He (Mu'adh) said: Allah and His Apostle know best. He (the Messenger of Allah) said: That Allah alone should be worshipped and nothing should be associated with Him. He (the Holy Prophet) said: What right have they (bondsmen) upon Him in case they do it? He (Mu'adh) said: Allah and His Apostle know best. He (the Holy Prophet) said: That He would not punish them.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Mu'adh bin Jabal ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) called, me and I replied to him. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Do you know the right of Allah upon the people? and then followed the hadith (mentioned above).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Aswad bin Hilal that he heard Mu'adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsay this:

text: He became blind. He sent a message to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that he should come and mark a place of worship for him. Thereupon came the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and his people and then there was a discussion among them about a man who was known as Malik b. Dukhshum, and subsequently the narrator described the hadith of Sulaiman bin Mughira as stated above.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Anas that 'Itban bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ told him:

text: He heard the Messenger of Allah saying: He has found the taste of faith (iman) who is content with Allah as his Lord, with Islam as his religion (code of life) and with Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as his Prophet.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abbas bin 'Abdul-Muttalib ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Iman has over seventy branches, and modesty is a branch of Iman.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ: вҖҢ

text: Faith has over seventy branches or over sixty branches, the most excellent of which is the declaration that there is no god but Allah, and the humblest of which is the, removal of what is injurious from the path: and modesty is the branch of faith.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) heard a man censuring his brother regarding modesty. Upon this the Prophet remarked: Modesty is part of Iman (faith).
narrator: Salim reported on the authority of his father:

text: He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) happened to pass by a mass of Ansar who was instructing his brother (about modesty).
narrator: Zuhri has narrated this hadith with the addition of these words:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) said: Modesty brings forth nothing but goodness. Bushair bin Ka'b said: It is recorded in the books of wisdom, there lies sobriety in it and calmness of mind in it, Imran said: I am narrating to you the tradition of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ) and you talk of your books.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Imran bin Husain вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Ishaq bin Ibrahim narrates this hadith of the Prophet on the authority of Imran b. Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, like the one narrated by Hammad bin Zaid.

I asked the Messenger of Allah п·ә to tell me about Islam a thing which might dispense with the necessity of my asking anybody after you. In the hadith of Abu Usama the (words) are: other than you. He (the Holy Prophet  п·ә) remarked: Say I affirm my faith in Allah and then remain steadfast to it.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Sufyan bin 'Abdulla al-Thaqafi вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ that he said:

text: A man asked the Messenger of Allah (п·ә ) which of the merits (is superior) in Islam. He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) remarked: That you provide food and extend greetings to one whom you know or do not know.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah bin 'Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Verily a person asked the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) who amongst the Muslims was better. Upon this (the Holy Prophet п·ә) remarked: From whose hand and tongue the Muslims are safe.
narrator: Abdullah bin Amr bin al-As вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ is reported to have said:

text: He heard the (Holy Prophet п·ә) say: A Muslim is he from whose hand and tongue the Muslims are safe.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I asked the Messenger of Allah which (attribute) of Islam is more excellent. Upon this he remarked: One in which the Muslims are safe, protected from the tongue and hand of (other Muslims).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Musa Ash'ari вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was asked as to who amongst the Muslims is better, and the rest of the hadith was narrated like this.
narrator: Ibrahim bin Sa'id al-Jauhari has narrated this hadith with the same words in addition to these:

text: The Prophet of Allah (п·ә) said: There are three qualities for which anyone who is characterised by them will relish the sweetness of faith: he to whom Allah and His Messenger are dearer than all else; he who loves a man for Allah's sake alone; and he who has as great an abhorrence of returning to unbelief after Allah has rescued him from it as he has of being cast into Hell.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: There are three qualities for which any one who is characterised by them will relish the savour of faith: that he loves man and he does not love him but for Allah's sake alone; he is to whom Allah and His Messenger are dearer than all else; he who prefers to be thrown into fire than to return to unbelief after Allah has rescued him out of it.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The messenger of Allah said..." a similar Hadith (as no. 166), except that he said: ".... then he returning to judaism or christianity.
narrator: It was narrated that Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) said: No bondsman believes, and, in the hadith narrated by Abdul Warith, no person believes, till I am dearer to him than the members of his household, his wealth and the whole of mankind.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: It is reported on the authority of Anas b. Malik that the Messenger of Allah said: None of you is a believer till I am dearer to him than his child, his father and the whole of mankind.
The Prophet (п·ә) observed: None amongst you believes (truly) until he loves for his brother - or he said for his neighbour - that which he loves for himself.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (may peace blessings be upon him) observed: By Him in whose Hand is my life, no, bondsman (truly) believes till he likes for his neighbour, or he (the Holy Prophet) said: for his brother, whatever he likes for himself.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: He will not enter Paradise whose neighbour is not secure from his wrongful conduct.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: He who believes in Allah and the Last Day should either utter good words or better keep silence; and he who believes in Allah and the Last Day should treat his neighbour with kindness and he who believes in Allah and the Last Day should show hospitality to his guest.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: He who believes in Allah and the Last Day does not harm is neighbour, and he who believes in Allah and the Last Day shows hospitality to his guest and he who believes in Allah and the Last Day speaks good or remains silent.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "The Messenger of Allah п·ә said...." a similar Hadith to that of Abu Husin, except that he said: Let him treat his neighbour bor.
narrator: It was narrated that narrated Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) observed: He who believes in Allah and the Last Day should do good to his neighbour and he who believes in Allah and the Last Day should show hospitality to the guest and he who believes in Allah and the Last Day should either speak good or better remain silent.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Shuraih al-Khuzai' вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: A similar Hadith was narrated concering the story of Marvan, and the Hadith Abu Saeed вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ from the Prophet ( п·ә).

The freed slave of the prophet п·ә, from Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ There was no prophet who did not have disciples who followed his guidance and his path," similar to the Hadith of Salih (no. 179), but he did not mention the coming of Ibn Mas'ud вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢor Ibn 'Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢmeeting with him.
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Rafi:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) pointed towards Yemen with his hand and said: Verily Iman is towards this side, and harshness and callousness of the hearts is found amongst the rude owners of the camels who drive them behind their tails (to the direction) where emerge the two horns of Satan, they are the tribes of Rabi'a and Mudar.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Ibn Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә observed: There have come the people of Yemen; they are tender of hearts, the belief is that of the Yemenites, the understanding (of the faith) is that of the Yemenites and sagacity is that of the Yemenites.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә said.... " a similar Hadith.
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) observed: There came to you the people from Yemen; they are tender of hearts and mild of feelings, the understanding is Yemenite, the sagacity is Yemenite.
narrator: Abu Huraira reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) remarked: The summit of unbelief is towards the East and the pride and conceitedness is found among the owners of horses and camels who are rude and uncivil, people of the tents, and tranquillity is found among those who rear goats and sheep.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: The belief is among the Yemenites, and the unbelief is towards the East, and tranquillity is among those who rear goats and sheep, and pride and simulation is among the uncivil and rude owners of horses and camels.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah saying this: Pride and conceitedness is found among the uncivil owners of the camels and tranquillity is found among the owners of sheep and goats.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: "(True) faith is that of the Yemenis, true wisdom is that of the Yemenis."
narrator: A similar Hadith was narrated from Az-Zuhri with his chain, and he added:

text: I heard the Prophet (п·ә) saying: There came the people of Yemen, they are tender of feelings and meek of hearts. The belief is that of the Yemenites, the sagacity is that of the Yemenites, the tranquillity is among the owners of goats and sheep, and pride and conceitedness is among the uncivil owners of the camels, the people of the tents in the direction of sunrise.
narrator: It was narrated from Az-Zuhri, Saeed bin Al-Musayyab told me that Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: There came the people of Yemen who are soft of hearts, tender in feelings: the belief is that of the Yemenites, the sagacity is that of the Yemenites and the summit of unbelief is towards the East.
narrator: It is reporter on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: but he did not mention: "The head of disbelief is towards the east.
narrator: Jarir narrated from Al-Amash (a Hadith similar to no. 189) with this chain:

text: Pride and conceitedness is among the owners of the camels and tranquillity and sobriety is found amongst the owners of sheep.
narrator: Shu'ba narrated the hadith as reported by Jarir with the same chain of narrators with this addition:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: The callousness of heart and sternness is in the East and faith is among the people of the Hijaz.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: You shall not enter Paradise so long as you do not affirm belief (in all those things which are the articles of faith) and you will not believe as long as you do not love one another. Should I not direct you to a thing which, if you do, will foster love amongst you: (i. e.) give currency to (the practice of paying salutation to one another by saying) as-salamu alaikum.
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: Zuhair b. Harb said: Jarir reported on the authority of A'mash with this chain of transmitters that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: By him in Whose hand is my life, you shall not enter Paradise unless you believe. The rest of the hadith is the same as narrated by Abd Mu'awiya and Waki'.

The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: Al-Din is a name of sincerity and well wishing. Upon this we said: For whom? He replied: For Allah, His Book, His Messenger and for the leaders and the general Muslims.
narrator: It was narrated on the authority of Tamim ad-Dari ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: Muhammad bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢand others narrate the same hadith of the Apostle (п·ә) on the authority of Tamim ad-Dari ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ.
A similar hadith was narrated from Tamim ad-Dari ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ , from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә).
He observed I gave pledge of allegiance to the Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) on the observance of prayer, payment of Zakat, and sincerity and well-wishing for every Muslim.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jarir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: I pledged allegiance to the Messenger of Allah п·ә on sincerity and well-wishing for every Muslim.
narrator: Sufyan narrated on the authority of Ziyad b. 'Ilaqa that he heard Jarir bin 'Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ saying:

text: I owed allegiance to the Messenger of Allah ( п·ә) on hearing ( is commands) and obeying (them) and the Prophet) instructed me (to act) as lay in my power, and sincerity and goodwill for every Muslim.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jarir вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢthat he observed:

text: The Messenger of Allah observed: The fornicator who fornicates is not a believer so long as he commits it and no thief who steals is a believer as long as he commits theft, and no drunkard who drinks wine is a believer as long as he drinks it. 'Abdul-Malik b. Abi Bakr' narrated this on the authority of Abu Bakr b. Abdur-Rahman b. Harith and then said: Abu Huraira made this addition: No plunderer who plunders a valuable thing that attracts the attention of people is a believer so long as he commits this act.
narrator: Abu Huraira reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah said that a fornicator does not fornicate, and then narrated the hadith like this, and he also made mention of plundering too, but did not mention of a thin having value. Ibn Shihab said: Sa'id b. al-Musayyib and Abu Salama narrated this hadith on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢa hadith like that of Abu Bakr вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ with the exception of (the mention) of plundering.
narrator: Abdul-Malik bin Shu'aib narrated this hadith on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢthat he observed:

text: A Hadith similar to that of 'Uquail Muhammad bin Az-Zuhri was narrated from Abu Hurairah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ вҖҢfrom the prophet п·ә (in which) he mention "plunder" but he did not mention" that which is precious."
Humaid bin 'Abdur-Rehman narrated from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ, from the prophet п·ә (the same as no.202).
Al-'Ala' bin Abdul-Rehman the same (the same as. 202) narrating from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, from the prophet п·ә.

A fornicator who fornicates is not a believer as long as he commits fornication, and no one who steals is a believer as long as he commits theft, and no one who drinks wine is a believer as long as he drinks it, and repentance may be accepted after that.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ from the prophet:

text: The prophet п·ә said: "No adulterer is a believers at the time he is committing adultery; no thief is a believer at the time he is stealing; no drinking of wine is a believer at the time he is drinking it; but repentance may be acceptance may be accepted afterwards."
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: No adulterter is a believer at the time he is committing adultery," then he mentioned a Hadith similar to that of Shobah.
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢand attributed to the prophet п·ә:

text: The Prophet observed: There are four characteristics, whoever has them all is a pure hypocrite, and whoever has one of its characteristics, he has one of the characteristics of hypocrisy, until he gives it up: When he speaks he lies, when he makes a covenant he betrays it, when he makes a promise he breaks it, and when he disputes he resorts to obscene speech. In the narration of Sufyan (one of the narrators) it is: And if he has one of them, he has one of the characteristics of hypocrisy.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin 'Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Three are the signs of a hypocrite: when he spoke he told a lie, when he made a promise he acted treacherously against it, when he was trusted he betrayed.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) observed: There are three characteristics of a hypocrite: when he spoke he told a lie, when he made promise he acted treacherously, and when he was trusted he betrayed.
narrator: Abu Huraira reported that:

text: He said: Three are the signs of a hypocrite, even if he observed fast and prayed and asserted that he was a Muslim.
narrator: Uqba bin Mukarram al-'Ami reported that he heard 'Ala' bin 'Abdur-Rahman narrating this hadith with this chain of transmitters:

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) made observations like them embodied in the hadith narrated by Yahya bin Muhammad on the authority of 'Ala', and added to it: even if he observed fast and prayed and asserted that he was a Muslim.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) observed: When a man calls his brother an unbeliever, it returns (at least) to one of them.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) said: Any person who called his brother: O unbeliever (has in fact done an act by which this unbelief) would return to one of them. If it were so, as he asserted (then the unbelief of man was confirmed but if it was not true), then it returned to him (to the man who labeled it on his brother Muslim).
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Ibn 'Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He heard the Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) saying: No person who claimed knowingly anyone else as his father besides (his own) committed nothing but infidelity, and he who made a claim of anything, which (in fact) did not belong to him, is not amongst us; he should make his abode in Fire, and he who labeled anyone with unbelief or called him the enemy of Allah, and he was in fact not so, it rebounded on him.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Dharr вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings be upon him) observed: Do not detest your fathers; he who detested his father committed infidelity.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Both of my ears heard the Messenger of Allah saying this: He who claimed the fatherhood of anyone else besides his real father knowingly (committed a great sin) ;Paradise is forbidden to him. Abu Bakra asserted that he too heard it from the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him ).
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: My ears heard and my hearing preserved it that Muhammad (п·ә) observed: He who claimed for another one his fatherhood besides his own father knowingly that he was not his father-to him Paradise is forbidden.
narrator: Sa'd and Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ each one of them said:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: Abusing a Muslim is an outrage and fighting against him is unbelief. Zubaid said: I asked Abu Wa'il: Did you hear it from Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ narrating if from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә)? He replied: Yes. But there is mention of the talk between Zubaid and Abu Wa'il in the hadith narrated by Shu'ba.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
Abu Bakr bin Abu Shaiba narrated a hadith like this from the Apostle (п·ә) on the authority of Abdullah.
A similar hadith was narrated from Abu Wail, from Abdullah, from the prophet п·ә.
The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) asked him on the occasion of the Farewell Pilgrimage to make the people silent and then said: Do not return to unbelief after me by striking the necks of one another.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jarir bin 'Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text:
A similar Hadith was narrated from Ibn-'Umer, from the prophet п·ә.
Abdullah b. Mu'adh narrated from the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) a hadith like this on the authority on Ibn Umar.
The Apostle (п·ә) observed on the occasion of the Farewell Pilgrimage Woe unto you distress unto you! Don't turn back as unbelievers after me by striking the necks of one another.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: Harmala bin Yahya, Abdullah bin Wahb, Umar bin Muhammad, Ibn Umar narrated like the hadith reported by Shu'ba on the authority of Waqid.
The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: Two (things) are found among men which are tantamount to unbelief: slandering one's lineage and lamentation on the dead.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The slave who fled from his master committed an act of infidelity as long as he would not return to him. Mansur observed: By God, this hadith was narrated from the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him), but I do not like that this should be narrated on my authority here in Basra.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jarir that he heard Ash-shabi say:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed: The slave who fled from his master, responsibility with regard to him was absolved.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jarir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Holy Prophet said: When the slave runs away from his master, his prayer is not accepted.
narrator: It was narrated that Jarir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Don't you know what your Lord said? He observed: I have never endowed My bondsmen with a favor, but a section amongst them disbelieved it and said: Stars, it was due to the stars.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessing be upon him) observed: Allah does not shower His blessings from the heaven that in the morning a group of men disbelieve it (to be a blessing from Allah). Allah sends down rain, but they (the disbelievers) say: Such and such star (is responsible for that).
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: There was (once) a downpour during the life of the Apostle п·ә Upon this the Apostle (п·ә) observed: Some people entered the morning with gratitude and some with ingratitude (to Allah). Those who entered with gratitude said: This is the blessing of Allah, and those who entered with ingratitude said: Such and such asterism was right. It was upon this that the verse was revealed: I swear by the setting of the stars to the end and make your provision that you should disbelieve it.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: It is reported on the authority of Anas that the Messenger of Allah (may peace and blessings Be upon him) observed:
The prophet п·ә said: The sign of a hypocrite is the hatred against the Ansar and the sign of a believer is the love for the Ansar.
narrator: It was narrated from Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: He remarked with regard to the Ansar: None but the believer loves them, none but the hypocrite hates them. He who loves them loves Allah and he who hates them hates Allah. I (the narrator) said: Did you hear this hadith from al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ ? He said: He narrated it to me.
narrator: Al-Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported from the Messenger (п·ә) :

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: A person who believes in Allah and the Last Day never nurses a grudge against the Ansar.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah observed: The person who believes in Allah and the Last Day never nurses a grudge against the Ansar.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Sa'id Khudri вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: 'Ali observed: By Him Who split up the seed and created something living, the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) gave me a promise that no one but a believer would love me, and none but a hypocrite would nurse grudge against me.
narrator: It was narrated that Zirr reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Tahir with this chain of transmitters.
A hadith like this as narrated by Ibn 'Umar has also been transmitted by Abu Sa'id al-Khudri. A hadith like this as narrated by Ibn 'Umar has also been transmitted by Abu Huraira.

The messenger of Allah п·ә said: When, the son of Adam recites the Ayat of Sajdah (prostration) and then falls down in prostration, the Satan goes into seclusion and weeps and says: Alas, and in the narration of Abu Kuraib the words are: Woe unto me, the son of Adam was commanded to prostrate, and he prostrated and Paradise was entitled to him and I was commanded to prostrate, but I refused and am doomed to Hell.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He (the Satan) said: I disobeyed and I am doomed to Hell.
narrator: A'mash narrated this hadith with the same chain of transmitters, with this change of words:

text: He heard the Apostle (may peace and blessings be upon him) saying. Verily between man and between polytheism and unbelief is the negligence of prayer.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observing this: Between man and polytheism and unbelief is the abandonment of salat.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Zubair that he heard Jabir b. 'Abdullah saying.

text: The Messenger of Allah  п·ә was asked about the best of deeds. He observed: Belief in Allah. He (the inquirer) said: What next? He (the Holy Prophet  п·ә) replied: Jihad (struggle to the utmost) in the cause of Allah. He (the inquirer) again said: What next? He (the Holy Prophet  п·ә) replied: Pilgrimage accepted into the grace of the Lord. In the. tradition narrated on the authority of Muhammad b. Ja'far (the words are) that he (the Holy Prophet) said: Belief in Allah and His Messenger.
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: Muhammad bin Rafi and 'Abd bin Humaid, 'Abdur-Razzaq and Ma'mar and Zuhri have narrated a hadith like this on the authority of the same chain of transmitters.

I said: Messenger of Allah п·ә, which of the deeds is the best? He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) replied: Belief in Allah and Jihad in His cause. I again asked: Who is the slave whose emancipation is the best? He (the Holy Prophet) replied: One who is valuable for his master and whose price is high. I said: If I can't afford to do it? He (the Holy Prophet) replied: Help an artisan or make anything for the unskilled (labourer). I (Abu Dharr) said: Messenger of Allah, you see that I am helpless in doing some of these deeds. He (the Holy Prophet) replied: Desist from doing mischief to the people. That is the charity of your person on your behalf.
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: Muhammad b. Abu Rafi' narrated the hadith on the authority of Abu Dharr with a slight difference.

He observed. I asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) which deed was the best. He (the Holy Prophet) replied: Prayer at its appointed hour. I (again) said: Then what? He (the Holy Prophet) replied: Kindness to the parents. I (again) said: Then what? He replied: Earnest endeavour (Jihad) in the cause of Allah. And I would have not ceased asking more questions but out of regard (for his feelings).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah b. Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: I said: Messenger of Allah, which of the deeds (takes one) nearer to Paradise? He (the Holy Prophet) replied: Prayer at its proper time, I said: What next, Messenger of Allah? He replied: Kindness to the parents. I said: What next? He replied: Jihad in the cause of Allah.
narrator: It was narrated that Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: He said: The owner of this house told me that he asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ): Which of the deeds are liked by Allah? He (the Holy Prophet) observed: Prayer at its proper time. I (again) said: What next? He replied: Then goodness to the parents. I (again) said: What then? He replied: Then Jihad in the cause of Allah. He ('Abdullah) said: This is what I was told (by the Holy Prophet). Had I questioned further, he would have made additions for me.
narrator: It was heard from Abu 'Amr Shaibani that, pointing towards the house of Abdullah:

text: He pointed towards the house of 'Abdullah, but he did not mention his name for us.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted by Muhammad bin Bashshar, Muhammad bin Ja'far Shu'ba with this chain of narrators, with the addition:

text: The Messenger of Allah observed: The best of' the deeds or deed is the (observance of) prayer at its proper time and kindness to the parents.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of 'Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We were in the company of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that he observed: Should I not inform you about the most grievous of the grave sins? (The Holy Prophet) repeated it three times, and then said: Associating anyone with Allah, disobedience to parents, false testimony or false utterance. The Prophet was reclining, then he sat up, and he repeated it so many times that we wished that he should become silent.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdur-Rahman bin Abu Bakra that his father said:

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the major sins. He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) observed: Associating anyone with Allah, disobedience to parents, killing a person and false utterance.
narrator: It was narrated from Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) talked about the major sins, or he was asked about the major sins. Upon this he observed: Associating anyone with Allah, killing of a person, disobedience to parents. He (the Prophet п·ә further) said: Should I not inform you about the gravest of the major sins, and (in this connection) observed: False utterance or false testimony. Shu'ba said. It was most probably false testimony .
narrator: Ubaidullah bin Abu Bakr said: I heard Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ saying:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: Avoid the seven noxious things. It was said (by the hearers): What are they, Messenger of Allah? He (the Holy Prophet) replied: Associating anything with Allah, magic, killing of one whom God has declared inviolate without a just cause, consuming the property of an orphan, and consuming of usury, turning back when the army advances, and slandering chaste women who are believers, but unwary.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: Abusing one's parents is one of the major sins. They (the hearers) said: Messenger of Allah, does a man abuse his parents too? He (the Holy Prophet) replied: Yes, one abuses the father of another man, who in turn abuses his father. One abuses his mother and he in turn abuses his (the former's) mother.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah bin Amr bin al-'As вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: This hadith has also been transmitted on the authority of Sa'd bin Ibrahim with this chain of narrators.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), observed: He who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of pride shall not enter Paradise. A person (amongst his hearers) said: Verily a person loves that his dress should be fine, and his shoes should be fine. He (the Holy Prophet) remarked: Verily, Allah is Graceful and He loves Grace. Pride is disdaining the truth (out of self-conceit) and contempt for the people.
narrator: It Is narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: None shall enter the Fire (of Hell) who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of Iman and none shall enter Paradise who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of pride.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) observed: He who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of pride shall not enter Paradise.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah had observed and Ibn Numair asserted: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saying: He who dies associating anything with Allah would enter the Fire (of Hell). 'Abdullah b. Mas'ud said: I say that he who died without associating anything with Allah entered Paradise.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ that Waki ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢtold (him):

text: A man came to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and said: Messenger of Allah, what are the two things quite unavoidable? He replied: He who dies without associating anyone with Allah would (necessarily) enter Paradise and he who dies associating anything with Allah would enter the (Fire of) Hell.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jabir вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) saying: He who met Allah without associating anything with Allah entered Paradise and he who met Him associating (anything) with Him entered Fire.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet of Allah п·ә said something similar (as no. 270).
narrator: It was narrated from Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that he observed: Gabriel came to me and gave me the tidings: Verily he who died amongst your Ummah without associating anything with Allah would enter Paradise. I (the narrator) said: Even if he committed adultery and theft. He (the Holy Prophet) said: (Yes), even if he committed adultery and theft.
narrator: I heard Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ narrating that:

text: The hadith transmitted by Auza'i and Ibn Juraij contains these words: I embraced Islam for Allah's sake. and in the hadith narrated by Ma'mar the words are: I knelt down to kill him, that he said; There is no god but Allah.
narrator: The same hadith has been transmitted by the same chain of narrators:

text: Messenger of Allah, here is a point: If I happened to encounter a person amongst the infidels (in the battle). Then he narrated a hadith similar to the one transmitted by Laith.
narrator: It is narrated by Miqdad, and he was an ally of Bin Zuhra and was of those who participated in the Battle of Badr along with the Messenger of Allah, that he said:

text: The Prophet of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) who said: He who took up arms against us is not of us.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: He who draws the sword against us is not of us.
narrator: Iyas bin Salama narrated from his father:

text: The prophet п·ә said: He who took up arms against us is not of us.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Musa Ash'ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) observed: He who took up arms against us is not of us and he who acted dishonestly towards us is not of us.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) happened to pass by a heap of eatables (corn). He thrust his hand in that (heap) and his fingers were moistened. He said to the owner of that heap of eatables (corn): What is this? He replied: Messenger of Allah, these have been drenched by rainfall. He (the Holy Prophet) remarked: Why did you not place this (the drenched part of the heap) over other eatables so that the people could see it? He who deceives is not of me (is not my follower).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Prophet observed: He is not one of us (one among the Ummah of Islam) who beat the cheeks or tore the front opening of the shirt or uttered the slogans of (the days of) Jahiliya (ignorance). Ibn Numair and Abu Bakr said (instead of the word au (or) it is wa [and] the words are) and tore and uttered (the slogans) of Jahiliya without alif .
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abdullah bin Mas'ud:

text: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwas afflicted with grave pain and he became unconscious and his head was in the lap of a lady of his household. One of the women of his household walled. He (Abu Musa вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ) was unable (because of weakness) to say anything to her. But when he was a bit recovered he said: I have no concern with one with whom the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) has no concern, Verily the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) has no concern with that woman who wails loudly, shaves her hair and tears (her garment in grief).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Burda bin Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢthat

text: Abu Musa вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ fell unconscious and his wife Umm Abdullah came there and wailed loudly. When he felt relief he said: Don't you know? -and narrated to her: Verily the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: I have no concern with one who shaved her hair, lamented loudly and tore (her clothes in grief).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Burda :

text: This hadith was also narrated from Rabi bin Hirash from Abu Musa вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, from the prophet п·ә, but in the Hadith of 'Iyad Al-Ashari (a narrator) it says: "He is not one of us..." and not, "I disavow myself..."

A certain man carried tales. Upon this Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢremarked: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saying: The tale-bearer shall not enter Paradise.
narrator: It is reported from Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢthat news reached him (the Holy Prophet п·ә):

text: A man used to carry tales to the governor. We were sitting in the mosque. the people said: He is one who carries tales to the governor. He (the narrator) said: Then he came and sat with us. Thereupon Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ remarked: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saying: The beater of false tales would never enter heaven.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Hammam b, al-Harith

text: We were sitting with Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢin the mosque. A man came and sat along with us. It was said to Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢthat he was the man who carried tales to the ruler. Hudhaifa ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ remarked with the intention of conveying to him: I have heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: The tale-bearer will not enter Paradise.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Hammam bin al-Harith:

text: When the Muslims came to Medina, they gathered and sought to know the time of prayer but no one summoned them. One day they discussed the matter, and some of them said: Use something like the bell of the Christians and some of them said: Use horn like that of the Jews. Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid: Why may not a be appointed who should call (people) to prayer? The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: O Bilal, get up and summon (the people) to prayer.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwas commanded (by the Messenger of Allah) to repeat (the phrases of) Adhan twice and once in Iqama. The narrator said: I made a mention of it before Ayyub who said: Except for saying: Qamat-is-Salat [the time for prayer has come].
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: They (the Companions) discussed that they should know the timings of prayer by means of something recognized by all. Some of them said that fire should be lighted or a bell should be rung. But Bilal was ordered to repeat the phrases twice in Adhan, and once in Iqama.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When the majority of the people discussed they should know, like the hadith narrated by al-Thaqafi (mentioned above) except for the words: They (the people) should kindle fire.
narrator: This hadith is transmitted by Khalid Hadhdha with the same chain of transmitters (and the words are):

text: Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ was commanded (by the Holy Prophet) to repeat the phrases twice in Adhan, and once in lqama.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had two Mu'adhdhins, Bilal and 'Abdullah bin Umm Maktum, who (latter) was blind.
narrator: Ibn Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'A'isha by another chain of transmitters.
Ibn Umm Maktum used to pronounce Adhan at the behest of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) (despite the fact) that he was blind.
narrator: A'isha reported:

text: A similar report  was narrated from Hisham with this chain.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) used to attack the enemy when it was dawn. He would listen to the Adhan; so if he heard an Adhan, he stopped, otherwise made an attack. Once on hearing a man say: Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) remarked: He is following al-Fitra (al-Islam). Then hearing him say: I testify that there is no god but Allah. there is no god but Allah, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) said: You have come out of the Fire (of Hell). They looked at him and found that he was a goat herd.
narrator: Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: When you hear the call (to prayer), repeat what the Mu'adhdhin pronounces.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: When you hear the Mu'adhdhin, repeat what he says, then invoke a blessing on me, for everyone who invokes a blessing on me will receive ten blessings from Allah; then beg from Allah al-Wasila for me, which is a rank in Paradise fitting for only one of Allah's servants, and I hope that I may be that one. If anyone who asks that I be given the Wasila, he will be assured of my intercession.
narrator: Abdullah bin Amr bin al-As reported Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: If anyone says on hearing the Mu'adhdhin: I testify that there is no god but Allah alone. Who has no partner, and that Muhammad is His servant and His Messenger, (and that) I am satisfied with Allah as my Lord, with Muhammad as Messenger. and with Islam as din (code of life), his sins would be forgiven. In the narration transmitted by Ibn Rumh the words are: He who said on hearing the Mu'adhdhin and verity I testify. ' Qutaiba has not mentioned his words: And I.
narrator: Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying The Mu'adhdhins will have the longest necks on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Yahya narrated it on the authority of his uncle that he had been sitting in the company of Mu'awiya bin Abu Sufyan when the Mu'adhdhin called (Muslims) to prayer. Mu'awiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: I heard Muaviyah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ say: The Messenger of Allah п·ә said......" A similar report (as no. 852).
narrator: It was narrated that Eisa bin Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: He had heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say: When Satan hears the call to prayer, he runs away to a distance like that of Rauha. Sulaimin said: I asked him about Rauha. He replied: It is at a distance of thirty-six miles from Medina.
narrator: Abu Sufyan reported it on the authority of Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Abu Mu'awiya narrated it on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) as saying: When Satan hears the call to prayer, he turns back and breaks the wind so as not to hear the call being made, but when the call is finished he turns round and distracts (the minds of those who pray), and when he hears the Iqama, he again runs away so as not to hear its voice and when it subsides, he comes back and distracts (the minds of those who stand for prayer).
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When the Mu'adhdhin calls to prayer, Satan runs back vehemently.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: His father sent him to Banu Haritha along with a boy or a man. Someone called him by his name from an enclosure. He (the narrator) said: The person with me looked towards the enclosure, but saw nothing. I made a mention of that to my father. He said: If I knew that you would meet such a situation I would have never sent you (there), but (bear in wind) whenever you hear such a call (from the evil spirits) pronounce the Adhan. for I have heard Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsay that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whenever Adhan is proclaimed, Satan runs back vehemently.
narrator: Suhail reported:

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said When the call to prayer is made, Satan runs back and breaks wind so as not to hear the call being made, and when the call is finished. he turns round. When Iqama is proclaimed he turns his back, and when it is finished he turns round to distract a man, saying: Re- member such and such; remember such and such, referring to something the man did not have in his mind, with the result that he does not know how much he has prayed.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The prophet п·ә except that he said: He (the man saying the prayer) does not know how much he has prayed.
narrator: A hadith like it has been narrated by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) raising his hands apposite the shoulders at the time of beginning the prayer and before bowing down and after coming back to the position after bowing. but he did not raise them between two prostrations.
narrator: Salim narrated it on the authority of his father who reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), when he stood up for prayer, used to raise his hands apposite the shoulders and then recited takbir (Allah-o-Akbar), and when he was about to bow he again did like it and when he raised himself from the ruku' (bowing posture) he again did like it, but he did not do it at the time of raising his head from prostration.
narrator: Ibn Umar reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stood up for prayer, he raised hands (to the height) apposite the shoulders and then recited takbir.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted with the same chain of transmitters by Az-Zuhri as narrated by Ibn Juraij (who) said:

text: He saw Malik bin Huwairith вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢraising his hands at the beginning of prayer and raising his hands before kneeling down, and raising his hands after lifting his head from the state of kneeling, and he narrated that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to do like this.
narrator: Abu Qilaba reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) raised his hands apposite his ears at the time of reciting the takbir (i. e. at the time of beginning the prayer) and then again raised his hands apposite the ears at the time of bowing and when he lifted his head after bowing he said: Allah listened to him who praised Him, and did like it (raised his hands up to the ears).
narrator: Malik bin Huwairith ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: He saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) doing this (i.e. raising his hands) till they were opposite the lobes of ears.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters:

text: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ led prayer for them and recited takbir when he bent and raised himself (in ruku' and sujud) and after completing (the prayer) he said: By Allah I say prayer which has the best resemblance with the prayer of the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) amongst you.
narrator: Abu Salama reported:

text: He had heard Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsay: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited takbir on standing for prayer, and the rest of the hadith is like that transmitted by Ibn Juraij (recorded above), but he did not mention Abu Huraira as saying: My prayer has the best resemblance amongst you with the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Ibn al-Harith reported:

text: On completing the prayer with salutation, and he turned to the people in the mosque and said.... 'By the one in whose hand in my soul! I am the among you whose prayer most closely resembles that of the Messenger of Allah п·ә.
narrator: Abu Salama bin 'Abd al-Rahman reported.. When Marwan appointed Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ as his deputy in Medina, he recited takbir whenever he got up for obligatory prayer, and the rest of the hadith is the same as transmitted by Ibn Juraij (but with the addition of these words):

text: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ recited takbir in prayer on all occasions of rising and kneeling. We said: O Abu Huraira  вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ, what is this takbir? He said: Verily it is the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Abu Salama reported:

text: He ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢused to recite takbir on all occasions of rising and bending (in prayer) and narrated that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to do like that.
narrator: Suhail reported on the authority of his father Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I and 'Imran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said prayer behind 'Ali bin Abu Talib ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ. He recited takbir when he prostrated, and he recited takbir when he raised his head and he recited takbir while rising up (from the sitting position at the end of two rak'ahs). When we had finished our prayer, 'Imran caught hold of my hand and said: He (Hadrat Ali) has led prayer like Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) or he said: He in fact recalled to my mind the prayer of Muhammad (п·ә).
narrator: Mutarrif reported:
