text: He who does not recite Fatihat al-Kitab is not credited with having observed the prayer.
narrator: Ubada bin as-Samit вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә):

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who does not recite Umm al-Qur'an is not credited with having observed the prayer.
narrator: Ubada bin as-Samit вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: On whose face the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) squirted water from the well, reported on the authority of 'Ubada bin as- Samit ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢthat the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) said: He who does not recite Umm al-Qur'an is not credited with having observed prayer.
narrator: Mahmud bin al-Rabi' ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: This hadith has also been transmitted by Ma'mar from al-Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters with the addition of these words: and something more .

It was narrated from Al-'Ala' bin Abdur Rehman that he heard Abu As-Saib, the freed slave of Hisham bin Zuhrah, say: I heard Abu Hurairah say: The Messenger of Allah п·ә say....(Similar to Hadith no. 876).

He had heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) say: He who observed prayer but he did not recite the Umm al-Qur'an in it, and the rest of the hadith is the same as transmitted by Sufyan, and in this hadith the words are: Allah the Most High said: the prayer is divided into two halves between Me and My servant. The half of it is for Me and the half of it is for My servant.
narrator: It is naratted on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: He who said his prayer, but did not recite the opening chapter of al-Kitab, his prayer is incomplete. He repeated it thrice.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: One is not credited with having observed the prayer without the recitation (of al-Fatiha). So said Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ: (The prayer in which) the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited in a loud voice, we also recited that loudly for you (and the prayer in which) he recited inwardly we also recited inwardly for you (to give you a practical example of the prayer of the Holy Prophet).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: Who said that one should recite (al-Fatiha) in every (rak'ah of) prayer. What we heard (i. e. recitation) from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), we made you listen to that. And that which he (recited) inwardly, we (recited) inwardly for you. A person said to him: If I add nothing to the (recitation) of the Umm al Qur'an (Surat al-Fatiha), would it make the prayer incomplete? He (AbuHuraira) said: If you add to that (if you recite some of verses of the Qur'an along with Surat at-Fatiha) that is better for you. But if you are contented with it (Surat al-Fatiha) only, it is sufficient for you.
narrator: Ata' narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira:

text: Recitation (of Surat al-Fatiha) in every (rak'ah) of prayer in essential. (The recitation) that we listened to from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) we made you listen to it. And that which he recited inwardly to us, we recited it inwardly for you. And he who recites Umm al-Qur'an, it is enough for him (to complete the prayer), and he who adds to it (recites some other verses of the Holy Qur'an along with Surat al-Fatiha), it is preferable for him.
narrator: Ata' reported it on the authority of Abu Huraira who said:

text: A person entered the mosque and said prayer while the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was sitting in a nook (of the mosque), and the rest of the hadith is the same as mentioned above, but with this addition: When you get up to pray, perform the ablution completely, and then turn towards the Qibla and recite takbir (Allah o Akbar =Allah is the Most Great).
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) led us In Zuhr or 'Asr prayer (noon or the afternoon prayer). (On concluding it) he said: Who recited behind me (the verses): Sabbih Isma Rabbik al-a'la (Glorify the name of thy Lord, the Most High)? There upon a person said: It was I, but I in- tended nothing but goodness. I felt that some one of you was disputing with me in it (or he was taking out from my tongue what I was reciting), said the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: lmran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed the Zuhr prayer and a person recited Sabbih Isma Rabbik al-a'la (Glorify the name of thy Lord, the Most High) behind him. When he (the Holy Prophet п·ә) concluded the prayer he said: Who amongst you recited (the above-mentioned verse) or who amongst you was the reciter? A person said: It was I. Upon this he (the Holy Prophet п·ә) observed: I thought as if someone amongst you was disputing with me (in what I was reciting).
narrator: Imran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed Zuhr prayer and said: I felt that someone amongst you was disputing with me (in what I was reciting).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters:

text: I observed prayer along with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and with Abu Bakr, Umar and Uthman (ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ), but I never heard any one of them reciting Bismillah-ir-Rahman-ir-Rahim loudly.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Did you hear it from Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ? He replied in the affir- mative and added: We had inquired of him about it.
narrator: Shu'ba reported it with the same chain of transmitters. with she addition of these words: I said to Qatada:

text: He had heard Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢnarrating this.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Abu Talha:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) dozed off, and the rest of the hadith is the same as transmitted by Mushir except for the words that he (the Holy Prophet) said: It (Kauthar) is a canal which my Lord the Exalted and the Glorious has promised me in Paradise. There is a tank over it, but he made no mention of the tumblers like the number of the stars.
narrator: Mukhtar bin Fulful reported that he had heard Anas bin Malik say:

text: He saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) raising his hands at the time of beginning the prayer and reciting takbir, and according to Hammam (the narrator), the hands were lifted opposite to ears. He (the Holy Prophet) then wrapped his hands in his cloth and placed his right hand over his left hand. And when he was about to bow down, he brought out his hands from the cloth, and then lifted them, and then recited takbir and bowed down, and when (he came back to the erect position) he recited: Allah listened to him who praised Him. And when he prostrated, he prostrated between his two palms.
narrator: Wa'il bin Hujr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Then he may choose any supplication which pleases him.
narrator: Shu'ba has narrated this on the authority of Mansur with the same chain of transmitters, but he made no mention of this:

text: Then he may choose any supplication which pleases him or which he likes.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Mansur with the same chain of transmitters and he made a mention of this:

text: We were sitting with the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) in prayer, and the rest of the hadith is the same as narrated by Mansur He (also said): After (reciting tashahud) he may choose any prayer.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) taught me tashahhud taking my hand within his palms, in the same way as he taught me a Sura of the Qur'an, and he narrated it as narrated above.
narrator: Ibn Mas'ud is reported to have said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to teach us tashahhud just as he used to teach us a Surah of the Qur'an, and he would say: All services rendered by words, acts of worship, and all good things are due to Allah. Peace be upon you, O Prophet, and Allah's mercy and blessings. Peace be upon us and upon Allah's upright servants. I testify that there is no god but Allah, and I testify that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah. In the narration of Ibn Rumh (the words are): As he would teach us the Qur'an.
narrator: Ibn `Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to teach us tashahhud as he would teach us a Sura of the Qur'an.
narrator: Tawus narrated it on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢthat he said:

text: Allah, the Exalted and the Glorious, commanded it through the tongue of His Apostle (п·ә): Allah listens to him who praises Him.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted by Qatida with the same chain of transmitters (and the words are):

text: Should I not offer you a present (and added): The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) came to us and we said: We have learnt how to invoke peace upon you; (kindly tell us) how we should bless you. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Say: O Allah: bless Muhammad and his family as Thou didst bless the family of Ibrahim. Verily Thou art Praiseworthy and Glorious, O Allah.
narrator: Ibn Abi Laila reported: Ka'b bin 'Ujra ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢmet me and said:

text: Bless Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and he did not say: (Allahhummah) O Allah.
narrator: A hadith like this has been narrated by al-Hakam except that he said:

text: They (the Companions of the Holy Prophet) said: Apostle of Allah, how should we bless you? He (the Holy Prophet) observed: Say: O Allah! bless Muhammad, his wives and his offspring as Thou didst bless Ibrahim, and grant favours to Muhammad, and his wives and his offspring as Thou didst grant favours to the family of Ibrahim; Thou art Praiseworthy and Glorious.
narrator: Abu Humaid as-Sa'idi вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: He who blesses me once, Allah would bless him ten times.
narrator: Abu Huraira reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When the Imam says: Allah listens to him who praises Him. you should say: O Allah, our Lord for Thee is the praise. for if what anyone says synchronises with what the angels say, his past sins will be forgiven.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: A hadith similar to that of Summayy was from Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢfrom the prophet п·ә.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Say Amin when the Imam says Amin, for it anyone's utterance of Amin synchronises with that of the angels, he will be forgiven his past sins.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) the hadith like one transmitted by Malik, but he made no mention of the words of Shihab.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When anyone amongst you utters Amin in prayer and the angels in the sky also utter Amin, and this (utterance of the one) synchronises with (that of) the other, all his previous sins are pardoned.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When anyone amongst you utters Amin and the angels In the heaven also utter Amin and (the Amin) of the one synchronises with (that of) the other, all his previous sins are pardoned.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this is transmitted by Ma'mar from Hammam bin Munabbih on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ who reported it from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When the reciter (Imam) utters: Not of those on whom (is Thine) wrath and not the erring ones, and (the person) behind him utters Amin and his utterance synchronises with that of the dwellers of heavens, all his previous sins would be pardoned.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) fell down from a horse and his right side was grazed. We went to him to inquire after his health when the time of prayer came. He led us in prayer in a sitting posture and we said prayer behind him sitting, and when he finished the prayer hesaid: The Imam is appointed only to be followed; so when he recites takbir, you should also recite that; when he prostrates, you should also prostrate; when he rises up, you should also rise up, and when he said God listens to him who praises Him, you should say: Our Lord, to Thee be the praise, and when he prays sitting, all of you should pray sitting.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) fell down from a horse and he was grazed and he led the prayer for us sitting, and the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) fell down from a horse and his right side was grazed, and the rest of the hadith is the same with the addition of these words: When he (the Imam) says prayer standing, you should also do so.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) rode a horse and fell down from it and his right side was grazed, and the rest of the hadith is the same, and (these words) are found in it: When he (the Imam) says prayer in an erect posture, you should also say it in an erect posture.
narrator: Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) fell down from his horse and his right side was grazed, and the rest of the hadith is the same. In this hadith there are no additions (of words) as transmitted by Yunus and Malik.
narrator: Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) fell ill and some of his Companions came to inquire after his health. The Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) said prayer sitting, while (his Companions) said it (behind him) standing. He (the Holy Prophet) directed them by his gesture to sit down, and they sat down (in prayer). After finishing the (prayer) lie (the Holy Prophet) said: The Imam is appointed so that be should be followed, so bow down when lie bows down, and rise rip when he rises up and say (prayer) sitting when he (the Imam) says (it) sitting.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: This hadith is narrated with the same chain of transmitters by Hisham bin 'Urwa.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led the prayer and Abu Bakr вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ was behind him. When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited the takbir, Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ also recited (it) in order to make it audible to us. And the rest of the hadith is like one transmitted by Laith.
narrator: Jabir вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The Imam is appointed, so that he should be followed, so don't be at variance with him. Recite takbir when he recites it; bow down when he bows down and when he says: Allah listens to him who praises Him, say: O Allah, our Lord, to Thee be the Praise. And when he (the Imam) prostrates, you should also prostrate, and when he says prayer sitting, you should all observe prayer sitting.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Hammam bin Munabbih from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ.

Abu Huraira reported: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) while teaching us (the principles of faith), said: Do not try to go ahead of the Imam, recite takbir when he recites it. and when he says: Nor of those who err, you should say Amin, bow down when lie bows down, and when he says: Allah listens to him who praises Him, say: O Allah, our Lord, to Thee be the praise .
Nor of those who err, say Amin and added: And don't rise up ahead of him.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) (a hadith) like it, except the words:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Verily the Imam is a shield, say prayer sitting when he says prayer sitting. And when he says: Allah listens to him who praises Him, say: O Allah, our Lord, to Thee be the praise. and when the utterance of the people of the earth synchronises with that of the beings of heaven (angels), all the previous sins would be pardoned.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: The Imam is appointed to be followed. So recite takbir when he recites it, and bow down when he bows down and when he utters: Allah listens to him who praises Him, say O Allah, our Lord, for Thee be the praise. And when he prays, standing, you should pray standing. And when he prays sitting, all of you should pray sitting.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I tried to dissuade the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) from it (i. e. from appointing Abu Bakr as the Imam.) and my insistence upon it was not due to the fact that I entertained any apprehension in my mind that the people would not love the man who would occupy his (Prophet's) place (i. e. who would be appointed as his caliph) and I feared that the people would be superstitious about one who would occupy his place. I, therefore, desired that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) should leave Abu Bakr aside in this matter.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), said:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) suffered from illness of which he died, and in the hadith transmitted by Ibn Mushir, the words are: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was brought till he was seated by his (Abu Bakr's) side and the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led the people in prayer and Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ was making takbir audible to them, and in the hadith transmitted by 'Isa the (words are): The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sat and led the people in prayer and Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ was by his side and he was making (takbir) audible to the people.
narrator: A'mash reported:

text: The last glance that I have had of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (before his death) was that when he on Monday drew the curtain aside. The hadith transmitted by Salih is perfect and complete.
narrator: Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: "When it was the Monday..." a similar hadith (as no. 944).
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ lifted his hands and praised Allah and retraced his (steps) till he stood in a row.
narrator: This hadith is transmitted by Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢin the same way as narrated by Malik, with the exception of these words:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went to Bani Amr bin 'Auf in order to bring about reconciliation amongst them. The rest of the hadith is the same but with (the addition of these words): The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) came and made his way through the rows till he came to the first row and Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ retraced his steps.
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd al-Sa'idi вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: I made up my mind to hold Abdul-Rahman bin 'Auf back, but the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Leave him.
narrator: This hadith is narrated by Hamza bin Mughira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters (but with the addition of these words):

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Glorification of Allah is for men and clapping of hands is meant for women (if something happens in prayer). Harmala added in his narration that Ibn Shihab told him: I saw some of the scholars glorifying Allah and making a gesture.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: This hadith is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters.
A similar report was narrated from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, from the prophet вҖҢп·ә, and he added: (The tasbih is for men and clapping is for women) while praying."

One day the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led the prayer. Then turning (towards his Companions) he said: O you, the man, why don't you say your prayer well? Does the observer of prayer not see how he is performing the prayer for he performs it for himself? By Allah, I see behind me as I see In front of me.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Do you find me seeing towards the Qibla only? By Allah, your bowing and your prostrating are not hidden from my view. Verily I see them behind my back.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Complete the bowing and prostration well. By Allah, I see you behind my back as to how you bow and prostrate or when you bow and prostrate.
narrator: Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The prophet of Allah п·ә said: "Complete the bowing and prostration, for by Allah, I can see you behind my back when you bow and prostrate."
narrator: It was narrated from Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) one day led us in the prayer. and when he completed the Prayer he turned his face towards us and said: O People, I am your Imam, so do not precede me in bowing and prostration and in standing and turning (faces, i. e. In pronouncing salutation), for I see you in front of me and behind me, and then said: By Him in Whose hand Is the life of Muhammad, if you could see what I see, you would have laughed little and wept much more. They said: What did you see, Messenger of Allah? He replied: (I saw) Paradise and Hell.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith is narrated by Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwith another chain of transmitters, and in the hadith transmitted by Jarir there is no mention of turning (faces) .

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Does the man who lifts his head ahead of the Imam (from prostration) not fear that Allah may change his head into the head of an ass?
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Does the man who lifts his head before the Imam not fear that Allah may change his face into that of an ass?
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: Allah may make his face like the face of an ass.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters except for the words narrated by Rabi' bin Muslim:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The people who lift their eyes towards the sky in Prayer should avoid it or they would lose their eyesight.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: People should avoid lifting their eyes towards the sky while supplicating in prayer, otherwise their eyes would be snatched away.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) came to us and said: How is it that I see you lifting your hands like the tails of headstrong horses? Be calm in prayer. He (the narrator) said: He then again came to us and saw us (sitting) in circles; he said: How is it that I see you in separate groups? He (the narrator) said: He again came to us and said: Why don't you draw yourselves up in rows as angels do in the presence of their Lord? We said: Messenger of Allah, bow do the angels draw themselves up in rows in the presence of their Lord? He (the Holy Prophet) said: They make the first rows complete and keep close together in the row.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.

When we said prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), we pronounced: Peace be upon you and Mercy of Allah, peace be upon you and Mercy of Allah, and made gesture with the hand on both the sides. Upon this the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him said: What do you point out with your hands as if they are the tails of headstrong horses? This is enough for you that one should place one's hand on one's thigh and then pronounce salutation upon one's brother on the right side and then on the left.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: We said our prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and, while pronouncing salutations, we made gestures with our hands (indicating) Peace be upon you, peace be upon you. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) looked towards us and said: Why is it that you make gestures with your hands like the tails of headstrong horses? When any one of you pronounces salutation (in prayer) he should only turn his face towards his companion and should not make a gesture with his hand.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: Keep straight, don't be irregular, for there would be dissension in your hearts. Let those of you who are sedate and prudent be near me, then those who are next to them, then those who are next to them. Abu Mas'ud вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said: Now-a-days there is much dissension amongst you.
narrator: Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported: The Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) used to touch our shoulders in prayer and say:

text: This hadith is narrated by Ibn Uyaina with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Let those who are sedate and prudent be near me, then those who are next to them (saying it tliree tinies), and beware of the tumult of the markets.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Straighten your rows. for the straightening of a row is a part of the perfection of prayer.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Complete the rows, for I can see you behind my back.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This is what was transmitted to us by Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢfrom the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and, while making a mention of a few ahadith, said: (The Messengerof Allah directed us thus): Establish rows in prayer, for the making of a row (straight) is one of the merits of prayer.
narrator: Hammam bin Munabbih reported:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) say: Straighten your rows, or Allah would create dissension amongst you.
narrator: Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to straighten our rows as it lie were straightening an arrow with their help until be saw that we had learnt it from him. One day he came out, stood up (for prayer) and was about to say: Allah is the Greatest, when he saw a man, whose chest was bulging out from the row, so he said: Servants of Allah, you hint straighten your rows or Allah would create dissension amongst you.
narrator: Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: Abu 'Awana reported this hadith with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to straighten our rows as it lie were straightening an arrow with their help until be saw that we had learnt it from him. One day he came out, stood up (for prayer) and was about to say: Allah is the Greatest, when he saw a man, whose chest was bulging out from the row, so he said: Servants of Allah, you hint straighten your rows or Allah would create dissension amongst you.
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw (a tendency ) among his Companions to go to the back, so he said to them: Come forward and follow my lead, and let those who come after you follow your lead. People will continue to keep back till Allah will put them at the back.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw people at the end of the mosque, and then the (above-mentioned hadith) was narrated.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: If you were to know, or if they were to know, what (excellence) lies in the first rows, there would have been drawing of lots (for filling them) ; and Ibn Harb said: For (occupying) the first row there would have been drawing of lots.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The best rows for men are the first rows, and the worst ones the last ones, and the best rows for women are the last ones and the worst ones for them are the first ones.
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: This hadith is narrated by Suhail with the same chain of transmitters.

Sahl b. Sa'd reported: I saw men having tied (the ends) of their lower garments around their necks, like children, due to shortage of cloth and offering their prayers behind the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). One of the proclaimers said: O womenfolk, do not lift your heads till men raise (them).
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When women ask permission for going to the mosque, do not prevent them.
narrator: Salim narrated it from his father ('Abdullah bin Umar):

text: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say: Don't prevent your women from going to the mosque when they seek your permission. Bilal bin 'Abdullah said: By Allah, we shall certainly prevent them. On this 'Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ turned towards him and reprimanded him to harshly as I had never heard him do before. He ('Abdullah bin Umar) said: I am narrating to you that which comes from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and you (have the audicity) to say: By Allah, we shall certainly prevent them.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: 'The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Do not prevent the maid-servants of Allah from going to the mosque.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say: When your women seek your permission for going to the mosque, you grant them (permission).
narrator: lbn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Do not prevent women from going to the mosque at night. A boy said to 'Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ : We would never let them go out, that they may not be caught in evil. He (the narrator) said: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reprimanded him and said.. I am saying that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said this, but you say: We would not allow!
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: Grant permission to women for going to the mosque in the night. His son who was called Waqid said: Then they would make mischief. He (the narrator) said: He thumped his (son's) chest and said: I am narrating to you the hadith of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), and you say: No!
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Do not deprive women of their share of the mosques, when they seek permission from you. Bilal said: By Allah, we would certainly prevent them. 'Abdullah said: I say that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said it and you say: We would certainly prevent them!
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When any one of you (women) participates in the 'Isha' prayer, she should not perfume herself that night.
narrator: Zainab Thaqafiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said to us: When any one of you comes to the mosque, she should not apply perfume.
narrator: Zainab, the wife of Abdullah (bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ), reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Whoever (woman) fumigates herself with perfume should not join us in the 'Isha' prayer.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:

text: I heard 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say: If the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had seen what new things the women have introduced (in their way of life) he would have definitely prevented them from going to the mosque, as the women of Bani Isra'il were prevented. She said: "Yes."
narrator: Amra, daughter of Abdul-Rahman, reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Yahya bin Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters.
So far as these words of (Allah) Glorious and High are concerned: And utter not thy prayer loudly, not be low in it relate to supplication (du'a').
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Hisham with the same chain of transmitters.

They (the Jinn) asked about their provision, and they were the Jinn of al-jazira, up to the end of the hadith, and the words of Sha'bi have been directly transmitted from the hadith of Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ.
narrator: This hadith has been reported by Dawud with the same chain of transmitters up to the word (s): The traces of their embers. Sha'bi said:

text: It was narrated from 'Abdullah from the prophet п·ә, up to the words: and the traces of their fires; he did not mention what come after that (from no. 1008).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah from the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) up to the words:

text: I was not with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) but I wish I were with him.
narrator: Abdullah (bin Mas'ud) said:

text: I asked Masruq who informed the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the night when they heard the Qur'an. He said: Your father, Ibn Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ , narrated it to me that a tree informed him about that.
narrator: Ma'n reported.. I heard it from my father who said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led us in prayer and recited in the first two rak'ahs of the noon and afternoon prayers Surat al-Fitiha and two (other) surahs. And he would sometimes recite loud enough for us the verses. He would prolong the first rak'ah more than the second. And he acted similarly in the morning prayer.
narrator: Abu Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) would recite in the first two rak'ahs of the noon and afternoon prayers the opening chapter of the Book and another surah. He would sometimes recite loud enough to make audible to us the verse and would recite in the last two rak'ahs Surat al-Faitiha (only).
narrator: Abu Qatada reported it on the authority of his father:

text: We used to estimate how long Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) stood in the noon and afternoon prayers, and we estimated hat he stood in the first two rak'ahs of the noon prayer as long as it takes to recite Alif Lam Mim, Tanzil, i. e. as-Sajda. We estimated that he stood half that time in the last two rak'ahs; that he stood in the first two of the afternoon as long as he did in the last two at noon; and in the last two of the afternoon prayer about half that time. Abu Bakr in his narration has made no mention of Alif Lam Mim, Tanzil, but said: As long as it takes to recite thirty verses.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in every rak'ah of the first two rak'ahs of the noon prayer about thirty verses and in the last two about fifteen verses or half (of the first rak'ah) and in every rak'ah of the 'Asr prayer of the first two rak'ahs about fifteen verses and in the last two verses half (of the first ones).
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported:

text: The people of Kufa complained to Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ about Sa'id and they made a mention of his prayer. 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ sent for him. He came to him. He ('Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ) totd him that the people had found fault with his prayer. He said: I lead them in prayer in accorance with the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). I make no decrease in it. I make them stand for a longer time in the first two (rak'ahs) and shorten it in the last two. Upon this 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ remarked: This is what I deemed of thee, O Abu Ishaq.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith his been narrated by 'Abdul-Malik with the same chain of transmitters.

'Umar said to Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ: They complain against you in every matter, even in prayer. He (Sa'd) said: I prolong (standing) in the first two (rak'ahs) and shorten it in the last two, and I make no negligence in following the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). He ('Umar) remarked: This is what is expected of you, or, that is what I deemed of you.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: (Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said): These bedouins presume to teach me prayer.
narrator: This hadith is narrated by Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ but with the addition of these words:

text: The noon prayer would start and one would go to al-Baqi' and after having relieved himself he would perform ablution and then come, while the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) would be in the first rak'ah, because he would prolong it so much.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: I came to Abu Sa'id al-Khudri вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢand he was surrounded by people. When the people departed from him I said: I am not going to ask you what these people have been asking you. I want to ask you about the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). He (Abu Sa'id) said: There is no good for you in this. He (Qaz'a), however, repeated (his demand). He then said: The noon prayer would start and one of us would go to Baqi' and, having relieved himself, would come to his home, then perform ablution and go to the mosque, and (he would find) The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) in the first rak'ah.
narrator: Qaz'a reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) led us in the morning prayer in Mecca and began Sarat al-Mu'minin (xxiii ) but when he came to the mention of Moses and Aaron (verse. 45) or to the mention of Jesus (verse 50), a cough got the better of him, and he bowed. 'Abdullah bin Sa'ib вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ was present there, and in the hadith narrated by Abdul-Razzaq (the words are): He cut short (the recitation) and bowed.
narrator: Abdullah bin Sa'ib вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recite in the morning prayer Wa'l-lail-i-idhd 'As'asa (ixxxi. 17).
narrator: Amr bin Huraith ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I said prayer and the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) led it and he recited Qaf. (I.). By the Glorious Qur'an, till he recited and the tall palm trees (l. 10). I wanted to repeat it but I could not follow its significance.
narrator: Qutba bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: He had heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reciting in the morning prayer this: And the tall palm trees having flower spikes piled one above another (Al-Qur'an 50:10).
narrator: Qutba bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He said the morning prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and he recited in the first rak'ah: And the tall palm trees having flower spikes piled one above another (l. 10) or perhaps Surah Qaf.
narrator: Ziyad bin 'Ilaqa reported it on the authority of his uncle:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in the morning prayer Qaf. By the Glorious Quran. and his prayer afterward shortened.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢabout the prayer of the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…). He said: He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) shortened the prayer and he did not pray like these people then, and he informed me that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite Qaf. By the (Glorious) Qur'an, and a passage of similar length.
narrator: Simak said:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in the noon prayer: By the night when it envelopes (xcii.), and in the afternoon like this, but he prolonged the morning prayer as compared to that (noon and afternoon prayers).
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to recite in the noon prayer: Glorify the name of thy Most High Lord in the morning prayer longer than this (lxxxvii.)
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in the morning prayer from sixty to one hundred verses.
narrator: Abu Barza вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to recite from sixty to one hundred verses in the morning prayer.
narrator: Abu Barza Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: Umm al-Fadl daughter of al-Harith heard him reciting: By those sent forth to spread goodness (lxxvii.). (Upon this) she remarked: O my son, you reminded me by the recitation of this surah (the fact) that it was the last surah that I heard from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and he recited it in the evening prayer.
narrator: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:

text: And he did not lead the player after this till his death.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters but with this addition:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reciting Surat al-Tur (Mountain) (lii) in the evening prayer.
narrator: Jubair bin Mut'im reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters.

I said: Messenger of Allah, which mosque was set up first on the earth? He said: Al-Masjid al-Haram (the sacred). I (again) said: Then which next? He said: It was the Masjid Aqsa. I (again) said: How long the space of time (between their setting up)? He (the Holy Prophet) said: It was forty years. And whenever the time comes for prayer, pray there, for that is a mosque; and in the hadith transmitted by Abu Kamil (the words are): Whenever time comes for prayer, pray, for that is a mosque (for you).
narrator: Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I used to read the Qur'an with my father in the vestibule (before the door of the mosque). When I recited the ayat (verses) concerning prostration, he prostrated himself. I said to him: Father, do you prostrate yourself in the path? He said: I heard Abu Dharr saying: I asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the mosque that was first set up on the earth. He said: Masjid Harim. I said: Then which next? He said: The Masjid al-Aqsa. I said: How long is the space of time between the two? He said: Forty years. He (then) further said: The earth is a mosque for you, so wherever you are at the time of prayer, pray there.
narrator: Ibrahim bin Yazid al-Tayml reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Jabir bin 'Abdullah related that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said, and he related like this.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: We have been made to excel (other) people in three (things): Our rows have been made like the rows of the angels and the whole earth has been made a mosque for us, and its dust has been made a purifier for us in case water is not available. And he mentioned another characteristic too.
narrator: Hudhaifa вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said like this.
narrator: Hudhaifa вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I have been given superiority over the other prophets in six respects: I have been given words which are concise but comprehensive in meaning; I have been helped by terror (in the hearts of enemies): spoils have been made lawful to me: the earth has been made for me clean and a place of worship; I have been sent to all mankind and the line of prophets is closed with me.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: I have been commissioned with words which are concise but comprehensive in meaning; I have been helped by terror (in the hearts of enemies): and while I was asleep I was brought the keys of the treasures of the earth which were placed in my hand. And Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ added: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) has left (for his heavenly home) and you are now busy in getting them.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saying a hadith like that of Yunus.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: This hadith has been narrated by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢby another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: I have been helped by terror (in the heart of the enemy) ; I have been given words which are concise but comprehensive in meaning; and while I was asleep I was brought the keys of the treasures of the earth which were placed in my hand.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: That is what Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported to us from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and he narrated (some) a hadith one of which is that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: I have been helped by terror (in the hearts of enemies) and I have been given words which are concise but comprehensive in meaning.
narrator: Hammam bin Munabbih reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to pray in the folds of the sheep and goats before the mosque was built.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard from Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ a narration like this from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Abu al-Tiyyah reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I said prayer with the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) turning towards Bait-ul-Maqdis for sixteen months till this verse of Surah Baqara wis revealed: And wherever you are turn your faces towards it (ii. 144). This verse was revealed when the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said prayer. A person amongst his people passed by the people of Ansar as they were engaged in prayer. He narrated to them (this command of Allah) and they turned their faces towards the Ka'ba.
narrator: Al-Bara' bin 'Azib вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ saying: We prayed with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) (with our faces) towards Bait-ul-Maqdis for sixteen months or seventeen months. Then we were made to change (our direction) towards the Ka'ba.
narrator: Abu Ishaq reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: As the people were praying at Quba' a man came to them and said: It has been revealed to file Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) during the night and he has been directed to turn towards the Ka'ba. So turn towards it. Their faces were towards Syria and they turned round towards Ka'ba.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: As the people were engaged in the morning prayer a man came to them. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to pray towards Bait-ul-Maqdis, that it was revealed (to him): Indeed We see the turning of the face to heaven, wherefore We shall assuredly cause thee to turn towards Qibla which shall please thee. So turn thy face towards the sacred Mosque (Ka'ba) (ii. 144). A person from Banu Salama was going; (he found the people) in ruk'u (while) praying the dawn prayer and they had said one rak'ah. He said in a loud voice: Listen! the Qibla has been changed and they turned towards (the new) Qibla (Ka'ba) in that very state.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Umm Habiba and Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ made a mention before the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) of a church which they had seen in Abyssinia and which had pictures in it. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When a pious person amongst them (among the religious groups) dies they build a place of worship on his grave, and then decorate it with such pictures. They would be the worst of creatures on the Day of judgment in the sight of Allah.
narrator: A'isha reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A'isha reported: They (some Companions of the Holy Prophet) were conversing with one another in the presence of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) (during his last) illness. Umm Salama and Umm Habiba made a mention of the church and then (the hadith was) narrated.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The wives of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) made a mention of the church which they had seen in Abyssinia which was called Mariya, and the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said during his illness from which he never recovered: Allah cursed the Jews and the Christians that they took the graves of their prophets as mosques. She ('A'isha) reported: Had it not been so, his (Prophet's) grave would have been in an open place, but it could not be due to the fear that it may not be taken as a mosque. According to the report of Ibn Abi Shaibah: Were it not for that...'' and he did not mention: "She said."
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Let Allah destroy the Jews for they have taken the graves of their apostles as places of worship.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Let there be curse of Allah upon the Jews and the Christians for they have taken the graves of their apostles as places of worship.
narrator: Abu Huraira reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: As the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was about to breathe his last, he drew his sheet upon his face and when he felt uneasy, he uncovered his face and said in that very state: Let there be curse upon the Jews and the Christians that they have taken the graves of their apostles as places of worship. He in fact warned (his men) against what they (the Jews and the Christians) did.
narrator: A'isha and Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) five days before his death and he said: I stand acquitted before Allah that I took any one of you as friend, for Allah has taken me as His friend, as he took Ibrahim as His friend. Had I taken any one of my Ummah as a friend, I would have taken Abu Bakr as a friend. Beware of those who preceded you and used to take the graves of their prophets and righteous men as places of worship, but you must not take graves as mosques; I forbid you to do that.
narrator: Jundub вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: 'Uthman bin 'Affan ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢlistened to the opinion of the people (which was not favourable) when he rebuilt the mosque of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). Thereupon he said: You have not been fair to me for I have heard from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saying: He who built a mosque for Allah, the Exalted, Allah would build for him a house in Paradise. Bukair said: I think he (the Holy Prophet) said: While he seeks the pleasure of Allah (by building the mosque). And in the narration of Ibn 'Isa (the words are): (a house) like that (mosque) in Paradise.
narrator: Ubaidullah al-Khaulini reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When 'Uthman bin 'Affan вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ intended to build the mosque (of the Prophet) the people did not approve of it. They liked that it should be kept in the same state. Thereupon he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) say: He who built a mosque for Allah, Allah would build a house for him like it in Paradise.
narrator: Mahmud bin Labid вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: "It is as if I can see the interlaced fingers of the messenger of Allah as was bowing."
narrator: It was narrated from Alqamah and Al-Aswad that they entered upon Abdullah... a hadith similar to that of Muaviyah (no. 1191). According to the hadith of Ibn-e-Mushir and Jarir:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Have (people) behind you said prayer? They said: Yes. He stood between them ('Alqama and Aswad). One was on his right aide and the other was on his left. We then bowed and placed our hands on our knees. He struck our hands and then putting his hands together, palm to palm, placed them between his thighs. When he completed the prayer he said: This is how the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to do.
narrator: Alqama and Aswad reported that they went to 'Abdullah. He said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I said prayer by the side of my father and placed my hands between my knees. My father said to me: Place your hands on your knees. I repeated that (the previous act) for the second time, and he struck at my hands and said: We have been forbidden to do so and have been commanded to place our palms on the knees.
narrator: Mus'ab bin Sa'd reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We have been forbidden from it and no mention of that has been made what follows it.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Abu Ya'fur with the same chain of transmitters up to these words:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I bowed and my hands were in this state, i.e. they were put together, palm to palm, and were placed between his thighs. My father said: We used to do like this but were later on commanded to place them on the knees.
narrator: Ibn Sa'd reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I said prayer by the side of my father. When I bowed I intertwined my fingers and placed them between my knees. He struck my hands. When he completed the prayer he said: We used to do that but then were commanded to lift (our palms) to the knees.
narrator: Mus'ab bin Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We asked Ibn Abbas about sitting on one's buttocks (in prayer). (ala alqad mein) He said: It is sunnah. We said to him: We find it a sort of cruelty to the foot. Ibn 'Abbas said: It is the sunnah of your Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Tawus reported ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: This hadith has been narrated by Yahya bin Abu Kathir with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We used to greet the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) while he was engaged in prayer and he would respond to our greeting. But when we returned from the Negus we greeted him and he did not respond to us; so we said: Messenger of Allah. we used to greet you when you were engaged in prayer and you would respond to us. He replied: Prayer demands whole attention.
narrator: Abdullah (bin Masu'd) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: This hadith has been reported by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We used to talk while engaged in prayer and a person talked with a companion on his side in prayer till (this verse) was revealed: And stand before Allah in devout obedience (ii, 238) and we were commanded to observe silence (in prayer) and were forbidden to speak.
narrator: Zaid bin Arqam ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Isma'il bin Abu Khalid.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sent me on an errand. I (having done the business assigned to me came back and) joined him as he was going (on a ride). Qutaiba said that he was saying prayer while he rode. I greeted him. He gestured to me. When he completed the prayer. he called me and said: You greeted me just now while I was engaged in prayer. (Qutaiba said): His (Prophet's face) was towards the east, as he was praying.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We were in the company of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), and he sent me on an errand, and when I came back (I saw him) saying prayer on his ride and his face was not turned towards Qibla. I greeted him but he did not respond to me. As he completed the prayer, he said: Nothing prevented me from responding to your greeting but the fact that I was praying.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: This hadith that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) sent Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ on an errand has been reported by him through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: A highly wicked one amongst the Jinn escaped yesternight to interrupt my prayer, but Allah gave me power over him, so I seized him and intended to tie him to one of the pillars of the mosque in order that you, all together or all, might look at him, but I remembered the supplication of my brother Sulaiman: My Lord, forgive me, give me such a kingdom as will not be possible for anyone after me..", so Allah caused him (the jinn) to be defeated." Ibn Mansoor said: Shuba narrated from Muhammad bin Ziyad.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: It was narrated from Shubah with this chain. In the hadith of Ibn Jafar it does not say, I grabbed him by the neck." Ibn Abi Shaibah said in his report: "So I pushed him away."
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying the prayer while he was carrying Umama, daughter of Zainab, daughter of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). and Abu'l-'As bin al-Rabi' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ . When he stood up, he took her up and when he prostrated he put her down, Yahya said: Malik replied in the affirmative.
narrator: Abu Qatadi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I saw the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) leading the people in prayer with Umima ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, daughter of Abu'l-'As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and Zainab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , daughter of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), on his shoulder. When he bowed, he put her down, and when he got up after prostration, he lifted her again.
narrator: Abu Qatada al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) leading the people in prayer with Umama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ daughter of Abu'l-'As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ on his neck; and when he prostrated he put her down.
narrator: Abu Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: As we were sitting in the mosque, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) came to us, and the rest of the hadith is the same except that he made no mention that he led people in this prayer.
narrator: Abu Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: They (the people) came to Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and they asked him of what thing the pulpit of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was made, and they quoted a Hadith like that of Ibn e Abi Hazim (no. 1216).
narrator: Abu Hazim reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He forbade keeping one's hand on one's waist while praying, and in the narration of Abu Bakr (the words are): The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade to do so.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ):
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) mentioning the removal of pebbles from the ground where he prostrated himself. He (the Prophet) said: It you must do so, do it only once.
narrator: Mu'aiqib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ quoted:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: They asked the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the removal of (pebbles) in prayer, whereupon he said: If you do it, do it only once.
narrator: Mu'aiqib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: It was narrated by Hisham with this chain, and he said: "Muayqib told me,"
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Muayqib told me that concerning a man who smoothes the ground where he is going to prostrate, the Messenger of Allah said: 'If you must do that, then do it only once."'
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Salamah said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw spittle on the wall towards Qibla, and scratched it away and then turning to the people said: When any one of you prays, he must not spit in front of him, for Allah is in front of him when he is engaged in prayer.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw sputum sticking to the Qibla wall of the mosque, a hadith similar to that of Malik (no. 1223).
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw sputum sticking to the Qibla of the mosque. He scratched it off with a pebble and then forbade spitting on the right side or in front, but (it is permissible) to spit on the left side or under the left foot.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw sputum, and a hadith similar to that of Ibn Uyaynah.
narrator: Abu Huraira and Abu Sa'id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ narrated:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( п·ә) saw spittle or snot or sputum, sticking to the wall towards Qibla and scratched it off.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saw some sputum in the direction of the Qibla of the mosque. He turned towards people and said: How Is it that someone amongst you stands before his Lord and then spits out in front of Him? Does any one of you like that he should be made to stand in front of someone and then spit at his face? So when any one of you spits, he must spit on his left side under his foot. But if he does not find (space to spit) he should do like this. Qasim (one of the narrators) spat in his cloth and then folded it and rubbed it.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I perceive as if I am looking at the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) folding up a part of his cloth with another one.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When any one of you is engaged in prayer, he is holding intimate conversation with his Lord, so none of you must spit in front of him, or towards his right side, but towards his left side under his foot.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Spitting in a mosque is a sin, and its expiation is that it should be buried.
narrator: Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say: Spitting in the mosque is a sin, and its expiation is that it should be buried.
narrator: Shu'ba reported I asked Qatada about spitting, in the mosque. He said: I heard Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsay:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The deeds of my people, good and bad, were presented before me, and I found the removal of something objectionable from the road among their good deeds, and the sputum mucus left unburied in the mosque among their evil deeds.
narrator: Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I said prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and saw him spitting and rubbing it off with his shoe.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shakhkhir reported on the authority of his father that he said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He said prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), and he spat and then rubbed it off with his left shoe.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shakhkhir narrated it on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Did the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) pray while putting on the shoes? He said: Yes.
narrator: Sa'd bin Yazid reported: I said to Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I said to Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ like (that mentioned above).
narrator: Sa'd bin Yazid Abu Mas'ama reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) prayed in a garment which had designs over it, so he (the Holy Prophet) said: Take it to Abu Jahm and bring me a plain blanket from him, because its designs have distracted me.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stood for prayer with a garment which had designs over it. He looked at these designs and after completing the prayer said: Take this garment to Abu Jahm bin Hudhaifa and bring me a blanket for it has distracted me just now.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah (п·ә) had a garment which had designs upon it and this distracted him in prayer. He gave it to Abu Jahm and took a plain garment in its place which is known anbijaniya.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: When the supper is brought and the prayer begins, one, should first take food
narrator: Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When the supper is brought before you, and it is also the time to say prayer, first take food before saying evening prayer and do not hasten (to prayer, leaving aside the food).
narrator: Anas bin Malik вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith similar to that narrated by Ibn 'Uyaynah (no. 1241), from Az-Zuhri, from Anas  вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ was narrated from Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , from the prophet п·ә.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When the supper is served to any one of you and the prayer also begins. (in such a case) first take supper, and do not make haste (for prayer) till you have (taken the food).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith like this has been narrated from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on the authority of Ibn 'Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ with another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Abdullah bin 'Atiq narrated from the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) on the authority of 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , but he made no mention of the account of Qasim.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said during the battle of Khaybar: He who ate of this plant, i. e. garlic, should not come to the mosques. In the narration of Zubair, there is only a mention of battle and not of Khaybar.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who eats of this (offensive) plant must not approach our mosque, till its odor dies: (plant signifies) garlic.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwas asked about the garlic; he stated that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said: He who eats of this plant (garlic) should not approach us and pray along with us.
narrator: Ibn Suhaib reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who eats of this plant (garlic) should not approach our mosque and should not harm us with the odour of garlic.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer
