text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade eating of onions and leek. When we were overpowered by a desire (to eat) we ate them. Upon this he (the Holy Prophet) said: He who eats of this offensive plant must not approach our mosque, for the angels are harmed by the same things as men.
narrator: Jabir вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who eats garlic or onion should remain away from us or from our mosque and stay in his house. A kettle was brought to him which had (cooked) vegetables in it, He smelt (offensive) odour in it. On asking he was informed of the vegetables (cooked in it). He said: Take it to such and such Companion. When he saw it, he also disliked eating it. (Upon this). he (the Holy Prophet) said: You may eat it, for I converse with one with whom you do not converse.
narrator: Jabir вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) mying: He who eats of this (offensive) plant, i.e. garlic, and sometimes he said: He who eats onion and garlic and leek, should not approach our mosque for the angels are harmed by the same things as the children of Adam.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He who eats of this plant, i. e. garlic, should not come to us in our mosque, and he made no mention of onions or leek.
narrator: Ibn Juraij has narrated it with the same chain of transmitters:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) along with his Companions happened to pass by a field in which onions were sown. The people stopped there and ate out of that, but some of them did not eat. Then they (Propbet's Companions) went to him. He (first) called those who had not eaten the onions and kept the others (who had taken onions) waiting till its odour vanished.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: This hadith has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: If anyone hears a man crying out in the mosque about something he has lost, he should say: May Allah not restore it to you, for the mosques were not built for this.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying like this.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A man cried out in the mosque saying: Who had called out for the red camel? Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: May it not be restored to you! The mosques are built for what they are meant.
narrator: Sulaiman bin Buraida narrated it on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said prayer a man stood up and said: Who called for a red camel? (Upon this) the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: May it not be restored to you! The mosques are built for what they are meant.
narrator: Sulaiman bin Buraida reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A Bedouin came when the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had completed the morning prayer. He thrust his head in the door of the mosque, and then the hadith (as narrated above) was narrated. This hadith has been reported by another chain of transmitters.
narrator: Ibn Buraida narrated it on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When any one of you stands up to pray, the devil comes to him and confuses him to that he does not know how much he has prayed. If any one of you has such an experience he should perform two prostrations while sitting down (in qa'da).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: This hadith has been narrated by al-Zubri with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When there is a call to prayer the devil runs back breaking the wind so that he may not hear the call, and when the call is complete he comes back. And when the takbir is pronounced he again runs back, and when takbir is over he comes back and distracts a man saying: Remember such and such, remember such and such, referring to something the man did not have in his mind. with the result that he does not know how much he has prayed; so when any one of you is not sure how much he has prayed. he should perform two prostrations while sitting (qa'da).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The devil takes to his heels breaking wind when the prayer begins. and the rest is the same but with this addition: He (the devil) makes him think of pleasant things (or things productive of enjoyment) and of the things wished for, and reminds him of such needs which he had forgotten.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led us two rak'ahs of prayer in one of the (obligatory) prayers and then got up and did not sit. and the people stood up along with him. When he finished the prayer and we expected him to pronounce salutation. he said: Allah is Most Great while sitting and made two prostrations before salutation and then pronounced (the, final) salutation.
narrator: Abdullah bin Buhaina вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stood up in the noon prayer (though) he hadith sit (after the two rak'ahs). When he completed the prayer he performed two prostrations and said, Allah is the Most Great in each prostration, while he was sitting before pronouncing salutation, and the people performed prostration along with him. That was a compensation for he had forgotten to observe jalsa (after two rak'ahs).
narrator: Abdullah bin Buhaina al-Asadi вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, the ally of Abual-Muttalib, reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stood up (at the end of two rak'ahs) when he had to sit and proceeded on with the prayer. But when he was at the end of the prayer, he performed a prostration before the salutation and then pronounced the salutation.
narrator: Abdullah bin Malik ibn Buhaina al-Asadi вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When any one of you is in doubt about his prayer and he does Dot know how much he has prayed, three or four (rak'ahs). he should cast aside his doubt and base his prayer on what he is sure of. then perform two prostrations before giving salutations. If he has prayed five rak'ahs, they will make his prayer an even number for him, and if he has prayed exactly four, they will be humiliation for the devil.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He should perform two prostrations before the salutation, as it was mentioned by Sulaiman bin Bilal.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Zaid bin Aslam with the same chain of transmitters and he said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: "Let him try to work out what is correct."
narrator: It was narrated from Mansur with this chain (no. 1274) And in the report of Ibn Bashr is:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He should aim at correct (prayer) and it is advisable.
narrator: This hadith is reported by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters, but with these words:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He should aim at what is correct and complete.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters with the words:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He should aim at correctness and that is right.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters and said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He should aim at what is according to him correct.
narrator: This hadith has been reported by Mansur with the same chain of transwitters and he said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said five rak'ahs of the noon prayer and when he completed the prayer, It was said to him: Has there been (commanded) an addition In prayer? He said: What is it? They said: You have said five rak'ahs, so he performed two prostrations.
narrator: Abdullah (bin Mas'ud) reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) had led them five rak'ahs in prayer.
narrator: Alqama reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led us five (rak'ahs in prayer). We said: Messenger of Allah, has the prayer been extended? He said: What is the matter? They said: You have said five (rak'ahs). He (the Holy Prophet) said: Verily I am a human being like you. I remember as you remember and I forget just as you forget. He then performed two prostrations as (compensation of) forgetfulness.
narrator: Abdullah (bin Mas'ud) вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said prayer and he omitted or committed (something). Ibrahim (one of the narrators of this hadith) said: It is my doubt, and it was said: Messenger of Allah, has there been any addition to the prayer? He (the Holy Prophet  п·ә) said: Verily I am a human being like you. I forget just as you forget so when any one of you forgets, he must perform two prostrations, and he (the Holy Prophet  п·ә) was sitting and then the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) turned (his face towards the Qibla) and performed two prostrations.
narrator: Abdullah (bin Mas'ud) вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) performed two prostrations for forgetfulness after salutation and talking.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We prayed along with the Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) and he committed or omitted (something). Ibrahim said: By Allah, this is a misgiving of mine only. We said: Messenger of Allah, is there something new about the prayer? He (the Holy Prophet) said: No. We told him about what he had done. He (the Holy Prophet) said: When a man commits or omits (something in prayer), he should perform two prostrations, and he then himself performed two prostrations.
narrator: Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led us in one of the evening prayers. And this hadith was narrated like one transmitted by Sufyan.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said two rak'ahs of the noon prayer and then gave salutation when a man from Band Sulaim came to him and said: Messenger of Allah. has the prayer been shortened, or have you forgotten? -and the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I offered with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) the noon prayer and the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) gave salutation after two rak'ahs. A person from Bani Sulaim stood up, and the rest of the hadith was narrated as mentioned above.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said the afternoon prayer and gave the salutation. at the end of three rak'ahs and then went into his house. A man called al-Khirbaq, who bad long aims, got up and went to him, and addressed him as Messenger of Allah and mentioned to him what he had done. He came out angrily trailing his mantle, and when he came to the people he said: Is this man telling the truth? They said: Yes. He then said one rak'ah and then gave salutation and then performed two prostrations and then gave salutation.
narrator: Imran bin Husain вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said three rak'ahs of the 'Asr prayer and then got up and went to his apartment. A man possessing large arms stood up and said: Messenger of Allah, bias the player been shortened? He came out angrily, and said the rak'ah which he had omitted and then gave salutation. then performed two prostrations of forgetfulness and then gave salutation.
narrator: Imran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) while reciting the Qur'an recited its surah containing sajda, and he performed prostration and we also prostrated along with him (but we were so overcrowded) that some of us could not find a place for our forehead (when prostrating ourselves).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Sometimes the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited the Qur'an, and would pass by (recite) the verse of sajda and performed prostration and he did this along with us, but we were so crowded in his company that none of us could find a place for performing prostration. (and it was done on occasions) other than prayer.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) recited (Surat) al Najm and performed prostration during its recital and all those who were along with him also prostrated themselves except one old man who took a handful of pebbles or dust in his palm and lifted it to his forehead and said: This is sufficient for me. 'Abdullah said: I saw that he was later killed in a state of unbelief.
narrator: Abdullah (bin 'Umar) reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: There should be no recital along with the Imam in anything, and alleged that he recited: By the star when it sets (Surah Najm) before the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and he did not prostrate himself.
narrator: Ata' bin Yasar reported that he had asked Zaid bin Thabit вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ about recital along with the Imam, to which he said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ recited before them: hen the heaven burst asunder (al-Qur'an, lxxxiv. 1) and performed prostration. After completing (the prayer) he informed them that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) has prostrated himself at it (this verse).
narrator: Abu Salama bin 'Abual-Rahman reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Salama on the authority of Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ .
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We performed prostration along with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (as he recited these verses: ) When the heaven burst asunder and Read in the name of Thy Lord (al-Qur'an, xcvi. 1).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) prostrated himself (while reciting these verses). When the heaven burst asunder ; Read in the name of Thy Lord .
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Abdul-Rahman al-Araj on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I said the night prayer along with Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢand -as he recited: When the heaven burst asunder, he performed prostration. Isaid to him: What prostration is this? He said: I prostrated myself (on this occasion of recital) behind Abu'I-Qasim (Muhammad. may peace be upon him), and Iwould go on doing this till I meet him (in the next world). Ibn 'Abu al-A'la said: (Abu Huraira uttered this: ) I would not abandon performing prostration.
narrator: Abu Rafi' reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: This hadith has been narrated by Tamimi with the same chain of transmitters except for this that they made no mention of: Behind Abu'l-Qasim ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I saw Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢperforming prostration (while reciting this verse:) When the heaven burst asunder. I said to him: Do you prostrate yourself (while reciting) i? He said: Yes, I saw my best Friend ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) prostrating himself on (the recital of this verse) and I shall continue prostrating till I meet him. Shu'ba asked: Do you mean (by Friend) the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ)? He said: Yes.
narrator: Abu Rafi' reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sat in prayer. he placed the left foot between his thigh and shank and stretched the right foot and placed his left hand on his left knee and placed his right hand on his right thigh, and raised his finger.
narrator: Abdullah bin Zubair вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ narrated on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sat for supplication, i. e. tashahhud (blessing and supplication), he placed his right hand on his right thigh and his left hand on his left thigh, and pointed with his forefinger, and placed his thumb on his (milddle) finger, and covered his knee with the palm of his left hand..
narrator: Abdullah bin Zubair narrated on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sat for tashahhud he placed his left hand on his left knee. and his right hand on his right knee. and he raised his right finger, which is next to the thumb, making supplication in this way, and he stretched his left hand on his left knee.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) sat for tashahhud, he placed his left hand on his left knee and placed his right hand on his right knee, and he formed a ring like (fifty-three) and pointed with his finger of attestation.
narrator: Another version on the authority of Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsays:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: 'Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ saw me playing with pebbles during prayer. After finishing the prayer he forbade me (to do it) and said: Do as the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to do. I said: How did Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) do? He said that he (the Messenger of Allah) sat at tashahhud, placed his right palm on the right thigh and closed all his fingers and pointed with the help of finger next to the thumb, and placed his left palm on his left thigh.
narrator: Ali bin 'Abdul-Rahman al-Mu'awi reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I prayed beside Ibn-Umar...." and he mentioned a hadith similar to that of Maalik (no. 1312), adding: "Sufyan said: Yahya bin Saeed narrated it to us from Muslim, then Muslim narrated it to me.
narrator: It was narrated (through another chain) that Ali bin ''Abdur Rehman Al-Muawi said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: There was an Amir in Mecca who pronounced taslim twice. Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid: Where did he get this sunnah? Al-Hakam said: There is a hadith to the effect that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) did like It.
narrator: Abu Ma'mar reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: An Amir or a person pronounced taslim twice. 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ said: Where did he get this sunnah?
narrator: Abdullah вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) pronouncing taslim on his right and on his left till I saw the whiteness of his cheek
narrator: Amir bin Sa'd reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We used to know that Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had finished his prayer when we heard the takbir (Allah-O-Akbar).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: We knew the finishing of the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) through takbir. 'Amr (bin Dinar) said: I made a mention of it to Abu Mabad and he rejected it and said: I never narrated it to you. 'Amr said: He did narrate it before this.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Dhikr (mentioning the name of Allah) in a loud voice after obligatory prayers was (a common practice) during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) ; and when I heard that I came to knew that they (the people) had finished the prayer
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) entered my house when a Jewess was with me and she was saying: Do you know that you would be put to trial in the grave? The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) trembled (on hearing this) and said: It is the Jews only who would-be put to trial. 'A'isha said: We passed some nights and then the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Do you know that it has been revealed to me: You would be put to trial in the grave ? 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said: 1 heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) seeking refuge from the torment of the grave after this.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) seeking refuge from the torment of the grave after this (after the revelation).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Never did he (the Holy Prophet) say prayer after this in which I did not hear him seeking refuge from the torment of the grave.
narrator: Masruq reported this hadith on the authority of 'A'isha who said:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: 1 heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) seeking refuge from the trial of Dajjal (Antichrist) in prayer.
narrator: A'isha reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: When any one of you utters tashahhud (in prayer) he must seek refuge with Allah from four (trials) and should thus say: O Allah! I seek refuge with Thee from the torment of the Hell, from the torment of the grave, from the trial of life and death and from the evil of the trial of Masih al-Dajjal (Antichrist).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to supplicate in prayer thus: O Allah! I seek refuge with Thee from the torment of the grave, and I seek refuge with Thee from the trial of the Masih al-Dajjal (Antichrist) and I seek refuge with Thee from the trial of life and death. O Allah! I seek refuge with Thee from sin and debt. She ('A'isha) reported: Someone said to him - (the Holy Prophet п·ә): Messenger of Allah! why is it that you so often seek refuge from debt? He said: When a (person) incurs debt, (he is obliged) to tell lies and break promise.
narrator: A'isha, the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When any one of you completes the last tashahhud. he should seek refuge with Allah from four (trials). I.e. from the torment of Hell, from the torment of grave, from the trial of life and death, and from the mischief of Masih at-Dajjal (Antichrist).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: But he said "When one of you has finished the Tashahhud," and he did not say: "final."
narrator: It was narrated from Al-Awzai with this chain (no. 1326):
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: O Allah! I seek refuge with Thee from the torment of the grave, and the torment of Hell, and the trial of life and death and the mischief of Masih al-Dajjal.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Seek refuge with Allah from the torment of Hell, seek refuge with Allah from the torment of the grave, and seek refuge with Allah from the trial of Masih al-Dajjal and seek refuge with Allah from the trial of life and death.
narrator: Abu Huraira reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Ibn Tawus from his father on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by A'raj on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ .
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to seek refuge from the torment of the grave, torment of Hell and the trial of Dajjal.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) finished his prayer. He begged forgiveness three times and said: O Allah! Thou art Peace, and peace comes from Thee; Blessed art Thou, O Possessor of Glory and Honour. Walid reported: I said to Auza'i: How is the seeking of forgiveness? He replied: You should say: I beg forgiveness from Allah, I beg forgiveness from Allah.
narrator: Thauban ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) pronounced salutation, he salutation longer than it took him to say: O Allah: Thou art Peace, and peace comes from Thee, blessed art Thou, Possessor of Glory and ]Honour; and in the narration of Ibn Numair the words are: O Possessor of Glory and Honour.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) used to say: 0 Possessor of Glory and Honour.
narrator: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Abdullah bin Harith on the authority of A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ except for the words:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) finished the prayer and pronounced salutation he uttered (this supplication): There is no god but Allah. He is alone, Who has no partner. To Him belongs the sovereignty and to Him praise is due and He is Potent over every. thing. O Allah! no one can withhold what Thou givest, or give what Thou with holdest, and the riches cannot avail a wealthy person with Thee.
narrator: Mughira bin Shu'ba вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwrote to Mu'awiya вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Mughira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢgave me dictation of it and 1 wrote it to Mu'awiya вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: A hadith like this has been narrated by Mughira bin Shu'ba вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwith another chain of transmitters. Abu Bakr and Abu Kuraib narrated in their narration (that Warrad reported):
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: When the salutation is pronounced. and the rest of the hadith is the same except this that he made no mention of: He is Potent over everything.
narrator: Warrad, the freed slave of Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, reported: Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwrote to Mu'awiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ(it was Warrad who wrote this letter for him, i. e. Mughira):
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Mu'awiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ wrote to Mughira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ (the contents) of the hadith as transmitted by Mansur and A'mash.
narrator: Warrad, the scribe of Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ , reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Write to me anything which you heard from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). So he (Mughira) wrote to him (Mu'awiya): I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) uttering (these words) at the completion of prayer: There is no god but Allah. He is alone and there is no partner with Him. Sovereignty belongs to Him and to Him is praise due and He is Potent over everything. O Allah! no one can withhold what Thou givest, or give what Thou withholdest, and riches cannot avail a wealthy person with Thee.
narrator: Warrad, the scribe of Mughira bin Shu'ba вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ, reported Mu'awiya вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢwrote to Mughira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢ: :
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Ibn Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ uttered at the end of every prayer after pronouncing salutation (these words): There is no god but Allah. He is alone. There is no partner with Him. Sovereignty belongs to Him and He is Potent over everything. There is no might or power except with Allah. There is no god but Allah and we do not worship but Him alone. To Him belong all bounties, to Him belongs all Grace, and to Him is worthy praise accorded. There is no god but Allah, to Whom we are sincere in devotion, even though the unbelievers should disapprove it. (The narrator said): He (the Holy Prophet п·ә) uttered it at the end of every (obligatory) prayer.
narrator: Abu Zubair reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Abdullah bin Zubair вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ used to say La ilaha il-Allah at the end of every prayer like the hadith narrated by Ibn Numair and he reported it in the end, and then reported Ibn Zubair saying: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) uttered La ilaha il-Allah at the end of every prayer.
narrator: Abu Zubair reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard Abdullah bin Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢaddressing (people) on the pulpit and saying: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) pronounced salutation at the end of the prayer or prayers, and then he made a mention of the hadith as transmitted by Hisham bin 'Urwa.
narrator: Abu Zubair вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: He had heard 'Abdullah bin Zubair вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢuttering (the words) like that of the hadith (narrated above) at the end of the prayer after pronouncing salutation. He at the conclusion also said that he was making a mention of that from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Abu Zubair al-Makki reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: They (the poor among the emigrants) said Messenger of Allah, the possessors of great wealth have obtained the highest ranks and lasting bliss, and the rest of the hadith is the same as transmitted by Qutaiba on the authority of Laith except that he inserted the words of Abu Salih in the narration of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ that the poor of the emigrants came back, to the end of the hadith,, but this addition was made that Suhail said (that every part of the supplication, i. e. Glorification of Allah, His Praise and declaration of His Greatness) should be uttered eleven times making the total as thirty-three.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢnarrated it from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) that :
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There are certain ejaculations, the repeaters of which or the performers of which after every prescribed prayer will never be caused disappointment: Glory be to Allah thirty-three times. Praise be to Allah thirty-three times, and Allah is most Great thirty-four times.
narrator: Ka'b bin 'Ujra вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There are certain ejaculations, the repeaters of which or the performers of which at the end of every prayer will never be caused disappointment: Glory be to Allah thirty-three times, Praise be to Allah thirty-three times, and Allah is most Great thirty-four times.
narrator: Ka'b bin 'Ujra вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A similar report (no. 1350) was narrated from Al-Hakam with this chain.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If anyone extols Allah after every prayer thirty-three times, and praises Allah thirty-three times, and declares His Greatness thirty-three times, ninety-nine times in all, and says to complete a hundred: There is no god but Allah, having no partner with Him, to Him belongs sovereignty and to Him is praise due, and He is Potent over everything, his sins will be forgiven even If these are as abundant as the foam of the sea.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: "The Messenger of Allah said..." a similar repot (as no.1353).
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe, silence for a short while between the takbir (at the time of opening the prayer) and the recitation of the Qur'an. I said to him: Messenger of Allah, for whom I would give my father and mother in ransom, what do you recite during your period of silence between the takbir and the recitation? He said: I say (these words): O Allah, remove my sins from me as Thou hast removed the East from the West. O Allah purify me from sins as a white garment is purified from filth. O Allah! wash away my sins with snow, water, and ice.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A hadith similar to that of Jarir (no. 1354) was narrated from Umrah bin Al-Qaba with this chain.
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stood up for the second rak'ah he opened it with the recitation of the praise of Allah, the Lord of universe (al-Fatiha), and he did not observe silence (before the recitation of al-Fatiha).
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: A man came panting and entered the row of worshippers and said: Praise be to Allah, much praised and blessed. When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) finished the prayer he said: Who amongst you uttered these words? The people remained silent. He (the Prophet again said) -: Who amongst you uttered these words? He said nothing wrong. Then a man said: I came and had a difficulty in breathing, so I uttered them. He replied: I saw twelve angels facing one another as to who will take them up (to Allah).
narrator: Anas вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: While we said prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), one among the people said: Allah is truly Great, praise be to Allah in abundance. Glory be to Allah in the morning and the evening. The Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon, him) said: Who uttered such and such a word? A person among the people said: It is I, Messenger of Allah (who have recited these words). He (the Holy Prophet) said: It (its utterance) surprised me, for the doors of heaven were opened for It. Ibn 'Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢsaid: I have not abandoned them (these words) since I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying this.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: When the Iqama has been pronounced for prayer, do not go running to it, but go walking in tranquillity and pray what you are in time for, and complete what you have missed.
narrator: Abu Huraira вҖҢШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When the words of Iqama are pronounced, do not come to (prayer) running, but go with tranquillity, and pray what you are in time for, and complete (what you have missed) for when one of you is preparing for prayer he is in fact engaged in prayer.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№ЩҶЫҒ вҖҢreported:
chapterName: The Book of Mosques and Places of Prayer

text: The prayer was prescribed as two rak'ahs, two rak'ahs both in journey and at the place of residence. The prayer while travelling remained as it was (originally prescribed), but an addition was made in the prayer (observed) at the place of residence.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), reported:

text: When Allah prescribed the prayer as two rak'ahs, then it was completed (to four rak'ahs) at the place of residence, but was retained in the same position in journey as it was first made obligatory.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said:

text: The prayer was prescribed as consisting of two rak'ahs, the prayer in travelling remained the same, but the prayer at the place of residence was completed. (Zuhri said he asked 'Urwa why 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said prayer in the complete form during journey, and he replied that she interpreted the matter herself as 'Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  did.)
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: I told 'Umar bin al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that Allah had said: You may shorten the prayer only if you fear that those who are unbelievers may afflict you (Qur'an, iv. 101), whereas the people are now safe. He replied: I wondered about it in the same way as you wonder about it, so I asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about it and he said: It is an act of charity which Allah has done to you, so accept His charity.
narrator: Ya'la bin Umayya said:

text: I said to 'Umar bin al-Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, and the rest of the hadlth is the same.
narrator: Ya'la bin Umayya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah has prescribed the prayer through the word of your Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as four rak'ahs when resident, two when travelling, and one when danger is present.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah has prescribed the prayer by the tongue of your Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as two rak'ahs for the traveller, four for the resident, and one in danger.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I asked Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : How should I say prayer when I am in Mecca, and when I do not pray along with the Imam? He said: Two rak'ahs (of prayer) is the Sunnah of Abu'l-Qasim ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Musa bin Salama Hudhali said:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Qatada with the same chain of transmitters.

I fell ill and lbn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came to inquire after my health, and I asked him about the glorification of Allah (i. e. prayer) while travelling. Thereupon he said: I accompanied the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) on a journey but I did not see him glorifying Him, and were I to glorify (Him). I would have completed the prayer. Allah, the Exalted, has said: Verily there is a model pattern for you in the Messenger of Allah.
narrator: Hafs bin 'Asim reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said four rak'ahs in the noon prayer while at Medina, but he offered two rak'ahs in the afternoon prayer at Dhu'l-Hulaifa.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I observed four rak'ahs in the noon prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) at Medina, and said two rak'ahs in the afternoon prayer at Dhu'l-Hulaifa.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is reported to have said:

text: I asked Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ about shortening of prayer. He said: When the Messenger of' Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had covered a distance of three miles or three farsakh (Shu'ba, one of the narrators, had some doubt about it) he observed two rak'ahs.
narrator: Yahya bin Yazid al-Huna'i reported:

text: I went along with Shurahbil bin al-Simt to a village which was situated at a distance of seventeen or eighteen miles, and he said only two rak'ahs of prayer. I said to him (about it) and he said: I saw 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ observing two rak'ahs at Dhu'l-Hulaifa and I (too) said to him (about it) and he said: I am doing the same as I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) doing.
narrator: Jubair bin Nufair reported:

text: This hadith has been transmitted by Shu'ba with the same chain of narrators and it is narrated from Simt, and the name of Shurahbil has not been mentioned, and he said that he had gone to a place called Dumin, situated at a distance of eighteen miles from Hims.

We went out from Medina to Mecca with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and he prayed two rak'ahs at each time of prayer till we returned to Medina. I said: For how long did he stay in Mecca? He said: (For) ten (days).
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters.
I heard Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ say: We went out for Pilgrimage from Medina. The rest is the same.
narrator: Yahya bin Abu Ishaq reported:

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, but no mention has been made of Pilgrimage.

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed the prayer of a traveller, i. e. two rak'ahs in Mina, and other places; so did Abu Bakr and 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and 'Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ too observed two rak'ahs at the beginning of his caliphate, but he then completed four.
narrator: Salim bin 'Abdullah (bin 'Umar) reported on the authority of his father:

text: He said: in "Mina," he did not say: "elsewhere.
narrator: It was narrted from Az-Zuhri with this chain (a similar hadith as no. 1590).

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said two rak'ahs at Mina, and Abu Bakr after him, and 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ after Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and 'Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ at the beginning of his caliphate; then 'Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ observed four rak'ahs, and when Ibn 'Umar prayed with the Imam, he said four rak'ahs, but when he observed prayer alone, he said two rak'ahs.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text:
A similar report (as no. 1592) was narrated from 'Ubaidullah with this chin.
hadith like this has been narrated by the same chain of transmitters.

The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said in Mina the prayer of a traveller (short prayer) ; Abu Bakr and 'Umar did the same and 'Uthmia did it for eight years or six years. Hafs (one of the narrators) said: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ would also say two rak'ahs at Mina and then go to bed. I said to him: O uncle, I wish you could have said two rak'ahs (of Sunnah prayer after shorenting the Fard prayer). He said: Were I to do that, I would have completed the prayer.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard 'Abdul-Rahman as saying; 'Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ led us four rak'ahs of prayer at Mina. It was reported to Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and he recited: Surely we are Allah's and to Him shall we return, and then said: I prayed with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) at Mina two rak'ahs of prayer. I prayed along with Abu Bakr al-Siddiq two rak'ahs of prayer at Mina. I prayed along with 'Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ two rak'ahs of prayer at Mina. I wish I had my share of the two rak'ahs acceptable (to God) for the four rak'ahs.
narrator: Ibrahim reported:

text: A hadith like this has been reported by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.
I prayed with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) two rak'ahs and most of them offered two rak'ahs only in Mina, while the people felt secure.
narrator: Haritha bin Wahb ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I prayed behind the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) at Mina, and there was the greatest number of people, and they prayed two rak'ahs on the occasion of the Farewell Pilgrimage. (Muslim said: Haritha bin Wahb al-Khuza'i ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is the brother of 'Ubaidullah bin 'Umar son of Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the side of his mother.)
narrator: Wahb al-Khuza'i ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ announced Adhan for prayer on a cold, windy night. Then added: Pray in your dwellings; and then said: When it was a cold, rainy night, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to command the Mu'adhdhin to say Pray in your dwellings.
narrator: It was narrated from Nafi:

text: He summoned (people) to pray on a cold, windy and rainy night, and then observed at the end of the Adhan: Pray in your dwellings, pray in your dwellings, and then said: When it was a cold night or it was raining in a journey the Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) used to command the Mu'adhdhin to announce: Pray in your dwellings.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He summoned (people) to prayer at a place (known as) Dajnan, and the rest of the hadith is the same, and then said: Pray in your dwellings, but he did not repeat for the second time words of Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (Pray in your dwellings).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: We set cut with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on a journey when it began to rain. Upon this he said: He who desires may pray in his dwelling.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He said to the Mu'adhdhin on a rainy day: When you have announced I testify that there is no god but Allah; I testify that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, do not say: Come to the prayer, but make this announcement: Say prayer in your houses. He (the narrator) said that the people disapproved of it. Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: Are you astonished at it? He (the Holy Prophet), who is better than I, did it. Jumu'a prayer is no doubt obligatory, but I do not like that I should (force you) to come out and walk in mud and slippery ground.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard 'Abdullah bin al-Harith say: 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ addressed us on a rainy day, and the rest of the hadith is the same, but he made no mention of Jumu'a prayer, and added: He who did it (who commanded us to say prayer in our houses), I. e. the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), is better than I. Abu Kalim sid: "Hammad narrated something similar to us from Asim, from Abdullah bin Harith."
narrator: Abdul-Hamid reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Ayyub and 'Asim al-Ahwal with the same chain of transmitters, but in this hadith it is not recorded: i. e. the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).

Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ commanded the Mu'adhdhin to (summon the people to prayer on Friday and make announcement to say prayer in their houses) when it was rainy, and the rest of the hadith is the same (except this) that he said: I do not like you should walk in muddy slippery place.
narrator: Abdullah bin Harith reported:

text: The Mu'adhdhin of Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said Adhan on Friday (and then made the announcement to say prayer in houses) because it was a rainy day; as it has been narrated by Ma'mar and others, and in this hadith it was mentioned: He who did it, i. e. the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), was better than I.
narrator: Abdullah bin Harith reported:

text: A hadith like this that Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ordered his Mu'adhdhin (to summon people to prayer and then make announcement to say prayer in their houses) on Friday which was a rainy day, has been transmitted by 'Abdullah bin Harith. Wuhaib, however, says that he did not hear it from him.
narrator: It was narrated that Abdullah bin Harith saod:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to say Nafl prayer on (the back of) his camel in whatever direction it took him.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to pray on (the back of) his camel in whatever direction it took him.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to say prayer on his camel while coming from Mecca to Medina, in whatever direction his face had turned; and its was (in this context) that this verse was revealed: So whether you turn thither is Allah's face (ii. 115).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ then recited: Whether you turn thither is Allah's face, and it was revealed in this context.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by another chain of transmitters and in the one narrated by Ibn Mubarak and Ibn Abu Za'ida (these words are narrated):

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) praying (Nafl prayer) on a donkey's back while his face was turned towards Khaibar.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I was travelling along with Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ on the way to Mecca. Sa'id said: When I apprehended dawn, I dismounted (the ride) and observed Witr prayer and then again joined him. Ibn 'Umar said to me: Where were you? I said: I apprehended the appearance of dawn, so I dismounted and observed Witr prayer. Upon this 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: Is there not a model pattern for you in the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ )? I said: Yes, by Allah, and (then) he said: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe Witr prayer on the camel's back.
narrator: Sa'id bin Yasar reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe prayer on his ride (no matter) in which direction it had its face turned. 'Abdullah b. Dinar said that Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to do like that.
narrator: Abdullah bin Dinar reported on the authority of Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe Witr prayer on his ride.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to observe Nafi (supererogatory) prayer on his ride no matter in what direction it turned its face, and he observed Witr too on it, but did not observe obligatory prayer on it.
narrator: Salim bin 'Abdullah reported on the authority of his father:

text: He had seen the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observing Nafl player at night on a journey on the back of his ride in whichever direction it turned its face.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Amir bin Rabi'a ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ has reported on the authority of his father:

text: We met Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as he came to Syria at a place known as 'Ain-al-Tamar and saw him observing prayer on the back of his donkey with his face turned in that direction. (Hammam one of the narrators) pointed towards the left of Qibla, so I said to him: I find you observing prayer towards the side other than that of Qibla. Upon this he said: Had I not seen the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) doing like this, I would not have done so at all.
narrator: Anas bin Sirin reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was in a state of hurry on a journey, he combined the sunset and 'Isha' prayers.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was in a state of hurry on a journey, he combined the sunset and 'Isha' prayers after the twilight had disappeared, and he would say that when the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was in a state of hurry on a journey, he combined the sunset and 'Isha' prayers.
narrator: Nafi' reported:

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) combining the sunset and Isha' prayers when he was in a hurry on a journey.
narrator: Salim reported from his father to be saying:

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) delaying the sunset prayer till he would combine it with the 'Isha' when he hastened to set out on a journey.
narrator: Salim bin 'Abdullah reported that his father had said:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) set out on a journey before the sun declined (from the meridian), he delayed the noon prayer till the afternoon prayer, and then dismounted (his ride) and combined them (noon and afternoon prayers), but if the sun had declined before his setting out on a journey, he observed the noon prayer and then mounted (the ride).
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) intended to combine two prayers on a journey, he delayed the noon prayer till came the early time of the afternoon prayer, and then combined the two.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had to set out on a journey hurriedly, he delayed the noon prayer to the earlier time for the afternoon prayer, and then he would combine them, and he would delay the sunset prayer to the time when the twilight would disappear and then combine it with the 'Isha' prayer.
narrator: Anas reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed the noon and afternoon prayers together, and the sunset and Isha' prayers together without being in a state of fear or in a state of journey.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed the noon and afternoon prayers together in Medina without being in a state of fear or in a state of journey. (Abu Zubair said: I asked Sa'id [one of the narrators] why he did that. He said: I asked Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as you have asked me, and he replied that he [the Holy Prophet] wanted that no one among his Ummah should be put to [unnecessary] hardship.)
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) combined the prayers as he set on a journey in the expedition to Tabuk. He combined the noon prayer with the afternoon prayer and the sunset prayer with the 'Isha' prayer. Sa'id (one of the rawis) said to Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : What prompted him to do this? He said: He wanted that his Ummah should not be put to (unnecessary) hardship.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: We set out with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on the Tabuk expedition, and he observed the noon and afternoon prayers together and the sunset and 'Isha' prayers together.
narrator: Mu'adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) combined in the expedition to Tabuk the noon prayer with the afternoon prayer and the sunset prayer with the 'Isha' prayer. He (one of the narrators) said: What prompted him to do that? He (Mu'adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) replied that he (the Holy Prophet) wanted that his Ummah should not be put to (unnecessary) hardship.
narrator: Mu'adh bin Jabal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) combined the noon prayer with the afternoon prayer and the sunset prayer with the 'Isha' prayer in Medina without being in a state of danger or rainfall. And in the hadith transmitted by Waki' (the words are): I said to Ibn 'Abbas: What prompted him to do that? He said: So that his (Prophet's) Ummah should not be put to (unnecessary) hardship. And in the hadith transmitted by Mu'awiya (the words are): It was said to Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : What did he intend thereby? He said he wanted that his Ummah should not be put to unnecessary hardship.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I observed with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) eight (rak'ahs) in combination, and seven rak'ahs in combination. I (one of the narrators) said: O Abd Sha'tha', I think that he (the Holy Prophet) had delayed the noon prayer and hastened the afternoon prayer, and he delayed the sunset prayer and hastened the 'Isha' prayer. He said: I also think so.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed in Medina seven (rak'ahs) and eight (rak'ahs), i. e. (be combined) the noon and afternoon prayers (eight rak'ahs) and the sunset and 'Isha' prayers (seven rak'ahs).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A person said to Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (as he delayed the prayer): Prayer. He kept silence. He again said: Prayer. He again kept silence, and he again cried: Prayer. He again kept silence and said: May you be deprived of your mother, do you teach us about prayer? We used to combine two prayers during the life of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq al-'Uqaili reported:

text: None of you should give a share to Satan out of your self. He should not deem that it is necessary for him to turn but to the right only (after prayer). I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) turning to the left.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by A'mash, with the same chain of transmitters.

I asked Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ how I should turn-to the right or to the left-when I say my prayers. He said: I have very often seen the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) turning to the right.
narrator: Suddi reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to turn to the right (at the end of the prayer).
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When we prayed behind the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) we cherished to be on his right side so that his face would turn towards us (at the end of the prayer), and he (the narrator) said: I heard him say: O my Lord! save me from Thy torment on the Day when Thoil, wouldst raise or gather Thy servants.
narrator: Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: But he made no mention the words: "So his face would turn towards us."
narrator: This hadith has been reported by Mis'ar with the same chain of transmitters:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When the prayer commences then there is no prayer (valid), but the obligatory prayer.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Warqa' with the same chain of transmitters.
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When the prayer commences, there is no prayer but the obligatory one.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been reported by Ishaq with the same chain of transmitters.
I then met 'Amr (the other narrator) and he narrated it to me, but it was not transmitted directly from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with another chain of transmitters. Hammad (one of the narrators) said:

text: The dawn prayer had commenced when the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw a person observing prayer, whereas the Mu'adhdhin had pronounced the Iqama. Upon this he (the Holy Prophet) remarked: Do you say four (rak'ahs) of Fard in the dawn prayer?
narrator: Ibn Buhaina ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A person entered the mosque, while the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was leading the dawn prayer. He observed two rak'ahs in a corner of the mosque, and then joined the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) in prayer. When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had pronounced salutations (he had concluded the prayer), he said: O, so and so, which one out of these two prayers did you count (as your Fard prayer), the one that you observed alone or the prayer that you observed with us?
narrator: Abdullah bin Sarjis reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When any one of you enters the mosque, he should say: O Allah! open for me the doors of Thy mercy ; and when he steps out he should say: 'O Allah! I beg of Thee Thy Grace. (Imam Muslim said: I heard Yahya saying: I transcribed this hadith from the compilation of Sulaiman bin Bilal.)
narrator: Abu Usaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) by Abu Usaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When any one of you enters the mosque, he should observe two rak'ahs (of Nafl prayer) before sitting.
narrator: Abu Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (a Companion of the Prophet) reported:

text: A Companion of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), said: I entered the mosque, when the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had been sitting among people, and I also sat down among them. Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: What prevented you from offering two rak'ahs (of Nafl prayer) before sitting down? I said: Messenger of Allah, I saw you sitting and people sitting (around you and I, therefore, sat in your company). He (the Holy Prophet) then said: When anyone among you enters the mosque, he should not sit till he has observed two rak'ahs.
narrator: Abu Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) owed me a debt; he paid me back and made an addition (of this). I entered the mosque and he (the Holy Prophet) said to me: Observe two rak'ahs of prayer.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) bought a camel from me. When he came back to Medina, he ordered me to come to the mosque and observed two rak'ahs of prayer.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I went with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on an expedition and my camel delayed me and I was exhausted. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) thus came earlier than I, whereas I came on the next day and went to the mosque and found him (the Holy Prophet) at the gate of the mosque. He said: It is now that you have come. I said. Yes. He said: Leave your camel and enter (the mosque) and observe two rak'ahs. He (the narrator) said: So I entered and observed (two rak'ahs) of prayer and then went back.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) did not come back from the journey but by day in the forenoon, and when he arrived, he went first to the mosque, and having prayed two rak'ahs in it he sat down in it.
narrator: Ka'b bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ whether the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe the forenoon prayer. She said: No, but when he came back from the journey.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq reported:

text: I aksed 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ whether the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe the forenoon prayer. She said: No, except when he came back from a journey.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq reported:

text: I have never seen the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observing the supererogatory prayer of the forenoon, but I observed it. And if the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) abandoned any act which he in fact loved to do, it was out of fear that if the people practised it constantly, it might become obligatory for them.
narrator: Urwa reported 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ to be saying:

text: How many rak'ahs Allah's Messenger ( п·ә) prayed at the forenoon prayer. She replied: Four rak'ahs, but sometimes more as he pleased.
narrator: Mu'adha asked 'A'isha ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ):

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe four rak'ahs in the forenoon prayer and he sometimes observed more as Allah pleased.
narrator: Mua'ada 'Adawiyya reported 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ as saying:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters.

No one has ever narrated to me that he saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observing the forenoon prayer, except Umm Hani  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ . She, however, narrated that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) entered her house on the day of the Conquest of Mecca and prayed eight rak'ahs (adding): I never saw a shorter prayer than it except that he performed the bowing and prostration completely. But (one of the narrators) Ibn Bashshar in his narration made no mention of the word: Never .
narrator: Abdul-Rahman bin Abu Laila reported:

text: Abu Murra narrated on the authority of Umm Hani that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) on the day of the Conquest of Mecca observed in her house eight rak'ahs of prayer in one cloth, its opposite corners having been tied from the opposite sides.

Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: In the morning charity is due from every bone in the body of every one of you. Every utterance of Allah's glorification is an act of charity. Every utterance of praise of Him is an act of charity, every utterance of profession of His Oneness is an act of charity, every utterance of profession of His Greatness is an act of charity, enjoining good is an act of charity, forbidding what is distreputable is an act of charity, and two rak'ahs which one prays in the forenoon will suffice.
narrator: Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: My friend (the Holy Prophet п·ә) has instructed me to do three things: three fasts during every month, two rak'ahs of the forenoon prayer, and observing Witr prayer before going to bed.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A similar (as no. 1672) was narrated from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , from the prophet п·ә.
My friend Abu'l-Qasim ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) instructed me to do three things, and the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: My Friend ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) instructed me in three (acts), and I would never abandon them as long as I live. (And these three things are): Three fasts during every month, the forenoon prayer, and this that I should not sleep till I have observed the Witr prayer.
narrator: Abu Murra, the freed slave of Umm Hani, narrated on the authority of Abu Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ:

text: Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the Mother of the Believers, informed him that when the Mu'adhdhin became silent after calling (people) to the dawn prayer, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) commenced the dawn (prayer) when it dawned by observing two short rak'ahs before the commencement of the (Fard) prayer.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been transmitted by Nafi' with the same chain of narrators.
When it was dawn, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) did not observe (any other prayers) but two short rak'ahs.
narrator: Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters.
When the dawn appeared, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed two rak'ahs (of Sunnah prayers).
narrator: Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe two rak'ahs of Sunnah (prayer) when he heard the Adhan and shortened them.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by the same chain of transmitters and in the hadith narrated by Usama the words are: When it was dawn .
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe two (supererogatory) rak'ahs in between the call to prayer and the Iqama of the dawn prayer.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed two rak'ahs of the dawn prayer and he shortened them (to the extent) that I (out of surprise) said: Did he recite in them Surah Fatiha (only)?
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When it was dawn, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed two rak'ahs, and I would say: Does he recite only the opening chapter of the Qur'an in it?
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was not so much particular about observing supererogatory rak'ahs as in case of the two rak'ahs of the dawn prayer.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: I have never seen the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) hastening as much in observing supererogatory as two rak'ahs before the (Fard) of the dawn prayer.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger as saying: The two rak'ahs at dawn are better than this world and what it contains.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said about the two (supererogatory) rak'ahs of the dawn: They are dearer to me than the whole world.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited in the two (supererogatory) rak'ahs of the dawn (prayer): Say: O unbelievers, (Qur'an, cix.) and Say: Allah is one (cxii.).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in first of the two (supererogatory) rak'ahs of the dawn: Say: We believed in Allah and what was revealed to us... verse 136 from Surah Baqara, and in the second of the two: I believe in Allah and I bear testimony that we are Muslims (iii. 52).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in the two (supererogatory) rak'ahs of the dawn prayer: Say: We believed in Allah and what was revealed to us and that which is found in Surah Al-i-'lmran: Come to that word (creed) which is common between you and us (iii. 64).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A Hadith similar to that of Mawran Al-Fazari (no. 1692) was narrated from 'Uthman bin Hakim with this chain.
He who observed twelve voluntary rak'ahs, a house will be built for him in Paradise.
narrator: Nu'man bin Salim reported with the same chain of transmitters:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If any Muslim servant (of Allah) prays for the sake of Allah twelve rak'ahs (of Sunan) every day, over and above the obligatory ones, Allah will build for him a house in Paradise, or a house will be built for him in Paradise; and I have not abandoned observing them after (hearing it from the Messenger of Allah). (So said also 'Amr and Nu'man.)
narrator: Umm Habiba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), reported :
