text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) having said: If any Muslim servant (of Allah) performed ablution, and performed it well, and then observed every day, the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Umm Habiba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: I prayed along with Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) two rak'ahs before and two rak'ahs after the noon prayer, two rak'ahs after the sunset prayer and two rak'ahs after the 'Isha' prayer and two rak'ahs after the Friday prayer; and so far as the sunset, 'Isha' and Friday prayers are concerned, I observed (them) along with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) in his house.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) would pray in the night for a long time, and when he prayed standing be bowed in a standing posture, and when he prayed sitting, he bowed in a sitting posture.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: I fell ill in Persia and therefore, prayed in a sitting posture, and I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ about it and she said: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) prayed for a long time in the night standing....' " And he mention the Hadith.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq reported:

text: I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ about the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) during the night (i. e. Tahajjud prayer) She replied: He used to pray for a long time standing and for a long time sitting in the night, and when he recited the Qur'an while standing, he would bow himself from the standing position, and when he recited while sitting, he would bow from the sitting position.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq al-'Uqaili reported:

text: I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ about the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). She said: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) would observe prayer (Nafl) in a standing position as well as in a sitting position, and when he commenced the prayer in a standing position, he bowed in this very position, and when he commenced the prayer in a sitting position, he bowed in this very position.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq al-'Uqaili reported:

text: I did not see the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reciting (the Qur'an) in the night prayer in a sitting position, till he grew old and then he recited (it) in a sitting position, but when thirty or forty verses were left out of the Surah, he would then stand up, recite them and then bowed.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to pray while sitting (when he grew old) and he recited in this position and when the recitation equal to thirty or forty verses was left, he would then stand up and recite (for this duration) in a standing position and then bowed himself and then prostrated himself and did the same in the second rak'ah.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in sitting position (while observing the Tahajjud prayer) and when he intended to bow, he would stand up and recite (for the duration in which) a man (ordinarily) recites forty verses.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ how the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) did in the two rak'ahs as he (observed them) sitting. She said: He would recite (the Qur'an) in them, and when he intended to bow, he would stand up and then bowed.
narrator: Alqama bin Waqqas reported:

text: I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ whether the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed (Nafl) sitting. She said: Yes, when the people had made him old.
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq reported:

text: I said to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and she made a mention of that (recorded above) about the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Abdullah bin Shaqiq reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) died (in this very state) that he observed most of his (Nafl) prayers in a sitting position.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) grew bulky and heavy he would observe (most of his Nafl) prayers sitting.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: Never did I see the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observing supererogatory prayer sitting till one year before his death when he would observe Nafl prayer in a sitting position, and he would recite the Surah (of the Qur'an) in such a slow-measured tone (that duration of its recital) became more lengthy than the one longer than this.
narrator: Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: A similar report (as no. 1712) was narrated from Az-Zuhri with chain, expect that they said: "One or two years (before his death)."
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed (Nafl) prayer sitting before his death.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: It was narrated to me that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had said: The prayer observed by a person sitting is half of the prayer. I came to him ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and found him praying in a sitting position. I placed my hand on his head. He said: O 'Abdullah bin 'Amr, what is the matter with you? I said: Messenger of Allah, it has been narrated to me that you said: The prayer of a man in a sitting position is half of the prayer, whereas you are observing prayer sitting. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Yes, it is so, but I am not like anyone amongst you.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Yahya al-A'raj with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) used to pray eleven rak'ahs at night, observing the Witr with a single rak'ah, and when he had finished them, he lay down on his right side, till the Mu'adhdhin came to him and he (the Holy Prophet) then observed two short rak'ahs (of Sunan of the dawn prayer).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) finished the 'Isha' prayer which is called 'Atama by the people, he used to pray eleven rak'ahs, uttering the salutation at the end of every two rak'ahs, and observing the Witr with a single one. And when the Mu'adhdhin had finished the call (for the) dawn prayer and he saw the dawn clearly and the Mu'adhdhin had come to him, he stood up and prayed two short rak'ahs. Then he lay down on his right side till the Mu'adhdhin came to him for lqama. (This hadith has been narrated with the same chain of transmitters by Ibn Shihab, but in it no mention has been made of Iqama).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), said that between the time:

text: "When he could see the down and he Mu'adhdhin came to him," and he did not mention: the Iqamah." The rest of the Hadith is like the Hadith of Amr.
narrator: It was narrated from Ibn Shihab with this chain, and Harmlah Quoted a similar Hadith (as no. 1718) except that he did not mention:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe thirteen rak'ahs of the night prayer. Five out of them consisted of Witr, and he did not sit, but at the end (for salutation).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Hisham with the same chain of transmitters.
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to pray thirteen rak'ahs during the night including the two rak'ahs (Sunan) of the dawn prayer.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ about the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) She said: He observed thirteen rak'ahs (in the night prayer). He observed eight rak'ahs and would then observe Witr and then observe two rak'ahs sitting, and when he wanted to bow he stood up and then bowed down, and then observed two rak'ahs in between the Adhan and lqama of the dawn prayer.
narrator: Abu Salama asked:

text: He asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ about the prayer of the Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) (during the night). The rest of the hadith is the same but with this exception that he (the Holy Prophet) observed nine rak'ahs including Witr.
narrator: Abu Salama reported:

text: I came to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ . I said: O mother, inform me about the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). She said: His (night prayer) in Ramadan and (during other months) was thirteen rak'ahs at night including two rak'ahs of fajr.
narrator: Abu Salama is reported to have said:

text: The prayer of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) in the night consisted of ten rak'ahs. He observed a Witr and two rak'ahs (of Sunan) of the dawn prayer, and thus the total comes to thirteen rak'ahs.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: He used to sleep in the early part of the night, and woke up in the latter part. If he then wished intercourse with his wife, he satisfied his desire, and then went to sleep; and when the first call to prayer was made he jumped up (by Allah, she, i. e. 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , did not say he stood up ), and poured water over him (by Allah she, i. e. 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , did not say that he took a bath but I know what she meant) and if he did not have an intercourse, he performed ablution, just as a man performs ablution for prayer and then observed two rak'ahs.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ thus reported about the (night prayer) of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ):

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe prayer in the night and the last of his (night) prayer was Witr.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ observed:

text: I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ about the action (most pleasing to) the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). She said: He (the Holy Prophet) loved (that action) which one keeps on doing regularly. I said (to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ): When did he pray (at night)? She replied: When he heard the cock crow, he got up and observed prayer.
narrator: Masruq is reported:

text: Never did the earlier part of the dawn find the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) but sleeping in my house or near me.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had prayed the two rak'ahs (Sunan) of the dawn prayer, he would talk to me if I was awake, otherwise he would lie down.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ by another chain of transmitters.
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to pray in the night and when he observed Witr, he said to me: O 'A'isha, get up and observe Witr.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to offer prayer at night while she lay in front of him, and when the Witr prayer was yet to be observed, he would awaken her and she observed Witr.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed the Witr prayer every night and he completed Witr at the time of dawn.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger Of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe the Witr prayer every night, maybe in the early part of night, at midnight and in the latter part, finishing his Witr at dawn.
narrator: Masruq reported on the authority of 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ that she said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe Witr every night, and he would (at times) complete his Witr at the end of the night.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: He divorced his wife, and then proceeded to Medina to sell his property, and the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Zurara bin Aufa said that Sa'd bin Hisham:

text: I went to 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and asked him about the Witr prayer, and the rest of the hadith is the same as recorded in this event. She (Hadrat 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ) said: Who is that Hisham? I said: Son of 'Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ. She said: What a fine man 'Amir was! He died as a martyr in the Battle of Uhud.
narrator: Sa'd bin Hisham reported:

text: Sa'd bin Hisham was his neighbour and he informed him that he had divorced his wife and he narrated the hadith like the one transmitted by Sa'd. She ('A'isha) said: Who is Hisham? He said: The son of 'Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . She said: What a fine man he was; he participated in the Battle of Uhud along with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). Hakim bin Aflah said: If I ever knew that you do not go to 'A'isha, I would not have informed you about her hadith (So that you would have gone to her and heard it from her orally).
narrator: Zurara bin Aufa reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) missed the night prayer due to pain or any other reason, he observed twelve rak'ahs during the daytime.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) decided upon doing any act, he continued to do it, and when he slept at night or fell sick he observed twelve rak'ahs during the daytime. I am not aware of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observing prayer during the whole of the night till morning, or observing fast for a whole month continuously except that of Ramadan.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Should anyone fall asleep and fail to recite his portion of the Qur'an, or a part of it, if he recites it between the dawn prayer and the noon prayer, it will be recorded for him as though he had recited it during the night.
narrator: Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Zaid bin Arqam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , on seeing some people praying in the forenoon, said: They well know that prayer at another time than this is more excellent, for Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The prayer of those who are penitent is observed when your weaned camels feel the heat of the sun.
narrator: It was narrated from Al-Qasim Ash-Shaibani:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) went out to the people of Quba' and saw them observing prayer; upon this he said: The prayer of the penitent should be observed when the young weaned camels feel heat of the sun.
narrator: Zaid bin Arqam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A person asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the night prayer. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Prayer during the night should consist of pairs of rak'ahs, but if one of you fears morning is near, he should pray one rak'ah which will make his prayer an odd number for him.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A person asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the night prayer. He said: It consists of pairs of rak'ahs, but if one fears morning is near, he should make it an odd number by praying one rak'ah.
narrator: Salim reported on the authority of his father:

text: A man stood up and said. Messenger of Allah, how is the night prayer? The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The night prayer consists of pair, but if you apprehend the rise of dawn, make it odd number by observing one rak'ah.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A person asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as I stood between him (the Holy Prophet) and the inquirer and he said: Messenger of Allah, how is the night prayer? He (the Holy Prophet) said: It consists of pairs of rak'ahs, but if you apprehend morning, you should pray one rak'ah and make the end of your prayer as Witr. Then a person asked him (the Holy Prophet) at the end of the year and I was at that place near the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ); but I do not know whether he was the same person or another person, but he (the Holy Prophet) gave him the same reply.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Then a person asked him at the end of the year, and what follows subsequently.
narrator: This hadith his been narrated by Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters but it does not have these words:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Hasten to pray Witr before morning.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He who prayed at night should make Witr the end of his prayer, for the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) ordered this.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Make Witr the end of your night prayer.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He who observed the night prayer should make Witr the end of his prayer before dawn. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to order them thus.
narrator: Nafi' reported Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Witr is a rak'ah at the end of the prayer.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Witr is a rak'ah at the end of the night prayer.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I asked Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ about the Witr prayer. He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: It is a rak'ah at the end of the night prayer.
narrator: Abu Mijlaz reported:

text: A person called (the attention) of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as he was in the mosque, and said: Messenger of Allah, how should I make the rak'ahs of the night prayer an odd number? Upon this the Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) said: He who prays (night prayer) he should observe it in pairs, but if he apprehends the rise of morning, he should observe one rak'ah; that would make the number odd (for the rak'ahs) observed by him. This was narrated by Abu Kuraib 'Ubaidullah bin 'Abdullah and Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ did not make mention of it.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I asked Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ like this (as recorded in the previous hadith) and he made this addition: And he (the Holy Prophet) made the end of the night prayer as odd number by one rak'ah. And there is also (this addition): Stop, stop, you are bulky.
narrator: Anas bin Sirin reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The night prayer consists of pairs and when you see the approach of dawn, make this number odd by one rak'ah. It was said to Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : What does the (word) pair imply? He said: (It means) that salutation is uttered after every two rak'ahs.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Observe Witr prayer before it is morning.
narrator: Abu Sa'id (al Khudri) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: They (some of the Companions) of the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about Witr. He said: Observe Witr before morning.
narrator: Abu Sa'id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text:
Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If anyone is afraid that he may not get up in the latter part of the night, he should observe Witr in the first part of it; and if anyone is eager to get up in the last part of it, he should observe Witr at the end of the night, for prayer at the end of the night is witnessed (by the angels) and that is preferable.
"Abu Muaviyah (in his narration) said: "is attended."
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who amongst you is afraid that he may not be able to get up at the end of the night should observe Witr (in the first part) and then sleep, and he who is confident of getting up and praying at night (i. e. Tahajjud prayer) should observe it at the end of it, for the recitation at the end of the night is witnessed*, and that is better.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The most excellent prayer is that in which the duration of standing is longer.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was asked about the prayer which was most excellent. He said: That in which the standing is longer. (This hadith is narrated by another chain of transmitters too.)
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) heard a person reciting the Qur'an at night. Upon this he said: May Allah show mercy to him; he has reminded me of such and such a verse which I had missed in such and such a surah.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) listened to the recitation of the Qur'an by a man in the mosque. Thereupon he said: May Allah have mercy upon him; be reminded me of the verse which I had been made to forget.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The example of a man who has memorised the Qur'an is like that of a hobbled camel. If he remained vigilant, he would be able to retain it (with him), and if he loosened the hobbled camel it would escape.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: When one who had committed the Qur'an to memory (or who is familiar with it) gets up (for night prayer) and recites it night and day, it remains fresh in his mind, but if he does not get up (for prayer and thus does not recite it) he forgets it.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), but in the hadith transmited by Musa bin 'Uqba, this addition is made:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: What a wretched person is he amongst them who says: I have forgotten such and such a verse. (He should instead of using this expression say): I have been made to forget it. Try to remember the Qur'an for it is more apt to escape from men's minds than a hobbled camel.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Keep refreshing your knowledge of the sacred books (or always renew your knowledge of these sacred books) and sometimes he would mention the Qur'an for it is more apt to escape from men's minds than animals which are hobbled, and the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: None of you should say: I forgot such and such a verse, but he has been made to forget.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is reported to have said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Wretched is the man who says: I forgot such and such a sura, or I forget such and such a verse, but he has been made to forget.
narrator: Ibn Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Keep refreshing your knowledge of the Qur'an, for I swear by Him in Whose Hand is the life of Mahammad that it is more liable to escape than camels which are hobbled.
narrator: Abu Musa al-Ash'ari reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: God has not listened to anything as He listens to a Prophet reciting the Qur'an in a sweet voice.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported this directly from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ):
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Shihab with the same chain of transmitters with words: As He listens to a Prophet reciting the Qur'an in a sweet voice.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Allah does not listen to anything, (more approvingly) as He listens to a Prophet reciting loudly the Qur'an in a sweet voice.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is reported to have heard:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated with the same chain of transmitters by Ibn al-Had except this that Abu Huraira reported Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying and he did not say: He heard it.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Allah has not heard anything (more pleasing) than listening to the Prophet reciting the Qur'an in a sweet loud voice.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A hadith similar to that of Yahya bin Abi Kathir (no. 1849) was narrated from Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had said: 'Abdullah bin Qais or al-Ash'ari has been gifted with a sweet melodious voice out of the voices of the family of David.
narrator: Buraida ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had said to Abu Musa: If you were to see me, as I was listening to your recitation (of the Qur'an) yester-night (you would have felt delighted). You are in fact endowed with a sweet voice like that of David himself.
narrator: Abu Burda narrated on the authority of Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) recited on his ride Surat al Fath during a journey in the year of the Conquest (of Mecca), and he repeated (the words) in his recitation. Mu'awiya said: If I were not afraid that the people would crowd around me, I would have given a demonstration of (the Prophet's) recitation before you.
narrator: Mu'awiya bin Qurra reported 'Abdullah bin Mughaffal al-Muzani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) reciting Surah Fath on his camel on the day of the Conquest of Mecca. He (the narrator) said: Ibn Mughaffal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ recited it and repeated it. Mu'awiya said: Had there been (no crowed of) people, I would have given a practical demonstration of that which Ibn Mughaffal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ had mentioned from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Mu'awiya bin Qurra is reported to have heard 'Abdullah bin Mughaffal as saying:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: (The Holy Prophet) was reciting Surat al-Fath as he was travelling on his mount.
narrator: This hadlth has been narrated by Khalid al-Harith with the same chain of transmitters (with these words):
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A person was reciting Surat al-Kahf and there was a horse tied with two ropes at his side, a cloud overshadowed him, and as it began to come nearer and nearer his horse began to take fright from it. He went and mentioned that to the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) in the morning, and he (the Holy Prophet) said: That was tranquillity which came down at the recitation of the Qur'an.
narrator: Al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I heard al-Bara' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying that a man recited al-Kahf when an animal was there in the house and it began to take fright. And as he looked around, he found a cloud overshadowing it. He mentioned that to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). Upon this he said: O so and so, recite on (the surah) as-Sakina descends at the (recitation of the Qur'an) or on account (of the recitation) of the Qur'an.
narrator: Ibn Ishaq reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: "I heard Al Bara ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ say...." and he mentioned something similar (to no. 1857), but they said: " (the horse) jumped."
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Ishaq said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: A believer who recites the Qur'an is like an orange whose fragrance is sweet and whose taste is sweet; a believer who does not recite the Qur'an is like a date which has no fragrance but has a sweet taste; and the hypocrite who recites the Qur'an is like a basil whose fragrance is sweet, but whose taste is bitter; and a hypocrite who does not recite the Qur'an is like the colocynth which has no fragrance and has a bitter taste.
narrator: Abu Musa al-Ash'ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters but with one alteration that instead of the word: hypocrite (Munafiq), there it is wicked (fajir).
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (as saying): One who is proficient in the Qur'an is associated with the noble, upright, recording angels; and he who falters in it, and finds it difficult for him, will have a double reward.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been reported with the same chain of transmitters by Qatada except with this change: He who finds it hard (to recite the Qur'an) will have a double reward.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying to Ubayy bin Ka'b: Allah has commanded me to recite the Qur'an to you. He said: Did Allah mention me to you by name? He (the Holy Prophet) said: Allah made a mention of your name to me. (On hearing this) Ubayy b. Ka'b wept.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying to Ubayy bin Ka`b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : Allah has commanded me to recite to you: Those who disbelieve were not... (al-Qur'an, xcviii. 1). He said: Did He mention me by name? He (the Prophet said): Yes. Upon this he shed tears (of gratitude).
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I heard Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ saying that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said to Ubayy ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ the same thing.
narrator: Qatada said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon (him) asked me to recite the Qur'an. He said: Messenger of Allah, (how) should I recite to you whereas it has been sent down to you? He (the Holy Prophet) said: I desire to hear it from someone else. So I recited Surat al-Nisa' till I reached the verse: How then shall it be when We shall bring from every people a witness and bring you against them as a witness? (verse 41). I lifted my head or a person touched me in my side, and so I lifted my head and saw his tears falling (from the Holy Prophet's eyes).
narrator: Abdullah (bin Mas'ud) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narratted by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters but with this addition: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was on the pulpit when he asked me to recite to him.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters but with an exception that it is not mentioned in it: He said to me: You recited (the Qur'an) well.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Would any one of you like, when he returns to his family, to find there three large, fat, pregnant she-camels? We said: Yes. Upon this he said: Three verses that one of you recites in his prayer are better for him than three large, fat, pregnant she-camels.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: He heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say: Recite the Qur'an, for on the Day of Resurrection it will come as an intercessor for those who recite It. Recite the two bright ones, al-Baqara and Surah Al 'Imran, for on the Day of Resurrection they will come as two clouds or two shades, or two flocks of birds in ranks, pleading for those who recite them. Recite Surah al-Baqara, for to take recourse to it is a blessing and to give it up is a cause of grief, and the magicians cannot confront it. (Mu'awiya said: It has been conveyed to me that here Batala means magicians.)
narrator: Abu Umama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: It has been conveyed to me... have not been mentioned.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Mu'awiya with the same chain of transmitters but with this exception that in this the words of Mu'awiya:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: He heard the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say: On the Day of Resurrection the Qur'an and those who acted according to it will be brought with Surah al-Baqara and AI 'Imran preceding them. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) likened them to three things, which I did not forget afterwards. He (the Holy Prophet) likened them to two clouds, or two black canopies with light between them, or like two flocks of birds in ranks pleading for one who recited them.
narrator: An-Nawwas bin Sam'an ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: While Gabriel was sitting with the Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) he heard a creaking sound above him. He lifted his head and said: This is a gate opened in heaven today which had never been opened before. Then when an angel descended through it, he said: This is an angel who came down to the earth who had never come down before. He greeted and said: Rejoice in two lights given to you which have not been given to any prophet before you: Fatihat al-Kitab and the concluding verses of Surah al-Baqara. You will never recite a letter from them for which you will not be given (a reward).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I met Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ near the House (Ka'ba) and said to him: A hadith has been conveyed to me on your authority about the two (concluding verses of Surah al-Baqara. He said: Yes. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (in fact) said: Anyone who recites the two verses at the end of Surah al-Baqara at night, they would suffice for him.
narrator: Abdul-Rahman bin Yazid reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated by Mansur with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If anyone recites the two verses at the end of Surah al-Baqara at night, they would suffice for him 'Abdul-Rahman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: I met Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and he was circumambulating the House (of Allah) and asked him about this (tradition) and he narrated it to me from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Mas'ud from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: It is through another chain of transmitters that this hadith has been reported by Abu Mas'ud from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If anyone learns by heart the first ten verses of the Surah al-Kahf, he will be protected from the Dajjal.
narrator: Abu Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: At the end of Surah al-Kahf, but Hammam said: At the beginning of Surah al-Kahf.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters. But Shu'ba (one of the narrators) said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: O Abu'al-Mundhir, do you know the verse from the Book of Allah which, according to you, is the greatest? I said: Allah and His Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) know best. He again said: Abu'l-Mundhir, do you know the verse from the Book of Allah which, according to you, is the greatest? I said: Allah, there is no god but He, the Living, the Eternal. Thereupon he struck me on my breast and said: May knowledge be pleasant for you, O Abu'l-Mundhir!
narrator: Ubayy bin Ka'b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Is any one of you incapable of reciting a third of the Qur'an in a night? They (the Companions) asked: How could one recite a third of the Qur'an (in a night)? Upon this he (the Holy Prophet) said: 'Say: He is Allah, One' (Qur'an. cxii) is equivalent to a third of the Qur'an.
narrator: Abu Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: He (the Messenger of Allah) said: Allah divided the Qur'an into three parts, and he made: Say: He, Allah is One. one part out of the (three) parts of the Qur'an.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters in these words:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) came out to us and said: I am going to recite before you one-third of the Qur'an. He (the Holy Prophet) then recited: Say: He is Allah, One--Allah, the Eternal, to the end of the Surah.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) sent a man in charge of an expedition and he would recite for his Companions during their prayer, ending (recitation) with: Say, He is God, One. When they returned mention was made of it to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). He (the Holy Prophet) told them to ask him why he had done like that. So they asked him and he said: Verily, it is an attribute of the Compassionate One, and (for this reason) I love to recite it. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) thereupon said: Inform him that Allah loves him.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: What wonderful verses have been sent down today. the like of which has never been seen! They are: Say: I seek refuge with the Lord of the dawn, and Say: I seek refuge with the Lord of men.
narrator: Uqba bin 'Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said to me: There have been sent down to me verses the like of which had never been seen before. They are the Mu'awwadhatain.
narrator: Uqba bin 'Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: "It was narrated from Uqbah bin Amr Al Juhani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who was one of those who narrated directly from Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: A similar report (as no.1892) was narrated from Ismail with this chain. In the report of Abu Usamah it says:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Envy is not justified but in case of two persons only: one who, having been given (knowledge of) the Qur'an by Allah, recites it during the night and day (and also acts upon it) and a man who, having been given wealth by God, spends it during the night and the day (for the welfare of others. seeking the pleasure of the Lord).
narrator: Salim narrated on the authority of his father (Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed: Envy is not justified but in case of two persons only: one who, having been given (knowledge of) the Qur'an by Allah, recites it during the night and during the day (and acts upon it), and the person who, having been given wealth by God, gives it in charity during the night and the day.
narrator: Salim son of Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is reported to have said on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: There should be no envy but only in case of two persons: one having been endowed with wealth and power to spend it in the cause of Truth, and (the other) who has been endowed with wisdom and he decides cases with the help of it and teaches it (to others).
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Amr bin Wathila Al Laith narrated that Nafi bin Abdul Harith Al Khuzai met Umar bin Al Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ in Usfan.." a hadith similar to that of Ibrahim bin Sad from Az-Zuhri (no. 1897).
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I heard Hisham bin Hakim reciting Surah al-Furqan during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). The rest is the same but with this addition: I was about to catch hold of him in prayer, but I exercised patience till he pronounced salutation.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted thus by 'Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (with a slight change of words):
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A report Similar to that of Yunus (no. 1900) was narrated from Az-Zuhri with the same chain.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Gabriel taught me to recite in one style. I replied to him and kept asking him to give more (styles), till he reached seven modes (of recitation). Ibn Shibab said: It has reached me that these seven styles are essentially one, not differing about what is permitted and what is forbidden.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated by Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: He was sitting in a mosque that a person entered it and he observed prayer, and made recitation, the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Ubayy bin Ka'b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated by Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A person came to 'Abdullah, who was called Nahik bin Sinan, and the rest of the hadith is the same but for this: Alqama came to him ('Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) and we said to him: Ask him about the manners in which he combined (two surahs) in one rak'ah. So he went to him and asked him and then came to us and said: Twenty are the mufassal surahs in the compilation (of the Qur'an) made by 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Abu Wa'il reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: This hadith has been narrated by A'mash with the same chain of transmitters in which ('Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) said: I know the manners in which the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) recited the two surahs in one rak'ah and then twenty surahs in ten rak'ahs.
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A person from Banu Bajila who was called Nabik bin Sinan came to Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and said: I recite mufassal surahs in one rak'ah. Upon this 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: (You recite) like the recitation of poetry. I know the manner in which the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited two surahs in one rak'ah.
narrator: Shaqiq reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A person came to 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and said: I recited all the mufassal surahs in one rak'ah during the night. 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: You must have recited hastily like the recitation of poetry. 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: I remember well the manner in which the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to combine them, and he then mentioned twenty of the mufassal surahs, and (their combinations in) two in every rak'ah.
narrator: Abu Wa'il reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I saw a man asking Aswad bin Yazid who taught the Qur'an in the mosque: How do you recite the verse (fahal min muddakir) whether (the word muddakir) Is with (d) or (dh)? He (Aswad) said: It was with (d). I heard Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ saying that he had heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reciting (muddakir) with (d).
narrator: Abu Ishaq reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to recite these words as (fahal min muddakir).
narrator: Ishaq is reported to have said on the authority of Aswad who quoted on the authority of 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: 'Alqama came to Syria and entered the mosque and prayed there and then went to a (place where people were sitting in a) circle and he sat therein. Then a person came there and I perceived that the people were annoyed and perturbed (on this arrival). and he sat on my side and then said: Do you remember how 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to recite (the Qur'an)? And then the rest of the hadith was narrated.
narrator: Ibrahim reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I met Abu Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and he said to me: To which country do you belong? I said: I am one of the people of Iraq. He again said: To which city? I replied: City of Kufa. He again said: Do you recite according to the recitation of 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ? I said: Yes. He said: Recite this verse (By the night when it covers) So I recited it: (By the night when it covers, and the day when it shines, and the creating of the male and the female). He laughed and said: I have heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reciting like this.
narrator: Alqama reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I came to Ash-Sham and I met Abu Ad-Dard'..." and he Quoted a Hadith similar to that of Ibn 'Ulayyah (no. 1918).
narrator: It was narrated that 'lqamah said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) prohibited to observe prayer after the 'Asr prayer till the sun is set, and after the dawn till the sun rises.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ is reported to have said::
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I heard it from so many Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and one among them is 'Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and he is most dear to me among them that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) prohibited observing of prayer after the dawn prayer till the sun rose and after the 'Asr till the sun set.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: "After Subh until the sun has risen brightly."
narrator: It was  narrated from Qatadah with this chain (a similar hadith as no. 1921) expect that in the Hadith of Saeed and Hisham it says:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: No prayer is valid after the 'Asr prayer till the sun sets and no prayer is valid after the dawn prayer till the sun rises.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Let not any one of you intend to observe prayer at the time of the rising of the sun or of the setting sun.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Do not intend to observe prayer at the time of the rising of the sun nor at its setting, for it rises between the horns of Satan.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: When the rim of the sun starts appearing defer prayer till it completely appears, and when the rim of the sun disappears defer prayer till it completely disappears.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) led us in the 'Asr prayer at (the place known as) Mukhammas, and then said: This prayer was presented to those gone before you, but they lost it, and he who guards it has two rewards in store for him. And no prayer is valid after till the onlooker appears (by onlooker is meant the evening star).
narrator: Abu Basra Ghifari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah led us in praying 'Asr"  similar report (as no. 1927).
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Basrah Al Ghifari said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: There were three times at which Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) forbade us to pray, or bury our dead: When the sun begins to rise till it is fully up, when the sun is at its height at midday till it passes over the meridian, and when the sun draws near to setting till it sets
narrator: Uqba bin 'Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ misconstrued the fact that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had prohibited the observance of prayer at the time of the rising sun and at the time of its setting.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) did not abandon observing two rak'ahs after 'Asr, but she reported Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Do not get used to observe prayer at the time of the rising sun and at the time of its setting and (exhorted the Muslims) to pray at their times.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ about the two prostrations (i. e. rak'ahs) which the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) made after the 'Asr. She said: He (the Holy Prophet) observed them before the 'Asr prayer, but then he was hindered to do so, or he forgot them and then he observed them after the 'Asr, and then he continued observing them. (It was his habit) that when he (the Holy Prophet) observed prayer, he then continued observing it. Isma'il said: It implies that he always did that.
narrator: Abu Salama asked:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) did not abandon at all observing two rak'ahs after the 'Asr in my house.
narrator: A'isha reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Two are the prayers which the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) always observed in my house-openly or secretly-two rak'ahs before the dawn and two rak'ahs after the 'Asr.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: We bear testimony to the fact that 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said: Never was there a day that he (the Holy Prophet) was with me and he did not observe two rak'ahs of prayer in my house, i.e. two rak'ahs after the Asr.
narrator: Aswad and Masruq reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: I asked Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ about the voluntary prayers after the afternoon prayer, and he replied: 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ struck hit hands on prayer observed after the 'Asr prayer and we used to observe two rak'ahs after the sun set before the evening prayer during the time of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). I said to him: Did the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observe them? He said: He saw us observing them, but he neither commanded us nor forbade us to do so.
narrator: Mukhtar bin Fulful said:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: When we were in Medina, the moment the Mu'adhdhin made the call to the sunset prayer, the people hastened to the pillars of the mosque and prayed two rak'ahs with the result that any stranger coming into the mosque would think that the obligatory prayer had been observed owing to the number who were praying then.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) u saying: There Is between the two calls (Adhan and Iqama) a prayer. And he mentioned it three times, and at the third time he said: This applies to those who wish to do it.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mughaffal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: He (the Holy Prophet) said at the fourth time: He who wishes (may do to).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Abdullah bin Mughaffal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters, but with this variation:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) led on of the two groups In one rak'ah of prayer in danger, while the other group faced the -enemy. Then they (the members of the first group) went back and replaced their companions who were facing the enemy. and then they (the members of the second group) came and the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) led them in one rak'ah of prayer. Then the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) pronounced salutation, and then they (the members of the Ant group) completed the rak'ah and they (the members of the second group) completed the rak'ah.
narrator: Salim bin Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: He used to narrate concerning the messenger of Allah at times of fear, and he said: "We prayed in this manner in the Messenger of Allah п·ә."
narrator: It was narrated from Salim bin Abdullah bin Umar that his father:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed prayer in danger on some day (in this way): a group stood with him (the Holy Prophet) (for prayer) and the other group stood In front of the enemy. Then those who were with (him) observed one rak'ah of prayer and they went back and the others came and they observed one rak'ah (with him). Then both the groups completed one rak'ah each. Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: When there is greater danger, then observe prayer even on the ride or with the help of gestures in a standing posture.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) led his Companions in prayer in danger. He made them stand in two rows behind him. He led them who were close to him in one rak'ah. He then stood up and kept standing till those who were behind them observed one rak'ah. Then they (those standing in the second row) came in front and those who were in front went into the rear. He then led them In one rak'ah. He then sat down, till those who were behind him observed one rak'ah and then pronounced salutation.
narrator: Sahl bin Abu Hathma ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: A group formed a row and prayed along with him, and a group faced the enemy. He led the group which was along with him in a rak'ah, then remained standing while they finished the prayer by themselves. Then they departed and formed a row facing the enemy. Then the second group came and he led them in the remaining rak'ah, after which he remained seated while they finished the prayer themselves. He then led them in salutation.
narrator: Yazid bin Ruman told on the authority of Salih bin Khawwat on the authority of one who prayed in time of danger with Allah's Messenger (п·ә) at the Battle of Dhat ar-Riqa' that:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: He had observed the prayer in danger with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) (first) led one of the two groups in two rak'ah of prayer. and then led the second group in two rak'ah of prayer. So the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed four rak'ah and led in two rak'ah each of the groups.
narrator: Abu Salama bin 'Abdul-Rahman reported that Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ told him:
chapterName: The Book Of the Merit Of The Holy Quran

text: When any one of you intends to come for Jumu'a prayer, he should take a bath.
narrator: Abdullah is reported to have heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was standing on the pulpit when he said this: He who comes for Jumu'a he should take a bath.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  is reported to have said:

text: A similar (report as no.1954) was narrated from Salim and Abdullah bin Umar, from Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , from the prophet п·ә.
It was narrated from Salim bin Abdullah that this father said: I heard the Messenger of Allah п·ә say a similar report (as no. 1952).

While he was addressing the people on Friday (sermon), a person, one of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), entered (the mosque). Umar said to him loudly: What is this hour (for attending the prayer)? He said: I was busy today and I did not return to my house when I heard the call (to Friday prayer), and I did no more but performed ablution only. Upon this Umar said: just ablution! You know that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) commanded (us) to take a bath (on Friday).
narrator: Abdullah (bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) reported from his father:

text: Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was delivering a sermon to the people on Friday when 'Uthman bin 'Affan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came there. 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ hinting to him said: What would become of those persons who come after the call to prayer? Upon this 'Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: Commander of the faithful, I did no more than this that after listening to the call, I performed ablution and came (to the mosque). 'Umar said: Just ablution! Did you not hear the Messenger of Allah (my peace be upon him) say this: When any one of you comes for Jumu'a, he should take a bath.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Taking a bath on Friday is essential for every adult person
narrator: Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The people came for Jumu'a prayer from their houses in the neighbouring villages dressed in woollen garments on which dust was settled and this emitted a foul smell. A person among them (those who were dressed so) came to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) while he was in my house. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to him: Were you to cleanse yourselves on this day.
narrator: Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The people (mostly) were workers and they had no servants. Ill-smell thus emitted out of them. It was said to them: Were you to take bath on Friday.
narrator: Aisha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Bathing on Friday for every adult, using of Miswak and applying some perfume, that is available-these are essential. So far as the perfume is concerned, it may be that used by a lady.
narrator: Abd al-Rahman son of Abd Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported on the authority of his father:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) about taking bath on Friday. Tawus said: I asked Ibn Abbas it one should apply to oneself perfume or oil which is available with his wife. He (Ibn Abbas) said: I do not know of it.
narrator: Tawus reported that Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrated the words:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Juraij with the came chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said. it is the right of Allah upon every Muslim that he should take a bath (at least) on one day (Friday) during the seven days (of the week) and he should wash his head and body.
narrator: Abu Huraira  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of, Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said. He who takes a bath on Friday, the bath which is obligatory after the sexual discharge and then goes (to the mosque), he is like one who offers a she-camel as a sacrifice, and he who comes at the second hour would be like one who offers a cow, and he who comes at the third hour is live one who offers a ram with horns, and he who comes at the fourth hour is like one who offers a hen, and he who comes at the fifth hour is like one who offers an egg. And when the Imam comes out, the angels are also present and listen to the mention of God (the sermon).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: If you (even) ask your companion to be quiet on Friday while the Imam is delivering the sermon, you have in fact talked irrelevance.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters.
A similar Hadith (as no. 1965) from Ibn Shihab with both chains, except that Ibn Juraij said: Ibrahim bin 'Abdullah bin Qariz.
narrator: Ibn Juraij narrated:

text: The prophet п·ә said: "If you say to your companion: Listen attentively on a Friday, While the Imam is delivering the Khutbah, then you have engaged in Idle speech."
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There is a time on Friday at which no Muslim servant would pray and would ask Allah for a thing (that is good) but He would give it to him. Qutaiba pointed with the help of his hand that it (the time) is short.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Abu'l-Qasim (the kunya of the Holy Prophet) ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: There is a time on Friday at which no Muslim would stand and pray and beg Allah for what Is good but He would give it to him; and he pointed with his hand that (this time) is short and narrow.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: It was narrated that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:..." a similar report (as no 1970).
This hadith has been narrated by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ by another chain of transmitters.

Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: There is a time on Friday at which no Muslim would ask Allah for what is good but He would give it to him. And further said: This is a very short time.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) but he did not say: That time is short.
narrator: Hammam bin Munabbih reported this hadith from Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who reported:

text: 'Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said to me: Did you hear anything from your father narrating something from the messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the time on Friday? I said: Yes, I heard him say from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (these words): It is between the time when the Imam sits down and the end of the prayer.
narrator: Abu Burda bin Abu Musa al-Ash'ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The best day on which the sun has risen is Friday; on it Adam was created, on it he was made to enter Paradise, on it he was expelled from it.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The best day on which the sun has risen is Friday; on it Adam was created. on it he was made to enter Paradise, on it he was expelled from it. And the last hour will take place on no day other than Friday.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: We who are the last shall be the first on the Day of Resurrection, except that every Ummah was given the Book before us and we were given it after them. It was this day which Allah prescribed for us and guided us to it and the people came after us with regard to it, the Jews observing the next day and the Christians the day following that.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: We are the last and would be the first on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: We are the last (but) we would be the first on the Day of Resurrection, and we would be the first to enter Paradise, but that they were given the Book before us and we were given after them. They disagreed and Allah guided us aright on whatever they disagreed regarding the truth. And it was this day of theirs about which they disagreed, but Allah guided us to it, and that is Friday for us; the next day is for the Jews and the day following for the Christians.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), as saying: We who are the last would be the first on the Day of Resurrection but they (other Ummahs) were given the Book before us and we were given after them, and this was the day that was prescribed for them but they disagreed on it. And Allah guided us to it. and they came after us with regard to it, the Jews observing the next day and the Christians the day following that.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: We were guided aright to Friday (as a day of prayer and meditation), but Allah diverted those who were before us from it. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When it is Friday, the angels stand at every door of the mosque and record the people in the order of their arrival, and when the Imam sits (on the pulpit for delivering the sermon) they fold up their sheets (manuscripts of the Qur'an) and listen to the mention (of Allah). And he who comes early is like one who offers a she-camel as a sacrifice, the next like one who offers a cow, the next a ram, the next a hen, the next an egg.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There is an angel on every door of the mosque recording him first who (comes) first (to the mosque for Friday prayer). And he [the Prophet] likened him to one who offers a camel as a sacrifice and then he went on in the descending order till he reached the point at which the minimum (sacrifice) is that of an egg. And when the Imam sits (on the pulpit) the sheets are folded and they (the angels) attend to the mention of Allah.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who took a bath and then came for Jumu'a prayer and then prayed what was fixed for him, then kept silence till the Imam finished the sermon, and then prayed along with him, his sins between that time and the next Friday would be forgiven, and even of three days more.
narrator: Abu-Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who performed ablution well, then came to Friday prayer, listened (to the sermon), kept silence all (his sins) between that time and the next Friday would be forgiven with three days extra, and he who touched pebbles caused an interruption.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: We used to observe (Jumu'a) prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and then we returned and gave rest to our camels used for carrying water. Hassan[ (one of the narrators) said: I asked Ja'far what time that was. He said.. It is the time when the sun passes the meridian.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed Jumu'a prayer. He said: He used to observe prayer, and we then went (back) to our camels and gave them rest. 'Abdullah made this addition in his narration: Till the sun passed the meridian. and the camels used for carrying water (took rest).
narrator: Ja'far reported on the authority of his father: that he asked Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: We did not have a siesta or lunch till after the Friday prayer. (Ibn Hajr added: ) During the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Sahl bin Said ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: We used to observe the Friday prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) when the sun passed the meridian. and we then returned and tried to find out afternoon shadow (of the walls for protecting themselves from the heat of the sun).
narrator: Iyas bin Salama bin al-Akwa' reported on the authority of his father:

text: We used to observe the Friday prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), and when we returned we did not find the shadow of the walls in which we could take protection (from the heat of the sun).
narrator: Iyas bin Salama bin Akwa' reported on the authority of his father, saying:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to deliver the sermon on Friday while standing. He would then sit and then stand (for the second sermon) as they (the Muslims) do nowadays.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) gave two sermons between which he sat, recited the Qur'an and exhorted the people.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to deliver the sermon while standing.
