He would then sit down and then stand up and address in a standing posture; and whoever informed you that he (the Holy Prophet) delivered the sermon while sitting told a lie. By Allah. I prayed with him more than two thousand times.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was delivering the sermon on Friday in a standing posture when a caravan from Syria arrived. The people flocked towards it till no one was left (with the Holy Prophet) but twelve persons, and it was on this occasion that this verse in regard to Jumu'a was revealed. And when they see merchandise or sport. they break away to it and leave thee standing.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Husain with the same chain of transmitters but with this alteration that he did not make mention of the standing position.

I was along with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) on Friday when a caravan arrived. The people went to it, and none but twelve persons were left behind and I was one of them; and it was on this occasion that this verse was revealed: And when they see merchandise or sport away to it, and leave thee standing (lxii. 1 1). they break
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: While the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was delivering (a sermon) on Friday, a caravan of merchandise came to Medina. The Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) rushed towards it till only twelve persons were left with him including Abu Bakr and 'Umar; and it was at this occasion that this verse was revealed. And when they see merchandise or sport, they break away to it.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He entered the mosque and saw Abd al-Rahman b. Umm Hakam delivering the sermon in a sitting posture. Upon this he said: Look at this wretched person; he delivers the sermon while sitting, whereas Allah said: And when they see merchandise or sport, they break away to it and leave thee standing.
narrator: Ka'b bin 'Ujra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: They heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) say on the planks of his pulpit: People must cease to neglect the Friday prayer or Allah will seal their hearts and then they will be among the negligent.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar and Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: I used to pray with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and both his prayer and sermon were of moderate length.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I used to observe prayer with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and his prayer was of moderate length and his sermon too was of moderate length.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ saying that in the sermon of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) he praised Allah, lauded Him (and subsequently said [other words] and raised his voice, and the rest of the hadith is the same).
narrator: Ja'far bin Muhammad said on the authority of his father:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), while delivering the sermon' to the people, praised Allah, and lauded Him for what He deserves, and would then say: He whom Allah guides aright, there is none to mislead him, and he who is led astray, there is none to guide him (aright), and the best of the talk is embodied in the Book of Allah. And the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: 'Ammar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ delivered to us the sermon. It was short and eloquent. When he (, Ammir) descended (from the pulpit) we said to him: 0 Abd al-Yaqzn, you have delivered a short and eloquent sermon. Would that you had lengthened (the sermon). He said: I have heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The lengthening of prayer by a man and the shortness of the sermon is the sign of his understanding (of faith). So lengthen the prayer and shorten the sermon, for there is charm (in precise) expression.
narrator: Abu Wa'il reported:

text: A person recited a sermon before the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) thus: He who obeys Allah and His Apostle, he in fact follows the right path, and he who disobeys both of them, he goes astray. Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: What a bad speaker you are; say: He who disobeys Allah and His Apostle. Ibn Numair added: He in fact went astray.
narrator: Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reciting (verses of the Qur'an) on the pulpit. and They cried: O Malik.
narrator: Safwan bin Ya'la ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported on the authority of his father that h

text: I memorised (surah) Qaf, by the glorious Qur'an from the mouth of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) on Friday for he recited it on the pulpit on every Friday.
narrator: Amra daughter of Abdul-Rahman reported on the authority of the sister of Amra:

text: The daughter of Haritha bin Nu'man said: I did not memorise (Surah) Qaf but from the mouth of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as he used to deliver the. sermon along with it on every Friday. She also added: Our oven and that of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was one.

Our oven and that of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was one for two years, or for one year or for a part of a year; and I learnt Qaf. By the Glorious Qur'an from no other source than the tongue of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) who used to recite it every Friday on the pulpit when he delivered the sermon to the people.
narrator: Umm Bisham hint Haritha bin Nu'man said:

text: Our oven and the oven of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) were the same for two years, or for one year and part of a year. And I only learned Surah Qaf. By the Glorious Quran from the tongue of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), who used to recited it every Friday from the Minbar, when he addresses the people.
narrator: It was narrated that Umm Hisham bin Harithah bin An-Nu'man ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: He saw Bishr bin Marwan on the pulpit raising his hands and said: Allah, disfigure these hands! I have seen Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) gesture no more than this with his hands, and he pointed with his forefinger.
narrator: Umara bin Ruwaiba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: "I saw Bishr bin Marwan on a Friday, raising his hand, and Umarah bin Ruaibah said..." and he mentioned something similar (to no. 2016).
narrator: It was narrated that Hussain bin Abdur Rahman said:

text: While Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was delivering the sermon on Friday a person came there, and the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said to him: So and so, have you prayed (two rak'ahs)? He said: No. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Then stand and pray.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith is narrated by Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters but Hammad (one of the narrators) made no mention of the two rak'ahs.

A person entered the Mosque while the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was delivering the sermon on Friday. Upon this he (the Holy Prophet) said: Have you observed prayer? He said: No. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Stand and offer the two rak'ahs. According to Qutaiba, the words are: He said: Offer two rak'ahs.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: A person came (in the Mosque) while the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was delivering the sermon on Friday (standing) on the pulpit. He (the Holy Prophet) said to him: Have you offered two rak'ahs? He said: No. Upon this he said: Then observe (them).
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) delivered the sermon and said: When any one of you comes for the Friday (prayer) and the Imam comes out (from his apartment), (even then) should observe two rak'ahs (of prayer).
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: Sulaik Ghatafani came on Friday (for prayer) while the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was sitting on the pulpit. Sulaik also sat down before observing prayer. The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Have you observed two rak'ahs? He said: No. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Stand and observe them
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Sulaik Ghatafani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came on Friday when the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was delivering the sermon. He (Sulaik) sat down. He (the Holy Prophet) said to him: 0 Sulaik I stand and observe two rak'ahs and make them short, and then said: When any one of you comes on Friday, while the Imam delivers the sermon, he should observe two rak'ahs and should make them short.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I came to the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) when he was delivering the sermon, and I said: Messenger of Allah, here is a stranger and he wants to learn about this religion and he does not know what this religion is. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) looked at me and left his sermon till he came to me, and he was given a chair and I thought that Its legs were made of iron. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) sat In it and he began to teach me what Allah had taught him. He then came (to the pulpit) for his sermon and completed it to the end.
narrator: Abu Rifa'a ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Marwan appointed Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as his deputy in Medina and he himself left for Mecca. Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ led us in the Jumu'a prayer and recited after Surah Jumu'a in the second rak'ah: When the hypocrites came to thee (Surah 63). I then met Abu Huraira as he came back and said to him: You have recited two surahs which 'Ali bin Abu Talib used to recite in Kufah. Upon this Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) reciting these two in the Friday (prayer).
narrator: Ibn Abi Rafi' said:

text: That he recited Surah Jumu'a (lxii.) in the first rak'ah and The hypocrites came in the second rak'ah.
narrator: This hadith is narrated by Abdullah bin Abu Rafi' with the same chain of transmitters but with this modification:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to recite on two 'Ids and in Friday prayer: Glorify The name of Thy Lord, the Most High (Surah lxxxvii.), and: Has there come to thee the news of the overwhelming event (lxxxviii.). And when the 'Id and Jumu'a combined on a day he recited these two (surah) in both the prayers.
narrator: Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Ibrahim bin Muhammad bin al-Muntashir with the same chain of transmitters.
What the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited on Friday besides Surah Jumu'a He said that he recited: Has there reached... (Surah lxxxviii, ).
narrator: Dahhak bin Qais wrote to Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asking him:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite in the morning prayer on Friday Surah Alif-Lam-Mim, Tanz'il ul-Sajda (Surah xxxii.): Surely there came over the man a time (Surah lxxvii) and he used to recite in Jumu'a prayer Surahs Jumu'a and al-Munafiqin.
narrator: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Sufyan with the same chain of transmitters.

A similar report (as no. 2031) was narrated from Mukhawwal with this chain concerning 
both prayers, as Sufyan said.
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to recite in the dawn prayer on Friday Alif-Lam-Mim, Tanzil and Surely there came.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to recite in the dawn prayer on Friday: Alif-Lam-Mim, Tanzil in the first rak'ah, and in the second one: Surely there came over the man a time when he was nothing that could be mentioned.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: When any one of you observes the Jumu'a prayer (two obligatory rak'ahs in congregation), he should observe four (rak'ahs) afterwards.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When you observe prayer after (the two obligatory rak'ahs) of Jumu'ah, you should observe four rak'ahs (and 'Amr in his narration has made this addition that Ibn Idris said this on the authority of Suhail): And if you are in a hurry on account of something, you should observe two rak'ahs in the mosque and two when you return (to your house).
narrator: Suhail reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: When any one amongst you observes prayer after Jumu'a, he should observe four rak'ahs. (In the hadith transmitted by Jarir the word minkum is not recorded.)
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When 'Abdullah (bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) observed the Friday prayer and came back he observed two rak'ahs in his house, and then said: The Messenger of Allah (may peace be updn him) used to do this.
narrator: Nafi' reported:

text: While describing the Nafl prayer of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), said: He did not observe (Nafl) prayer after Jumu'a till he went back and observed two rak'ahs in his house. Yahya said: I guess that I uttered these words (before Imam Malik) that he of course observed (them).
narrator: Abdullah bin 'UmarШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to observe two rak'ahs after Jumu'a.
narrator: Salim narrated on the authority of his father:

text: Nafi bin Jubair sent him to As Saib bin Yazid the son of the sister of Namir... and he quoted a similar Hadith (as no. 2042), that he said: "When he said the Taslim I stood up where I was," and he did not mention the Imam.
narrator: 'Umar bin Ata' narrated:

text: I bear testimony to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) offering prayer before Kbutba. He (after saying prayer) delivered the Kutba, and he found that the women could not hear it, so he came to them and exhorted them and preached them and commanded them to give alms, and Bilal had stretched his cloth and the women were throwing rings, earrings and other things. This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ayyub with the same chain of transmitters.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: A similar report (as no. 2045) was narrated from Ayyub with this chain.
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: There was no Adhan on the (occasion) of Id-ul-Fitr and Id-ul-Adha. I (Ibn Juraij) said: I asked him after some time about it. He ('Ata', one of the narrators) said: Jabir b. 'Abdullah al-Ansari told me: There is neither any Adhan on Id-ul-Fitr when the Imam comes out, nor even after his coming out; their is neither lqama nor call nor anything of the sort of calling on that day and nor Iqama.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas and Jaibir bin 'Abdullah al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ sent (him) to Ibn Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ at the commencement of the oath of allegiance to him (for Caliphate saying): As there is no Adhan on 'Id-ul-Fitr, so you should not pronounce it. Ibn Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ did not pronounce Adhan on that day. He (Ibn 'Abbas) also sent him (with this message) that sermon (is to be delivered) after the prayer, and thus it was done. So lbn Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ observed prayer before Khutba.
narrator: Ata' reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: I prayed with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) prayers on two I'ds wore than once or twice without there being Adhan and Iqama.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to observe the two 'Id prayers before the sermon.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: He (the Messenger of Allah) commanded us that we should take out unmarried women and purdah-observing ladies for 'Id prayers, and he commanded the menstruating women to remain away from the place of worship of the Muslims.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: We were commanded to bring out on old days purdah-observing ladies and those unmarried, and menstruating women came out but remained behind people and pronounced takbir (Allah-o-Akbar) along with them.
narrator: Umm Atiyy ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) commanded us to bring out on'Id-ul-Fitr and 'Id-ul-Adha young women, menstruating women and purdah-observing ladies, menstruating women kept back from prayer, but participated in goodness and supplication of the Muslims. I said: Messenger of Allah, one of us does not have an outer garment (to cover her face and body). He said: Let her sister cover her with her outer garment.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went out on the day of Adha or Fitr and observed two rak'ahs, and did not observe prayer (at that place) before and after that. He then came to the women along with Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and commanded them to give alms and the women began to give their rings and necklaces. This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: A similar report (as no. 2057) was narrated from Shubah with this chain.
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: 'Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked Abu Waqid al-Laithi what the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to recite on 'Id-ul-Adha and 'Id-ul-Fitr. He said: He used to recite in them: Qaf. By the Glorious Qur'an (Surah 1), The Hour drew near, and the moon was rent asunder (Surah liv.).
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported that (his father):
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: 'Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked me what the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) recited on 'Id day. I said: The Hour drew near and Qaf. By the Glorious Qur'an .
narrator: Utba reported that his father Waqid al-Laithi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came to see me and I had two girls with me from among the girls of the Ansar and they were singing what the Ansar recited to one another at the Battle of Bu'ath. They were not, however, singing girls. Upon this Abu Bakr said: What I (the playing of) this wind instrument of Satan in the house of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and this too on 'Id day? Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , every people have a festival and it is our festival (so let them play on).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: BY Allah, I remember the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) standing on the door of my apartment screening me with his mantle enabling me to see the sport of the Abyssinians as they played with their daggers in the mosque of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). He (the Holy Prophet) kept standing for my sake till I was satiated and then I went back; and thus you can well imagine how long a girl tender of age who is fond of sports (could have watched it).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: Some Abyssinians came and gave a demonstration of armed fight on the 'Id day in the mosque. The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) invited me (to see that fight). I placed my head on his shoulder and began to see their sport till it was I who turned away from watching them.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: This hadith has been narrated by Hisham with the same chain of transmitters but (the narrators) did not make mention of the mosque.
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: She sent a message to the players (of this armed fight) saying: I like to see them (fighting). She further said: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) stood up and I stood at the door (behind him) and saw (this fight) between his ears and his shoulders they played in the mosque. 'Ata' (one of the narra- tors) said: Were they persians or Abyssinians? Ibn 'Atiq told me they were Abyssinians.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: While the Abyssinians were busy playing with their arms in the presence of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) 'Umar b. Khattab came there. He bent down to take up pebbles to throw at them (in order to make them go off). The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said to him: 'Umar, leave them alone.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Prayer - Two Eids

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went to the place of prayer and prayed for rain and turned round his mantle while facing the Qibla.
narrator: Abdullah bin Zaid bin Mazini ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went out to the place of prayer and prayed for rain and faced towards Qibla, and turned round his mantle and prayed two rak'ahs.
narrator: Ibn Tamim narrated on the authority of his uncle ('Abdullah bin Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ):

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went out to the place of prayer in order to offer prayer for rainfall. And when he intended to make supplication he faced Qibla and turned round his mantle.
narrator: Abdullah bin Zaid al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went out one day in order to pray for rain. He turned his back towards people, supplicated before Allah, facing towards Qibla, and turned his mantle round and then observed two rak'ahs of prayer.
narrator: Abbad bin Tamim Mazini heard his uncle, who was one of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), as saying:

text: I saw the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) raising his hands (high enough) in supplication (for rain) that the whiteness of his armpits became visible.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) prayed for rain pointing the back of his hands to the sky.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was not accustomed to raice his hands in any supplication he made except when praying for rain. (He would then raise [his hands] high enough) that the whiteness of his armpits became visible. 'Abd al-A'la said that (he was in doubt whether it was) the whiteness of his armpit or armpits.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.

While the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was delivering the sermon on Friday, people stood up before him and said in a loud voice: Apostle of Allah, there is a drought and the trees have become yellow, the animals have died; and the rest of the hadith is the same, and in the narration transmitted by 'Abd al-A'la the words are: The clouds cleard from Medina and it began to rain around it and not a single drop of rain fell in Medina. And as I looked towards Medina, I found it hollow like (the hollowness of) a basin.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah gathered the clouds and as we (were obliged) to stay back I saw that even the strong man, impelled by a desire to go to his family, (could not do so).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ but with this addition:

text: A bedouin came to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on Friday as he was (delivering the sermon on his) pulpit; and the rest of the hadith is the same but with this addition: I saw the cloud clearing just as a sheet is folded.
narrator: Ubaidullah bin Anas bin Malik heard (his father) Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:

text: It rained upon us as we were with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). The Messenger of Allah (ШөЩ„ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…) removed his cloth (from a part of his body) till the rain fell on it. We said: Messenger of Allah, why did you do this? He said: It is because it (the rainfall) has just come from the Exalted Lord.
narrator: Anas (bin Malik) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He heard 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah (way peace be upon him), as saying: When there was on any day windstorm or dark cloud (its effects) could be read on the face of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), and he moved forward and backward (in a state of anxiety) ; and when it rained, he was delighted and it (the state of restlessness) disappeared. 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said: I asked him the reason of this anxiety and he said: I was afraid that it might be a calamity that might fall upon my Ummah, and when he saw rainfall he said: It is the mercy (of Allah).
narrator: Ata' bin Abi Rabah reported:

text: I never saw Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) laugh to such an extent that I could see his uvula-whereas he used to smile only-and when he saw dark clouds or wind, (the signs of fear) were depicted on his face. I said: Messenger of Allah, I find people being happy when they ace the dark cloud in the hope that it would bring rain, but I find that when you see that (the cloud) there is an anxiety on your face. He said: 'A'isha, I am afraid that there may be a calamity in it, for a people was afflicted with wind, when the people saw the calamity they said: It is a cloud which would give us rain (Qur'an. xlvi. 24).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: I have been helped by the east wind and the 'Ad were destroyed by the west wind.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
Verily the sun and the moon are among the signs of Allah. And similarly this addition was made: He then lifted his hands and said: O Allah! have I not conveyed it?
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Hisham bin 'Urwa with the same chain of transmitters but with this addition:

text: There was a solar eclipse during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah (way peace be upon him) and he sent the announcer (to summon them) for congregational prayer. The people gathered together and he pronounced takbir and he observed four rak'ahs, in the form of two rak'ahs (i. e. he observed two qiyams and two ruku's in one rak'ah) and four prostrations.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) recited loudly in the eclipse prayer, and he observed four rak'ahs in the form of two rak'ahs and four prostrations.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The prophet п·ә prayed with four bowing and four prostration in two Rakah.
narrator: It was narrated that Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to narrate a narration similar to that of Urwah from Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ concerning the prayer of the Messenger of Allah п·ә on the day of the sun was eclipsed.
narrator: It was narrated that Kasir Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah prayed with six bowings and four prostration.
narrator: It was narrated from Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: This hadith has been narrated by Yahya bin Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters.
"I saw in Hell a tall, black, Himyari woman," and he did not say: "From the children of Israel."
narrator: A similar report (as no. 2100) was narrated from Hisham with this chain, but he said:

text: I came to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ when the people were standing (in prayer) and she was also praying. I said: What is this excitement of the people for? And the rest of the hadith was narrated like one, (narrated above). 'Urwa said: Do not say Kasafat-ush-Shamsu, but say Khasafat-ush-Shamsu.
narrator: Asma' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: ''Do not say: 'Kasafat Ash-Shams,' rather say:'Kasafat Ash-Shams.'"
narrator: It was narrated that 'Urwah:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was one day (i. e. on the day when the sun eclipsed) so perturbed that he (in haste) took hold of the outer garment (of a female member of his family) and it was later on that his (own) cloak was sent to him. He stood in prayer along with people for such a long time that if a man came he did not realise that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had observed ruku', as it has been narrated about ruku' in connection with long qiyam.
narrator: Asma' bint Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: It was for a long duration that he (the Holy Prophet) observed qiyam and he would then observe ruku'. (The narrator also added) I (Asma') looked at a woman who was older than I, and at another who was weaker than I.
narrator: Abu Juraij narrated this hadith with the same chain of transmitters (but with the addition of these words):

text: When there was a solar eclipse the Messenger of Allah (way peace be upon him) observed eight ruku's and four prostrations (in two rak'ahs). This has been narrated by 'Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ also.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed prayer while it was (solar) eclipse. He recited (the Qur'an in qiyam) and then bowed. He again recited and again bowed. He again recited and again bowed and again recited and again bowed, and then prostrated; and the second (rak'ah) was like this.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When the sun eclipsed during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), they (the people) were called to congregational prayers. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed two ruku's in one rak'ah. He then stood and observed two ruku's in (the second) rak'ah. The sun then became bright, and 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said; Never did I observe, ruku' and prostration longer than this (ruku' and prostration).
narrator: Amr bin al-'As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Verily the sun and the moon are the two signs among the signs of Allah by which He frightens his servants and they do not eclipse on account of the death of any one of the people. So when you see anything about them, observe prayer, supplicate Allah till it is cleared from you.
narrator: Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Verily the sun and the moon do not eclipse on account of the death of any one of the people, but they are the two signs among the signs of Allah. So when you see it, stand up and observe prayer.
narrator: Abu Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The sun eclipsed on the day when Ibrahim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ died, and the people said: It has eclipsed on the death of Ibrahim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Isma'il with the same chain of transmitters and in the hadith narrated by Sufyan and Waki' (the words are):

text: During the lifetime of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) I was shooting my arrows in Medina, when an eclipse of the sun took place. I, therefore, threw them away and said, I must see how the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) acts in a solar eclipse today. When I came to him, he had been supplicating with his hands, raised, pronouncing Allah-o-Akbar, praising Him, acknowledging that He is One God till the eclipse was over, then he recited two surahs and prayed two rak'ahs.
narrator: Abdul-Rahman bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: During the lifetime of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) I was shooting some of my arrows in Medina, when the sun eclipsed. I threw (the arrows) and said: By Allah, I must see how the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) acts in solar eclipse. So I came to him and he was standing in prayer, raising his hands, glorifying Him, praising Him, acknowledging His Oneness, declaring His greatness, and supplicating Him, till the sun cleared. When the eclipse was over, he recited two surahs and prayed two rak'ahs.
narrator: Abdul-Rahman bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , who was one of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said:

text: I was shooting some of my arrows during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that the sun eclipsed. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Abdul-Rahman bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed: Verily the sun and the moon do not eclipse on account of the death or life of anyone. They are in fact the signs among the signs of Allah. So when you see them, observe prayer.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ saying that the sun eclipsed during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on the day when Ibrahim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ died. Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Verily the sun and the moon are the two signs among the signs of Allah. They do not eclipse on account of the death of anyone or on account of the birth of anyone. So when you see them, supplicate Allah, and observe prayer till it is over.
narrator: Ziyad bin 'Ilaqa reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Exhort to recite There is no god but Allah to those of you who are dying.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Sulaiman bin Bilal with the same chain of transmitters.
Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Exhort to recite There is no god but Allah to those of you who are dying.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saying like the hadith transmitted by Abu Usama, but with this addition that she said: When Abu Salama died I said: Who is better than Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , the Companion of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), and Allah decided for me and I said (these words contained in the supplication mentioned above) and I was married to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Whenever you visit the sick or the dead, supplicate for good because angels say Amen to whatever you say. She added: When Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ died, I went to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and said: Messenger of Allah, Abu Salama has died. He told me to recite: O Allah! forgive me and him (Abu Salama) and give me a better substitute than he. So I said (this), and Allah gave me in exchange Muhammad, who is better for me than him (Abu Salama).
narrator: Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә came to Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (as he died). His eyes were fixedly open. He closed them, and then said: When the soul is taken away the sight follows it. Some of the people of his family wept and wailed. So he said: Do not supplicate for yourselves anything but good, for angels say Amen to what you say. He then said: O Allah, forgive Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , raise his degree among those who are rightly guided, grant him a successor in his descendants who remain. Forgive us and him, O Lord of the Universe, and make his grave spacious, and grant him light in it.
narrator: Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: (O Allah! ) let Thee be the caretaker of what is left by him, and he said: Grant him expansion of the grave, but he did not say: Make his grave spacious. Khalid said: He supplicated for the seventh (thing too) which I have forgotten.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Khalid al Hadhdha' with the same chain of transmitters but with this alteration that he said:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Did you not see when the man died and his eyes were fixedly open? He (Abu Huraira) said: Yes. He (the Holy Prophet) said: It is due to the fact that when (the soul leaves the body) his eyesight follows the soul.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith is narrated on the authority of 'Ala' with the same chain of transmitters.

When Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ died I said: I am a stranger in a strange land; I shall weep for him in a manner that would be talked of. I made preparation for weeping for him when a woman from the upper side of the city came there who intended to help me (in weeping). She happened to come across the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and he said: Do you intend to bring the devil into a house from which Allah has twice driven him out? I (Umm Salama), therefore, refrained from weeping and I did not weep.
narrator: Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by another chain of transmitters on the authority of 'Asim al-Ahwal.
Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Endurance is to be shown at the first blow.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) came to a woman who had been weeping for her (dead) child, and said to her: Fear Allah and show endurance. She (not recognising him) said: You have not been afflicted as I have been. When he (the Holy Prophet) had departed, it was said to her that he was the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), she was mortally shocked. She came to his door and she did not find doorkeepers at his door. She said: Messenger of Allah. I did not recognise you. He said: Endurance is to be shown at first blow, or at the first blow.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) happened to pass by a woman (who was sitting) by the side of a grave.
narrator: Shobah narrated a Hadith similar to that of Uthman bin Umar with this chain: In the Hadith of Abdus-Samad says:

text: Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ wept for 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (when he was about to die). He ('Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) said: Be quiet, my daughter. Don't you know that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said: The deceased is punished because of his family's weeping over the death ?
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The dead is punished in the grave because of wailing on it.
narrator: Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: "The decreased is tormented in his grave because of wailing for him."
narrator: It was narrated from Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that the prophet п·ә said:

text: When 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was wounded he fainted, and there was a loud lamentation over him. When he regained consciousness he said: Didn't you know that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The dead is punished because of the weeping of the living ?
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was wounded Suhaib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ uttered (loudly in lamentation): O brother! Upon this 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: Suhaib, did you not know that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The dead is punished because of the lamentation of the living ?
narrator: Abu Burda narrated on the authority of his father:

text: When 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was wounded, there came Suhaib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from his house and went to 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and stood by his side, and began to wail. Upon this 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: What are you weeping for? Are you weeping for me? He said: By Allah, it is for you that I weep, O Commander of the believers. He said: By Allah, you already know that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said: He who is lamented upon is punished. I made a mention of it to Musa b. Talha, and he said that 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ told that it concerned the Jews (only).
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When 'Umar bin Khattab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was wounded Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ lamented for him. Upon this he said: O Hafsa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , did you not hear the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) saying: One who is lamented would be punished ? Suhaib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ also lamented over him. 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ told him also: O Suhaib, didn't you know that one who is lamented is punished?
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: We were with the bier of Umm Aban, daughter of 'Uthman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , and the rest of the hadith is the same, but he did not narrate it as a marfu' hadith on the authority of 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as it was narrated by Ayyub and Ibn Juraij, and the hadith narrated by them (Ayyub and Ibn Juraij) is more complete than that of 'Amr.
narrator: Amr reported on the authority of Ibn Abu Mulaika:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The dead is punished because of the lamentation of the living.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The saying of Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , viz. The dead would be punished because of the lamentation of his family over him was mentioned to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ . Upon this she said: May Allah have mercy upon Abu 'AbdUl-Rahman (the kunya of Ibn 'Umar) that he heard something but could not retain it (well). (The fact is) that the bier of a Jew passed before the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and (the members of his family) were waiting over him. Upon this he said: You are wailing and he is being punished.
narrator: Hisham bin 'Urwa narrated on the authority of his father:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Ibn 'Urwa with the same chain of transmitters. The hadith narrated by Abu Usama is more complete.

She heard (from) 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and made a mention to her about 'Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying: The dead is punished because of the lamentation of the living. Upon this 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said: May Allah have mercy upon the father of 'Abdul-Rahman (Ibn 'Umar). He did not tell a lie, but he forgot or made a mistake. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) happened to pass by a (dead) Jewess who was being lamented. Upon this he said: They weep over her and she is being punished in the grave.
narrator: Amra daughter of 'Abdul Rahman narrated:

text: The first one who was lamented upon in Kufa was Qaraza bin Ka'b. Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: He who is lamented upon would be punished because of the lamentation for him on the Day of judgment.
narrator: Ali bin Rabi'a reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
This hadith has been narrated from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) through another chain of transmitters.

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Among my people there are four characteristics belonging to pre-Islamic period which they do not abandon: boasting of high rank, reviling other peoples' genealogies, seeking rain by stars, and walling. And he (further) said: If the wailing woman does not repent before she dies, she will be made to stand on the Day of Resurrection wearing a garment of pitch and a chemise of mange.
narrator: Abu Malik al-Ash'ari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: You did not spare the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) the botheration.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Yahya bin Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters like one narrated by 'Abdul-'Aziz (with the change of these words):

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) took a promise from us along with the oath of Allegiance that we would not lament. But only five among us fulfilled the promise (and they are) Umm Sulaim, and Umm al-'Ala', and the daughter of Abu Sabra the wife of Mu'adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , or daughter of Abu Sabra and wife of Mu'adh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) took pledge from us (including this promise) that we would not lament. Only five amongst us fulfilled the promise, and one of them (who fulfilled the promise) was Umm Sulaim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ .
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When this verse was revealed: When believing women came to thee giving thee a pledge that they will not associate aught with Allah, and will not disobey thee in good (lx. 12), she (Umm Atiyya) said: In (this pledge) was also included wailing. I said: Messenger of Allah, except members of such a tribe who helped me (in lamentation) during pre-Islamic days, there is left no alternative for me, but that I should also help them. Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: (Yes) but only in case of the members of such a tribe.
narrator: Hafsa narrated on the authority of Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ that she said:

text: We were forbidden to follow the bier, but it was not made absolute on us.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: We were refrained from following the bier, but it was not made absolute on us.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) came to us when we were bathing his daughter, and he told us: Wash her with water and (with the leaves of) the lote tree, three or five times, or more than that if you think fit, and put camphor or something like camphor in the last washing; then inform me when you have finished. So when we had finished, we informed him, and he gave to us his (own) under-garment saying: Put it next her body.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: One of the daughters of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) died. And in the hadith transmitted by Ibn 'Ulayya (the words are): The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) came to us and we were washing his daughter. And in the hadith transmitted by Malik (the words are): There came in (our apartment) the Messenger of Allah (way peace be upon him) when his daughter died. The rest of the hadith is the same as narrated by Yazid bin Zurai' from Ayyub from Muhammad from Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: Three times, five times, seven times, or more than that, if you deem fit: Hafsa (further) said on the authority of Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ : We braided (the hair) of her head in three plaits.
narrator: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Hafsa on the authority of Umm 'Atiyya with the exception (of these words that the Prophet asked them to wash her dead body):

text: We washed her an odd number of times, i.e. three, five or seven times; and Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (further) said: We braided her hair in three plaits.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When Zainab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ the daughter of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) died, he said to us: Wash her odd number of times, i. e. three or five times, and put camphor or something-like camphor at the fifth time, and after you have washed her inform me. So we informed him and he gave us his under-garment, saying: Put it next her body.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: There came to us the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as we were washing one of his daughters. So he said: Wash her (dead body) an odd number of times, five times or more than that, the rest of the hadith is the same. She (further) said: We braided her hair in three plaits: (two) on the sides of her head and one on her forehead.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) asked her to wash his daughter, he told her to start from the right side, and with those parts of the body over which Wudu' is performed.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ Ші reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said to them (the women) in regard to the washing of his daughter to start from the right side and with those parts of the body over which Wudu' is performed.
narrator: Umm 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by 'Uyaina on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was wrapped in a Yamani wrapper which belonged to 'Abdullah bin Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ; then it was removed from him, and he was shrouded in three cotton sheets of white Yamani stuff from Sahul among which was neither a shirt nor a turban. 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took up the Hullah and said: I would be shrouded in it, but then said: How is it that I should be shrouded in it in which the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was not shrouded! So he gave it in charity.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: This hadith is narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters, but in the hadith narrated by him there is no mention of the story of 'Abdullah bin Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .

I asked 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ with how many garments the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was shourded. She said: With three garments of Sahul.
narrator: Abu Salama said:

text: When the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) died, he was covered with a Yamani wrapper.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The same was narrated from Az-Zuhri with this chain.

Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Make haste at a funeral; if the dead person was good, it is a good state to which you are sending him on; but if he was otherwise it is an evil of which you are ridding yourselves.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: "I know only that he attributed it to the prophet п·ә.
narrator: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah from the prophet п·ә (a similar hadith as no. 2186) except that in the Hadith of Mamar it says:

text: Allah's Messenger as saying: Hasten at a funeral, for if (the dead person) is good, you would (soon) bring him close to the good. And if it is otherwise, it is an evil of which you are ridding yourselves.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who attends the funeral till the prayer is offered for (the dead), for him is the reward of one qirat, and he who attends (and stays) till he is buried, for him is the reward of two qirats. It was said: What are the qirats? He said: They are equivalent to two huge mountains. Two other narrators added: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to pray and then depart (without waiting for the burial of the dead). When the tradition of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reached him, he said: We have lost many qirats.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Two great mountains. No mention is made of what followed (these words) ; and in the hadith transmitted by 'Abdul- A'la (the words are): till (the burial) is complete. In the hadith transmitted by 'Abdur-Razzaq (the words are): till he is placed in the grave.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of narrators up to these words:

text: "Whoever follows (the funeral) until (the deceased) is buried."
narrator: A hadith similar to that of Mamar (no. 2190) was narrated from Abu Hurirah from the prophet п·ә, and he said:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who offered prayer over the dead, but did not follow the bier, for him is the reward of one qirat, and he who followed it, for him is the reward of two qirats. It was asked what the qirats were. He said: The smaller amongst the two is equivalent to Uhud.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported to have heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: He who follows the bier, for him is the reward of one qirat. Ibn 'Umar said: Abu Huraira narrated it too often. So he sent (a messenger to) 'A'isha to ascertain (the fact). She ('A'isha) testified Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . Ibn 'Umar said: We missed so many qirats.
narrator: Nafi' narrated that it was said to Ibn 'Umar that

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who offers prayer for the dead, for him is (the reward of) one qirat; and he who follows the bier till it is placed in the grave, for him (is the reward of) two qirats. I (Abu Hazim, one of the narrators) said: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , what is a qirat? He said: It is like the hill of Uhud.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who offered prayer for the dead, for him is the reward of one qirat, and he who attended its burial, he would have two qirats as his reward. And qirat is equivalent to Uhud.
narrator: Thauban, the freed slave of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) was asked about qirat, and he said: It is equivalent to Uhud.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Qatada with the same chain of transmitters. And in the hadith transmitted by Sa'id and Hisham, (the words are):

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: If a company of Muslims numbering one hundred pray over a dead person, all of them interceding for him, their intercession for him will be accepted.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: His son died in Qudaid or 'Usfan. He said to Kuraib to see as to how many people had gathered there for his (funeral). He (Kuraib) said: So I went out and I informed him about the people who had gathered there. He (Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) said: Do you think they are forty? He (Kuraib) said: Yes. Ibn 'Abbas then said to them: Bring him (the dead body) out for I have heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: If any Muslim dies and forty men who associate nothing with Allah stand over his prayer (they offer prayer over him), Allah will accept them as intercessors for him.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: "A funeral  passed by the Messenger of Allah п·ә..." and he narrated a hadith similar to that of Abdul Aziz is more complete.
narrator: It was narrated that Anas said:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Whenever a bier passed before him, he said: He is the one to find relief and the one with (the departure of him) other will find relief. They said: Apostle of Allah, who is al-Mustarih and al-Mustarah? Upon this he said: The believing servant finds relief from the troubles of the world, and in the death of a wicked person, the people, towns, trees and animals find rellef.
narrator: Qatada bin Rib'i ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: (The believing servant) finds relief from the troubles of the world and its hardships and (gets into) the Mercy of Allah.
narrator: In the hadith transmitted by Yahya bin Sa'id on the authority of Qatada (the words are):

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) gave the people news of the death of Negus on the day he died, and he took them out to the place of prayer and observed four takbirs.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) gave us the news of the death of Negus, the ruler of Abyssinia, on the day when he died, and he said (to us): Beg pardon for your brother. Ibn Shihab said that Sa'id bin Musayyib had told that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ had narrated to him that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) drew them up in a row in a place of prayer, and offered prayer and recited four takbirs for him.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith is narrated through another chain of transmitters.
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) offered prayer for Ashama, the Negus, and recited four takbirs.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There died today the pious servant of Allah, Ashama. So he stood up and led us in (funeral prayer) over him.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: A brother of yours has died, so stand up and offer prayer over him. So we stood up and drew ourselves up into two rows.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: A brother of yours has died; so stand up and offer prayer for him, i. e. Negus. And in the hadith transmitted by Zuhair (the words are): Your brother.
narrator: Imran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The prophet п·ә said the Takbir four times.
narrator: A similar report (as no. 2211) was narrated from Ash-Shab'i fromm Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, from the prophet п·ә, but it does not say in the Hadith of any of them:

text: A hadith similar to that of Ash-Shabi (no.2011), from Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , from the prophet п·ә, concerning his prayer at the graveside but it does not say in their Hadith that he п·ә  said the Takbir four times.
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed prayer on the grave.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Zaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to recite four takbirs on our funerals and he recited five takbirs on one funeral. I asked him the reason (for this variation), to which he replied: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) recited thus.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Abdul-Rahman bin Abu Laila:

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Whenever you see a funeral procession, stand up for that until it moves away or is lowered on the ground.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Amir Ibn Rabi'a ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ):

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Should any one of you come across a funeral procession, and if he does not intend to accompany it, he must stand up until it passes by him or is placed upon the ground before it passes him.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of 'Amir ibn Rabi'a (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ):

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Should anyone amongst you see a bier he must stand up so long as it is within sight in case he does not intend to follow it.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Ibn Juraij:

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When you follow a bier, do not sit until it is placed on the (ground).
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Whenever you come across a bier you should stand up, and he who follows it should not sit down till it is placed on the ground.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: There passed a bier and the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) stood up for it and we also stood up along with him. We said: Messenger of Allah, that was the bier of a Jewess. Upon this he remarked: Verily, death is a matter of consternation, so whenever you come across a bier stand up.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Jabir ibn 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) kept standing for a bier until it disappeared.
narrator: Ibn Juraij told me that Abu Zubair heard Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ say:

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and his Companions kept standing for a bier of a Jew until it disappeared from sight.
narrator: Abu Zubair heard Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ say:

text: While Qais bin Sa'd and Sahl bin Hunaif ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ were both in Qadislyya a bier passed by them and they both stood up. They were told that it was the bier of one of the people of the land (non-Muslim). They said that a bier passed before the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and he stood up. He was told that he (the dead man) was a Jew. Upon this he remarked: Was he not a human being or did he not have a soul? And in the hadith narrated by 'Amr bin Murra with the same chain of transmitters, (the words) are: There passed a bier before us.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Ibn Abu Laila:

text: We were the Messenger of Allah п·ә when a funeral passed by him.
narrator: It was narrated from Amr bin Murrah (a similar Hadith as no.2225) with this chain, and according to this report they said:

text: Nafi' bin Jubair saw me and we were standing for a bier, while he was sitting and waiting for the bier to be placed on the ground. He said to me: What makes you keep standing? I said: I am waiting that the bier may be placed on the ground (and I am doing that) on the hadith narrated to me by Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . Upon this Nafi' said: Verily, Mas'ud bin Hakam reported to me on the authority of Hadrat 'Ali bin Abu Talib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stood up first (for a bier) and then sat down.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Waqid:

text: He had heard Hadrat 'Ali (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ), son of Abu Talib, say about the biers: Verily, the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to stand first but later on kept sitting; but it is also narrated that Nafi' ibn Jubair saw Waqid bin 'Amr standing for a bier till it was placed down.
narrator: Mas'ud bin al-Hakam al-Ansari informed Nafi':

text: This hadith has been narrated by Yahya bin Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters.

I heard from Mas'ud bin al-Hakam who narrated it on the authority of Hadrat 'Ali  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that he said: We saw the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stood up for a (bier) and we also stood up; he sat down and we too sat down.
narrator: It is narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Munkadir that he said:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters.
A hadith similr to that of Ibn Wahb (no.2232)was narrated from Muawiyah bin Salih with these two chain.

I prayed behind the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) When he offer the funeral prayer for Umm K'ab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ who had died in the state of delivery. The prophet п·ә stood to pray for her in line with her middle.
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Husain with the same chain of transmitters, but no mention is made of Umm Ka'b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ .

An unsaddled horse was brought to the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and he rode on it when he returned after having offered the funeral prayer of Ibn Dahdah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and we walked on foot around him.
narrator: It is reported on the authority of Jabir Ibn Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said (funeral) prayer on Ibn Dahdah: then an unsaddled horse was brought to him and a person hobbled it, and he (the Messenger of Allah) rode upon it and it bounded and we followed it and ran after it. One of the people said that the Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) remarked: How many among hanging bunches in the Paradise are meant for Ibn Dahdah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ?
narrator: Jabir ibn Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said during his illness of which he died: Make a niche for me in the side of the grave and set up bricks over me as was done in case of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Amir bin Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ told:

text: A piece of red stuff was put in the grave of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: When we were with Fadala bin 'Ubaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ in the country of the Romans at a place (known as) Rudis, a friend of ours died. Fadala bin 'Ubaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ordered to prepare a grave for him and then it was levelled; and then he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) commanding (us) to level the grave.
narrator: Thumama bin Shafayy reported:

text: Should I not send you on the same mission as Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) sent me? Do not leave an image without obliterating it, or a high grave without levelling It.
narrator: Abu'l-Hayyaj al-Asadi told that 'Ali (bin Abu Talib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) said to him:

text: This hadith has been reported by Habib with the same chain of transmitters and he said: (Do not leave) a picture without obliterating it.

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade that the graves should be plastered or they be used as sitting places (for the people), or a building should be built over them.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: It is better that one of you should sit on live coals which would burn his clothing and come in contact with his skin than that he should sit on a grave.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Suhail with the same chain of transmitters.
Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Do not sit on the graves and do not pray facing towards them.
narrator: Abu Marthad al-Ghanawi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Do not pray facing towards the graves, and do not sit on them.
narrator: Abu Marthad al-Ghanawi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ordered the bier of Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ to be brought into the mosque so that she should pray for him. The people disapproved this (act) of hers. She said: How soon the people have forgotten that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) offered not the funeral prayer of Suhail bin al-Baida' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ but in a mosque.
narrator: Abbad bin 'Abdullah bin Zubair reported:

text: When Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ died 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§  said: Bring it (the bier) into the mosque so that I offer prayer for him. But, this act of hers was disapproved. She said: By Allah, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) offered prayer in the mosque for the two sons of Baida', viz, for Suhail and his brother.
narrator: Abu Salama bin 'Abdul-Rahman reported:

text: (that whenever it was her turn for Allah's Messenger п·ә to spend the night with her) he would go out towards the end of the night to al-Baqi' and say: Peace be upon you, abode of a people who are believers. What you were promised would come to you tomorrow, you receiving it after some delay; and God willing we shall join you. O Allah, grant forgiveness to the inhabitants of Baqi' al-Gharqad. Qutaiba did not mention his words: would come to you .
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to teach them when they went out to the graveyard. One of the narrators used to say this in the narration transmitted on the authority of Abu Bakr: Peace be upon the inhabitants of the city (i. e. graveyard). In the hadith transmitted by Zuhair (the words are): Peace be upon you, the inhabitants of the city, among the believers, and Muslims, and God willing we shall join you. I beg of Allah peace for us and for you.
narrator: Sulaiman bin Buraida ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrated on the authority of his father:

text: Allah's Messenger, ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: I sought permission to beg forgiveness for my mother, but He did not grant it to me. I sought permission from Him to visit her grave, and He granted it (permission) to me.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) visited the grave of his mother and he wept, and moved others around him to tears, and said: I sought permission from my Lord to beg forgiveness for her but it was not granted to me, and I sought permission to visit her grave and it was granted to me so visit the graves, for that makes you mindful of death.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: I forbade you to visit graves, but you may now visit them; I forbade you to eat the flesh of sacrificial animals after three days, but you way now keep it as along as you feel inclined; and I forbade you nabidh except in a water-skin, you may drink it from all kinds of water-skins, but you must not drink anything intoxicating.
narrator: Ibn Buraida reported on the authority of his father:

text: Abdullah bin Buraidah narrated from his father from the prophet п·ә -a Hadith similar to that of Sinan (no. 2260).

(The dead body) of a person who had killed himself with a broad-headed arrow was brought before the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), but he did not offer prayers for him.
