narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When there comes the month of Ramadan, the gates of mercy are opened, and the gates of Hell are locked and the devils are chained,
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: When it is the month of Ramadan, the gates of mercy are opened, and the gates of Hell are locked and the devils are chained.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith is reported by Abu Huraira (with a slight alteration of words) that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When (the month of) Ramadan begins.

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying in connection with Ramadan: Do not fast till you see the new moon, and do not break fast till you see it; but if the weather is cloudy calculate about it.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) made a mention of Ramadan and he with the gesture of his hand said: The month is thus and thus. (He then withdrew his thumb at the third time). He then said: Fast when you see it, and break your fast when you see it, and if the weather is cloudy calculate it (the months of Sha'ban and Shawwal) as thirty days.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith is narrated on the authority of 'Ubaidullah with the same chain of transmitters, and he said: If (the sky) is cloudy for you, then calculate thirty days (for the month of Ramadan).

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) made a mention of Ramadan and said: The month may consist of twenty-nine days, and it may be thus, thus and thus, and (he further) said: Calculate it, but he did not say thirty.
narrator: Ubaidullah narrated on the authority of the same chain of transmitters:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The month of Ramadan may consist of twenty-nine days. So do not fast till you have sighted it (the new moon) and do not break fast, till you have sighted it (the new moon of Shawwal), and if the sky is cloudy for you, then calculate.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The month (of Ramadan) may consist of twenty nine days; so when you see the new moon observe fast and when you see (the new moon again at the commencement of the month of Shawwal) then break It, and if the sky is cloudy for you, then calculate it (and complete thirty days).
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Measenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: When you see the new moon, observe fast, and when you see it (again) then break it, and if the sky is cloudy for you, then calculate it.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ЩҶ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The month may consist of twenty-nine nights. So do not fast till you have sighted it (the new moon) and do not break it till you have sighted it, except when the sky is cloudy for you, and if it is so, then calculate it.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The month is thus and thus and thus (i. e. pointing with his fingers thrice), and he held back his thumb at the third time (in order to show that it can also consist of twenty-nine days).
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The month may consist of twenty-nine days.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The month (of Ramadan) is thus and thus, and thus. i.e. ten, ten and nine.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The month is thus, and thus, and thus, and he flapped his hands with all their fingers twice. but at the third turn, folded his right thumb or left thumb (in order to give an idea of twenty-nine).
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The month (of Ramadan) may consist of twenty nine days, and Shu'ba (one of the narrators) (gave a practical demonstration how the Holy prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) explained to them) by unfolding his hands thrice and folding his thumb at the third turn. 'Uqba (one of the narrators in this chain of transmitters) said: I think that he said that the month consists of thirty days and unfolded his palm three times.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Apostle as saying: We are an unlettered people who can neither write nor count. The month is thus, and thus. folding his thumb when he said it the third time; and the month is like this, and like this, and like this" indication a total of thirty.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Aswad bin Qais with the same chain of transmitters, but herein no mention has been made of the other month (consisting of) thirty days.

Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ heard a person saying: This night is the midnight (of the month). Upon this he ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said to him: How do you know that it is the midnight (of the month), for I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The month is thus and thus (and he pointed with his ten fingers twice) and thus (i. e. at the third time he pointed with all his fingers but withdrew or folded his thumb)?
narrator: Sa'd bin 'Ubaida ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Whenever you sight the new moon (of the month of Ramadan) observe fast. and when you sight it (the new moon of Shawwal) break it, and if the sky is cloudy for you, then observe fast for thirty days.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Observe fast on sighting it (the new moon) and break (fast) on sighting it (the new moon), but if the sky is cloudy for you, then complete the number (of thirty).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Observe fast on sighting it (the new moon) and break it on sighting it. But if (due to clouds) the actual position of the month is concealed from you, you should then count thirty (days).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him) made a mention of the new moon and (in this connection) said: Observe fast when you see it (the new moon) and break fast when you see it (the new moon of Shawwal), but when (the actual position of the month is) concealed from you (on account of cloudy sky), then count thirty days.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrated:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Do not observe fast for a day, or two days ahead of Ramadan except a person who is in the habit of observing a particular fast; he may fast on that day.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Yahya bin Abi Kathir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) took an oath that he would not go to his wives for one Month. Zuhri said that 'Urwa narrated to him from 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ that she said: When twenty-nine nights were over, which I had counted, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) came to me (he came to me first of all). I said: Messenger of Allah, you had taken an oath that you would not come to us for a month, whereas you have come after twenty nine days which I have counted. Whereupon he said: The month may also consist of twenty-nine days.
narrator: Zuhri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) separated himself from his wives for a month. (His wives said: ) He came to us on the twenty-ninth day, whereupon we said: It is the twenty-ninth (day) today. Thereupon he said: So far as the month is concerned, (and he, with a view to explaining it) flapped his hands thrice, but held back one finger at the last turn.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrated:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) separated himself from his wives for a month. (His wives said: ) He came to us on the morning of the twenty-ninth. Upon this some, of the people said: It is the morning of twenty- ninth (according to our calculation). Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) said: The month. may also consist of twenty-nine days. The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) then flapped his bands thrice, twice with all the fingers of both his hand (to indicate twenty-nine) and by the third time with nine (fingers).
narrator: Abu Zubair is reported to have heard Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) took an oath that he would not go to some of his wives for the whole of the month. When twenty-nine days bad passed he (the Holy Prophet) went to them in the morning or in the evening. Upon this it was said to him: Apostle of Allah, you took an oath that you would not come to us for a month, whereupon he said: The month may also consist of twenty-nine days.
narrator: Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Juraij with the same chain of transmitters.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) struck his hand against the other and (then with the gesture of his two hands) said: The month is thus, thus (two times). He then withdrew (one of) his fingers at the third turn.
narrator: Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said: The month is thus and thus, and thus, i.e. ten, ten and nine.
narrator: Muhammad bin Sa'd reported on the authority of his father (Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas):

text: This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Abu Khalid with the same chain of transmitters.

We went out to perform Umra and when we encamped in the valley of Nakhla, we tried to see the new moon. Some of the people said: It was three nights old, and others (said) that it was two nights old. We then met Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and told him we had seen the new moon, but that some of the people said it was three nights old and others that it was two nights old. He asked on which night we had seen it; and when we told him we had seen it on such and such night, he said the Prophet of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said: Verily Allah deferred it till the time it is seen, so it is to be reckoned from the night you saw it.
narrator: Abu'l-Bakhtari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: We saw the new moon of Ramadan as we were at Dhit-i-'Irq. We sent a man to Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ to ask him (whether the sighting of a small moon had something of the nature of defect in it). Upon this Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had said: Verily Allah deferred its sight, but if (the new moon) is hidden from you, then, complete its number (thirty).
narrator: Abu'l-Bakhtari reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had said: The two months of 'Id, Ramadan and Dhu'l-Hijja (are not incomplete).
narrator: The son of Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported it on the authority of his father:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had said: The months of 'Id are not incomplete. And in the hadith narrated by Khalid (the words are): The months, of 'Id are Ramadan and Dhu'l-Hijja.
narrator: Abdur-Rahman bin Abu Bakra reported on the authority of Abu Bakra ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: When (this verse) was revealed: Until the white streak of the dawn becomes distinct from the dark streak (ii. 187) Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: Messenger of Allah, verily I keep underneath my pillow two strings, one white and the other black, by which I distinguish night from dawn. Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Your pillow seems to be very large. For the word khait implies the blackness of the night and the whiteness of the dawn.
narrator: Adi bin Hatim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When this verse was revealed: Eat and drink till the white streak is distinct from the dark streak, a person would take hold of a white thread and a black thread and keep on eating till he could find them distinct (in the light of the dawn). It was then that Allah, the Majestic and Great, reveiled (the words) min al-fajr (from the dawn), and then it became clear (that the word khait refers to the streak of light in the dawn).
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: When this verse was revealed. Eat and drink till the white streak becomes distinct from the dark streak for you, the person who decided to observe fast tied on one of his feet a black thread and on the other a white thread. And he went on eating and drinking till he could distinguish (between their colour) on seeing them. It was after this that Allah reveal- ed (the words): min al-fajr. And they (the Muslims) came to know that (the word khait) refers to the night and day.
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had said: Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ would pronounce Adhan (at the fag end of the night in order to inform the people about the time of the Sahri). So you eat and drink till you hear the Adhan of Ibn Umm Maktum ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (which was pro- nounced at the conclusion of the Sahri and the commencement of the fast).
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ announces Adhan during the night, so you eat and drink, till you hear the Adhan of Ibn Umm Maktum ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had two Mu'adhdhins, Bilal and son of Umm Maktum ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , the blind. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Bilal  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ announces Adhan at (the fag end of the) night (i. e. Sahri), so eat and drink till the son of Umm Maktum announces Adhan. And he (the narrator) said: And the (difference of time) between their (Adhans) was not more than this that one climbed down (from the minaret) and the other climbed up (to announce Adhan).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted on the authority of 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ .
A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of 'Ubaidullah on the two chains of transmitters.

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying The Adhan of Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ should not restrain anyone among you from eating Sahur (last meal before daybreak during the month of Ramadan) for he announces Adhan (or he calls) at (the fag end of) the night to make him turn who stands for prayer among you, and to awaken those who are sleeping among you. And he said: The dawn is not like it, as one says (and he lifted his hand) till he (dispersed his fingers) and said: It is like this.
narrator: Ibn Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The dawn is not like it as it is said; he then gathered his fingers and lowered them. But he said, it is like this (and he placed the index finger upon the other one and spread his hand).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Sulaiman al-Taimi with the same chain of transmitters (but with a slight variation of words) that he (the Holy Prophet) said:

text: The first Adhan was meant to awaken those who were in slumber amongst them and in order to make them turn who stand in (prayer) among them (towards food at the commencement of the fast). Jarir (one of the narrators) said that the Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) did not say like this but he said like it (true dawn) that the streaks of (true dawn ) are horizontal and not vertical.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sulaiman Taimi with the same chain of transmitters and, at the end, it was said:

text: Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying. The call of Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ may not mislead any one of you (and he may, under the wrong impression gathered from it, refrain) from taking meal before the commencement of the fast (for the streaks) of this whiteness (which are vertical indicate the false dawn and the true dawn with which the fast commences is that when the streaks of light are) spread.
narrator: Samura bin Jandub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The Adhan of Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ should not mislead you nor the whiteness (of the pillar) of dawn, for it is not the whiteness of the true dawn, but that of the false dawn which is vertical like a pillar and you can eat food till the streaks of whiteness spread like it.
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The Adhan of Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ may not mislead you with regard to your food at the commencement of the fast, nor the vertical (streaks) of whiteness in the horizon (for it is an indication of false dawn). You should stop eating (food) till (the whiteness) spreads like it. Hammad narrated it and with the gesture of his band he explained, the horizontal position (of the streaks of light).
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Neither the call of Bilal ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ should mislead you nor this whiteness (of false dawn) till (the true) dawn appears (or he said) till the dawn breaks.
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ addressed and narrated from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) having said (these words):

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Take meal a little before dawn, for there is a blessing in taking meal at that time.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The difference between our fasting and that of the people of the Book is eating shortly before dawn.
narrator: Amr bin al-'As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Musa bin 'Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ has narrated this hadith through the same chain of transmitters.

We took meal shortly before dawn along with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). We then stood up for prayer. I said: How much span of time was there between the two (acts, i. e. taking of Sahri and observing of prayer)? He said (a span of reciting) fifty verses.
narrator: Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Qatada ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ too.
Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The people will continue to prosper as long as they hasten the breaking of the fast.
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ repotted:

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .

I and Masruq went to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and said to her: Mother of the Believers, there are two persons among the Companions of Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) one among whom hastens in breaking the fast and in observing prayer, and the other delays breaking the fast and delays observing prayer. She said: Who among the two hastens in breaking fast and observing prayers? We said, It is 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . i. e. son of Mas'ud. whereupon she said: This is how the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) did. Abu Kuraib added: The second one was Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Abu 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I and Misruq ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ went to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and Masruq ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said to her: There are two persons among the Companions of Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) none of whom abandons the good, but one of them hastens to observe sunset prayer and break the fast, and the other delays in observing the sunset prayer and in breaking the fast, whereupon she said: Who hastens to observe sunset prayer and break the fast? He said: It is 'Abdullah. Upon this she said: This is how the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to do.
narrator: Abu 'Atiyya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When the night approaches and the day retreates and the sun sinks down, then the observer of the fast should break it. Ibn Numair made no mention of the word then .
narrator: Umar (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: We travelled with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as he had been observing fast. When the sun sank he said: So and so, get down and prepare barley meal for us. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Abdullah bin Abi Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Abi Aufa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters (but with a sight alteration of words): In this hadith transmitted by one of the narrators (neither these words are found): During the month of Ramadan. nor his statement: And the night prevails from that side (the eastern side). (These words are found in the narration of) Hushaim only.

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade uninterrupted fasting. They (some of the Companions) said: You yourself fast uninterruptedly, whereupon he said: I am not like you. I am fed and supplied drink (by Allah).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed fasts uninterruptedly in Ramadan and the people (in his wake) did this. But he forbade them to do so. It was said to him (to the Holy Prophet): You yourself observe the fasts uninterruptedly (but you forbid us to do so) Upon this he said: I am not like you; I am fed and supplied drink (by Allah).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been transmitted by Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , but he did not make mention of (the words): During the month of Ramadan.

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Abstain from Saum-Wisal. They (his Companions) said: Messenger of Allah, but you observe Saum Wisal. Upon this he said: You are not like me in this matter, for I spend my night (in a state) that my Lord feeds me and provides me drink Devote yourselves to the deeds (the burden of which) you can bear.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying (the words as said in the previous hadith) but with this alteration (of words): Take upon yourselves (the burden of the deeds) for which you have the strength to bear.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade (his Companions) to observe Saum Wisal.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed Saum Wisal during the early part of the month of Ramadan. The people among Muslims also observed uninterrupted fast. This (news) reached him (the Holy Prophet) and he said: Had the month been lengthened for me I would have continued observing Saum Wisal, so that those who act with forced hardness would (have been obliged) to abandon it. You are not like me (or he said): I am not like you. I continue to do so (in a state) that my Lord feeds me and provides me drink.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Apostle of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade them (his Companions) to observe Saum Wisal out of mercy for them. They said: You (Holy Prophet) yourself observe it. Upon this he said: I am not like you. My Lord feeds me and provides me drink.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) kissed one of his wives while he was fasting, and then she ('A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) smiled (as she narrated).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: I said to 'Abdul-Rahman bin Qasim: Have you heard from your father narrating from 'A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) that he kissed her while observing fast? He ('Abdul-Rahman bin Qasim) kept silence for a short while and then said: Yes.
narrator: Sufyan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to kiss me while observing fast; and who among you can control his desire as the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) could control his desire.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to kiss (his wives) while fasting and embraced (them) while fasting; but he had the greatest mastery over his desire among you.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ) reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to kiss (his wives) while fasting; and he had the greatest control over his desire (as compared with you).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to embrace (his wives) while fasting.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: I and Masruq went to 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and asked. her if the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) embraced (his wives) while fasting. She said: Yes; but he had the greatest control over his desire among you: or he was one of those who had control over his desire.
narrator: Aswad reported:

text: It is further narrated on the authority of Aswad and Masruq that they went to the Mother of the Believers and they asked her (and the rest of the hadith is the same)

'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ the Mother of the Believers (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) informed him that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) kissed her while fasting.
narrator: Urwa bin Zubair narrated:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Yahya bin Abu Kathir with the same chain of transmitters.
The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to kiss her during the month of fasting.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) kissed (his wives) during Ramadan while observing fast.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: Allah's Apostle (п·ә) kissed (his wives) while fasting.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) kissed (his wives) while fasting.
narrator: Hafsa (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated by Hafsa (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) through another chain of transmitters.

He asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ): Should one observing fast kiss (his wife)? The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said to him: Ask her (Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ). She informed him that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) did that, where upon he said: Messenger of Allah, Allah pardoned thee all thy sins, the previous and the later ones. Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: By Allah, I am the most God conscious among you and I fear Him most among you.
narrator: Umar bin Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The dawn broke upon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) during the Ramadan in a state of junub not because of sexual dream (but on account of intercourse) and he washed himself and observed fast.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), said:

text: Marwan sent him to Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ to ask whether a person should observe fast who is in a state of junub and the dawn breaks upon him, whereupon she said that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (was at times) junbi on account of intercourse and not due to sexual dream, and the dawn broke upon him, but he neither broke the fast nor recompensed.
narrator: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) at times got up in the morning in a state of junub on account of having a sexual intercourse (with his wives during night) but not due to sexual dreams in the month of Ramadan, and would observe fast.
narrator: Abu Bakr bin 'Abd al-Rahman bin al-Harith bin Hisham reported on the authority of 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wives of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ):

text: He asked Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ whether a person (who gets up) in the morning in a state of junub should observe fast. She said: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (at times) got up in the morning in a state of junub, not because of sexual dreams (but on account of intercourse at night), and then observed fast.
narrator: Sulaiman bin Yasar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: There was brought an 'araq containing dates, an 'araq being a huge basket. But in this hadith no mention has been made of (the fact) that the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) laughed till his molar teeth became visible.
narrator: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Muhammad bin Muslim al-Zuhri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with the same chain of transmitters, and he said:

text: A person had intercourse with his wife during Ramadan (while fasting). He asked for the religious verdict (about it) from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), whereupon he (the Holy Prophet) said: Can you find a slave (to grant him freedom)? He said: No. He (the Prophet again) said: Can you afford to observe fasts for two (consecutive) months? He said: No. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Then feed sixty poor men.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A person broke fast in Ramadan whereupon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) commanded him to free a slave (as an atonement), and the rest of the hadith is the same as narrated by Ibn Uyaina.
narrator: It was narrated from Az-Zuhri with this chain:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) commanded the person (who) broke the fast in Ramadan to free a slave or observe fasts for two (consecutive) months or feed sixty poor persons.
narrator: Humaid bin 'Abdul-Rahman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ had narrated to him:

text: A similar hadith to that of Ibn-Uyaynah (no. 2595) was narrated Az-Zuhri with this chain.

A person came to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and said: I am burnt, whereupon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: How is it? He (the person) said: I had intercourse with my wife during the day in Ramadan. Upon this (the Holy Prophet) said: Give charity, give charity. He (the person) said: There is nothing with me. He commanded him to sit down, (In the meanwhile) there were brought to him (to the Holy Prophet) two baskets containing eatables, whereupon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) told him to give them as sadaqa.
narrator: A'isha ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ) reported:

text: A person came to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), and he then narrated the hadith. But (neither these words are found): Give charity, give charity (nor) his words: during the day time .
narrator: Abbad bin Abdullah bin Zubair narrated that he heard 'A'isha ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) saying:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went out during the month of Ramadan in the year of Victory (when Mecca was conquered) and was fasting till he reached Kadid (a canal situated at a distance of forty-two miles from Mecca) and he then broke the fast. And it was the habit of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) to follow him in every new thing (or act). So they followed him also (in this matter).
narrator: Ibn Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: I do not know whose statement it is: It is the last word of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) which is accepted as (final as it abrogates the previous ones).
narrator: This hadith is narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters. Yahya (one of the narrators) said that Sufyan (the narrator) had stated:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) marched on Mecca on the morning of 14th of Ramadan (lit. when thirteen nights had passed).
narrator: It has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters that breaking of fast (in a journey) is the final of the two commands (whether one may fast or one may break it), and it is the last command of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) which is to be accepted as final. Zuhri said:

text: They (the Compnions of the Holy Prophet) followed the latest of his commands and looked upon it as one abrogating (the previous ones) and the most firm.
narrator: A hadlth like this has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn Shibab said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) journeyed during the month of Ramadan in a state of fasting till he reached 'Usfan. He then ordered a cup containing drinking water and he drank that openly so that the people might see it, and broke the fast (and did not resume it) till he reached Mecca. Ibn 'Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) said: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) fasted and broke the fast, so he who wished fasted and he who wished to break it broke it.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: Do not condemn one who observes fast, or one who does not observe (in a journey). for the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed fast in a journey or he did not observe it (too).
narrator: Ibn Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went out to Mecca in Ramadan in the year of Victory, and he and the people fasted till he came to Kura' al-Ghamim and the people also fasted. He then called for a cup of water which he raised till the people saw it, and then he drank. He was told afterwards that some people had continued to fast, and he said: These people are the disobedient ones; these are the disobedient ones.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: It was said to him (to the Holy Prophet): There are people to whom fasting has become unbearable and they are waiting how you do. He (the Holy Prophet) then called for a cup of water when it was afternoon. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated by Ja'far with the same chain of transmitters and he added:

text: In the course of a journey Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw a man, people crowding around him and providing him a shade. Upon this he (the Holy Prophet п·ә) said: What is the matter with him? They said: He is a person observing fast. Whereupon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: It is no righteousness that you fast on journey.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: Amr bin al-Hasan is reported to have said that he heard Jabir bin 'Abdullah ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) as saying: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saw a man. The rest of the hadith is the same as mentioned above.

"I was informed about Yahya bin Abi Kathir that he used to add to this Hadith. And with this chain, in it said: 'You should avail yourselves of the concession that Allah has granted to you.'" he said: "So when I asked him, he did not remember it."

This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters but with this addition that he (the Holy Prophet) said: Take advantage of the concession of Allah Who Wanted it to you. When he (one of the narrators) asked him (the other one, Yabya bin Abi Kathar) he did not retain it in his mind.
narrator: It was narrated from Shobah with this chain (a Hadith similar to no. 2613), but Shobah said:

text: We went out on an expedition with Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on the 16th of Ramadan. Some of us fasted and some of us broke the fast. But neither the observer of the fast found fault with one who broke it, nor the breaker of the fast found fault with one who observed it.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Qatada with the same chain of transmitters by different narrators (except this difference) that in the hadith transmitted by Taimi and Umar bin Amir and Hisham (the date of setting out is) 18th, and in the hadith transmitted by Sa'id it is the 12th, and in the one transmitted by Shu'ba it is the 17th or 19th.

We went out on an expedition with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) during Ramadan and neither the observer of the fast was found fault with for his fasting, nor the breaker of the fast for breaking it.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: We went out on an expedition with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) during Ramadan. Some of us observed the fast and some of us broke it. Neither the observer of the fast had any grudge against one who broke it, nor the breaker of the fast had any grudge against one who had fasted They knew that he who had strength enough (to bear its rigour) fasted and that was good, and they also found that he who felt weakness (and could not bear the burden) broke it, and that was also good.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: We travelled with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). The observer of the fast observed it, and the breaker of the fast broke it, but none of them found fault with each other.
narrator: Abu Nadra reported Abu Sa'id al. Khudri and Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:

text: Anas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) was asked about fasting during Ramadan while travelling. He said: We travelled with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) during the month of Ramadan, but neither the observer of the fast found fault with the breaker of the fast, nor the breaker of the fast found fault with the observer of the fast.
narrator: Humaid reported:

text: The Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to set out on a journey and neither the observer of the fast found fault with the breaker of the fast, nor the breaker of the fast found fault with the observer of the fast. (One of the narrators Humaid said): I met Ibn Abi Mulaika who informed me the same thing on the authority of 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ .
narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated from Humaid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ who said: I went out and was fasting; they said to me: Break (lit. go back, repeat). He said that Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: We were with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on a journey. Some of us had been observing the fast and some of us had not been fasting. We got down at a place on a hot day. Most of us had the cloth for shelter. There were also those amongst us who sheltered (themselves against the rays of the) sun with the help of their hands. The observers of the fast fell down (on account of weakness). Those who had not observed it got up and pitched tents and watered the mounts. Thereupon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The breakers of the fast have taken away the reward today.
narrator: Anas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was journeying (along with his Companions). Some of them had observed the fast whereas the others had broken it. Those who did not fast girded up their loins and worked, but the observers of the fast were too weak to work. Upon this he (the Messenger of Allah) said: Today the breakers of the fast have gone with the reward.
narrator: Anas ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: Hamza bin 'Amr al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) about fasting on a journey, and he (the Holy Prophet) said: Fast if you like and break it if you like.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: Hamza bin Amr al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) thus: Messenger of Allah, I am a person devoted much to fasting. Should I fast during the journey? He (the Holy Prophet) said: Fast if you like and break it if you like.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ) reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters.
I am a person much used to fasting. Should I fast during the journey? (The rest of the hadith is the same.)
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters that Hamza ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: Messenger of Allah, I find strength in me for fasting on a journey; is there any sin upon me (in doing it)? Thereupon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) said: It is a concession from Allah. He who took advantage of it, it is good for him, and he who preferred to observe fast, there is no sin upon him. Harun (one of the narrators) in his narration said: 'lt is a concession, and he made no mention of from Allah .
narrator: Hamza bin 'Amr al-Aslami (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) said:

text: We set out during the month of Ramadan with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) in such an intense heat that one of us would place his hand over his head (in order to protect himself) against the excessive heat, and none among us was observing the fast, except the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and 'Abdullah bin Rawaha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Abu Darda' (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: We were with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) on some of his journeys on an intensely hot day so much so that a person would place his hand on his head (in order to protect himself) against excessive heat, and none amongst us was fasting but the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and Abdullah bin Rawaha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Abu Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Some people argued about the fasting of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) on the day of 'Arafa. Some of them said that he had been fasting, whereas the others said that he had not been fasting. I sent a cup of milk to him while he was riding his camel at 'Arafa, and he drank it.
narrator: Umm al-Fadl bint-al-Harith reported:

text: But he did not mention that he was mounting (riding on) his camel.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Nadr with the same chain of transmitters:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Abu Nadr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ on the authority of Umair, the freed slave of Umm al-Fadl, through the same chain of transmitters.

Some people among the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) were in doubt about fasting on the day of 'Arafa and we were with him on that day. I (Umm al-Fadl) sent him a cup of milk and he was halting at 'Arafa, and he drank that.
narrator: Umm al-Fadl (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§) is reported to have said:

text: People had doubt about the fasting of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) on the day of 'Arafa. Maimuna ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ sent him a cup of milk and he was halting at a place and he drank it and the people were seeing him.
narrator: Kuraib, the freed slave of Ibn 'Abbas ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ), reported from Maimuna ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ):

text: The Quraish used to fast on the day of 'Ashura in the pre-Islamic days and the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) also observed it. When he migrated to Medina, he himself observed this fast and commanded (others) to observe it. But when fasting during the month of Ramadan was made obligatory he said: He who wishes to observe this fast may do so, and he who wishes to abandon it may do so.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe fast, and said about the second part that he abandoned the (fast) of Ashura, and he who wished observed the fast and who wished otherwise abandoned it, and he did not hold it as the words of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as mentioned in the narration transmitted by Jarir.
narrator: This hadith is narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters, but he made no mention in the first part of the hadith:

text: In the pre-Islamic days fast was observed on the day of Ashura, but with the advent of Islam (its position was ascertained as that of a voluntary fast). Then he who wished to fast fasted, and he who liked to abandon it abandoned it.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had ordered to observe fast (on 'Ashura) before the fasting in Ramadan was made obligatory. But when it became obligatory, then he who wished fasted on the day of Ashura, and he who wished did not observe it (on that day).
narrator: A'isha ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: The Quraish used to fast on the day of Ashura during the pre-Islamic days. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) then commanded to fast on that day till (fasting) in Ramadan became obligatory. Then the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who wishes to fast should do so, and he who wishes to break it may do so.
narrator: A'isha ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: Pre-Islamic days used to observe fast on the day of Ashura and the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) observed it and the Muslims too (observed it) before fasting in Ramadan became obligatory. But when it became obligatory, the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: 'Ashura is one of the days of Allah, so he who wished should observe fast and he who wished otherwise should abandon it.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Abdullah through the same chain of transmitters.

The day of 'Ashura was mentioned before the Messenger of Allah (may peace he upon him). Thereupon the Messenger of Allah, ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: That was a day on which the people of pre-Islamic days used to observe fast. So he who amongst you likes to observe fast should do so, and he who does not like it should abandon it.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) said:

text: He heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ)bin say about the day of Ashura: It is a day on which the people of pre-Islamic days observed fast. So he who liked to fast on this day should do so, and he who liked to abandon it should abandon it. 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ did not observe fast except when it coincided (with the days when he was in the habit of observing voluntary fasts during every month).
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: The day of 'Ashura was mentioned before the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and he narrated a hadith like one (narrated above).
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: The day of 'Ashura was mentioned before the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and he said: It is a day when the people in the pre-Islamic days need to observe fast, so he who wishes to observe fast should do so, and he who wishes to abandon it should do so.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: When al-Ash'ath bin Qais entered the house of 'Abdullah he was having his breakfast. He ('Abdullah bin Umar) said: Abd Muhammad (al-Asha'th), come near to the breakfast. Thereupon he said: Is not today the day of 'Ashura? He ('Abdul-Rahman) said: Do you know what the day of 'Ashura is? He said: What is it? He said: It is a day on which the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) used to observe fast before the (fasting) in the month of Ramadan (became) obligatory. But when it became obligatory the (fasting of 'Ashura) was abandoned (as compulsory). Abu Kuraib said: He (the Holy Prophet) abandoned it.
narrator: Abdul-Rahman bin Yazid said:

text: When (fasting) in Ramadan was (made) obligatory, he aban- doned it (the practice of observing fast on Ashura).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated from Jarir on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters and he said (these words with a little bit of variation from the previous hadith):

text: Al-Ash'ath bin Qais ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ went to 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ on the day of 'Ashura while he was eating. He said: Abu Muhammad, come near and dine. Upon this he said: I am fasting. Thereupon he said: We used to observe fast and then (this practice) was abandoned.
narrator: Qais bin Sakan ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Ash'ath bin Qais ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ went to Ibn Mas'udd while he was eating on the day of Ashura. Thereupon he said: Abu Abdul-Rahman, it is the day of 'Ashura (and you are eating). Upon this he said: Fast was observed on (this day) before the (fasting) in Ramadan was made obligatory, but when it was made obligatory, (fasting on the day of 'Ashura) was abandoned. So if you are not fasting, then take food.
narrator: Alqama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) commanded us to observe fast on the day of Ashura and exhorted us to do it and was particular about it But when (fasting) in Ramadan was made obligatory, he hence. forth neither commanded us nor forbade us, nor was he so particular about it.
narrator: Jabir bin Samura ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He heard Mu'awiya bin Abu Sufyan delivering a sermon in Medina. i.e. when he came there (for Hajj). He delivered a sermon on the day of 'Ashura and said: People of Medina, where are your scholars? I heard the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say on this very day: It is the day of 'Ashura. Allah has not made fasting on This day obligatory for you but I am fasting. He who likes to observe fast among you should do so, and he who likes not to observe it may not observe it.
narrator: Abdul-Rahman ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Shihab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ  through the same chain of transmitters.

He heard Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying on a similar day: I am fasting today, so he who wishes to observe fast should do so; but he did not make mention of the rest of the hadith.
narrator: It was narrated from Az-Zuhri with this chain of transmitters:

text: When Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) came to Medina, he found the Jews observing the fast on the day of Ashura. They (the Jews) were asked about it and they said: It is the day on which Allah granted victory to Moses and (his people) Bani Isra'il over the Pharaoh and we observe fast out of gratitude to Him. Upon this the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: We have a closer connection with Moses than you have, and he commanded to observe fast on this day.
narrator: Ibn Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated by Ibn Bishr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with the same chain of transmitters (but with a slight variation) that he (the Holy Prophet) inquired of them (Jews) about it (fasting on the day of 'Ashura).
This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ayyub with the same chain of transmitters.
The day of 'Ashura was one which the Jews respected and they treated it as Id. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: You also observe fast on this day.
narrator: Abu Musa ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: The people of Khaibar (most of them were Jews) observed fast on the day of 'Ashura and they treated it as 'Id and gave their women ornaments and beautiful dresses to wear. The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: You (only) observe fast on this day.
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I do not know Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) singling out any day's fast and considering it more excellent than another, except this day (the day of Ashura) and this month, meaning the month of Ramadan.
narrator: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was asked about observing of fast on the day of Ashura, whereupon he said:

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of 'Ubaidullah bin Abi Yazid.

I went to Ibn 'Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) and he was reclining using his mantle as a pillow near the fountain of Zamzam. I said to him: Tell me about fasting on Ashura. He said: When you see the new moon of Muharram then count the (days) and observe fast on the 9th. I said to him: Is it how the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) observed the fast? He said: Yes.
narrator: Hakam bin al-'Araj reported:

text: I asked Ibn Abbas ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) as he was reclining using his cloak as a pillow near Zamzam about fasting on 'Ashura. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Hakam bin 'Araj reported:

text: When the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) fasted on the day of 'Ashura and commanded that it should he observed as a fast, they (his Companions) said to him: Messenger of Allah, it is a day which the Jews and Christians hold in high esteem. Thereupon the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When the next year comes, God willing, we would observe fast on the 9th But the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) died before the advent of the next year.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had said: If I live till the next (year), I would definitely observe fast on the 9th, and the narration transmitted by Abu Bakr is: He meant the day of Ashura.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sent a person of the tribe of Aslam on the day of 'Ashura and commanded him to declare to the people to observe fast in case they had not observed it, and to complete fast till evening if they had taken food
narrator: Salama bin al-Akwa' (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sent (a person) on the morning of Ashura to the villages of Ansar around Medina (with this message): He who got up in the morning fasting (without eating anything) he should complete his fast, and he who had had his breakfast in the morning, he should complete the rest of the day (without food). The Companions said; We henceforth observed fast on it (on the day of 'Ashura) and, God willing, made our children observe that. We went to the mosque and made toys out of wool for them and when anyone felt hungry and wept for food we gave them these toys till it was the time to break the fast.
narrator: Rubayyi' daughter of Mu'awwidh bin 'Afra' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: I asked Rubayyi' daugther of Mu'awwidh ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ about fasting on the day of 'Ashura. Thereupon she said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) sent his messenger to the villages of the Ansar, and the rest of the hadith is the same (but with this variation that one of the Companions) said: We used to make toys out of wool and took (them to the mosque) along with us. When they (the children) asked us for food, we gave them these toys to play with, and these made them forgetful till they completed their fast.
narrator: Khalid bin Dhakwan reported:

text: I observed Id along with Umar bin al-Khattab (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ). He came (out in an open space) and prayed and (after) completing it addressed the people and said: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) has forbidden the observing of fast on these two days. One is the day of Fitr (at the end of your fasts), and the second one, the day when you eat (the meat) of your sacrifices.
narrator: Abu Ubaid, the freed slave of Ibn Azhar, reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) forbade fasting on these two days. 'Id-ul-Adha and 'Id-ul-Fitr.
narrator: Abu Huraira (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: I heard from him (Abu Sa'id) a hadith which impressed me, and I said to him: Did you hear it from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ)? Thereupon he said: (Is it possible) that (I should) say about the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) that which I have not heard? I heard him saying: It is not proper to fast on two days, Adha and Fitr (at the end) of Ramadan.
narrator: Qaza'a related from Abu Sa'id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ He said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade to observe fast on two days the day of Fitr and the day of Sacrifice ('Id-ul-Adha).
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: A person came to Ibn 'Umar (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) and said: I had taken a vow that I would fast on the day (but it accidentally) synchronises with the day of Adha or the day of Fitr. Thereupon Ibn 'Umar (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) said: Allah, the Exalted, has commanded fulfilling of the vow, but the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) has forbidden the observance of fast on this day.
narrator: Ziyad bin Jubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Prophet ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade to observe fast on two days-the day of Fitr and the day of Adha.
narrator: A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) said:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The days of Tashriq are the days of eating and drinking
narrator: Nubaisha al-Hudhali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Nabaisha reported that Khalid said: I met Abu Malih and asked him and he narrated it to me from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). a hadith like one (narrated above) with this addition: And remembrance of Allah

The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) sent him and Aus bin Hadathan during the days of Tashriq to make this announcement: None but the believer would be admitted into Paradise, and the days of Mina' are the days meant for eating and drinking.
narrator: Ibn Ka'b bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported on the authority of his father:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibrahim bin Tahman with the same chain of transmitters but with this variation that he said: Both of them made the announcement.

I asked Jabir bin 'Abdullah (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) as he was circumambulating the House (Ka'ba) whether the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had forbidden the fasting on Friday, whereupon he said: Yes, by the Lord of this House.
narrator: Muhammad bin 'Abbad bin Ja'far ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: He asked Jabir bin Abdullah ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ), whether he had heard like this from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Muhammad bin 'Abbas bin Ja'far reported:

text: The Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: None among you should observe fast on Friday, but only that he observes fast before it and after it.
narrator: Abu Huraira (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Do not single out the night (preceding) Friday among the nights for prayer and do not single out Friday among days for fasting but only when anyone among you is accustomed to fast (on dates) which coincide with this day (Friday).
narrator: Abu Huraira ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: When this verse was revealed: And as for those who can fast (but do not) expiation is the feeding of a needy person (ii. 183), (he who liked to observe fast did observe it) and he who felt reluctant to observe it ate and expiated till the verse was revealed which abrogated it.
narrator: Salama bin Akwa' (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) reported:

text: We, during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), in one month of Ramadan (observed fast according to our liking). He who wished to fast lasted and he who wished to break broke it and fed a needy person as an expiation 1544 till this verse was revealed: He who witnesses among you the month (of Ramadan) he should observe fast during it (ii. 184).
narrator: Salama bin Akwa' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: I had to complete some of the fasts of Ramadan, but I could not do it but during the month of Sha'ban due to my duties to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) or with the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ).
narrator: Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported: I heard 'A'isha (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) as saying:

text: This hadith is narrated on the authority of Yahya bin Sa'id with the same chain of transmitters but with this variation that he said that ('A'isha did not observe fast but in Sha'ban) out of regard for the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).

It was narrated that Ibn-Juraij: "Yahya bin Saeed narrated to us..." - with this chain (a Hadith similar to no. 2687), except that in it he said: "That was because circumstances with the Messenger of Allah п·ә.

If one amongst us had to break fasts (of Ramadan due to natural reasons, i. e. menses) during the life of the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) she could not find it possible to complete them so long she had been in the presence of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) till Sha'ban commenced.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If anyone dies in a state (that he had to complete) some fasts, his heir must fast on his behalf.
narrator: A'isha ( ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§) reported:

text: A woman came to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and said: My mother has died, and fasts of a month are due from her. Thereupon he said: Don't you see that if debt was due from her, would you not pay it? She said: Yes (I would pay on her behalf). Thereupon he said: The debt of Allah deserves its payment more than (the payment of anyone else).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ ) reported:

text: A man came to the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and said: Messenger of Allah, my mother has died (in a state) that she had to observe fasts of a month (of Ramadan). Should I complete (them) on her behalf? thereupon he (the Holy Prophet) said: Would you not pay the debt if your mother had died (without paying it)? He said: Yes. He (the Holy Prophet) said: The debt of Allah deserves more that it should he paid.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩ° Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§) reported:

text: A person at Baqi' called another person as Abu'l- Qasim, and Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) turned towards him. He (the person who had uttered these words) said: Messenger of Allah, I did not mean you, but I called such and such (person), whereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: You may call yourself by my name, but not by my kunya.
narrator: Anas  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The names dearest to Allah are 'Abdullah and 'Abdul-Rahman.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: This hadith has been reported on the authority of Husain With the same chain of transmitters but no mention is made of these words: (I have been sent as a distributor), so I distribute amongst you.
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Give the name after my name, but do not give (the kunya of Abu'l-Qasim after my) kunya, for I am Abu'l-Qasim (in the sense) that I distribute amongst you (the spoils of war) and disseminate the knowledge (of revelation). This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Bakr but with a slight variation of wording.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: It was narrated from Al-Amash with this chain of narrators, and he said: "I have only been appointed as a Qasim (distributor), I distribute among you.
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: A child was born to a person from the Ansar and he made up his mind to give him the name of Muhammad. He came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and, asked him (about it), whereupon he said: The Ansar have done well to give the name (to your children) after my name, but do not give them the kunya after my kunya.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: 'The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: "I have only been sent as a Qasim (distributor), I distribute among you." And Sulaiman said: "I am only a Qasim (distributor), I distribute ('Allah blessings) among you."
narrator: It was narrated from Jabir bin AbdullahШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the prophet п·ә... a Hadith lie that of Zikriyya. In the Hadith of An-Nadr it is narrated that Shubah said: "And Husain and sulaiman added Husain said:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: A child was born in the house of a person amongst us, and he gave him the name of Qasim. We said: We will not allow you (to give the name) to your child as Qasim (and thus adopt the kunya of Abu'l-Qasim) and coal your eyes. He (that person) came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and made a mention of that to him, whereupon he said: Call your son 'Abd al-Rahman.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: We will not allow you to cool your eyes.
narrator: A hadith like that of Ibn 'Uyaynah was narrated from Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , except that he did not mention:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: Abu'l-Qasim ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Give name (to your children) after my name but do not give the kunya (of Abu'l- Qasim) after my kunya. 'Amr reported from Abu Huraira  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that he did not say that he had heard it directly from Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: When I came to Najran, they (the Christians of Najran) asked me: You read O sister of Harun (i.e. Hadrat Maryam) in the Qur'an, whereas Moses was born much before Jesus. When I came back to Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) I asked him about that, whereupon he said: The (people of the old age) used to give names (to their persons) after the names of Apostles and pious persons who had gone before them.
narrator: Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) forbade us to give names to our servants as these four names: Aflah (Successful), Rabdh (Profit), Yasar (Wealth), and Nafi' (Beneficial).
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: Auah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Don't give names to your servants as Rabdh, 'Yasar, Aflah and Nafi'.
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The dearest phrases to Allah are four: Subhan Allah (Hallowed be Allah), Al-Hamdulillah (Praise be to Allah), La ilaha illa-Allah (There is no deity but Allah), Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greater). There is no harm for you in which of them begin with (while remembering Allah). And do not give these names to your servants: Yasar and Rabah and Najih and Aflah. "There are only four, and do not ask me any more.
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: This hadith has been reported on the authority of Shu'ba and there is no mention but of the fact about giving the name to the servant and there is no mention of the four expressions (of remembrance) and he did not mention the four words
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) decided to forbid (his followers) to name persons as Ya'la (Elevated), Baraka (Blessing), Aflah (Successful), Yasar and Nafi', but I saw that he kept silent after that and he did not say anything until Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) died. And he did not forbid (his followers to do this), then 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ decided to prohibit (people) from giving these names, but later on gave up the idea.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) changed the name of 'Asiya (Disobedient) and said: You are Jamila (i.e. good and handsome). Ahmad (one of the narrators) narrated it with a slight variation of wording.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ had a daughter who was called 'Asiya. Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) gave her the name of Jamila.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: The name of Juwairiya ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ (the wife of the Holy Prophet п·ә) was Barra (Pious). Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) changed her name to Juwairiya and said: I did not like that it should be said: He had come out from Barra (Pious). The hadith transmitted on the authority of Ibn Abi 'Umar is slightly different from it.
narrator: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: The name of Zainab was Barra. It was said of her: She presents herself to be innocent. Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) gave her the name of Zainab.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: My name first was Barra. Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) gave me the name of Zainab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§. Then there entered (into the house of Allah's Prophet as a wife) Zainab, daughter of Jahsh, and her name was also Barra, and he gave her the name of Zainab.
narrator: Zainab, daughter of Umm Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Manners and Etiquette

text: I had given the name Barra to my daughter. Zainab, daughter of Abu Salama ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ told me that Allah's' Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) had forbidden me to give this name. (She said): I was also called Barra, but Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Don't hold yourself to be pious.
