text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) is reported to have said: By Him in Whose Hand is the life of Muhammad, a day would come to you when you would not be able to see me, and the glimpse of my face would be dearer to one than one's own family, one's property and in fact everything. This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ishaq with a slight variation of wording.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported so many 'ahadith from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and one among them:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: I am most akin to the son of Mary among the whole of mankind and the Prophets are of different mothers, but of one religion, and no Prophet was raised between me and him (Jesus Christ).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: I am most akin to Jesus Christ among the whole of mankind, and all the Prophets are of different mothers but belong to one religion and no Prophet was raised between me and Jesus.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: I am most close to Jesus, son of Mary, among the whole of mankind in this worldly life and the next life. They said: Allah's Messenger how is it? Thereupon he said: Prophets are brothers in faith, having different mothers. Their religion is, however, one and there is no Apostle between us (between I and Jesus Christ).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported many ahadith from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and one is:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: No child is born but he is pricked by the satan and he begins to weep because of the pricking of the satan except the son of Mary and his mother. Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ then said: You may recite if you so like (the verse): I seek Thy protection for her and her offspring against satan the accursed (iii. 36).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The newborn child is touched by the satan (when he comes in the world) and he starts crying because of the touch of satan. In the hadith transmitted on the authority of Shu'aib there is a slight variation of wording.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters (and the words are):

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The satan touches every son of Adam on the day when his mother gives birth to him with the exception of Mary and her son.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The crying of the child (starts) when the satan begins to prick him.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Jesus son of Mary saw a person committing theft; thereupon Jesus said to him: You committed theft. He said: Nay. By Him besides Whom there is no god (I have not committed theft). Thereupon Jesus said: I affirm my faith in Allah It is my own self that deceived me.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported A hadith from the Messenger of Allah ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) (and one of them was):

text: A person came to Allah, 's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and said: Who among the people is most deserving of a fine treatment from my hand? He said: Your mother. He again said: Then who (is the next one)? He said: Again it is your mother (who deserves the best treatment from you). He said: Then who (is the next one)? He (the Holy Prophet) said: Again, it is your mother. He (again) said: Then who? Thereupon he said: Then it is your father. In the hadith transmitted on the authority of Qutalba, there is no mention of the word the people .
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person said: Allah's Messenger, who amongst the people is most deserving of my good treatment? He said: Your mother, again your mother, again your mother, then your father, then your nearest relatives according to the order (of nearness).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). The rest of the hadith is the same as transmitted by jarir but with this addition: By your father, you would get the information.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Who amongst the people deserves the best treatment from me? Then he mentioned a Hadith lie that of Jarir (no. 6502).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Shubruma with the same chain of transmitters and the hadith transmitted on the authority of Wuhaib there is a slight variation of wording. Same is the case with the hadith transmitted on the authority of Muhammad bin Talha (and the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and sought permission (to participate) in Jihad, whereupon he (the Holy Prophet п·ә) said: Are your parents living? He said: Yes. Thereupon he (the Holy Prophet п·ә) said: You should put in your best efforts (in their) service.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text:
It was narrated from Abul-Abbas: I heard 'Abdullah bin Amr bin Al-As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ saying: A man came to the prophet п·ә...." And he mention aa similar report (as Hadith no. 6504).
Muslim said: Abul Abbas' name is As-saib bin Farrukh Al-Makki.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A similar report (as Hadith similar to no. 6505) was narrated from Habib with this chain of narrators.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: There came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) a person and said: I owe allegiance to you for migration and Jihad seeking reward only from Allah. He (the Holy Prophet) said: Is one from amongst your parents living? He said: Yes, of course, both are living. He further asked: Do you want to seek reward from Allah? He said: Yes. Thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Go back to your parents and accord them benevolent treatment.
narrator: Yazid bin Abu Habib reported that Na'im, the freed slave of Umm Salama, reported to him that 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin 'As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Let him be humbled into dust; let him be humbled into dust. It was said: Allah's Messenger, who is he? He said: He who sees either of his parents during their old age or he sees both of them, but he does not enter Paradise.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Let him be humbled, let him be humbled. It was said: Allah's Messenger, who is he? He said. He who finds his parents in old age, either one or both of them, and does not enter Paradise.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: May his nose be rubbed in the dust.... Let him be humbled thrice, and the rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The finest act of goodness is that a person should treat kindly the loved ones of his father.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I asked Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) about virtue and vice. He said: Virtue is a kind disposition and vice is what rankles in your heart and that you disapprove that people should come to know of it.
narrator: Nawwas bin Sam'an al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I stayed with Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) for one year. What obstructed me to migrate was (nothing) but (persistent) inquiries from him (about Islam). (It was a common observation) that when anyone of us migrated (to Medina) he ceased to ask (too many questions) from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). So I asked him about virtue and vice. Thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Virtue is a kind disposition and vice is what rankles in your mind and that you disapprove of its being known to the people.
narrator: Nawwas bin Sam'an ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The tie of kinship is suspended to the Throne and says: He who unites me Allah would unite him and he who severed me Allah would sever him.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The severer would not enter Paradise. Ibn Umar said that Sufyan (explained it as): One who severs the tie of kinship would not enter Paradise.
narrator: Jubair bin Mut'im reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The severer of the tie of kinship would not get into Paradise.
narrator: Jubair bin Mutlim reported that his father narrated to him:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A similar report (as Hadith no. 6521) was narrated from Az-Zuhri with this chain of narrators, but he said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah п·ә."
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who is desirous that his means of sustenance should be expanded for him or his age may be lengthened, should join the tie of relationship.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who likes that his sustenance should be expanded and his age may be lengthened should join the tie of kinship.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person said: Allah's Messenger, I have relatives with whom I try, to have close relationship, but they sever (this relation). I treat them well, but they treat me ill. I am sweet to them but they are harsh towards me. Upon this he (the Holy Prophet) said: If it is so as you say, then you in fact throw hot ashes (upon their faces) and there would always remain with you on behalf of Allah (an Angel to support you) who would keep you dominant over them so long as you adhere to this (path of righteousness).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Neither nurse mutual hatred, nor jealousy, nor enmity, and become as fellow brothers and servants of Allah. It is not lawful for a Muslim that he should keep his relations estranged with his brother beyond three days.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported that Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying like this. This hadith has been narrated through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: "Do not envy one another, Do not hate one another and be, Do not turn away from one another."
narrator: This hadith has been narrated through another chain of transmitters and the hadith transmitted on the authority of Abdul-Razziq (the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Nurse no grudge, nurse no aversion and do not sever ties of kinship and live like fellow-brothers as servants of Allah.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: It is not permissible for a Muslim to have estranged relations with his brother beyond three nights, the one turning one way and the other turning the other way when they meet; the better of the two is one who is the first to give a greeting.
narrator: Abu Ayyub Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: The one turning away and the other turning away when they meet and one avoids the other and the other also avoids him.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Zuhri with a slight variation of wording (and the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: It is not permissible for a Muslim to have estranged relations with his brother beyond three days.
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There should be no estranged relations beyond three days.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Avoid suspicion, for suspicion is the gravest lie in talk and do not be inquisitive about one another and do not spy upon one another and do not feel envy with the other, and nurse no malice, and nurse no aversion and hostility against one another. And be fellow-brothers and servants of Allah.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Don't have estranged relations (with the others) and don't nurse enmity and don't enter into a transaction when the other (has already entered) and be fellow-brothers and servants of Allah.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying. Don't nurse malice against one another, don't nurse aversion against one another and don't be inquisitive about one another and don't outbid one another (with a view to raising the price) and be fellow-brothers and servants of Allah.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Don't sever relations of kinship, don't bear enmity against one another, don't bear aversion against one another and don't feel envy against the other and live as fellow-brothers as Allah has commanded you.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters (and the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Don't bear aversion against one another and don't be jealous of one another and be servants of Allah.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Verily Allah does not look to your bodies nor to your faces but He looks to your hearts, and he pointed towards the heart with his fingers.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with some addition (and it is this):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Verily Allah does not look to your faces and your wealth but He looks to your heart and to your deeds.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: "Except who two forsake one another," from the report of Ibn Abdah. And Qutaibah said: "Except two who forsake one another."
narrator: A similar Hadith (as no. 6544) was narrated from Suhail, from his father, with the chain of narrators of Malik, except that in the Hadith of Ad-Darawardi it says: bu Huraira reported it as a marfu' hadith (and the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying The deeds of people would be presented every week on two days, viz. Monday and Thursday, and every believing servant would be granted pardon except the one in whose (heart) there is rancour against his brother and it would he said: Leave them and put them off until they are turned to reconciliation.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying The deeds of people would be presented every week on two days, viz. Monday and Thursday, and every believing servant would be granted pardon except the one in whose (heart) there is rancour against his brother and it would he said: Leave them and put them off until they are turned to reconciliation.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Verily. Allah would say on the Day of Resurrection: Where are those who have mutual love for My Glory's sake? Today I shall shelter them in My shadow when there is no other shadow but the shadow of Mine.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: A person visited his brother in another town and Allah deputed an Angel to wait for him on his way and when he came to him he said: Where do you intend to go? He said: I intend to go to my brother in this town. He said: Have you done any favour to him (the repayment of which you intend to get)? He said: No, excepting this that I love Mm for the sake of Allah, the Exalted and Glorious. Thereupon he said: I am a messenger to you from, Allah: (to inform you) that Allah loves you as you love him (for His sake).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hammid bin Salama with the same of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle (п·ә) as saying: The one who visits the sick is in fact like one who is in the fruit garden of Paradise so long as he does not return.
narrator: Abu Rabi' reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: He who visits the sick continues to remain in the fruit garden of Paradise until he returns.
narrator: Thauban ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, the freed slave of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Verily, when a Muslim visits his brother in Islam he is supposed to remain in the fruit garden of Paradise until he returns.
narrator: Thauban ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: He who visits the sick is supposed to remain in the fruit garden of Paradise. It was said: Allah's Messenger, what is this Khurfat-ul-jannah? He said: It is a place abounding in fruits.
narrator: Thauban ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, the freed slave of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Asim al-Ahwal with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I did not see anyone else being afflicted with more severe illness than Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). In the narration transmitted by 'Uthman there is a slight variation of wording.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash through other chains of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: He п·ә said: Yes, by Him in Whose Hand is my life, there is no Muslim upon the earth. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of jarir and the hadith transmitted on the authority of Abu Mu'awiya there is an addition of these words:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Some young men from the Quraish visited 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ as she was in Mina and they were laughing. She said: What makes you laugh? They said: Such and such person stumbled against the rope of the tent and he was about to break his neck or lose his eyes. She said: Don't laugh for I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: If a Muslim runs a thorn or (gets into trouble) severe than this, there is assured for him (a higher) rank and his sins are obliterated.
narrator: Aswad reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: A believer does not receive (the trouble) of running a thorn or more than that but Allah elevates him in rank or effaces his sins because of that.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: A believer does not undergo (the trouble) of running a thorn or more than that when Allah effaces his sins.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: The above hadith has been transmitted by Hisham with the same chain.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: There is no trouble that comes to a believer except that it obliterates from his sins, even if it is the pricking of a thorn
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: No trouble comes to a believer even if it is the pricking of a thorn that it becomes (the means) whereby his sins are effaced or his sins are obliterated. Yazid says: He does not know which word 'Urwa said (whether he said Qussa or Kuffira).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: There is nothing (in the form of trouble) that comes to a believer even if it is the pricking of a thorn that there is decreed for him by Allah good or his sins are obliterated.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: They heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Never a believer is stricken with discomfort, hardship or illness, grief or even with mental worry that his sins are not expiated for him.
narrator: Abu Sa'id and Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: When this verse was revealed: Whoever does evil will be requited for it , and when this was conveyed to the Muslims they were greatly perturbed. Thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Be moderate and stand firm in trouble that falls to the lot of a Muslim (as that) is an expiation for him; even stumbling on the path or the pricking of a thorn (are an expiation for him). Muslim said that 'Umar bin Abdul-Rahman Muhaisin was from amongst the people of Mecca.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) visited Umm Sa'ib or Umm Musayyib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and said: Umm Sa'ib or Umm Musayyib. why is it that you are shivering? She said: It is fever and may it not be blessed by Allah, whereupon he (the Holy Prophet) said: Don't curse fever for it expiates the sin of the children of Adam just as furnace removes the alloy of iron.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said to me: May I show you a woman of Paradise? I said: Yes. He said: Here is this dark-complexioned woman. She came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and said: I am suffering from falling sickness and I become naked; supplicate Allah for me, whereupon he (the Holy Prophet) said: Show endurance as you can do and there would be Paradise for you and, if you desire, I supplicate Allah that He may cure you. She said: I am prepared to show endurance (but the unbearable trouble is) that I become naked, so supplicate Allah that He should not let me become naked, so he supplicated for her.
narrator: Ata' bin Abi Rabih said:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: The hadith is narrated through Abu Mushir from Sa'id bin 'Abdul'Aziz except that the previous hadith through Marwan was the more complete of the two.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith is also transmitted through Ibna Bashr and Muhammad bin Muhammad through Abu Mashur through the same chain, narrated to its (full) extent
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying that he reported it from his Lord, the Exalted and Glorious: Verily I have made oppression unlawful for Me and for My servants too, so do not commit oppression. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Be on your guard against committing oppression, for oppression is a darkness on the Day of Resurrection, and be on your guard against stinginess for stinginess destroyed those who were before you, as it incited them to shed blood and make lawful what was unlawful for them.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Oppression is the darkness on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Ibu 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: A Muslim is the brother of a fellow-Muslim. He should neither commit oppression upon him nor ruin him, and he who meets the need of a brother, Allah would meet big needs, and he who relieved a Muslim from hardship Allah would relieve him from the hardships to which he would be put on the Day of Resurrection, and he who did not expose (the follies of a Muslim) Allah would conceal his follies on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Salim reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The claimants would get their claims on the Day of Resurrection so much so that the hornless sheep would get its claim from the horned sheep.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, grants respite to the oppressor. But when He lays Hand upon him, He does not then let him off. Re (the Holy Prophet) then recited this verse: Such is the chastisement of thy Lord when He chastises the towns (inhabited by) wrongdoing persons. Surely, His punishment is painful, severe (xi. 103).
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person from the emigrants struck the back of an Ansari. He came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and asked for compensation. Thereupon Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Leave it. for it is something disgusting. Ibn Mansur said that in the narration transmitted on the authority of Amr (these words are also found): I heard Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: A believer is like a brick for another believer, the one supporting the other.
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The similitude of believers in regard to mutual love, affection, fellow-feeling is that of one body; when any limb of it aches, the whole body aches, because of sleeplessness and fever.
narrator: Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported a hadith like this from Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The believers are like one person; if his head aches, the whole body aches with fever and sleeplessness.
narrator: Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Muslims are like one body of a person; if the eye is sore, the whole body aches, and if the head aches, the whole body aches.
narrator: Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Nu'man bin Bashir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: When two persons indulge in hurling (abuses) upon one another, it would be the first one who would be the sinner so long as the oppressed does not transgress the limits.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Charity does not in any way decrease the wealth and the servant who forgives Allah adds to his respect, and the one who shows humility Allah elevates him in the estimation (of the people).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Do you know what is backbiting? They (the Companions) said: Allah and His Messenger know best. Thereupon he (the Holy Prophet) said: Backbiting implies your talking about your brother in a manner which he does not like. It was said to him: What is your opinion about this that if I actually find (that failing) in my brother which I made a mention of? He said: If (that failing) is actually found (in him) what you assert, you in fact backbited him, and if that is not in him it is a slander.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The servant (whose fault) Allah conceals in this world, Allah would also conceal (his faults) on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The servant (who conceals) the faults of others in this world, Allah would conceal his faults on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person sought permission from Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) to see him. He said: Grant him permission. (and also added: ) He is a bad son of his tribe or he is a bad person of his tribe. When he came in he used kind words for him. 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported that she said: Allah's Messenger, you said about him what you had to say and then you treated him with kindness. He said: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, verily in the eye of Allah, worst amongst the person in rank on the Day of Resurrection is one whom the people abandon or desert out of the fear of indecency.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: What a bad man of his tribe, or what a bad members of the tribe he is!
narrator: A similar hadith (as no. 6596) was narrated from Ibn Al-Munkadir with this chain of narrators. But he did not say:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: He who is deprived of tenderly feelings is in fact deprived of good
narrator: Jarir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: He who is deprived of tenderly feelings is in fact deprived of good
narrator: Jarir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who is deprived of tenderly feelings is in fact deprived of good and he who is deprived of tenderly feelings is in fact deprived of good.
narrator: Jarir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , verily Allah is kind and He loves kindness and confers upon kindness which he does not confer upon severity and does not confer upon anything else besides it (kindness).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, the wife of Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Kindness is not to be found in anything but that it adds to its beauty and it is not withdrawn from anything but it makes it defective.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, the wife of Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ mounted upon a wild camel and she began to make that go round and round. Thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: You should show kindness, and then he made a mention of this hadith.
narrator: This hadith has been reported by Miqdam bin Shuraih bin Hani with the same chain of transmitters but with this addition:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: We were with Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) in some of his journeys and there was a woman from the Ansar riding a she-camel that it shied and she invoked curse upon that. Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) heard it and said: Unload that and set it free for it is accursed. 'Imran ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: I still perceive that (dromedary) walking amongst people and none taking any notice of that.
narrator: Imran bin Husain ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I perceive as if I am looking towards that dromedary, and in the hadith transmitted on the authority of Thaqafi (the words are): Unload it and make its back bare for it is accursed.
narrator: Imran ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A slave-girl was riding a dromedary and there was also the luggage of people upon it. that she suddenly saw Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). The way of the mountain was narrow and she said (to that dromedary): Go ahead (but that dromedary did not move). She (that slave-girl), out of anger, said: O Allah, let that (dromedary) be damned. Thereupon Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Let the dromedary on which the curse has been invoked not proceed with us.
narrator: Abu Burza al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: By Allah, let that accompany us not which has been damned, or he said like it.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Sulaiman Taimi with the same chain of transmitters but with a variation of words (and that is):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: It does not seem proper for a Siddiq that he should be an invoker of curse.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A similar report (as no. 6608) was narrated from Al-Ala bin Abdur Rahman with this chain of narrators.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: 'Abdul-Malik bin Marwan sent some domestic goods for decoration to Umm Darda' on his own behalf, and when it was night 'Abd al-Malik got up and called for the servant. It seemed as if he (the servant) was late (in responding to his call), so he ('Abd al-Malik) invoked curse upon him, and when it was morning Umm Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said to him: I heard you cursing your servant during the night when you called him, and she said: I heard Abu Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying that Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The invoker of curse would neither be intercessor nor witness on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Zaid bin Aslam reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zaid bin Aslam with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The invoker of curse would neither be witness nor intercessor on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Umm Darda' reported on the authority of Abu Darda' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: It was said to Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ): Invoke curse upon the polytheists, whereupon he said: I have not been sent as the invoker of curse, but I have been sent as mercy.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Two persons visited Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and both of them talked about a thing, of which I am not aware, but that annoyed him and he invoked curse upon both of them and hurled malediction, and when they went out I said: Allah's Messenger, the good would reach everyone but it would not reach these two. He said: Why so? I said: Because you have invoked curse and hurled malediction upon both of them. He said: Don't you know that I have made condition with my Lord saying thus: O Allah, I am a human being and that for a Muslim upon whom I invoke curse or hurl malediction make it a source of purity and reward.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: He had a private meeting with them and hurled malediction upon them and cursed them and sent them out.
narrator: This hadith has been reported on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters and the hadith transmitted on the authority of 'Isa (the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: O Allah, I am a human being and for any person amongst Muslims upon whom I hurl malediction or invoke curse or give him whipping make it a source of purity and mercy.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) a hadith like it but with a slight variation of wording.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of A'mash and in the hadith transmitted on the authority of 'Isa the words are: Make it a source of reward, and in the hadith transmitted on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (the words are): Make it a source of mercy.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: O Allah, I make a covenant with Thee against which Thou wouldst never go. I am a human being and thus for a Muslim whom I give any harm or whom I scold or upon whom I invoke curse or whom I beat, make this a source of blessing, purification and nearness to Thee on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Zinad with a slight variation of wording.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A hadith like this has been reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: He heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: O Allah, Muhammad is a human being. I lose my temper just as human beings lose temper, and I have held a covenant with Thee which Thou wouldst not break: For a believer whom I give any trouble or invoke curse or beat, make that an expiation (of his sins and a source of) his nearness to Thee on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Salim, the freed slave of Nasriyyin, said: I heard Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: He heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: O Allah, for any believing servant whom I curse make that as a source of nearness to Thee on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: I have held covenant with Thee which Thou wouldst not break, so for any believer whom I curse or beat, make that an expiation on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: I am a human being and I have made this term with my Lord, the Exalted and Glorious: For any servant amongst Muslims whom I curse or scold, make that a source of purity and reward.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ibn Juraij with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I was playing with children that Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) happened to pass by (us). I hid myself behind the door. He (the Prophet) came and patted my shoulders and said: Go and call Mu'awiya. I returned and said: He is busy in taking food. He again asked me to go and call Mu'awiya to him. I went (and came back) and said that he was busy in taking food, whereupon he said: May Allah not fill his belly! Ibn Muthanna, said: I asked Umm Umayya what he meant by the word Hatani. He said: It means he patted my shoulders.
narrator: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with a slight variation of wording.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The worst amongst the people is the double-faced one; he comes to some people with one face and to others with the other face.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: He heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The worst amongst people is one with the double face. He comes to some people with one face and to others with the other face.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: You will find the worst amongst the people one having double face. He comes to some people with one face and to the others with the other face.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: "She said: I did not hear him grant any concession concerning anything that people call lies except in three cases'" like the report narrated by yunus from Ibn Shihab.
narrator: A similar report was narrated by Muhammad bin Muslim bin Ubidullah bin Abdullah bin Shihab with this chain of narrators, except that in the Hadith of Salih it say:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Zuhri with a slight variation of wording.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Muhammad ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Should I inform you that slandering, that is in fact a tale-carrying which creates dissension amongst people, (and) he (further) said: The person tells the truth until he is recorded as truthful, and lie tells a lie until lie is recorded as a liar.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Truth leads one to Paradise and virtue leads one to Paradise and the person tells the truth until he is recorded as truthful, and lie leads to obscenity and obscenity leads to Hell, and the person tells a lie until he is recorded as a liar.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Telling of truth is a virtue and virtue leads to Paradise and the servant who endeavours to tell the truth is recorded as truthful, and lie is obscenity and obscenity leads to Hell-Fire, and the servant who endeavours to tell a lie is recorded as a liar. Ibn Abu Shaiba reported this from Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: It is obligatory for you to tell the truth, for truth leads to virtue and virtue leads to Paradise, and the man who continues to speak the truth and endeavours to tell the truth is eventually recorded as truthful with Allah, and beware of telling of a lie for telling of a lie leads to obscenity and obscenity leads to Hell-Fire, and the person who keeps telling lies and endeavours to tell a lie is recorded as a liar with Allah.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been reported on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters and no mention is made in the hadith transmitted on the authority of 'Isa (of these words): He who endeavours to tell the truth and endeavours to tell a lie, and in the hadith transmitted on the authority of Mushir (the words are): Until Allah records it .
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Whom do you count as Raqub amongst you? They (his Companions) said: One who has no children (the children are born unto him but they do not survive). Thereupon he (the Holy Prophet) said: He is not a Raqub but Raqub is one who does not find his child as the forerunner (in Paradise). He then said: Whom do you count as a wrestler amongst you? We said: He who wrestles with persons. He said: No, it is not he but one who controls himself when in a fit of rage.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The strong-man is not one who wrestles well but the strong man is one who controls himself when he is in a fit of rage.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: One is not strong because of one's wrestling skillfully. They said: Allah's Messenger, then who is strong? He said: He who controls his anger when he is in a fit of rage.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been reported on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Two persons abused each other in the presence of Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and the eyes of one of them became red as embers and the veins of his neck were swollen. Thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: I know of a wording, if he were to utter that, his fit of rage (would be no more and that wording is): I seek refuge with Allah from Satan the accursed. The person said: Do you find any madness in me? Ibn al-'Ala' said: Do you see it? And he made no mention of the person.
narrator: Sulaiman bin Surad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been reported on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When Allah fashioned Adam in Paradise, He left him as He liked him to leave. Then Iblis roamed round him to see what actually that was and when he found him hollow from within, he recognised that he had been created with a disposition that he would not have control over himself.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of Humaid with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When any one of you fights with his brother he should avoid striking at the face.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: When one amongst you strikes (at the face).
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Zinad and he said:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When any one of you fights with his brother, he should spare his face.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When any one of you fights with his brother, he should not slap at the face.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: When any one of you fights with his brother, he should avoid his face for Allah created Adam in His own image.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and in the hadith transmitted on the authority of Ibn Hatim Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) is reported to have said:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When any one of you fights with his brother, he should avoid the face.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Hisham bin Hakim bin Hizam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ happened to pass by some people in Syria who had been made to stand in the sun and olive-oil was being poured upon their heads. He said: What is this? It was said: They are being punished for (not paying) the Kharaj (the government revenue). Thereupon he said: Allah would punish those who torment people in this world (without any genuine reason).
narrator: Urwa reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Hisham bin Hakim bin Hizam ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ happened to pass by people, the farmers of Syria, who had been made to stand in the sun. He said: What is the matter with them? They said: They have been detained for Jizya. Thereupon Hisham ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: I bear testimony to the fact that I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Allah would torment those who torment people in the world.
narrator: Hisham reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: (Hisham bin Hakim) went to Umair bin Sa'd who was then ruler in Palestine and he narrated to him this hadith and he (submitting before the words of the Prophet) commanded that they should be let off and so they were let off.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hisham with the same chain of transmitters and he made this addition of Jarir:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Hisham bin Hakim ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ found a person (the ruler of Hims) who had been detaining some Nabateans in connection with the dues of Jizya. He said: What is this? I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Allah would torment those persons who torment people in the world.
narrator: Urwa bin Zubair reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person happened to come to the mosque with an arrow; thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said to him: Take hold of its pointed head.
narrator: Amr heard Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A person happened to come to the mosque with arrows and their iron-ends were exposed, so he was commanded that he should grasp the pointed heads so that these might not do any harm to a Muslim.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) commanded a person who had been distributing arrows freely in the mosque that he should not move about in the mosque but by catching hold of their iron-heads. Ibn Rumh narrated this with a slight variation of wording.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When any one of you happens to go to a meeting or the bazar with an arrow in his hand he must grasp its pointed head; then (he again said): He must grasp its pointed head. Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: By Allah, we did not court death until some of us had flung arrows upon the faces of one another.
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) assaying: He who amongst you moves in the mosque or in the bazar and there is an arrow with him he should take hold of its iron-head in his palm, so that none amongst the Muslims should receive any injury from it, or he said, should catch its iron-head.
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Abu'l-Qasim (the kunya of Allah's Messenger, п·ә), said: He who pointed a weapon towards his brother the angels invoke curse upon him even if he is his real brother so long as he does not abandon it (the pointing of weapon towards one's brother Muslim).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported a hadith like this from Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: None amongst you should point a weapon towards his brother, for he does not know that Satan might cause the weapon (to slip) from his hand and (he may injure anyone) and thus he may fall into Hell-Fire.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported a hadith from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ); (one of them was this):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: While a person was going along the path he found a thorny branch upon it. He pushed it to a side and Allah approved (this action) of his and (as a mark of appreciation) granted him pardon.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: A person while walking along the path saw the branches of a tree lying there. He said: By Allah, I shall remove these from this so that these may not do harm to the Muslims, and he was admitted to Paradise.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying that he saw a person enjoying himself in Paradise because of the tree that he cut from the path which was a source of inconvenience to the people.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying that there was a tree which caused inconvenience to the Muslims; a person came there and cut that (tree) (and thus entered ) Paradise).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: I said: Allah's Messenger, teach me something so that I may derive benefit from it. He said: Remove the troublesome thing from the paths of the Muslims.
narrator: Abu Barza ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: He said to Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ): Allah's Messenger, I do not know whether I would survive after you, so confer upon me something by which Allah should benefit me. Thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Do this and that and remove the troublesome things from the paths.
narrator: Abu Barza ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: A woman was tormented because of a cat which she had confined until it died and she had to get into Hell. She did not allow it either to eat or drink as it was confined, nor did she free it so that it might eat the vermin of the earth.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A Hadith like that of Jawairiya was narrated from Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, from the prophet п·ә.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: A woman was tormented because of a cat which she had tied and thus allowed it neither to eat or drink nor set it free so that it might eat the vermin of the earth.
narrator: This hadith has been reported on the authority of Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters. And Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: A woman got into Hell-Fire because of a cat whom she had tied, and thus it could not eat, and she did not let it free so that it could devour the vermin of the earth, until it died.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) A hadith out of which one was this:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, said: Glory is His lower garment and Majesty is His cloak and (Allah says, ) He who contends with Me in regard to them I shall torment him.
narrator: Abu Sa'id Khudri and Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) stated that a person said: Allah would not forgive such and such (person). Thereupon Allah the Exalted and Glorious, said: Who is he who adjures about Me that I would not grant pardon to so and so; I have granted pardon to so and so and blotted out his deeds (who took an oath that I would not grant pardon to him).
narrator: Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger (may peace he upon him) as saying: Many a person with disheveled hair and covered with dust is turned away from the doors (whereas he is held in such a high esteem by Allah) that if he were to adjure in the name of Allah (about anything) Allah would fulfil that.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When a person says that people are ruined he is himself ruined. Abu Ishaq said: I do not know whether he said ahlakahum or ahlakuhum.
narrator: Abu Huraira reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Suhail with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Gabriel impressed upon me (kind treatment) towards the neighbour (so much) that I thought as if he would confer upon him the (right) of inheritance.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Gabriel impressed upon me (the kind treatment) towards the neighbour (so much) that I thought as if he would soon confer upon him the (right) of inheritance.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Abu Dharr, when you prepare the broth, add water to that and give that (as a present) to your neighbour.
narrator: Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) commanded me thus: Whenever you prepare a broth, add water to it, and have in your mind the members of the household of your neighbours and then give them out of this with courtesy.
narrator: Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) said to me: Don't consider anything insignificant out of good things even if it is that you meet your brother with a cheerful countenance.
narrator: Abu Dharr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: When any needy (person) came to Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) with a need he commanded him to his Companions, saying: Make a recommendation for him, and you would get the reward. Allah, however, gives the verdict through the tongue of His Apostle what He likes most.
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The similitude of good company and that of bad company is that of the owner of musk and of the one (iron-smith) blowing bellows, and the owner of musk would either offer you free of charge or you would buy it from him or you would. smell its pleasant odour, and so far as one who blows the. bellows is concerned, he would either burn your clothes or you shall have to smell its repugnant smell.
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A woman came to me along with her two daughters. She asked me for (charity) but she found nothing with me except one date, so I gave her that. She accepted it and then divided it between her two daughters and herself ate nothing out of that. She then got up and went out, and so did her two daughters. (In the meanwhile) Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) visited me and I narrated to him her story. Thereupon Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who is involved (in the responsibility) of (bringing up) daughters, and he accords benevolent treatment towards them, there would be protection for him against Hell-Fire.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the wife of Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: A poor woman came to me along with her daughters. I gave her three dates. She gave a date to each of them and then she took up one date and brought that to her mouth in order to eat that, but her daughters expressed desire to eat it. She then divided the date that she intended to eat between them. This (kind) treatment of her impressed me and I mentioned that which she did to Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). Thereupon he said: Verily Allah has assured Paradise for her, because of (this act) of her, or He has rescued her from Hell-Fire.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: He, who brought up two girls properly till they grew up, he and I would come (together) (very closely) on the Day of Resurrection, and he interlaced his fingers (for explaining the point of nearness between him and that person).
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Anyone amongst the Muslims, three of whose children die, and he resigns himself calmly to the will of God, Fire will not touch him but for the fulfilment of the oath.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: He would enter into Fire, except for the fulfilment of the oath.
narrator: This hadith has been reported by Zuhri on the authority of Malik, and in the hadith transmitted on the authority of Sufyan (the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said to a woman of the Ansar: In case anyone amongst you sees the sad demise of three children of (hers) and she resigns herself to the will of God hoping to get reward, she would be admitted to Paradise. A woman from amongst them said: Allah's Messenger, even if they (the children who die) are two. Thereupon, he (the Holy Prophet п·ә ) said: Even if they are two.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Virtue, Enjoining Good Manners, and Joining of the Ties of Kinship
