text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: When it will be the Day of Resurrection Allah would deliver to every Muslim a Jew or a Christian and say: That is your rescue from Hell-Fire.
narrator: Abu Musa' ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: No Muslim would die but Allah would admit in his stead a Jew or a Christian in Hell-Fire. 'Umar bin Abdul-'Aziz took an oath: By One besides Whom there is no god but He, thrice that his father had narrated that to him from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) and he swore to him. Saeed did not tell me that he asked him to swear, but he did not object to what 'Awn said.
narrator: Abu Burda reported on the authority of his father:

text: Qatadah narrated a Hadith like that of 'Affan (no.7012), with this chain of narrators, and he said: "Aun bin Utba."

Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There would come people amongst the Muslims on the Day of Resurrection with as heavy sins as a mountain, and Allah would forgive them and He would place in their stead the Jews and the Christians. (As far as I think), Abu Raub said: I do not know as to who is in doubt. Abu Burda said: I narrated it to 'Umar bin 'Abdul-'Aziz, whereupon he said: Was it your father who narrated it to you from Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ)? I said: Yes.
narrator: Abu Burda reported:

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Zuhri with the same chain of transmitters.

A person was charged with fornication with the slave girl of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). Thereupon Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said to 'Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ: Go and strike his neck. 'Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came to him and he found him in a well making his body cool. 'Ali said to him: Come out, and as he took hold of his hand and brought him out, he found that his sexual organ had been cut. Hadrat 'Ali ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ refrained from striking his neck. He came to Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) and said: Allah's Messenger, he has not even the sexual organ with him.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) came to the grave of 'Abdullah bin Ubayy, brought him out from that, placed him on his knee and put his saliva in his mouth and shrouded him in his own shirt and Allah knows best.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) came to the grave of 'Abdullah bin Ubayy as he was placed in that. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: He then abandoned offering (funeral) prayer for them.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Abaidullah with the same chain of transmitters but with this addition:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: There gathered near the Ka'bah three persons amongst whom two were Quraishi and one was a Thaqafi or two were Thaqafis and one was a Quraishi. They lacked understanding but wore more flesh. One of them said: Do you think that Allah hears as we speak? The other one said: He does hear when we speak loudly and He does not hear when we speak in undertones, and still the other one said: If He listens when we speak loudly, He also listens when we speak in undertones. It was on this occasion that this verse was revealed: You did not conceal yourselves lest your ears, your eyes and your skins would stand witness against you (xli. 22).
narrator: Ibn Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) set out for Uhud. Some of those persons who were with them came back. The Companions of Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) were divided in two groups. One group said: We would kill them, and the other one said: No, this should not be done, and it was on this occasion that this verse was revealed: Why should you, then, be two parties in relation to hypocrites? (iv. 88).
narrator: Zaid bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who would climb this hill of Murar. The rest of the hadith is the same but with this variation that it was a desert Arab who was finding out his lost thing.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) came back from a journey and as he was near Medina, there was such a violent gale that the mountain seemed to be pressed. Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: This wind has perhaps been made to blow for the death of a hypocrite, and as he reached Medina a notorious hypocrite from amongst the hypocrites had died.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: We went along with Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) to visit a person suffering from fever. When I placed my hand upon him, I said: By Allah, I have never seen, till this day, a person running higher temperature than he. Thereupon Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ), turning his face to his companions, said: May I not inform you of a severer temperature than this which these two persons would run on the Day of Resurrection? These two man who were riding with their backs towards the prophet п·ә (heading away from him)" -referring to two man who were among his companions at that time.
narrator: Iyas reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The similitude of a hypocrite is that of a sheep which roams aimlessly between two flocks. She goes to one at one time and to the other at another time.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying like this but with this change of words: She sometimes finds a way in one flock and then in another flock.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: A bulky person would be brought on the Day of judgment and he would not carry the weight to the eye of Allah equal even to that of a gnat. Nor shall We set up a balance for them on the Day of Resurrection (xviii. 105).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: A Jew scholar came to Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ). The rest of the hadith is the same, but there is no mention of then He would stir them. But there is this addition: I saw Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) smiling so much that his front teeth appeared and testifying him (the Jew scholar); then Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited the verse: And they honour not Allah with the honour due to Him (xxxix. 67).
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Mansur with the same chain of transmitter (and the words are):
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: A person from the People of the Book came to Allah's Apostle (п·ә) and said: Abu al-Qasim, verily, Allah holds the Heavens upon one finger and the earths upon one finger and the trees and moist earth upon one finger and in fact the whole of the creation upon one finger and then say: I am the King. I am the King. And he (the narrator) further said: I saw Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) smiling until his front teeth became visible and then he recited the verse: And they measure not the power of Allah with His true measure (39:67).
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The trees no one finger, the soil on one finger. In the Hadith of Jarir it does not say: "And the creation on one finger," but in his Hadith it says: "The mountains on one finger." In the Hadith of Jarir it add. "Confirming it and liking what he said."
narrator: It was narrated from Al-Amash with this chain of narrators in (a narration similar to no. 7048), except that their Hadith it says:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, will take in His grip the Earth on the Day of Judgment and He would roll up the sky in His right hand and would say: I am the Lord; where are the sovereigns of the world?
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) saying: Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, would fold the Heavens on the Day of Judgment and then He would place them on His right hand and say: I am the Lord; where are the haughty and where are the proud (today)? He would fold the' earth (placing it) on the left hand and say: I am the Lord; where are the haughty and where are the proud (today)?
narrator: Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: He saw Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as he narrated Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, would take in His hand His Heavens and His Earth, and would say: I am Allah. And He would clench His fingers and then would open them (and say): I am your Lord. I saw the pulpit in commotion from underneath because of something (vib-ating) there. And (I felt this commotion so much) that I said (to myself): It would fall with the Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) upon it.
narrator: Abdullah bin Miqsam reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: 'Abdullah bin 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported: I saw Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) upon the pulpit and he was saying that the Mighty Lord, the Exalted and Glorious would take hold of the Heavens and earth in His hand. The rest of the hadith is the same,
narrator: Abdullah bin Miqsam reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…) took hold of my hands and said: Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, created the clay on Saturday and He created the mountains on Sunday and He created the trees on Monday and He created the things entailing labour on Tuesday and created light on Wednesday and He caused the animals to spread on Thursday and created Adam (peace be upon him) after 'Asr on Friday; the last creation at the last hour of the hours of Friday, i.e. between afternoon and night. This hadith is narrated through another chain of transmitters.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: The people will be assembled on the Day of Resurrection on a white plain with a reddish tinge like the loaf of white bread with no marks set up for anyone.
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: I asked Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) about the words of Allah, the Exalted and Glorious: The day when the earth would be changed for another earth and Heaven would be changed for another Heaven (XiV. 48), (and inquired: ) (Allah's Messenger), where would the people be on that day? He said: They would be on the Sirat.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If ten scholars of the Jews would follow me, no Jew would be left upon the surface of the earth who would not embrace Islam.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: I was walking along with Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) in a field of Medina. a Hadith lie that of Hafs (no. 7059).
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text:
The prophet п·ә was among the date palms, leaving on a palm branch...' then he mention a hadith like the Hadith narrated from Al-Amash (no. 7060).
Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) was reclining against a tree in the garden. The rest of the hadith is the same with a slight variation of wording.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Al-`As bin Wa'il owed debt to me. I came to him in order to demand that. He said: I will never repay you unless you belie Muhammad. I said: I would never belie Muhammad until you die and you are again raised up. He said: When I would be raised up after death, I would repay your debt when I would get my property and children back. Waki` said: This is how Al-A`mash has narrated and it was on this occasion that this verse was revealed: Hast thou seen him who disbelieves in Our message and says: I shall certainly be given wealth and children (xix, 77) up to he would come to Us alone (xix, 80).
narrator: Khabbab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: "I was a blacksmith during the jahiliyyah, I did some work for Al-As bin Wail, I came to him to ask him to pay me.
narrator: A Hadith like that of Waki (no. 7062) was narrated from Al-Amash with this chain of narrators, and in the hadith of Jarir it says:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Abu Jahl said: O Allah, if he is true, then shower upon us the volley of stones from the sky or inflict upon us a grievous torment, and it was on this occasion that this verse was revealed: 'Allah would never torment them so long as you are amongst them. And Allah is not going to torment them as long as they seek forgiveness. And why is it that Allah should not torment them and they prevent people from coming to the sacred mosque.... (viii. 34) to the end.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Five signs have (become things) of the past (and have proved the truth of the Holy Prophet): (Enveloping) by the smoke, inevitable (punishment to the Meccans at Badr), (the victory of) Rome, (violent) seizing (of the Meccans at Badr) and (the splitting up of) the Moon.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The words of Allah, the Exalted and Glorious: We will, surely, make them taste the lesser punishment before the severer punishment (that haply they may return) (xxxii. 21) imply the torments of the world. (victory of) Rome, seizing (of the Meccans), or smoke. And Shubah was in doubt about seizing or smoke.
narrator: Ubayy bin Ka'b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The moon was split up during lifetime by Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) in two parts and Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Bear testimony to this.
narrator: Abu Ma'mar reported on the authority of Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: We were along with Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) at Mina, that moon was split up into two. One of its parts was behind the mountain and the other one was on this side of the mountain. Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said to us: Bear witness to this.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ (who said):
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The moon was split up in two parts during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ). The mountain covered one of its parts and one part of it was above the mountain and Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: 'O Allah, Bear witness to this.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: A similar report (as Hadith no. 7073) was narrated from Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , from the Prophet.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: "And he said: 'Bear witness, Bear witness.'"
narrator: A similar Hadith (as no. 7073) was narrated from Shobah, but in the Hadith of Ibn 'Adiyy  it says:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The people of Mecca demanded from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) that he should show them (some) signs (miracles) and he showed twice the splitting of the moon.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The moon was split up in two parts and in the hadith recorded in Abu Dawud, the words are: The moon was split up into two parts during the life of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ).
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The moon was split up during the lifetime of Allah's Messenger (п·ә).
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: There is none to show more patience at listening to the most irksome things than Allah, the Exalted and Glorious. 'Partnership is associated to Him (polytheism), and (fatherhood) of a child is attributed to Him, but in spite of this He protects them (people) and provides them sustenance.'
narrator: Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: A similar report (as Hadith no. 7080) was narrated from Abu Musa ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the prophet п·ә, except the words, "a son is attributed to him," which he did not mention.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that none is more forbearing in listening to the most irksome things than Allah, the Exalted. They associate rivals with him, attribute sonhood to Him, but in spite of this He provides them sustenance, grants them safety, confers upon them so many things.
narrator: Abdullah bin Qais ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Allah, the Exalted and High, would say to one who shall have to undergo the least torture (on the Day of Resurrection): Would you like to go as ransom if you had all worldly riches; he would say: Yes. Allah would say to him: When you were in the loins of Adam, I demanded from you something easier than this that you should not associate anything with Me. (The narrator says): I think He also said: I would not cause you to enter Hell-Fire but you defied and attributed Divinity (to others besides Me).
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: I shall not cause him to enter Hell. (The words subsequent to these) have not been mentioned.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters, but with a slight variation of wording (and the words are):
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger (п·ә) said: It would be said to the non-believers on the Day of Resurrection: If you were to possess gold, filling the whole earth, would you like to secure your freedom by paying that? He would say: Yes. Thereupon it would be said to him: Something easier (than this) was demanded from you (but you paid no heed to it).
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: It would be said to him: You have told a lie; what had been demanded from you was quite easier than this (the belief in the Oneness of Allah).
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported this hadith through another chain of transmitters and the words are:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: A person said: Allah's Messenger, how the non-believers be made to assemble on the Day of Resurrection (by crawling) on their faces? Thereupon he said: Is He Who is powerful to make them walk on their feet not powerful enough to make them (crawl) upon their faces on the Day of Resurrection? Qatada said: Of course, it is so. (He adjured): By the might of our Lord.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Verily, Allah does not treat a believer unjustly in regard to his virtues. He would confer upon him (His blessing) in this world and would give him reward in the Hereafter. And as regards a non-believer, he would be made to taste the reward (of virtue in this world) what he has done for himself so much that when it would be the Hereafter, he would find no virtue for which he should be rewarded.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) thus told him: When a non-believer does good he is made to taste Its reward in this world. And so far as the believer is concerned, Allah stores (the reward) of his virtues for the Hereafter and provides him sustenance in accordance with his obedience to Him.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The Similitude of a believer is that of (a standing) crop which the air continues to toss from one side to another; in the same way a believer always (receives the strokes) of misfortune. The similitude of a hypocrite is that of a cypress tree which does not move until it is uprooted.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ  Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: It was narrated from Az-Zuhri with this chin of narrators (a Hadith similar to no. 7092).
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said that the similitude of a believer is that of a standing crop in a field which is shaken by wind and then it comes to its original position but it stands at its roots. The similitude of a non-believer is that of a cypress tree which stands on its roots and nothing shakes it but it is uprooted (with) one (violent stroke).
narrator: Ka'b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: The similitude of a believer is that of a standing crop. The wind sometimes shakes it and sometimes raises it up and then it comes to its destined end. And the similitude of a hypocrite is that of a cypress tree which is not affected by anything but is uprooted once for all.
narrator: Ka'b bin Malik reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: "The likeness of the disbelievers is that of a cedar," and Ibn Hatim said: "The likeness of the Hypocrites," the Zuhair said.
narrator: It was narrated from 'Abdullah bin K'ab bin Malik from his father from the prophet п·ә (a Hadith similar to no. 7095), except that Mahmud said in his report from Bishr:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: "The likeness of the disbeliever is that of a cedar.
narrator: It was narrated from Abdullah bin K'ab bin Malik, and Ibn Bash-shar: It was narrated from the son of K'ab bin Malik, from his father, from the prophet п·ә" -a similar Hadith (as no. 7095). They both said in their hadith from Yahya:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) one day said to his Companions: Tell me about a tree which has resemblance with a believer. The people began to mention (different) trees of the forest. Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: It was instilled in my mind or in my heart and it stuck therein that it implied the date- palm tree. I made up my mind to make a mention of that but could not do that because of the presence of the elderly people there. When there was a hush amongst them (after they had expressed their views), Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: It Is the date-palm tree.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: (I have had the privilege) of accompanying Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ up to Medina but I did not hear him narrate anything from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) except one hadith. And he said: We were in the presence of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) that there was brought to him the kernel of a date. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Mujahid said:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: I heard Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying: There was brought to Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) the kernel. The rest of the hadith is tile same.
narrator: Mujahid reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: We were in the company of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that he said: Tell me of a tree which has resemblance to a Muslim and the leaves of which do not wither. Ibrahim said that perhaps Imam Muslim had stated like this: It constantly bears fruit but I have, however, seen [It does not bear fruit constantly]. Ibn Umar  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: It crossed my mind that it could be the date-palm tree, but as I saw Aba Bakr and Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ observe silence, I did not deem it fit that I should speak or I should say something. 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: Had you said so, it would have been dearer to me than such and such thing.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: I heard Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Verily, the Satan has lost all hopes that the worshippers would ever worship (him) in the peninsula of Arabia, but he (is hopeful) that he would sow the seed of dissension amongst them.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) as saying: The throne of Iblis is upon the ocean and he sends detachments (to different parts) in order to put people to trial and the most important figure in his eyes is one who is most notorious in sowing the seed of dissension.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Iblis places his throne upon water; he then sends detachments (for creating dissension) ; the nearer to him in rank are those who are most notorious in creating dissension. One of them comes and says: I did so and so. And he says: You have done nothing. Then one amongst them comes and says: I did not spare so and so until I sowed the seed of discord between a husband and a wife. The Satan goes near him and says: You have done well. A'mash said: He then embraces him.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Apostle (п·ә) said: The Satan sends detachments of his own in order to put people to trial and the highest in rank, in his eyes, is one who is most notorious in sowing the seed of dissension.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: There is none amongst you with whom is not an attach from amongst the jinn (devil). They (the Companions) said: Allah's Messenger, with you too? Thereupon he said: Yes, but Allah helps me against him and so I am safe from his hand and he does not command me but for good.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: "There is appointed over him his companion from among the jinn nd his companion from among the angels.
narrator: A similar Hadith (as no. 7108) was narrated from Mansur with this chin of Jarir, but in the Hadith of Sufyan it says:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: None amongst you would attain salvation purely because of his deeds. A person said: Allah's Messenger, even you? Thereupon he said: Yes, not even I except that Allah wraps me in Mercy, but you should act with moderation.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: "... with his mercy and grace." And he did not mention (the words): "But aims to do good."
narrator: It was narrated from Bukair bin al-Ashajj with this chain (a Hadith similar to no. 7111), except that he said:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There is none whose deeds alone would entitle him to get into Paradise. It was said to him: And, Allah's Messenger, not even you? Thereupon he said: Not even I, but that my Lord wraps me in Mercy.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There is none amongst you whose deeds alone would attain salvation for him. They (the Companions) said: Allah's Messenger, not even you? He (the Holy Prophet) said: Not even I, but that Allah wraps me in Mercy and He grants me pardon. Ibn 'Aun pointed towards his head with his hand saying: Not even I, but that Allah wraps me in His Forgiveness and Mercy.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There is none whose deeds alone can'secure salvation for him. They said: Allah's Messenger, not even you? Thereupon he said: Not even I, but that, the Mercy of Allah should take hold of me.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: None amongst you can get. into Paradise by virtue of his deeds alone. They said: Allah's Messenger, not even you? Thereupon he said: Not even I, but that Allah should wrap me in His Grace and Mercy.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Observe moderation in deeds (and if it is not possible, try to be near moderation) and understand that none amongst you can attain salvation because of his deeds alone. They said: Allah's Messenger, not even you? Thereupon he said: Not even I, but that Allah should wrap me in His Mercy and Grace.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: A hadith like this has been narrated on the authority of A'mash through two other chains of transmitters. The wording is, however, the same.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: I heard Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: None of you would get into Paradise because of his good deeds alone, and he would not be rescued from Fire, not even I, but because of the Mercy of Allah.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) used to say: Observe moderation (in doing deeds), and if you fail to observe it perfectly, try to do as much as you can do (to live up to this ideal of moderation) and be happy for none would be able to get into Paradise because of his deeds alone. They (the Companions of the Holy Prophet) said: Allah's Messenger, not even you? Thereupon he said: Not even I, but that Allah wraps me in His Mercy, and bear this in mind that the deed loved most by Allah is one which is done constantly even though it is small.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ the wife of Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) worshipped so much that his feet were swollen. It was said to him: (Why do you undergo so much hardship despite the fact that) Allah has pardoned for you your earlier and later sins? Thereupon he said: May I not (prove myself) to be a grateful servant (of Allah)?
narrator: Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) kept standing in prayer (for such long hours) that his feet were swollen. They (his Companions) said: Verily, Allah has pardoned for thee the earlier and the later of thine sins. Thereupon he said: Should I not prove myself to be a grateful servant (of Allah)?
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Mughira bin Shu'ba ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and the words are:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: When Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) occupied himself in prayer, he observed such a (long) qiyam (posture of standing in prayer) that his feet were swollen. A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said: Allah's Messenger you do this (in spite of the fact) that your earlier and later sins have been pardoned for you? Thereupon, he said. A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ should I not prove myself to be a thanksgiving servant (of Allah)?
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through other chains of transmitters.
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: 'Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ used to give us sermon on every Thursday. A person said: Abu 'Abd al-Rahman, we love your talk and so we yearn (to listen to you) and earnestly desire that you should deliver us lecture every day. Thereupon he said: There is nothing to hinder me in giving you talk (every day) but the fact that you may be bored. Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) did not deliver sermons on certain days (fearing that we might be bored).
narrator: Shaqiq bin Wa'il reported:
chapterName: Characteristics of The Hypocrites And Rulings Concerning Them

text: "The Messenger of Allah п·ә said: The Paradise is surrounded by hardships and the Hell-Fire is surrounded by temptations.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying that: Allah the Exalted and Glorious, said: I have prepared for My pious servants which no eye has ever seen, and no ear has ever heard, and no human heart has ever perceived but it is testified by the Book of Allah. He then recited: No soul knows what comfort has been concealed from them, as a reward for what they did . (xxxii. 17)
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, said: I have prepared for My pious servants which no eye (has ever) seen, no ear has (ever) heard and no human heart has ever perceived those bounties leaving apart (those bounties) about which Allah has informed you.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said that Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, said: I have prepared for My pious servants which the eye has seen not, and the ear has heard not and no human heart has ever perceived such bounties leaving aside those about which Allah has informed you. He then recited: No soul knows what comfort has been hidden for thein .
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I was in the company of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) that he gave a description of Paradise and then Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) concluded with these words: There would be bounties which the eye has not seen and the ear has not heard and no human heart has ever perceived them. He then recited this verse: They forsake (their) beds, calling upon their Lord in fear and in hope, and spend out of what We have given them. So no soul knows what refreshment of the eyes is hidden for them: a reward for what they did (xxxii. 16-17)
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd as-Sa'idi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: In Paradise, there is a tree under the shadow of which a rider can travel for a hundred years.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: "He will not be able to cover this distance."
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters with the addition of these words:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: In Paradise, there is a tree under the shadow of which a rider can travel for a hundred years without covering (the distance) completely.
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: In Paradise, there is a tree under the shadow of which a rider of a fine and swift-footed horse would travel for a hundred years without covering the distance completely.
narrator: This hadith has also been transmitted on the authority of Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The inmates of Paradise will look to the upper apartment of Paradise as you see the planets in the sky.
narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as saying: As you see the shining planets in the eastern and western (sides of) horizon.
narrator: Abi 'Ayyash and he said:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Hazim through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The inmates of Paradise would see the inmates of the apartment over them just as you see the shining planets which remain in the eastern and the western horizon because of the superiority some have over others. They said: Allah's Messenger, would in these abodes of Apostles others besides them not be able to reach? He said: Yes, they will, by Him, in Whose hand is my life, those who believe in God and acknowledge the Truth, will reach them.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The people most loved by me from amongst my Ummah would be those who would come after me but everyone amongst them would have the keenest desire to catch a glimpse of me even at the cost of his family and wealth.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: In Paradise there is a street to which they would come every Friday. The north wind will blow and would scatter fragrance on their faces and on their clothes and would add to their beauty and loveliness, and then they would go back to their family after having an added lustre to their beauty and loveliness, and their family would say to them: By Allah, you have been increased in beauty and loveliness after leaving us, and they would say: By Allah, you have also increased in beauty and loveliness after us.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Some (persons) stated with a sense of pride and some discussed whether there would be more men in Paradise or more women. It was upon this that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported that Abu'l Qasim (the Holy Prophet) ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The (members) of the first group to get into Paradise would have their faces as bright as full moon during the night, and the next to this group would have their faces as bright as the shining stars in the sky, and every person would have two wives and the marrow of their shanks would glimmer beneath the flesh and there would be none without a wife in Paradise.
narrator: Muhammad reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: "Men and women disputed as to which of them would from the majority in Paradise. They ask Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and he said:... '" a Hadith like that of Ibn 'Ulayyah (no. 7147).
narrator: It was narrated that Ibn sirin said:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The (members of the) first group which would get into Paradise will have their faces as bright as stars in the sky. They would neither pass water, nor void excrement, nor will they suffer from catarrh, nor will they spit, and their combs would be made of gold, and their sweat will be musk, the fuel of their brazier will be aloes, and their wives will be large-eyed maidens and their form would be alike as one single person after the form of their father (Adam) sixty cubits tall.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) as saying that the inmates of Paradise would eat and drink but would neither spit, nor pass water, nor void excrement, nor suffer catarrah. It was said: Then, what would happen with food? Thereupon he said: They would belch and sweat (and it would be over with their food), and their sweat would be that of musk and they would glorify and praise Allah as easily as you breathe.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: It was narrated from Al-Amash with this chain of narrators (a Hadith similar to no. 7152), as far as the words "...like musk."
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said that the inmates of Paradise would eat therein and they would also drink, but they would neither void excrement, nor suffer catarrh, nor pass water, and their eating (would be digested) in the form of belching and their sweat would be musk aged they would glorify and praise Allah as easily as you breathe.
narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: "....And they will glorify and praise Allah as easily as breathing."
narrator: A similar report (as Hadith no. 7154) was from Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the Prophet п·әexcept that he said:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: He who would get into Paradise (would be made to enjoy such an everlasting) bliss that he would neither become destitute, nor would his clothes wear out, nor his youth would decline.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: There would be an announcer (in Paradise) who would make this announcement: Verily I there is in store for you (everlasting) health and that you should never fall ill and that you live (for ever) and do not die at all. And that you would remain young and never grow old. And that you would always live in affluent circumstances and never become destitute, as words of Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, are: And it would be announced to them: This is the Paradise. You have been made to inherit it for what you used to do . (VII; 43)
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri and Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ both reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said that in Paradise there would be for a believer a tent of a single hollowed pearl the breadth of which would be sixty miles. It would be meant for a believer and the believers would go around it and none would be able to see the others.
narrator: Abu Bakr bin Abdullah bin Qais reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said that in Paradise there would be a tent made of a single hollowed pearl, the breadth of which would be sixty miles from all sides and there would live a family in each corner and the other would not be able to see the believer who goes around them.
narrator: Abu Bakr bin Abdullah bin Qais reported on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: The Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: there would be a tent made of a pearl whose height towards the sky would be sixty miles. In each corner, there would be a family of the believer, out of sight for the others.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Abu Bakr bin Abu Musa bin Qais who, on the authority of his father:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Saihan, Jaihan, Euphrates and Nile are all among the rivers of Paradise.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There would enter Paradise people whose hearts would be like those of the hearts of birds.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: Hell would be brought on that day (the Day of Judgment) with seventy thousand bridles, and seventy thousand angels dragging each bridle.
narrator: Abdullah bin Mas`ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The fire which sons of Adam burn is only one-seventieth part of the Fire of Hell. His Companions said: By Allah, even ordinary fire would have been enough (to burn people). Thereupon he said: It is sixty-nine parts in excess of (the heat of) fire in this world each of them being equivalent to their heat.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters with a slight variation of wording.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: We were in the company of Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that we heard a terrible sound. Thereupon Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Do you know what (sound) is this? We said: Allah and His Messenger know best. Thereupon he said: That is a stone which was thrown seventy years before in Hell and it has 'been constantly slipping down and now it has reached its base.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: It reached at its base and you heard its sound.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ with the same chain of transmitters but with this change of wording that the Prophet (п·ә) said:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle (п·ә) as saying: There will be some to whose ankels the fire will reach, some to whose knees, some to whose waist the fire will reach, and some to whose collar-bone the fire will reach.
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: There would be among them those to whom the fire will reach up to their ankels and to some of them the fire would reach their knees and to some it would reach their waists and to some it would reach up to their collar-bones.
narrator: Samura bin Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Sa'id narrated it with this chain of narrators (a Hadith similar to no. 7170), but instead of "waist" he said "groin".
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: There was a dispute between the Hell and the Paradise and it (the Hell) said: The haughty and the proud would find abode in me. And the Paradise said: The meek and the humble would find their abode in me. Thereupon Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, (addressing the Hell) said: You are (the means) of My punishment by which I punish those of My servants whom I wish. (And addressing the Paradise) He said: You are only My Mercy by means of which 1 shall show mercy to those whom I wish, but each one of you would be full.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) as saying: The Paradise and the Hell disputed with each other. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The Paradise and the Hell disputed with each other. The rest of the hadith is the same as transmitted by Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ up to the words'. It is essential for Me to fill up both of you.
narrator: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said that the Hell would continue to say: Is there anything more, until Allah, the Exalted and High, would place His foot therein and that would say: Enough, enough, by Your Honour, and some parts of it would draw close to the other.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through another chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: There would be left some space in Paradise as Allah would like that to be left. Then Allah would create another creation as He would like.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When the inmates of Paradise would be admitted to Paradise and the inmates of Hell would be admitted to Hell, it would be said (to the inmates of Paradise): O inmates of Paradise. The rest of the hadith is the same but with this variation (that he only) said. That is the word of Allah, the Exalted. And he did not say: Then Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) recited, and he did not make a mention of his having pointed with his hand towards the (material) world.
narrator: Abu Sa'id ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: Allah would admit the inmates of Paradise into Paradise and the inmates of Hell into Hell. Then the announcer would stand between them and say: O inmates of Paradise, there is no death for you, O inmates of Hell, there is no death for you. You would live for ever therein.
narrator: Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When the inmates of Paradise would go to Paradise and the inmates of Hell would go to Hell, death would be called and it would be placed between the Paradise and the Hell and then slaughtered and then the announcer would announce: O inmates of Paradise, no death. O inmates of Hell-Fire, no death. And it would increase the delight of the inmates of Paradise and it would increase the grief of the inmates of Hell-Fire.
narrator: `Umar bin Muhammad bin Zaid bin `Abdullah bin `Umar bin al-Khattab reported on the authority of his father `Abdullah bin `Umar  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: The molar tooth of an unbeliever or the canine teeth of an unbeliever will be like Uhud and the thickness of his skin a three night's journey.
narrator: It is transmitted on the authority of Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: The distance of the two shoulders of the non-believer in Hell will be a three-day journey for a swift rider.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported directly from Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) that he said:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: May I not inform you about the inmates of Paradise? They said: Do this, of course. Thereupon Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: Every humble person who is considered to be humble if he were to adjure In the name of Allah, He would fulfil it. He then said: May I not inform you about the denizens of Hell-Fire? They said: Yes. And he said: Every haughty, fat and proud (person).
narrator: Haritha bin Wahb ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Shu'ba with the same chain of transmitters but with a slight variation of wording.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: May I not inform you about the inmates of Paradise? (And then informing about them) said: Every meek person who is considered to be humble and if they were to adjure in the name of Allah, Allah would certainly fulfil it. May I not inform you about the inmates of Hell-Fire? They are all proud, mean and haughty.
narrator: Haritha bin Wahb al-Khuzai ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Many a people with dishevelled hair are driven away from the door (but they are so pious) that if they are to swear in the name of Allah, He would definitely fulfil that.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: I saw 'Amr bin Luhayy bin Qam'a bin Khindif, brother of Bani Ka'b, dragging his Intestines in Fire.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Explained al-bahira as that animal which is not milked but for the idols. and none amongst the people milks them, and as-sa'iba as that animal which is let loose for the deities. Nothing is loaded over it, and Ibn Musayyib narrated that Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ stated that Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: I saw 'Amr bin 'Amir al-Khuzai dragging his intestines in fire and he was the first who devoted animals to deity.
narrator: Sa'id bin Musayyib said:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Two are the types amongst the denizens of Hell, the one possessing whips like the tail of an ox and they flog people with their help. (The second one) the women who would be naked in spite of their being dressed, who are seduced (to wrong paths) and seduce others with their hair high like humps. These women would not get into Paradise and they would not perceive the odour of Paradise, although its fragrance can be perceived from such and such distance (from great distance).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If you survive for a time you would certainly see people who would have whips in their hands like the tail of an ox. They would get up in the morning under the wrath of Allah and they would get into the evening with the anger of Allah.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard the Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ) as saying: If you live for a time, you would certainly see people get up (in the morning) in the wrath of Allah and getting into the evening under the curse of Allah, and there would be in their hands (whips) like the tail of an ox.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) said: By Allah, this world (is so insignificant in comparison) to the Hereafter that if one of you should dip his finger- (and while saying this Yahya pointed with his forefinger) -in the ocean and then he should see as to what has stuck to it. This hadith has been narrated through another chain of transmitters also but with a slight variation of wording.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated through five different chains of transmitters and all of them are narrated on the authority of Mustaurid, brother of Bani Fihr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , that:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: She heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The people would be assembled on the Day of Resurrection barefooted, naked and uncircumcised. I said: Allah's Messenger, will the male and the female be together on the Day and would they be looking at one another? Upon this Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: 'A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , the matter would be too serious for them to look to one another.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Hatim bin Abi Saghira with the same chain of transmitters and there is no mention of the word uncircumcised.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: He heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) deliver an address and he was saying that they would meet Allah barefooted, naked and uncircumcised.
narrator: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying, The people will be assembled in three categories. Those desirous (of Paradise), fearing (Hell), coming two upon the came], three upon the camel, four upon the camel, ten upon the camel and the rest will be assembled, Hell-Fire being with them when they are at midday where they would spend the night and where they would spend the morning and where they would spend the evening.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When the people stand before Allah, the Lord of the worlds, each one of them would stand submerged into perspiration up to half of his ears, and there is no mention of the day in the hadith transmitted on the authority of Ibn Muthanni.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: One of them would be completely submerged in perspiration up to half of his ears.
narrator: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ but with a slight variation of wording (and the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The perspiration would spread on the Day of Resurrection upon the earth to the extent of seventy cubits and it would reach up to their mouths or up to their ears. Thawr is not sure (which words) he used (mouth or ears).
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard Allah's Messenger (п·ә) as saying: On the Day of Resurrection, the sun would draw so close to the people that there woum be left only a distance of one mile. Sulaim b. Amir said: By Allah, I do not know whether he meant by mile the mile of the (material) earth or dn instrument used for applying collyrium to the eye. (The Prophet is, however, reported to have said): The people would be submerged in perspiration according to their deeds, some up to their. knees, Some up to the waist and some would have the bridle of perspiration and, while saying this, Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) pointed his hand towards his mouth.
narrator: Miqdad bin Aswad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: "all the wealth that I have bestowed upon a slave (of Allah) is permissible".
narrator: It was narrated from Qatada with this chain narrators (a Hadith similar to no. 7207) but he did not mention in his hadith:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been transmitted on the authority of 'Iyad bin Himad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ that Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) gave an address one day. The rest of the hadith is the same.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When any one of you dies, he is shown his seat (in the Hereafter) morning and evening; if he is amongst the inmates of Paradise (he is shown the seat) from amongst the inmates of Paradise and if he is one from amongst the denizens of Hell (he is shown the seat) from amongst the denizens of Hell, and it would be said to him: That is your seat until Allah raises you on the Day of Resurrection (and sends you to your proper seat).
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When a person dies, he is shown his seat morning and evening. If he is one amongst the inmates of Paradise (he is shown his seat) in Paradise and if he is one amongst the denizens of Hell-Fire (he is shown his seat) in the Hell-Fire. Then it is said to him: That is your seat where you would be sent on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: If you were not (to abandon) the burying of the dead (in the grave), I would have certainly supplicated Allah that He should make you listen the torment of the grave.
narrator: Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) went out after the sun had set and he heard some sound and said: It is the Jews who are being tormented in their graves.
narrator: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Abu Ayyub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through some other chains of transmitters (and the words are):
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When the dead body. is placed in the grave, he listens to the sound of the shoes (as his friends and relatives return after burying him).
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: When the servant is placed in his grave and his friends retrace their steps. The rest of the hadith is the same as transmitted by Qatada.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: This verse: Allah grants steadfastness to those who believe with firm word, was revealed in connection with the torment of the grave. It would be said to him: Who is your Lord? And he would say: Allah is my Lord and Muhammad is my Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ), and that is (what is implied) by the words of Allah, the Exalted: Allah keeps steadfast those who believe with firm word in this world and in the Hereafter.
narrator: Al-Bara' bin `Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This verse: Allah keeps those who believe steadfast with firm word in this world and the Hereafter was revealed in connection with the torment of the grave.
narrator: Al-Bara' bin 'Azib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: When it was the Day of Badr and Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ ) had gained victory over them (the Meccans), he commanded more than twenty persons, and in another hadith these are counted as twenty-four persons, from the non-believers of the Quraish to be thrown into the well of Badr. The rest of the hadith is the same.
narrator: Aba Talha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) said: He who is taken to account on the Day of Resurrection is in fact put to torment. I said: Has Allah, the Exalted and Glorious, not said this: 'He will be made subject to an easy reckoning (Ixxxiv. 8)? Thereupon he said: (What it implies) is not the actual reckoning, but only the presentation of one's deeds to Him. He who is thoroughly examined in reckoning is put to torment.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of Ayyub with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: Everyone who is reckoned thoroughly is undone. I said: Allah's Messenger, has Allah not called (reckoning) as easy reckoning? Thereupon he said.. It implies only presentation of (one's deeds to Him), but if one is thoroughly examined in reckoning, he in fact is undone.
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: "Whoever examined thoroughly reckoning will be doomed." Then he mentioned a hadith like that of Abu Yunus (no. 7227).
narrator: A'isha ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying three days before his death: None of you should court death but only hoping good from Allah
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: This hadith has been narrated on the authority of A'mash with the same chain of transmitters.
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) say three days before his death: None of you should die but hoping only good from Allah, the Exalted and Glorious.
narrator: Jabir bin 'Abdullah al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard Allah's Apostle ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying. Every servant would be raised (in the same very state) in which he dies.
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: A similar report (as hadith no. 7232) was narrated from Al-A'mash with this chain of narrators, and he said: The prophet п·ә said, but he did not say: "I heard."
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: I heard Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: When Allah intends to chastise a people, He chastises all of them then they would be raised according to their deeds.
narrator: Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Paradise, its Description, its Bounties and its Inhabitants

text: Allah's Messenger ( вҖҢШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҢ) as saying: The world is a prison-house for a believer and Paradise for a non-believer.
narrator: Abu Huraira ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Zuhd and Softening of Hearts

text: "Even if it were alive, the smallness of its ears is a defect."
narrator: Jabir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: The Book of Zuhd and Softening of Hearts
