They said: Are those the Prophets? He said: Yes. By the One in Whose Hand is my soul, and groups who had faith in Allah and His Messenger, and believed in the Messengers.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Attiyah narrated from Abu Sa'eed in Marfu' form: When it is the Day of Resurrection, Death shall be brought as a mixed black-white ram. It shall be stood between Paradise and the Fire, and then slaughtered while they watch. If anyone were to die of joy, then surely the people of Paradise and the Fire, and then slaughtered while they watch. If anyone were to die of joy, then surely the people of Paradise would die, and if anyone were to die of grief, then surely the people of the Fire would die.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Anas narrated that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w) said: Paradise is surrounded by hardships, and the Fire is surrounded by desires.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w) said: Paradise and the Fire debated. Paradise said: 'The weak and the poor shall enter me,' and the Fire said: 'The tyrants and the proud shall enter me.' So He said to the Fire: 'You are My Punishment, I take vengeance through you from whom I will,' and He said to Paradise: 'You are My Mercy I show mercy through you to whom I will.'
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri narrated that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w) said: The believer, when he desires a child in Paradise, he shall be carried (in pregnancy), born, and complete his aging in an hour as he desires.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Ali narrated that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w) said: Indeed in Paradise there is a gathering for Al-Hur Al-'Ein wherein they rasie their voices. The creatures have not heard the likes of them. [He said:] They say: 'We are the eternal ones, we shall not die. We are the ones who live in joy and comfort, we have no needs. We are the pleased ones, we do not get angry. Tuba(good news) to the one who belongs to us and we to him.'
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: [ From Yahya bin Abi Kathir, concerning His (Allah's), the Mighty and Glorious, statement: Then they shall be in gardens living luxuriously He said: Listening. And the meaning of listening is similar to what has been mentioned in the Hadith that Al-Hur Al-'Ein raise their voices].
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: ['Abdullah] Ibn 'Umar narrated that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w) said: Three shall be on dunes of musk - it seems to me that he said:- on the Day of Resurrection. The first and the last of peoples shall envy them: A man who calls to the five [prayers] every day and night; a man who leads a group of people and they are pleased with him, and a slave who fulfills the right of Allah and the right of his masters.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Abdullah bin Mas'ud narrated a Marfu'narration: There are three whom Allah, the Mighty and Glorious, loves: A man who stood at night reciting the Book of Allah; a man who spent in charity with his right hand, concealing it - it seems to me he said from his left hand - and a man who was in a small expedition while his companions were decimated, yet he faced enemy.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah(s.a.w) said: The Euphrates shall soon uncover a treasure of gold, so whoever witnesses it, let him not take anything from it.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Abu Hurairah narrated that the Prophet(s.a.w) said similar to it, except that he said,: uncover a pot of gold.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Hakim bin Mu'awiyah narrated from his father, that the Prophet (s.a.w) said: Indeed in Paradise there is a sea of water, and a sea of honey,and a sea of milk, and a sea of wine, then the rivers shall split off afterwards.
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Anas bin Malik narrated that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w) said: Whoever asks Allah (s.w.t) Paradise three times, Paradise says: 'O Allah, admit him into Paradise', and whoever seeks refuge from the Fire three times, the Fire says: 'O Allah, save him from the Fire.'
chapterName: Chapter on the description of Paradise

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I have been ordered to fight the people until they say La Ilaha Illallah , and if they say that, then their blood and wealth will be protected from me, except what it makes obligatory upon them, and their reckoning is up to Allah.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I have been ordered to fight the people until they bear witness to La Ilaha Illallah, and that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger, and they face our Qiblah, eat our slaughtered (meat), and perform our Salat. And if they do that, then their blood and wealth will be unlawful to us, except with its due right. For them shall be whatever is for the Muslims, and they shall be obliged with that which the Muslims are obliged.

Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Islam is based upon five: the testimony of La Ilaha Illallah, and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, the establishment of the Salat, giving the Zakat, fasting (the month of) Ramadan, and performing Hajj to the House.

Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: A delegation of Abdul-Qais came to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and said: 'We are a tribe from Rabi'ah, and we cannot come to you except during the sacred months. So order us with something that we can take from you, and then we call those who are behind us to it.' So he said: 'I order you with four things: To testify to La Ilaha Illallah, and that I am the Messenger of Allah; to establish the Salat, to give the Zakat, and to give the Khumus from the spoils of war that you gain.'
Narrated 'Aishah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed among the believers with the most complete faith is the one who is the best in conduct, and the most kind to his family.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Faith has seventy-some odd doors, the lowest of which is removing something harmful from the road, and its highest is the statement 'La Ilaha Illallah.'
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) passed by a man and he was chastising his brother about modesty, so the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Al-Haya' is part of faith.

Narrated Abu Sa'eed: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: If you see a man who comes to the Masjid then bear witness to his faith. Because Allah, the Exalted, says: Only those who believe in Allah, and the Last Day, and establish the Salat, and give the Zakat (should) maintain the Masajid until the end of the Ayah (9:18)
Narrated Jabir: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Between disbelief and faith is abandoning the Salat.
Narrated Al-A'mash: Similar to the previous chain and said: Between a slave and Shirk or disbelief is abandoning the Salat.
Narrated Jabir: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Between a slave of Allah and disbelief is abandoning the Salat.
Narrated Abdullah bin Buraidah narrated from his father: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The covenant between us and them is the Salat, so whoever abandons it he has committed disbelief.
Narrated Abdullah bin Shaqiq Al-'Uqayli: The Companions of Muhammad (п·ә) didn't consider leaving anything to be disblief except for the Salat.

Narrated Al-Abbas bin Abdul-Muttalib: that he heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) say: Whoever is pleased with Allah as (his) Lord, and Islam as (his) religion, and Muhammad as (his) Prophet, then he has tasted the sweetness of faith.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: There are three things for which whomever has them, then he has tasted the sweetness of faith: The one for whom Allah and His Messenger are more beloved than anything else; whoever loves someone and he does not love him except for the sake of Allah, and whoever hates to return to disbelief after Allah has saved him from it, just as he hates to be thrown into fire.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The adulterer is not a believer while he is committing adultery, and the thief is not a believer while he is stealing, but there is a chance for repentance; (if he repents, Allah will accept the repentance).

Narrated Ali bin Abu Talib: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever is penalized (for a crime) then his punishment has been hastened for him in the world, for Allah is more just than to double the punishment upon His slave in the Hereafter. And whoever does a punishable act and then Allah covers it for him and forgives him, then Allah is more kind than to recount something which He has already forgiven.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The Muslim is the one from (the harm of) whose tongue and hand (other) Muslims are safe, and the believer is the one with whom the people trust their blood and their wealth.
Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari: that the Prophet (п·ә) was asked: Which of the Muslims is most virtuous? He said: The one from (the harm of) whose tongue and hand (other) Muslims are safe.
Narrated 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Islam began as something strange and it will return to being strange as it began. So Tuba is for the strangers.

Narrated Kathir bin 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin 'Awf bin Zaid bin Milhah narrated from his father, from his grandfather: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed the religion with creep into the Hijaz just like a snake creeps into its hole, and the religion will cling to the Hijaz just like the female mountain goat cling to the peak of a mountain. Indeed the religion began as something strange and it will return to being strange. So Tuba is for the strangers who correct what the people have corrupted from my Sunnah after me.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The sign of a hypocrite is that whenever he speaks he lies, and whenever he makes a promise he does not fulfill it, and if he is entrusted he betrays.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: There are four things that whoever has them, then he is a hypocrite, and whoever has one attribute from among them, then he has an attribute of hypocrisy,until he leaves it: Whoever lies whenever he speaks, he does not fulfill whenever he promises, he is vulgar whenever he argues, and whenever he makes an agreement he proves treacherous.
Narrated Zaid bin Arqam: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whenever a man makes a promise and he intends to fulfill it, but he does not fulfill it, then there is no burden upon him.
Narrated Ibn Mas'ud: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: A Muslim's fighting his brother is disbelief, and verbally abusing him is disobedience.
Narrated Ibn Mas'ud: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Verbally abusing a Muslim is disobedience and fighting him is disbelief.

Narrated Ad-Dhahak: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: It is not for a slave (of Allah) to vow about something he does not possess, and cursing a believer is like killing him, and whoever accuses a believer of disbelief, then it is like he has killed him, and whoever kills himself with something, then Allah will punish him with whatever he killed himself with on the Day of Judgement.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: Ibn 'Umar narrated that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever says to his brother 'disbeliever' then it will have settled upon one of them. Sahih

Narrated As-Sunabihi: from Ubadah bin As-Samit, he said: I entered upon him while he was dying, so I cried, and he said: There now, why are you crying? For by Allah, if I am a martyr , then I will intercede for you, and if I can I will benefit you,' then he said: 'By Allah! There is no Hadith which I heard from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) which is good for you but I narrate it to you today, while I am near death. I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) say: Whoever testifies to La Ilaha Illallah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, then Allah has forbidden the fire for him.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The Jews split into seventy-one sects, or seventy-two sects, and the Christians similarly, and my Ummah will split into seventy-three sects.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: What befell the children of Isra'il will befall my Ummah, step by step, such that if there was one who had intercourse with his mother in the open, then there would be someone from my Ummah who would do that. Indeed the children of Isra'il split into seventy-two sects, and my Ummah will split into seventy-three sects. All of them are in the Fire Except one sect. He said: And which is it O Messenger of Allah? He said: What I am upon and my Companions.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created His creation in darkness, then He cast His Light upon them, so whoever is touched by that light he is guided, and whoever is not, he goes astray. It is for this reason that I say that the pens have dried with Allah's knowledge.

Narrated Mu'adh bin Jabal: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Do you know what Allah's right upon His slaves is? I said: Allah and His Messenger know best. He said: His right upon them is that they worship Him alone and do not associate any partners with Him. He said: And do you know what their right over Allah is if they do that? I said: Allah and His Messenger know best. He said: That He will not punish them.

Narrated Abu Dharr: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Jibril came to me and gave me glad tidings, that whoever dies without associating anything with Allah, then he will enter Paradise. I said: Even if he commits adultery and theft? He said: Yes.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: For whomever Allah wants good, he gives him understanding in the religion.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever takes a path upon which to obtain knowledge, Allah makes the path to Paradise easy for him.
Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever goes out seeking knowledge, then he is in Allah's cause until he returns.
Narrated 'Abdullah bin Sakhbarah: narrated that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever seeks knowledge, he is atoning for what has passed (of sins while doing so).
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever is asked about some knowledge that he knows, then he conceals it, he will be bridled with bridle of fire.

Narrated Abu Harun [Al-'Abdi]: We went to Abu Sa'eed and he said: 'Welcome with the exhortation of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). Indeed the Prophet (п·ә) said: Surely, the people are followers of you, and men will certainly come to you from the regions of the earth to gain understanding in the religion. So when they come to you exhort them with good.

Narrated Abu Harun [Al-'Abdi]: from Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Men will come to you from the direction of the east to learn. So when they come to you then exhort them to good. He said: When Abu Sa'eed saw us he would say: 'Welcome with the exhortation of the Messenger of Allah, may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family.'

Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr bin Al-'As: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah does not take away knowledge by removing it from the people, but He takes away knowledge by taking the scholars, until there remains no scholar and the people begin to ask the ignorant leaders, so they give their verdict without knowledge. They will go astray and lead the people astray.

Narrated Ibn Ka'b bin Malik: from his father that he heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: Whoever seeks knowledge to contend with the scholars, or to use it to argue with the fools, and to have people's faces turn towards him, then he shall be admitted to the Fire.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever learns knowledge for other than (the sake of) Allah, or intends by it other than Allah, then let him take his seat in the Fire.

Narrated 'Abdur-Rahman bin Aban bin 'Uthman: from his father who said: Zaid bin Thabit left to go to Marwan during the middle of the day. We said: 'He did not send for him [during] this hour except to ask him about something.' So we got up to ask him, and he said: 'Yes, he asked us about something we heard from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: May Allah gladden a man who hears a Hadith from us, so he memorizes it until he conveys it to someone else. Perhaps he carries Fiqh to one who is more understanding than him, and perhaps the one who carries the Fiqh is not a Faqih.

Narrated Simak bin Hard: I heard 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud narrating from his father who said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: 'May Allah gladden a man who hears something from us, so he conveys it as he heard it. Perhaps the one it is conveyed to is more understanding than the one who heard it.'

Narrated 'Abdul-Malik bin 'Umair: from 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud that he narrated from his father, from the Prophet (п·ә) who said: May Allah beautify a man who hears a saying of mine, so he understands it, remembers it, and conveys it. Perhaps he carries the Fiqh to one who has more understanding than him. There are three with which the heart of a Muslim shall not be deceived. Sincerity in deeds for Allah, giving Nasihah to the A'immah of Muslims, and sticking to the Jama'ah. For indeed the call is protected from behind them.
Narrated 'Abdullah [bin Mas'ud]: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever lies upon me, then let him take his seat in the Fire.
Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Do not lie upon me, for indeed whoever lies upon me, he will be admitted into the Fire.
Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever lies upon me - I think he said - purposely, then let him take his seat in the Fire.
Narrated Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever narrates a Hadith from me which he knows is a lie, then he is one of the liars.

Narrated 'Ubaidullah bin Abu Rafi: from Abu Rafi' and others, from the Prophet (п·ә) who said: Let me not find one of you reclining on his couch when a command I ordered, or a prohibition from me comes to him, and he says: 'I do not know. What we find in the Book of Allah, we follow it.'

Narrated Al-Miqdam bin Ma'dikarib: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Lo! Soon a Hadith from me will be conveyed to a man, while he is reclining on his couch, and he says: 'Between us and you is Allah's Book. So whatever we find in it that is lawful, we consider lawful, and whatever we find in it that is unlawful, we consider it unlawful.' Indeed whatever the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) made unlawful, it is the same as what Allah made unlawful.
Narrated Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri: We sought permission from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) for writing but he did not permit us.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: There was a man among the Ansar who would sit with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), and he would listen to the Ahadith of the Prophet (п·ә) and he was amazed with them but he could not remember them. So he complained about that to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). He said: 'O Messenger of Allah! I listen to your Ahadith and I am amazed but I can not remember them.' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Help yourself with your right hand' and he motioned with his hand as if writing.'

Narrated Abu Hurairah: That the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) gave an address. So he mentioned a story in the Hadith, and Abu Shah said: 'Have it written for me O Messenger of Allah! So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Write it for Abu Shah.'

Narrated Hammam bin Munabbih: that the heard Abu Hurairah say: None of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) narrated more Ahadith from him than me, except 'Abdullah bin 'Amr. For he used to write them down and I did not write

Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Convey from me, even if it be an Ayah, and narrate from the Children of Isra'il, and there is no harm, And whoever lies upon me purposely, then let him take his seat in the Fire.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: A man came to the Prophet (п·ә) to get a mount, but he (п·ә) did not have anything to mount him on with him. So he was lead to another person to give him a mount. He came to the Prophet (п·ә) to inform him about that and he said: 'Whoever leads to good, he is like the one who does it.'

Narrated Abu Mas'ud Al-Badri: that a man came to the Prophet (п·ә) looking for a mount, he said: 'Mine has been ruined.' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Go to so-and-so.' So he went to him and he gave him a mount. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Whoever leads to good, then for him is the same reward as the one who does it - or - who acts upon it.'
Narrated Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Intercede, and you will be rewarded, and Allah will fulfill what He wills upon the tongue of His Prophet.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: No soul is wrongfully taken except that some of the burden of its blood is upon the son of Adam, because he was the first to institute murder.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever calls to guidance, then he receives the reward similar to the reward of whoever follows him, without that diminishing anything from their rewards. And whoever calls to misguidance, then he receives of sin similar to the sins of those who followed him, without that diminishing anything from their sins.

Narrated Ibn Jarir bin 'Abdullah: from his father that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever starts a good tradition which is followed, then for him is a reward, and the likes of their rewards of whoever follows him, there being nothing diminished from their rewards. And whoever starts a bad tradition which is followed, then for him is the sin, and the likes of the sins of whoever follows him, there being nothing diminished from their sins.

Narrated Al-'Irbad bin Sariyah: One day after the morning Salat, the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) exhorted us to the extent that the eyes wept and the hearts shuddered with fear. A man said: 'Indeed this is a farewell exhortation. [So what] do you order us O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'I order you to have Taqwa of Allah, and to listen and obey, even in the case of an Ethiopian slave. Indeed, whomever among you lives, he will see much difference. Beware of the newly invented matters, for indeed they are astray. Whoever among you sees that, then he must stick to my Sunnah and the Sunnah of the rightly guided Khulafa', cling to it with the molars.'

Narrated Anas bin Malik: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to me: 'O my son! If you are capable of (waking up in) the morning and (ending) the evening, while there is nothing of deception in your heart for anything, then do so.' Then he said to me: 'O my son! That is from my Sunnah. Whoever revives my Sunnah then he has loved me. And whoever loved me, he shall be with me in Paradise.'

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Leave me with what I left you. When I narrated a Hadith to you, then take it from me. The people before you were only destroyed by their excessive questioning and disagreeing with their Prophets.

Narrated Abu Salih reported a narration from Abu Hurairah: It shall soon be that people are beating the livers of camels (meaning that they are hastening and traveling upon them) seeking knowledge. But they will not find anyone more knowledgeable than a scholar of Al-Madinah.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The Faqih is harder on Ash-Shaitan than a thousand worshipers.

Narrated Ibn Ashwa: from Yazid bin Salamah Al-Ju'fi, he said: Yazid bin Salamah 'O Messenger of Allah! I heard so many narrations from you that I am afraid the last of them will cause me to forget the first of them. So narrate a statement to me that will encompass them.' So he said: 'Have Taqwa of Allah with what you learn.'
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Two things will not be together in a hypocrite: Good manners, and Fiqh in the religion.

Narrated Abu Umamah Al-Bahili: Two men were mentioned before the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). One of them a worshiper, and the other a scholar. So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'The superiority of the scholar over the worshiper is like my superiority over the least of you.' Then the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Indeed Allah, His Angels, the inhabitants of the heavens and the earths - even the ant in his hole, even the fish - say Salat upon the one who teaches the people to do good.'
Narrated Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The believer will never be satisfied with the good he hears, until he ends up in Paradise.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The wise statement is the lost property of the believer, so wherever he finds it, then he is more worthy of it.

Narrated 'Ali: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: There are six courtesies due from a Muslim to another Muslim: To give Salam to him when he meets him, to accept his invitation when he invites him, to reply to him when he sneezes, to visit him when he is ill, to follow his funeral when he dies, and to love for him what he loves for himself.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: There are six things due from the believer to another believer: Visiting him when he is ill, attending (his funeral) to him when he dies, accepting his invitation when he invites, giving him Salam when he meets him, replying to him when he sneezes, and wishing him well when he is absent and when he is present.

Narrated Hadrami, the freed slave of the family of Al-Jarud: from Nafi: A man sneezed beside Ibn 'Umar and said: 'Al-Hamdulillah Was-Salamu 'Ala Rasulillah. (All praise is due to Allah, and peace upon the Messenger of Allah)'. So Ibn 'Umar said: 'I too say Al-Hamdulillah Was-Salamu 'Ala Rasulillah, but this is not what the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) taught us. He taught us to say: Al-Hamdulillah 'Ala Kulli Hal (All praise is due to Allah in every circumstance)

Narrated Abu Musa: The Jews used to sneeze in the presence of the Prophet (п·ә) hoping that he would say: 'Yarhamukumullah (May Allah have mercy upon you).' So he said: 'Yahdikumullahu Wa Yuslihu Balakum (May Allah guide you and rectify your affairs).'

Narrated Abu Ayyub: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: When one of you sneezes let him say: 'Al-Hamdulillahi 'Ala Kulli Hal (All praise is due to Allah in every circumstance).' And let the one replying to him say: Yarhamukallah (May Allah have mercy upon you).' And let him say to him: 'Yahdikumullah Wa Yuslihu Balakum (May Allah guide you and rectify your affairs).'

Narrated Anas bin Malik: that two men sneezed in the presence of the Prophet (п·ә); he responded to one of them and did not respond to the other. The one who was not responded to said: O Messenger of Allah! You responded to this person and did not respond to me?' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: He praised Allah while you did not praise Him.

Narrated Iyas bin Salamah: from his father: A man sneezed in the presence of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) while I was present, so the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Yarhamukallah (May Allah have mercy upon you).' Then he sneezed a second and third time. So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: This man is suffering from a cold.'

Narrated 'Umar bin Ishaq bin Abi Talhah: from his mother, from her father, who said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Reply three times to the sneezing person. If he (sneezes) more, then if you wish reply and if you do not then do not.'
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that when the Prophet (п·ә) would sneeze, he would cover his face with his hand or with his garment, and muffle the sound with it.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The sneeze is from Allah and the yawn is from Ash-Shaitan. So when one of you yawns let him cover his mouth with his hand. For when he says 'Ah, Ah' Ash-Shaitan laughs from inside his opening. [And indeed Allah loves the sneeze and He dislikes the Yawn, so when a man says Ah, Ah when yawning, indeed Ash-Shaitan laughs from inside his opening. ]

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah loves sneezing and He dislikes the yawn. So when one of you sneezes and says 'Al-Hamdulillah (All praise is due to Allah),' then it is a right due from every one who hears him to say: 'Yarhamukallah (May Allah have mercy upon you)' As for yawning then when one of you yawns let him suppress it as much as possible and not say: Hah Hah' for that is only from Ash-Shaitan laughing at him.

Narrated Abu Yaqzan: from 'Adi - and he is Ibn Thabit - from his father, from his grandfather - [from the Prophet (п·ә)]: Sneezing, dozing and yawning during Salat, and menstruation, regurgitation and nosebleeds are from Ash-Shaitan.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Let one of you not have his brother stand from his seat then sit in it.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Let one of you not have his brother stand from his seat then sit in it.
Narrated Wahb bin Hudaifah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: A man has more right to his seat. If he leaves for some need of his, then he returns, then he has more right to his seat.
Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: It is not lawful for a man to separate two people except with their permission.

Narrated Abu Miljaz: that a man sat in the middle of a circle so Hudhaifah said: Cursed upon the tongue of Muhammad - or - Cursed, by Allah upon the tongue of Muhammad (п·ә), is he who sits in the middle of a the circle.
Narrated Anas: There was no person more beloved to them than the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). [He said:] And they would not stand when they saw him because they knew that he disliked that.

Narrated Abu Miljaz: that Mu'awiyah came out and 'Abdullah bin Az-Zubair and Ibn Safwan stood for him when they saw him, so he said: Sit, I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: 'Whoever wishes that he be received by men standing then, let him take his seat in the Fire.'

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Five are from the Fitrah: Cutting the pubic hair, circumcision, paring the mustache, plucking the under arm hair and trimming the fingernails.

Narrated 'Aishah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Ten are from the Fitrah: Paring the mustache, leaving the beard to grow, Siwak, cleaning the nose with water, paring the fingernails, washing the knuckles, plucking the underarm hair, shaving the pubic hairs, and Intiqas with water.
Narrated Anas: from the Prophet (п·ә), that he (п·ә) fixed the time as every forty nights for them to trimming the fingernails, taking from the mustache, and shaving the pubic hairs.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: [The Messenger of Allah (п·ә)] fixed the time for us paring the mustache, trimming the fingernails, shaving the pubic hairs and plucking the underarm hairs - that we not leave it for more than forty days.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet (п·ә) would pare, or take from his mustache, and Ibrahim Khalilur-Rahman would do it (as well).
Narrated Zaid bin Arqam: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever does not take from his mustache, then he is not from us.
Narrated 'Amr bin Shu'aib: from his father, from his grandfather that he Prophet (п·ә) would take from his beard; from its breadth and its length.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Trim the mustache and leave the beard to grow.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) ordered trimming the mustache and leaving the beard to grow.
Narrated 'Abbad bin Tamim: from his paternal uncle, that he saw the Prophet (п·ә) reclining in the Masjid, and placing one of his feet atop another.
Narrated Jabir: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) prohibited Ishtimal As-Samma, Al-Ihtiba in one garment, and that a man raise one of his feet atop the other while he is reclining on his back.
Narrated Jabir: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) prohibited Ishtimal As-Samma, Al-Ihtiba in one garment, and that a man raise one of his feet atop the other while he is reclining on his back.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saw a man laying on his stomach so he said: Indeed such laying is not loved by Allah.

Narrated Bahz bin Hakim: My father narrated to me from my grandfather, who said: 'I said: O Messenger of Allah! Regarding our 'Awrah, what of it must we cover and what of it may we leave? He said: Protect your 'Awrah except from your wife or what your right hand possesses. ' He said: What about a man with another man? He said: If you are able to not let anyone see it, then do so. I said: What about a man when he is alone? He said: Allah is most deserving of being shy from Him.
Narrated Jabir bin Samurah: I saw the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) reclining upon as pillow, on his left side.
Narrated Jabir bin Samurah: I saw the Prophet (п·ә) reclining upon a pillow.
Narrated Abu Mas'ud: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: A man is not lead in his Sultanah, and his seat of honor in his house is not sat in without his permission.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Buraidah: I heard my father Buraidah saying: 'I was walking with the Prophet (п·ә) when a man came to him with a donkey, so he said: O Messenger of Allah! Ride and the man moved toward the back. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: No, you have more right to the front of your beast, unless you allot it for me. He said: I have allotted it for you.' He said: 'So he rode.'

Narrated Jabir: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Do you have Anmat? I said: Where would we get Anmat from? He said: Soon you will have Anmat He said: I would say to my wife: 'Remove your Anmat from my sight.' But she would say: 'Did not the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) say: Indeed you shall soon have Anmat? He said: So I left it.

Narrated Iyas bin Salamah: from his father who said: I guided Allah's Prophet (п·ә) and Al-Hasan and Al-Husain, on his gray mule until I brought him to the apartment of the Prophet (п·ә), this one was in front of him, and this one behind him.
Narrated Jabir bin 'Abdullah: I asked the Messenger Of Allah (п·ә) about the unintentional glance, so he ordered me that I divert my sight.
Narrated Ibn Buraidah: from his father (from the Prophet (п·ә)) who said: O 'Ali! Do not follow a look with a look, the first is for you, but the next is not for you.

Narrated Nabhan the freed slave of Umm Salamah: to Ibn Shihab, that Umm Salamah narrated to him, that she and Maimunah were with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), she said: So when we were with him, Ibn Umm Maktum came, and he entered upon him, and that was after veiling had been ordered for us. So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Veil yourselves from him.' So I said: 'O Messenger of Allah! Is he not blind such that he can not see us or recognize us?' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Are you two blind such that you can not see him?'

Narrated Dhakwan: from the freed slave of 'Amr bin Al-'As that 'Amr bin Al-'As sent him to 'Ali seeking his permission to enter upon Asma bint Umais, so he permitted him. When he was finished from what he needed, the freed slave of 'Amr bin Al-'As asked about that, so he said: Indeed the Prophet (п·ә) prohibited us - or - prohibited that we enter upon women, without the permission of their husbands.
Narrated Usamah bin Zaid, and Sa'eed bin Zaid bin 'Amr bin Nufail: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: I have not left among the people after me, a Fitnah more harmful upon men than women.

Narrated Humaid bin 'Abdur-Rahman: that he heard Mu'awiyah giving a Khutbah in Al-Madinah, and saying: Where are your scholars, O people of Al-Madinah? [Indeed] I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) forbidding from these locks (of hair), and saying: 'The Children of Isra'il were only ruined when their women used them.'

Narrated 'Abdullah: that the Prophet (п·ә) cursed the women who practice tattooing and those who seek to be tattooed, the women who remove hair from their faces seeking beautification by changing the creation of Allah.

Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Allah's curse is upon the woman who lengthens hair and the women who seeks to have her hair lengthened, and the woman who tattoos and the woman who seeks to have her herself tattooed.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) cursed the women who imitate men and the men who imitate women.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) cursed those men who behave effeminately and those women whose behavior is masculine.
Narrated Abu Musa: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Every eye commits adultery, and when the woman uses perfume and she passes by a gathering, then she is like this and that.' Meaning an adulteress.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Fragrance for men is that which its scent is apparent and its color is hidden, and fragrance for women is that which its color is visible and its scent is hidden. (Meaning when leaving the home as indicated by the previous chapter. As far as in the presence of the husband, then the woman may wear fragrant perfume.)

Narrated 'Imran bin Husain: The Prophet (п·ә) said [to me]: 'Indeed the best fragrance for men is what's scent is apparent and its color is hidden, and the best fragrance for women is what's color is visible and its scent is hidden.' And he prohibited Mitharatil-Urjawan (Mitharah was some type of saddle cloth. Some of the people of knowledge say it was a certain kind of cloth made of silk, and it preceded earlier under no. 1760. They disagree over Al-Urjawan, and perhaps it means whatever is red, meaning the Red Mitahrah, see Tuhfat Al-Ahwadhi).
Narrated Thumamah bin 'Abdullah: Anas would not refuse perfume, and Anas said: 'Indeed the Prophet (п·ә) would not refuse perfume.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: There are three that are not refused: Cushions, oils [Duhn (fragrance)], and milk.
Narrated Abu 'Uthman An-Nahdi: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'When one of you is given some fragrance then do not refuse it, for indeed it comes from Paradise.
Narrated 'Abdullah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: A woman is not to touch a woman such that she can describe her to her husband as if he is looking at her.

Narrated 'Abdur-Rahman bin Abi Sa'eed [Al-Khudri] from his father who said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'A man is not to look at the 'Awrah of a man, and a woman is not to look at the 'Awrah of a woman. A man is not to be alone with a man under one garment, and a woman is not to be alone with a woman under one garment.'

Narrated Bahz bin Hakim: from his father, from his grandfather, who said: I said: 'O Prophet of Allah! Regarding our 'Awrah, what of it must we cover and what of it may we leave?' He said: 'Protect your 'Awrah except from your wife or what your right hand possesses.' He said: I said: 'O Messenger of Allah! What about when some people are with others?' He said: 'If you are able to not let anyone see it then do not let them see it.' He said: I said: 'O Prophet of Allah! What about when one of us is alone?' He said: 'Allah is more deserving of being shy from Him than the people.'

Narrated Zur'ah bin Muslim bin Jardah Al-Aslami: about his grandfather Jarhad, he said: The Prophet (п·ә) passed by Jarhad in the Masjid and his thigh was exposed, so he said: 'Indeed the thigh is 'Awrah.'
Narrated 'Abdullah bin Jardah Al-Aslami: from his father, from the Prophet (п·ә) who said: The thigh is 'Awrah.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: The thigh is 'Awrah.

Narrated Abu Az-Zinad: Ibn Jarhad informed me from his father, that the Prophet (п·ә) passed by him while his thigh was exposed, so the Prophet (п·ә) said: 'Cover your thigh, for indeed it is 'Awrah.'

Narrated Salih bin Abi Hassan: I heard Sa'eed bin Musayyab saying: 'Indeed Allah is Tayyib (good) and he loves Tayyib (what is good), and He is Nazif (clean) and He loves cleanliness, He is Karim (kind) and He loves kindness, He is Jawad (generous) and He loves generosity. So clean' - I think he said - 'your courtyards, and do not resemble the Jews.' He said: I mentioned that to Muhajir bin Mismar, and he said: Amir bin Sa'd [bin Abi Waqqas] narrated it to me from his father from the Prophet (п·ә), similarly, except that he did not say: Clean your courtyards.

Narrated Abu Muhayyah: from Laith, from Nafi, from Ibn 'Umar that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Beware of nakedness! For indeed there are with you, those who do not part from you except at the place of defecation, and when a man goes into his wife. So be shy of them and honor them.

Narrated Jabir: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, then he is not to let his wife enter the Hammam, and whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, then he is not to enter the Hammam without an Izar. And whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, then he is not to sit at a spread in which Khamr is circulated.

Narrated Abu 'Udhrah [and he lived during the time of the Prophet (п·ә)]: from 'Aishah, that the Prophet (п·ә) prohibitied the men and women from the Hammamat (plural of Hammam), then he permitted it for the men in Izar.

Narrated Abu Al-Malih Al-Hudhali: that some women from the inhabitants of Hims, or from the inhabitants of Ash-Sham entered upon 'Aishah, so she said: Are you those whose women enter the Hammamat? I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: 'No woman removes her garments in other than the house of her husband except that she has torn the screen between herself and her Lord.'
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: I heard Abu Talhah saying: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: The angels do not enter a house in which there is a dog or an object of images.

Narrated Ishaq bin 'Abdullah bin Abi Talhah: that Rafi' bin Ishaq informed him, saying: I and 'Abdullah bin Abi Talhah entered upon Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri to visit him. So Abu Sa'eed said: 'The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) informed us: The angels do not enter a house in which there is an image or a picture.
Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: A man passed by while wearing two red garments. He gave Salam to the Prophet (п·ә) but he did not return the Salam.
Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) prohibited the gold ring, Al-Qassi, Al-Mitharah, and Al-Ji'ah (beer).

Narrated Al-Bara bin 'Azib: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) ordered us with seven things and he forbade us from seven. He ordered us to follow the funeral, visit the ill, reply to the sneezing person, accept the invitation, assist the oppressed, to help the one who made an oath, and to return the Salam. And he forbade us from seven things: From the gold ring, or ringlets of gold, silver vessels, wearing silk, Ad-Dibaj, Al-Istabraq, And Al-Qassi.
Narrated Samurah bin Jundab: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Wear white, for indeed it is very pure and cleaner, and shroud your dead in it.'

Narrated Jabir Bin Samurah: I saw the Prophet (п·ә) on a clear night, so I looked at the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and at the moon, and he was wearing a red Hullah, and he looked better than the moon to me.
Narrated Abu Rimthah: I saw the Messenger of Allah wearing two green Burud.
Narrated 'Aishah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) went out during the morning wearing a Mirt made of black hair.
Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) prohibited saffron for men.
Narrated Ya'la bin Murrah: The Prophet (п·ә) saw a man wearing Khuluq and said: 'Go and wash it, then wash it, then do not use it again.'
Narrated The freed slave of Asma: from Ibn 'Umar who said: I heard 'Umar mentioning that the Prophet (п·ә) said: 'Whoever wears silk in the world he shall not wear it in the Hereafter.'

Narrated Al-Miswar bin Makhramah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) distributed some cloaks but he did not give anything to Makhramah. Makhramah said: 'O my son! Let us go to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә).' So I went with him. He said: 'Enter and call him for me.'So I called the Prophet (п·ә) for him, then the Prophet (п·ә) came out wearing one of the cloaks. He (п·ә) said: 'I kept this one for you.' He said: So he looked at him and said: 'Makhramah is pleased.'
Narrated 'Amr bin Shu'aib: from his father, from his grandfather who said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Indeed Allah loves to see the results of his favors upon His Slaves.'
Narrated Ibn Buraidah: from his father: An-Najashi gave the Prophet (п·ә) two black plain Khuff. So he wore them, then he performed Wudu and wiped over them.
Narrated 'Amr bin Shu'aib: from his father, from his grandfather: The Prophet (п·ә) prohibited plucking gray hair. And he said: It is the Muslim's light.'
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The one whose counsel is sought is entrusted.
Narrated Umm Salamah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'The one whose council is sought is entrusted.'
Narrated Salim and Hamzah, the sons of 'Abdullah bin 'Umar: from their father that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: An omen is in three: A woman, a dwelling, and a (riding) beast.
Narrated 'Abdullah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whenever there are three of you, then let two not converse in exclusion of their companion.

Narrated Isma'il bin Abi Khalid: that Abu Juhaifah said: I saw the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) (he was) white and turning grey. Al-Hasan bin 'Ali resembles him most. He had promised thirteen young she-camels for us, so we went to get them. When we arrived he had died without giving us anything. So when Abu Bakr (became the Khalifah) he said: 'If there is anyone to whom the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) made a promise, then let him come forth.' I stood to inform him about it, and he ordered that they be given to us.
Narrated Isma'il bin Abi Khalid: that Abu Juhaifah said: I saw the Prophet (п·ә), and Al-Hasan bin 'Ali resembles him the most.

Narrated 'Ali: I did not hear the Prophet (п·ә) mentioning both of his parents (that is including both in the saying May my father and mother be ransomed for you ) for anyone other than Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas.

Narrated 'Ali: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) did not mention both of his parents for anyone except Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas. On the Day of Uhud he said: 'Shoot, may my father and mother be ransomed for you.' And he said to him: 'Shoot! O young man.'
Narrated Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) mentioned both of his parents to me on the Day of Uhud.
Narrated Anas: that the Prophet (п·ә) said to him: O my little son.

Narrated 'Amr bin Shu'aib: from his father, from his grandfather that the Prophet (п·ә) ordered naming the child on the seventh day, removing the harm from him, and Al-'Aqq (removing the hair and slaughtering the animal for 'Aqiqah).
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: The most loved names to Allah are 'Abdullah and 'Abdur-Rahman.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: The most loved names to Allah are 'Abdullah and 'Abdur-Rahman.
Narrated 'Umar [bin Al-Khattab]: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I forbid naming with Rafi, Barakah and Yasar.
Narrated Samurah bin Jundab: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Do not name your boy Rabah, nor Aflah, nor Yasar, nor Najih, so that it may be said: 'Is he there?' and it may be said: 'No.'

Narrated Al-A'raj: that Abu Hurairah conveyed to him that the Prophet (п·ә) said: The most despicable (Akhna) name to Allah on the Day of Judgement is that of a man named King of Kings. (Malikil-Amlak).
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Prophet (п·ә) changed the name of 'Asiyah, he said: You are Jamilah.

Narrated 'Aishah: that the Prophet (п·ә) would change offensive names. Abu Bakr bin Nafi said: Sometimes 'Umar bin 'Ali would narrated this Hadith: Hisham bin 'Urwah from his father, from the Prophet (п·ә),' in Mursal form, and he would not mention 'from 'Aishah' in it.

Narrated Muhammad bin Jubair bin Mut'im: from his father that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I have some names: I am Muhammad, I am Ahmad, I am Al-Mahi, the one by whom Allah wipes out disbelief, I am Al-Hasir, the one whom the people are gathered at his feet, and I am Al-'Aqib, the one after whom there is no Prophet.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Prophet (п·ә) prohibited that one use his name and his Kunyah; naming themselves Muhammad Abul-Qasim.
Narrated Jabir: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә): When you name yourself after me, then do not use my Kunyah.
Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: that he said: O Messenger of Allah (п·ә)! If I have a son after you do you think I could name him your Kunyah? He said: Yes. So he said: So that was permitted for me.
Narrated 'Abdullah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed there is wisdom in (some) poetry.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed there is wisdom in (some) poetry.

Narrated 'Aishah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) had a Minbar placed in the Masjid for Hassan to stand to boast (poetically) about the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) - or she said: to defend the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). And the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Indeed Allah has aided Hassan with the holy spirit (Jibril) as he boasts about - or - defends the Messenger of Allah (п·ә).'

Narrated Anas: that the Prophet (п·ә) entered Makkah during 'Umratil-Qada and 'Abdullah bin Rawahah was walking in front of him reciting verses of poetry. O tribes of disbelievers get out of his way - today we will strike you about its revelation; a strike that removes the heads from the shoulders - and makes the friend not concerned about his friend. 'Umar said to him: O Ibn Rawahah! Before the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), and in the sanctuary of Allah you utter poetry? the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Leave him O 'Umar! For it is quicker upon them than the raining arrow.

Narrated Al-Miqdam bin Shuraih: from his father, that 'Aishah was asked: Did the Prophet (п·ә) used to say any poetry? She said: He would say parables with the poetry of Ibn Rawahah, saying: 'News shall come to you from where you did not expect it.'
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: The best statement spoken by the Arab is the saying of Labid: 'Everything aside from Allah perishes.'

Narrated Jabir bin Samurah: I sat with the Prophet (п·ә) more than one-hundred times. His Companions used to recite poetry and talk about things that occurred during Jahiliyyah, and he would remain silent, and sometimes he would smile along with them.
Narrated Muhammad bin Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas: from his father that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: It is better that one of you fill his insides with puss than to fill it with poetry.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: It is better that one of you fill his insides with puss than to fill it with poetry.
Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah detests the excessive among men, who slaps his tongue around like the cow slaps his tongue.
Narrated Jabir: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) prohibited that a man sleep on a terrace without something to obstruct him (so that he will not roll off and fall).
Narrated 'Abdullah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to take care of us by preaching during the days fearing that we may get bored.
Narrated Abu Salih: I asked 'Aishah and Umm Salamah about which deed did the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) like to do most. They said: 'Whatever he could do regularly, even if it was little.'

Narrated Jabir bin 'Abdullah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Cover the vessels, tie the water-skins, close the doors, extinguish the torches, for indeed the vermin may drag away the wick, causing a fire for the inhabitants of the house.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: When you travel through fertile land, then give the camels their fill of the land, and when you travel through drought, then hasten while they are fresh, and when you camp late, then stay away from the road, for indeed it is the route of the beasts and the abode of poisonous vermin in the night.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Do not turn your houses into graves. Indeed Ash-Shaitan does not enter the house in which Surat Al-Baqarah is recited.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә): For everything there is a hump (pinnacle) and the hump (pinnacle) of the Qur'an is Surat Al-Baqarah, in it there is an Ayah which is the master of the Ayat in the Qur'an; [it is] Ayat Al-Kursi.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever recites Ha Mim Al-Mu'min - up to - To Him is the return (40:1-3) and Ayat Al-Kursi when he reaches (gets up in) the morning, he will be protected by them until the evening. And whoever recites them when he reaches the evening, he will be protected by them until the morning.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Mas'ud Al-Ansari: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever recites the last two Ayat of Surat Al-Baqarah during the night, they shall suffice him.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated An-Nu'man bin Bashir: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah wrote in a book two thousand years before He created the heavens and the earth, and He sent down two Ayat from it to end Surat Al-Baqarah with. If they are recited for three nights in a home, no Shaitan shall come near it.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated An-Nawwas bin Sam'an: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: The Qur'an shall come, and its people who acted according to it in the world. Surat Al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran shall be in front of it. An-Nawwas said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) stated three parables about them which I have not since forgotten, he said: They will come as if they are two shades between which there is illumination, or as if they are two shady clouds, or as if they are shadows of lines of birds arguing on behalf of their people.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud: Allah has not created in the heavens nor in the earth what is more magnificent than Ayat Al-Kursi. Sufyan said: Because Ayat Al-Kursi is the Speech of Allah, and Allah's Speech is greater than Allah's creation of the heavens and the earth.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Ishaq: hearing Al-Bara say: There was a man reciting [Surat] Al-Kahf when he saw his riding animal stamping his feet, so he looked and there was something like a shadow or cloud, so he went to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and mentioned that to him. The Prophet (п·ә) said: That was the tranquility which descends with the Qur'an, or descends because of the Qur'an.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Ad-Darda: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever recites three Ayat from the beginning of Al-Kahf he is protected from the turmoil of the Dajjal.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Anas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed for everything there is a heart, and the Qur'an's heart is Ya Sin. Whoever recites Ya Sin, then for its recitation, Allah writes for him that he recited the Qur'an ten times.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever recites Ha Mim Ad-Dukhan during the night, in the morning seventy thousand angels seek forgiveness for him.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever recites Ha Mum Ad-Dukhan during Friday night, he shall be forgiven.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: One of the companions of the Prophet (п·ә) pitched a tent on a grave without knowing that it was a grave. Suddenly he heard a person from the grave reciting Surah al-Mulk till he completed it. So he went to the Prophet (п·ә) and said: 'Oh Messenger of Allah, I pitched my tent on a grave without realizing that is was a grave. Then suddenly I heard a person from the grave reciting Surah al-Mulk till he completed it.' The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'It is the defender, it is the deliverer - it delivers from the punishment of the grave.'
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Indeed there is a Surah in the Qur'an of thirty Ayat, which intercedes for a man until he is forgiven. It is [Surah] Tabarak Alladhi Biyadihil-Mulk.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever recites Idha Zulzilat, it equals half of the Qur'an for him. Whoever recites: Qul Ya Ayyuhal-Kafirun it equals a fourth of the Qur'an for him. And whoever recites: Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad it equals a third of the Qur'an for him.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Idha Zulzilat is equal to half of the Qur'an, Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad is equal to a third of the Qur'an, and Yul Ya Ayyuhal-Kafirun is equal to a fourth of the Qur'an.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Ayyub: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Would one of you like to recite a third of the Qur'an during a night? Whoever recited: Allaahu Al-Wahid As-Samad then he has recited a third of the Qur'an.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: I went out with the Messenger of Allah and heard a man reciting Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad [Allahus-Samad] so the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'It is obligatory.' I said: 'What is obligatory?' He said: 'Paradise.'
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever recited Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad two hundred times everyday, fifty years worth of his sins will be removed - unless he owed a debt.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad is equal to a third of the Qur'an.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Gather and I shall recite to you one third of the Qur'an. He said: So whoever was to gather did so, then the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) came out and recited Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad. Then he went back in. Some of them said to each other: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'I shall recite to you one third of the Qur'an I thought that this was news from the Heavens. Allah's Prophet (п·ә) came out and said: Indeed I said that I would recite to you one third of the Qur'an, and it is indeed equal to one third of the Qur'an.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Uqbah bin 'Amir Al-Juhani: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Some Ayat have been revealed to me the likes of which have not been seen: Qul A'udhu Birabbin-Nas until the end of the Surah and Qul A'udhu Birabbil-Falaq until the end of the Surah.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Uqbah bin 'Amir: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) ordered me to recite Al-Mu'awwidhatain at the end of every Salat.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Aishah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The one who recites the Qur'an and he is proficient with it, then he is with the noble and blessed Angels (As-Safaratil-Kiramil-Bararah), and the one who recites it - Hisham said: And it is hard for him - Shu'bah said: And it is difficult for him, - then he gets two rewards.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Ali bub Abi Talib: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever recites the Qur'an and memorizes it, making lawful what it makes lawful, and unlawful what it makes unlawful, Allah will admit him to Paradise due to it, and grant him intercession for ten of his family members who were to be consigned to the Fire.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu 'Abdur-Rahman: from 'Uthman bin 'Affan that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The best of you is he who learns the Qur'an and teaches it. Abu 'Abdur-Rahman said: So that is why I sit at this seat of mine. And he taught the Qur'an during the time of 'Uthman until Al-Hajjaj bin Yusuf came.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Uthman [bin 'Affan]: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The best of you - or the most virtuous of you - is he who learns the Qur'an and teaches it.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The best of you is he who learns the Qur'an and teaches it.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Muhammad bin Ka'b Al-Qurazi: I heard 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud saying: 'The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: [Whoever recites a letter] from Allah's Book, then he receives the reward from it, and the reward of ten the like of it. I do not say that Alif Lam Mim is a letter, but Alif is a letter, Lam is a letter and Mim is a letter.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Zaid bin Artah: narrated from Abu Umamah, that he Prophet (п·ә) said: Allah does not listen to anything more virtuous from the worshiper than the two Rak'ah of Salat he performs. And the righteousness spreads over the head of a worshiper as long as he remains in his Salat. And the worshipers shall not draw nearer to Allah, Mighty and Sublime is He, with similar to what came from Him.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Jubair bin Nufair: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: You shall not return to Allah with what is more virtuous than what came from Him. Meaning the Qur'an.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed, the one who does not have the Qur'an inside him (his heart), is like the ruined house.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: It shall be said - meaning to the one who memorized the Qur'an - 'Recite, and rise up, recite (melodiously) as you would recite in the world. For indeed your rank shall be at the last Ayah you recited.'
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: The one who memorized the Qur'an shall come on the Day of Judgement and (the reward for reciting the Qur'an) says: 'O Lord! Decorate him. So he is donned with a crown of nobility. Then it says: O Lord! Give him more!' So he is donned with a suit of nobility. Then it says: O Lord! Be pleased with him.' So He is pleased with him and says: Recite and rise up, and be increased in reward with every Ayah.'
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Al-Muttalib bin 'Abullah bin Hantab: from Anas bin Malik that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The rewards for my Ummah were displayed before me, even (the reward for) the dust that a man comes out of the Masjid with. The sins of my Ummah were displayed before me, and I have not seen a sin worse than a Surah or Ayah of the Qur'an which a man learned and then forgot.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Al-Hasan: that 'Imran bin Husain passed by a reciter reciting then he began begging. So he ('Imran) said: 'Indeed we are from Allah and to Him shall we return.' Then he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: 'Whoever recites the Qur'an, then let him ask Allah by it. For indeed there will come a people, who will recite the Qur'an, asking from the people because of it.'
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Suhaib: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: He does not believe in the Qur'an who makes lawful what it prohibits.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Uqbah bin 'Amir: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The one who recites the Qur'an aloud is like the one who gives charity publicly, and the one who recites the Qur'an quietly is like the one who gives charity secretly.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Aishah: The Prophet (п·ә) would not sleep until he recited Surat Bani Isra'il and Az-Zumar.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Irbad bin Sariyah: that the Prophet (п·ә) would recite the Musabbihat (Chapters 17, 57, 59, 61-64 & 87) before sleep and say Indeed there is an Ayah in them that is better than one thousand Ayat.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Ma'qil bin Yasar: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Whoever says three times when he gets up in the morning: 'A'udhu Billahis-Sami Al-'Alim Min Ash-Shaitanir-Rajim' and he recites three Ayat from the end of Surah Al-Hashr - Allah appoints seventy-thousand angels who say Salat upon him until the evening. If he dies on that day, he dies a martyr, and whoever reaches the evening, he holds the same status.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Ya'la bin Mamlak: that he asked Umm Salamah, the wife of the Prophet (п·ә), about the recitation of the Prophet (п·ә) and his Salat. She said: What can you do compared to his Salat? He would pray and then sleep as long as he had prayed. Then he would pray as long as he had slept. Then he would pray as long as he had slept. Then he slept as long as he had prayed until the morning.' Then she described his recitation. So she described his recitation as word by word.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated Jabir bin 'Abdullah: During the Mawqif (hajj season), the Prophet (п·ә) would present himself sand say: 'Which man will bring me to his people? For indeed the Quraish have prevented me from conveying the Speech of my Lord.'
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Narrated 'Atiyyah: from Abu Sa'eed, that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The Lord, Blessed and Most High is He, has said: 'Whoever is too busy with the Qur'an for remembering Me and asking Me, then I shall give him more than what I give to those who ask.' And the virtue of Allah's Speech over the speech of others is like the virtue of Allah over His creation.
chapterName: Chapter on The Virtues of the Qur`an

text: Abu Hurairah narrated that: The Prophet said: вҖңThere is nothing more honorable with Allah [Most High] than supplication.вҖқ
Anas bin Malik narrated that : the Prophet said: вҖңThe supplication is the essence of worship.вҖқ

An-Nu`man bin Bashir narrated that: The Prophet said: вҖңThe supplication, is worship.вҖқ Then he recited: And Your Lord said: вҖңCall upon me, I will respond to you. Verily, those who scorn My worship, they will surely enter Hell humiliated.
Abu Hurairah (ra) narrated that : the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңIndeed, he who does not ask Allah, he gets angry with him.вҖқ

Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari (ra) said: вҖңWe were with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) on a military expedition. When we returned, we overlooked Al-Madinah, and the people were pronouncing the TakbД«r, and they raised their voices with it. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖҳVerily, your Lord is not deaf nor absent, He is between you and between the heads of your mounts.вҖҷ Then he said: вҖҳO `Abdullah bin Qais, should I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of Paradise: LДҒ бёҘawla wa lДҒ quwwata illДҒ billДҒh (There is no might or power except by Allah).вҖҷвҖқ

`Abdullah bin Busr (ra) narrated that: A man said: вҖңO Messenger of Allah (п·ә), indeed, the legislated acts of Islam have become too much for me, so inform me of a thing that I should stick to.вҖқ He (п·ә) said: вҖңLet not your tongue cease to be moist with the remembrance of Allah.вҖқ

Abu SaвҖҷeed Al-Khudri narrated that: The Messenger of Allah was asked: вҖңWhich of the worshippers is superior in rank with Allah on the Day of Judgment?вҖқ He said: вҖңThose men who remember Allah much [and women].вҖқ He said: вҖңI said: вҖҳO Messenger of Allah! What about the fighter in the cause of Allah?вҖҷ He said: вҖҳIf he were to strike with his sword among the disbelievers and the idolater, until it breaks, and he (or it) is dyed with blood, those who remember Allah much would still be superior in rank.вҖқ

Abu Ad-Darda [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that : the Prophet said: вҖңShould I not inform you of the best of your deed, and the purest of them with your Master, and the highest of them in your ranks, and what is better for you than spending gold and silver, and better for you than meeting your enemy and striking their necks, and they strike your necks?вҖқ They said: вҖңOf course.вҖқ He said, вҖңThe remembrance of Allah [Most High].вҖқ [Then] MuвҖҷadh bin Jabal [may Allah be pleased with him] said: вҖңThere is nothing that brings more salvation from the punishment of Allah than the remembrance of Allah.вҖқ

Al-Agharr Abu Muslim narrated that: He bears witness, from Abu Hurairah and Abu SaвҖҷeed Al-Khudri, that they bear witness, from the Messenger of Allah, that he said: вҖңThere is no group that remembers Allah, except that the angels encompass them, mercy covers them, and tranquility descends upon them: and Allah remembers (mentions) them before those who are with Him.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that : the Prophet said: вҖңNo group gather in a sitting in which they do not remember Allah, nor sent Salat upon their Prophet, except it will be a source of remorse for them. If He wills, He will punish them, and if He wills, He will forgive them.вҖқ

Jabir narrated that : the Messenger of Allah said: вҖңThere is none who utters a supplication, except that Allah gives him what he asked, or prevents evil from him that is equal to it вҖ“ as long as he does not supplicate for something evil, or the cutting of ties of the womb.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that: The Messenger of Allah said: вҖңWhoever wishes that Allah would respond to him during hardship and grief, then let him supplicate plentifully when at ease.вҖқ

Jabir bin `Abdullah (ra) narrated that : the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңThe best remembrance is: вҖҳthere is none worthy of worship except Allah (LДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh)вҖҷ and the best supplication is: вҖҳAll praise is due to Allah (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒh).вҖҷвҖқ
`A'ishah (ra) narrated that : the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to remember Allah in all of his affairs.
Ibn `Abbas narrated from Ubayy bin Ka`b that : whenever the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) would mention someone and supplicate for him, he would begin with himself (п·ә).

Umar bin Al-Khattab [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated: вҖңWhenever the Messenger of Allah would raise his hands in supplication, he would not lower them until he had wiped his face with them.вҖқ
Abu Hurairah narrated that : the Prophet said: вҖңOne of you will be responded to, so long as he is not hasty, saying: вҖҳI supplicated, and I was not responded to.вҖҷвҖқ

Thawban (ra) said: вҖңThe Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖҳWhoever says when he reaches the evening: вҖңI am pleased with Allah as (my) Lord, with Islam as (my) religion, and with Muhammad (п·ә) as (my) Prophet (RaбёҚД«tu billДҒhi rabban wabil-IslДҒmi dД«nan wa bi-MuбёҘammadin nabiyyan) it is a duty upon Allah to please him.вҖҷвҖқ (Hasan Gharib)

Abu Hurairah (ra) said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to teach his Companions, saying: вҖңWhen one of you reached the morning, then let him say: вҖҳO Allah, by You we enter the morning, and by You we enter the evening, and be You we live, and by You we died, and to You is the Return (AllДҒhumma bika aб№ЈbaбёҘnДҒ wa bika amsainДҒ wa bika naбёҘyДҒ wa bika namЕ«tu wa ilaikal-maб№ЈД«r). And when he reaches the evening let him say: вҖҳO Allah, by You we enter the evening, and by You we enter the morning, and by You we live, and by You we die, and to You is the Resurrection (AllДҒhumma bika amsainДҒ wa bika aб№ЈbaбёҘnДҒ wa bika naбёҘyДҒ wa bika namЕ«tu wa ilaikan-nushЕ«r).вҖҷвҖқ

Rafi` bin Khadij narrated that: The Prophet said: вҖңWhen one of you lies down on his right side, then says: вҖҳO Allah, I have submitted myself to You, and I have turned my face to You, and I lay myself down relying upon You, and I have entrusted my affair to You, there is no refuge [nor escape] from You except to You. I believe in Your Book and Your Messengers (AllДҒhumma innД« aslamtu nafsД« ilaika wa wajjahtu wajhД« ilaika, wa aljaвҖҷtu бә“ahrД« ilaika, wa fawwaбёҚtu amrД« ilaika, lДҒ malja'a [wa lДҒ manjДҒ] minka illДҒ ilaik, Е«minu bikitДҒbika wa birusulika)вҖҷ вҖ“ then if he dies that night, he shall enter Paradise.вҖқ

Anas bin Malik (ra) narrated that : when he went to his bed, the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to say: вҖңAll praise is due to Allah, who has fed us and given us to drink, and has sufficed and granted us refuge, and how many are they who have none to suffice them and none to grant them refuge (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒhi alladhi aб№ӯ`amanДҒ wa saqДҒnДҒ wa kafДҒnДҒ wa ДҒwДҒnДҒ, wakam mimman lДҒ kДҒfiya lahu wa lДҒ mu'wiy).вҖқ

Abu Sa`eed (ra) narrated that : the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says, when he goes to his bed: вҖҳI seek forgiveness from Allah, [the Magnificent] the One whom there is none worthy of worship except for Him, the Living, the Sustainer, and I repent to Him (AstaghfirullДҒha [al-`Aбә“im] alladhi lДҒ ilДҒha illДҒ huw, al-бёӨayyul-QayyЕ«m, wa atЕ«bu ilaihi)вҖҷ three times, Allah shall forgive him his sins if they were like the foam of the sea, even if they were the number of leaves of the trees, even if they were the number of sand particles of `Alij, even if they were the number of the days of the world.вҖқ

Hudhaifah bin Al-Yaman (ra) narrated that: When the Prophet (п·ә) would sleep, he would put his hand under his head then say: вҖңO Allah, safeguard me from Your Punishment the Day You gather [Your slaves] (AllДҒhumma qinД« `adhДҒbaka yawma tajma`u [`ibДҒdak])вҖқ or вҖңyou resurrect your slaves (tab`athu `ibДҒdak).вҖқ

Al-Bara' bin `Azib (ra) narrated that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to lay his head upon his right hand when going to sleep, then say: вҖңMy Lord, safeguard me from Your punishment the Day You resurrect Your slaves (Rabbi qinД« `adhДҒbaka yawma tab`athu `ibДҒdak).

Aishah narrated that: Every night, when the Prophet (п·ә) would go to his bed, he would join his hands, then blow in them, as he recited in them: вҖңSay: He is Allah, the One.вҖқ And вҖңSay: I seek refuge in the Lord of Al-FalaqвҖқ and вҖңSay: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind.вҖқ Then he would wipe as much as he was able to of his body with them, beginning with them first of his head and face, and the front of his body. He would do this three times.
Jabir said: вҖңThe Prophet would not sleep until he reached Tanzil as-Sajdah and Tabarak.вҖқ

`A'isha (ra) said: вҖңThe Prophet (п·ә) would not sleep until he recited Az-Zumar and Banu IsraвҖҷil.вҖқ Muhammad bin Ismail informed me: вҖңThis Abu LubabahвҖҷs (a narrator in the chain) name is Marwan, the freed slave of Abdur-Rahman bin Ziyad. He heard from Aisha, and Hammad bin Zaid heard from him.вҖқ
Ar-Irbad bin Sariyah (ra) narrated that: The Prophet (п·ә) would not sleep until he had recited Al-Musabbihat, and he would say: вҖңIn them is an Ayah that is better than a thousand Ayah.вҖқ

Ali [may Allah be pleased with him] said: вҖңFatimah complained to me about her hands blistering from grinding flour. So I said: вҖҳIf you were to approach your father and ask him for a servant?вҖҷ So he (the Prophet) said: вҖҳShould I not direct the two of you, to that which is better for you than a servant? When the two of you lay down to sleep, say thirty-three, thirty-three, thirty-four, of At-TaбёҘmД«d, At-TasbД«бёҘ, and At-TakbД«r.вҖқ
Ali [may Allah be pleased with him] said: вҖңFatimah went to the Prophet complaining of her hands blistering, so he ordered her to say At-TasbД«бёҘ, At-TakbД«r, and At-TaбёҘmД«d.вҖқ
Abdullah bin Amr [may Allah be pleased with him] said: вҖңI saw the Messenger of Allah counting At-TasbД«бёҘ.вҖқ

KaвҖҷb bin Ujrah narrated that: The Prophet said: вҖңThere are MuвҖҷaqqibat, he who says them shall not be miserable. Glorify Allah at the end of every prayer thirty-three times, and praise him thirty-three times, and extol His greatness thirty-four times.вҖқ

Zaid bin Thabit (ra) said: вҖңWe were ordered to say the TasbД«бёҘ at the end of every Salat thirty-three times, and to say the TakbД«r thirty-four times.вҖқ He said: вҖңThen a man from the Ansar had a dream in which someone said: вҖҳThe Messenger of Allah (п·ә) ordered you to say the TasbД«бёҘ at the end of every Salat thirty-three times, and to say the TaбёҘmД«d thirty-three times, and to say the TakbД«r thirty-four times?вҖҷ He said: вҖҳYes.вҖҷ He said: вҖҳThen make them twenty-five and add the TahlД«l (saying LДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh) to them.вҖҷ The next day he went to the Prophet (п·ә) and informed him, so he said: вҖңDo it.вҖқ
Maslamah bin `Amr said: вҖң`Umair bin Hani used to perform a thousand prostrations every day and recite a hundred thousand TasbД«бёҘs every day.вҖқ

Rabi`ah bin Ka`b Al-Aslami said: вҖңI used to spend the night at the door of the Prophet (п·ә), so that I may give him his water for Wudu', and I would hear him, for a long period of the night, saying: вҖҳAllah hears the one who praise Him (Sami` AllДҒhu liman бёҘamidah).вҖҷ And I would hear him, for a long period saying: вҖҳAll praise is due to Allah, Lord of the all that exists. (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒhi rabbil-`ДҒlamД«n).вҖҷвҖқ

Hudhaifah bin Al-Yaman [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that when the Prophet (п·ә) wanted to sleep, he would say: вҖңO Allah, in Your Name I die and I live (AllДҒhumma bismika amЕ«tu wa aбёҘyДҒ).вҖқ And when he would wake, he would say: вҖңAllah praise is due to Allah who revived my soul after causing its death and to Him is the resurrection (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒh, alladhД« aбёҘyДҒ nafsД« ba`da mДҒ amДҒtahДҒ wa ilaihin-nushЕ«r).вҖқ

Aishah narrated: вҖңWhen the Messenger of Allah would prostrate (for recitation of) the QurвҖҷan, he would say: вҖҳI have prostrated my face to the One Who created it, and made its hearing and vision, through His ability and power (Sajada wajhД« lilladhД« khalaqahЕ« wa shaqqa sam`ahЕ« wa baб№ЈarahЕ« bi бёҘawlihД« wa quwwatih).вҖҷвҖқ

Anas bin Malik narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says вҖ“ that is: when he leaves his house вҖ“ вҖҳIn the Name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah, there is no might or power except by Allah (BismillДҒh, tawakkaltu `alallДҒh, lДҒ бёҘawla wa lДҒ quwwata illДҒ billДҒh)вҖҷ it will be said to him: вҖҳYou have been sufficed and protected,вҖҷ and Shaitan will become distant from him.вҖқ

Umm Salamah narrated that : When the Prophet (п·ә) would leave his house, he would say: вҖңIn the Name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah. O Allah!
