We seek refuge in You from slipping unintentionally or becoming misguided, or committing oppression or being oppressed, or acting ignorantly or being treated ignorantly (BismillДҒh, tawakkaltu `alallДҒh. AllДҒhumma, innДҒ na`Е«dhu bika min an nazilla aw naбёҚilla, aw naбә“lima aw nuбә“lam, aw najhala aw yujhala alainДҒ).вҖқ

Umar narrated that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever sees a person afflicted and say: вҖҳAll praise is due to Allah Who saved me from that which He has afflicted you with, and blessed me greatly over many of those whom He has created, (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒhi alladhД« `ДҒfДҒnД« mimmabtalДҒka bihД« wa faбёҚбёҚalanД« `alДҒ kathД«rin mimman khalaqa tafбёҚД«la)вҖҷ then he shall be saved from that affliction for as long as he lives.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever sees an afflicted person then says: вҖҳAll praise is due to Allah who saved me from that which He has afflicted you with, and blessed me greatly over many of those whom He has created, (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒhi alladhД« `ДҒfДҒnД« mimmabtalДҒka bihД« wa faбёҚбёҚalanД« `alДҒ kathД«rin mimman khalaqa tafбёҚД«la)вҖҷ he shall not be struck by that affliction.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever sits in a sitting and engages in much empty, meaningless speech and then says before getting from that sitting of his: вҖҳGlory is to You, O Allah, and praise, I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship except You, I seek You forgiveness, and I repent to You, (SubбёҘДҒnaka AllДҒhumma wa biбёҘamdika, ashhadu an lДҒ ilДҒha illДҒ anta, astaghfiruka wa atЕ«bu ilaik)вҖҷ whatever occurred in that sitting would be forgiven to him.вҖқ

Ibn Umar said: вҖңIn one sitting of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), one could count that he said a hundred time, before he would get up: вҖҳO my Lord, forgive me, and accept my repentance. Verily, You are the Oft-Returning, the Most Forgiving (RabbighfirlД« watub `alayya innaka antat-TawwДҒbul-GhafЕ«r).вҖҷвҖқ

bn Abbas narrated that : when he was in distress, the Prophet of Allah (п·ә) would supplicate: вҖңThere is none worthy of worship except Allah, the Forbearing, the Wise, there is none worthy of worship except Allah, the Lord of the Magnificent Throne, there is none worthy of worship except Allah, the Lord of the heavens and the earth, and the Lord of the Noble Throne (LДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh al-`aliyyul бёҘalД«m, lДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh, rabbul-`arshil-`aбә“Д«m, lДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh, rabbus-samДҒwДҒti wal-arбёҚi wa rabbul-`arshil-karД«m).вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that: When a matter would worry the Prophet (п·ә), he would raise his head up toward the sky and say: вҖңGlory is to Allah, the Magnificent (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒhil-`Aбә“Д«m).вҖқ And when he would strive in supplication; he would say: вҖңO the Living, O Sustainer (YДҒ бёӨayyu yДҒ QayyЕ«m).вҖқ

Sa`d bin Abi Waqqas narrated: From Khawlah bint Al Hakim As-Sulamiyyah, that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever stops at a stopping place and then says: вҖҳI seek refuge in AllahвҖҷs Perfect Words from the evil of what He has created, (A`Е«dhu bi-kalimДҒtillДҒhit-tДҒmmДҒti min sharri mДҒ khalaq)вҖҷ nothing shall harm him until he departs from that stopping place of his.вҖқ

Ar-Rabi` bin Al-BaraвҖҷ bin `Azib reported from, his father: That whenever the Prophet (п·ә) would return from a trip, he would say: вҖң(We are) Returning, repenting, worshipping, and to our Lord directing the praise (ДҖвҖҷibЕ«na tДҒвҖҷbЕ«na `ДҒbidЕ«na lirabbinДҒ бёҘДҒmidЕ«n).вҖқ

Anas narrated that: When the Prophet would return from a trip and see the walls of Al-Madinah, he would speed up his riding camel, and if he was upon a beast, he would agitate it, out of his love for Al-Madinah.

Ibn Umar narrated, saying: вҖңWhen the Prophet (п·ә) would bid farewell to a man, he would take his hand, and not let it go until the man let go of the hand, of the Prophet, and he would say: вҖҳI entrust to Allah your religion, your trusts, and the last of your deeds (Astawdi`ullДҒha dД«naka wa amДҒnataka wa ДҒkhira `amalik).вҖҷвҖқ

Salim narrated that : when he intended to undertake a journey, Ibn `Umar used to say to a person to вҖңCome close to me so that I may bid you farewell as the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to bid us farewell.вҖқ Then he would say: вҖңI entrust to Allah your religion, and your trusts, and the last of your deeds (Astawdi`ullДҒha dД«naka wa amДҒnataka wa khawДҒtД«ma `amalik).вҖқ

Anas said: вҖңA man came to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and said: вҖҳI intend to undertake a journey, so give me provision. He said: вҖҳMay Allah grant you Taqwa as your provision (Zawwadak AllДҒhut-taqwДҒ).вҖҷ He said: вҖҳGive me more.вҖҷ He said: вҖҳAnd may He forgive your sin (Wa ghafara dhanbak).вҖҷ He said: вҖҳGive me more, may my father be ransomed for you, and my mother.вҖҷ He said: вҖҳAnd may He make goodness easy for you wherever you are (Wa yassara lakal-khaira бёҘaithu mДҒ kunt).вҖҷвҖқ

Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that : a man said: вҖңO Messenger of Allah (п·ә), I intend to travel, so advise me.вҖқ He said, вҖңHold fast to the Taqwa of Allah, and (say the) Takbir upon every elevated place.вҖқ So when the man turned away he said: вҖңO Allah make near for him the distance, and ease for him the journey (AllДҒhummaб№ӯwi lahul-arбёҚa wa hawwin `alaihis-safar).вҖқ

Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated : that the Messenger of Allah said: вҖңThree supplications are responded to: The supplication of the oppressed, the supplication of the traveler, and the supplication of the parent against his child.вҖқ Ali bin Hujr narrated to us (he said): Ismail bin Ibrahim reported to us from Hisham Ad-DastawaвҖҷi, from Yahya bin Abu Kathir with this chain, narrating similar to it, but he added to it: вҖңresponded to, there is no doubt in them.вҖқ

Aishah [may Allah be pleased with her] narrates, saying: вҖңWhen the Prophet (п·ә) would see the wind he would say: вҖҳO Allah, indeed, I ask You for its good, the good of what is in it, and the good of what it has been sent with. And I seek refuge in You from its evil, the evil of what is in it, and the evil of what it has been sent with (AllДҒhumma innД« asвҖҷaluka min khairihДҒ wa khairi mДҒ fД«hДҒ, wa khairi mДҒ ursilat bihД«, wa a`Е«dhu bika min sharrihДҒ wa sharri mДҒ fД«hДҒ, wa sharri mДҒ ursilat bih).вҖҷвҖқ

Salim bin Abdullah bin Umar narrated : from his father, that when the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) would hear the sound of thunder and lightning bolts, he would say: вҖңO Allah, do not kill us with Your wrath, and do not destroy us with Your punishment, and pardon us before that (AllДҒhumma lДҒ taqtulnДҒ bi-ghaбёҚabika wa lДҒ tuhliknДҒ bi-`adhДҒbika wa `ДҒfinДҒ qabla dhДҒlik).вҖқ

Bilal bin Yahya bin Talhah bin `Ubaidullah narrated : from his father, from his grandfather Talhah bin `Ubaidullah that when the Prophet (п·ә) would see a crescent moon, he would say: вҖңO Allah, bring it over us with blessing and faith, and security and Islam. My Lord and your Lord is Allah (AllДҒhumma ahlilhu `alainДҒ bil-yumni wal-ДӘmДҒn, was-salДҒmati wal-IslДҒm, rabbД« wa rabbuk AllДҒh).вҖқ

Mu`adh bin Jabal narrated : that one of the two men cursed the other next to the Prophet (п·ә), until anger could be recognized in the face of one of them. So the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңVerily, I know a statement, that if he were to say it, his anger would leave: вҖҳI seek refuge in Allah from Shaitan, the rejected (A`Е«dhu billДҒhi minash-shaiб№ӯДҒnir-rajД«m).вҖҷвҖқ

Abu SaвҖҷeed Al-Khudri narrated that: He heard the Prophet saying: вҖңWhen one of you sees a dream that he likes, then it is from Allah, so let him praise Allah for it, and speak concerning what he saw. And when he sees other than that of what he dislikes, then it is from Shaitan, so let him seek refuge in Allah from its evil, and not mention it to anyone for, surely, it shall not harm him.вҖқ

Abu Umamah narrated that : when the table spread would be lifted from in front of him, the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) would say: вҖңAll praise is due to Allah, abundant, good, blessed praise, without being left off, nor being without need of it, O our Lord (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒhi бёҘamdan kathД«ran б№ӯayyiban mubДҒrakan fД«hi, ghaira muwadda`in, wa lДҒ mustaghnan `anhu rabbanДҒ).вҖқ

Abu Sa`eed [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that: When the Prophet (п·ә) used to eat or drink, he would say: вҖңAll praise is due to Allah who fed us and gave us drink, and made us Muslims (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒh, alladhД« aб№ӯ`amanДҒ, wa saqДҒnДҒ, wa ja`alanДҒ muslimД«n).вҖқ

Sahl bin Mu`adh bin Anas narrated from his father that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever eats food and then says: вҖҳAll praise is due to Allah who fed me this and granted it as provision to me, without any effort from me nor power, (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒh, alladhД« aб№ӯ`amanД« hДҒdha wa razaqanД«hi min ghairi бёҘawlin minnД«, wa lДҒ quwwatin)вҖҷ his past sins shall be forgiven.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that: The Prophet said: вҖңwhen you hear the crowing of the roosters, then ask Allah of His bounty, for verily they have seen an angel. When you hear the braying of a donkey, then seek refuge in Allah from Shaitan, [the rejected] for, verily, it has seen a Shaitan.вҖқ

Abdullah bin Amr narrated that : the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңthere is not anyone upon the earth who says: вҖҳNone has the right to be worshipped but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest, and there is no might nor power except by Allah, (LДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh, wa AllДҒhu akbar, wa lДҒ бёҘawla wa lДҒ quwwata illДҒ billДҒh) except that his sins shall be pardoned, even if they were like the foam of the sea.вҖқ

Abu Musa Al-Ash`ari said: вҖңWe were with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) on a military expedition. When we returned, we overlooked Al-Madinah, and the people were pronouncing the Takbir, and they raised their voices with it. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖҳVerily, your Lord is not deaf nor absent, [and] He is between you and between the heads of your mounts.вҖҷ Then he said: вҖҳO `Abdullah bin Qais, should I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of Paradise: LДҒ бёҘawla wa lДҒ quwwata illДҒ billДҒh (There is no might or power except by Allah).вҖҷвҖқ

Ibn Mas`ud narrated that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңI met Ibrahim on the night of my ascent, so he said: вҖҳO Muhammad, recite Salam from me to your nation, and inform them that Paradise has pure soil and delicious water, and that it is a flat treeless plain, and that its seeds are: вҖңGlory is to Allah (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒh) [and] all praise is due to Allah (Al-бёҘamdulillДҒh) and вҖҳnone has the right to be worshipped but AllahвҖҷ (LДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh), and Allah is the greatest (AllДҒhu Akbar).вҖқ

Mus`ab bin Sa`d narrated from his father, that : the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to those sitting with him: вҖңIs one of you incapable of attaining a thousand good deeds?вҖқ So a questioner among those seated with him asked him: вҖңHow can one of us earn a thousand good deeds?вҖқ He said: вҖң(When) one of you recites a hundred TasbД«бёҘДҒt a thousand good deeds are written for him, and a thousand evil deeds are wiped away from him.вҖқ

Jabir narrated that: The Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says: вҖҳGlory is to Allah, the Magnificent, and with His Praise (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒhil-Aбә“Д«m, wa biбёҘamdih)вҖҷ a date-palm tree is planted for him in Paradise.вҖқ

Jabir narrated that : The Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says: вҖҳGlory is to Allah, the Magnificent, and with His Praise (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒhil-Aбә“Д«m, wa biбёҘamdih)вҖҷ a date-palm tree is planted for him in Paradise.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that : the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says: вҖҳGlory is to Allah, and with His Praise (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒh, wa biбёҘamdih)вҖҷ a hundred times, his sins are forgiven, even if they were like the foam of the sea.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңThere are two statements that are light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale, and beloved to Ar-RaбёҘmДҒn: вҖңGlory is to Allah and the praise; Glory is to Allah, the Magnificent. (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒhi wa biбёҘamdih, SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒhil-Aбә“Д«m)вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that: The Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says in the morning and in the evening вҖҳGlory is to Allah and with His Praise (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒh, wa biбёҘamdih)вҖҷ a hundred times, none shall bring better than him on the Day of Judgment except one who did the same as him, or increased upon it.вҖқ

Ibn `Umar narrated that one day, : The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to his Companions: вҖңSay вҖҳGlory is to Allah and with His Praise (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒh, wa biбёҘamdih)вҖҷ a hundred times. Whoever says [it] one time, it is written for him ten, and whoever says it ten (times), it is written for him a hundred, and whoever says it a hundred (times), it is written for him a thousand, and whoever increases, Allah will increase for him, and whoever seeks AllahвҖҷs forgiveness, [Allah] will forgive him.вҖқ
Az-Zuhri said: вҖңA Tasbihah in Ramadan is better than a thousand Tasbihah in other that it.вҖқ

Tamim Ad-Dari narrated that: The Messsenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says ten times: вҖҳI bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah. Alone, without partner, One Deity, the One, As-Samad, He did not take a wife, nor a child, nor is there anyone like Him, (Ash-hadu an lДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh, waбёҘdahu lДҒ sharД«ka lahu, ilahan wahidan, aбёҘadan б№Јamadan lam yattakhidh б№ЈДҒбёҘibatan wa lДҒ waladan wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan aбёҘad)вҖҷ Allah will write for him forty million good deeds.вҖқ

Fadalah bin `Ubaid narrated: вҖңWhile the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was seated, a man entered and performed Salat, and he said: вҖҳO Allah, forgive me, and have mercy upon me.вҖҷ The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖҳYou have rushed, O praying person. When you perform Salat and then sit, then praise Allah with what He is deserving of, and send Salat upon me, then call upon Him.вҖҷвҖқ He said: вҖңThen another man performed Salat after that, so he praised Allah and sent Salat upon the Prophet (п·ә). The Prophet (п·ә) said to him: вҖҳO praying person! Supplicate, and you shall be answered.вҖҷвҖқ

`Amr bin Malik Al-Janbi narrated that he heard Fadalah bin `Ubaid saying: вҖңThe Prophet (п·ә) heard a man supplicating in his Salat but he did not send Salat upon the Prophet (п·ә), so the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖҳThis one has rushed.вҖҷ Then he called him and said to him, or to someone other than him: вҖҳWhen one of you performs Salat, then let him begin by expressing gratitude to Allah and praising Him. Then, let him send Salat upon the Prophet (п·ә), then let him supplicate after that, whatever he wishes.вҖҷвҖқ

Asma bint Yazid narrated that the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңAllah's greatest name is in these two Ayah: вҖҳAnd your deity is One deity, there is none who has the right to be worshipped but He, Ar-RaбёҘmДҒn, Ar-RaбёҘД«m.вҖҷ And the Opening of Al-`ImrДҒn: вҖҳAlif. LДҒm. MД«m. Allah, None has the right to be worshipped but He, the Ever living, the Sustainer.вҖҷвҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңCall upon Allah while being certain of being answered, and know that Allah does not respond to a supplication from the heart of one heedless and occupied by play.вҖқ

Aishah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to say: вҖңO Allah, grant me health in my body, and grant me health in my sight, and make it the inheritor from me, there is none has the right to be worshipped but Allah, the Forbearing, the Generous, Glory is to Allah, the Lord of the Magnificent Throne, and all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of all that exists (AllДҒhumma `ДҒfinД« fД« jasadi, wa `ДҒfinД« fД« baб№ЈarД«, waj`alhul-wДҒritha minnД«, lДҒ ilДҒha illДҒllah, al-бёҘalД«mul-karД«m, subбёҘДҒn AllДҒhi rabbil-`arshil-aбә“Д«m, wal-бёҘamdulillДҒhi rabbil-`alamД«n).вҖқ

`Abdullah bin `Amr narrated that: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to say: вҖңO Allah, I seek refuge in You from a heart that does not humble itself, and from a supplication that is not heard, and from a soul that is never satisfied, and from knowledge that does not benefit, I seek refuge in You from these four (AllДҒhumma innД« a`Е«dhu bika min qalbin lДҒ yakhsha`u, wa min du`ДҒвҖҷin lДҒ yusma`u, wa min nafsin lДҒ tashba`u, wa min `ilmin lДҒ yanfa`u, a`Е«dhu bika min hДҒвҖҷulaвҖҷil-arba`).вҖқ

Anas bin Mallik [may Allah be pleased with him] narrates, saying: вҖңI used to often hear the Prophet (п·ә) supplicating with these words: вҖҳO Allah, I seek refuge in You from sadness, grief, helplessness, laziness, being stingy, overwhelming debt, and the overpowering of men (AllДҒhumma innД« a`Е«dhu bika minal-hammi wal-бёҘazani wal-`ajzi wal-kasali wal-bukhli wa бёҚala`id-dain wa ghalabatir-rijДҒl).вҖҷвҖқ

Anas narrated that : The Prophet (п·ә) used to supplicate, saying: вҖңO Allah, indeed, I seek refuge in You from laziness, weakness of old age, cowardice, stinginess, the trial of Al-Masih, and the punishment of the grave (AllДҒhumma innД« a`Е«dhu bika minal-kasali wal-harami wal-jubni wal-bukhli wa fitnatil-masД«бёҘi wa `adhabil-qabr).вҖқ
Abdullah bin Amr narrated: вҖңI saw the Prophet counting the Tasbih on his hand.вҖқ

Hisham bin Hassan narrated from Al-Hasan : concerning the saying of Allah: O our Lord, give us good in this world, and good in the Hereafter. He said: вҖңKnowledge and worship in this world, and Paradise in the Hereafter.вҖқ

`Abdullah narrated that : the Prophet (п·ә) used to supplicate: вҖңO Allah, indeed, I ask You for guidance, piety, chastity, and sufficiency (AllДҒhumma innД« asвҖҷalukal-hudДҒ wat-tuqДҒ, wal-`afДҒfa wal-ghinДҒ).вҖқ

Shakal bin Humaid said: I came to the Prophet and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, teach me a way of seeking refuge so that I may seek refuge by it. He said: So he took my hand and said: 'Say: O Allah, indeed I seek refuge in You from the evil of my hearing and the evil of my sight, and the evil of my tongue and the evil of my heart, and the evil of my semen (AllДҒhumma innД« a`Е«dhu bika min sharri sam`Д« wa min sharri baб№ЈarД«, wa min sharri lisДҒnД«, wa min sharri qalbД«, wa min sharri maniyyД«).'

Aishah narrated: вҖңI was sleeping at the side of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), then I lost him during the night. So I felt around for him, and my hand fell upon his feet while he was prostrating, and he was saying: вҖҳI seek refuge in Your pleasure from Your anger, and in Your pardon from Your punishment. I cannot count Your praises. You are as You have praised Yourself (A`Е«dhu biriбёҚДҒka min sakhaб№ӯika wa bi mu`ДҒfДҒtika min `uqЕ«batika, lДҒ uбёҘб№Јi thanДҒвҖҷan `alaika anta kamДҒ athnaita `alДҒ nafsik).вҖҷвҖқ

`Abdullah bin `Abbas narrated: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used [to teach them this supplication as he used] to teach them a Surat of the Quran: вҖңO Allah, indeed, I seek refuge in you from the punishment of Hell, and from the punishment of the grave, and I seek refuge in You from the trial of the false Masih, and I seek refuge in You from the trial of living and dying. (AllДҒhumma innД« a`Е«dhu bika min `adhДҒbi jahannam, wa min `adhДҒbil-qabr, wa a`Е«dhu bika min fitnatil-masД«бёҘid-dajjДҒl, wa a`Е«dhu bika min fitnatil-maбёҘyДҒ wal-mamДҒt)вҖқ

Aishah said: вҖңI heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying at his death: вҖҳO Allah, forgive me and have mercy on me, and join me with the Highest Company (AllДҒhummaghfirlД« warбёҘamnД« wa alбёҘiqnД« bir-rafД«qil a`lДҒ).вҖҷвҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah said: вҖңNone of you should say: вҖҳO Allah forgive me if You wish. O Allah have mercy on me if You wish.вҖҷ Let him be firm in asking, for there is none that can compel Him to do things.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah said: вҖңOur Lord descends every night to the nearest heaven, until the last third of the night remains, so He says: вҖҳWho is calling upon Me so that I may answer him? Who is asking from Me so that I may give him? And who is seeking forgiveness from Me, so that I may forgive him.вҖҷвҖқ

Abu Umamah narrated: вҖңIt was said: вҖҳO Messenger of Allah, which supplication is most likely to be listened to?вҖҷ He said: вҖҳ(During) the last part of the night, and at the end of the obligatory prayers.вҖҷвҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that a man said: вҖңO Messenger of Allah, I heard your supplication last night, and the part of it that reached me of it, was that you said: вҖҳO Allah, forgive me my sin, and expand for me my abode, and bless for me that which You have provided meвҖҷ (AllДҒhummaghfirlД« dhanbД«, wa wassi` lД« fД« dДҒrД«, wa bДҒrik lД« fД«mДҒ razaqtanД«). He said: вҖҳDo you see that they leave off anything?вҖҷвҖқ

Muslim bin Abi Bakrah said: вҖңMy father heard me while I was saying: вҖңO Allah, I seek refuge in You from sadness and laziness and the punishment of the grave (AllДҒhumma, innД« a`Е«dhu bika minal-hammi wal-kasali wa `adhДҒbil-qabr).вҖҷ He said: вҖҳO my son, from who did you hear this?вҖҷвҖқ He said: вҖңI said: вҖҳI heard you saying them.вҖҷ He said: вҖҳStick to them, for indeed, I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying them.вҖҷвҖқ

Ali [may Allah be pleased with him] said: вҖңThe Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to me: вҖҳShould I not teach you some words that if you say them, Allah will forgive you, even if you were already forgiven?вҖҷ He said: вҖҳSay: None has the right to be worshipped by Allah, the Most High, the Magnificent. None has the right to be worshipped by Allah, the Forbearing, the Generous. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah. Glory to Allah, the Lord of the Magnificent Throne. (LДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒhul-`aliyul-`aбә“Д«m, lДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒhul-бёҘalД«mul-karД«m, lДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh, subбёҘДҒn AllДҒhi rabbil-`arshil-`aбә“Д«m.)вҖҷвҖқ

Ibrahim bin Muhammad bin Sa`d narrated from his father, from Sa`d that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңThe supplication of Dhun-Nun (Prophet Yunus) when he supplicated, while in the belly of the whale was: вҖҳThere is none worthy of worship except You, Glory to You, Indeed, I have been of the transgressors. (LДҒ ilДҒha illДҒ anta subбёҘДҒnaka innД« kuntu minaбә“-бә“ДҒlimД«n)вҖҷ So indeed, no Muslim man supplicates with it for anything, ever, except Allah responds to him.вҖқ
Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated the the Prophet said: вҖңIndeed Allah has ninety-nine Names, one hundred less one, whoever counts them shall enter Paradise.вҖқ
Abu Hurairah narrated that the Prophet said: вҖңIndeed, Allah has ninety-nine Names, whoever counts them shall enter Paradise.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhen you pass by the gardens of Paradise, then feast.вҖқ I said: вҖңO Messenger of Allah, and what are the gardens of Paradise?вҖқ He said, вҖңThe Masajid.вҖқ I said: вҖңAnd what is feasting, O Messenger of Allah?вҖқ He said: вҖңвҖҳGlory is to Allah, (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒh)вҖҷ and вҖҳAll praise is due to Allah, (Al-бёӨamdulillДҒh)вҖҷ and вҖҳNone has the right to be worshipped but Allah, (LДҒ IlДҒha IllallДҒh)вҖҷ and вҖҳAllah is the Greatest (AllДҒhu Akbar).вҖҷвҖқ

Anas bin Malik [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated that the Messenger of Allah said: вҖңWhen you pass by the gardens of Paradise, them feast.вҖқ They said: вҖңAnd what are the gardens of Paradise?вҖқ He said: вҖңThe circles of remembrance.вҖқ

Anas bin Malik narrated that a man came to the Prophet (п·ә) and said: вҖңO Messenger of Allah, which supplication is the best?вҖқ He (п·ә) said: вҖңAsk Your Lord For Al-`ДҖfiyah and Al-Mu`ДҒfДҒh in this world and in the Hereafter.вҖқ Then he came to him on the second day and said: вҖңO Messenger of Allah, which supplication is the best?вҖқ So he (п·ә) said to him similar to that. Then he came to him on the third day, so he (п·ә) said to him similar to that. He (п·ә) said: вҖңSo when you have been given Al-`ДҖfiyah in this world, and you have been given it in the Hereafter, then you have succeeded.вҖқ

Aishah narrated: вҖңI said: вҖҳO Messenger of Allah, what is your view if I know when the Night of Al-Qadr is, then what should I say in it?вҖқ He said: вҖҳSay: вҖңO Allah, indeed You are Pardoning, [Generous,] You love pardon, so pardon me (AllДҒhumma innaka `Afuwwun [KarД«mun], tuбёҘibbul-`afwa fa`fu `annД«).вҖҷвҖқ

Al-`Abbas bin `Abdul-Muttalib said: вҖңI said: вҖҳO Messenger of Allah, teach me something that I may ask Allah, [Mighty and Sublime] for.вҖҷ He (п·ә) said: вҖҳAsk Allah for Al-`ДҖfiyah.вҖҷ Then I remained for a day, then I came and said: вҖҳO Messenger of Allah, teach me something that I may ask Allah for.вҖҷ So he (п·ә) said to me: вҖңO Abbas, O uncle of the Messenger of Allah! Ask Allah for Al-`ДҖfiyah in the world and in the Hereafter.вҖқ
[Ibn Umar narrated that the Messenger of Allah said (п·ә): вҖңAllah has not been asked for anything more beloved to Him than being asked for Al-`ДҖfiyah.]
Aishah narrated from Abu Bakr As-Siddiq,: that whenever the Prophet (п·ә) wanted to do a matter, he would say: вҖңO Allah, make it good for me and choose for me. (AllДҒhumma khir lД« wakhtar lД«)вҖқ

Abu Malik Al-Ash`ari narrated that the Messenger of Allah said: вҖңAl-Wudu is half of faith, and All praise is due to Allah (Al-бёӨamdulillДҒh) fills the Scale, and Glory is to Allah and all praise is to Allah (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒh wal-бёӨamdulillДҒh)вҖҷ fillвҖқ - or - вҖңfills what is between the heavens and the earth, and Salat is light and charity is an evidence, and patience is an illumination, and the Quran is a proof for you or against you. And all people shall come to the morning selling their souls, either setting it free or destroying it.вҖқ

Abdullah bin Amr narrated that the Messenger of Allah said: вҖңAt-TasbД«бёҘ is half of the Scale, and All praise is due to Allah (Al-бёӨamdulillДҒh)вҖҷ fills it, and (as for) None has the right to be worshipped but Allah (LДҒ IlДҒha IllallДҒh)вҖҷ - there is no barrier to it from Allah until it reaches Him.вҖқ

A man from Banu Sulaim narrated: вҖңThe Messenger of Allah (п·ә) counted them out in my handвҖқ - or - вҖңin his hand: вҖҳAt-TasbД«бёҘ is half of the Scale, and вҖңAll praise is due to Allah (Al-бёӨamdulillДҒh)вҖқ fills it, and At-TakbД«r (AllДҒhu Akbar) fills what is between the sky and the earth, and fasting is half of patience, and purification is half of faith.вҖқ

Anas bin Malik said: вҖңWhenever a matter would distress him, the Prophet (п·ә) would say: вҖҳO Living, O Self-Sustaining Sustainer! In Your Mercy do I seek relief (YДҒ бёӨayyu yДҒ QayyЕ«m, bi-raбёҘmatika astaghД«th).вҖҷвҖқ And with this chain, that he said: вҖңThe Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖҳBe constant with: вҖңO Possessor of Majesty and Honor (YДҒ Dhal-JalДҒli wal-IkrДҒm).вҖҷвҖқ
Anas narrated that the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңBe constant with: вҖҳO Possessor of Majesty and Honor (YДҒ Dhal-JalДҒli wal-IkrДҒm).вҖҷвҖқ

Abu Umamah Al-Bahili said: вҖңI heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: вҖңWhoever goes to his bed while in a state of purity and remembering Allah, until slumber overtakes him, he shall not get up at any hour of the night and ask Allah for something from the good of the world and the Hereafter except that Allah shall grant it to him.вҖҷвҖқ

`Amr bin Murrah said: вҖңI heard Abu WaвҖҷil say: вҖҳ`Abdullah bin Mas`ud saidвҖҷ and I said to him: вҖҳDid you hear it from `Abdullah?вҖҷ He said: вҖҳYes.вҖҷ And he narrated it in Marfu` form that he said: вҖҳThere is none with more Ghirah than Allah, and due to this He prohibited the lewd sins, that which is apparent of them and that which is hidden. And there is none to whom praise is more beloved than Allah, and due to this, He praised Himself.вҖҷвҖқ

`Abdullah bin `Amr narrated from Abu Bakr As-Siddiq that he said: вҖңO Messenger of Allah, teach me a supplication that I may supplicate with in my Salat.вҖқ He (п·ә) said: вҖңSay: вҖҳO Allah, I have wronged myself much, and none forgives sins except You. So forgive me with forgiveness from You, and have mercy upon me, indeed, You are the Forgiving, the Merciful (AllДҒhumma innД« бә“alamtu nafsД« бә“ulman kathД«ran wa lДҒ yaghfirudh-dhunЕ«ba illДҒ anta faghfirlД« maghfiratan min `indika warбёҘamnД« innaka antal-GhafЕ«rur-RaбёҘД«m).вҖҷвҖқ

Al-Muttalib bin Abi Wada`ah said: вҖңAl-`Abbas came to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and it is as if he heard something, so the Prophet (п·ә) stood upon the Minbar and said: вҖҳWho am I?вҖҷ They said: вҖҳYou are the Messenger of Allah, upon you be peace.вҖҷ He said: вҖҳI am Muhammad bin `Abdullah bin `Abdul-Muttalib, indeed Allah created the creation, and He put me in the best group of them, then He made them into two groups, so He put me in the best group of them, the He made them into tribes, so He put me in the best of tribes, then He made them into houses, so He put me in the best of them in tribe and lineage.вҖҷвҖқ

Anas bin Malik narrated that: the Prophet (п·ә) passed by a tree with dry leaves, so he struck it with his staff, making the leaves fall. Then he said: вҖңIndeed, вҖҳall praise is due to Allah, (Al-бёӨamdulillДҒhh)вҖҷ вҖҳglory to Allah, (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒh)вҖҷ вҖҳnone has the right to be worshipped by Allah, (LДҒ IlДҒha IllallДҒh)вҖҷ and вҖҳAllah is the greatest (AllДҒhu Akbar)вҖҷ cause the sins to fall from the worshipper, just as the leaves of this tree fall.вҖқ
Ibn `Umar narrated that the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңIndeed Allah accepts the repentance of a slave as long as (his soul does not reach his throat).вҖқ
Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңAllah is more delighted with the repentance of one of you, than one of you is, when finding his lost animal.вҖқ

Abu Sirmah narrated from Abu Ayyub, that: when death reached him, he said: вҖңI have concealed something I heard from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) from you. I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: вҖҳIf you did not sin, Allah would create a creation that would sin, so He will forgive them.вҖҷвҖқ

Anas bin Malik narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңAllah, Blessed is He and Most High, said: вҖҳO son of Adam! Verily as long as you called upon Me and hoped in Me, I forgave you, despite whatever may have occurred from you, and I did not mind. O son of Adam! Were your sins to reach the clouds of the sky, then you sought forgiveness from Me, I would forgive you, and I would not mind. So son of Adam! If you came to me with sins nearly as great as the earth, and then you met Me not associating anything with Me, I would come to you with forgiveness nearly as great as it.вҖҷвҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңAllah created a hundred mercies, and He placed one mercy among his creation, they show mercy to one another by it, and there are ninety-nine mercies with Allah.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңIf the believer knew what is with Allah of punishment, none would hope for Paradise, and if the disbeliever knew what is with Allah of mercy, none would despair of (attaining) Paradise.вҖқ
Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңVerily, Allah when He created the creation, He wrote with His Hand concerning Himself, that: вҖҳMy mercy prevails over My wrath.вҖҷвҖқ

Anas said that: The Prophet (п·ә) entered the Masjid and there was a man who had performed Salat and was supplicating. He was saying in his supplication: вҖңO Allah, none has the right to be worshipped but Allah, You are the One Who gives blessings, Originator of the heavens and the earth, Possessor of glory and generosity (AllДҒhumma lДҒ ilДҒha illДҒ ant, al-MannДҒn, BadД«`us-samДҒwДҒti wal-arбёҚ, Dhal-JalДҒli wal IkrДҒm).вҖқ So the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңDo you know what he has supplicated Allah with? He has supplicated to Allah by His Greatest Name, the one which if He Is called upon by it, He responds, and when He is asked by it, He gives.вҖқ

Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңMay the man before whom I am mentioned - and he does not send Salat upon me - be humiliated. And may a man upon whom Ramadan enters and then passes, before he is forgiven, be humiliated. And may a man whose parents reached old age in his presence, and they were not a cause for his entrance to Paradise, be humiliated.вҖқ
`Ali bin Abi Talib narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңThe stingy person is the one before whom I am mentioned, and he does not send Salat upon me.вҖқ

`Abdullah bin [Abi] Awfa narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to say: вҖңO Allah, cool my heart with snow and hail and cold water. O Allah, cleanse my heart of sins as You cleanse a white garment of filth (AllДҒhumma barrid qalbД« bith-thalji wal-baradi wal-mДҒвҖҷil-bДҒrid. AllДҒhumma naqqi qalbД« min al-khaб№ӯДҒyДҒ kamДҒ naqqaitath-thawbal-abyaбёҚa min ad-dannas).вҖқ

Ibn `Umar narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhomsoever of you the door of supplication is opened for, the doors of mercy have been opened for him. And Allah is not asked for anything - meaning - more beloved to Him, than being asked for Al-`ДҖfiyah.вҖқ And the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңThe supplication benefits against that which strikes and that which does not strike, so hold fast, O worshippers of Allah, to supplication.вҖқ

That was narrated to us by Al-Qasim bin Dinar Al-Kufi (who said): вҖңIshaq bin Mansur narrated to us, from IsraвҖҷilвҖқ with this (Another chain) Bilal narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңHold fast to Qiyam al-Lail, for it is the practice of the righteous before you, and indeed Qiyam al-Lail is a means of nearness to Allah, a means of prevention from sin, an expiation for bad deeds, and a barrier for the body against disease.вҖқ
Abu Hurairah narrates, saying: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңThe lifespan of my nation is between sixty to seventy, and the least of them are those who surpass that.вҖқ
Aishah narrated, saying: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever supplicates against the one who wronged him has triumphed.вҖқ

Abu Ayyub Al-Ansari narrated that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңWhoever says ten times: вҖҳNone has the right to be worshipped by Allah, Alone, without partner, to Him belongs all that exists, and to Him belongs the praise, [He gives life and causes death,] and He has power over all things, (LДҒ ilДҒha illallДҒh, waбёҘdahu lДҒ sharД«ka lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-бёҘamdu, [yuбёҘyД« wa yumД«tu,] wa huwa `alДҒ kulli shaiвҖҷin qadД«r)вҖҷ it is for him equal to freeing four slaves among the offspring of Isma`il.вҖқ

Saffiyah narrated: вҖңThe Messenger of Allah (п·ә) entered upon me and before me were four thousand date pits, I was making Tasbih with them. He said: вҖҳYou have made Tasbih with these? Should I not teach you that which is more than what you have made Tasbih with?вҖҷ So I said: вҖҳIndeed, teach me.вҖҷ So he said: вҖҳSay: glory is to Allah according to the number of His creation (SubбёҘДҒn AllДҒhi `adada khalqihi).вҖҷвҖқ
Salman Al-Farsi narrated that the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңIndeed, Allah, is Hayy, Generous, when a man raises his hands to Him, He feels too shy to return them to him empty and rejected.вҖқ
Abu Hurairah narrated that: A man was supplicating with his two fingers so the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңMake it one, make it one.вҖқ

Mu`adh bin Rifa`ah narrated from his father, that he said: вҖңAbu Bakr stood upon the Minbar, then wept, and said: вҖҳThe Messenger of Allah (п·ә) stood upon the Minbar the first year (of Hijrah), then wept, and said: вҖңAsk Allah for pardon and Al-`ДҖfiyah, for verily, none has been given anything better than Al-`ДҖfiyah.вҖҷвҖқ
Abu Bakr narrated: That the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңHe who seeks forgiveness has not been persistent in sin, even if he does it seventy times in a day.вҖқ

`Umar bin Al-Khattab narrated: That the Prophet (п·ә) sent an expedition in the direction of Najd. They gained many spoils of war and returned quickly. A man among those who did not go out said: вҖңWe have not seen an expedition quicker in return or greater in spoils than this expedition.вҖқ So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: вҖңShould I not direct you to a group greater in spoils and quicker in return? A group who attended Salat As-Subh, then sat remembering Allah until the sun rose, for these are quicker in return and greater in spoils.вҖқ
Ibn `Umar narrated from `Umar: that he sought permission from the Prophet (п·ә) concerning `Umrah, so he said: вҖңO my little brother, include us in your supplication and do not forget us.вҖқ

Ali [may Allah be pleased with him] narrated: that a Mukatib came to him and said: вҖңIndeed I am not capable of my Kitabah so aid me.вҖқ He said: вҖңShould I not teach you words that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) taught me? If you had a debt upon you similar to the mountain of Sir, Allah would fulfill it for you. He said: вҖҳSay: O Allah, suffice me with Your lawful against Your prohibited, and make me independent of all those besides You (AllДҒhummakfinД« biбёҘalДҒlika `an бёҘarДҒmika, wa aghninД« bi faбёҚlika `amman siwДҒka).вҖҷвҖқ

Ali narrated, saying: вҖңWhenever the Prophet (п·ә) would visit an ill person, he would said:вҖҳ[O Allah,] make the harm go away, Lord of mankind, and heal him, You are the Healer, there is no healing except your healing, a healing that does not leave any sickness ([AllДҒhumma] adh-hibil-baвҖҷsa rabban-nДҒs, washfi antash-shДҒfi, lДҒ shifДҒвҖҷa illДҒ shifДҒвҖҷuka shifДҒвҖҷan lДҒ yughДҒdiru saqama).вҖҷвҖқ

Ali bin Abi Talib narrated: that the Prophet (п·ә) used to say in his Witr: вҖңO Allah, I seek refuge in your pardon from Your Punishment, and I seek refuge in You from You, I am not capable of extolling You as You have extolled Yourself (AllДҒhumma innД« a`Е«dhu bi-riбёҚДҒka min sakhaб№ӯika, wa a`Е«dhu bi-mu`ДҒfДҒtika min `uqЕ«batika, wa a`Е«dhu bika minka, lДҒ uбёҘsД« thanДҒвҖҷan `alaika, anta kamДҒ athnaita `alДҒ nafsik).вҖқ

Mus`ab bin Sa`d and `Amr bin Maimun narrated: вҖңSa`d used to teach his children these words just as the Muktib teaches children, he would say: вҖҳIndeed, the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to seek refuge by saying them at the end of (every) Salat: O Allah I seek refuge in You from cowardliness, I seek refuge in You from miserliness, I seek refuge in You from feeble old age, and I seek refuge in You from the trial of the world, and the punishment of the grave (AllДҒhumma innД« a`Е«dhu bika minal-jubn, wa a`Е«dhu bika minal-bukhl, wa a`Е«dhu bika min ardhalil-`umur, wa a`Е«dhu bika min fitnatid-dunyДҒ wa `adhДҒbil qabr).вҖҷвҖқ

Az-Zubair bin `Awwam narrated: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: вҖңThere is not a morning that the slave of Allah reached except that a caller calls out: вҖҳGlorify the King, the Free of Deficiencies. (SabbiбёҘul Malikal-QuddЕ«s)вҖҷвҖқ

Narrated Wathilah bin Al-Asqa': that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah has chosen Isma'il from the children of Ibrahim, and He chose Banu Kinanah from the children of Isma'il, and He chose the Quraish from Banu Kinanah, and He chose Banu Hashim from Quraish, and He chose me from Banu Hashim.

Narrated Wathilah bin Al-Asqa': that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed, Allah chose Kinanah from the children of Isma'il, and He chose Quraish from Kinanah, and He chose Hashim from Quraish, and He chose me from Banu Hashim.

Narrated Al-'Abbas bin 'Abdul-Muttalib: I said: 'O Messenger of Allah! Indeed the Quraish have sat and spoken between themselves about the best of them, and they made your likeness as that of a palm tree in a wasteland.' So the Prophet (п·ә) said: 'Indeed, Allah created the creation and made me [from the best of them,] from the best of their categories, and the best of the two categories (Arabs and Non-Arabs), then He chose between the tribes and made me from the best tribe, then He chose between the houses and made me from the best house. So I am the best of them in person and the best of them in house.'

Narrated Al-Muttalib bin Abi Wada'ah: Al-Abbas came to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), and it is as if he heard something, so the Prophet (п·ә) stood upon the Minbar and said: 'Who am I?' They said: 'You are the Messenger of Allah, upon you be peace.' He said: 'I am Muhammad bin 'Abdullah bin 'Abdul-Muttalib, indeed Allah created the creation, and He put me in the best [group] of them, then He made them into two groups, so He put me in the best group of them, then He made them into tribes, so He put me in the best of them in tribe, then He made them into houses, so He put me in the best of them in tribe and lineage.'
Narrated Abu Hurairah: They said: 'O Messenger of Allah (п·ә)! When was the Prophethood established for you?' He said: 'While Adam was between (being) soul and body.'

Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I am the first of the people to appear upon their being resurrected, and I am their spokesman whenever they gather, and I am the one that gives them glad tidings whenever they give up hope. And the Banner of Praise will be in my hand that day, and I am the most noble of the children of Adam with my Lord, and I am not boasting.

Narrated Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I am the first for whom the earth will be split, and then I will be adorned with garments from the garments of Paradise. Then I will stand at the right of the Throne. No one from the creation will in that place other than I.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Ask Allah to grant me Al-Wasilah. They said: O Messenger of Allah! And what is Al-Wasilah? He said: The highest level of Paradise. No one will attain it except for one man, and I hope that I am him.

Narrated At-Tufail bin Ubayy bin Ka'b: from his father that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The parable of me and the Prophets is that of a man who built a house, and he built it well, completing it, and beautifying it, but he left a space for one brick. So the people began going around the house amazed at it, saying If only the space for that brick was filled.' And I am, with regards to the Prophets, in the position of that brick. And with this chain, from the Prophet (п·ә) that he said: Upon the Day of Judgement I will be the leader of the Prophets and their spokesman, and the bearer of their intercession, without bragging.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin 'Amr: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: If you hear the Muadh-dhin then say as he says. Then send Salat upon me, because whoever sends Salat upon me, Allah will send Salat upon him ten times due to it. Then ask Allah that He gives me Al-Wasilah, because it is a place in Paradise which is not for anyone except for a slave from the slaves of Allah, and I hope that I am him. And whoever asks that I have Al-Wasilah, then (my) intercession will be made lawful for him.

Narrated Abu Sa'eed: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I am the master of the children of Adam on the Day of Judgement, and I am not boasting. The Banner of Praise will be in my hand, and I am not boasting. There will not be a Prophet on that day, not Adam nor anyone other than him, except that he will be under my banner. And I am the first one for whom the earth will be opened for, and I am not bragging.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Salam: The description of Muhammad is written in the Tawrah, [and the description that] 'Eisa will be buried next to him. (One of the narrators) Abu Mawdud said: [And] there is a place for a grave left in the house.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: On the day in which the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) entered Al-Madinah, everything in it was illuminated. Then, on the day in which he died, everything in it was dark. And we did not remove our hands from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), while we were burying him because our hearts felt so estranged.

Narrated Al-Muttalib bin 'Abdullah bin Qais bin Makhramah: from his father, from his grandfather, that he said: I and the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), were born in the Year of the Elephant - he said: And 'Uthman bin 'Affan asked Qubath bin Ashyam, the brother of Banu Ya'mar bin Laith - 'Are you greater (in age) or the Messenger of Allah (п·ә)?' He said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) is greater than me, but I have an earlier birthday. He said: And I saw the defecation of the birds turning green.

Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) received Revelation when he was forty years old. So he stayed in Makkah for thirteen years and in Al-Madinah for ten. And he died when he was sixty-three years old.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet (п·ә) died when he was sixty-five years old.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was not very tall nor was he [very] short, nor was he pale white, nor was he brown, nor was the wave of his hair completely curly nor straight. Allah sent him at the beginning of his forties and he stayed in Makkah for ten years, and in Al-Madinah for ten years. And Allah took him at the beginning of his sixties, and there were not more than twenty white hairs on his head or in his beard.

Narrated Simak bin Harb: from Jabir bin Samurah, that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed in Makkah there is a rock that used to give me Salam during the night of my advent, and I know it even now.

Narrated Abu Al-'Ala: from Samurah bin Jundab that he said: We were with the Prophet (п·ә) and we would take turns (eating) from a bowl from the morning till the evening. Ten would stand and ten would sit. We said: So what was filling it up?' He said: What are you amazed at? It wasn't filled up from anywhere but here, and he pointed with his hand towards the sky.

Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: I was with the Prophet (п·ә) in Makkah. We departed to one of its suburbs, and no mountain or tree was before him, except that it said: 'Peace be upon you O Messenger of Allah.'

Narrated Anas bin Malik: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to give Khutbah next to a tree, and then they made a Minbar for him, so he gave Khutbahs on it, so the tree whimpered like a camel. So the Prophet (п·ә) rubbed it, and it quieted.

Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: A Bedouin came to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and said: 'How shall I know that you are a Prophet?' He said: 'If I were to call this date cluster from this palm tree, would you bear witness that I am the Messenger of Allah?' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) called it and they started to fall from the tree, until they fell towards the Prophet (п·ә), then he said: 'Go back,' and it went back. So the Bedouin accepted Islam.

Narrated Abu Zaid bin Akhtab: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) wiped his hand over my face and supplicated for me. 'Azrah (one of the narrators) said: Indeed he lived for one-hundred and twenty years, and there weren't upon his head except for a few small grey hairs.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: I saw the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) at the time when the 'Asr prayer had drawn near, and the people were searching for water for Wudu, bu they did not find any. So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was brought some water for Wudu, and the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) put his hand in the container and ordered that the people make Wudu from it. He said: So I saw water springing out from under his fingers. The people performed Wudu until the last of them made Wudu.

Narrated 'Aishah: The first of what the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) initiated with of Prophethood, when Allah wanted to honor him and grant His mercy upon His creatures, was that he would not see anything (in a dream) except that it would occur like the break of dawn. So he continued upon that for as long as Allah willed for him to continue, and seclusion was made beloved to him, such that there was not anything more beloved to him than being alone.

Narrated 'Abdullah: You consider the signs to be punishment, whereas we used to think of them as a blessing during the time of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). We used to eat food with the Prophet (п·ә) and we would hear the food's Tasbih. He said: And the Prophet (п·ә) was brought a container, so he put his hand it in, and the water began to spring from between his fingers. So the Prophet (п·ә) said: 'Hasten to the blessed Wudu and the blessing from the heavens' until all of had performed Wudu.

Narrated 'Aishah: that Al-Harith bin Hisham asked the Prophet (п·ә): 'How does the Revelation come to you?' The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Sometimes it comes to me like the ringing of a bell and that is the hardest upon me, and sometimes the angel will appear to me like a man, and he will speak to me such that I understand what he says.' 'Aishah said: I saw the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) while the Revelation was descending upon him on an extremely cold day. Then it ceased and his forehead was flooded with sweat.

Narrated Al-Bara: I have not seen anyone with hair past his shoulders in a red Hullah more handsome than the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). He had hair that would flow on his shoulders, having broad shoulders, not too short and not too tall.
Narrated Abu Ishaq: A man asked Al-Bara: 'Was the face of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) like a sword?' He said: 'No, like the moon.'

Narrated 'Ali: The Prophet (п·ә) was not tall nor was he short, his hands and feet were thick, his head was large, he was big-boned, he had a long Masrubah (the line of hair from the chest to the navel), and whenever he walked, he leaned forward as if he was going down a decline. I have not seen anyone before him nor after him that resembled him (п·ә).
Narrated 'Urwah: that 'Aishah said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) did not speak quickly like you do now, rather he would speak so clearly, unmistakably, that those who sat with him would memorize it.
Narrated Anas bin Malik: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) would repeat a statement three times so that it could be understood.
Narrated Ibn Jaz: I have not seen anyone who smiled more than the Messenger of Allah (п·ә).
Narrated Ibn Jaz: The laughter of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was not but smiling.

Narrated As-Sa'ib bin Yazid: My maternal aunt took me to the Prophet (п·ә), and said: 'O Messenger of Allah! Indeed my nephew is in pain.' So he wiped over my head and supplicated for blessings for me. And he performed Wudu and I drank from the water of his Wudu. Then I stood behind his back, and I looked at the seal between his two shoulder blades, and it resembled the egg of a partridge.
Narrated Jabir bin Samurah: The seal of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) - meaning the one which was between his two shoulder blades - was fleshy and red, resembling the egg of a pigeon.

Narrated Jabir bin Samurah: The two shins of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) were thin, and he would not laugh except as a smile, and whenever I looked at him I would say: He put kohl on his eyes,' but he (п·ә) did not use kohl.
Narrated Jabir bin Samurah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) had a wide mouth (Dali' Al-Fam), his eyes were Ashkal, and he had thin heals (Manhus Al-'Aqib).

Narrated Jabir bin Samurah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) had a wide mouth (Dali' Al-Fam), his eyes were Ashkal, and he had thin heels (Manhus Al-'Aqib). Shu'bah (one of the narrators) said: I said to Simak: 'What is Dali' Al-Fam? ' He said: 'A wide mouth.' I said: 'What is Ashkal Al-'Ainain? ' He said: 'Having long eyes.' [He said:] I said: 'What is Manhus Al-'Aqib? ' He said: 'Little flesh.'

Narrated Abu Hurairah: I have not seen anything more beautiful than the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). It was as if the sun flowed upon his face. And I have not seen anyone quicker in his walking than the Messenger of Allah (п·ә). It was as if the earth was made easy for him. We would be exerting ourselves while he would not be struggling.

Narrated Jabir: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: The Prophets were presented to me, and Musa was a thin man, it was as if he was from the men of Shanu'ah. And I saw 'Eisa bin Mariam, and the closest of the people in resemblance to him. from those I have seen, is 'Urwah bin Mas'ud. And I saw Ibrahim, and the closest of the people in resemblance to him, from those I have seen, is your companion - meaning himself - And I saw Jibril, and the closest of the people in resemblance to him, from those I have seen, is Dihyah. [And he is Ibn Khalifah Al-Kalbi.]
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet (п·ә) died when he was sixty-five years old.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet (п·ә) died when he was sixty-five years old.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet (п·ә) stayed in Makkah for thirteen years - meaning while he was receiving Revelation - and he died when he was sixty-three years old.

Narrated Jabir [bin 'Abdullah]: that he heard Mu'awiyah bin Abi Sufyan giving a Khutbah, saying: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) died when he was sixty-three years old, and so did Abu Bakr and 'Umar, and I am sixty-three years old.
Narrated 'Aishah [may Allah be pleased with her]: The Prophet (п·ә) died when he was sixty-three years old.

Narrated 'Abdullah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I free myself of the friendship of every Khalil, and if I were to take a Khalil then I would have taken Ibn Abi Quhafah as a Khalil. And indeed your companion is Allah's Khalil.
Narrated 'Umar bin Al-Khattab: Abu Bakr is our chief, and the best of us, and the most beloved of us to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә).

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Shaqiq: I said to 'Aishah: 'Which of the Companions of the Prophet (п·ә) were the most beloved to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә)?' She said: 'Abu Bakr.' I said: 'Then who?' She said: 'Then Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah.' He said: I said: 'Then who?'' He said: Then she was silent.

Narrated Abu Sa'eed: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed the people of the higher levels, will be seen by those who are beneath them like the stars which appear far off in the sky. And indeed Abu Bakr and 'Umar are among them, and they have done well.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: There is no favor due upon us from anyone, except that we have repaid him, with the exception of Abu Bakr. Verily upon us, there is a favor due to him, which Allah will repay him on the Day of Judgement. No one's wealth has benefited as Abu Bakr's wealth has benefited me. And if I were to take a Khalil, then I would have taken Abu Bakr as a Khalil, and indeed your companion is Allah's Khalil.
Narrated Hudhaifah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Stick to the two after me, Abu Bakr and 'Umar.

Narrated Hudhaifah [may Allah be pleased with him]: We were sitting with the Prophet (п·ә) and he said: 'I do not know how long I will be with you, so stick to the two after me,' and he signaled towards Abu Bakr and 'Umar.

Narrated Anas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said about Abu Bakr and 'Umar: These two are the masters of the elder people among the inhabitants of Paradise. From the first ones and the last ones, not including the Prophets and the Messengers. But do not inform them O 'Ali.

Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: I was with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), and Abu Bakr and 'Umar came up (in discussion), so the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'These two are the masters of the elder people among the inhabitants of Paradise. From the first ones and the last ones, not including the Prophets and the Messengers. But do not inform them O 'Ali.'

Narrated 'Ali: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Abu Bakr and 'Umar are the masters of the elder people among the inhabitants of Paradise, from the first ones and the last ones, not including the Prophets and the Messengers. Do not inform them O 'Ali.

Narrated Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri: that Abu Bakr said: Am I not the most deserving of it among the people, am I not the first to become Muslim, am I not the person of such and such, am I not the person of such and such.

Narrated Anas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to go out to his Companions from The Muhajirin and the Ansar while they were sitting, and Abu Bakr and 'Umar would be with them. No one would lift their sight towards him except Abu Bakr and 'Umar, because they used to look at him, and he would look at them, and they would smile at him, and he would smile at them.

Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) departed one day and entered the Masjid, along with Abu Bakr and 'Umar. One was on his right and the other was on his left, and he was holding their hands, and he said: This is how we will be resurrected on the Day of Judgement.'
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to Abu Bakr: You are my companion at the Hawd, and my companion in the cave.
Narrated 'Abdullah bin Hantab: that the Prophet (п·ә) saw Abu Bakr and 'Umar and said: These two are the hearing and the seeing.
Narrated 'Aishah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: It is not befitting that a group, among whom is Abu Bakr, be led by other than him.

Narrated Zaid bin Aslam: I heard 'Umar bin Al-Khattab saying: 'We were ordered by the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) to give in charity, and that coincided with a time in which I had some wealth, so I said, Today I will beat Abu Bakr, if ever I beat him. ' So I came with half of my wealth, and the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: What did you leave for your family? I said: The like of it. And Abu Bakr came with everything he had, so he said: O Abu Bakr! What did you leave for your family? He said: I left Allah and His Messenger for them. I said: '[By Allah] I will never be able to beat him to something.'

Narrated Jubair bin Mut'im: that a woman came to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) to speak to him about something. Then he ordered her with something, and she said: What should I do O Messenger of Allah if I do not find you? He said: If you do not find me, then go to Abu Bakr.

Narrated Abu Salamah bin 'Abdur-Rahman: from Abu Hurairah, who said that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: While a man was riding a cow it said: I was not created for this, I was only created to till.' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I believe in that, myself, and Abu Bakr, 'Umar. Abu Salamah said: And the two of them were not among the people that day [and Allah knows best].
Narrated 'Aishah: that the Prophet (п·ә) ordered the closing of all the gates, except for the gate of Abu Bakr. And there is a nation on this topic from Abu Sa'eed.
Narrated 'Aishah: that Abu Bakr entered upon the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), so he said: You are Allah's 'Atiq from the Fire. From that day on he was called 'Atiq.

Narrated Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: There is no Prophet except that he has two ministers among the inhabitants of the heavens, and two ministers among the inhabitants of the earth. As for my two ministers from the inhabitants of the heavens, then they are Jibril and Mika'il, and as for my two ministers from the inhabitants of the earth, then they are Abu Bakr and 'Umar.

Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: O Allah! Honor Islam through the most dear of these two men to you: Through Abu Jahl or through 'Umar bin Al-Khattab. He said: And the most dear of them to Him was 'Umar.

Narrated Nafi': from Ibn 'Umar, that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah has put the truth upon the tongue and in the heard of 'Umar. He said: And Ibn 'Umar said: 'No affair occurred among the people, except that they said something about it, and 'Umar said something about it' or he said - Ibn Al-Khattab - Kharijah (one of the narrators) had a doubt about it - except that the Qur'an was revealed in line with what 'Umar had said.

Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: O Allah honor Islam through Abu Jahl bin Hisham or through 'Umar bin Al-Khattab. He said: So it happened that 'Umar came the next day to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and accepted Islam.

Narrated Jabir bin 'Abdullah: that 'Umar said to Abu Bakr: O best of people after the Messenger of Allah (п·ә)! So Abu Bakr said: If you say that, then I have heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) say: 'The sun has not risen upon a man better than 'Umar.'
Narrated Muhammad bin Sirin: I don't think that a man who degrades Abu Bakr and 'Umar loves the Prophet (п·ә).
Narrated 'Uqbah bin 'Amir: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: If there was to have a Prophet after me, it would have been 'Umar bin Al-Khattab.

Narrated Ibn 'Umar: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'I saw that I was brought a cup of milk, so I drank from it, and I gave my leftover to 'Umar bin Al-Khattab.' They said: 'So what did you interpret it as O Messenger of Allah?' He said: '(It is) Knowledge.'

Narrated Anas: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: I entered Paradise and it was as if I was in a palace of gold. So I said: 'Whose palace is this?' They said: 'A youth's, from the Quraish.' So I thought that I was him. I said: 'And who is he?' They said: 'Umar bin Al-Khattab.

Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I am the first for whom the earth will split, then Abu Bakr, then 'Umar. Then the people of Al-Baqi; they will be gathered with me. Then I will await the people of Makkah until they are resurrected between the Two Sacred areas.

Narrated 'Aishah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Among the nations, there used to be Muhaddathun (those who were given understanding), and if there were one in my nation, it would be 'Umar bin Al-Khattab.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: A man among the inhabitants of Paradise will appear before you. So Abu Bakr appeared. Then he said: A man among the inhabitants of Paradise will appear before you. So 'Umar appeared.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: While a man was tending some of his sheep, a wolf came and took a sheep. So its owner came and retrieved it. The wolf said: 'What will you do for it on the Day of the Predator, the day when there will be no shepherd for it other than me?' The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: So I believe in that, I and Abu Bakr, and 'Umar. (One of the narrators) Abu Salamah said: And the two of them were (present) not among the people that day.

Narrated Abu Hurairah [may Allah be pleased with him]: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was at Hira, him, Abu Bakr, 'Umar, 'Uthman, 'Ali, Talhah and Az-Zubair (may Allah be pleased with them all), and the boulder (mount Hira) shook. So the Prophet (п·ә) said: Be calm, for there is none upon you except a Prophet, or a Siddiq, or a martyr.

Narrated Anas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), Abu Bakr, 'Umar, and 'Uthman climbed Uhud (mountain) and it shook them, so the Prophet of Allah (п·ә) said: Be firm O Uhud! For there is none upon you except a Prophet, a Siddiq, and two martyrs.
Narrated Talhah bin 'Ubaidullah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: For every Prophet there is a friend (Rafiq), and my friend - meaning in Paradise - is 'Uthman.

Narrated 'Abdur-Rahman bin Samurah: that 'Uthman went to the Prophet (п·ә) with one-thousand Dinar - Al-Hasan bin Waqi (one of the narrators) said: And in another place in my book: 'In his garment when the 'army of distress' was being prepared. So he poured them into his lap.' - 'Abdur-Rahman said: So I saw the Prophet (п·ә) turning them over in his lap, saying: 'Whatever 'Uthman does after today will not harm him,' two times.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: that when the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) ordered the pledge of Ridwan, 'Uthman bin 'Affan was the messenger of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) to the people of Makkah. He said: So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Indeed 'Uthman is busy with the affair of Allah and the affair of His Messenger' then he (п·ә) put one of his hands on the other. The hand of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) on behalf of 'Uthman, was better than their own hands for themselves.

Narrated Abu Al-Ash'ath As-Sana'ani: Some people were delivering Khutbah in Ash-Sham, and among them were Companions of the Prophet (п·ә). So the last of them, a man called Murrah bin Ka'b, stood, and he said: 'If it were not for a Hadith I heard from the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), I would not have stood (to address you). He (п·ә) mentioned the tribulations, and that they would be coming soon. Then a man who was concealed by a garment passed by. So he said: This one will be upon guidance that day. So I went towards him, and it was 'Uthman bin 'Affan. I turned, facing him, and I said: This one? He said: Yes.
Narrated 'Aishah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: O 'Uthman! Indeed Allah may give you a shirt, and if they wish that you take it off, do not take it off for them.
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: While the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) alive, we used to say: 'Abu Bakr, and (then) 'Umar, and (then) 'Uthman.'
Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) mentioned the Fitnah and said: This one will be wrongfully killed during it, about 'Uthman bin 'Affan [may Allah be pleased with him].

Narrated Jabir: that the Prophet (п·ә) was brought the body of a deceased man, to perform Salat for him, but he did not pray over him. It was said: O Messenger of Allah! We have not seen you avoiding prayer over anyone before this? He said: He used to hate 'Uthman, so Allah hates him.
Narrated Abu Sahlah: The day of the house (when he was besieged), 'Uthman said: 'Indeed the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) took a covenant from me, and I will abide by it.'
Narrated Abu Sarihah, or Zaid bin Arqam - Shu'bah had doubt: from the Prophet (п·ә): For whomever I am his Mawla then 'Ali is his Mawla.

Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: May Allah have mercy upon Abu Bakr, he married me to his daughter, and he carried me to the land of Hijrah, and he freed Bilal with his wealth. May Allah have mercy upon 'Umar, he says the truth even if it is sour. The truth caused him to be left without a friend. May Allah have mercy upon 'Uthman, the angels are shy of him. May Allah have mercy upon 'Ali. O Allah! Place the truth with him wherever he turns.
Narrated Al-Bara bin 'Azib: that the Prophet (п·ә) said to 'Ali bin Abi Talib: You are from me, and I am from you. And there is a story along with this Hadith.
Narrated Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri: We, the people of the Ansar, used to recognize the hypocrites, by their hatred for 'Ali bin Abi Talib.

Narrated Buraidah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed Allah has ordered me to love four, and He informed me that He loves them. It was said: O Messenger of Allah! Name them for us. He said: 'Ali is among them, saying that three times, And Abu Dharr, Al-Miqdad, and Salman. And He ordered me to love them, and He informed me that He loves them.
Narrated Hubshi bin Junadah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Ali is from me and I am from 'Ali. And none should represent me except myself or 'Ali.

Narrated Ibn 'Umar: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) made bonds of brotherhood among his Companions. So 'Ali came crying saying: O Messenger of Allah! You have made a bond of brotherhood among your Companions, but you have not made a bond of brotherhood with me and anyone. So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to him: I am your brother, in this life and the next.
Narrated Anas bin Malik: There was a bird with the Prophet (п·ә), so he said: 'O Allah, send to me the most beloved of Your creatures to eat this bird with me.' So 'Ali came and ate with him.
Narrated 'Ali: When I would ask the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), he would give me, and when I would be silent, he would initiate (speech or giving) with me.
Narrated 'Ali: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I am the house of wisdom, and 'Ali is its door.

Narrated Jabir: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) called 'Ali on the Day (of the battle) of At-Ta'if, and spoke privately with him, so the people said: 'His private conversation with his cousin has grown lengthy.' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'I did not speak privately with him, rather Allah spoke privately with him.'
Narrated Abu Sa'eed: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to 'Ali: O 'Ali! It is not permissible for anyone to be Junub in this Masjid except for you and I.
Narrated Anas bin Malik: The advent of the Prophet (п·ә) was on Monday and 'Ali performed Salat on Tuesday.
Narrated Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas: that the Prophet (п·ә) said to 'Ali, You are to me, in the position that Harun was to Musa, [except that there is no Prophet after me].
Narrated Jabir bin 'Abdullah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said to 'Ali: You are to me in the position that Harun was to Musa, except that there is no Prophet after me.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Prophet (п·ә) ordered that the gates be closed, except the gate of 'Ali.

Narrated 'Ali bin Husain: from his father, from his grandfather, 'Ali bin Abi Talib: The Prophet (п·ә) took Hasan and Husain by the hand and said: 'Whoever loves me and loves these two, and their father and mother, he shall be with me in my level on the Day of Judgement.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: Ibn 'Abbas said: The first to perform Salat was 'Ali.

Narrated A man from the Ansar: that Zaid bin Al-Arqam said: The first to accept Islam was 'Ali. 'Amr bin Murrah said: So I mentioned that to Ibrahim An-Nakha'i, so he rejected that and said: 'The first to accept Islam was Abu Bakr As-Siddiq.'

Narrated 'Ali: The Prophet (п·ә) - the Unlettered Prophet - exhorted me (saying): 'None loves you except a believer and none hates you except a hypocrite.' 'Adi bin Thabit (a narrator) said: I am from the generation whom the Prophet (п·ә) supplicated for.

Narrated Umm 'Atiyyah: The Prophet (п·ә) sent an army in which was 'Ali. She said: While he was raising his hands, I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: 'O Allah! Do not cause me to die until You allow me to see 'Ali.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Az-Zubair: that Az-Zubair said: On the Day of (the battle of) Uhud, the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) wore two coats of mail. He tried to get up on a boulder, but was not able to, so Talhah squatted under him, lifting the Prophet (п·ә) upon it, such that he could sit on the boulder. So he said: 'It (Paradise) is obligatory for Talhah.'
Narrated Jabir bin 'Abdullah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Whoever would be pleased to look at a martyr walking upon the face of the earth, then let him look at Talhah bin 'Ubaidullah.
Narrated 'Ali bin abi Talib: My ear heard from the mouth of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), while he was saying: 'Talhah and Az-Zubair are my neighbors in Paradise.'

Narrated Musa bin Talhah: I entered upon Mu'awiyah and he said: 'Shall I not give you some good news?' I said: 'Of course!' He said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: 'Talhah is among those fulfilled their vow (referring to 33:23).'

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Az-Zubair: from Az-Zubair, who said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) gathered together his parents for me the Day of Quraizah, (i.e. the battle of Ahzab) and said: 'May my mother and father be ransomed for you.'
Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib [may Allah be pleased with him]: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed, every Prophet has a Hawari, and my Hawari is Az-Zubair bin Al-'Awwam.

Narrated Jabir [may Allah be pleased with him]: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Indeed, every Prophet has a Hawari and, [indeed], my Hawari is Az-Zubair [bin Al-'Awwam]. And Abu Na'im added in it: On the Day of Al-Ahzab, he (п·ә) said: 'Who will bring us news about their party?' Az-Zubair said: 'I will.' He said it three times. Az-Zubair said (each time): 'I will.'

Narrated Hisham bin 'Urwah: On the Day of (the battle of) Al-Jamal, Az-Zubair echorted his son 'Abdullah, saying: 'There is not a part of me except that it has been injured while with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә),' until that ended with his private parts.

Narrated 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Awf: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Abu Bakr is in Paradise, 'Umar is in Paradise, 'Uthman is in Paradise, 'Ali is in Paradise, Talhah is in Paradise, Az-Zubair is in Paradise, 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Awf is in Paradise, Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas is in Paradise, and Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah is in Paradise.

Narrated 'Abdur-Rahman bin Humaid: from his father, that Sa'eed bin Zaid reported to him, while in a group of people, that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Ten are in Paradise: Abu Bakr is in Paradise, 'Umar is in Paradise, 'Uthman is in Paradise, Az-Zubair and Talhah, 'Abdur-Rahman, Abu 'Ubaidah and Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas - He said: So he counted these nine and was silent concerning the tenth - so the people said: 'We implore you by Allah, O Abu Al-A'war, who is the tenth?' He said: 'You have implored me by Allah. Abu Al-A'war is in Paradise.'

Narrated Abu Salamah: from 'Aishah that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to say: Indeed your [feminine plural: referring to the wives of the Prophet (п·ә)] affair is from that which concerns me after me, and none shall be able to be patient concerning you except the patient ones. He said: Then 'Aishah said: 'So may Allah give your father drink from the Salsabil of Paradise intending 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Awf (Abu Salamah is the son of 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Awf). And he had maintained ties with the wives of the Prophet (п·ә) with property that had been sold for forty-thousand.
Narrated Abu Salamah: that 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Awf left a garden for the Mothers of the Believers that was sold for for-hundred thousand.
Narrated Sa'd: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: O Allah, respond to Sa'd when he supplicates to You.
Narrated Jabir bin 'Abdullah: Sa'd came, so the Prophet (п·ә) said: This is my maternal uncle, so let a man show me his maternal uncle.

Narrated 'Ali: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) did not mention both (his) parents for anyone except Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas. On the Day of (the battle of) Uhud he said: 'Shoot, may my father and mother be ransomed for you.' And he said to him: 'Shoot O young man.'
Narrated Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) mentioned both of his parents for me on the Day of Uhud.

Narrated 'Ali bin Abi Talib: I never heard the Prophet (п·ә) mentioning both of his parents being ransomed for anyone except for Sa'd. On the Day of Uhud, I heard him saying: 'Shoot, Sa'd, may my father and mother be ransomed for you.

Narrated 'Aishah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) did not sleep one night upon arriving in Al-Madinah. So he said: 'If only a righteous man would guard me tonight.' She said: So we were like that, when we heard the clanging of weapons. He said: 'Who is this?' So he said: 'Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas.' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'What has brought you?' Sa'd said: 'Fear for the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) came upon me, so I came to protect him.' So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) supplicated for him, then slept.

Narrated 'Abdullah bin Zalim Al-Mazini: that Sa'eed bin Zaid bin 'Amr bin Nufail said: I bear witness for nine people, that they are in Paradise, and if I were to bear witness for a tenth, I would not be sinful. It was said: How is that? He said: We were with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) at (mount) Hira when he said, 'Be firm, Hira! There is not upon you any but a Prophet, or a Siddiq, or a martyr. It was said: And who were they? He said: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә), Abu Bakr, 'Umar, 'Uthman, 'Ali, Talhah, Az-Zubair, Sa'd, and 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Awf. It was said: And who is the tenth? He said: Me.
Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Al-'Abbas is from me and I am from him.
Narrated 'Ali: that concerning Al-'Abbas, the Prophet (п·ә) said to 'Umar: Indeed, the uncle of a man is the Sinw of his father. And 'Umar had spoken to him concerning his charity.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Prophet (п·ә) said: Al-'Abbas is the uncle of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә), and indeed, the uncle of a man is the Sinw of his father or from the Sinw of his father.

Narrated Ibn 'Abbas: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to Al-'Abbas: 'On the night of Monday, come to me, you and your offspring, so that I may supplicate for them with a supplication that Allah will benefit you and your children by.' So he went, and we went with at night, so he (п·ә) covered us in a Kisah (shawl), then said: 'O Allah, forgive Al-'Abbas and his offspring, for what is open and what is secret, with a forgiveness that does not leave any sins. O Allah! Take care of him concerning the affair of his offspring.'
Narrated Abu Hurairah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: I saw Ja'far flying in Paradise with the angels.
Narrated Abu Hurairah: None has put on sandals - nor worn them, nor ridden a mount, nor a Kur, after the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) - better than Ja'far [bin Abi Talib].
Narrated Al-Bara bin 'Azib: that the Prophet (п·ә) said to Ja'far bin Abi Talib: You share similarity with me in appearance and in character.

Narrated Abu Hurairah: I used to ask a man from among the Companions of the Prophet (п·ә) concerning Ayat of the Qur'an which I would be more knowledgeable about than him, so that he might inform me something (more about them). So when I would ask Ja'far bin Abi Talib, he would not answer me until he would go with me to his place and say to his wife: 'O Asma, give us some food.' Once she had given us some food, he would answer me. And Ja'far used to love the poor and sit with them, and speak with them, and they would speak with him, so the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to call him Abu Al-Masakin (the Father of the Poor).

Narrated Abu Hurairah: We used to call Ja'far bin Abi Talib the Father of the Poor, so when we used to come to him, he would draw us close to him as long as he was present. One day we came to him, and he did not find anything with him, so he brought a jar of honey and broke it, so we began to lick out of it.
Narrated Abu Sa'eed: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: Al-Hasan and Al-Husain are the chiefs of the youth of Paradise.

Narrated Usamah bin Zaid: I came to the Prophet (п·ә) one night concerning some need, so the Prophet (п·ә) came out while he was covering up something, and I did not know what it was. Once I had tended to my need, I said: 'What is this that you were covering up?' So he uncovered it, and I found it was Hasan and Husain [peace be upon them] upon his hips. So he said: 'These are my two sons, and the sons of my daughter. O Allah! Indeed, I love them, so love them, and love those who love them.'

Narrated 'Abdur-Rahman bin Abu Nu'm: that a man from the people of Al-'Iraq asked Ibn 'Umar about the blood of a gnat that gets on the clothes. Ibn 'Umar said Look at this one, he asks about the blood of a gnat while they killed the son of the Messenger of Allah (п·ә)! And I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: 'Indeed Al-Hasan and Al-Husain - they are my two sweet basils in the world.'

Narrated Salma: I entered upon Umm Salamah while she was crying, so I said: 'What causes you to cry?' She said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah - that is, in a dream - and there was was dirt on his head and his beard. so I said: What is wrong with you, O Messenger of Allah? He said: 'I just witnessed the killing of Al-Husain.'

Narrated Anas bin Malik: That the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was asked: Which of the people of your house are most beloved to you? He said: Al-Hasan and Al-Husain. And he used to say to Fatimah: Call my two sons for me. And he would smell them and hug them.
Narrated Abu Bakrah: that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) ascended the Minbar and said: Indeed, this son of mine is a chief, Allah shall bring peace between two [tremendous] parties through his hands.

Narrated Buraidah: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was delivering a Khutbah to us when Al-Hasan and Al-Husain [peace be upon them] came, wearing red shirts, walking and falling down. So the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) descended from the Minbar and carried them, and placed them in front of him. Then he said: 'Allah spoke the Truth: Indeed, your wealth and your children are a trial (64:15). I looked at these two children walking and falling down, and I could not bear patiently anymore until I interrupted my talk and picked them up.
