text: A man asked the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… whether one who was fasting could embrace (his wife) and he gave him permission; but when another man came to him, and asked him, he forbade him. The one to whom he gave permission was an old man and the one whom he forbade was a youth.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… would be overtaken by the dawn when he was in a state of sexual defilement. The narrator Abdullah al-Adhrami said in his version: During Ramadan, due to sexual intercourse and no owing to a dream (i. e. nocturnal emission), and would fast. Abu Dawud said: How brief is this sentence uttered by the narrator, this is, he was overtaken by daw when he was in the state of sexual defilement ? The tradition says: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… was overtaken by dawn in the state of sexual defilement when he was fasting.
narrator: Narrated Aishah and Umm Salamah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ , wives of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… :

text: A man said to Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…: Messenger of Allah, I was overtaken by dawn while I was sexually defiled, and I want to keep fast. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: I am also overtaken by dawn while I am in the state of sexual defilement ; I also want to keep fast. I take a bath and I keep fast. The man said: Messenger of Allah, you are not like us ; Allah has forgiven you your past and future sins. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… became angry and said: I swear by Allah, I hope I shall be the most fearful of you of Allah, and most familiar of you with what I follow.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , wife of Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…:

text: This was a special concession for him. If a man commits this act today, the expiation is necessary for him. Abu Dawud said: Al-Laith bin Saad, al-Awzai, Mansur bin al-Mu'tamir and 'Irak bin Malik have narrated this tradition like the one narrated by Ibn Uyainah. Al-Awzai narrated in his version the words: Beg pardon of Allah.
narrator: This tradition has also been transmitted by al-Zuhri through a different chain of narrators to the same effect. Al-Zuhri added in his version:

text: A man came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. He broke his fast during Ramadan. He then narrated the rest of this tradition adding: Then a huge basket containing fifteen sa's of dates was brought to him. He said: Eat it yourself and your family and keep one fast and beg pardon of Allah.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: A huge basket containing twenty sa's (of dates) was brought.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ through a different chain of narrators. This version adds:

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone breaks his fast one day in Ramadan without a concession granted to him by Allah, a perpetual fast will not atone for it.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through a different chain of narrators similar to the tradition narrated by Ibn Kathir and Sulaiman. Abu Dawud said: Sufyan and Shubah differed among themselves on the name of the narrator Ibn al-Mutawwas and Abu al-Mutawwas.

A man came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I ate and drank in forgetfulness when I was fasting. He said: Allah had fed you and given you drink.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: If I had some part of the fast of Ramadan to make up, I would not be able to atone for it except in Sha'ban.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: If anyone dies when some fast is due from him (i. e. which he could not keep) his heir must fast on his behalf. Abu Dawud said: This applies to the fast which a man vows; and this is the opinion of Ahmad bin Hanbal.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: If a man falls ill during Ramadan and he dies, while he could not keep the fast, food will be provided (for the poor men) on his behalf; there is no atonement (for his fasts) due from him. If there is some vow which he could not fulfill, his heir must atone on his behalf.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Hamzat al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…: Messenger of Allah, I am a man who keeps perpetual fast, may I fast while on a journey? He replied: Fast if you like, or break your fast if you like.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: I said: 'O Messenger of Allah. I am a master of mounts and I use them! I myself travel on them and I rent them. This month, that is, Ramadan, happened to come to me (while I am on a journey), and I find myself strong enough (to fast) as I am young, and I find that it is easier for me to fast than to postpone it, and i becomes debt due from me. Does it bring me more reward, Messenger of Allah, if I fast, or if I break ? He replied: Whichever you like, Hamzah.
narrator: Narrated Hamzat al-Aslami ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… left Madina for Makkah till he reached 'Usfan, He then called for a vessel (of water). It was raised to his mouth to show it to the people, and that was in Ramadan. Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ used to say: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… fasted and he broke his fast. He who likes may fast and he who likes may break.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas:

text: We travelled along with the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… during Ramadan. Some of us were fasting and other broke their fast. Those who fasted did not find fault with those who broke, and those who broke their fast did not find fault with those who fasted.
narrator: Narrated Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… saw a man who had been put in the shade and saw a crowd of people around him (in the course of a journey). He said: Fasting while on journey is not part of righteousness.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: We went out along with the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… for some battle in intense heat, so much so that one of us placed his hand on his head, or placed his palm on his head, due to intense heat, No one of us fasted except the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and Abdullah bin Rawahah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ .
narrator: Narrated Abu al-Darda ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone has a riding beast which carries him to where he can get sufficient food, he should keep the fast of Ramadan wherever he is when it comes.
narrator: Narrated Salamah Ibn al-Muhabbaq al-Hudhali:

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: If anyone is on a journey and Ramadan comes. . . He then narrated the rest of the tradition to the same effect.
narrator: Narrated Salamah bin al-Muhabbaq ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I accompanied Abu Busrah al-Ghifari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ , a Companion of the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…, in a boat proceeding from al-Fustat (Cairo) during Ramadan. He was lifted (to the boat), then his meal was brought to him. The narrator Jafar said in his version: He did not go beyond the houses (of the city) but he called for the dining sheet. He said (to me): Come near. I said: Do you not see the houses? Abu Busrah said: Do you detest the sunnah (practice) of the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…? The narrator Jafar said in his version: He then ate (it).
narrator: Narrated Jafar Ibn Jubayr said:

text: Dihyah Ibn Khalifah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ once went out from a village of Damascus at as much distance as it measures between Aqabah and al-Fustat during Ramadan; and that is three miles. He then broke his fast and the people broke their fast along with him. But some of them disliked to break their fast. When he came back to his village, he said: I swear by Allah, today I witnessed a thing of which I could not even think to see. The people detested the way of the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and his Companions. He said this to those who fasted. At this moment he said: O Allah, make me die.
narrator: Narrated Mansur al-Kalbi said:

text: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ used to go out to al-Ghabah (jungle), but he neither broke his fast, nor shortened his prayer.
narrator: Nafi said:

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: One of you should not say: I fasted the whole of Ramadan, and I prayed during the night in the whole of Ramadan. I do not know whether he disliked the purification; or he (the narrator) said: He must have slept a little and taken rest.
narrator: Narrated Abu Bakrah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I attended the Eid (prayer) along with Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . He offered prayer before the sermon. He then said: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… prohibited fasting on these two days. As regards Id al-Adha, you eat the meat of your sacrificial animals. As for Eid al-Fitr, you break (i.e. end) your fast.
narrator: Narrated Abu Ubaid:

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… forbade fasting on two days, al-Fitr (breaking the fast of Ramadan) and al-Adha (the day of sacrifice), and wearing a tight single garment the raising of which discloses private parts, and sitting with one's legs drawn up and wrapped in one's garment, and forbade praying at two hours, after the Fajr prayer and after the Asr prayer.
narrator: Narrated Abu Saeed Al Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: He entered along with Abdullah bin Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ upon his father Amr bin 'As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and he brought food for him. He said: Eat. He said: I am fasting. Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: Eat, these are the days on which the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to command us to break fast, and forbid us to keep fast. The narrator Malik said: These are the day of al-tashriq (i.e. 11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhu al-Hijjah).
narrator: It was reported from Malik, from Yazid bin Al-had, from Abu Murrah, the client of Umm Hani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: The day of Arafah, the day of sacrifice, the days of tashriq are (the days of) our festival, O people of Islam. These are the days of eating and drinking.
narrator: Narrated Uqbah Ibn Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: None of you must fast on Friday unless he fasts the day before or the day after.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Do not fast on Saturday except what has been made obligatory on you; and if one of you can get nothing but a grape skin or a piece of wood from a tree, he should chew it.
narrator: Narrated As-Samma ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ sister of Abdullah Ibn Busr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… entered upon her on Friday while she was fasting. He asked: Did you fast yesterday ? She said: No. He again asked: Do you intend to fast tomorrow ? She said: No. He said: So break your fast.
narrator: Narrated Juwairiyah, daughter of al-Harith ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Fasting on Saturday had been prohibited, he would say: This is a Himsi tradition.
narrator: Narrated Al-Laith: When it was mentioned to Ibn Shihab (al-Zuhri):

text: I always concealed it, but I found that it became known widely, that is, the tradition on Ibn Busr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ about fasting on Saturday. Abu Dawud said: Malik said: This is a false (tradition).
narrator: Al-Auzai said:

text: He said: Messenger of Allah, tell me about keeping fast on Monday and Thursday. He said: On it I was born, and on it the Quran was first revealed to me.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Abu Qatadah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through a different chain of narrators. This version add:

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: The most excellent fast after Ramadan is Allah's month al-Muharram, and the most excellent prayer after the prescribed prayer is the prayer during night.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I asked Saeed bin Jubair about fasting during Rajab. He said: Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ told me that the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to fast to such an extent that we thought that he would never break his fast ; and he would go without fasting to such and extent that he would never fast.
narrator: Narrated Uthman bin Hakim:

text: The month most liked by the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… for fasting was Sha'ban. He then joined it with Ramadan.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, Ummul Muminin:

text: I asked or someone asked the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… about perpetual fasting. He replied: You have a duty to your family. Fast during Ramadan and the following month, and every Wednesday and Thursday. You will then have observed a perpetual fast.
narrator: Narrated Muslim al-Qurashi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: If anyone fasts during Ramadan, then follows it with six days in Shawwal, it will be like a perpetual fast.
narrator: Narrated Abu Ayyub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to fast to such an extent that we thought that he would never break his fast, and he would go without fasting to such an extent that we thought he would never fast. I never saw the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… fast a complete month except in Ramadan, and I never saw his fast more in any month than in Sha'ban.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, wife of Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… :

text: He would fast all but a little of Sha'ban, rather he used to fast the whole of Sha'ban.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through a different chain of narrators to the same effect. This version adds:

text: He went along with Usamah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ to Wadi al-Qura in pursuit of his camels. He would fast on Monday and Thursday. His client said to him: Why do you fast on Monday and Thursday, while you are an old man? He said: The Prophet of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to fast on Monday and Thursday. When he was asked about it, he said: The works of the servants (of Allah) are presented (to Allah) on Monday and Thursday.
narrator: Narrated Usamah ibn Zayd The client of Usamah Ibn Zayd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:

text: On the authority of one of the wives of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… who said: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to fast the first nine days of Dhul-Hijjah, Ashura' and three days of every month, that is, the first Monday (of the month) and Thursday.
narrator: Narrated Hunaydah Ibn Khalid narrated from his wife

text: One of the wives of the Prophet: Hunaydah Ibn Khalid narrated from his wife on the authority of one of the wives of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… who said: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to fast the first nine days of Dhul-Hijjah, Ashura' and three days of every month, that is, the first Monday (of the month) and Thursday.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: There is no virtue more to the liking of Allah in any day than in these days, that is, the first ten days of Dhu al-Hijjah. They (the Companions) asked: Messenger of Allah, not even the struggle in the path of Allah (Jihad) ? He said: (Yes), not even the struggle in the path of Allah, except a man who goes out (in the path of Allah) with his life and property, and does not return with any of them.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I never saw the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… prohibited fasting during the first ten days of Dhu al-Hijjah.
narrator: Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: On the day of 'Arafah some people near her argued whether the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was fasting, some saying that he was, and others saying that he was not. I, therefore, sent him a cup of milk while he was observing the halt at 'Arafah on his camel, and he drank it.
narrator: Umm al-Fadl ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, daughter of al-Harith, said:

text: The Quraish used to fast on the day of 'Ashurah in pre Islamic days. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) would fast on it in pre-Islamic period. When the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) came to Medina, he fasted on it and commanded to fast on it. When the fast of Ramadan was prescribed, that became obligatory, and (fasting on) 'Ashurah was abandoned. He who wishes may fast on it and he who wishes may leave it.
narrator: 'Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: 'Ashurah was a day on which we used to fast in pre-Islamic days. When (fasting of) Ramadan was prescribed, the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: This is one of the days of Allah ; he who wishes may fast on it.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:

text: When the Prophet (п·ә) came to Medina, he found the Jews observing fast on the day of 'Ashurah; so they were asked about it (by the Prophet). They said: This is a day on which Allah gave Moses domination over Pharaoh. We fast on it out of reverence to him. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: We have a closer connection with Moses than you have. He then gave orders that it should be observed.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:

text: When the Prophet (п·ә) on the day of 'Ashurah and commanded us to fast on it, they (i.e. Companions) said: Messenger of Allah, this is a day which is considered great by Jews and Christians ? The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: When the next year comes, we shall fast on the 9th of Muharram. But the next year the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) breathed his last.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:

text: I came to Ibn 'Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ who was leaning against his sheet of cloth in the Sacred Mosque (al-Masjid al-Haram). I asked him about fasting on the day of 'Ashurah. He said: When you sight the moon of al-Muharram, count (the days). When the 9th of Muharram comes, fast from the morning. I said: Would Muhammad (п·ә) observe this fast ? He replied: Thus Muhammad (п·ә) used to fast.
narrator: Al-Hakam bin al-A'raj said:

text: The people of the tribe Aslam came to the Prophet (п·ә). He said (to them): Did you fast on this day? They replied: No. He said: Complete the rest of your day, and make atonement for it.
narrator: Abdur Rahman reported on the authority of his uncle:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said to me: The fast most liked by Allah is the one observed by Dawud (David), and the prayer dearer to Allah is the one offered by Dawud (David): he would sleep half the night, and stand (in prayer) one-third of it, and sleep one-sixth of it. He would go without fasting one day, and fast the other day.
narrator: 'Abdullah bin 'And (bin al-'As) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to command us to fast the days of the white (nights): thirteenth, fourteenth and fifteenth of the month. He said: This is like keeping perpetual fast.
narrator: Narrated Qatadah Ibn Malhan al-Qaysi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to fast three days every month.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Mas'ud ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to fast three days every month: Monday, Thursday and Monday in the next week.
narrator: Narrated HafsahШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , Ummul Muminin:

text: I entered upon Umm Salamah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ and asked her about fasting. She said: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to command me to fast three days every month beginning with Monday or Thursday.
narrator: Narrated Hunaydah al-Khuzai reported on the authority of her mother who said:

text: I asked Aishah  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ : Would the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… fast three days every month ? She replied: Yes. I asked: Which days in the month he used to fast ? She replied: He did not care which days of the month he fasted.
narrator: Muadhah said:

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: He who does not determine to fast before dawn does not fast.
narrator: Narrated Hafsah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , Ummul Muminin:

text: When the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… entered upon me, he would ask: Do you have food ? When we said: No, he would say: I am fasting. Waki added in his version: Another day when he entered upon us, we said: Messenger of Allah, some pudding (hair) has been presented to us and we have retained it for you. He said: Bring it to me. Talha said: He fasted in the morning, but broke his fast (that day).
narrator: Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: On the days of the conquest of Makkah, when Makkah was captured, Fatimah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ came and sat on the left side of the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…, and Umm Hani was on his right side. A slave-girl brought a vessel which contained some drink; she gave it to him and he drank of it. He then gave it to Umm Hani who drank of it. She said: Messenger of Allah, I have broken my fast; I was fasting. He said to her: Were you making atonement for something? She replied: No. He said: Then it does not harm you if it was voluntary (fast).
narrator: Narrated Umm Hani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :

text: Some food was presented to me and Hafsah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ . We were fasting, but broke our fast. Then the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… entered upon us. We said to him: A gift was presented to us; we coveted it and we broke our fast. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: There is no harm to you; keep a fast another day in lieu of it.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , Ummul Muminin:

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: It is not allowable for a woman to keep (voluntary) fast when her husband is present without his permission, and she may not allow anyone to enter his house without his permission.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text:
The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: When one of you receives an invitation (for a meal), he should accept it. If he isn to fasting, he should eat, and if he is fasten, he should pray. Hisham said: The word salat means to pray (for him to Allah). 
Abu Dawud said: This tradition has also been narrated by Hafs bin Ghiyath from Hisham.
narrator: Abu Hurairah reported:

text: "The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: 'If one of you is fasting, and is inviting to  meal, then let him say: I am fasting.'"
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to observe retirement (i'tikaf) to the mosque during the last ten days of Ramadan till Allah took him, and then his wives observed retirement to the mosque after his death.
narrator: 'Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to observe i'tikaf during the last ten days of Ramadan. One year he did not observe i'tikaf. When the next year came, he observed i'tikaf for twenty nights (i.e. days).
narrator: Narrated Ubayy Ibn Ka'b ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to observe I'tikaf during the last ten days of Ramadan. Nafi' said: 'Abdullah (bin 'Umar) showed me the place in the mosque where Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to observe I'tikaf.
narrator: Ibn 'Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: The Prophet (п·ә) used to observe I'tikaf during ten days of Ramadan every year. But when the year in which he died, he observed I'tikaf for twenty days.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: When the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) observed I'tikaf, he would put his head near me, and I would comb it. and he entered the house only to fulfill human needs (i.e. to urinate or to relieve himself).
narrator: 'Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: And Yunus also narrated in a similar way from al-Zuhri, and no one supported Malik in his narration from 'Urwah from 'Umrah; and Ma'mar, Ziyad bin Sad and others have also narrated it from al-Zuhri from 'Urwah on the authority of 'Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ .
narrator: Abu Dawud said:

text: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to observe I'tikaf in the mosque and put his head near me through the opening of the apartment, and I would wash his head. Musaddad said: "And I would comb it while I was menstruating."
narrator: 'Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: вҖңWhen he was at the gate of the mosque which was near the gate of Umm Salamah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , two men passed them. The narrator then transmitted the tradition to the same effect.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Al Zuhri through a different chain of narrators. In this version she said:

text: вҖңThe Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to pass by a patient while he was observing IвҖҷtikaf (in the mosque) but he passed as usual and did not stay asking about him. вҖқ According to the version of Ibn Isa she said вҖңThe Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… would visit a patient while he was observing IвҖҷtikaf. вҖқ
narrator: Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ said:

text: The sunnah for one who is observing Itikaf (in a mosque) is not to visit a patient, or to attend a funeral, or touch or embrace one's wife, or go out for anything but necessary purposes. There is no Itikaf without fasting, and there is no Itikaf except in a congregational mosque.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , Ummul Muminin:

text: Umar (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) took a vow in the pre-Islamic days to spend a night or a day in devotion near the Kabah (in the sacred mosque). He asked the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… about it. He said: Observe Itikaf (i.e. spend a night or a day near the Kabah) and fast.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: While he (Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ) was observing Itikaf (in the sacred mosque), the people uttered (loudly): Allah is most great. He said: What is this, Abdullah? He said: These are the captives of the Hawazin whom the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… has set free. He said: This slave-girl too? He sent her along with them.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above (No. 2468) has also been transmitted by Abdullah Ibn Budayl through a different chain of narrators in a similar way. This version adds:

text: вҖңOne of the wives of the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… observed IвҖҷtikaf along with him (in the mosque). She would see yellowness and redness. Sometimes we would place a vessel while she prayed. вҖқ
narrator: Aishah (ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ ) said:

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone swears a false oath in confinement, he should make his seat in Hell on account of his (act).
narrator: Narrated Imran Ibn Husayn ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A man of Kindah and a man of Hadramawt brought their dispute to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… about a land in the Yemen. Al-Hadrami said: Messenger of Allah, the father of this (man) usurped my land and it is in his possession. The Prophet asked: Have you any evidence? Al-Hadrami replied: No, but I make him swear (that he should say) that he does not know that it is my land which his father usurped from me. Al-Kindi became ready to take the oath. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone usurps the property by taking an oath, he will meet Allah while his hand is mutilated. Al-Kindi then said: It is his land.
narrator: Narrated Al-Ashath Ibn Qays ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: One should not take a false oath at this pulpit of mine even on a green tooth-stick; otherwise he will make his abode in Hell, or Hell will be certain for him.
narrator: Narrated Jabir Ibn Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: If anyone swears on oath is the course which he says: By al-Lat he should say: There is no god but Allah, and that if anyone says to his friend: Come and let me play for money with you, he should give something in charity (sadaqah).
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Do not swear by your fathers, or by your mothers, or by rivals to Allah; and swear by Allah only, and swear by Allah only when you are speaking the truth.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… found Umar al-Khattab in a caravan while he was swearing by his father. So he said: Allah forbids you to swear by forefathers. If anyone swears, he must swear by Allah or keep silence.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: I swear by Allah, I never swore by it personally or reporting it from others.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through a different chain of narrators to the same effect up to the words by your fathers . This version adds: Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ heard a man swearing: No, I swear by the Kabah. Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said to him: I heard the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… say: He who swears by anyone but Allah is polytheist.
narrator: Sa'd Ibn Ubaydah said:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Referring to the story of a bedouin, Talhah bin Ubaidullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: He became successful, by his father, if he speaks the truth, he will enter paradise, by his father, if he speaks truth.
narrator: Narrator Malik bin Abi Amir from his father:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: He who swears by Amanah (faithfulness) is not one of our number.
narrator: Narrated Buraydah Ibn al-Hasib ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: Your oath should be about something regarding which your companion will believe you. Musaddad said: Abdullah bin Abi Salih narrated to me. Abu Dawud said: Both of them refer to the same person: 'Abbad bin Abu Salih and Abdullah bin Abi Salih.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: We went out intending (to visit) the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and Wail Ibn Hujr  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ was with us. His enemy caught him. The people desisted from swearing an oath, but I took an oath that he was my brother. So he left him. We then came to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…, and I informed him that the people desisted from taking the oath, but I swore that he was my brother. He said: You spoke the truth: A Muslim is a brother of a Muslim.
narrator: Narrated Suwayd Ibn Hanzalah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: He took oath of allegiance to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… under the tree. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone swears by religion other than Islam falsely, he is like what has has said. If anyone kills himself with something, he will be punished with it on the Day of Resurrection. A vow over which a man has no control is not binding on him.
narrator: Narrated Thabit bin Adh-Dahhak ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone takes an oath and says: I am free from Islam; now if he is a liar (in his oath), he will not return to Islam with soundness.
narrator: Narrated Buraydah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from his father:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: I saw that the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… put a date on a loaf and said: This is a thing eaten with bread (condiments).
narrator: Narrated Yusuf Ibn Abdullah Ibn Salam:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A similar tradition has also been transmitted by Yusuf bin Abdullah bin Salam through a different chain of narrators.
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone says when swearing an oath: If Allah wills, he makes an exception.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone swears an oath and makes an exception, he may fulfil it if he wishes and break it if he wishes without any accountability for breaking.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The oath which the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… often used was this: No, by Him who overturns the hearts.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: When the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… swore an oath strongly, he said: No, by Him in Whose hand is the soul of Abul Qasim.
narrator: Narrated Abu Saeed al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: When the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… swore an oath, it was: No, and I beg forgiveness of Allah.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Laqit bin Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ came to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… in a delegation. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… then said: By the age of thy god.
narrator: Narrated Asim bin Laqit:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ adjured the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Do not adjure an oath.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said: I had a dream last night, and he then mentioned it. So Abu Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ interpreted it. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: You are partly right and partly wrong. He then said: I adjure you, Messenger of Allah, may my father be sacrificed on you, do tell me the mistake I have committed. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Do not adjure.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: But there is no mention of the word qasam (oath). It has the words: He did not inform him.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ through a different chain of narrators.
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Expiation (for breaking the oath) has not reached me.
narrator: A similar tradition has also been transmitted by Abdur-Rahman bin Abi Bakr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ through a different chain of narrators. This version adds on the authority of Salim:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: There were two brothers among the Ansar who shared an inheritance. When one of them asked the other for the portion due to him, he replied: If you ask me again for the portion due to you, all my property will be devoted to the decoration of the Kabah. Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said to him: The Kabah does not need your property. Make atonement for your oath and speak to your brother. I heard the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… say: An oath or vow to disobey the Lord, or to break ties of relationship or about something over which one has no control is not binding on you.
narrator: Saeed bin al-Musayyab said:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: A vow is binding in those things by which the pleasure of Allah is sought, and an oath to break ties of relationship is not binding.
narrator: Narrated Amr bin Shubah, from his father, from his grandfather:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Two men brought their dispute to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… asked the plaintiff to produce evidence, but he had no evidence. So he asked the defendant to swear. He swore by Allah There is no god but He. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Yes, you have done it, but you have been forgiven for the sincerity of the statement: There is no god but Allah. Abu Dawud said: This tradition means that he did not command him to make atonement
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: I swear by Allah that if Allah wills I shall swear on an oath and then consider something else to be better than it without making atonement for my oath and doing the thing that is better. Or he said (according to another version): But doing the thing that is better and making atonement for my oath.
narrator: Narrated Abu Burdah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, from his father:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said to me: Abdur-Rahman bin Samurah, when you swear an oath and consider something else to be better than it, do the thing that is beter and make atonement for your oath. Abu Dawud said: I heard Ahmad (b. Hanbal) permitting to make atonement before breaking the oath.
narrator: Narrated Abdur-Rahman bin Samurah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Make atonement for your oath and then do the thing that is better. Abu Dawud said: The version of this tradition transmitted by Abu Musa al-Ashari, Adi bin Hatim and Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ… are variant. Some of them indicate breaking the oath before making atonement, and other making atonement before breaking the oath.
narrator: A similar tradition has been transmitted by Abdur-Rahman bin Samurah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through a different chain if narrators. This version has:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Umm Habib bint Dhu'aib bin Qais Al-Muzzaniyawho was married to a man of Aslam, then she was married to a nephew of Safiyyah, the wife of the prophet п·ә. Ibn Harmalah (one of the narrators) said: "Umm Habib gave us a sa' and told us narration from the nephew of Safiyyah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ on the authority of Safiyyah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ that it was the sa' of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. Anas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: I tested it and found its capacity two and half mudd according to the mudd of Hisham.
narrator: Narrated Abdur Rahman bin Harmalah said:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: We had a makkuk which was called Makkuk Khalid. Its capacity was two measurements according to the measurements of Harun. The narrator said: The sa' of Khalid was the sa' of Hisham bin Abdul-Malik.
narrator: Narrated Muhammad bin Muhmmad bin Khallad Abu Umar:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: When Khalid al-Qasri was made ruler (of Hijaz and Kufah), he doubled the measure of sa'. The sa' then measured sixteen rotls. Abu Dawud said: Muhammad bin Muhammad bin Khattab was slain by Negroes in confinement. He said while signing with his hand: in this way . Abu Dawud extended his hand and turned his palms towards earth and said: I saw him in the dream and asked him: How did Allah deal with you ? He replied: He admitted to Paradise. I said: Your detention did not harm you.
narrator: Narrated Umayyah bin Khalid:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: I said: Messenger of Allah, I have a slave girl whom I slapped. This grieved the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. I said to him: Should I not emancipate her? He said: Bring her to me. He said: Then I brought her. He asked: Where is Allah ? She replied: In the heaven. He said: Who am I ? She replied: You are the Messenger of Allah. He said: Emancipate her, she is a believer.
narrator: Narrated Muawiyah bin al-Hakam al-Sulami:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: She's mother left a will to emancipate a believing slave on her behalf. So he came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said: Messenger of Allah, my mother left a will that I should emancipate a believing slave for her, and I have a black Nubian slave-girl. He mentioned a tradition about the test of the girl. Abu Dawud said: Khalid bin Abdullah narrated this tradition direct from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. He did not mention the name of al-Sharid.
narrator: Narrated Ash-Sharid:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A man brought the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… a black slave girl. He said: Messenger of Allah, emancipation of believing slave is due to me. He asked her: Where is Allah ? She pointed to the heaven with her finger. He then asked her: Who am I ? She pointed to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and to the heaven, that is to say: You are the Messenger of Allah. He then said: Set her free, she is a believer.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: I swear by Allah, I shall fight against the Quraysh; I swear by Allah, I shall fight against the Quraysh; I swear by Allah, I shall fight against the Quraysh. He then said: If Allah wills. Abu Dawud said: A number of persons have narrated this tradition from Sharik, from Simak, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ who reported from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…: But he did not fight against them.
narrator: Narrated Ikrimah:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: I swear by Allah, I shall fight against the Quraish. The then said: If Allah wills. He again said: I swear by Allah, I shall fight against the Quraish if Allah wills. He again said: I swear by Allah, I shall fight against the Quraish. He then kept silence. Then he said: If Allah wills. Abu Dawud said: Al-Walid bin Muslim said on the authority of Sharik: He then said: But he did not fight against them.
narrator: Narrated Ikrimah:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… forbade to make a vow. He said: It has not effect against fate, it is only from the miserly that it is means by which something is extracted. Musaddad said: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: A vow does not avert anything (i. e. has no effect against fate).
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: A vow does not provide for the son of Adam anything which I did not decree for him, but a vow draws it. A Divine decree is one which I have destined, it is extracted from a miser. He is given what he was not given before.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: If anyone vows to obey Allah, let him obey Him, but if anyone vows to disobey Him, let him not disobey Him.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: No vow must be taken to do an act of disobedience, and the atonement for it is the same as for an oath.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§, Ummul Muminin:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: I heard Ahmad bin Shabbuyah say: Ibn al-Mubarak said about this tradition that Abu Salamah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ had transmitted it. This indicates that al-Zuhri did not hear it from Abu Salamah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ . Ahmad bin Muhammad said: This is verified by what Ayyub bin Sulaiman narrated to us. Abu Dawud said: I heard Ahmad bin Hanbal say: I have corrupted this tradition for us. He was asked: Do you think that it is correct that this tradition has been corrupted? Has any person other than Ibn Abi Uwais transmitted it ? He replied: Ayyub was similar to him in respect of reliability, and Ayyub transmitted it.
narrator: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by al-Zuhri through a different chain of narrators to the same effect. Abu Dawud said:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: He consulted the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… about his sister who took a vow to perform hajj barefooted and bareheaded. So he said: Command her to cover her head and to ride, and to fast three days.
narrator: Narrated Uqbah Ibn Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Abu Saeed al-Ru'aini with the same chain as narrated by Yahya (bin Saeed) and to the same effect.
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A man came to Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said: Messenger of Allah, my sister has taken a vow to perform hajj on foot. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Allah gets no good from the affliction your sister imposed on herself, so let her perform hajj riding and make atonement for her oath.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The sister of Uqbah bin Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ took vow to walk on foot to the Kabah. Thereupon the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ordered her to ride and slaughter a sacrificial animal.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: When the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… was informed that the sister of Uqbah bin Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ had taken a vow to perform Hajj on foot, he said: Allah is not in need of her vow. So ask her to ride. Abu Dawud said: Saib bin 'Arubah has transmitted a similar tradition. Khalid has also transmitted a similar tradition on the authority of Ikrimah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§  from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ….
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The tradition about the sister of Uqbah bin Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ as narrated by Hisham, but he made no mention of the sacrificial animal. In his version he said: Ask your sister to ride. Abu Dawud said: Khalid narrated it from Ikrimah to the same effect as narrated by Hisham.
narrator: Narrated Ikrimah:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: My sister took a vow to walk on foot to the House of Allah (i. e. Kabah). She asked me to consult the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… about her. So I consulted the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. He said: Let her walk and ride.
narrator: Narrated Uqbah bin Amir al-Juhani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: While the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… was preaching a man was standing in the sun. He asked about him. They said: He is Abu Isra'il who has taken a vow to stand and not to sit, or go into shade, or speak, but to fast. Thereupon he said: Command him to speak, to go into the shade, sit and complete his fast.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… saw a man that he was supported between his sons. He asked about him, and (the people) said: He has taken a vow to walk (on foot). Thereupon he said: Allah has no need that this man should punish himself, and he ordered him to ride. Abu Dawud said: Amr bin Abi Amir has also narrated a similar tradition from al-Araj on the authority of Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ….
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… while going round the Kabah passed a man who was led with a ring of bridle in his nose. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… cut it off with his hand and ordered to lead him by catching his hand.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The sister of Uqbah Ibn Amir took a vow that she would perform hajj on foot, and she was unable to do so. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Allah is not in need of the walking of your sister. She must ride and offer a sacrificial camel.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: He said to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…: My sister has taken a vow that she will walk to the House of Allah (the Kabah). Thereupon he said: Allah will not do anything of the walking of your sister to the House of Allah (i.e. the Kabah).
narrator: Narrated Uqbah Ibn Amir al-Juhani ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A man stood on the day of Conquest (of Makkah) and said: Messenger of Allah, I have vowed to Allah that if He grants conquest of Makkah at your hands, I shall pray two rak'ahs in Jerusalem. He replied: Pray here. He repeated (his statement) to him and he said: Pray here. He again repeated (his statement) to him. He (the Prophet) replied: Pursue your own course, then. Abu Dawud said: A similar tradition has been narrated by Abdur-Rahman bin Awf ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ….
narrator: Narrated Jabir Ibn Abdullah:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: By Him Who sent Muhammad with truth, if you prayed here, this would be sufficient for you like the prayer in Jerusalem. Abu Dawud said: This tradition has also been transmitted by al-Ansari, from Ibn-Juraij. He said: Jafar bin Umar and Amr bin Hayyah. He said: They transmitted from Abdur-Rahman bin Awf  ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ and from the Companions of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ….
narrator: The tradition mentioned above (No. 3299) has also been transmitted by Umar ibn Abdur-Rahman Ibn Awf on the authority of his father and the Companions of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. This version has:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: Saad bin Ubadah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ asked the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…: My Mother has died and she could not fulfill her vow which she had taken. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Fulfill it on her behalf.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A woman made a voyage and vowed that she would fast one month if Allah made her reach her destination with peace and security. Allah made her reach her destination with security but she died before she could fast. Her daughter or sister (the narrator doubted) came to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. So he commanded to fast on her behalf.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A woman came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said: I gave a slave girl to my mother, but she died and left the salve-girl. He said: Your reward became certain for you, and she (the slave-girl) returned to you as inheritance. She said: She died and one month's fast was due from her. He (the narrator) then mentioned the tradition similar to the one mentioned by Amr bin 'Awn.
narrator: Narrated Buraidah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A woman came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said (to him) that one month's fast was due from her mother who had died. May I fulfill them on her behalf? He asked: Suppose some debt was due from your mother, would you pay it ? She replied: Yes. He said: So the debt due to Allah is the one which most deserves to be paid.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: If anyone dies when some fast due from him has been unfulfilled, his heir must fast on his behalf.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A woman came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said: Messenger of Allah, I have taken a vow to play the tambourine over you. He said: Fulfil your vow. She said: And I have taken a vow to perform a sacrifice in such a such a place, a place in which people had performed sacrifices in pre-Islamic times. He asked: For an Idol? She replied: No. He asked: For an image? She replied: No. He said: Fulfil your vow.
narrator: Narrated Amr bin Suhaib, from his father, from his grandfather said:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: In the time of the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… a man took a vow to slaughter a camel at Buwanah. So he came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and said: I have taken a vow to sacrifice a camel at Buwanah. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… asked: Did the place contain any idol worshipped in pre-Islamic times? They (the people) said: No. He asked: Was any pre-Islamic festival observed there? They replied: No. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Fulfil your vow, for a vow to do an act of disobedience to Allah must not be fulfilled, neither must one do something over which a human being has no control.
narrator: Narrated Thabit Ibn ad-Dahhak:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: (The Prophet asked): Does it contain an idol or was a festival of pre-Islamic times celebrated there ? He replied: No. I said: This mother of mine has taken a vow and walking (is binding on her). May I fulfill it on her behalf ? Sometimes the narrator Bashshar said: May we fulfill in on her behalf ? He said: Yes.
narrator: A similar tradition has also been transmitted in brief by Maimunah daughter of Kardam son of Sufyan on the authority of her father through a different chain of narrators. This version adds:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: I said: Messenger of Allah, to make my repentance complete I should divest myself of my property as sadaqah (alms) for Allah and His Messenger. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Retain some of your property, for that will be better for you. So he said: I shall retain the portion I have at Khaybar.
narrator: Narrated Kab Ibn Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: To the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… when his repentance was accepted: I should divest myself of my property. He then mentioned a similar tradition up to the words, better for you .
narrator: Narrated Kab bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, from his father:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: He said to AbuLubabah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ or someone else whom Allah wished; or to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…: To make my repentance complete I should depart from the house of my people in which I fell into sin, and that I should divest myself of all my property as sadaqah (alms). He said: A third (of your property) will be sufficient for you.
narrator: Narrated Kab Ibn Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: He then mentioned the tradition to the same effect. This versions attributes this story to Abu Lubabah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ. Abu Dawud said: This tradition has been narrated by Yunus from Ibn Shihab from some of the children of al-Saib son of Abu Lubabah. A similar tradition has also been transmitted by al-Zabidi from al-Zuhri from Husain bin al-Saib son of Abu Lubabah.
narrator: This tradition has also been transmitted by Ibn Kab bin Malik through a different chain of narrators. This version has:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: I said: Messenger of Allah, to make my atonement complete I should divest myself of my all property as sadaqah (alms) for Allah and His Messenger. He said: No. I said: The half of it. He said: No. I said: Then a third of it. He said: Yes. I said: I shall retain the portion I have at Khaybar.
narrator: Narrated Abdur Rehman bin Abdullah bin Kab Ibn Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ, from his father, from his grand father:
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone takes a vow but does not name it, its atonement is the same as that for an oath, if anyone takes a vow to do an act of disobedience, its atonement is the same as that for an oath, if anyone takes a vow he is unable to fulfill, its atonement is the same as that for an oath, but if anyone takes a vow he is able to fulfill, he must do so. Abu Dawud said: This tradition has been transmitted by Waki and others on the authority of Abdullah bin Saeed bin Abi al-Hind, but they traced it no farther back than Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: The atonement for a vow is the same as for an oath. Abu Dawud said: This tradition has also been transmitted by Amr bin al-Harith from Kab bin Alqamah, from Ibn Shamasah on the authority of Uqbah.
narrator: Narrated Uqbah bin Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: A similar tradition has also been transmitted by Uqbah bin Amir ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ from the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… through a different chain of narrators.
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: He said: Messenger of Allah, I took a vow in pre-Islamic times that I would stay in the sacred mosque (Masjid Haram) as a devotion (Itikaf). The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Fulfill your vow.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Oaths and Vows (Kitab Al-Aiman Wa Al-Nudhur)

text: The tradition mentioned above has also been transmitted by Abu al-Darda ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ through a different chain of narrators to the same effect from the Holy Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… .

The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: If anyone pursues a path in search of knowledge, Allah will thereby make easy for him a path to paradise; and he who is made slow by his actions will not be speeded by his genealogy.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: When he was sitting with the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… and a Jew was also with him, a funeral passed by him. He (the Jew) asked (Him): Muhammad, does this funeral speak? The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Allah has more knowledge. The Jew said: It speaks. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: Whatever the people of the Book tell you, do not verify them, nor falsify them, but say: We believe in Allah and His Messenger. If it is false, do not confirm it, and if it is right, do not falsify it.
narrator: Narrated Abu Namlah al-Ansari ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ordered me (to learn the writing of the Jews), so I learnt for him the writing of the Jews. He said: I swear by Allah, I do not trust Jews in respect of writing for me. So I learnt it, and only a fortnight passed that. I mastered it. I would write for him when he wrote (to them), and read to him when something was written to him.
narrator: Narrated Zayd bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I used to write everything which I heard from the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. I intended (by it) to memorize it. The Quraysh prohibited me saying: Do you write everything that you hear from him while the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… is a human being: he speaks in anger and pleasure? So I stopped writing, and mentioned it to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. He signaled with his finger to him mouth and said: Write, by Him in Whose hand my soul lies, only right comes out from it.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Amr bin al-As ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: Zayd ibn Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ entered upon Muawiyah and asked him about a tradition. He ordered a man to write it. Zayd ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ordered us not to write any of his traditions. So he erased it.
narrator: Narrated Al-Muttalib bin Abdullah bin Hantab said:

text: "We used not to write anything but the Tasha-hud and the Qur'an."
narrator: It was narrated that Abu Saeed Al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: When Mecca was conquered, the Holy Prophet (п·ә) stood up. He (Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ) then mentioned the sermon of the Holy Prophet (п·ә). He said: A man of the Yemen, who was called Abu Shah, got up and said: Messenger of Allah! Write it for me. He said: Write it for Abu Shah.
narrator: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said :

text: I asked Abu Amr: What are they writing? He said: The sermon which he heard that day.
narrator: Al-Walid said:

text: I asked al-zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ : What prevents you from narrating traditions from the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as his Companions narrate from him. But I heard him say: He who lies about me deliberately will certainly come to his abode in Hell.
narrator: Abdullah bin al-Zubair ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ said that his father said:

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone interprets the Book of Allah in the light of his opinion even if he is right, he has erred.
narrator: Narrated Jundub ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: Abu Salam said on the authority of a man who served the Holy Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… that whenever he talked, he repeated it three times.
narrator: Narrated Sabiq bin Najiyah:

text: Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ sat beside the apartment of Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ while she was praying. He then began to say: Listen, O lady of the apartment, saying twice. When she finish her prayer, she said: Are you not surprised at him and his narrate traditions from the Messenger of the Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… gave a talk, a man could count it if he wished to count.
narrator: Urwah said:

text: Are you not surprised at Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ ? He came and sat beside my apartment, and began to narrate traditions from the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… making me hear them. I am saying supererogatory prayer. He got up (and went away) before I finished my prayer. Had I found him, I would have replied to him. The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… did not narrate traditions quickly one after another as you narrate quickly.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ , Ummul Muminin:

text: The Holy Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… forbade the discussion of thorny questions.
narrator: Narrated Muawiyah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone is given a legal decision ignorantly, the sin rests on the one who gave it. Sulayman al-Mahri added in his version: If anyone advises his brother, knowing that guidance lies in another direction, he has deceived him. These are the wordings of Sulayman.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: He who is asked something he knows and conceals it will have a bridle of fire put on him on the Day of Resurrection.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: You hear (from me), and others will hear from you; and people will hear from them who heard from you.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… say: May Allah brighten a man who hears a tradition from us, gets it by heart and passes it on to others. Many a bearer of knowledge conveys it to one who is more versed than he is; and many a bearer of knowledge is not versed in it.
narrator: Narrated Zayd bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: I swear on Allah, it will be better for you that Allah should give guidance to one man through your agency than that you should acquire the red ones among the camels.
narrator: Sahl bin Saad ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: relate traditions from the children of Isra'il; there is no harm.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to relate to us traditions from the children of Isra'il till morning came; he would not get up except for obligatory prayer.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Amr ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: If anyone acquires knowledge of things by which Allah's good pleasure is sought, but acquires it only to get some worldly advantage, he will not experience the arf, i.e. the odour, of Paradise.
narrator: Narrated Abu Hurairah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: I heard the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… say: Only a ruler, or one put in charge, or one who is presumptuous, gives instructions.
narrator: Narrated Awf bin Malik al-Ashjai ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: That I sit in the company of the people who remember Allah the Exalted from morning prayer till the sun rises is clearer to me than that I emancipate four slaves from the children of Isra'il, and that I sit with the people who remember Allah from afternoon prayer till the sun sets is dearer to me than that I emancipate four slaves.
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…said to me: recite Surat al-NisaвҖҷ. I asked: Shail I recite to you what was sent down to you ? He replied: I like to here it from someone else. So I recite (it) until I reached this verse вҖңHow then shall it be when We bring from every people a witness ?вҖқ. Then raised my head and saw tears falling from his eyes.
narrator: Abdullah (bin Masud) ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read the Quranic verse, And take ye the Station of Abraham as a place of prayer.
narrator: Narrated Jabir bin Abdullah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: A man got up (for prayer) at night, he read the Quran and raised his voice in reading. When the morning came, the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: May Allah have mercy on so-and-so! Last night he reminded me a number of verses which I was about to forget.
narrator: Narrated Aishah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The verse And no Prophet could (ever) be false to his trust was revealed about a red velvet. When it was found missing on the day of Badr, some people said; Perhaps the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… has taken it. So Allah, the Exalted, sent down And no prophet could (ever) be false to his trust to the end of the verse. Abu Dawud said: In the word yaghulla the letter ya has a short vowel a.
narrator: Narrated Abdullah bin Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as saying: O Allah, I seek refuge in Thee from niggardliness and old age.
narrator: Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ reported:
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: I came in the deputation of Banu al-Muntafiq to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. He then narrated the rest of the tradition. The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: la tahsibanna (do not think) and did not say: la tahsabanna (do not think).
narrator: Narrated Laqit bin Sabirah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Muslims met a man with some sheep of his. He said: Peace be upon you. But they killed him and took those few sheep. Thereupon the following Quranic verse was revealed: . . . And say to anyone who offers you a salutation: Thou art none of believer, coveting the perishable good of this life. meaning these few sheep.
narrator: Narrated Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… used to read: Not equal are those believers who sit (at home) and receive no hurt (ghayru ulid-darari) but the narrator Saeed did not say the words used to read
narrator: Narrated Zayd bin Thabit ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read the verse: eye for eye (al-'aynu bil-'ayn).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read the verse: We ordained therein for them: Life for life and eye for eye (an-nafsa bin-nafsi wal-'aynu bil-'ayn).
narrator: Narrated Anas bin Malik ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: I recited to Abdullah bin Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ the verse: It is Allah Who created you in a state of (helplessness) weakness (min da'f). He said: (Read) min du'f. I recited it to the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… as you recited it to me, and he gripped me as I gripped you.
narrator: Narrated Atiyyah bin Saad al-Awfi said:
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read the verse mentioned above, min du'f.
narrator: Narrated Abu Saeed al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: Say, in the bounty of Allah, and in His mercy- in that let you rejoice.
narrator: Narrated Ubayy bin Kab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read the verse: Say: In the bounty of Allah and in His mercy--in that let you rejoice: that is better than the wealth you hoard.
narrator: (There is another chain) from Ubayy ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ said:
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: She heard the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read the verse: He acted unrighteously. (innahu 'amila ghayra salih).
narrator: Narrated Asma daughter of Yazid ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: I asked Umm Salamah ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒШ§ : How did the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read this verse: For his conduct is unrighteous (innahu 'amalun ghayru salih . She replied: He read it: He acted unrighteously (innahu 'amila ghayra salih). Abu Dawud said: This tradition has been transmitted by Harun al-Nahwi and Musa bin Khalaf from Thabit as reported by the narrator Abdul-Aziz.
narrator: Narrated Shahr bin Hawshab said:
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: When the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… prayed, he began with himself and said: May the mercy of Allah be upon us and upon Moses. If he had patience, he would have seen marvels from his Companion. But he said: (Moses) said: If ever I ask thee about anything after this, keep me not in they company: then wouldst thou have received (full) excuse from my side . Hamzah lengthened it.
narrator: Narrated Ubayy ibn Kab:
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… read the Quranic verse: Thou hast received (full) excuse from me (min ladunni) and put tashdid (doubling of consonants) on nun (n).
narrator: Narrated Ubayy bin Kab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: I heard Ibn Abbas ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ say: Ubayy bin Kab ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ made me read the following verse as the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… made him read: in a spring of murky water (fi 'aynin hami'atin) with short vowel a after h.
narrator: Narrated Misda Abu yahya said:
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: The Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ… said: A man from the Illiyyun will look downwards at the people of Paradise and Paradise will be glittering as if it were a brilliant star. He (the narrator) said: In this way the word durriun (brilliant) occurs in this tradition, i. e. the letter dal has short vowel u and it has no hamzah'. Abu Bakr and Umar ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒЩ…Ш§ will be of them and will have some additional blessings.
narrator: Narrated Abu Saeed al-Khudri ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)

text: I came to the Prophet ШөЩ„ЫҢ вҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ вҖҢШ№Щ„ЫҢЫҒ вҖҢЩҲШіЩ„Щ…. He then narrated the rest of the tradition. A man from the people said: Messenger of Allah! tell us about Saba'; what is it: land or woman? He replied: It is neither land nor woman; it is a man to whom ten children of the Arabs were born: six of them lived in the Yemen and four lived in Syria. The narrator Uthman said al-Ghatafani instead of al-Ghutayfi. He said: It has been transmitted to us by al-Hasan bin al-Hakam an-Nakha'i.
narrator: Narrated Farwah bin Musayk al-Ghutayfi ШұШ¶ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЫҒ Ш№ЩҶЫҒ :
chapterName: Dialects and Readings of the Qur'an (Kitab Al-Huruf Wa Al-Qira'at)
